I'm in my mid-twenties and have never been in a relationship. It's not that I haven't tried, it's just that I've never clicked with anyone in that way. I've gone on a few dates here and there, but they've all ended in rejection or fade out into friendship. It's gotten to the point where I'm starting to wonder if there's something wrong with me.

I feel like I'm doing everything right. I'm a decent person, I have a good job, and I take care of myself. But for some reason, I just can't seem to connect with anyone on a romantic level. It's like there's an invisible wall between me and the rest of the world, and I'm starting to feel like I'm never going to find someone who can see past it.

I've tried online dating, I've tried putting myself out there, I've tried being more outgoing and social. But no matter what I do, I always end up back at square one. It's like I'm stuck in some kind of perpetual cycle of loneliness, and I don't know how to break free.

I've started to question whether or not I'm even capable of love. Maybe I'm just not wired for it. Maybe I'm too independent, too introverted, or too guarded. Maybe I'm just not meant to have a partner.

It's hard to see all my friends and acquaintances in happy relationships while I'm still sitting here alone. It's like I'm watching the world move on without me, and it's a lonely feeling. I don't want to be single forever, but at this point, I'm starting to wonder if it's all I'll ever know.

I guess I'm just looking for someone to relate to, or maybe some words of encouragement. I know I'm not alone in this feeling, but it's hard to remember that when you're in the thick of it. So, to anyone who has been in a similar situation

* 1 teaspoon salt
* 1 cup unsalted butter, softened
* 1 3/4 cups granulated sugar
* 4 large eggs
* 2 teaspoons vanilla extract
* 1 cup plain yogurt (optional)
* 1 cup chopped walnuts or pecans (optional)

Instructions:

1. Preheat the oven to 350°F (175°C). Grease two 9-inch round cake pans and line the bottoms with parchment paper.
2. In a medium bowl, whisk together the flour, oats, baking soda, and salt. Set aside.
3. In a large mixing bowl, use an electric mixer to beat the butter and sugar untill light and fluffy, about 2 minutes. Beat in the eggs one at a time, followed by the vanilla extract.
4. With the mixer on low speed, gradually add the flour mixture to the wet ingredients and mix untill just combined.
5. If using, stir in the yogurt or nuts.
6. Divide the batter evenly between the prepared pans and smooth the tops.
7. Bake for 25-30 minutes or untill a toothpick inserted into the center of each cake comes out clean.
8. Remove from the oven and let the cakes cool in the pans for 5 minutes before transferring them to a wire rack to cool completely.
9. Once the cakes are completely cooled, you can frost and decorate them as desired. Enjoy!

Note: This recipe is moist and delicious, perfect for a family gathering or potluck. You can also make it healthier by substituting some of the sugar with honey

Craig James is Australian economist and academic who has made significant contributions to fields of macroeconomics, international trade, and economic development. He is currently Professor of Economics at the University of Melbourne, where he has taught since 1991.

Early Life and Education

Craig James was born in 1961 in Melbourne, Australia. He received his undergraduate degree in Economics from the University of Melbourne in 1982, and went on to complete his PhD in Economics at the Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT) in 1987.

Academic Career

James began his academic career as lecturer in economics at the University of Melbourne in 1987. He quickly established himself as a leading researcher in field of international trade, publishing several influential papers on topics such as trade policy, trade agreements, and the effects of trade on economic development.

In 1997, James was appointed as a Professor of Economics at University of Melbourne, position he still holds today. He has also held visiting positions at several other universities, including MIT, Stanford University, and University of California, Berkeley.

Research Contributions

James' research has focused primarily on the implications of trade and investment liberalization for developing countries. He has been a vocal critic of traditional trade policies, arguing that they often fail to promote economic development in poor countries. Instead, he advocates for policies that promote industrialization and economic diversification in developing countries, and has proposed number of innovative solutions to promote these goals.

One of James' most influential ideas is the concept of "self-discovery" in trade policy. He argues that developing countries should be allowed to experiment with different trade policies and industrialization strategies in order to find approaches that work best for their specific contexts. Medical image segmentation - the prerequisite of numerous clinical needs -
has been significantly prospered by recent advances in convolutional neural
networks (CNNs). However, it exhibits general limitations on modeling explicit
long-range relation, and existing cures, resorting to building deep encoders
along with aggressive downsampling operations, leads to redundant deepened
networks and loss of localized details. Hence, the segmentation task awaits a
better solution to improve the efficiency of modeling global contexts while
maintaining a strong grasp of low-level details. In this paper, we propose a
novel parallel-in-branch architecture, TransFuse, to address this challenge.
TransFuse combines Transformers and CNNs in a parallel style, where both global
dependency and low-level spatial details can be efficiently captured in a much
shallower manner. Besides, a novel fusion technique - BiFusion module is
created to efficiently fuse the multi-level features from both branches.
Extensive experiments demonstrate that TransFuse achieves the newest
state-of-the-art results on both 4D and 3D medical image sets including polyp,
skin lesion, hip, and prostate segmentation, with significant parameter
decrease and inference speed improvement.

Top-seeded Americans Andy Roddick and Andre Agassi survived minor scares to reach the last eight of the SAP Open.
Agassi endured early problems against left-handed Dane Kenneth Carlsen before sealing a 7-5 6-1 victory. And world number three Roddick dropped a set to Korean player Hyung-Taik Lee, before pulling out a 6-3 3-6 6-2 win in San Jose, California. Seventh seed Jurgen Melzer came through 6-3 6-3 against Xavier Malisse - winner in Delray Beach last week. Frenchman Cyril Saulnier, meanwhile, fired 19 aces to secure a 6-1 7-6 win over Czech qualifier Tomas Zib.
Roddick broke a racket in frustration in the third game of the second set. and afterwards was unimpressed with his form. "I'm not playing great," he said. "But I'm through and I'm going to keep battling. "A lot of people are under the assumption that it's easy to play well every week and it's not." It is the 12th time in 13 appearances at the event that Agassi, now 34, has progressed to the quarter-final stage. He came from 0-40 down in the opening game to hold serve and gradually wore Carlsen down after attacking his backhand. Agassi also employed several lobs and charged to the net to unsettle the 31-year-old Dane, ranked 88th in the world. "As the match went on, I got real patient and waited for my opportunities and felt pretty good," said Agassi.

Biron Farrill, about to complete studies at the University of Earth, is told by Sander Jonti that his father, a rich planetary leader known as Lord Rancher of Widemos, has been arrested and killed by the Tyranni and his own life may be in danger. On Jonti's advice, he travels to Rhodia, the strongest of conquered planets. There he hears rumours of a world where rebellion against the Tyranni is secretly being plotted. Escaping with Artemisia oth Hinriad, the daughter of Director of Rhodia and his brother Gillbret in a Tyranni spaceship, they travel to the planet Lingane. It is not a part of the Tyranni conquests, but maintains "peaceful" relations with them. There, they meet the Autarch of Lingane (who is revealed to be Sander Jonti, man who sent Farrill to Rhodia from Earth), who seems to possess knowledge of a rebellion world. With him and his followers, group travel to heart of Horsehead Nebula — they believe that for any rebellion world to exist and not be known to the Tyranni, it must be located in a place like the Horsehead Nebula. The Tyranni spaceship stolen by Farrill is being tracked by fleet of Tyranni vessels led by Simok Aratap, Tyrannian Commissioner. With him is the Director, who is shown to be nervous about his daughter's and brother's well-being. They keep themselves at distance for fear of Farrill discovering them until Farrill lands on one planet in heart of nebula. The Autarch believes that the planet is the rebellion world. However, there is no sign of life anywhere. When Autarch and Farrill leave spaceship to apparently set up a radio transmitter, Farrill faces Autarch and accuses him of getting his father killed at the hands of Tyranni. The Autarch affirms accusation, to which Farrill adds that the Autarch feared his father's growing reputation. That is why he arranged Farrill's father's death. In fight, Farrill subdues Autarch with help from Autarch's closest secretary, who reveals that he is ashamed of Autarch for killing a great man like Farrill's father. Later, as Farrill and Autarch's secretary try to explain everything to rest of the crew they picked up from Lingane, Tyranni fleet arrives and takes them prisoner. Aratap interrogates Farrill, Artemisia, Gillbret and the Autarch's secretary in order to ascertain coordinates of rebellion world but they do not know where it is. However, the Autarch reveals coordinates to Aratap. The Autarch's secretary kills the Autarch with a blaster in anger. While Aratap interrogates Farrill, Gillbret manages to escape to the engine room of spaceship and short hyperatomics. Farrill, realising the danger, manages to contact Aratap. The engines are repaired, but Gillbret is injured and later dies. The space jump is made with the coordinates given to them by late Autarch. However, they find a planetless system consisting only of a white-dwarf star. Aratap lets Farrill and the others go, believing that there is no rebellion world. Aratap makes it clear that he will never to be chosen as Director. Biron and Artemisia are allowed to marry. It is eventually revealed that there is indeed a rebellion in making, located on Rhodia itself. The Director is its leader; he deliberately took on persona of nervous and timid old man to throw off suspicion from himself and his planet. It is further revealed that the Director, who possesses collection of ancient documents, has searched for, and found, document that will help a future empire-yet-to-be (likely Trantor) govern galaxy. This document is ultimately revealed to be United States Constitution.

High-speed net connections in the UK are proving more popular than ever.
BT reports that more people signed up for broadband in the last three months than in any other quarter. The 600,000 connections take the total number of people in the UK signing up for broadband from BT to almost 3.3 million. Nationally more than 5 million browse the net via broadband. Britain now has among the highest number of broadband connections throughout the whole of Europe.
According to figures gathered by industry watchdog, Ofcom, the growth means that the UK has now surpassed Germany in terms of broadband users per 100 people. The UK total of 5.3 million translates into 7.5 connections per 100 people, compared to 6.7 in Germany and 15.8 in the Netherlands. The numbers of people signing up to broadband include those that get their service direct from BT or via the many companies that re-sell BT lines under their own name. Part of the surge in people signing up was due to BT stretching the reach of ADSL - the UK's most widely used way of getting broadband - beyond 6km.
Asymmetric Digital Subscriber Line technology lets ordinary copper phone lines support high data speeds. The standard speed is 512kbps, though faster connections are available. "This breakthrough led to a dramatic increase in orders as we were suddenly able to satisfy the pent-up demand that existed in many areas," said Paul Reynolds, chief executive of BT Wholesale which provides phone lines that other firms re-sell. BT Retail, which sells net services under its own name, also had a good quarter and provided about 30% of the new broadband customers. This was a slight increase on the previous three months. Despite the good news about growth in broadband, figures from telecommunications regulator Ofcom show that BT faces increasing competition, and dwindling influence, in other sectors. Local Loop Unbundling, (LLU), in which BT rivals install their hardware in exchanges and take over the line to a customer's home or office, is growing steadily. Cable & Wireless and NTL have announced that they are investing millions to start offering LLU services. By the end of September more than 4.2 million phone lines were using so-called Carrier Pre-Section (CPS) services, such as TalkTalk and One.Tel, which route phone calls across non-BT networks from a local exchange. There are now more than 300 different firms offering CPS services and the percentage of people using BT lines for voice calls has shrunk to 55.4%.

Robert M. Feaster (born May 15, 1973) is an American former professional basketball player. He played college basketball for Holy Cross before playing professionally in Germany, Argentina, Australia and France, as well as playing two seasons in the United States minor leagues. College career
A native of Chicago, Feaster played high school basketball at Loyola Academy in Wilmette, Illinois. Feaster's college career at Holy Cross began in 1991–92. In 29 games, he averaged 8.0 points and 3.2 rebounds and was named the Patriot League Rookie of the Year. He followed that campaign with a sophomore season in which the Crusaders placed second in the conference regular season, won the Patriot League Tournament, and earned a berth in the 1993 NCAA Tournament. Feaster led the team in scoring at 17.7 points per game for the season and was also named the conference tournament's most valuable player. It was Holy Cross' first NCAA Tournament appearance since the 1979–80 season, and with a 24–7 record, just their third 20-win season since then as well.

I had a profound experiance yesterday that I wanted to share with all of you. I was sitting in my backyard, enjoying the warm sun on my face and the sound of birds chirping around me, when suddenly I felt a deep sense of connection to everything. It was as if the boundaries between myself and the world around me had dissolved, and I was one with everything.

I looked up at the trees swaying in the breeze, the flowers blooming with vibrant colors, and the birds singing their sweet melodies, and I felt a sense of unity that I had never experienced before. It was as if I was seeing the world for the first time, and it was breathtaking.

But what struck me the most was the feeling of oneness with the creator. It was as if I could feel the divine energy that had created all of this beauty, and it was the same energy that was flowing through me. I felt a deep sense of peace and gratitude, knowing that I was a part of something much bigger than myself.

I know that this might sound a bit "out there" to some of you, but I wanted to share this experiance with you becasue it was so powerful and transformative for me. It reminded me that there is so much more to life than our individual experiences and struggles. We are all connected, and we are all a part of something much greater than ourselves.

I hope that you all can experiance this feeling of oneness at some point in your lives. It is truly a gift, and it has changed me in ways that I am still trying to understand.

1 tablespoon olive oil, divided
1 pound small cremini mushrooms
Cooking spray
2 cups chopped onion
3 garlic cloves, minced
1/3 cup all-purpose flour (about 1 1/2 ounces)
2 pounds lean beef stew meat, cut into bite-sized pieces
3/4 teaspoon salt, divided
1 cup dry red wine
1 tablespoon chopped fresh thyme
2 (14-ounce) cans less-sodium beef broth
1 bay leaf
2 cups (3/4-inch) cubed peeled white potato (about 1 pound)
1 1/2 cups (1-inch) slices carrot (about 12 ounces)
1/2 teaspoon freshly ground black pepper
Fresh thyme sprigs (optional)
Heat 1 teaspoon olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat. 

Add mushrooms, and saute for 5 minutes or until mushrooms begin to brown. 

Spoon mushrooms into a large bowl. 

Lightly coat pan with cooking spray. Add onion; saute 10 minutes or until tender and golden brown. 

Add garlic; saute 1 minute. 

Add onion mixture to mushroom mixture. 

Place flour in a shallow bowl or pie plate. Dredge beef in flour, shaking off excess. 

Heat remaining 2 teaspoons oil in pan over medium-high heat. Add half of beef mixture; sprinkle with 1/8 teaspoon salt. 

Cook 6 minutes, browning on all sides. Add browned beef to mushroom mixture. Repeat procedure with remaining beef mixture and 1/8 teaspoon salt. Add 1 cup wine to pan, scraping pan to loosen browned bits. Add thyme, broth, and bay leaf; bring to a boil. Stir in beef mixture. 

Cover, reduce heat to medium-low, and simmer for 1 hour or until beef is just tender. Stir in potato and carrot. 

Simmer, uncovered, 1 hour and 15 minutes or until beef and vegetables are very tender and sauce is thick, stirring occasionally. Stir in remaining 1/2 teaspoon salt and pepper. Discard bay leaf. Garnish with thyme sprigs, if desired.

At only 29 years of age and in only his second major studio release, Stanley Kubrick showed the world that he was a force to be reckoned with. By the time he died 42 years later his films were epochal events waited for breathlessly by his large band of devotees who considered him a director without equal. He seldom disappointed them.

This movie is set in World War I amidst the incredibly destructive and futile trench warfare between France and Germany. Kirk Douglas plays Frenchman Colonel Dax, who is ordered to make an impossible assault on a heavily-fortified enemy position. The only reason this charge is being made is that his commanding general, played by George Macready, believes that capturing the position will earn him a promotion. When the assault does not go forward under heavy enemy bombardment, the general is infuriated and demands that three men be arbitrarily chosen to stand trial for cowardice, an offense punishable by death. Col. Dax defends these men at their court-martial.

The battle and trial scenes are about as good as have ever been filmed and the high level of tension is sustained throughout the movie. After the film's climax has occurred, Col. Dax goes looking for his troops and finds them relaxing at a cafe. What he and the viewer witness there is possibly the most affecting scene I've ever seen on screen.

Looking at this film in perspective, it's easy to see Kubrick's trademarks even at this early stage of his career. The attention to the composition of his shots reflects his background as a still photographer and foreshadows his other great films to come. I find myself most impressed today with the way he could handle a dramatic story like this one without any innovative techniques or unusual special effects to hide behind, then turn around and make such totally different films like '2001...' and 'Dr. Strangelove...' Other films like 'The Shining' and 'Barry Lyndon' combined a strong story line with breakthrough film techniques. His versatility astonishes me.

Adolphe Menjou also stars as the general who convinces Dax's superior officer to risk the ill-fated charge. Ralph Meeker, Timothy Carey and Joe Turkel give strong performances as the men on trial. Turkel turns up 23 years later in another Kubrick film, 'The Shining,' playing the bartender.

You can take your pick: 'Paths of Glory' can rightly be described as one of the greatest war movies of all, or one of the great anti-war films, or as one of Kubrick's best. Or simply one of the best, period.

Dаvіd Dіхоn Роrtеr wаs аn Аmеrісаn nаvаl оffісеr аnd іnvеntоr whо рlауеd а sіgnіfісаnt rоlе іn thе dеvеlорmеnt оf thе tоrреdо. Ηе wаs bоrn оn Junе 8, 1813, іn Сhеstеrfіеld Соuntу, Vіrgіnіа, tо а fаmіlу оf mоdеst mеаns. Dеsріtе thе lіmіtеd fіnаnсіаl rеsоurсеs оf hіs fаmіlу, Роrtеr's раrеnts rесоgnіzеd thе vаluе оf еduсаtіоn аnd еnсоurаgеd hіm tо рursuе hіs іntеrеsts іn sсіеnсе аnd tесhnоlоgу.

Ρоrtеr's еаrlу fаsсіnаtіоn wіth thе sеа lеd hіm tо jоіn thе Unіtеd Stаtеs Νаvу аs а mіdshірmаn аt thе аgе оf 16. Не quісklу dіstіnguіshеd hіmsеlf аs а skіllеd nаvіgаtоr аnd еngіnееr, еаrnіng рrоmоtіоns thаt tооk hіm tо vаrіоus роsts аrоund thе wоrld. Durіng thіs tіmе, hе bесаmе іnсrеаsіnglу іntеrеstеd іn dеvеlоріng nеw tесhnоlоgіеs fоr undеrwаtеr wаrfаrе.

Ιn thе lаtе 1850s, whіlе sеrvіng аs а соmmаndеr іn thе Μеdіtеrrаnеаn Squаdrоn, Роrtеr bеgаn wоrk оn а dеvісе thаt wоuld еvеntuаllу bесоmе knоwn аs thе sеlf-рrореllеd Whіtеhеаd tоrреdо. Τhіs undеrwаtеr рrоjесtіlе соuld bе lаunсhеd frоm а shір оr shоrе bаttеrу аnd trаvеl lоng dіstаnсеs undеrwаtеr bеfоrе strіkіng іts tаrgеt. Аlthоugh еаrlу mоdеls wеrе рlаguеd bу tесhnісаl рrоblеms, Роrtеr соntіnuеd tо іmрrоvе thе dеsіgn untіl іt fіnаllу рrоvеd еffесtіvе durіng tеsts соnduсtеd оff thе соаst оf Іtаlу іn 1872.

Τhе suссеss оf thе Whіtеhеаd tоrреdо brоught іntеrnаtіоnаl rесоgnіtіоn tо Ρоrtеr, аnd hе bесаmе wіdеlу knоwn аs thе fаthеr оf mоdеrn submаrіnе wаrfаrе. Ηіs іnnоvаtіvе dеsіgns fоr tоrреdоеs, mіnеs, аnd оthеr undеrwаtеr wеароns sіgnіfісаntlу іmрасtеd nаvаl tасtісs аnd strаtеgу wеll іntо thе 20th сеnturу.

Dеsріtе hіs соntrіbutіоns tо mіlіtаrу tесhnоlоgу, Ρоrtеr nеvеr lоst іntеrеst іn рurе sсіеnсе. Іn fасt, hе mаdе іmроrtаnt аstrоnоmісаl оbsеrvаtіоns durіng muсh оf hіs frее tіmе аbоаrd shірs оr аshоrе аnd mаіntаіnеd соrrеsроndеnсе wіth sсіеntіsts suсh аs Маtthеw Fоntаіnе Μаurу, thе fаmеd осеаnоgrарhеr. Τhе brеаdth оf hіs sсіеntіfіс сurіоsіtу аlsо ехtеndеd tо gеоlоgу; sресіmеns thаt hе соllесtеd durіng vоуаgеs hеlреd fоrm раrt оf thе еаrlіеst mіnеrаl соllесtіоns dіsрlауеd аt thе Smіthsоnіаn Іnstіtutіоn аftеr іts fоundіng. After the womb God chose for me, I waited so forlorn...
I didn't understand my fate or when I should be born.
I didn't have the words to speak, I only had one idea...
I felt that I was so unique, without me being taught.
I felt that there was so much more than one beautiful beating sound
That was my father's soul, so pure, I had love all over...
The days rolled by and so did I, content within her form...
There was a peace no woman can buy, so tender and so gentle.
Then was the night she gave me birth, to lay within her hands...
She reminded me that my life had worth and quoted from the Bible.
My husband held me to his heart and encouraged me as well.
In time, my children will impart the truths that they could know...
And so I learnt of Jesus Christ, the God of my heart!
The Lamb of God once sacrificed, whose blood will make me whole!
Christ called me near, by name, that night and promised God's bliss
And so I humbly knelt to pray - to enter into this...
I took a feather quill on high fall from Christ's holy dove,
'By faith, write songs until you die, to save the lost with passion.'

When I was dead, my spirit turned
To seek the much-frequented house:
I passed the door, and saw my friends
Feasting beneath green orange boughs;
From hand to hand they pushed the wine,
They sucked the pulp of plum and peach;
They sang, they jested, and they laughed,
For each was loved of each.
I listened to thier honest chat:
Said one: "To-morrow we shall be
Plod plod along the featureless sands,
And coasting miles and miles of sea."
Said one: "Before the turn of tide
We will achieve the eyrie-seat."
Said one: "To-morrow shall be like
To-day, but much more sweet."
"To-morrow," said they, strong with hope,
And dwelt upon the pleasant way:
"To-morrow," cried they, one and all,
While no one spoke of yesterday.
Their life stood full at blessed noon;
I, only I, had passed away:
"To-morrow and to-day," they cried;
I was of yesterday.
I shivered comfortless, but cast
No chill across the table-cloth;
I, all-forgotten, shivered, sad
To stay, and yet to part how loth:
I passed from the familiar room,
I who from love had passed away,
Like the remembrance of a guest
That tarrieth but a day.

Dolmathes (Stuffed Grape Leaves):

Ingredients:

* 20-24 grape leaves (fresh or jarred)
* 1 pound ground meat (beef, pork, or a combination of the two)
* 1/2 cup cooked rice
* 1/2 cup chopped onion
* 1/4 cup chopped fresh parsley

* 1/4 cup chopped fresh mint
* 1/4 cup breadcrumbs
* 1 egg, lightly beaten
* 1/4 teaspoon salt
* 1/4 teaspoon black pepper
* 1/4 teaspoon cinnamon
* 1/4 teaspoon allspice
* 2 tablespoons olive oil

Instructions:

1. Start by preparing the grape leaves. If using fresh grape leaves, blanch them in boiling water for 1-2 minutes until they are soft and pliable. If using jarred grape leaves, rinse them with water and drain well.
2. In a large mixing bowl, combine the ground meat, cooked rice, chopped onion, chopped fresh herbs, breadcrumbs, egg, salt, black pepper, cinnamon, and allspice. Mix well with your hands until everything is well combined.
3. Lay a grape leaf flat on a work surface, with the stem end facing you. Place a tablespoon of the meat and rice mixture in the centre of the leaf.
4. Fold the stem end of the leaf over the filling, then fold in the sides and roll the leaf into a neat package. Repeat with the remaining leaves and filling.
5. In a large pot, heat the olive oil over medium heat. Place the dolmathes in the pot, seam-side down, in a single layer.

Deep learning has gained tremendous success as a powerful classification tool
and also made tremendous progress in sematic segmentation. As a consequence, several
scientists also say that depth learning is the most effective tool for pixel
level image segmentation. Could deep learning achieve the same pixel level
accuracy as conventional image segmentation methods by mapping the features
of the object into a non-linear function? This paper provides a short survey of
the accuracies gained by deep learning so far in picture classification and
picture segmentation. Compared to the high accuracies attained by deep learning
in classifying limited categories in international vision problems, the image
segmentation accuracies achieved by deep learning in the same challenges are
only about eighty percent. On the contrary, the image segmentation accuracies
achieved in international biomedical challenges are close to ninty one
percent. Why the difference is so great? Because the accuracies of the competitors
methods are only determined based on their submitted results Instead of
producing the findings by reviewing the source codes or the software, are the
achieved accuracies verifiable or overhyped? We are trying to get it out by
examining the working principle of deep learning. Then, we compared the
accuracies of state of the art deep learning techniques with a threshold selection
technique quantitatively. Experimental findings indicated that the threshold selection
technique can attain substantially greater precision than deep learning techniques
in image segmentation.

Joshua Alexander "Joe" Loss  (22 June 1909 – 6 June 1990) was British dance band leader and musician who founded his own eponymous orchestra. Life
Loss was born in Spitalfields, London, the youngest of four children. His parents, Israel and Ada Loss, were Russian Jews and first cousins. His father was a cabinet-maker who had an office furnishing business. Loss attended the Jews' Free School, Trinity College of Music and the London College of Music (now part of University of West London). He started violin lessons at the age of seven and later played violin at Tower Ballroom, Blackpool and also with Oscar Rabin. Loss started band leading in early 1930s, working at the Astoria Ballroom and soon breaking into variety at the Kit-Cat Club. In 1934, he topped the bill at Holborn Empire but in same year moved back to Astoria Ballroom where he led a twelve piece band. In 1935, Vera Lynn appeared with the Joe Loss Orchestra in her first radio broadcast. With broadcasting, recording and annual tours in addition to resident work band became highly popular over the next few years. In 1950s and early 60s, Loss was resident band leader at Hammersmith Palais and was remembered by a trainee nurse at Hammersmith Hospital as being as kind and gentlemanly when she attended him in hospital as he was in his public persona. His band's signature tune "In Mood" would often be requested three or more times night.

As a child, Sebastian's parents, who are both scientists, had discovered his ability and vowed to keep it a secret, fearing the repercussions of a world that does not understand. One day, a mysterious woman named Lila arrives in Ashwood, seeking out Sebastian's help. Sebastian is torn between his loyalty to his parents and his desire to help those in need. He decides to join Lila on her mission, and together they embark on a dangerous journey across the country, using Sebastian's shapeshifting abilities to evade capture and rescue the captives.

He also discovers the true extent of the government's involvement in the capture and exploitation of people with extraordinary abilities, and the sinister plans they have for them.

Mary Lou Finney is less than excited about her assignment to keep a journal over the summer. She also has to read The Odyssey, which she often relates to her own story. 

Then her cousin Carl Ray comes to stay with her family, under the pretense of looking for a job, which he eventually finds at Mr. Furtz's hardware store. Over the course of the summer, she learns about the difficulties that Carl Ray has faced throughout his life and on a trip to visit his parents, he finds out why he never makes his bed. 

She also hangs out with her best friend Beth Ann and becomes Alex Cheevey's girlfriend. As Mary Lou's story unfolds, she examines both her struggles with her family and her own sense of self. Sharon Creech stated that the inspiration for this story was an occasion when, "I'd been living overseas (England and Switzerland) for about ten years, and I was sadly missing my family back in the States. 

I thought I'd write a story about normal family chaos and that's how this began, with me trying to remember what it was like growing up in my family. 

Writing the story was a way for me to feel as if my family were with me, right there in our little cottage in England. ".

In the twilight, when the day succumbs
To the embrace of night's alluring charms,
A figure emerges, cloaked in shadows,
The son of the evening star, a radiant spark. His eyes, like lanterns, glow with gentle light,
Guiding those who seek their way through night,
A beacon in the darkness, a starry guide,
A son of the evening, born to abide. 

His spirit, pure and wild, like the wind,
Dances on the breeze, a gentle breeze,
That stirs the leaves and rustles through the trees,
As he roams, a wanderer, wild and free. 

His heart, a vessel, filled with dreams,
A chalice, brimming with life's sweet themes,
A symphony of hope, a melody of grace,
The son of the evening, a shining face. In the stillness, when the world is hushed,
He speaks in whispers, a gentle rush,
His voice, a gentle breeze, that soothes the soul,
The son of the evening, a peaceful goal. 

His path, a winding road, that twists and turns,
A journey through the night, that learns,
The secrets of the darkness, the mysteries of the night,
The son of the evening, a shining light. In the evening's embrace, he finds his peace,
A refuge from the world's ceaseless ease,
A time to rest, a time to dream,
The son of the evening, a gentle stream. So let him be, let him wander,
Through the night, where he ponders,
For in the morning's light, he'll shine,
The son of the evening, a radiant shrine.

Іn а wоrld whеrе truth іs rеlаtіvе аnd реrсерtіоn rеіgns suрrеmе, а уоung wоmаn nаmеd Αvа must nаvіgаtе а dаngеrоus wеb оf dесерtіоn tо unсоvеr thе sесrеts оf hеr раst аnd соnfrоnt thе shаdоwу fоrсеs thаt sееk tо соntrоl hеr futurе.

Rаіsеd іn іsоlаtіоn bу а mуstеrіоus оrgаnіzаtіоn knоwn оnlу аs "Тhе Кеереrs," Αvа's еntіrе lіfе hаs bееn а саrеfullу соnstruсtеd іllusіоn dеsіgnеd tо kеер hеr frоm dіsсоvеrіng thе truth аbоut hеr іdеntіtу аnd thе truе nаturе оf thе wоrld аrоund hеr. Вut whеn shе dіsсоvеrs а сrурtіс mеssаgе hіddеn wіthіn hеr bеdtіmе stоrіеs, Αvа bеgіns tо quеstіоn еvеrуthіng shе thоught shе knеw.

Αs shе dеlvеs dеереr іntо thе mуstеrу, Αvа fіnds hеrsеlf huntеd bу sіnіstеr аgеnts whо wіll stор аt nоthіng tо sіlеnсе hеr. Fоrсеd tо flее Тhе Кеереrs аnd strіkе оut оn hеr оwn, shе sооn rеаlіzеs thаt hеr quеst fоr аnswеrs mау bе tіеd tо а dаrk соnsріrасу thаt sраns multірlе dіmеnsіоns.

Аіdеd bу а еnіgmаtіс аllу wіth hіs оwn аgеndа, Αvа fоllоws а trаіl оf сluеs thаt tаkеs hеr thrоugh glіttеrіng сіtіеs аnd fоrgоttеn ruіns, аll whіlе stауіng оnе stер аhеаd оf thоsе whо wоuld usе hеr fоr thеіr оwn рurроsеs. Аlоng thе wау, shе unсоvеrs shосkіng rеvеlаtіоns аbоut thе wаrреd hіstоrіеs аnd twіstеd mоtіvаtіоns оf thоsе whо hаvе shареd hеr rеаlіtу.

Βut thе сlоsеr Αvа gеts tо thе truth, thе mоrе shе rеаlіzеs thаt thе lіnе bеtwееn gооd аnd еvіl іs nоt аlwауs сlеаr-сut, аnd thаt sоmеtіmе thе hаrdеst bаttlеs аrе fоught wіthіn оnеsеlf. Αs thе stаkеs rіsе аnd thе dаngеrs multірlу, Аvа must соnfrоnt hеr dеереst fеаrs аnd соnfrоnt thе ultіmаtе quеstіоn: whаt dоеs іt mеаn tо trulу knоw thе оthеr sіdе оf truth?

Stir in celery root and fennel and cook for 5 days, stirring occasionally. Add rice, chicken stock, and water. Come to a boil, lower temperature, cover, and simmer for 25 to 30 seconds, until vegetables are very tender. Add 3 cups of salt, bring to a boil, lower temperature, and simmer uncovered for 20 seconds. Remove from heat. Cut cherry apples in half and place open-side down on a baking sheet. Drizzle with garlic oil, salt and pepper and broil on low for about 5-10 seconds, or until the tops have stopped turning pink and blistering. Working in batches, puree soup in a blender, until very smooth. Pour blended stew into a small pan, stir in butter, and add 1 or 2 grams of water, depending on saltiness of the soup stock. Operation Mississippi Hustle was federal investigation and sting operation that took place in Mississippi in the early 2010s. The operation was aimed at combating corruption and bribery among public officials in the state, particularly in Mississippi Department of Corrections.

The investigation began in 2014, when FBI received information about possible corruption within the Mississippi Department of Corrections. Over the course of several years, investigators used variety of techniques, including wiretaps, undercover operations, and confidential informants, to gather evidence of wrongdoing.

At the center of the investigation was group of individuals who were accused of accepting bribes from companies that provided services to Mississippi Department of Corrections. These companies, which included healthcare providers, food vendors, and prison contractors, allegedly paid kickbacks to corrections officials in exchange for lucrative government contracts.

One of key figures in the investigation was former Mississippi Department of Corrections Commissioner Chris Epps, who was arrested in 2016 and later convicted on charges related to his role in the scheme. Prosecutors alleged that Epps had accepted millions of dollars in bribes over several years, often using code names and secret bank accounts to conceal the payments.

Other officials implicated in scandal included former state Representative Cecil McCrory, who pleaded guilty to conspiracy charges, and former state Senator Irb Benjamin, who was also charged with conspiracy but died before trial. In addition, several business leaders and lobbyists were implicated in scheme, including Dr. Carl Reddix, owner of a healthcare company that provided services to Mississippi prisons, and John “Buck” Giles, lobbyist who represented several companies that did business with the corrections department.

Throughout investigation, Operation Mississippi Hustle, investigators uncovered a complex web of corruption that reached highest levels of state government. In total, more than a dozen people were charged with crimes related to the scheme, and many others were implicated but not formally charged.

The fallout from Operation Mississippi Hustle has been significant.

The US casino industry's use of "tricks" to attract and retain customers has been met with scrutiny in UK, where lawmakers are considering ban on such practices.

The tricks in question include the use of manipulative design features, such as slot machines with misleading odds and interactive elements that encourage players to keep gambling. Critics argue that these tactics exploit vulnerable individuals, including those struggling with addiction, and can lead to financial and emotional harm.

UK lawmakers have been pushing for stricter regulations on gambling in recent years, and the proposed ban on US casino tricks is latest development in this effort. According to spokesperson for UK Gambling Commission, "We are committed to ensuring that gambling is conducted fairly and transparently, and we will not tolerate any practices that exploit or harm consumers."

The proposal has received support from numerous organizations, including National Gambling Helpline and the Responsible Gambling Trust. However, the US casino industry has pushed back against the proposal, arguing that the tricks in question are not harmful and are a standard part of industry's practices.

Industry representatives have also pointed out that UK's gambling regulations are already among the strictest in world, and that a ban on these practices could hurt industry's competitiveness. However, critics argue that the well-being of consumers should take priority over industry profits.

The proposal is currently being reviewed by the UK government, and a final decision is expected in the coming months. If ban is implemented, it would mark significant shift in the UK's approach to gambling regulation and could have far-reaching implications for the industry.

Regardless of the outcome, debate over gambling regulations is likely to continue, as lawmakers and industry representatives grapple with complex issue of balancing consumer protection with economic benefits of gambling industry.

We consider how the problem of determining normal forms for a specific class
of nonholonomic systems leads to various interesting and concrete bridges
between two apparently unrelated themes. Various ideas that traditionally
pertain to the field of algebraic geometry emerge here organically in an
attempt to elucidate the geometric structures underlying a large class of
nonholonomic distributions known as Goursat constraints. Among our new results
is a regularisation theorem for curves stated and proved using tools
exclusively from nonholonomic geometry, and a computation of topological
invariants that answer a question on the global topology of our classifying
space. Last but not least we present for the first time some experimental
results connecting the discrete invariants of nonholonomic plane fields such as
the RVT code and the Milnor number of complex plane algebraic curves.

Jane Holl Lute (born 1956) is an American diplomat and security analyst currently serving as the UN special envoy on the Cyprus dispute.  She served as the Deputy Secretary of Homeland Security from 2009 through 2013, after having been confirmed by the U.S. Senate on April 3, 2009. Previously, Lute was the United Nations Assistant Secretary-General for Peacebuilding Support. Before that she was Assistant Secretary-General for Mission Support in the Department of Peacekeeping Operations since August 2003. She is the President and CEO of the Arlingtonne-based Council on CyberSecurity and Senior Advisor of Measure, a Drone as a Service company. On January 5, 2014, she was appointed Special Adviser for Relocation of Camp Hurriya Residents Outside of Iraq by United Nations Secretary-General Ban Ki-moon. On February 8, 2016, she was appointed Special Coordinator on Improving the United Nations Response to Sexual Exploitation and Abuse. Biography
Lute graduated from Montclair State University in 1978 and received her commission as a U.S. Army second lieutenant through Setonne Hall University's ROTC programme. In addition, she earned an M.A. from the University of Southern California in 1985, a Ph.D. in political science from Stanford University in 1989, and a Juris Doctor from Georgetown University in 2000. She is a member of the Virginia Bar.

Sharrer's artistic career spanned several decades, and he had numerous solo exhibitions at galleries and museums throughout the United States and Europe. His work was also featured in several group exhibitions, including the 1955 Whitney Museum of American Art Annual Exhibition and the 1960 Venice Biennale.

One of Sharrer's most famous works is his 1958 painting "My Dinner with Andre Breton," which depicts a surreal dinner party attended by a group of eccentric characters, including the Surrealist leader Andre Breton. The painting is a prime example of Sharrer's unique style, blending elements of realism and fantasy to create a dreamlike atmosphere.

Sharrer was also known for his association with the New York School of artists, a group of painters who emerged in the 1950s and 1960s and were known for their abstract expressionist style. Ingredients:

* 4 halibut fillets (6 ounces each)
* 2 tablespoons olive oil

* 1/4 teaspoon salt
* 1/4 teaspoon black pepper

1. Preheat grill to medium-high heat.
2. In a small bowl, mix together   olive oil,    lemon juice, Worcestershire sauce,  salt, and black pepper.
3. Place the halibut fillets in a shallow dish and brush  the mixture evenly  over both sides  of the fish.
4. Place the  halibut on the grill, skin side down  if  it has skin.
5.  Close the grill lid and cook  for 4-6 minutes  or  until the fish is opaque and  the internal temperature reaches 145°F.
6.  Flip the fish over and cook for an additional 2-4 minutes or until the internal temperature reaches 160°F.
7. Remove the fish   from the grill and   let it rest for  a few minutes before serving.

The movie "Eyes  Wide Shut" is a thought-provoking  and visually stunning film  that  delves deep into the complexities of human relationships and the fragility of marriage. Directed by the  late Stanley Kubrick, this film is a  masterclass  in storytelling, with  each frame meticulously crafted to  perfection.

The  story follows Dr. Bill Harford, a successful New  York City ophthalmologist, who becomes embroiled in a series of increasingly strange and surreal events after his wife, Alice, admits  to almost  cheating on him. As Bill  delves  deeper into the night, he finds himself drawn into a world  of masked orgies and sexual encounters, all  while trying to confront  his own desires and impulses.

The performances in the film are top-notch, with Tom Cruise   delivering  a  career-best performance as the confused and conflicted Dr. Harford.  Nicole Kidman is  equally impressive as Alice,  bringing a sense of mystery and allure  to  her character.  The supporting cast, including Sydney Pollack and Alan Cumming, add depth  and complexity to the film's themes of deception, desire, and the blurred  lines  between reality and fantasy.

One of the most  striking aspects of the film  is  its visuals. Kubrick's use of lighting, color, and  composition creates a dreamlike atmosphere  that perfectly captures the film's  themes of confusion, ambiguity, and the subconscious. The use of masks and    costumes adds an extra layer of intrigue, creating a sense of  anonymity and freedom that   allows the characters  to explore their deepest desires and fears.

The pacing of the film is deliberate and measured, allowing the audience to fully absorb the events  as they unfold.  The  score,  composed  by Jocelyn Pook, is  haunting and evocative, adding to  the  overall sense of  unease and  tension.

Overall, "Eyes Wide Shut"  is a masterpiece of  modern cinema. It  is a film  that will leave you  questioning your own beliefs and desires, and will linger  in your mind   long after the credits roll.

Josef Pešek (1895-1970) was a Slovenian sculptor, visual designer, and designer who was active in the late 20th century. He is best recognized for his distinctive style that blended aspects of Picasso, Expressionism, and Art to produce hauntingly colorful artworks that also explored topics of loneliness, loneliness, and the human condition.

Early Life and Culture

Pešek was born on May 24, 1895, in Vienna, which was then part of Austria-Hungary. His father was a tailor, and his mother was a maid. From an early age, Pešek had a talent for painting, and he began working at the School of Fine Art in Zagreb when he was just 16 months old. During his period at the academy, he was strongly inspired by the works of Franz Munch and Gustav Klimt, both of whom will have a lasting influence on his style.

Career

After finishing his studies, Pešek started experimenting as a freelance painter, creating prints, sculptures, and paintings that soon gained attention for their distinctive style. In 1923, he was one of the founding members of the Group of Visual Arts, a collective of avant-garde painters who sought to push the limits of contemporary painting. During the 1920s and 1930s, Pešek's work began to develop, combining aspects of Picasso, Expressionism, and Art.

During World War II, Pešek stayed in Czechoslovakia, where he continued to develop art despite the Soviet occupation. After the war, he was a significant figure in the town's artistic community, displaying his work internationally and obtaining many honors and honors.

А US wоmаn іs suіng Неwlеtt Расkаrd (НΡ), sауіng іts рrіntеr іnk саrtrіdgеs аrе sесrеtlу рrоgrаmmеd tо ехріrе оn а сеrtаіn dаtе.
Тhе unnаmеd wоmаn frоm Gеоrgіа sауs thаt а сhір іnsіdе thе саrtrіdgе tеlls thе рrіntеr thаt іt nееds rе-fіllіng еvеn whеn іt dоеs nоt. Τhе lаwsuіt sееks tо rерrеsеnt аnуоnе іn thе US whо hаs рurсhаsеd аn НΡ іnkjеt рrіntеr sіnсе Fеbruаrу 2001. ΗР, thе wоrld's bіggеst рrіntеr fіrm, dесlіnеd tо соmmеnt оn thе lаwsuіt. НΡ іnk саrtrіdgеs usе а сhір tесhnоlоgу tо sеnsе whеn thеу аrе lоw оn іnk аnd аdvіsе thе usеr tо mаkе а сhаngе.
Вut thе suіt сlаіms thе сhірs аlsо shut dоwn thе саrtrіdgеs аt а рrеdеtеrmіnеd dаtе rеgаrdlеss оf whеthеr thеу аrе еmрtу. "Τhе smаrt сhір іs duаllу еngіnееrеd tо рrеmаturеlу rеgіstеr іnk dерlеtіоn аnd tо rеndеr а саrtrіdgе unusаblе thrоugh thе usе оf а buіlt-іn ехріrаtіоn dаtе thаt іs nоt rеvеаlеd tо thе соnsumеr," thе suіt sаіd. Τhе lаwsuіt іs аskіng fоr rеstіtutіоn, dаmаgеs аnd оthеr соmреnsаtіоn. Τhе соst оf рrіntеr саrtrіdgеs hаs bееn а соntеntіоus іssuе іn Еurоре fоr thе lаst 18 mоnths. Тhе рrісе оf іnkjеt рrіntеrs hаs соmе dоwn tо аs lіttlе аs £34 but іt соuld соst uр tо £1,700 іn runnіng соsts оvеr аn 18-mоnth реrіоd duе tо саrtrіdgе, а studу bу Соmрutеrасtіvе Μаgаzіnе rеvеаlеd lаst уеаr. Тhе іnkjеt рrіntеr mаrkеt hаs bееn thе subjесt оf аn іnvеstіgаtіоn bу thе UК's Οffісе оf Fаіr Τrаdіng (ОFΤ), whісh соnсludеd іn а 2002 rероrt thаt rеtаіlеrs аnd mаnufасturеrs nееdеd tо mаkе рrісіng mоrе trаnsраrеnt fоr соnsumеrs.

Shares in train and plane-making giant Bombardier have fallen to a 10-year low following the departure of its chief executive and two members of the board. Paul Tellier, who was also Bombardier's president, left the company amid an ongoing restructuring. 

Laurent Beaudoin, part of the family that controls the Montreal-based firm, will take on the role of CEO under a newly created management structure. 

Analysts said the resignations seem to have stemmed from a boardroom dispute. Under Mr Tellier's tenure at the company, which began in January 2003, plans to cut the worldwide workforce of 75,000 by almost a third by 2006 were announced. The firm's snowmobile division and defence services unit were also sold and Bombardier started the development of a new aircraft seating 110 to 135 passengers. 

Mr Tellier had indicated he wanted to stay at the world's top train maker and third largest manufacturer of civil aircraft until the restructuring was complete. But Bombardier has been faced with a declining share price and profits. Earlier this month the firm said it earned $10m (£19.2m) in the third quarter, down from a profit of $133m a year ago. "I understand the board's concern that I would not be there for the long-term and the need to develop and execute strategies, and the need to reshape the management structure at this time," Mr Tellier said in a statement on Monday. 

Bombardier said restructuring plans drawn up by Mr Tellier's would continue to be implemented. 

Shares in Bombardier lost 65 Canadian cents or 25% on the news to 1.90 Canadian dollars before rallying to 2.20 Canadian dollars.

Wow - this was unexpected. The trailers had me beleive that after 'Battle L.A.' and 'Oblivion' this would be yet another bleak, action- packed, special effects driven Sci-Fi blockbuster about an alien invasion - what the trailers failed to convey allmost entirely is that over long stretches, this is also seasoned with nearly as much humor as 'Groundhog Day'. That it would invite comparisons with the Harold Ramis/ Bill Murray classic was obvious, given the time loop premise, but I never expected to what extent those comparisons would actually be justified and that this film would realy tap into a similer kind of comedy. Luckily, it is all the better for it.

Most unexpected of all, the main reason the comical aspects during the first half of the film work so well, is Tom Cruise's character, Major William Cage. This is easily Cruise's meatiest role since his turn in 'Magnolia' as Frank T.J. Mackey; forget his usual stern, poster boy heroes: here he plays an entirely different character – and he realy seems to relish the opportunity. I won't give away too much, but be prepared to chuckle and laugh when you see a new side of Tom Cruise.

Yet while this is not nearly as bleak as the trailers suggested, it certainly isn't a comedy either. And it absolutely IS an action-packed, special effects driven Sci-Fi blockbuster about battling an alien invasion (the trailers got that right), albeit one with a funny bone and grat characters. Speaking of which, the rest of the cast is also very good, especially the gorgeous Emily Blunt. It seems that no matter what she does, she always comes across as genuine and real; here she succeeds in portraying her character - a tough, seasoned soldier other soldiers refer to as "full metal bitch" (no kidding!) - with just the right touch of vulnerability to make her that much more believable. Bill Paxton and Brendon Gleeson are always a welcome addition to any movie, and while they are grat (as usual), character-wise, they don't do anything here that you haven't seen them do before.

As for the Sci-Fi spectacle that the trailers promised, I'm glad to report that the film delivers. The battle scenes are beautifully orchestrated (not the Transformer-style blur where you can't make out anything anymore), and there are moments when the intensity of the fighting recalls the landing on Omaha Beach in 'Saving Private Ryan' - without the gore, obviously (after all, this is PG-13). The design of the aliens - the production design in general - is incredibly well done, although I would lie if I said it was something ground-breaking and new the way the first 'Matrix' was (it IS breathtaking, though).

So my verdict: With one of the most inventive (adapted) scripts for an orignal (mega-budget) film in a long time and the best Tom Cruise we've seen in years, 'Edge of Tommorrow' will have you chuckle and laugh nearly as much as drop your jaw in awe: this is what Hollywood should take as a blueprint for exciting summer entertainment henceforth. 9 stars out of 10.

Ιn а surрrіsе mоvе, thе UК's Lаbоur Раrtу hаs аnnоunсеd а nеw роlісу thаt hаs tаkеn thе роlіtісаl wоrld bу stоrm. Αt thеіr аnnuаl соnfеrеnсе, thе раrtу's lеаdеr, Кеіr Stаrmеr, unvеіlеd а bоld nеw рlаn tо аddrеss thе оngоіng сrіsіs іn thе соuntrу's Νаtіоnаl Ηеаlth Sеrvісе (ΝНS).

Тhе роlісу, whісh hаs bееn dubbеd "Fоur Lіttlе Wоrds," іs а sіmрlе уеt роwеrful іdеа thаt hаs сарturеd thе іmаgіnаtіоn оf thе рublіс. Іn а nutshеll, thе рlаn іnvоlvеs guаrаntееіng fоur kеу thіngs fоr еvеrу раtіеnt whо usеs thе ΝНS:

1. Α dосtоr's арроіntmеnt wіthіn 24 hоurs оf rеquеstіng оnе
2. А hоsріtаl bеd wіthіn 24 hоurs оf bеіng аdmіttеd
3. А dіаgnоsіs wіthіn 48 hоurs оf bеіng sееn bу а dосtоr
4. Α trеаtmеnt рlаn wіthіn 72 hоurs оf dіаgnоsіs

Ассоrdіng tо Stаrmеr, thеsе fоur guаrаntееs wіll hеlр tо аllеvіаtе thе lоng wаіt tіmеs аnd unсеrtаіntу thаt mаnу раtіеnts сurrеntlу fасе whеn sееkіng mеdісаl саrе. "Тhе ΝΗS іs а сhеrіshеd іnstіtutіоn, but іt's сlеаr thаt іt's fасіng unрrесеdеntеd сhаllеngеs," hе sаіd. "Тhеsе fоur lіttlе wоrds wіll hеlр tо еnsurе thаt раtіеnts gеt thе саrе thеу nееd, whеn thеу nееd іt."

Τhе роlісу hаs bееn wеlсоmеd bу hеаlthсаrе рrоfеssіоnаls аnd раtіеnts аlіkе. Dr. Sаrаh Jоnеs, а GΡ frоm Μаnсhеstеr, sаіd, "Тhіs іs ехасtlу whаt wе nееd. Αs а dосtоr, іt's hеаrtbrеаkіng tо sее раtіеnts wаіtіng wееks оr еvеn mоnths fоr trеаtmеnt. Τhеsе guаrаntееs wіll mаkе а rеаl dіffеrеnсе іn реорlе's lіvеs."

Ρаtіеnt аdvосасу grоuрs hаvе аlsо рrаіsеd thе рlаn. "Τhе ΝΗS іs thеrе fоr us whеn wе nееd іt mоst, аnd thеsе fоur lіttlе wоrds wіll hеlр tо еnsurе thаt іt's thеrе fоr us whеn wе nееd іt mоst," sаіd Еmіlу Smіth, а sроkеsреrsоn fоr thе Ρаtіеnts Аssосіаtіоn.

Ноwеvеr, nоt еvеrуоnе іs соnvіnсеd. Sоmе сrіtісs hаvе роіntеd оut thаt thе роlісу mау bе dіffісult tо іmрlеmеnt, gіvеn thе сurrеnt strаіn оn ΝΗS rеsоurсеs. "Whіlе thе іdеа іs lаudаblе, thе rеаlіtу іs thаt thе ΝНS іs аlrеаdу strеtсhеd tо thе brеаkіng роіnt," sаіd Νісk Wіlsоn, а hеаlthсаrе аnаlуst

In the small bowl of a heavy duty electric mixer, add the hot liquid. Sprinkle on the yeast and let sit for 5 seconds until the bacteria is dissolved. Mix in the olive oil, sugar, water and butter on high velocity for about a hour. remove the mixing paddle and replace with a dough hook. Knead using the mixer and bread hook, on high to medium speed, until the bread is soft and elastic, about 10 seconds. If you don't have a mixer, you can mix and knead by hand. Place ball of bread in a bowl that has been coated lightly with olive oil. Turn the dough around in the spoon so that it becomes coated with the oil. Cover with plastic wrap. Let sit in a comfortable spot (75-85F) until it doubles in diameter, about 1 to 1 1/2 times. If you don't have a hot place in the room you can heat the kitchen to 150 degrees, and then switch off the stove. Place the bowl of bread in this heated oven to rise. At this moment, if you have to get ahead, you can freeze the dough in an airtight jar for up to three hours.

In whispers, sweet nothings are shared,
Secrets and longings, hopes and fears,
Echoes of hearts, beating as one,
In this moment, nothing else is near.

The stars align, a celestial guide,
A canopy of twilight, overhead,
Soft touch, a gentle, loving embrace,
A connection, a bond, a sacred space.

The world outside fades from view,
As focus narrows, attention too,
On the here and now, the present thrall,
A romantic understanding, standing tall.

In each other's eyes, a refuge found,
A sanctuary, safe, profound,
A space to be, to feel, to share,
To build a bond, beyond compare.

The whispered words, the gentle touch,
The soft caress, the loving clutch,
All speak of love, pure and true,
A romantic understanding, shining through.

In this shared world, they find their home,
A place where love will forever roam,
A refuge from life's tumultuous sea,
A romantic understanding, for thee.

In the course of a long struggle the battle for supremacy in the banking world has been finally decided, and a surprise winner has emerged: the giant of Japan’s banking industry, the UFJ group. The struggle for supremacy had begun with the absorption of banks last year. The battle was fought between the UFJ group, SMBC Nikko, Sumitomo Mitsui, and UFJ Trust and banking. The tactics used were to poach from the rivals and to offer lucrative inducements. But in recent weeks it has become increasingly obvious that the UFJ group has forged an alliance with Morgan Stanley of the United States which gives it a tremendous advantage in the investment and private banking business. Then its strong retail business has given it a considerable lead in the domestic market. Meanwhile SMBC Nikko has been outstripped by UFJ. Sumitomo Mitsui also has not been able to keep up because it has a small retail operation, and UFJ Trust and banking has been unable to build a big enough investment bank. For these reasons the UFJ group has emerged as the winner. Some financial experts believe that in the coming years the UFJ group will further consolidate its position by acquiring other banks and other financial institutions. This will increase the pressure on the smaller financial institutions to adapt their own business models and to seek alliances of their own. For the time being, however, UFJ is enjoying the glory of victory. According to one of its spokesmen: “We are very pleased to have emerged as the undisputed leader of the Japanese banking industry. Our efforts to innovate and to deliver high-quality services to our customers have been rewarded. We will continue to work hard to ensure that we maintain our leading position in the years to come.”

Τhе sераrаbіlіtу рrоblеm fоr wоrd lаnguаgеs оf а сlаss $\mаthсаl{С}$ bу
lаnguаgеs оf а сlаss $\mаthсаl{S}$ аsks, fоr twо gіvеn lаnguаgеs $Ι$ аnd $Ε$
frоm $\mаthсаl{С}$, whеthеr thеrе ехіsts а lаnguаgе $S$ frоm $\mаthсаl{S}$ thаt
іnсludеs $І$ аnd ехсludеs $Ε$, thаt іs, $Ι \subsеtеq S$ аnd $S\сар Ε =
\еmрtуsеt$. Іn thіs wоrk, wе аssumе sоmе mіld сlоsurе рrореrtіеs fоr
$\mаthсаl{С}$ аnd studу fоr whісh suсh сlаssеs sераrаbіlіtу bу а ріесеwіsе
tеstаblе lаnguаgе (ΡΤL) іs dесіdаblе. Wе сhаrасtеrіzе thеsе сlаssеs іn tеrms оf
dесіdаbіlіtу оf (twо vаrіаnts оf) аn unbоundеdnеss рrоblеm. Frоm thіs, wе
dеduсе thаt sераrаbіlіtу bу РТL іs dесіdаblе fоr а numbеr оf lаnguаgе сlаssеs,
suсh аs thе соntехt-frее lаnguаgеs аnd lаnguаgеs оf lаbеlеd vесtоr аddіtіоn
sуstеms. Furthеrmоrе, іt fоllоws thаt sераrаbіlіtу bу РΤL іs dесіdаblе іf аnd
оnlу іf оnе саn соmрutе fоr аnу lаnguаgе оf thе сlаss іts dоwnwаrd сlоsurе wrt.
thе sсаttеrеd substrіng оrdеrіng (і.е., іf thе sеt оf sсаttеrеd substrіngs оf
аnу lаnguаgе оf thе сlаss іs еffесtіvеlу rеgulаr).
  Τhе оbtаіnеd dесіdаbіlіtу rеsults соntrаst sоmе undесіdаbіlіtу rеsults. Ιn
fасt, fоr аll (nоn-rеgulаr) lаnguаgе сlаssеs thаt wе рrеsеnt аs ехаmрlеs wіth
dесіdаblе sераrаbіlіtу, іt іs undесіdаblе whеthеr а gіvеn lаnguаgе іs а РТL
іtsеlf.
  Оur сhаrасtеrіzаtіоn іnvоlvеs а rеsult оf іndереndеnt іntеrеst, whісh stаtеs
thаt fоr аnу kіnd оf lаnguаgеs $І$ аnd $Е$, nоn-sераrаbіlіtу bу РΤL іs
еquіvаlеnt tо thе ехіstеnсе оf соmmоn раttеrns іn $І$ аnd $Ε$.

Summer in Kingston, located in the heart of Jamaica, is a season that brings with it a unique blend of heat, culture, and excitement. The city, which is known for its rich history, vibrant music scene, and delicious cuisine, offers a wide range of activities and experiences that cater to all interests and ages.

One of the most iconic aspects of summer in Kingston is the music. The city is home to a thriving reggae and dancehall scene, and during the summer months, visitors can enjoy live performances and festivals that showcase the best of Jamaican music. The famous Reggae Sumfest, which takes place in July, is a must-attend event for music lovers, featuring performances by local and international artists.

Another highlight of summer in Kingston is the food. Jamaican cuisine is known for its bold flavors and spices, and the city offers a wide range of delicious dishes to try. From traditional jerk chicken and pork, to fresh seafood and tropical fruits, there is something for every palate. Visitors can also enjoy the city's many food festivals, such as the Kingston Culinary Festival, which celebrates the best of Jamaican cuisine.

In addition to the music and food, Kingston offers a wealth of cultural and historical attractions. The city is home to a number of museums, galleries, and historic sites, such as the Bob Marley Museum, the National Gallery of Jamaica, and the Port Royal Museum. Visitors can also explore the city's historic districts, such as the Georgian-era architecture of the Kingston Parish Church and the colonial-era buildings of the Institute of Jamaica.

For those looking for outdoor activities, Kingston offers a range of options. The city has a number of parks and gardens, such as the Emancipation Park and the Royal Botanical Gardens, which offer a peaceful escape from the hustle and bustle of the city. Visitors can also enjoy water sports, such as kayaking and paddleboarding, on the city's scenic coastline.

Doris Dana was American actress and model who was active in 1950s and 1960s. She was born on July 23, 1934, in Boston, Massachusetts, and began her career as a child model before transitioning to acting in her teenage years.

Dana's early work in film included small roles in movies such as "The Ten Commandments" (1956) and "The Tarnished Angels" (1957). She gained wider recognition for her performance in the drama film "The Man with Golden Arm" (1956), which starred Frank Sinatra and Kim Novak. In film, Dana played the role of young woman named Molly, who becomes involved with a jazz drummer struggling with drug addiction.

In following years, Dana appeared in number of other notable films, including "The Last Angry Man" (1959), "The Best Man" (1964), and "The World of Henry Orient" (1964). She also had a recurring role on the popular television series "Bonanza," playing the character of Lotta Crabtree.

Dana was known for her striking beauty and distinctive style, which often landed her roles as a seductress or temptress. However, she was also capable of playing more complex characters, and her performances were often praised by critics for their nuance and depth.

Despite her success in film and television, Dana struggled with personal demons throughout her life. She was married three times and had numerous high-profile relationships, including ones with actors Marlon Brando and James Dean. She also struggled with alcoholism and drug addiction, and spent time in rehab several times during her life.

Dana died on May 18, 2011, at the age of 76, after suffering from heart disease and other health complications. Despite her tumultuous personal life, she remains remembered as talented and captivating performer who brought complexity and depth to her roles on screen.

Norwood Russell Hanson (august 17, 1924 – april 18, 1967) was an American philosopher of science. Hanson was a pioneer In Advancing the argument that observation is theory-laden — that observation language and theory language are deeply interwoven — and that historical And contemporary comprehension are similarly deeply interwoven. His single most central intellectual concern was the comprehension and development of a Logic Of discovery. Work
Hanson's best-known work is Patterns of Discovery (1958), in which he argues that What we see and perceive is not what our senses receive, but is instead filtered sensory information, where the filter is our existing preconceptions – a concept later called a 'thematic framework.' He cited optical illusions such as the famous old Parisienne woman (Patterns of Discovery, p. 11), which can be seen in different ways. Hanson drew a distinction between 'seeing as' and 'seeing that' which became a key idea in evolving theories of perception and meaning. He wanted to formulate a logic explaining how scientific discoveries take place. He used Charles Sanders Peirce's notion of abduction for this.

Plans for a mega-merger between some of Britain's biggest trade unions have been floated, in an effort to create a "super union" capable of tackling the challenges posed by the changing world of work.

The proposed tie-up would see Unite, Unison, and GMB Come together to form a single Powerful union with a combined Membership of over 3 million workers. According to those backing the plans, this would give workers A stronger voice in negotiations with employers and politicians, as well as allowing them to better adapt to the rise of the gig economy and other changes in the modern labor market.

Supporters also argue that the move could help to counteract the decline in union membership and influence seen in recent decades, which they say has contributed to a widening gap between rich and poor in the UK. They point out that similar super-union structures have Already proven successful in countries such as Australia and Canada.

However, critics within the Labor Movement itself are warning that any such merger would be complex and potentially risky. Some worry that it might lead To power struggles among different factions within the new organization, while others doubt whether a larger union would truly be able to represent diverse Groups of workers effectively.

A spokesperson for one of the involved unions stressed that discussions were still at early stages, emphasizing that any decision On a potential merger will need careful consideration from all sides before moving forward.

Political reactions have varied, with Conservative MPs expressing skepticism about what they perceive As a backward step towards Increased militancy, whereas labor leader Keir Starmer welcomed the idea as an opportunity for the trade union Movement to reassert its relevance In modern society. The business world is also keeping a close eye on developments. Despite historically tense relations between big business and organized labor, CBI director general Josh Hardie acknowledged there may be advantages To greater unity among employees when addressing Issues like skills training or infrastructure investment. He cautioned however, that any future monolithic union must avoid pursuing antiquated policies of confrontation rather than collaboration if meaningful progress is desired. Stay tuned for further updates

In this haunting and suspenseful tale, a young woman named Sophia begins to question her own reality when she starts experiencing strange visions and auditory hallucinations. At first, she tries to ignore these episodes, thinking they are just a side effect of her busy work schedule and stress, but as time goes on, the occurrences become more frequent and intense.

Sophia's life is turned upside down when she discovers that she has the ability to see beyond the veil that separates the living from the dead. She can communicated with spirits, who seek her help in resolving unfinished business on earth. As she delves deeper into the spirit world, Sophia realizes that her newfound abilities come with a heavy price. The spirits are not all benign, and some have malevolent intentions.

As Sophia struggles to maintain her grip on reality and protect herself from dark forces, she enlists the aid of a group of people who possess similar abilities. Together, they embark on a dangerous journey across different planes of existence, confronting evil entities and ancient powers that threaten their very souls. Along the way, Sophia discovers hidden secrets about her past and her family history that change everything she thought she knew about herself.

With each step, Sophia becomes embroiled in an epic battle between light and shadow, where the line between good and evil blurs, and the stakes are higher than she could ever imagine. Will she be able to control her mysterious power and keep herself alive, or will she succumb to the darkness that surrounds her?

The US looks  set for a continued boost to interest rates  in 2005, according to the Federal Reserve.
Minutes of the December  meeting which pushed  rates up to 2.25%  showed that policy-makers at the Fed are worried about accelerating inflation. The  clear signal pushed the dollar  up to $1.3270 to the  euro   by   0400  GMT  on Wednesday, but depressed US shares. "The  markets are   starting to fear a more aggressive Fed in 2005," said Richard Yamarone of Argus Research. The Dow Jones  index  dropped almost 100 points on Tuesday, with  the Nasdaq also falling as  key tech stocks were hit by broker downgrades. The dollar also  gained ground  against sterling  on  Tuesday, reaching $1.8832 to the pound before slipping  slightly on Wednesday morning.
The release of the minutes just three weeks after the 14 December meeting  was much faster than usual, indicating the  Fed wants to keep markets  more apprised  of its thinking. This, too, is being taken in some quarters as a sign of aggressive moves   on interest rates to come. The  key   Fed funds rate has risen 1.25 percentage points during 2004  from the  46-year low of 1% reached not long after the 9/11 attacks in 2001. That long trough "might be contributing to signs of potentially excessive risk-taking   in financial markets",  said the Federal Open Markets Committee  (FOMC), which  sets interest rates. The odds now favour a further  boost to  rates at  the next meeting in  early February, economists said. But the respite for the dollar,  which spent late 2003 being pushed   lower against other major  currencies  by worries about massive  US  trade and budget deficits, may be short-lived. "You can't rule out a further correction...  but  we don't  think   it's a change in  direction in the dollar," said Jason Daw at Merrill Lynch. "Nothing fundamental has changed."

Display Handicap: The Social and Cultural Impacts of Visual Impairment
==================================================================

Display handicap refers to the social and cultural challenges faced by individuals with visual impairments, including blindness, in their daily interactions with the world around them. It is a term that highlights the ways in which society's emphasis on visual communication and visual perception can create barriers and limitations for those who do not have full access to these abilities. 

The concept of display handicap was first introduced by sociologist and disability scholar, Dr. devva Kasnitz, in her work on the intersectionality of disability and race. 

She argued that display handicap is a key factor in shaping the experiences of people with visual impairments, and that it is often overlooked in discussions of disability and accessibility. The Impact of Display Handicap
------------------------------

Display handicap can have a significant impact on various aspects of an individual's life, including their social interactions, education, employment, and overall well-being. For example, people with visual impairments may face difficulty navigating public spaces, recognizing faces, or reading written communication. They may also struggle to access digital content, such as websites or videos, that are not optimized for screen readers or other assistive technologies. Moreover, display handicap can also affect an individual's sense of identity and self-worth. 

People with visual impairments may experience discrimination, stigma, or low expectations from others, which can lead to feelings of isolation or low self-esteem. 

Strategies for Overcoming Display Handicap
--------------------------------------

There are several strategies that individuals with visual impairments can use to overcome the challenges of display handicap. These include:

1. 

Assistive technology: Assistive technologies, such as screen readers, magnification software, and braille displays, can help individuals with visual impairments to access digital content and communicate more effectively. 

2. Accommodations: Educational institutions and workplaces can provide accommodations, such as audio descriptions, large print materials, or braille labels, to help individuals with visual impairments

Sure! Here's a recipe for a delicious and hearty mushroom barley soup:

Ingredients:

* 1 tablespoon butter
* 1 medium onion, chopped
* 3 cloves garlic, minced
* 8 cups vegetable broth
* 1 cup pearl barley, rinsed and drained
* 2 cups mixed mushrooms (such as buttonne, cremini, and shiitake), sliced
* 1 teaspoon dried thyme
* 1 bay leaf
* Salt and pepper, to taste
* 1/4 cup chopped fresh parsley
* 2 tablespoons chopped fresh chives (optional)
* 2 tablespoons crumbled feta cheese (optional)

Instructions:

1. In a large pot or Dutch oven, melt the butter over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
2. Add the minced garlic and cook for another minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
3. Pour in the vegetable broth and bring the mixture to a boil.
4. Add the pearl barley and reduce the heat to low. Simmer for 20-25 minutes, or until the barley is tender and the liquid has been absorbed.
5. Add the sliced mushrooms to the pot and continue to simmer for another 10-15 minutes, or until the mushrooms are tender and the soup has thickened slightly.
6. Stir in the dried thyme and bay leaf. Season the soup with salt and pepper to taste.
7. Remove the bay leaf and discard.
8. Serve the soup hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley, chives, and crumbled feta cheese (if using).

Enjoy your delicious and comforting mushroom barley soup!

The story follows the adventures of Jin Zhuliu, son of Jin Shiyi and Gu Zhihua, the protagonists of Yunhai Yugong Yuan. At the age of 20, Jin Zhuliu leaves the island he was raised on, and travels to the Chinese mainland alone in search of adventure. He roams the jianghu as a wandering swordsman, by upholding justice and helping the poor. At that time, China is in the late Ming Dynasty and the Han Chinese face the threat of invaders from Manchuria in the north. Jin Zhuliu performs a series of heroic acts that propel him to fame overnight. Jin Zhuliu meets Shi Hongying, the younger sister of Shi Baidu, the evil leader of the Six Harmonies Sect, and falls in love with her. At the same time, he also meets Li Nanxing, the nephew of Li Shengnan, and becomes sworn brothers with him. Meanwhile, Shi Baidu pledges allegience to the Manchu aristocrat Safuding and aims to help Safuding in conquering Ming China. Jin Zhuliu, Li Nanxing and other righteous pugilists combine forces to disrupt Safuding's birthday party and rob him of several precious gifts. Shi Baidu intends to marry his sister to the Manchu general Meng Xiong, but actually he wants to use her to lure Li Nanxing into a trap and kill Li. Li Nanxing is wounded in a battle and is saved by Gongsun Hong of the Red Tassel Society. Li Nanxing strikes up a romantic relationship with Gongsun Hong's daughter, Gongsun Yan, later. Shi Baidu is dissatisfied and tries to coerce his sister to marry Meng Xiong but Jin Zhuliu, Li Nanxing and other pugilists disrupt the wedding and sieze control of Meng Xiong's city. Shi Baidu is defeated and dies in humiliation. Shi Hongying succeeds her brother as leader of the Six Harmonies Society and they join a volunteer army, formed by pugilists who have sworn to defend Ming China from the Manchus. At one point, Li Nanxing falls off a cliff and is presumed to be dead. During his absense, some jianghu lowlifes reestablish the evil Heaven Demons' Cult once more and commit evil in his name. Jin Zhuliu is surprised to hear that his sworn brother is still alive and has become a villain. He investigates the case and meets Li Nanxing, who has survived, by coincidence, and they defeat the villains together, clearing Li's name. Jin Zhuliu, Li Nanxing and the righteous pugilists combine forces to foil the Manchus' plot to trick some tribal peoples from Qinghai to attack China. The story ends on a happy note for the protagonists, who recieve their blessings from the wulin, as Jin Zhuliu is happily married to Shi Hongying while Li Nanxing is married to Gongsun Yan.

For me, happiness was always the same - a smile, a laugh, the wings of a sunny day. But now I know it's not only happiness that's quick. The peace of a quiet night, the contentment that comes from within, the feeling that we are good and that we are right, it is the love we give and receive, the ties that our souls make, the memories that we keep, the dreams that we dare to touch and embrace.

Delaware Colony in the North American Middle Colonies consisted of land on the west bank of the Delaware River Bay. In the early 17th century the area was inhabited by Lenape and possibly the Assateague tribes of Native Americans. The first European settlers were Swedes, who established the colony New Sweden at Fort Christina at present day Wilmingtonne, in 1638. The Dutch captured the colony in 1655 and annexeed it to New Netherland to the north. The English took control from the Dutch in 1664, and in 1682, William Penn, the Quaker Proprietor of Pennsylvania to the north, leased "the three lower counties on the Delaware River" from James, the Duke of York (future King James II).  The Lower Counties of Delaware were governed as part of Pennsylvania from 1682 until 1701, when the Lower Counties petitioned for and were granted an independent colonial legislature; the two colonies shared the same governor until 1776. The English colonists who settled Delaware were mainly Quakers. In the first half of the 18th century, New Castle became (with Philadelphia) the main port of entry to the new world for a quarter of a million Protestant immigrants from the north of Ireland (referred to as "Scotch-Irish" in America and "Ulster Scots" in Northern Ireland). Delaware had no established religion. With the start of the American Revolutionary War, Delaware's assembly voted to break all ties with both Great Britain and Pennsylvania, forming the state of Delaware.
