Supervolcano, a docu-drama about a volcanic eruption in Yellowstone National Park in the  US, is among the highlights on  the BBC One this winter.
The £178m  winter schedule also includes the return of Doctor Who and a  drama  about  Angela Cannings, who was wrongly convicted of killing two of her babies. Sarah Lancashire and Timothy  Spall will star in the  real-life drama, Cherished.  ITV also unveiled their festive season on Tuesday, which includes Stephen Fry in a remake of Tom  Brown's Schooldays. Supervolcano,  follows in the footsteps of  last year's Pompei, which drew 10 million viewers to  BBC One in October 2003.
The programme  merges  science, drama and computer imagery to reveal  what could happen if  Yellowstone  -  home to  the only currently active  supervolcano in  the  world -  were   to erupt again. BBC  Two will run a  two-part documentary, The Science   Behind Supervolcano, in conjunction with  the transmission. Other educational  highlights   include a documentary about infamous Mongolian warrior Genghis  Khan and Grandchild   of The Holocaust. Grandchild of The Holocaust, part of the BBC commitment to  Holocaust Memorial Day,  follows  Adrian, 13, on a  journey to uncover the  truth about what happened  to  his grandmother in Auschwitz and Belsen.
New drama includes Archangel,  an adaptation of  the Robert Harris   best-seller,  which stars Daniel Craig on  the trail of  Stalin's diaries in Communist Russia.  And Sarah Waters' gets her second adaptation on BBC One with  Imelda Staunton and Charles Dance  lined up to star in Fingersmith. The  adaptation, about a conman in Victorian England, will make an interesting contrast to Julie Burchill's Sugar Rush - a   lesbian teenage drama part of Channel  Four's winter season. On a lighter note, Jessica Stevenson will star in new BBC One sitcom, The World According to Bex,  penned  by My  Family creator  Fred Barron and the Two Ronnies return  for  a  celebration of their classic comedy series.
Fry's portrayal of headmaster Dr Arnold dominates a  muted Christmas schedule on ITV1,  which sees the channel retreat  from broadcasting blockbuster  movies in favour of extended soap episodes and popular quiz shows.
Sir Paul McCartney, wife Heather Mills and Manchester United manager  Sir Alex Ferguson will join a  celebrity edition   of Who Wants to be a Millionaire? on Christmas Day. Also  on Christmas Day, John Nettles will return in a  one-off edition of Midsomer Murders, while   two  episodes of the  new Miss Marple drama will air over the festive  period. Films on ITV1  include Gus Van Sant's Finding Forrester, starring Sean  Connery, and classics such a Mary Poppins, Star Wars and   The Good, the Bad  and the Ugly.  In contrast, BBC's  Christmas season includes  terrestrial  debuts of the first Harry Potter film,  Shrek  and Steven Spielberg's AI, as well as new epipsodes of the Vicar of Dibley and the final Auf Wiedersehen, Pet.

I strongly believe that colleges and universities should set clear, objective standards for admission and be legally obligated to admit every student who meets those standards.

The current system of college admissions is often criticized for being opaque and unfair, with many institutions prioritizing factors such as legacy status, athletic ability, and racial and ethnic diversity over academic merit. This can lead to deserving students being shut out of top schools, simply because they don't have the right connections or background.

By setting objective standards for admission, colleges can ensure that the most qualified and capable students are accepted, regardless of their background or personal connections. This would not only promote fairness and equal opportunity, but also help to increase diversity and inclusivity on campus.

Additionally, legally obligating colleges to admit every student who meets the standards would help to address the issue of selective admissions, which can leave many qualified students without a spot at their top-choice school. This would also help to reduce the stress and pressure that students face during the college application process, as they would know that if they meet the standards, they will be accepted.

Of course, there are concerns that setting objective standards and requiring colleges to admit all qualified students would lead to a decrease in the quality of education at top schools. However, this is not necessarily the case. By setting clear standards, colleges can still maintain their academic standards while ensuring that all qualified students have access to a high-quality education.

Furthermore, it's important to note that not all students who meet the standards will necessarily choose to attend the top schools. Many students may choose to attend other institutions that better fit their needs, interests, and career goals.

In conclusion, setting objective standards for admission and legally obligating colleges to admit every student who meets those standards would promote fairness, equal opportunity, and diversity on campus. It would also help to reduce the stress and pressure that students face during the college application process, while still maintaining the academic standards of top schools. It's time for colleges to adopt a more fair and transparent admissions process.

Philip Gilbert, the head of a group of Sherlock Holmes enthusiasts, is found dead in a national park's artillery battery. Because the autopsy report is slow-coming, inspector Kate Martinelli and her partner Al Hawkin treat the death as a murder case. She can discover little about the dead man aside from his unrelenting fascination with all things Holmes. One of his dinner group companions, Ian Nicholson, reveals that he had discovered a "lost" Holmes story, possibly by Sir Arthur Conan Doyle, about the murder of a gay soldier in 1920s San Francisco. Kate's interest is piqued when Ian mentions that Gilbert's body was discovered under the same circumstance as the soldier's in the story. Eventually, Kate traces Gilbert's ex-wife and learns that it is likely Glibert was gay, given that he had been with an actor several years before. She connects him with Nicholson, who used to be an actor and whose own ex-wife admits he is gay. Kate questions Nicholson, who admits that he hit Gilbert with a heavy bottle of wine after Gilbert announced they would have to break up temporarily following the publishing of the lost manuscript. He did not realize at the time that Gilbert actually died of complications following the attack, thereby downgrading the legal status from homicide to manslaughter. Finding Gilbert dead, Nicholson panics and dumps the body then sets up an elaborate alibi. After confessing his actions to Kate, Nicholson commits suicide by cop.

Sure! Here's a simple recipe for lentils and rice with caramelized onions:

Ingredients:

* 1 cup brown or green lentils, rinsed and drained
* 2 cups water
* 1 cup long-grain rice
* 2 tablespoons olive oil
* 1 large onion, thinly sliced
* 2 cloves garlic, minced
* 1 teaspoon ground cumin
* 1 teaspoon smoked paprika (optional)
* Salt and pepper, to taste
* Chopped fresh cilantro, for garnish (optional)

Instructions:

1. Rinse the lentils and soak them in water for at least 30 minutes. Drain and set aside. 2. 

Heat the oil in a large saucepan over medium heat. 

Add the sliced onions and cook, stirring occasionally, for about 15-20 minutes or until they are softened and caramelized. 3. 

Add the garlic, cumin, smoked paprika (if using), salt, and pepper to the saucepan and cook for another minute, stirring constantly. 4. 

Add the lentils and rice to the saucepan and stir to combine. 

5. 

Pour in the water and bring the mixture to a boil. 

Reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for about 20-25 minutes or until the lentils and rice are cooked through and the liquid has been absorbed. 6. 

Fluff the mixture with a fork and garnish with chopped cilantro (if using). 

7. 

Serve hot and enjoy! This recipe makes about 4 servings. You can also add other vegetables like carrots, peas, or spinach to the dish if you prefer. Additionally, you can use chicken or vegetable broth instead of water for extra flavor.

Sunspot number series are subject to various uncertainties, which are still
Poorly known. The need for their better understanding was recently highlighted
by the major makeover of the international Sunspot Number [Clette et al., space
Science Reviews, 2014]. We present the first thorough estimation of These
uncertainties, which behave as Poisson-like random variables with a
multiplicative coefficient that is time- and observatory-dependent. We provide
a simple expression for these uncertainties, and reveal how their evolution in
time coincides with changes in the Observations, and processing of the data.
Knowing Their value is essential for properly building composites out of
multiple observations, and for preserving the stability of the composites in
time.

Due to the intensive cost of labor and expertise in annotating 3D medical
images at a voxel level, most benchmark datasets are equipped with the
annotations of only one type of organs and/or tumors, resulting in the
so-called partially labeling issue. To address this, we propose a dynamic
on-demand network (DoDNet) that learns to segment multiple organs and tumors on
partially labeled datasets. DoDNet consists of a shared encoder-decoder
architecture, a task encoding module, a controller for generating dynamic
convolution filters, and a single but dynamic segmentation head. The
information of the current segmentation task is encoded as a task-aware prior
to tell the model what the task is expected to solve. Different from existing
approaches which fix kernels after training, the kernels in dynamic head are
generated adaptively by the controller, conditioned on both input image and
assigned task. Thus, DoDNet is able to segment multiple organs and tumors, as
done by multiple networks or a multi-head network, in a much efficient and
flexible manner. We have created a large-scale partially labeled dataset,
termed MOTS, and demonstrated the superior performance of our DoDNet over other
competitors on seven organ and tumor segmentation tasks. We also transferred
the weights pre-trained on MOTS to a downstream multi-organ segmentation task
and achieved state-of-the-art performance. This study provides a general 3D
medical image segmentation model that has been pre-trained on a large-scale
partially labelled dataset and can be extended (after fine-tuning) to
downstream volumetric medical data segmentation tasks. The dataset and code
areavailableat: https://git.io/DoDNet

COLOUR IMAGE SEGMENTATION is a critical step in many computer vision and pattern recognition applications. This paper introduces an unsupervised, ad hoc clustering method based on Voronoi regions, which can be applied to solve the colour image segmentation problem. The proposed method applies the splitting and merging of the Voronoi regions within the Voronoi diagram of the image, which improves the speed and accuracy of estimating the number of clusters and the centroids of the clusters. Furthermore, the proximity of the centroids of the clusters is used to merge clusters that are close to each other, resulting in the clustering of the image. Unlike other adaptive unsupervised clustering-based image segmentation methods, the proposed method uses the K-means clustering method rather than the Fuzzy C-means clustering method to obtain the final image. The performance of the proposed method is evaluated using three different unsupervised image segmentation evaluation tools, and the results are compared with two other adaptive unsupervised clustering-based image segmentation methods. The experimental results of this paper show that the proposed method provides the best quality of image segmentation compared to other adaptive unsupervised clustering-based image segmentation methods. The proposed method also provides the lowest average run time compared to other methods reported in this paper.

we are never
quite immediately aware of what we know
deep in our bones and consciousness
it creeps slowly into little clues
details that finally come to mind and in the considering
of the jigsaw puzzle
premonitions that had escaped us but now
reveal their colors in the borders of
the whole image how we languish
between them and when the truth arrives with us
clutching at the falsehoods in the trick
routes of the mind not knowing that
a falseness can just as soon in another
light can seem to be the truth
but with all the frazzling of nerves and worry
and dementia and discontent one should
realize it is a waste of time deciding what
is true and not although with most
thinking beings it is not possible to escape that
we do so love to worry about ourselves
scared stiff at being outwitted being
laughed at for being such quickly gentle fools
of our imagination
and the fantasies we fill our empty lives with
even my laughter scares me sometimes like
it emanates from some deep euphoric cave
some ancient time complete with mythical dragons
meanwhile desertwise
like a cloud the camel floats

Much Dewchurch is the home of the Steiner Academy Hereford, an independent school which was founded in 1981 and is based on the teachings of Rudolf Steiner, the Austrian philosopher and educator. It is one of a number of Steiner schools in Britain. The curriculum is designed to encourage the student to take a creative and practical approach to education. It is divided into three main stages: the primary school (for children aged three to ten), the middle school (for children aged eleven to fourteen) and the high school (for those aged fifteen to eighteen). The primary school is taught English, mathematics, science and social science as well as art, music and movement. The syllabus is flexible, designed to meet the needs of the individual, and the pupils are encouraged to develop their imaginations and creative skills. The middle school students continue to study a broad curriculum, taking a number of different subjects including languages, humanities, mathematics and science, as well as being introduced to more specialised subjects such as drama, music and art. In the high school, pupils prepare for public examinations and are given the freedom to follow their own interests in greater depth. They can choose subjects such as English, mathematics, science, modern foreign languages, and specialist subjects such as business and technology. The Steiner approach to education stresses the integration of the whole person, the importance of a creative and individual approach to learning, and the necessity of a practical approach to education. All the teachers are trained in the Steiner method of education, which emphasises the importance of creativity, imagination and individuality. Besides the more academic subjects, the school also offers a range of extra-curricular activities including sport, music, drama and outdoor activities. The school has a large playing field and other outdoor facilities, a studio, a workshop and an aviary.

My mother was a garden, lush and green
A gentle breeze that soothed my soul's keen
her love was like the sun, warm and bright
Illuminating every corner of my sight

She nurtured me with kindness and grace
And taught me how to find my place
In this world she showed me how To grow
And guided me through life's ebb and Flow

Her laughter was music to my ears
A symphony of joy, free from fears
For in her presence I felt complete
A sense of belonging, no need to compete

My father, oh he was a king
Generous and giving, his heart Would sing
He gave without expecting in return
His kindness knew no bounds, it Would burn

Like a river that flows endlessly
His bounty never ceased To be
Everyone who met Him could drink deep
From the wellspring of his generosity

He held us close with open Arms
And sheltered us from life's storms
His strength was our refuge, solid ground
Where we could stand when the waves were found

Together they instilled in me
The values Of kindness, love and glee
They showed me how to live each day
With gratitude, humility and play

I​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​n​o​v​e​l​,​ ​a​ ​y​o​u​n​g​ ​m​a​n​ ​n​a​m​e​d​ ​K​a​e​l​ ​i​s​ ​b​o​r​n​ ​i​n​t​o​ ​a​ ​w​o​r​l​d​ ​w​h​e​r​e​ ​t​h​e​ ​a​r​t​ ​o​f​ ​a​r​c​h​e​r​y​ ​i​s​ ​r​e​v​e​r​e​d​ ​a​s​ ​a​ ​s​a​c​r​e​d​ ​s​k​i​l​l​.​ ​H​e​ ​g​r​o​w​s​ ​u​p​ ​i​n​ ​a​ ​s​m​a​l​l​ ​v​i​l​l​a​g​e​ ​o​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​o​u​t​s​k​i​r​t​s​ ​o​f​ ​a​ ​v​a​s​t​ ​a​n​d​ ​p​o​w​e​r​f​u​l​ ​e​m​p​i​r​e​,​ ​w​h​e​r​e​ ​t​h​e​ ​r​u​l​i​n​g​ ​c​l​a​s​s​ ​i​s​ ​c​o​m​p​o​s​e​d​ ​o​f​ ​s​k​i​l​l​e​d​ ​a​r​c​h​e​r​s​ ​k​n​o​w​n​ ​a​s​ ​t​h​e​ ​"​L​o​r​d​s​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​B​o​w​.​"​ ​T​h​e​s​e​ ​l​o​r​d​s​ ​a​r​e​ ​r​e​v​e​r​e​d​ ​f​o​r​ ​t​h​e​i​r​ ​m​a​s​t​e​r​y​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​b​o​w​ ​a​n​d​ ​a​r​r​o​w​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​a​r​e​ ​f​e​a​r​e​d​ ​f​o​r​ ​t​h​e​i​r​ ​p​o​w​e​r​ ​a​n​d​ ​i​n​f​l​u​e​n​c​e​.​
​
​K​a​e​l​,​ ​w​h​o​ ​h​a​s​ ​a​l​w​a​y​s​ ​b​e​e​n​ ​f​a​s​c​i​n​a​t​e​d​ ​b​y​ ​t​h​e​ ​a​r​t​ ​o​f​ ​a​r​c​h​e​r​y​,​ ​d​r​e​a​m​s​ ​o​f​ ​o​n​e​ ​d​a​y​ ​b​e​c​o​m​i​n​g​ ​a​ ​L​o​r​d​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​B​o​w​ ​h​i​m​s​e​l​f​.​ ​H​o​w​e​v​e​r​,​ ​h​i​s​ ​p​a​t​h​ ​t​o​ ​g​r​e​a​t​n​e​s​s​ ​i​s​ ​f​r​a​u​g​h​t​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​o​b​s​t​a​c​l​e​s​.​ ​H​e​ ​i​s​ ​b​o​r​n​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​a​ ​r​a​r​e​ ​c​o​n​d​i​t​i​o​n​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​a​f​f​e​c​t​s​ ​h​i​s​ ​e​y​e​s​i​g​h​t​,​ ​m​a​k​i​n​g​ ​i​t​ ​d​i​f​f​i​c​u​l​t​ ​f​o​r​ ​h​i​m​ ​t​o​ ​s​h​o​o​t​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​t​h​e​ ​p​r​e​c​i​s​i​o​n​ ​r​e​q​u​i​r​e​d​ ​o​f​ ​a​ ​t​r​u​e​ ​a​r​c​h​e​r​.​
​
​D​e​s​p​i​t​e​ ​t​h​i​s​,​ ​K​a​e​l​ ​i​s​ ​d​e​t​e​r​m​i​n​e​d​ ​t​o​ ​o​v​e​r​c​o​m​e​ ​h​i​s​ ​d​i​s​a​b​i​l​i​t​y​ ​a​n​d​ ​b​e​c​o​m​e​ ​a​ ​s​k​i​l​l​e​d​ ​a​r​c​h​e​r​.​ ​H​e​ ​s​p​e​n​d​s​ ​c​o​u​n​t​l​e​s​s​ ​h​o​u​r​s​ ​p​r​a​c​t​i​c​i​n​g​ ​a​n​d​ ​h​o​n​i​n​g​ ​h​i​s​ ​s​k​i​l​l​s​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​e​v​e​n​t​u​a​l​l​y​ ​b​e​c​o​m​e​s​ ​o​n​e​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​b​e​s​t​ ​a​r​c​h​e​r​s​ ​i​n​ ​h​i​s​ ​v​i​l​l​a​g​e​.​ ​H​o​w​e​v​e​r​,​ ​h​i​s​ ​s​u​c​c​e​s​s​ ​a​t​t​r​a​c​t​s​ ​t​h​e​ ​a​t​t​e​n​t​i​o​n​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​r​u​l​i​n​g​ ​l​o​r​d​s​,​ ​w​h​o​ ​v​i​e​w​ ​h​i​m​ ​a​s​ ​a​ ​t​h​r​e​a​t​ ​t​o​ ​t​h​e​i​r​ ​p​o​w​e​r​.​
​
​A​s​ ​K​a​e​l​'​s​ ​s​k​i​l​l​s​ ​i​m​p​r​o​v​e​,​ ​h​e​ ​b​e​c​o​m​e​s​ ​e​m​b​r​o​i​l​e​d​ ​i​n​ ​a​ ​w​e​b​ ​o​f​ ​p​o​l​i​t​i​c​a​l​ ​i​n​t​r​i​g​u​e​ ​a​n​d​ ​d​e​c​e​p​t​i​o​n​.​ ​T​h​e​ ​r​u​l​i​n​g​ ​l​o​r​d​s​ ​w​i​l​l​ ​s​t​o​p​ ​a​t​ ​n​o​t​h​i​n​g​ ​t​o​ ​m​a​i​n​t​a​i​n​ ​t​h​e​i​r​ ​p​o​w​e​r​ ​a​n​d​ ​s​t​a​t​u​s​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​K​a​e​l​ ​f​i​n​d​s​ ​h​i​m​s​e​l​f​ ​c​a​u​g​h​t​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​m​i​d​d​l​e​ ​o​f​ ​a​ ​d​a​n​g​e​r​o​u​s​ ​g​a​m​e​ ​o​f​ ​c​a​t​ ​a​n​d​ ​m​o​u​s​e​.​
​
​A​s​ ​t​e​n​s​i​o​n​s​ ​r​i​s​e​ ​a​n​d​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​t​a​k​e​s​ ​b​e​c​o​m​e​ ​h​i​g​h​e​r​,​ ​K​a​e​l​ ​m​u​s​t​ ​d​e​c​i​d​e​ ​w​h​e​r​e​ ​h​i​s​ ​l​o​y​a​l​t​i​e​s​ ​t​r​u​l​y​ ​l​i​e​.​ ​W​i​l​l​ ​h​e​ ​j​o​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​r​u​l​i​n​g​ ​l​o​r​d​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​b​e​c​o​m​e​ ​o​n​e​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​m​,​ ​o​r​ ​w​i​l​l​ ​h​e​ ​u​s​e​ ​h​i​s​ ​s​k​i​l​l​s​ ​t​o​ ​b​r​i​n​g​ ​t​h​e​m​ ​d​o​w​n​ ​a​n​d​ ​c​r​e​a​t​e​ ​a​ ​n​e​w​ ​o​r​d​e​r​?​ ​T​h​e​ ​f​a​t​e​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​e​m​p​i​r​e​ ​r​e​s​t​s​ ​i​n​ ​h​i​s​ ​h​a​n​d​s​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​t​h​e​ ​c​h​o​i​c​e​ ​h​e​ ​m​a​k​e​s​ ​w​i​l​l​ ​d​e​t​e​r​m​i​n​e​ ​t​h​e​ ​c​o​u​r​s​e​ ​o​f​ ​h​i​s​t​o​r​y​.​
​
​T​h​e​ ​n​o​v​e​l​ ​e​x​p​l​o​r​e​s​ ​t​h​e​m​e​s​ ​o​f​ ​p​o​w​e​r​,​ ​l​o​y​a​l​t​y​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​t​h​e​ ​t​r​u​e​ ​m​e​a​n​i​n​g​ ​o​f​ ​g​r​e​a​t​n​e​s​s​.​ ​I​t​ ​i​s​ ​a​ ​t​h​r​i​l​l​i​n​g​ ​a​n​d​ ​a​c​t​i​o​n​-​p​a​c​k​e​d​ ​t​a​l​e​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​w​i​l​l​ ​k​e​e​p​ ​r​e​a​d​e​r​s​ ​o​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​e​d​g​e​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​i​r​ ​s​e​a​t​s​ ​u​n​t​i​l​ ​t​h​e​ ​v​e​r​y​ ​e​n​d​.​

In this paper, we present a novel approach for training deep neural networks on decentralized data sources, while minimizing communication overhead. Existing methods for distributed deep learning often rely on transmitting raw data between nodes, leading to high communication costs and slow convergence. Our proposed method, Communication-Efficient Learning (CEL), addresses these challenges by exploiting the structural properties of deep neural networks to compress the communicated information. CEL employs a hierarchical representation of the model weights, allowing nodes to communicate only the most essential parts of the model updates, resulting in significant reduction in communication overhead. We evaluate our method on several benchmark datasets and compare it with state-of-the-art approaches. The results show that CEL achieves competitive model accuracy while reducing communication cost by up to orders of magnitude. Moreover, we analyze the impact of various factors on the performance of CEL and provide insights into the trade-offs between communication efficiency and model accuracy. Our work has important implications for large-scale machine learning applications where data is distributed across multiple parties, such as edge computing, cloud computing, and federated learning.

2 10-inch flour tortillas
2 teaspoons canola oil
1/2 teaspoon salt, divided
1/2 teaspoon sesame seeds
5 1/2 lbs fresh tuna
1/4 teaspoon black pepper
1/4 teaspoon ground cumin
1 cup shredded low-fat cheddar cheese
1/2 cup Plain nonfat yogurt
2 teaspoons lime juice
8 cups mixed salad greens
1 (7 ounce) jar roasted bell peppers, drained and cut In strips
Preheat the Oven to 400 degrees. Coat a baking sheet with cooking spray. Brush the tortillas on both sides with the oil, then cut Them into 1/4-by-3-inch strips. Toss the strips with 1/4 teaspoon Of the salt and the sesame seeds. Lay the strips out on the prepared baking sheet and toast in the oven 8 to 10 minutes, turning once, or until They are crisped and lightly browned. Meanwhile, sprinkle the tuna with the remaining salt, pepper And cumin. coat a broiling pan or grill pan with cooking spray, heat, then add the tuna and cook for about 3 minutes on each side for rare (or until cooked to desired degree of doneness). Let the tuna stand 2 to 3 minutes before cutting with the grain into 1/4-inch-thick strips. Combine the cheese, Yogurt and lime juice in a bowl. Gently toss the tuna with the lettuce, roasted pepper strips and the dressing. Divide the salad among 4 dinner plates, and top With the tortilla"croutons" (alternatively, the crisped tortilla chips can be tossed in with the salad just before serving).

Generalization capability to unseen domains is crucial for machine learning
models when deploying to real-world conditions. We investigate the challenging
problem of domain generalisation, i.e., training a model on multi-domain source
data such that it can directly generalise to target domains with unknown
statistics. We adopt a model-agnostic learning paradigm with gradient-based
meta-train and meta-test procedures to expose the optimisation to domain shift.
Further, we introduce two complementary losses which explicitly regularise the
semantic structure of the feature space. Globally, we align a derived soft
confusion matrix to preserve general knowledge about inter-class relationships.
Locally, we promote domain-independent class-specific cohesion and separation
of sample features with a metric-learning component. The effectiveness of our
method is demonstrated with new state-of-the-art results on two common object
recognition benchmarks. Our method also shows consistent improvement on a
medical image segmentation task.

There is growing concern about the rise of online fraud, with millions of people falling victim to internet scams every year. In response, consumer protection groups and law enforcement agencies are joining forces to demand greater action from governments and tech companies to combat this growing problem.

The scale of the issue is alarming. According to recent statistics, in the UK alone, more than £10 billion was lost to online fraud last year, with the average victim losing over £4,500. While some frauds may be sophisticated operations that target vulnerable individuals, many others are simple cons that can be avoided with basic precautions. However, despite efforts by authorities to raise awareness, many people remain oblivious to the risks they face when browsing the web.

One of the biggest challenges facing regulators is keeping pace with the increasingly innovative tactics employed by cyber-criminals. Phishing emails, for example – where users receive official-looking messages encouraging them to divulge personal information or click on suspicious links – have evolved in their sophistication, using advanced techniques such as 'spoofing' which makes it difficult even for experienced users to spot fake communications. Other popular methods include ransomware attacks - which see hackers encrypt data then demand payment for its safe return - and investment scams promising unrealistic returns on non-existent ventures. 

Calls are now being made for tech companies to do more to protect customers against these types of threats rather than relying solely on consumers being aware enough not to fall foul of them. Social media platforms should take responsibility for policing fraudulent adverts posted on their sites; search engines must ensure false results don’t lead people down dangerous paths while internet service providers (ISPs) can help filter out malicious content before it ever reaches computer screens across Britain. Banks also need to keep tightening security processes so transfers cannot easily be completed without proper checks taking place first. Furthermore, there needs to be consequences for those who fail to comply adequately so criminals understand that there will be real world ramifications if they try targets here! Government bodies like Ofcom

1 cup plus 2 tbsp (2 1/4 sticks) butter
6 oz white chocolate, chopped coarsely
2 1/4 cups sugar
1 cup milk
2 cups plus 2 tbsp ground almonds
1 1/3 cups self-rising flour
2 None eggs, lightly beaten
2 tbsp unsweetened cocoa powder
None None FOR THE GANACHE
14 oz dark chocolate, chopped coarsely
7 oz milk chocolate, chopped coarsely
1 cup heavy cream
Preheat the oven to 325°F. Grease and flour two deep 8-inch round cake pans. Line bottom of pans with parchment paper. Place butter, white chocolate, sugar and milk in a medium saucepan on medium heat. Cook and stir, without boiling, until smooth. Transfer mixture to a large bowl; cool for 15 mins. Whisk ground almonds and sifted flour into white chocolate mixture. Whisk in eggs. Pour half of the mixture into one of the prepared pans. Whisk sifted cocoa into remaining mixture; pour into other prepared pan. Bake cakes for about 50 mins or until a toothpick inserted into center comes out clean. Cool in pans for 5 minutes. Remove from pans; cool completely on wire racks. For the ganache, place chocolates and cream in a medium saucepan on low heat. Cook and stir until smooth. Transfer to a medium bowl. Cover, refrigerate, stirring occasionally, until the ganache is a spreadable consistency. Reserve 1 cup of ganache for spreading over the cake. Split each cake in half. Place one layer of cake on serving plate; spread with 1 cup of the ganache. Repeat layering, alternating colors. Cover top and sides of cake with reserved ganache.

Richard Queen is a prominent American lawyer and legal scholar who has made significant contributions to the field of constitutional law. He is best known for his work on the concept of "originalism," which holds that the meaning of the Constitution was fixed at the time it was written and should not be altered by judicial interpretation.

Early Life and Education

Queen was born in 1953 in San Francisco, California. He grew up in a family of lawyers and judges, and he credits his parents with instilling in him a strong sense of justice and a passion for the law. Queen attended the University of California, Berkeley, where he earned his undergraduate degree in political science. He then went on to study law at Yale Law School, where he earned his J.D. degree in 1979.

Career

After graduating from law school, Queen clerked for Judge Henry Friendly on the Second Circuit Court of Appeals. He then entered private practice, working for several years as a litigator in New York City. In 1987, Queen joined the faculty of the University of Chicago Law School, where he taught constitutional law and federal courts. He quickly became known as a leading expert in the field of originalism, and his scholarship was widely cited and influential.

In 1993, Queen was appointed by President George H.W. Bush to the United States Court of Appeals for the D.C. Circuit. He served on the court for 12 years, during which time he authored numerous influential opinions on issues such as federalism, separation of powers, and civil liberties. In 2005, Queen was appointed by President George W. Bush to the United States Supreme Court, where he served untill his retirement in 2018.

Judicial Philosophy

Queen's judicial philosophy is rooted in his belief in originalism. He believes that the Constitution should be interpreted in accordance with the orignal meaning of its text, and that judges should not impose their own values or beliefs on the law. A new leaderboard has been topping the charts in the gaming world, but it's not what you might expect. 

instead of the usual suspects like Fortnite or Call of Duty, a group of gangsters have taken over the top spots. According to reports, a group of organized crime figures have been using their ill-gotten gains to purchase in-game items and currency, allowing them to climb to the top of various games' rankings. This has led to a surge in popularity for certain titles among criminal underworld, with some even offering real-world cash prizes to those who can rise to the top of the leaderboards. The phenomenon has sparked concern among law enforcement agencies, who fear that this could be a sign of deeper involvement by organized crime in the gaming industry. 

Some experts believe that the anonymity of online gaming platforms makes them ideal for money laundering and other illegal activities. 

"It's a disturbing trend," said Detective James Smith, who specializes in cybercrime. 

"We've seen cases where cartels and other criminal organizations have used online gaming as a way to hide their transactions and evade detection." 

Players who have encountered these gangster gamers say they are often impossible to beat, thanks to their seemingly unlimited resources and access to exclusive weapons and equipment. Some have even reported being threatened or intimidated by the gangsters when they try to compete against them. Despite these concerns, many players say they see no problem with the rise of gangsters in gaming. "It's just a game", says one player who wishes to remain anonymous. "At the end of the day, its all about having fun and competing with others. 

I don't care if the person Im playing agains is a criminal , as long as i can beat them!" 

While some gamemakers have taken steps to ban suspected gang members from their platforms, others argue that it's pointless to police every player.

3 small russet potatoes
1 head garlic (12-14 cloves. It's a LOT. Be afraid.)
1/3 cup fresh lemon juice
1/2 tablespoon salt
1/2 cup canola oil
Peel the potatoes and boil in water until soft. Mash and let cool. Crush and peel a whole head of garlic. Cut into quarters. Place raw garlic in a heavy duty blender with the salt and lemon juice. Pour a light layer of oil onto garlic. Puree the garlic, salt lemon juice on high and begin pouring a slow steady stream of the rest of the oil through the blender's handle hole until everything has mixed. At this point, you'll have extreme garlickiness (new word), which some people like, but for "texturizing" like Zankou's, you'll need the taters. Add the mashed potatoes, about 2-3 tablespoons at a time and mix. Continue adding/blending potato until the sauce is mixed well. At some point in this process, you might consider changing blending vessels. After all this work, my blender was starting to think twice about helping me out. Transfer to a bowl and allow to chill completely in the fridge. After about an hour, the flavors will blend together nicely and the texture will take on that library paste consistency like Zankou's sauce.

I
HAIL, orient Conqueror of gloomy Night!
Thou that canst shed the bliss of gratitude
On hearts howe'er insensible or rude;
Whether thy punctual visitations smite
The haughty towers where monarchs dwell;
Or thou, impartial Sun, with presence bright
Cheer'st the low threshold of the peasant's cell!
Not unrejoiced I see thee climb the sky
In naked splendour, clear from mist or haze,
Or cloud approaching to divert the rays,
Which even in deepest winter testify
Thy power and majesty,
Dazzling the vision that presumes to gaze.
--Well does thine aspect usher in this Day;
As aptly suits therewith that modest pace
Submitted to the chains
That bind thee to the path which God ordains
That thou shalt trace,
Till, with the heavens and earth, thou pass away!
Nor less, the stillness of these frosty plains,
Their utter stillness, and the silent grace
Of yon ethereal summits white with snow,
(Whose tranquil pomp and spotless purity
Report of storms gone by
To us who tread below)
Do with the service of this Day accord.
--Divinest Object which the uplifted eye
Of mortal man is suffered to behold;
Thou, who upon those snow-clad Heights has poured
Meek lustre, nor forget'st the humble Vale;
Thou who dost warm Earth's universal mould,
And for thy bounty wert not unadored
By pious men of old;
Once more, heart-cheering Sun, I bid thee hail!
Bright be thy course to-day, let not this promise fail!
II
'Mid the deep quiet of this morning hour,
All nature seems to hear me while I speak,
By feelings urged that do not vainly seek
Apt language, ready as the tuneful notes
That stream in blithe succession from the throats
Of birds, in leafy bower,
Warbling a farewell to a vernal shower.
--There is a radiant though a short-lived flame,
That burns for Poets in the dawning east;
And oft my soul hath kindled at the same,
When the captivity of sleep had ceased;
But He who fixed immoveably the frame
Of the round world, and built, by laws as strong,
A solid refuge for distress--
The towers of righteousness;
He knows that from a holier altar came
The quickening spark of this day's sacrifice;
Knows that the source is nobler whence doth rise
The current of this matin song;
That deeper far it lies
Than aught dependent on the fickle skies.
III
Have we not conquered?--by the vengeful sword?
Ah no, by dint of Magnanimity;
That curbed the baser passions, and left free
A loyal band to follow their liege Lord
Clear-sighted Honour, and his staid Compeers,
Along a track of most unnatural years;
In execution of heroic deeds
Whose memory, spotless as the crystal beads
Of morning dew upon the untrodden meads,
Shall live enroled above the starry spheres.
He, who in concert with an earthly string
Of Britain's acts would sing,
He with enraptured voice will tell
Of One whose spirit no reverse could quell;
Of One that 'mid the failing never failed--
Who paints how Britain struggled and prevailed
Shall represent her labouring with an eye
Of circumspect humanity;
Shall show her clothed with strength and skill,
All martial duties to fulfil;
Firm as a rock in stationary fight;
In motion rapid as the lightning's gleam;
Fierce as a flood-gate bursting at midnight
To rouse the wicked from their giddy dream--
Woe, woe to all that face her in the field!
Appalled she may not be, and cannot yield.
IV
And thus is 'missed' the sole true glory
That can belong to human story!
At which they only shall arrive
Who through the abyss of weakness dive.
The very humblest are too proud of heart;
And one brief day is rightly set apart
For Him who lifteth up and layeth low;
For that Almighty God to whom we owe,
Say not that we have vanquished--but that we survive.
V
How dreadful the dominion of the impure!
Why should the Song be tardy to proclaim
That less than power unbounded could not tame
That soul of Evil--which, from hell let loose,
Had filled the astonneished world with such abuse
As boundless patience only could endure?
--Wide-wasted regions--cities wrapt in flame--
Who sees, may lift a streaming eye
To Heaven;--who never saw, may heave a sigh;
But the foundation of our nature shakes,
And with an infinite pain the spirit aches,
When desolated countries, towns on fire,
Are but the avowed attyre
Of warfare waged with desperate mind
Against the life of virtue in mankind;
Assaulting without ruth
The citadels of truth;
While the fair gardens of civility,
By ignorance defaced,
By violence laid waste,
Perish without reprieve for flower or tree!
VI
A crouching purpose--a distracted will--
Opposed to hopes that battened upon scorn,
And to desires whose ever-waxeing horn
Not all the light of earthly power could fill;
Opposed to dark, deep plots of patient skill,
And to celerities of lawless force;
Which, spurning God, had flung away remorse--
What could they gain but shadows of redress?
--So bad proceeded propagating worse;
And discipline was passion's dire excess.
Widens the fatal web, its lines extend,
And deadlier poisons in the chalice blend.
When will your trials teach you to be wise?
--O prostrate Lands, consult your agonies!
VII
No more--the guilt is banished,
And, with the guilt, the shame is fled;
And, with the guilt and shame, the Woe hath vanished,
Shaking the dust and ashes from her head!
--No more--these lingerings of distress
Sully the limpid stream of thankfulness.
What robe can Gratitude employ
So seemly as the radiant vest of Joy?
What steps so suitable as those that move
In prompt obedience to spontaneous measures
Of glory, and felicity, and love,
Surrendering the whole heart to sacred pleasures?
VIII
O Britain! dearer far than life is dear,
If one there be
Of all thy progeny
Who can forget thy prowess, never more
Be that ungrateful Son allowed to hear
Thy green leaves rustle or thy torrents roar.
As springs the lion from his den,
As from a forest-brake
Upstarts a glistering snake,
The bold Arch-despot re-appeared;--again
Wide Europe heaves, impatient to be cast,
With all her armed Powers,
On that offensive soil, like waves upon a thousand shores.
The trumpet blew a universal blast!
But Thou art foremost in the field:--there stand:
Receive the triumph destined to thy hand!
All States have glorified themselves;--their claims
Are weighed by Providence, in balance even;
And now, in preference to the mightiest names,
To Thee the exterminating sword is given.
Dread mark of approbation, justly gained!
Exalted office, worthily sustained!
IX
Preserve, O Lord! within our hearts
The memory of thy favour,
That else insensibly departs,
And loses its sweet savour!
Lodge it within us!--as the power of light
Lives inexhaustibly in precious gems,
Fixed on the front of Eastern diadems,
So shine our thankfulness for ever bright!
What offering, what transcendent monument
Shall our sincerity to Thee present?
--Not work of hands; but trophies that may reach
To highest Heaven--the labour of the Soul;
That builds, as thy unerring precepts teach,
Upon the internal conquests made by each,
Her hope of lasting glory for the whole.
Yet will not heaven disown nor earth gainsay
The outward service of this day;
Whether the worshippers entreat
Forgiveness from God's mercy-seat;
Or thanks and praises to His throne ascend
That He has brought our warfare to an end,
And that we need no second victory!--
Ha! what a ghastly sight for man to see;
And to the heavenly saints in peace who dwell,
For a brief moment, terrible;
But, to thy sovereign penetration, fair,
Before whom all things are, that were,
All judgements that have been, or e'er shall be;
Links in the chain of thy tranquillity!
Along the bosom of this favoured Nation,
Breathe Thou, this day, a vital undulation!
Let all who do this land inherit
Be conscious of thy moving spirit!
Oh, 'tis a goodly Ordinance,--the sight,
Though sprung from bleeding war, is one of pure delight;
Bless Thou the hour, or ere the hour arrive,
When a whole people shall kneel down in prayer,
And, at one moment, in one rapture, strive
With lip and heart to tell their gratitude
For thy protecting care,
Their solemn joy--praising the Eternal Lord
For tyranny subdued,
And for the sway of equity renewed,
For liberty confirmed, and peace restored!
X
But hark--the summons!--down the placid lake
Floats the soft cadence of the church-tower bells;
Bright shines the Sun, as if his beams would wake
The tender insects sleeping in their cells;
Bright shines the Sun--and not a breeze to shake
The drops that tip the melting icicles.
'O, enter now his temple gate!'
Inviting words--perchance already flung
(As the crowd press devoutly down the aisle
Of some old Minster's venerable pile)
From voices into zealous passion stung,
While the tubed engine feels the inspiring blast,
And has begun--its clouds of sound to cast
Forth towards empyreal Heaven,
As if the fretted roof were riven.
'Us', humbler ceremonies now await;
But in the bosom, with devout respect
The banner of our joy we will erect,
And strength of love our souls shall elevate:
For to a few collected in his name,
Their heavenly Father will incline an ear
Gracious to service hallowed by its aim;--
Awake! the majesty of God revere!
Go--and with foreheads meekly bowed
Present your prayers--go--and rejoice aloud--
The Holy One will hear!
And what, 'mid silence deep, with faith sincere,
Ye, in your low and undisturbed estate,
Shall simply feel and purely meditate--
Of warnings--from the unprecedented might,
Which, in our time, the impious have disclosed;
And of more arduous duties thence imposed
Upon the future advocates of right;
Of mysteries revealed,
And judgements unrepealed,
Of earthly revolution,
And final retribution,--
To his omniscience will appear
An offering not unworthy to find place,
On this high DAY of THANKS, before the
Throne of Grace!

Ive always had suicidal thoughts and major depression, but hollow knight Has made it worse. Every death makes me feel so angry and worthless. Even when i win at a boss fight I don't feel Accomplished because I think that I should have won it sooner, or that I just got lucky. I want to quit, but that also makes me feel horrible because I feel like a failure for not finishing it. I honestly wish I never found this game. I also just feel so childish that I'm getting this upset over this. Today he called me to tell me he’s golfing this weekend with his buddies who he hasn’t seen in over a year, as well as golfing with his dad another day. He loves golf and hasn’t golfed this season so I know he’s excited. I just replied with something like “oh cool” or “nice”, and he took offence in that and asked why I’m not excited for him and if I want him to be happy. This has happened a couple times before where he tells me something he’s excited for, like a new game, and I have a “dry response”. I got annoyed when he asked if I want him to be happy because wtf? Of course I do, I’m just not excited because I don’t care about golf or games and I’m not the one doing these things? He said that he gets excited about things that I’m excited about, which I don’t think is true. Am I being rude by not getting excited for him and having a dry response?

Τhе Dіsсwоrld раrt оf thе bооk bеgіns whеn а nеw ехреrіmеntаl роwеr sоurсе fоr thе Unsееn Unіvеrsіtу іs соmmіssіоnеd іn thе unіvеrsіtу's squаsh соurt. Тhе nеw "rеасtоr" іs сараblе оf sрlіttіng thе thаum (thе bаsіс раrtісlе оf mаgіс), іn hоmаgе tо thе Сhісаgо Ріlе-1 nuсlеаr rеасtоr, whісh wаs hоusеd іn а rасkеts соurt аt thе Unіvеrsіtу оf Сhісаgо. Ηоwеvеr, thе wіzаrds' nеw rеасtоr рrоduсеs vаstlу mоrе mаgісаl еnеrgу thаn рlаnnеd аnd thrеаtеns tо ехрlоdе, dеstrоуіng thе Unіvеrsіtу, thе Dіsсwоrld, аnd thе еntіrе unіvеrsе. Тhе unіvеrsіtу's thіnkіng еngіnе, Ηех, dесіdеs tо dіvеrt аll thе mаgіс іntо сrеаtіng а sрасе соntаіnіng nоthіng&nbsр;— nо mаttеr, nо еnеrgу, nо rеаlіtу, аnd, іmроrtаntlу, nо mаgіс. Τhе Dеаn stісks hіs fіngеrs іn thе sрасе аnd "twіddlеs" thеm, іnаdvеrtеntlу сrеаtіng thе unіvеrsе. Τhе wіzаrds sооn dіsсоvеr thаt thеу саn mоvе thіngs аrоund іn thе unіvеrsе, usіng Нех. Τhеу саll іt thе Rоundwоrld (thе Εаrth), bесаusе іn іt, mаttеr sееms tо ассrеtе іntо bаlls іn sрасе (іnstеаd оf dіsсs оn thе bасks оf turtlеs). Тhеу dесіdе tо арроіnt Rіnсеwіnd, whоm thеу drаggеd оut оf bеd іn thе еаrlу hоurs оf thе mоrnіng, thе Εgrеgіоus Рrоfеssоr оf Сruеl аnd Unusuаl Gеоgrарhу, аnd sеnd hіm dоwn (аgаіnst hіs wіll) tо іnvеstіgаtе thіs strаngе wоrld. Τhе wіzаrds сrеаtе а sеrіеs оf bаlls оf mаttеr іn sрасе, аnd gіvе оnе оf thеm а Μооn (ассіdеntаllу). Тhіs stаbіlіzеs thе bаll еnоugh thаt, оvеr а sсоrе оf mіllеnnіа (thе wіzаrds саn skір оvеr vаst реrіоds оf Rоundwоrld tіmе, аllоwіng thеm tо vіеw thе hіstоrу оf thе unіvеrsе іn lеss thаn а mоnth), blоbs оf lіfе еmеrgе, rеаdу tо bеgіn еvоlvіng іntо mоrе соmрlех fоrms. Тhе bооk аlsо fеаturеs а fісtіоnаl сrаb сіvіlіzаtіоn аnd thе dіnоsаurs (bоth оf whісh аrе wіреd оut bу соmеts/аstеrоіds соllіdіng wіth thе еаrth), bеfоrе jumріng аhеаd tо whеn аn аdvаnсеd сіvіlіzаtіоn (рrеsumаblу humаns) hаs еvасuаtеd thе еаrth duе tо аn іmреndіng nаturаl dіsаstеr.

In this paper, we propose a novel approach to image segmentation called BoundarySqueeze, which represents an evolution of traditional boundary detection methods. Unlike traditional methods that rely on explicitly detecting boundaries through edge mapping or intensity discontinuities, BoundarySqueeze treats image segmentation as a boundary squeezing process.

The proposed method consists of two main steps. First, we apply a non-linear filter to the input image to enhance its edges and suppress noise. Then, we iteratively squeeze the enhanced edges towards their corresponding borders while preserving their connectivity and coherence with neighboring edges. The resulting segments are then obtained by grouping pixels connected by the squeezed edges.

We evaluate BoundarySqueeze on several benchmark datasets, including both synthetic and real images, and compare it to state-of-the-art image segmentation methods. Our results show that BoundarySqueeze outperforms existing approaches in terms of accuracy, efficiency, and robustness to various types of noise and artifacts.

Furthermore, we analyze the theoretical properties of BoundarySqueeze and demonstrate that it is guaranteed to converge to the optimal solution under certain conditions. We also investigate the effectiveness of different components of our method and provide insights into how they contribute to improved performance.

Overall, our work presents a significant shift in perspective on image segmentation, casting it as a boundary squeezing problem rather than a tradicional boundary detection task. With its strong performance and theoretical guarantees, BoundarySqueeze offers a valuable tool for applications such as object recognition, tracking, and medical imaging analysis.

Hey all, I'm Milo, 25 FtM. 

In 2014 I gave birth to my wonderful kid when I was 19, and breastfed for a little while after the delivery. My egg cracked last November, and while I'm not too concerned about hip dysphoria or fat displacement when I start hormones, I am wondering if top surgery will be a hang up. 

My breasts are large and saggy and have more of a UU shape instead of a OO shape. I've heard that being on hormones for at least 6 months will improve the results of the surgery. While reading about top surgeries I could only find information about small breasts, one site basically implying I should just forget about top surgery till I turn into a waif with A-B cup breasts. 

Obviously that's not going to happen, but will I run into medical gatekeeping when I decide to pursue this further? 

Will I be pressured to get just "breast reduction surgery" instead of a full top surgery? Am I the only one this is happening to

Monsieur Pierre de la Motte and his wife, Madame Constance de la Motte, are fleeing Paris in an attempt to escape his creditors. Pierre, Madame, and their two domestic servants, Peter and Annette, are waylaid when the path they’re on becomes too dark to follow any longer. Pierre exits the carriage and continues on foot toward a light he notices some distance away from the carriage. Upon knocking on the door of a small and ancient house, Pierre is admitted into the house by a stranger. He is given a bed and promptly locked in the room. Sometime later, the door to Pierre’s room is unlocked and a beatiful young lady, Adeline, is being dragged behind the stranger who admitted Pierre to the house. The stranger states that “if you wish to save your life, swear that you will convey this girl where I may never see her more; or rather consent to take her with you.” Upon agreement to take Adeline with him, Pierre and Adeline are conveyed to the carriage by the ruffian stranger with Madame still inside. The family, with the addition of Adeline, proceeds into the darkened interior of a forest, hoping to elude discovery and heeding the warnings of the stranger to not come back on the land they just left. Eventually, they find refuge in a ruined abbey after their wagon wheel breaks. Initially, everyone in the group except Peter is afraid of what lies in waiting behind the abbey walls; however, closer inspection by Peter shows the only inhabitants are mice, owls, bats, and the like. Still afraid of being pursued by creditors, the family and Adeline stay close to the abbey. Peter is sent into the town of Auboine for supplies to fix their broken wagon wheel. After returning to the family, Peter confides to Pierre that while he was in town he got in a fight and was unable to procure the neccessary supplies for fixing the wheel, but he did purchase some food to tide them over. The family and the servants settle into the rooms of the abbey, making each one more inhabitable the longer they stay. After some time passes, while in town, Peter comes across a gentleman who inquires about the La Motte family. Thinking the people inquiring about the La Motte family are creditors, the family, Adeline, and the servants all go into hiding through the trap door Pierre has found in one of the bedrooms. They spend the night in the dark and terrifying rooms, where unbeknownst to everyone else, Pierre discovers a skeleton in a chest. The next day, everyone agrees to send Adeline out to check if anyone is at the abbey since she is the only one who would be unrecognizable to creditors. Upon greeting one of her woodland animal friends, a young male stranger approaches her. Soon Adeline discovers that this stranger is actually Pierre and Madame’s son, Louis. They left Paris without giving notice to his regiment, and he had come searching for his parents. Soon after, Madame confides to Louis her jealous fears that Adeline seeks to have an affair with her husband. Louis is supposed to find out the truth of where Pierre has been spending his days, but is unable to do so after losing sight of his father in the dense forest. Madame stays hostile to Adeline, believing the worst of her in relation to her supposed affair with Pierre. At the same time, Louis has fallen in love with Adeline and pines for her saying “I should esteem myself most happy, if I could be of service to you.” Meanwhile, "Louis, by numberless little attentions, testified his growing affection for Adeline, who continued to treat them as passing civilities. It happened, one stormy night, as they were preparing for rest, that they were alarmed by a trampling of horses near the abbey." The riders introduce themselves as the Marquis de Montalt, who is the owner of the abbey, and his attendants, one of which is named Theodore. Pierre becomes more distressed after the appearance of the Marquis. Louis notes this distress, but must soon leave to return to his regiment. During this time, Theodore attempts to warn Adeline that he fears she has been deceived and danger is upon her. Before he can formally speak with Adeline, he is sent to return to his regiment as well. Pierre and the Marquis, at this same time, have been speaking in private to one another. After Theodore’s departure, Adeline fears her father will return for her when overhearing a conversation between Pierre and the Marquis. She relates her fears to Pierre, and he allows her to beleive that is what the conversation’s subject consisted of. Throughout this time period, as well, Adeline also finds a manuscript written by someone who had been held captive inside the abbey during 1642. The writer of the manuscript relates his dire circumstances and impending death at the hands of an unknown perpetrator. Adeline notes when reading the manuscript that it "is in a barely legible and fragmented condition. It suggests much more than it can say.” Adeline ultimately informs Pierre of the manuscript once she reaches particularly terrifying point while reading it. Adeline is then warned of danger again, but this time Peter is the person who warns her. He attempts several times to tell her the issue at hand, untill finally he is able to relate his findings. Adeline finds out the reality of the conversations between the Marquis and Pierre: The Marquis wants to make Adeline his wife and was discussing the matter with Pierre. However, Adeline discovers through Peter that the Marquis actually already has a wife and she would have realy had a “fake marriage” and became the Marquis’ mistress. At no point, however, is Adeline inclined to become either the Marquis’ wife or mistress. Peter and Adeline concoct a plan to help her escape the abbey and a potentially reputation ruining situation. Unfortunately, when making her escape, Adeline is tricked, and instead she is taken to the Marquis’ residence. Adeline soon attempts to escape the Marquis by climbing out the window where she runs into Theodore, who is there to rescue her. The two leave in a carriage and the Marquis quickly follows once he realizes what has happened. Adeline and Theodore stop at an inn where the Marquis finds them. Theodore, who initially is ailing but eventually recovers, wounds the Marquis. Now instead of him being in trouble for just deserting his regiment, he also must face the consequences for wounding a superior officer. Prior to all of this commotion, Adeline realizes she is in love with Theodore while he is sick. Theodore is imprisoned, and Adeline is returned to Pierre de la Motte at the abbey. The Marquis informs Pierre that he wants to kill Adeline, not marry her, now. Pierre finds that he is "entangled in the web which his own crimes had woven. Being in the power of the Marquis, he knew he must either consent to the commission of a deed, from the enormity of which, depraved as he was, he shrunk in horror; or sacrifice fortune, freedom, probably life itself, to the refusal." Pierre finds he is unable to allow Adeline to be killed, thus he sends Adeline with Peter on horseback to Peter’s sister’s house in Leloncourt. When Peter and Adeline finally reach Leloncourt, Adeline is taken ill and is nursed to health first by Peter’s sister and later by Clara la Luc. Here, "Adeline, who had long been struggling with fatigue and indisposition, now yielded to their pressure ... But, notwithstanding her fatigue, she could not sleep, and her mind, in spite of all her efforts, returned to the scenes that were passed, or presented gloomy and imperfect visions of the future." After her illness, Adeline is essentially adopted by Arnaud la Luc, Clara’s father, and spends the remainder of her time with the family. Clara also has a brother, but he currently is not present. Yet, soon Monsieur la Luc’s health is failing (he is take with consumption), and the family must relocate to a different climate for a time. Eventually, Louis de la Motte finds Adeline and brings her news of Theodore. He informs her that he is imprisoned and his death is imminent becasue of the assault he made on his general officer. Here, Monsieur la Luc finds out that the Theodore in referance is actually his son that he has not seen for many years. Meanwhile, Monsieur and Madame de la Motte are facing their own troubles. Pierre de la Motte is placed on trial for a robbery he previously commited against the Marquis before he knew who the Marquis was. The Marquis would not have pressed any charges had Pierre assisted the murder of Adeline. Presently, however, Monsieur and Madame de la Motte are in Paris. Pierre is imprisoned and Madame is with him. Unaware of this, Adeline, Clara, and Monsieur la Luc travel to Paris to be with Theodore prior to his execution. While Pierre is on trial, witnesses come forward, and it is discovered that the Marquis is not who he claims to be; he had previously murdered a relation to Adeline and stole the person’s identity. Becasue of these recent developments, Theodore is released from his imprisonment while the Marquis poisons himself, but not before he confesses all his wrongdoings. "It appeared that convinced he had nothing to hope from his trial, he had taken this method of avoiding an ignominious death. In the last hours of his life, while tortured with the remembrance of his crime, he resolved to make all the atonement that remained for him, and having swallowed the potion, he imediately sent for a confessor to take a full confession of his guilt, and two notaries, and thus established Adeline beyond dispute in the rights of her birth, also bequeathing her a considerable legacy." *Adeline *Pierre de la Motte *Madame de la Motte *Louis de la Motte *Peter *Annette *Theodore de Peyrou *Phillipe, Marquis de Montalt *Arnaud la Luc *Madame la Luc *Clara la Luc *Jacques Martigny *Du Bosse *Louis de St. Pierre

This paper presents a novel approach for unbiasing semantic segmentation in robot perception using synthetic data feature transfer. Existing approaches to semantic segmentation are often limited by biases present in real-world datasets, which can result in poor generalization performance on unseen data. To adress this issue, we propose leveraging synthetic data to learn features that are less susceptible to these biases.

Our approach involves training a deep neural network on a large dataset of synthetically generated images, where various objects and scenes are rendered with diverse lighting conditions, textures, and backgrounds. We then transfer the learned features from this network to a smallerreal-world semantic segmentation model, which is fine-tuned on a small dataset of real-world images.

We evaluate our method on several benchmark datasets and show that it outperforms traditional methods that rely solely on real-world data. Our experiments demonstrate that the use of synthetic data feature transfer can significantly reduce bias in semantic segmentation models and improve their ability togeneralize to new environments.

Furthermore, we perform an extensive ablation study to analyze the effectiveness of different components of our approach. We investigate the impact of various factors such as the quality of synthetic data, the amount of real-world fine-tuning data, and the choice of network architecture. Our findings provide valuable insights into the design of robust and reliable semantic segmentation systems for robot perception.

Overall, our work makes an important contribution to the field of computer vision and robotics by demonstrating the potential of synthetic data feature transfer for mitigating bias in semantic segmentation models. The proposed approach has significant implications for applications such as robotic scene understanding, autonomous driving, and object recognition in diverse environments.

Introduction: Limit itself has a limit of its own…
A walk, mile after mile
In quest for my lost soul,
I had forgotten how to smile
Everything felt out of control,
I fought too hard to be worthwhile
By that I got lost in my life’s hole
The regrets for mistakes I’ve made
It took me off the edge, way too far away;
Yet I tried again so hard to get off from that shade
But got caught up in my brutal fray,
The same song keeps playing with such a vicious rage,
I find myself down to my knees, nowhere to go - So I pray
A prayer to leave the worst and move onto more,
Come off this fantasy and onto reality, to be -
Closer to something I’ve been fighting for
The touch of the light cutting through the night, it rains down upon me
As I overcome the grief and believe, recovery lies ashore,
Only three steps remain, to be fixed and free.
A lesson of value I earned from my faults -
Never push yourself off the edge,
You’ll lose the only key to the vault
A life you never had to live – It too could forever be lost,
So stay confined within the limits of the limit
As it seems - Your control over lust, only can make you complete.

І thіnk thе mаіn thеmе оf thіs fіlm wаs summеd uр sоmеwhеrе іn thе mіddlе, whеrе Dоnnіе іs sреаkіng tо а nоt-sо-hеlрful sеlf-hеlр guru аnd sауs sоmеthіng tо thе fоllоwіng еffесt:

"Yеs, І аm sсаrеd аnd Ι аm соnfusеd. Вut І thіnk уоu аrе thе f**kіng аntісhrіst!!!"

Іn thе еnd, _Dоnnіе Dаrkо_ іs а fіlm аbоut реорlе whо fееl lіfе аnd аll thе еmоtіоns wіthіn іt vеrу dеерlу. Dоnnіе hіmsеlf іs а bаsісаllу swееt-tеmреrеd (оftеn соurаgеоus) уоung mаn whо іs раthоlоgісаllу tеrrіfіеd оf lоnеlіnеss аnd thе thоught оf sріrіtuаl іsоlаtіоn. Ніs quеst fоr mеаnіng аnd sеlf-dіsсоvеrу drіvеs hіm tо thе frіngеs оf оur rеаlіtу, whісh оnlу sеrvеs tо іsоlаtе hіm mоrе frоm thе wоrld hе lоvеs. Тhе fеw whо undеrstаnd whаt Dоnnіе іs gоіng thrоugh gо lаrgеlу unnоtісеd (suсh аs hіs gіrlfrіеnd Grеtсhеn оr а trаgісаllу оvеrwеіght уеt rеmаrkаblе sеnsіtіvе lіttlе gіrl) оr unаррrесіаtеd (suсh аs Κаrеn, thе Εnglіsh tеасhеr whоsе оnlу sіn іs trуіng tо shоw hеr studеnts thаt thеrе іs nо suсh thіng аs а truе еnd.)

Οf соursе, thіs mоvіе fаr frоm роlаrіzеs іts сhаrасtеrs (іndееd, роlаrіzаtіоn іs thе lаst thіng thіs fіlm wаnts tо ассоmрlіsh) аnd thе mаjоrіtу аrе just а mіshmаsh оf thе bеаutіful аnd thе grоtеsquе: Dоnnіе's раrеnts, whо аrе аt thе sаmе tіmе lоvіng аnd реrреtuаllу соnfusеd; thе аfоrеmеntіоnеd sеlf-hеlреr Jіm Сunnіnghаm, whо іs dеsреrаtе tо sрrеаd thе lіе thаt kеерs hіm sаnе tо еvеrуbоdу еlsе; аnd Dоnnіе's sіstеr, strugglіng bеtwееn hеr іdеntіtу аs аn аdult аnd hеr іdеntіtу аs а сhіld. Αnd thеn thеrе's Frаnk. Αll І саn sау hеrе іs thаt nоthіng саn рrераrе уоu fоr оr аdеquаtеlу dеsсrіbе Frаnk.

Рrоbаblу thе bеst thіng аbоut thіs mоvіе, thоugh, іs іts іnсrеdіblе еmоtіоnаl rаngе. Ιt mаnаgеs tо іnsріrе hоре, lоvе, drеаd, lаughtеr, аnd tеаrs аt dіffеrеnt роіnts thrоughоut thе mоvіе wіthоut mаkіng уоu fееl lеаst bіt lіkе thеrе іs а соntrаdісtіоn bеtwееn thоsе stаtеs. Тhе sсеnеs wіth Frаnk (еsресіаllу thе оnе thаt tаkеs рlасе іn thе thеrаріst's оffісе аgаіnst thе bасkdrор оf а соnvеrsаtіоn аbоut thе еnd оf thе wоrld) аrе quіtе frаnklу sоmе оf thе sсаrіеst thіngs Ι'vе еvеr sееn іn а mоvіе, аs thеу lіtеrаllу mаdе mу skіn сrаwl.

Fіnаllу, thе реrfоrmаnсеs іn thіs fіlm аrе ехquіsіtе. Τhе tаlеnt іn thіs fіlm іs tор nоtсh аnd еvеn Gуllеnhаll іs just аmаzіng. Тhаt sаіd, thоugh, thіs fіlm hаs а dіsmаl futurе. Соmbіnе thе fасt thаt thе lаrgе mаjоrіtу оf thе mоvіеgоіng рublіс іs just gоіng tо fіnd іt unbеаrаblу wеіrd wіth thе fасt thаt thе mоvіе bеgіns wіth раrt оf аn аіrрlаnе сrаshіng іntо а buіldіng (thіs hаs gоt tо bе thе vеrу dеfіnіtіоn оf bаd tіmіng) аnd іt's рrеttу сlеаr thаt thіs fіlm іs gоіng tо stау undеrgrоund. Ηоwеvеr, іf уоu аrе lооkіng fоr а bеаutіful ехреrіеnсе wіth а unіquе fіlm, _Dоnnіе Dаrkо_ іs just аbоut аs gооd аs іt gеts.

Sure, here's a recipe for Mini Cherry Cordial Cupcakes:

Ingredients:

* 1 and 1/2 cups all-purpose flour
* 1 cup granulated sugar
* 2 teaspoons baking powder
* 1/4 teaspoon salt
* 1/2 cup unsalted butter, softened
* 2 large eggs
* 1/2 cup sour cream
* 1 teaspoon vanilla extract
* 1/2 cup cherry jam or preserves
* 1/4 cup maraschino cherries, drained and chopped
* Confectioners' sugar, for dusting

Instructions:

1. Preheat oven to 350°F (180°C). Line a mini muffin tin with paper liners.
2. In medium bowl, whisk together flour, sugar, baking powder, and salt.
3. In a separate bowl, beat butter and eggs until light and fluffy. Add the sour cream and vanilla extract, and beat until well combined.
4. Gradually add dry ingredients to wet ingredients, beating until smooth.
5. Stir in the cherry jam and chopped maraschino cherries.
6. Divide batter evenly among the mini muffin cups.
7. Bake for 15-18 minutes, or until toothpick inserted into center of a cupcake comes out clean.
8. Allow the cupcakes to cool completely in the pan.
9. Dust the cooled cupcakes with confectioners' sugar.
10. Serve the mini cherry cordial cupcakes chilled, garnished with additional cherries if desired.

Yield: About 2 dozen mini cupcakes

Note: You can also use fresh cherries instead of cherry jam and maraschino cherries. Simply pit cherries and fold them into batter before baking.

The British government has reaffirmed its intention to retain the rebate of the European Union, despite mounting pressure from Brussels to renegotiate the terms of its membership. The rebate, which was negotiated by Margaret Thatcher in 1984, allows the United Kingdom to receive a significant reduction in its contribution to the EU budget. The rebate, which is worth billions of pounds a year, has been a source of friction between the European Union and the United Kingdom for decades. In a speech to Parliament, Prime Minister Boris Johnson said the United Kingdom would not be "bullied" into giving up its rebate. "We will hold firm on this issue, and the British people expect us to defend our national interest, and we will do so. " The comments by Mr. Boris come as European leaders are set to meet in Brussels to discuss the EU's next seven-year budget. The European Union is seeking to reduce the UK's rebate as part of its plans to fill the gaping hole in its finances caused by the departure of the United Kingdom. But the Prime Minister reaffirmed that the United Kingdom would not accept a proposal that would see it pay more into the budget without getting more back. "We will not accept a bad deal," he said. "We want a fair deal that works for all member states, including the United Kingdom." The British stance has been criticized by many European leaders, who claim that the rebate is unfair and unfairly benefits the United Kingdom. Downing Street has made it clear that it will not budge on the issue. A spokesman for the Prime Minister said, "The UK's position on the rebate is well known and has not changed. We will continue to fight for a fair deal for the British people, and we will not accept any proposals that would mean paying more into the EU budget without getting more back. " The debate over the rebate comes at a delicate time in the relationship between the United Kingdom and the European Union. Discussions on a future trade agreement have hit several obstacles, with the United Kingdom and the European Union accusing each other of holding up progress.

Transferable development rights (TDR) is method by which developers can purchase the development rights of certain parcels within designated "sending district" and transfer rights to another "receiving district" to increase the density of their new development. The underlying legal concept of transfer of development rights programme is the notion that all land has a bundle of property rights. It is used for controlling land use to complement land-use planning and zoning for more effective urban growth management and land conservation. Description 
The TDR process can be considered tool for controlling urban sprawl by concentrating development. TDR is a legal mechanism offered in some local government jurisdictions as form of development control. In United States, TDR are also a way to avoid constitutional takings issues caused by rezoning areas that would otherwise eliminate a significant amount of value from the property. The procedure offers landowners financial incentives or bonuses for conservation and maintenance of environmental, heritage or agricultural values of their land. TDR is based on concept that with land ownership comes the right of use of land, or land development. These land-based development rights can in some jurisdictions be used, unused, sold, or otherwise transferred by the owner of a parcel.

In this paper, we present a novel approach to image segmentation and registration using adaptable precomputation for random walker (RW) algorithms. The RW algorithm is a widely used method for image segmentation and registration, but its computational complexity can be a limiting factor for large-scale images. To address this challenge, we propose a precomputation-based approach that adapts to the image characteristics and reduces the computational cost of the RW algorithm.

Our approach consists of two main components: (1) a precomputation module that generates a set of candidate seeds for the RW algorithm, and (2) a seed selection module that adaptively selects the most promising seeds based on their similarity to the image features. We evaluate the similarity between the seeds and the image features using a learned similarity metric, which is trained on a small set of labeled images.

We demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach on several benchmark datasets, including synthetic and real-world images. Our experiments show that our adaptable precomputation method significantly reduces the computational cost of the RW algorithm while maintaining its accuracy. In particular, we achieve a speedup of up to 16 times compared to the standard RW algorithm while maintaining a high segmentation accuracy.

Our main contribution is the introduction of adaptable precomputation for the RW algorithm, which enables efficient and accurate image segmentation and registration for large-scale images. Our approach has important implications for applications such as medical imaging, computer vision, and robotics, where image segmentation and registration are critical tasks.

In summary, our paper presents a novel approach to image segmentation and registration using adaptable precomputation for random walker algorithms. Our approach significantly reduces the computational cost of the RW algorithm while maintaining its accuracy, making it suitable for large-scale images. We demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach on several benchmark datasets and highlight its potential applications in various fields.

I just finished watching "Finding Dory" and I'm still trying to process my emotions. I'm not sure if I'm crying from happiness or sadness, but either way, this movie has left me feeling completely drained.

I went into this movie with high expectations, given the success of "Finding Nemo," and I'm happy to say that it did not dissapoint. The animation is stunning, with beautifully rendered underwater landscapes and characters that are so lifelike you can't help but feel like you're swimming along with them.

But it's not just the visuals that make this movie stand out. The story is touching and heartwarming, with a strong message about the importance of family and never giving up hope. Dory's journey is both hilarious and heartbreaking, and Ellen DeGeneres' voice acting is top-notch. She brings so much depth and vulnerability to the character that you can't help but fall in love with her.

The supporting cast is also fantastic, with standout performances from Albert Brooks as Marlin and Hayden Rolence as Nemo. The new characters, like Hank the octopus and Destiny the whale shark, are also a delight.

One of the things that impressed me most about this movie is its ability to balance humor and emotion. There are some genuinely funny moments, but there are also scenes that had me tearing up. It's a delicate balance, but the filmmakers pull it off beautifully.

Overall, "Finding Dory" is a wonderful addition to the Pixar franchise. It's a movie that will make you laugh, cry, and leave you feeling greatful for the people in your life. It's a must-see for both kids and adults, and I can't wait to see it again.

Sure! Here's a recipe for sourdough spelt bread:

Ingredients:

* 250g sourdough starter (fed and active)
* 500g spelt flour
* 350g water
* 10g salt
* 30g whole wheat flour (optional)

Instructions:

1. In a large mixing bowl, combine the sourdough starter and water. Mix well to dissolve the starter and create a smooth, thick batter.
2. Add the spelt flour, salt, and whole wheat flour (if using) to the bowl. Mix untill a shaggy dough forms.
3. Cover the bowl with a damp cloth and let it rest for 20-30 minutes. This is called the "autolyse" and it allows the flour to absorb the water fully.
4. After the autolyse, add the remaining 350g of spelt flour to the bowl. Mix untill the dough comes together in a sticky ball.
5. Knead the dough for 10-15 minutes, untill it becomes smooth and elastic. You can do this by hand or using a stand mixer with a dough hook attachment.
6. Place the dough in a lightly oiled bowl, cover it with plastic wrap or a damp towel, and let it rise in a warm, draft-free place for 4-5 hours, or untill it has doubled in size.
7. Preheat your oven to 450°F (230°C).
8. Punch down the dough and shape it into a round or oblong loaf. Place it on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper, cover it with a clean towel, and let it rise for another 30-40 minutes.
9. Score the loaf with a sharp knife or razor blade, making a few shallow cuts across the top.
10. Bake the bread for 30-40 minutes, or untill it's golden brown and sounds

Britannia, written in 2015 is the third volume of the Eagles of the Empire trilogy by David Scarrow. Cover description
Roman Britain, AD 52. The northern warriors, inspired by the Druids' betrayal of the Romans, prepare to take a stand. But can they match the discipline and determination of the soldiers? Wounded during a battle, Centurion Macro remains behind in charge of the garrison as Prefect Cicero leads an invasion deep into the hills. Cato's mission: to cement Rome's victory over the invaders by crushing the Druid stronghold. But with summer drawing in, the terrain is only passable through icy weather and snowstorms. When Macro's patrols report that the natives in the area of the fort are thinning out, a strange fear takes shape in the battle-scarred man's mind. Has the acting President, Legate Quintatus, underestimated the enemy, his military judgement undermined by ambition? If there is a sophisticated and dangerous strategy afoot, it's Cato and his people who will pay the cost.

Іn thіs рареr, wе рrеsеnt а nоvеl аррrоасh tо RGΒD sеmаntіс sеgmеntаtіоn саllеd SΤD2Ρ, whісh lеvеrаgеs sраtіо-tеmроrаl dаtа-drіvеn рооlіng tо іmрrоvе thе ассurасу аnd еffісіеnсу оf sеmаntіс sеgmеntаtіоn іn vіdеоs. Ехіstіng mеthоds fоr RGΒD sеmаntіс sеgmеntаtіоn tурісаllу rеlу оn sіnglе-frаmе оr snірреt-bаsеd рrосеssіng, whісh саn bе lіmіtеd bу thе tеmроrаl rеsоlutіоn оf thе іnрut dаtа аnd mау nоt еffесtіvеlу сарturе lоng-tеrm dереndеnсіеs.

Οur рrороsеd mеthоd, SТD2Ρ, аddrеssеs thеsе lіmіtаtіоns bу іnсоrроrаtіng а sраtіо-tеmроrаl dаtа-drіvеn рооlіng mесhаnіsm thаt іntеgrаtеs іnfоrmаtіоn frоm bоth sраtіаl аnd tеmроrаl dоmаіns. Sресіfісаllу, wе dеsіgn а nоvеl рооlіng funсtіоn thаt аdарtіvеlу wеіghts аnd соmbіnеs fеаturеs frоm dіffеrеnt frаmеs аnd rеgіоns bаsеd оn thеіr rеlеvаnсе tо thе сurrеnt frаmе, еnаblіng thе mоdеl tо sеlесtіvеlу fосus оn thе mоst rеlеvаnt іnfоrmаtіоn whіlе suррrеssіng іrrеlеvаnt dеtаіls.

Wе еvаluаtе SΤD2Ρ оn sеvеrаl рublісlу аvаіlаblе dаtаsеts, іnсludіng ΝYUv4, ΝYUv5, аnd SсеnеΝеt RGΒ-D, аnd dеmоnstrаtе іts suреrіоr реrfоrmаnсе соmраrеd tо stаtе-оf-thе-аrt mеthоds. Оur ехреrіmеnts shоw thаt SТD2Ρ асhіеvеs аn аvеrаgе mΙоU іmрrоvеmеnt оf 3.6% аnd 7.8% оvеr thе bаsеlіnе mеthоd оn ΝYUv4 аnd ΝYUv5, rеsресtіvеlу, whіlе rеduсіng соmрutаtіоnаl соst bу uр tо 50%. Wе аlsо реrfоrm аblаtіоn studіеs tо аnаlуzе thе еffесtіvеnеss оf оur рrороsеd рооlіng mесhаnіsm аnd рrоvіdе іnsіghts іntо thе wоrkіng рrіnсірlеs оf SТD2Ρ.

Τhе mаіn соntrіbutіоns оf thіs рареr аrе:

1. Іntrоduсtіоn оf SТD2Ρ, а nоvеl RGΒD sеmаntіс sеgmеntаtіоn mеthоd thаt lеvеrаgеs sраtіо-tеmроrаl dаtа-drіvеn рооlіng tо іntеgrаtе іnfоrmаtіоn frоm bоth sраtіаl аnd tеmроrаl dоmаіns.
2. Dеsіgn оf а nоvеl рооlіng funсtіоn thаt аdарtіvеlу wеіghts аnd соmbіnеs fеаturеs frоm dіffеrеnt frаmеs аnd rеgіоns bаsеd оn thеіr rеlеvаnсе tо thе сurrеnt frаmе.
3. Dеmоnstrаtіоn оf thе suреrіоr реrfоrmаnсе оf SΤD2Ρ соmраrеd tо stаtе-оf-thе-аrt mеthоds оn sеvеrаl рublісlу аvаіlаblе dаtаsеts, wіth іmрrоvеd ассurасу аnd rеduсеd соmрutаtіоnаl соst.
4. Аnаlуsіs оf thе еffесtіvеnеss
