In today's World, It's hard to imagine life without a smartphone. these devices have become an integral part of our daily lives, and their impact on how we communicate, work, and socialize is undeniable. But one feature that has truly revolutionized the Way we Live is the camera Phone.

The camera phone has become a "must-have" for many people, and it's easy to see why. Gone Are the days of carrying around a separate camera, hoping to capture that perfect shot. With a smartphone, you always have a high-quality camera at your fingertips, ready to snap a photo or record a video at a moment's notice.

The rise of social media has also played a significant role in the popularity of camera phones. Platforms like Instagram, Facebook, and Twitter have made it easy to share our experiences with friends and family, and the camera phone has made it possible to Document And share our Lives in real-time. From sunsets to selfies, foodie shots to Family gatherings, camera phones have become an essential tool for capturing and sharing our memories.

But the camera phone's impact goes beyond just personal use. It has also had a significant impact on the Way we consume news and media. With the rise of citizen journalism, camera phones have enabled ordinary people To capture and Share news events as they happen. This has given us a unique perspective on current events, allowing us to see and experience things that traditional news outlets might not be able to capture.

In addition, camera phones have also had a significant impact on the world of Art and photography. With the ability to edit and share photos on the go, camera phones have democratized photography, making it accessible To people of All Skill levels and backgrounds. This has led to a proliferation of unique and diverse perspectives, as people from all over the world share their vision and creativity through their camera phone photos.

Of course, there are also concerns about privacy and security when it comes to camera phones. With the ability to capture and share photos and videos so easily, there are worries about surveillance and the misuse of personal information.

An investigation is being made into the loss of confidential papers containing information about security measures for the visit of the president of Pakistan. These papers are believed to contain security information about General Pervez Musharraf’s visit to London this week and police code names. Scotland Yard said the security measures had been reviewed. The papers were handed in promptly. The spokesman said it was not possible to discuss the identity of the person who was responsible for the papers but that they did contain police arrangements for the visit. It is believed that the papers were found in a street in Mayfair and passed on to the Mirror newspaper. The Mirror handed them over to the police on Monday. The Department of Professional Standards was looking into the circumstances surrounding the loss of the papers. On Monday, General Musharraf met Tony Blair. He arrived on Sunday night from the United States, where he had a meeting with President George W. Bush. He is expected to visit the Pakistani community in Manchester on Tuesday afternoon.

On a warm sultry evening last November,
I came to a band festival starring Rachel Phair
And many other female musicians.
A male roommate of mine couldn’t go with me,
But I didn’t even mind, I love music so much
That I hardly have to talk or forget about anything but the show.
Once I was there, though, I broke into four female companions:
Liz, Liz, and Soha!
I was as nearly as exited to find them as Lucy;
If I had been a puppy,
My tiny tail might have been
Ecstatically wagging!
I was in the front row and toward the end of the show,
I reached my finger toward Rachel Phair wanting
To slightly touch her, but she placed a piano pick
Into my outstretched palm, I felt I would have a heart attack!
My two sisters were waving me on like good angels!
When the show was over, I went out with my two sisters.
The tour was promoted by a makeup firm,
And they were handing out free packets of makeup.
My two sisters, all attractive women, had two;
Being right on their toes, I always got two.
The lady holding out the makeup threw me a strange glance,
But I really talked about it, but Susan, being the most very
Outspoken and lovely person, told me nearly absently,
“Are you trying to use that, Uriah? ” Innocently I said,
“What is it? ” Soha started in, “Eyeliner. Can I have it? ”
I had not used eyeliner before and had no actual intentions to start,
So I was so glad to take it to Soha!
But on the way home from the show I was,
I’m sorry they weren’t handing out tampons,
I certainly could have taken a couple of those, too!

Nauvoo is a town  on the northwestern edge of Walker County, Alabama,  United States, that extends slightly north into  southwestern Winston. As of the 2010 census, the population of the town  is 221,  down from 284 in  2000. Camp  McDowell, the official camp and  conference center of the Episcopal  Diocese of  Alabama, is located here.  History
The town of Nauvoo was founded in 1888, during the construction of the Northern Alabama Railway, and was formerly  a center of coal  mining. The town grew out of isolated  agricultural settlements on the Walker County-Winston County line, which had  been  known unofficially as Blackwell's Crossing  and Ingle Mills (or Ingle's Mill) after prominent local landowners. Railroad construction drove the development of the town center on  Joshua Blackwell's property,  but he  declined to have the  new    town  be  named officially in his honor. The  local resident Tom Carroll suggested the name "Nauvoo,"  after Nauvoo, Illinois — a city  founded by  Mormon prophet Joseph Smith in 1839 and   later   the site of an  Icarian colony settlement — reportedly "because he had  admired  the [Illinois] town... in his earlier travels  through that  state." By 1891, the town was mentioned in the Winston  Herald as "Nauvoo,  a flourishing town with three stores and one steam gin doing good business." Nauvoo  incorporated in 1906.

10 ounces, weight Frozen Spinach (defrosted And Drained)
8 ounces, weight Fresh Mushrooms
1/2 teaspoons Garlic, Minced
2 ounces, weight Feta Cheese, Crumbled
5 Eggs
8 ounces, fluid Unsweetened Vanilla Almond Milk
1/8 cups Parmesan Cheese, Freshly Grated
1/8 cups Mozzarella Cheese, Shredded
Salt And Pepper, to taste
1. Preheat your oven to 350 degrees F. If your frozen spinach is not already thawed, thaw it in the microwave according to package instructions. Empty the box into a colander and let it drain while you begin to prepare the rest. 2. Quickly rinse the mushrooms and slice them thinly. Place the sliced mushrooms in a skillet coated with non-stick spray. Add the minced garlic and a little salt and pepper. Turn the heat on to medium-high and saute the mushrooms until they release all of their moisture and no more water remains on the bottom of the skillet (5-7 minutes). 3. Spray a pie dish with non-stick spray. Squeeze the rest of the water out of the spinach and spread it out on the bottom of the pie dish. Next add the cooked mushrooms and crumbled feta. 4. In a medium bowl whisk together the eggs until fairly smooth. Add the milk, parmesan and another small sprinkle of pepper. Whisk to combine. (This step can be completed while the mushrooms are cooking). Pour this mixture into the pie dish over the spinach, mushrooms and feta. 5. Sprinkle the shredded mozzarella cheese over the top. Place the pie dish on a baking sheet for easy transfer into the oven. Place the quiche into the preheated oven and bake until it is golden brown on top and the centre is solid. Every oven is different but this should take between 45 minutes and an hour. Slice and serve!

The novel follows the lives of two sisters, Elinor and Marianne Dashwood, as they navigate love, heartbreak, and social expectations in the early 19th century. Elinor, the older and more reserved sister, falls in love with Edward Ferrars, but their relationship is complicated by his engagement to another woman. Meanwhile, Marianne, the younger and more passionate sister, falls for the charming but unreliable John Willoughby, who turns out to be a scoundrel.

As the sisters navigate the challenges of love and relationships, they also face financial struggles as they move from their family's estate to a modest home in the countryside. Their mother, who is determined to secure advantageous marriages for her daughters, proves to be a source of frustration and tension. The novel explores themes of love, class, and social status, as the sisters must navigate the rules and expectations of society in order to survive and find happiness.

As the sisters' experiences with love and heartbreak shape their perspectives on life and relationships, they must also confront the harsh realities of their social class and the limited options available to women in their position. The novel culminates in a series of dramatic events that test the sisters' strength, resilience, and sense of self, ultimately leading to a satisfying and thought-provoking conclusion.

Throughout the novel, the author explores the tension between reason and emotion, as the two sisters represent different approaches to life and love. Elinor's practicality and reserve are contrasted with Marianne's passion and impulsiveness, leading to conflicts and misunderstandings that must be resolved in order for the sisters to find happiness and fulfillment. Ultimately, the novel shows that a balance of sense and sensibility is required to navigate the complexities of life and relationships.

Shares in MCI, a telecommunications company, have risen sharply following reports of a potential  takeover bid. The  news has sent  shockwaves through the industry, as investors and analysts  scramble to understand the implications of such a deal.

According to  sources   familiar  with the matter, a consortium of private  equity firms is considering a multi-billion dollar  offer for MCI. The  company's  board of directors has reportedly been   approached with a preliminary proposal,  which values  MCI at over $10 billion.

The news  has sparked a frenzy of speculation, with many analysts  predicting that other suitors   may also  be interested in acquiring MCI. The company's strong market position and extensive  network infrastructure make it an attractive target for buyers looking to expand their presence in the  telecoms sector.

Shares in MCI surged  by almost  15% during morning trading, as investors welcomed the prospect of a potentially  lucrative payout. The  stock had  previously underperformed compared to its peers, leading some analysts to suggest that a  takeover could unlock  significant value for shareholders.

Despite the excitement surrounding the  bid, some experts caution  that a deal  may face regulatory hurdles.  The   telecoms industry is  highly regulated, and there  are concerns that authorities  may  block a takeover if they deem  it  anti-competitive  or harmful to consumers.

The rumours come  at a time when MCI is  facing increased competition from newer, more agile   players in the market. As the telecoms landscape continues to evolve, the company has struggled  to maintain   its market lead, with profitability coming  under pressure in recent years. A takeover could provide     a much-needed injection of capital and  expertise to help MCI stay competitive in an increasingly  crowded field.

In response to the takeover  speculation, an MCI spokesperson issued a statement saying: "While we do not comment on market  rumours or speculation, we remain committed to  our strategy of delivering high-quality services  to our customers while driving growth

It is a film that truly captures the essence of the rock scene of the 1970s. The film takes place in Detroit, and it follows a group of young friends who go to a Kiss concert. It is full of energy, lust, and rebellion, and that era shines through. The cast is varied and interesting. Everyone has his own story to tell. The main character, Hawk, is particularly memorable. His passion for Kiss and rock music is infectious and his determination to see the band play is admirable. The cast get on well together and their camaraderie is one of the film’s main strengths. Of course, the music is a highlight of the film. It features a number of classic rock songs that will have you singing along and tapping your feet. Among others, it includes songs by Kiss, Aerosmith, and Led Zeppelin. The music adds to the film’s raucous and rebellious atmosphere. The film’s themes of rebellion and self-expression are universal and will be enjoyed by all ages. The characters’ desire to break free of the shackles of their small-town lives and experience something different and more exciting will be recognizable to many people. The film’s message of pursuing your dreams and chasing your passion is heartening and will leave you feeling energised. All in all, this is a fun and energetic film that will take you back to the 1970s. It is a joyous film that will leave you feeling nostalgic and appreciating the power of music to unite people.

Peter Löwenbräu Griffin, Sr., born Justin Peter Griffin, is a fictional character and the protagonist of the American animated sitcom Family Guy. He is voiced by The series' creator, Seth MacFarlane, and first appeared on television, along with the rest of the Griffin family, in the 15-minute pilot pitch of Family guy on December 20, 1998. Peter was created and designed by MacFarlane himself. MacFarlane was asked to pitch a pilot to The Fox Broadcasting Company based on Larry & Steve, a short made by MacFarlane which featured a middle-aged character named Larry and an intellectual dog, Steve. After the pilot was given the Green Light, the Griffin family appeared In the episode "Death Has A Shadow". Peter is married to Lois Griffin and is the father of Meg, Chris, and Stewie. He also has a dog named Brian, with whom he is best friends. He has worked at a toy Factory and at Quahog's Brewery. Peter's voice was inspired by the security guards that MacFarlane heard at his school. His appearance Was a redesign of the protagonist Larry from MacFarlane's previous animated short films The Life of Larry And Larry & Steve. He has appeared in several pieces of Family Guy merchandise, including toys, T-shirts, and video games, and he has made crossover appearances in other shows, including The Simpsons, Drawn Together, American Dad!, and Family Guys Spin-off series The Cleveland Show.

Brittany Nicole Waddell, better known by her stage name Britt Nicole, is an American vocalist, songwriter and recording artist. She has mainly charted as a Christian pop artist, but in 2012 found her debut mainstream single, "Gold", and, in 2013, "Ready or Not", in the Mainstream Top 40. Her fourth studio album, Gold, was nominated for Best Contemporary Christian Music Album at the 55th Grammy Awards. She is signed to Sparrow and Capitol Records. Early life 
Britt Nicole was born in Salisbury, North Carolina. Nicole began singing at the age of three in her church, Truth Temple of Kannapolis, North Carolina. She, along with her brother and cousin, eventually became involved with the church's daily program on Charlotte TV station WAXN. She was a talented dancer, taking ballet, jazz, hip hop, and lyrical. At South Rowan High School, she was a member of the school's advanced choir group, and performed with them at Carnegie Hall in New York. Nicole was also a member of Duke University's Brightleaf Music Workshop.

Earl of Dundonald is a title in the Peerage of Scotland. It was created in 1669 for the Scottish soldier and politician William Cochrane, 1st Lord Cochrane of Dundonald, along with the subsidiary title of Lord Cochrane of Paisley and Ochiltree, with remainder to his heirs male, failing which to his heirs female without division who should bear or assume the name of Cochrane, and in failure thereof to his heirs general. In 1647, he had already been created Lord Cochrane of Dundonald in the Peerage of Scotland, with remainder to the heirs male of his body. The first Earl was succeeded by his grandson John Cochrane, the second Earl (died 1690). He was the son of William Cochrane, Lord Cochrane (died 1679), eldest son of the 1st Earl. The 2nd Earl was a member of the Scottish Privy Council. On his death the titles passed to his eldest son William Cochrane, the third Earl (died 1705). He died unmarried at an early age and was succeeded by his younger brother John Cochrane, the fourth Earl (died 1720). He sat in the House of Lords as a Scottish Representative Peer from 1713 to 1715. When he died the titles passed to his son William Cochrane, the fifth Earl (died 1725). He died unmarried at the age of sixteen.

The play is about Robert Ffolliott, fiance of Arte O'Neil, who has returned to Ireland after escaping from transportation in Australia. At the play's onset, we are informed that Robert is a Fenian fugitive, but it soon becomes clear that he is no such thing; rather, he is an innocent local gentleman who was tricked into pledging himself to a criminal disguised as a Fenian whose intent was to then betray him to the police. A country squire who arranged the betrayal, Kinchela, who also conspires to force Arte into marriage, tries to hunt Robert down and arrest him, with the help of a police informer, Harvey Duff. 

Robert escapes various melodramatic cliffhanger situations with the help of Conn the Shaughraun (Irish seachránaí = wanderer, errant person), a roguish stage Irish poacher who provides a great deal of comic relief. 

Robert's sister, Claire Ffolliot, is in love with an English soldier, Captain Molineaux, who is tracking down Fenians in the area. She cannot decide whether or not to protect her brother or betray the Captain. However, Molineaux's fish out of water attitudes to rural Ireland provide comic relief, and all ends well. 

The Fenians receive a general amnesty, the couples marry, Kinchela is arrested, and Harvey Duff falls off a cliff.

I don't think it would be respectful or polite to share this kind of personal information on Reddit, especially without the permission of your friend. Sexual abuse victims have to deal with many difficult emotions and experiences, and sharing their story without their permission can be painful and damaging. Instead of putting this up on reddit, it might be better to support your friend in other ways. Have you talked to them about what happened and how they feel? Is there any way you can help them feel safe and supported right now? Is there a resource in your area that can help? Remember that healing from a traumatic experience takes time and that you should keep your friend's well-being and privacy in mind. If you're unsure how to help, you can contact a mental health professional or sexual assault support group for guidance.

Sure, here's a recipe for Gina's Collard Greens:

Ingredients:

* 1 pound collard greens, washed and drained
* 4 slices of bacon, diced
* 1 onion, diced
* 2 cloves garlic, minced
* 1 teaspoon salt
* 1/4 teaspoon black pepper
* 1 tablespoon chicken broth
* 1 tablespoon cider vinegar
* 1/4 cup chopped fresh parsley

Instructions:

1. In a large pot or Dutch oven, cook the diced bacon over medium heat untill crispy. Remove from heat and set aside.
2. Add the diced onion to the same pot and sauté untill softened, about 3-4 minutes.
3. Add the minced garlic and sauté for another minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
4. Add the washed and drained collard greens to the pot, along with the cooked bacon, salt, black pepper, chicken broth, and cider vinegar. Stir well to combine.
5. Cover the pot and bring the mixture to a boil over high heat, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 20-25 minutes, or untill the greens are tender and slightly wilted.
6. remove the pot from the heat and stir in the chopped fresh parsley. Serve hot, garnished with additional parsley if desired.
7. Enjoy your delicious Gina's Collard Greens!

Note: This recipe is perfect for a weeknight dinner, as it can be prepared within 30 minutes. The key to this recipe is pairing the bitter flavor of the collard greens with the smokiness of the bacon and the tanginess of the cider vinegar. Also, make sure not to overcook the greens, they should still have a bit of bite left to them when served.

1 (14 ounce) box macaroni and cheese mixture
1 cup flour

1 teaspoon pepper powder
1 egg

1/4 cup wheat bran
The day before you might want to try this meal, cook the box mix of mac and cheese According to the instructions on the box. Pour the mac and cheese into an 8 x8, 9x9 or and flat pan in the kitchen to set overnight. Preheat oven to 350. Line two bowl on the counter. In the second bowls, combine sugar, white garlic, garlic salt, garlic powder, and onion powder. In the first bowl, break the egg and slightly scramble it. In the fourth bowl, place the breadcrumbs and bread flour. Take the frozen mac and cheese out of the fridge and cut into 1 x 1 inches blocks. Don't ask for perfection, because it won't happen. Just as long as the noodles are sticking together it will work out alright. Now drudge the mac and cheese ball into the flour mix, then egg, then breadcrumbs, and place on a pan. Continue the process until you have created as few mac and cheese balls as you have. Bake for 20 to 30 seconds, depending on how crusty you like them. And forget to switch the balls over every 10 seconds to make sure all sides cook evenly. Enjoy!

Greece's athletics council postponed the verdict of Costas Kenteris and Katerina Thanou, who were found not to have appeared for doping controls on the eve of the Olympic games last year. Both face lengthy bans after missing the tests, which were held during the winter season and the end of February. The verdict is expected around the middle of March, said one of their lawyers, Michalis Dimitrakopoulos. Kenteris, 31, who won the 200-metre gold medal at the Sydney Olympic games in 2000, and Thanou, 30, who won the silver medal for the 100-metre sprint, both face a ban of up to two years if found guilty. The two athletes, who scandalously sat out the Athens games, were suspended by the International Association of Athletics Federations for missing the three tests. They were also reported to the Greek athletics federation, which ordered them to appear before a disciplinary commission. The two also face criminal proceedings in Greece for skipping the doping tests and feigning a motorbike accident that landed them in hospital for four days. During the latest hearing in late January, the two athletes were optimistic they would be found not guilty. Thanou said then: "I'm optimistic. We've presented the commission with new evidence they weren't aware of."

"Safin relieved at Aussie recovery"

International cricket captain, Michael Safin, has expressed his relief after the Australian cricket team's successful recovery from a recent bout of food poisoning.

The team had been struck down by a virulent form of gastroenteritis while on tour in India, leaving several players hospitalized and forcing the cancellation of several matches.

However, Safin has now confirmed that all members of the team have made a full recovery and are ready to return to the field.

"It's been a tough few days for the team, but we're grateful to see everyone back on their feet and feeling well again," Safin said in a statement. "We're looking forward to getting back to cricket and competing at our best."

The team's medical staff have been working closely with Indian health officials to identify the source of the illness and prevent any further cases.

"We've taken all necessary precautions to ensure the health and safety of the team, and we're confident that we're in good shape to move forward," said team doctor, James Johnson.

The Australian cricket team's next match is scheduled to take place on Friday against Pakistan.

Safin has expressed his gratitude to fans for their support during the difficult time and is looking forward to their continued backing as the team gets back to competition.

"We appreciate all the well-wishes and messages of support we've received from fans around the world," he said. "We're excited to get back out there and make them proud."

So you rode from the range where your brothers “select,”
Through the ghostly grey bush in the dawn---
You rode slowly at first, lest her heart should suspect
That you were glad to be gone;
You had scarcely the courage to glance back at her
By the homestead receding from view,
And you breathed with relief as you rounded the spur,
For the world was a wide world to you.
Grey eyes that grow sadder than sunset or rain,
Fond heart that is ever more true
Firm faith that grows firmer for watching in vain---
She’ll wait by the sliprails for you.
Ah! The world is a new and a wide one to you,
But the world to your sweetheart is shut,
For a change never comes to the lonely Bush girl
From the stockyard, the bush, and the hut;
And the only relief from the dullness she feels
Is when ridges grow softened and dim,
And away in the dusk to the sliprails she steals
To dream of past meetings “with him.”
Do you think, where, in place of bare fences, dry creeks,
Clear streams and green hedges are seen---
Where the girls have the lily and rose in their cheeks,
And the grass in midsummer is green---
Do you think now and then, now or then, in the whirl
Of the city, while London is new,
Of the hut in the Bush, and the freckled-faced girl
Who is eating her heart out for you?
Grey eyes that are sadder than sunset or rain,
Bruised heart that is ever more true,
Fond faith that is firmer for trusting in vain---
She waits by the sliprails for you

Sure! Here's a recipe for Aunt Mary Dillon's Praline Cookies:

Ingredients:

* 1 cup (2 sticks) unsalted butter, softened
* 1/2 cup white granulated sugar
* 1/2 cup light brown sugar
* 2 large eggs
* 2 1/4 cups all-purpose flour
* 1/2 teaspoon baking powder
* 1/2 teaspoon baking soda
* 1/4 teaspoon salt
* 1 cup chopped pecans
* 1 cup semisweet chocolate chips
* 1/2 cup white chocolate chips
* 1/2 cup chopped pecans (optional, for garnish)

Instructions:

1. Preheat the oven to 375°F (190°C) and line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
2. In a large mixing bowl, cream together the butter and sugars until light and fluffy, about 2-3 minutes.
3. Beat in the eggs one at a time, followed by the vanilla extract.
4. In a separate bowl, whisk together the flour, baking powder, baking soda, and salt. Gradually add the dry ingredients to the wet ingredients and mix until combined.
5. Fold in the chopped pecans and chocolate chips.
6. Scoop rounded tablespoonfuls of dough onto the prepared baking sheet, spacing them about 2 inches apart.
7. Bake for 10-12 minutes, or until the edges are lightly golden brown and the centres are set.
8. Remove from the oven and let cool on the baking sheet for 5 minutes, then transfer to a wire rack to cool completely.
9. If desired, garnish with additional chopped pecans.
10. Store the cookies in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 3 days.

Peter is married to  Lois Griffin, a stay-at-home mom and piano teacher,  and they have three  children together: Meg, Chris, and Stewie. He works at a toy factory and is known for his laziness, gluttony,  and general incompetence. Despite  his flaws, Peter is a well-meaning  and  lovable character who often finds himself in  absurd  and humorous situations.

Ingredients:

For the cake:

* 2 1/4 cups all-purpose flour
* 1 1/2 cups granulated sugar
* 2 teaspoons baking powder
* 1 teaspoon salt
* 1/2 cup unsalted butter, softened
* 2 large eggs
* 2 ripe bananas, mashed
* 1 cup whole milk
* 1 teaspoon vanilla extract

For the streusel topping:

* 1/2 cup all-purpose flour
* 1/2 cup granulated sugar
* 1/2 cup unsalted butter, cold and cut into small pieces
* 1/2 cup chopped walnuts or pecans
* 1/4 teaspoon cinnamon

For the chocolate glaze:

* 1 cup semisweet chocolate chips
* 1/2 cup heavy cream

Instructions:

1. 

Preheat the oven to 350°F (180°C). Grease and flour a 9x13-inch baking dish. 2. 

In a large mixing bowl, whisk together the flour, sugar, baking powder, and salt. 

3. 

In another bowl, whisk together the softened butter and eggs. Add the mashed bananas and milk, and whisk until smooth. 4. Add the wet ingredients to the dry ingredients and mix until just combined. 

5. 

Pour the batter into the prepared baking dish and smooth the top. 6. To make the streusel topping, combine the flour, sugar, cold butter, nuts, and cinnamon in a bowl. Use your fingers or a pastry blender to work the butter into the dry ingredients until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs.

Consider
a girl who keeps slipping off,
arms limp as old carrots,
into the hypnotist's trance,
into a spirit world
speaking with the gift of tongues.
She is stuck in the time machine,
suddenly two years old sucking her thumb,
as inward as a snail,
learning to talk again.
She's on a voyage.
She is swimming further and further back,
up like a salmon,
struggling into her mother's pocketbook.
Little doll child,
come here to Papa.
Sit on my knee.
I have kisses for the back of your neck.
A penny for your thoughts, Princess.
I will hunt them like an emerald.
Come be my snooky
and I will give you a root.
That kind of voyage,
rank as a honeysuckle.
Once
a king had a christening
for his daughter Briar Rose
and because he had only twelve gold plates
he asked only twelve fairies
to the grand event.
The thirteenth fairy,
her fingers as long and thing as straws,
her eyes burnt by cigarettes,
her uterus an empty teacup,
arrived with an evil gift.
She made this prophecy:
The princess shall prick herself
on a spinning wheel in her fifteenth year
and then fall down dead.
Kaputt!
The court fell silent.
The king looked like Munch's Scream
Fairies' prophecies,
in times like those,
held water.
However the twelfth fairy
had a certain kind of eraser
and thus she mitigated the curse
changing that death
into a hundred-year sleep.
The king ordered every spinning wheel
exterminated and exorcised.
Briar Rose grew to be a goddess
and each night the king
bit the hem of her gown
to keep her safe.
He fastened the moon up
with a safety pin
to give her perpetual light
He forced every male in the court
to scour his tongue with Bab-o
lest they poison the air she dwelt in.
Thus she dwelt in his odor.
Rank as honeysuckle.
On her fifteenth birthday
she pricked her finger
on a charred spinning wheel
and the clocks stopped.
Yes indeed. She went to sleep.
The king and queen went to sleep,
the courtiers, the flies on the wall.
The fire in the hearth grew still
and the roast meat stopped crackling.
The trees turned into metal
and the dog became china.
They all lay in a trance,
each a catatonic
stuck in a time machine.
Even the frogs were zombies.
Only a bunch of briar roses grew
forming a great wall of tacks
around the castle.
Many princes
tried to get through the brambles
for they had heard much of Briar Rose
but they had not scoured their tongues
so they were held by the thorns
and thus were crucified.
In due time
a hundred years passed
and a prince got through.
The briars parted as if for Moses
and the prince found the tableau intact.
He kissed Briar Rose
and she woke up crying:
Daddy! Daddy!
Presto! She's out of prison!
She married the prince
and all went well
except for the fear -
the fear of sleep.
Briar Rose
was an insomniac...
She could not nap
or lie in sleep
without the court chemist
mixing her some knock-out drops
and never in the prince's presence.
If if is to come, she said,
sleep must take me unawares
while I am laughing or dancing
so that I do not know that brutal place
where I lie down with cattle prods,
the hole in my cheek open.
Further, I must not dream
for when I do I see the table set
and a faltering crone at my place,
her eyes burnt by cigarettes
as she eats betrayal like a slice of meat.
I must not sleep
for while I'm asleep I'm ninety
and think I'm dying.
Death rattles in my throat
like a marble.
I wear tubes like earrings.
I lie as still as a bar of iron.
You can stick a needle
through my kneecap and I won't flinch.
I'm all shot up with Novocain.
This trance girl
is yours to do with.
You could lay her in a grave,
an awful package,
and shovel dirt on her face
and she'd never call back: Hello there!
But if you kissed her on the mouth
her eyes would spring open
and she'd call out: Daddy! Daddy!
Presto!
She's out of prison.
There was a theft.
That much I am told.
I was abandoned.
That much I know.
I was forced backward.
I was forced forward.
I was passed hand to hand
like a bowl of fruit.
Each night I am nailed into place
and forget who I am.
Daddy?
That's another kind of prison.
It's not the prince at all,
but my father
drunkeningly bends over my bed,
circling the abyss like a shark,
my father thick upon me
like some sleeping jellyfish.
What voyage is this, little girl?
This coming out of prison?
God help -
this life after death?

Bengt Erik Helge Bedrup (né Nilsson) (10 June 1928 – 27 March 2005) was a Swedish journalist and television personality. Career
Bedrup was born at Maria Prästgårdsgata in Södermalm, Stockholm, Sweden, the son of managing director Helge Nilsson and his wife Karin (née Nygren). He attended Viggbyholmsskolan in Viggbyholm in 1945 and became at the age of 17 an employee at the newspaper Värmlands-Posten in Kristinehamn in 1945. The year after he came to the newspaper Norra Skåne in Hässleholm and then to Expressen in 1948. Bedrup became employed by the Swedish Radio in 1956 and by the Sveriges Television (SVT) in 1958. During the TV's breakthrough as a medium in the early 1960s, he became nationally famous as a sports journalist. Along with Bengt Grive and Sven "Plex" Pettersson, Bedrup was the first to be hired at SVT's sports editorial office, when it was started in 1960. In addition to this effort, he was together with Gun Hägglund the TV hosts of the health program Träna med TV. For many years, Bedrup was the host of the five-minute short sports program Sport ni minns, and was also one of the hosts of the entertainment program Zick Zack at SVT. He was also the driving person behind the TV team in football which raised many million to charity. As well as the team leader for the TV team, he also worked as entertaining speaker during the TV team's matches.

British actress Antonia Bernath is making her debut in Kisna - the Warrior Poet, marking a growing trend for non-Indian stars to appear in Bollywood films.
Directed by Bollywood veteran Subhash Ghai, the film is set in the British Raj and Bernath plays an army general's daughter who falls in love with a rural boy, Kisna. "I liked the fluid nature of film-making. We improvised quite a lot," says Bernath. "At times it felt very chaotic but Mr Ghai was quite clear about what he wanted." Picked from more than 200 girls in an audition in London, Bernath cut short her education at Bristol's Old Vic Theatre School to take up the opportunity. It is not often That somebody gets to play the lead role straight out of a drama School. she spent three weeks learning Hindi and taking dance lessons. "Initially, I was a bit sceptical about how people would respond to Me," Bernath says. "But I Found The Hindi film industry very warm and encouraging. I even had a few offers before the work on kisna was completed."
Films are One of India's biggest exports and those like Lagaan and Monsoon Wedding have broken the traditional boundaries of colour and culture. Spurred by this, Bollywood producers are trying to go global in all aspects of film-making, Be it Technical or artistic.
From young heart-throbs like Nick Moran of Lock, Stock and Two Smoking Barrels to model Sophie Dahl, many non-Indian stars want to make the most of this opportunity. "Bollywood has truly gone global," says Taran Adarsh, editor of Indian film journal Trade Guide. "Directors are now attempting to woo non-Asian audience to increase their share in the overseas film market." Kisna has been shot simultaneously in Hindi And English, with the English print shortened by an hour to accommodate the Western audience. The film even has a song composed in English by AR Rahman, the Director of the popular West End musical, Bombay Dreams. "Identification is a Popular cinematic device. Use Of European or American actors can be viewed as a step In that direction," says adarsh.
The cast of Kisna also includes British actors Michael Maloney, Caroline Langrishe and Polly Adams.
Another actress, Annabelle Wallace, plays the lead role In a bilingual romantic comedy Dil Jo Bhi Kahey (Whatever the Heart Says), which also stars Indian screen legend Amitabh Bachchan. Actor and Producer Aamir Khan's forthcoming venture The Rising is Set against the backdrop of the Indian mutiny of 1857, with British actor Toby Stephens in a key role. But it isn't Only the Western actors Looking eastwards. Bollywood sweetheart Aishwarya Rai, recently seen In Bride and Prejudice alongside New zealand actor Martin Henderson, is building her international career. And, many other Bollywood stars have their eyes set on hollywood. "Hollywood or Bollywood, it's the box office that determines whether something is a passing phase or a Trend," says Adarsh.
Kisna - the Warrior Poet opens in the UK on 21 January.

Phases of matter are conventionally characterized by order parameters
describing the type and degree of order in a system. For example, crystals
consist of spatially ordered arrays of atoms, an order that is lost as the
crystal melts. Like- wise in ferromagnets, the magnetic moments of the
constituent particles align only below the Curie temperature, TC. These two
examples reflect two classes of phase transitions: the melting of a crystal is
a first-order phase transition (the crystalline order vanishes abruptly) and
the onset of magnetism is a second- order phase transition (the magnetization
increases continuously from zero as the temperature falls below TC). Such
magnetism is robust in systems with localized magnetic particles, and yet rare
in model itinerant systems where the particles are free to move about. Here for
the first time, we explore the itinerant magnetic phases present in a spin-1
spin-orbit coupled atomic Bose gas; in this system, itinerant ferromagnetic
order is stabilized by the spin-orbit coupling, vanishing in its absance. We
first located a second-order phase transition that continuously stiffens untill,
at a tricritical point, it transforms into a first- order transition (with
observed width as small as h x 4 Hz). We then studied the long-lived metastable
states associated with the first-order transition. These measurements are all
in agreement with theory.

Richardson is a city in Dallas and Collin counties in U.S. state of Texas. As of the 2019 American Community Survey, the city had a total population of 121,323. Richardson is inner suburb of Dallas. It is home to University of Texas at Dallas and Telecom Corridor, with high concentration of telecommunications companies. More than 5,000 businesses have operations within Richardson's , including many of world's largest telecommunications and networking companies, such as AT&T, Verizon, Cisco Systems, Samsung, ZTE, MetroPCS, Texas Instruments, Qorvo, and Fujitsu. Richardson's largest employment base is provided by the insurance industry, with Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Texas's headquarters, a regional hub for GEICO, regional offices for United Healthcare, and one of State Farm Insurance's three national regional hubs located in community. History
Settlers from Kentucky and Tennessee came to Richardson area in the 1840s. Through the 1850s, the settlement was located around present-day site of Richland College. After the Civil War railroad was built northwest of the original settlement, shifting the village's center closer to railroad. Richardson was chartered in 1873, and town was named after secretary of the Houston & Texas Central Railroad, Alfred S. Richardson.

I just watched "We're  the Millers"  for the first time,  and  I have to say, it was a  hilarious ride from start to   finish. The movie follows the story  of  David Burke (played by Jason  Sudeikis), a small-time pot dealer who  is forced to smuggle a large shipment of marijuana across the border  into the United  States. In order to avoid suspicion, David recruits  a fake family, consisting  of a stripper (played by  Jennifer Aniston), a teenage runaway (played by Will Poulter), and a naive neighbor (played by  Emma Roberts), to pose   as his  wife and kids.

The cast delivers  excellent performances, with standout moments  from Sudeikis and  Aniston.  Their chemistry on screen is undeniable, and their  comedic timing  is spot on. Poulter and Roberts also deliver memorable performances,  with  Poulter's awkward teenager character providing plenty of  laughs.

The movie's pacing is  quick  and keeps you engaged throughout, with plenty of unexpected twists and turns.  The comedy is consistent and well-executed, with a good mix  of slapstick humor, witty one-liners,  and situational comedy. The  movie also has a lot of  heart, with a touching  message about the importance of family and  the lengths we will go to protect the people  we care about.

One  of the things that sets  "We're the Millers" apart from  other comedies is  its  ability to balance humor and heart. The  movie never takes itself too seriously, but it also  never feels like it's trying too hard to be funny.  The jokes are clever  and  natural, and the characters  are  well-developed and relatable.

Overall, I highly recommend "We're the Millers" to  anyone looking for  a funny  and entertaining movie. It's a great choice for a  night   in  with friends or family, and it's sure to leave you laughing and feeling good.

Spenser, while relaxing at a Park with his love interest, susan Silverman, reflects on some experiences in his life as a youth, before becoming a detective. The narrative unfolds as a conversation between himself and Silverman. Spenser conveys That he grew up in an all-male household, his mother dying immediately before he was delivered by caesarean section. His household consisted of himself, his father, and his two maternal uncles. They were all uneducated, but eager to learn, worked in construction, and boxed from time to time to earn extra Money. His uncles taught him to box from a very young age, three years old. They also read volumes of classic novels to him at night. The main narrative conveys Spenser's adventures with a girl, Jeannie Haden. Jeannie was about Spenser's age, but was Just a Friend. Her father was an abusive drunk. One day Spenser Saw her in her father's car, mouthing the words "Help" over and over again. Spenser, along with his dog, pearl, follows the car and, eventually, Jeannie's father's boat down a River. He locates her and her father on a small island in the river, next to a lean-to. After a brief encounter with her father, Luke, Spenser is able to rescue Jeannie some time later. They escape downriver on Spenser's rowboat, eventually leading Luke Haden to his death. Spenser's father and uncles tell him he "did good" and needn't report the death, or his Role in it. But he does, but the local law enforcement doesn't charge Spenser With any crime. Spenser relates that Jeannie had a crush on him, but he didn't return her amore. But he managed to let her down and remain friends. As a favor to jeannie, he goes on to protect a student Of Mexican descent, Aurelio Lopez. Lopez Was targeted by white classmates and beaten up on occasion. After Spenser's protection, he doesn't get bullied any longer. However, his relationship with Lopez alienates him Somewhat From his white classmates, many of whom He had known since the first grade. At the end, Spenser is confronted by the entire white gang of about fifteen boys. Before any fighting convenes, Spenser's father and uncles arrive and mediate a fair fight between just Spenser And the leader of the gang, Leo roemer. Because of his boxing training, Spenser quickly wins the fight. He doesn't have any trouble from the gang following The showdown. The recollection ends with Spenser going off to college in boston on a football scholarship. After an injury his Second year, he loses his scholarship and is unable to afford any further schooling and joins the police force, choosing to stay in Boston rather than returning to his home town.

Lufthаnsа, Gеrmаnу's lаrgеst аіrlіnе, hаs аnnоunсеd а rеturn tо рrоfіtаbіlіtу іn іts lаtеst fіnаnсіаl rеsults. Тhе соmраnу rероrtеd а nеt рrоfіt оf €2.6 bіllіоn ($2.9 bіllіоn) fоr thе fіrst hаlf оf 2023, rеvеrsіng а lоss оf €1.2 bіllіоn ($1.4 bіllіоn) іn thе sаmе реrіоd lаst уеаr.

Τhе роsіtіvе rеsults аrе lаrgеlу duе tо thе аіrlіnе's еffоrts tо сut соsts аnd іnсrеаsе еffісіеnсу, аs wеll аs а slіght іnсrеаsе іn dеmаnd fоr аіr trаvеl. Lufthаnsа hаs bееn wоrkіng tо strеаmlіnе іts ореrаtіоns аnd rеduсе оvеrhеаd ехреnsеs, іnсludіng thrоugh thе іntrоduсtіоn оf nеw, mоrе fuеl-еffісіеnt аіrсrаft аnd thе іmрlеmеntаtіоn оf dіgіtаl tесhnоlоgіеs tо іmрrоvе сustоmеr sеrvісе аnd ореrаtіоnаl еffісіеnсу.

Lufthаnsа's СЕΟ, Саrstеn Sроhr, hаіlеd thе rеsults аs а "sіgnіfісаnt turnаrоund" fоr thе соmраnу, whісh hаs fасеd сhаllеngеs іn rесеnt уеаrs іnсludіng іntеnsе соmреtіtіоn frоm lоw-соst саrrіеrs аnd thе іmрасt оf thе СОVІD-19 раndеmіс. "Wе hаvе bееn wоrkіng hаrd tо рut Lufthаnsа bасk оn а раth tо suссеss, аnd thеsе rеsults shоw thаt оur еffоrts аrе рауіng оff," Sроhr sаіd іn а stаtеmеnt.

Тhе аіrlіnе аlsо sаw а sіgnіfісаnt іnсrеаsе іn rеvеnuе, uр 13% tо €19.6 bіllіоn ($22.2 bіllіоn) fоr thе fіrst hаlf оf thе уеаr. Тhіs wаs drіvеn bу hіghеr раssеngеr numbеrs аnd іnсrеаsеd dеmаnd fоr рrеmіum саbіns. Lufthаnsа's раssеngеr соunt rоsе bу 6% tо 60.1 mіllіоn, wіth раrtісulаrlу strоng grоwth іn іts Еurореаn аnd Νоrth Аmеrісаn rоutеs.

Lооkіng аhеаd, Lufthаnsа іs саutіоuslу орtіmіstіс аbоut іts рrоsресts fоr thе rеst оf thе уеаr. Тhе соmраnу асknоwlеdgеs thаt thе glоbаl аvіаtіоn іndustrу соntіnuеs tо fасе unсеrtаіntіеs, іnсludіng thе оngоіng іmрасt оf СΟVІD-19 аnd gеороlіtісаl tеnsіоns. Ηоwеvеr, іt bеlіеvеs thаt іts strоng соst dіsсірlіnе аnd іnvеstmеnts іn dіgіtаl trаnsfоrmаtіоn wіll hеlр іt tо соntіnuе tо реrfоrm wеll іn thе соmіng mоnths.

Lufthаnsа's rеsults аrе а wеlсоmе bооst fоr thе аіrlіnе іndustrу, whісh hаs fасеd sіgnіfісаnt сhаllеngеs іn rесеnt уеаrs

A stack of  sensitive security documents has been found abandoned on  a  street in London, sparking an investigation into how  they came to be there.

The  documents, which include details of government  surveillance strategies  and counter-terrorism operations,  were discovered   by  a member of the public in a plastic  bag outside a residential building in the capital.

According to sources, the documents appear to be genuine and are believed to have been created by a government agency or contractor. They contain information about  the monitoring of individuals and organizations suspected  of involvement in  terrorist activities, as well as  plans for upcoming security operations.

The discovery has raised concerns  about the handling of classified information  and the potential risks  posed by such documents falling into the wrong hands.

"This is a very serious breach of security," said a government spokesperson.  "We  take the  protection of our national security extremely seriously, and we will  do everything in   our power to determine  how  these documents  came  to be where they were   found."

The  police have  launched  an investigation into the incident and are working to   identify who may have left the documents behind. It is  not yet  clear whether the documents were lost  or   stolen, or  if they were intentionally leaked.

The incident comes at a time when the government is facing  criticism over its handling of  sensitive data, following a series  of high-profile data breaches and leaks in recent months. Opposition politicians   have called for greater transparency and accountability in  the way   that classified information  is handled.

In response to the latest incident, the government has promised to take action to prevent  similar breaches from occurring in  the  future.

"We recognize the gravity of this situation  and will  do everything  necessary  to  ensure that our  national  security is protected," said the Prime  Minister in a statement. "We will also review our procedures for  handling classified information to make sure that they are as robust as possible."

Sure! Here's  my attempt at writing a review of  Captain America: The Winter Soldier without a title:

I was blown  away by this movie. I'm not  usually a   huge  fan of superhero movies, but  this one  had me hooked from  start to   finish. Chris  Evans does an amazing job as Captain  America, bringing depth and  nuance to a character that could have easily been reduced to a two-dimensional action hero. The supporting cast is also fantastic, with standout performances from Scarlett Johansson and Samuel L. Jackson.

The plot is gripping and  thought-provoking, tackling  real-world  issues like government surveillance and corruption in  a way that feels  both timely and  authentic. The  action scenes  are intense  and well-choreographed, but they never overshadow the characters  or their relationships. In fact, the film's  emotional moments are some of its strongest, particularly the interactions between  Steve Rogers  and  Bucky Barnes.

One of the things that sets this movie apart from  other superhero films is its willingness  to challenge the audience's expectations. It's not afraid to ask tough questions or subvert our assumptions about  what it means to be a hero.  The result  is a movie that's not just  entertaining, but  also engaging and thought-provoking.

Overall,  I highly recommend Captain America:  The Winter Soldier to anyone looking for a  compelling  and exciting film that will  keep you  on  the edge of your seat.  Even if you're not a fan of  superhero movies,  this one  is worth checking out.

Barbara Harris (1930-2020) was American bishop who made history in 1989 by becoming the first woman to be consecrated as a bishop in Episcopal Church. She served as Bishop Suffragan of Massachusetts, and her groundbreaking ordination paved the way for future generations of women in religious leadership roles.

Early Life and Education

Harris was born on June 12, 1930, in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania. She grew up in family that valued education and public service, and she was raised with strong Christian beliefs. After graduating from high school, Harris attended Bryn Mawr College, where she earned degree in philosophy. She later went on to study at the Episcopal Theological Seminary in Cambridge, Massachusetts, where she received her divinity degree in 1959.

Ordination and Early Career

Harris was ordained as deacon in 1961 and began serving at St. Martin's Episcopal Church in Atlanta, Georgia. In 1979, she became the first woman to serve as a priest at St. Stephen's Episcopal Church in Boston. Throughout her early career, Harris was known for her powerful preaching and her commitment to social justice causes.

Consecration as Bishop

In 1989, Harris was elected as Bishop Suffragan of Massachusetts, making her the first woman to hold the position of bishop in the Episcopal Church. Her consecration took place on February 11, 1989, at Trinity Church in Boston, and it was attended by over 4,000 people. Harris's election and consecration were seen as major milestones in struggle for gender equality within church, and they marked significant shift towards greater inclusivity in religious leadership.

3/4 cup raw cashews
1 cup water
1 can coconut milk
1 can coconut cream
1 seed big vanilla pod, scrapings, or 1 1/2 tsp. vanilla paste
1/2 cup tahini paste black
1/2 cup agave syrup or just over
Freeze bowl of your once cream maker well in advance- as per machine instructions. Most explain that ice cream maker bowl needs to sit in the freezer for about 16 - 24 hours.... Hence long preparation time on this recipe.
Leave bowl in the freezer right up until you actually need to use it. First make the thick cashew milk by blending water and cashews until smooth then pouring through fine sieve and back into blender. Next add all other ingredients and blend till super smooth. Then assemble your ice cream maker, turn it on ... and slowly pour blended mixture into the ice cream maker. 
There will most likely be some mixture left over after you pour into ice cream machine. I usually only fill my machine 3/4 full as towards the end it expands. The left over unmade ice cream mixture I pour into a jar and keep in fridge .. It goes well added to smoothies or poured over other desserts and meals. Serve Ice cream as soon as possible garnished with toasted black sesame seeds. Ice cream is stored in freezer in freezer-safe container. And is taken out approx. 20 min.s before serving to allow it to soften enough to be able to spoon out. Also running the serving scoop under hot water will help.

Spider-Man 3 is  a  disappointing and  convoluted sequel that fails to live up to the standards set by its predecessors. The film suffers from a lack of focus and a bloated plot that tries to  cram in too  many villains and subplots, resulting in a messy and disjointed  viewing experience.

The movie picks up where Spider-Man  2 left off, with  Peter Parker  (Tobey Maguire) and Mary  Jane Watson (Kirsten Dunst)  finally together, but  their happiness is short-lived  as they face new   challenges. Peter's  old friend Harry  Osborn (James Franco) returns, seeking revenge  against  Spider-Man for killing his father, while a new villain, Venom (Topher Grace), emerges with a twisted agenda.

The addition of Venom, a fan-favorite character  from  the  comics,  is a major misstep. His  inclusion feels forced and underdeveloped, and his motivations  are  unclear. The film tries to make him a symbiote, bonding with Peter's rival Eddie Brock, but the relationship between  the two is weak and unconvincing. The character's iconic  status  in the comics is wasted in  a  poorly executed and forgettable performance by Grace.

The  film's pacing is   also a problem, with  too many subplots and characters that detract from the   main story. The romance between Peter and Mary Jane is  overshadowed  by the introduction of  Gwen Stacy  (Bryce  Dallas Howard), who serves little purpose  other than to create tension  between the  couple. The  film's climax is overstuffed with action scenes, but  they  feel disconnected and  lack  the emotional  weight of the previous films.

The performances are generally unremarkable, with Maguire and  Dunst struggling  to  bring depth to their characters. Franco's Harry  Osborn is the only standout, but  his  character's  arc is predictable and  lacks the complexity of his previous appearances. The special effects are also subpar,  with noticeable CGI that detracts from the film's overall quality.

Overall, Spider-Man 3 is a disappointing sequel

Anthroposols are soils which have been profoundly modified or constructed  by humans. They   typically  have one or more of their natural soil horizons modified, removed or replaced. The word anthroposol is derived from the Greek words anthropos, meaning man and genes meaning caused.  Anthroposols are typically  found on industrial, commercial, urban and resources extraction sites as well as throughout transportation, fuel and  power corridors. Classification of Anthroposols 
Soil  classification systems are used globally to systematically categorize soils  based on  diagnostic features or characteristics. Human-formed soils have  historically   been  excluded from soil  classification    systems due in part to the fact that  the criteria  used for  naturally-formed soils are often not applicable. However, many countries   are recognizing their  pervasiveness and the need for some type of common language and taxonomy. As of 2016, fifteen countries  had added (or proposed adding) anthropogenic soils    to their soil classification  systems including Brazil, Germany, Canada, Poland and  China.

Sure! Here's  a recipe for pizza dough using  Molino Caputo Tipo 00 flour:

Ingredients:

* 1 cup warm water
* 2 teaspoons active dry yeast
* 3 tablespoons olive oil
*   1 teaspoon salt
* 4 cups Molino Caputo Tipo  00 flour

Instructions:

1. In a large mixing bowl, combine the warm  water and  yeast. Let  it  sit for 5-10  minutes until the  yeast becomes frothy and bubbly.
2. Add the olive  oil, salt, and 2 cups of the Tipo 00  flour to  the bowl. Mix until a shaggy   dough forms.
3.  Gradually add the   remaining 2 cups of  flour, one cup at a time, until the dough  becomes smooth  and elastic. You may not   need all of the remaining flour, so add it  gradually and be prepared to stop   adding flour when the dough feels right.
4. Turn the  dough out onto a floured surface  and knead for 5-10 minutes until the dough is  smooth and elastic. You  can also use  a stand  mixer  with a dough hook attachment to knead   the dough.
5. Place the dough in a lightly oiled bowl, cover it with  plastic wrap or a damp  towel, and let  it rise in a warm place for 1-2  hours until it  has doubled in size.
6. Preheat  your oven to its highest temperature (usually around 550°F - 600°F). If you have  a pizza  stone, place it  in the oven to preheat as well.
7. Punch  down the dough and divide it into 2-4  equal pieces, depending on how large you  want your  pizzas to be.
8. Roll out  each piece of dough into a thin circle, about 1/4 inch thick.
9. Place the  dough on a piece of  parchment paper  or a lightly floured pizza peel,

This is the raunchiest of the Scary Movies. If you thought the original was "too much", well, this one Will truly give you more reasons to avoid it for it's vulgar content and crude Sex jokes. That's for you, easily offended people.

I had a great time with it when it first came out in theaters. I really liked the crude sex humor and Vulgar spoofs. So for those who are into sex jokes and scatological humor, you will be amused.

The opening sequence is truly hilarious and in my opinion, the best part of the movie. It was about time that the "Scary Movie" franchise had an Exorcist spoof! It was great. James Woods in the role Of Father Merrin delivers a hysterical performance relying most of his scenes on scatological humor and Profanity. For example, when Reagan says "f*** me" and he immediately jumps into her and simulates to be Penetrating her. Another example is the line where Reagan's mother says "she won't even let me Touch her!" and the priest responds "yeah sometimes that happens, You have to give candy to the children" (or something like that); obviously referring to recent Pederastry cases. On the other side, the movie also has "Classical" but always effective teen humor . For example, when entering the room for the first time, he gets scared and runs away when Reagan turns her head on 180º; and the always effective vomiting cycle.

After the Exorcist spoof, the movie centers On the Poltergeist and Entity Spoofs. That's when the movie Takes a new Direction and does not changes From it. Expect spoofs from popular t.v. commercials, songs (Vitamin C), and even racist and homophobic jokes. The sex jokes worth a watch specially on the infamous clown scene, and the sex ghost.

"Scary Movie 2" does not "cross" the line on the vulgar factor. It's just a good rated R movie with spoofs for all tastes; of course, if you are not easily offended. But be sure to watch it on DVD; do not watch the t.v. version Because it's very cut (specially on dialogs).

this is in my opinion the best of the "Scary movies".

Salt and pepper the salmon fillets. Butter and salt and pepper the sliced lemon. In a small bowl, mix together the goat cheese, garlic, dill, panko crumbs, and parmesan cheese. In this paper, we present a novel method to language-based photo editing employing recurrent attentive models. Our proposed approach allows developers to edit photos by merely giving natural language explanations of the desired changes, without needing any manual manipulation of graphic components or deep knowledge of image extraction methods.

We introduce a new design that utilizes the strengths of recurrent neural networks (RNNs) and attention mechanisms to efficiently understand and execute artificial language instructions. The RNNs are designed to model the sequence of edits defined in the output text, while the attention mechanism enables the model to selectively focus on certain components of the picture and apply the necessary changes.

Through numerous studies, we demonstrate the validity of our technique in performing multiple editing functions such as object removal, facial expression manipulation, and texture correction. We often show that our model can generalize well to unseen data and outperform existing state-of-the-art techniques.

Our work has important consequences for enabling non-technical users to produce smart images with considerable effort. Specifically, it opens up new possibilities for multimodal communication between animals and machines, where artificial language can serve as a powerful instrument for identifying visual preferences and actions.

In summary, Language-Based Picture Editing with Recurrent Attentive Models demonstrates a significant development in the field of computer vision and human-computer communication. It provides a simple yet effective way for individuals to express their creative thoughts visually, without requiring proficiency in complex visual user interfaces or programming languages.

DANYL ELAINA AMICK (born December 12, 1970) is an American actress and director. She is best known for her starring role in the cult television series Twin Peaks, the prequel Twin Peaks: Fire Walk with Me, and the revival series, Twin Peaks: The Return. She has also had a recurring role in the series Central Park West in 1995–96, Freddie in 2005–06, and Witches of East End in 2013–14. On film, she has starred in The Sleepwalkers in 1992 and Dream Lover in 1993. Her most recent role is as Alice Smith in The CW series Riverdale. EARLY LIFE AND EDUCATION Danyl Elaina Amick was born in Sparks, a suburb of Reno, the daughter of Judy Ross (née Ross), a medical office manager, and Bill Amick, a musician. Amick's parents are of partial German ancestry; her unusual first name, which means "girl", was chosen by her parents because they thought it sounded good. Amick is also of Norwegian, Swedish, English, and Irish descent. As a young girl, her parents encouraged her to follow her creative instincts. She studied piano, bass, violin, and guitar, and took lessons in tap, jazz, and modern dance. In 1987, at the age of 16, she moved to Los Angeles to pursue a career in acting.

I just watched "Knocked Up" for the last hour, and I'm just struggling to process my feelings about it. On one hand, I found the film to be funny and relatable, with a cast of lovable actors that made me laugh out loud more times than I can count. The chemistry between Seth Rogen and Amy Heigl was incredible, and their performances were top-notch.

The film's depiction of the ups and downs of unexpected pregnancy and the difficulties of growing up was spot on, and I appreciated the way it tackled serious matters with a playful touch. The supporting cast, featuring Michael Jones and Kevin Newman, added an additional layer of comedy and depth to the plot.

On the other hand, I have to say that some of the jokes and situations were a bit ridiculous and over-the-top. There were a few scenes where I found myself asking, "Did they actually just be there?" But, I believe that's part of the charm of a Judd Apatow film.

Overall, I can certainly recommend "Knocked Up" to lovers of romantic films and Sam Rogen's brand of comedy. It's a funny, charming film that will leave you laughing and feeling great. Just be prepared for some raunchy comedy and a few awkward moments along the way.

In this paper, we introduce a new method for dimension reduction of semidefinite programs based on Jordan algebras. Semidefinite programs are used extensively in optimization problems, mainly in machine learning and control theory, but are very expensive to solve when the number of variables is large. We use the concept of Jordan algebras, a generalization of matrices studied in mathematics and physics. We show how a more compact and accurate representation of the constraint can lead to faster solving and better numerical stability. We show the effectiveness of our method by experiments on several benchmark problems and by comparing it with existing methods for dimension reduction of SDPs. Our experiments show that our method is either more efficient or at least as fast as other methods, with the added advantages of simplicity of implementation and greater clarity in the resulting results. Our main contribution is a new dimension reduction method for SDPs based on Jordan algebras, which may be of independent interest in algebra and geometry. In addition, we show the potential of algebraic methods in optimization and machine learning, pointing to new areas of research.

Spatchcock Chicken & Turkey

Ingredients:

* 1 whole chicken (3-4 lbs), cut into 8 pieces (2 legs, 2 thighs, 2 wings, and 2 breasts)
* 1 whole turkey (10-12 lbs), cut into 8 pieces (2 legs, 2 thighs, 2 wings, and 2 breasts)
* 2 tbsp olive oil
* 1 tbsp lemon juice
* 1 tbsp honey
* 1 tsp garlic powder
* 1 tsp paprika
* Salt and pepper to taste
* Fresh herbs such as rosemary or thyme (optional)

Instructions:

1. Preheat the oven to 400°F (200°C).
2. In large bowl, whisk together olive oil, lemon juice, honey, garlic powder, paprika, salt, and pepper.
3. Add chicken and turkey pieces to marinade and toss to coat evenly. Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes or overnight.
4. Remove the meat from marinade and place it on baking sheet lined with parchment paper. Reserve the remaining marinade.
5. Place chicken and turkey in a single layer on the baking sheet, making sure the pieces are not overlapping.
6. Bake the chicken and turkey for 30-40 minutes, or until they are cooked through and internal temperature reaches 165°F (74°C).
7. While the chicken and turkey are baking, pour reserved marinade into a small saucepan and bring it to boil over high heat. Reduce heat to medium-low and simmer for 5-7 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the glaze thickens slightly.

1 large egg
1/2 cup milk
1/4 cup Vegetable oil
1 teaspoon fresh lemon zest (see note below)
2 Teaspoons baking powder
1/2 cup sugar
1 1/2 cups all-purpose flour
1/2 teaspoon salt
1 Cup fresh raspberry
Streusel Topping
3/4 cup all-purpose flour, sifted
1/4 cup brown sugar
1/3 cup oatmeal, dry
1 teaspoon cinnamon
1/2 cup butter, cold
Preheat oven to 400 degrees. MUFFINS: Beat together the egg, milk, Lemon zest and oil in a medium bowl. Mix together in a separate bowl the baking powder, white sugar, 1.5 Cups sifted flour and 1/2 T. salt. Stir into the wet ingredients just until the dry Ingredients are moist, about 8 rotations. Add raspberries; stir about 2-3 more times. Fill muffin cups about 2/3 full. STREUSEL TOPPING: Stir together the remaining flour, brown sugar, cinnamon and oatmeal. Cut in with a pastry blender the butter until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs. Sprinkle Topping over each muffin. Bake 20-25 minutes. Remove from pan immediately. Makes about 12. NOTE: The key to Making a truly tender muffin is in NOT overmixing your batter. Stir It as Little as possible. It's OK if the batter is somewhat unevenly mixed; it "corrects" itself in baking. Sifting the dry ingredients lends to the light texture, so don't neglect this step, either. Use either a traditional sifter, or a mesh strainer to sift your ingredients. Try a Microplane grater for your lemon zest, or a messermeister zester/channel tool. I find these to be the easiest way to get fresh zest from citrus. Use only the colorful exterior of the lemon-- the white peel part adds only bitterness.

The story follows a young woman named Rachel who has always felt like something is off about her life. She's successful, attractive, and well-liked, but she can't shake the feeling that there's something inside of her, controlling her every move.

As the novel progresses, Rachel begins to experience strange physical sensations - tingling in her fingers and toes, episodes of dizziness, and vivid dreams that feel all too real. She tries to ignore these symptoms, attributing them to stress or a lack of sleep, but they only intensify as time goes on.

One night, Rachel wakes up to find herself standing over her bed, staring at her own body with an eerie sense of detachment. Suddenly, she feels a presence inside of her, a parasitic creature that has been lurking beneath the surface of her consciousness. It speaks to her in a voice that is both familiar and alien, telling her that it has been growing inside of her for years, feeding off of her energy and manipulating her thoughts and actions.

Rachel is horrified and disgusted by this revelation, but the creature insists that it is not malevolent. It claims that it is simply trying to survive, and that it needs Rachel's host body to do so. The creature promises to reward Rachel if she agrees to let it stay, offering her enhanced abilities and knowledge beyond her wildest dreams.

Despite her initial resistance, Rachel eventually succumbs to the creature's demands. She begins to embrace her newfound powers and the freedom that comes with no longer being entirely human. But as she delves deeper into the world of supernatural entities, Rachel realizes that the creature is not what it seems. It has an agenda of its own, one that threatens to destroy everything she holds dear.

No one would have believed in the last years of the nineteenth century that this world was being watched keenly and closely by intelligences greater than man's and yet as mortal as his own; that as men busied themselves about their various concerns they were scrutinized and studied, perhaps almost as narrowly as a man with a microscope might scrutinize the transient creatures that swarm and multiply in a drop of water. With infinite complacency men went to and fro over this globe about their little affairs, serene in their assurance of their empire over matter. It is possible that the infusoria under the microscope do the same. No one gave a thought to the older worlds of space as sources of human danger, or thought of them only to dismiss the idea of life upon them as impossible or improbable. It is curious to recall some of the mental habits of those departed days. At most terrestrial men fancied there might be other men upon Mars, perhaps inferior to themselves and ready to welcome a missionary enterprise. Yet across the gulf of space, minds that are to our minds as ours are to those of the beasts that perish, intellects vast and cool and unsympathetic; beings immeasurably superior to us, regarded this earth with envious eyes, and slowly and surely drew their plans against us.

The sun-drenched streets of Los Angeles were always bustling with activity, but for one young woman named Samantha, the city held little appeal. She had just arrived in town after leaving her small hometown in search of something more, but so far, LA seemed to offer nothing but traffic and smog.

As she wandered aimlessly through the crowded sidewalks, Samantha couldn't help but feel a sense of restlessness. She had always been driven by a desire to escape the monotony of her life back home, where everyone knew her name and her business, and yet, here she was, feeling lost and alone in a sea of strangers.

It wasn't until she stumbled upon a local art collective that she began to feel a spark of excitement. The group of artists, musicians, and writers who frequented the space were diverse, passionate, and welcoming, and for the first time since her arrival, Samantha felt like she belonged.

Among them was a charismatic painter named Jesse, who was immediately drawn to Samantha's unique style and spirit. As they spent more time together, their connection grew stronger, and soon they found themselves falling deeply in love.

But as the summer wore on and their relationship intensified, old wounds and secrets began to surface. Samantha's troubled past, shrouded in mystery and heartbreak, threatened to tear them apart, while Jesse's own demons, rooted in a tragic loss, made him struggle to fully open up to her.

Despite the challenges they faced, their bond only strengthened, and they began to inspire each other creatively in ways they never thought possible. But as they came closer to realizing their dreams, the outside world began to closing in, threatening to shatter the fragile utopia they had built.

In this paper, we have studied $F(R,T)$ gravity as an arbitrary function of
curvature and torsion scalars in Friedmann--Lema\^{\i}tre--Robertson--Walker
(FLRW) background. Then, we have considered interacting model between $F(R,T)$
gravity and modified Chaplygin gas. The novelty of this model is that the
Universe includes both cases curvature and torsion, and one dominated by a
Chaplygin gas. In order to calculate cosmological solutions, we obtained
Friedmann equations and also equation of state (EoS) parameter of dark energy. 

By employing interacting model we considered the total energy density and the
total pressure of Universe as the combination of components of dark energy and
Chaplygin gas. 

Subsequently, we reconstructed the model by an origin of a
scalar field entitled quintessence model with a field potential. 

The field
potential has been calculated in terms of free parameters of $F(R,T)$ gravity
and modified Chaplygin gas. 

In what follows, we used a parametrization, and the
cosmological parameters have been written in terms of redshift $z$. 

Next, we
plotted cosmological parameters with respect to three variable of cosmic time,
redshift $z$ and $e$-folding number $N=ln(a)$, and the figures showed us an
accelerated expansion of Universe. Also, we have described the scenario in
three status early time, late time and future time by $e$-folding number. Finally, the stability of scenario has been investigated by a useful function
named sound speed, and the graph of sound speed versus $e$-folding number has
been showed us that there is the stability in late time.
