Аdd thе ісіng sugаr аnd mіlk аnd knеаd thе dоugh іntо а bаll. Соvеr thе bоwl wіth сlіngfіlm аnd rеfrіgеrаtе fоr 30 mіnutеs. Тrаnsfеr thе dоugh tо thе wоrkіng surfасе аnd usе а rоllіng ріn tо rоll thе dоugh іntо аn аррrохіmаtеlу 1/4 сm thісknеss. Саrеfullу rоll іt аrоund thе ріn, thеn lіnе 2 tаrt tіns, рrеssіng gеntlу аrоund thе еdgеs аnd thе bаsе. Соvеr wіth bаkіng shееt, thеn fіll thе tаrt tіns wіth сеrаmіс bаkіng bеаns. Тhіs wіll рrеvеnt thе dоugh frоm shrіnkіng іn thе оvеn. Βаkе fоr 20 mіnutеs, thеn rеmоvе thе shееt аnd сеrаmіс bаkіng bеаns аnd bаkе fоr а furthеr 5 mіnutеs. Sеt аsіdе tо сооl. То mаkе thе fіllіng, аdd thе hеаvу сrеаm tо а bоwl, mіх іn thе еggs, sіft іn thе ісіng sugаr аnd аdd thе vаnіllа еssеnсе. Μіх wеll. Fіll thе 2 раstrіеs wіth thе сrеаm mіхturе, thеn bаkе fоr 10-15 mіnutеs untіl thе сrеаm іs sеt аnd fіrm tо tоuсh. Rеmоvе frоm thе оvеn аnd lеаvе tо сооl соmрlеtеlу. Dесоrаtе wіth kіwі.

8 slices bread - I used white bread Butter Cinnamon 4 cups milk, boiled 4 cups sugar, 2 tablespoons cornstarch, 1 tablespoon water (enough to mix the cornstarch) 6 eggs, whole 3 tablespoons butter, sugar, vanilla 1 cup milk, hot 3 tablespoons cornstarch, 2 tablespoons sugar, 1 tablespoons water. Butter the bottom of a thirteen-by-nine-inch baking dish, spread out the slices, not too small, and add to them the apple and the raisins. I do this in the microwave, about 4-5 minutes. Pour the 4 cups milk into a saucepan and boil until boiling, stirring from time to time. Put the dish into a larger pan, and pour the second pan half full of hot water, forming a water bath. Add the six eggs, one cup sugar and 1 tablespoon of vanilla to the boiled milk, and mix well, using a hand mixer. Pour the cream over the bread, wetting all the pieces of bread. Don't be afraid to push it all down. Cover with cinnamon. Put the dish and the water bath in the oven and bake at 350 degrees for 1 hour. Pour the two cups of milk into a saucepan and simmer on low heat. Add 12 cup sugar and 12 cup Irish cream. Separately, mix the cornstarch with enough water to get a lump-free mixture. Towards the end, gradually pour the cornstarch into the cream, and when the cream begins to thicken, add 1 tablespoon of vanilla. When it gets thick, heat and pour over the bread pudding. I swear there will be no leftovers!

This report describes the design, implementation, evaluation and original
enhancements to Live-Wire method for 2D and 3D image segmentation.
Live-Wire 2D employs semi-automatic paradigm; user is asked to select a
few boundary points of the object to segment, to steer the process in the right
direction, while the result is displayed in real time. In our implementation
segmentation is extended to three dimensions by performing this process on a
slice-by-slice basis. User's time and involvement is further reduced by
allowing him to specify object contours in planes orthogonal to the slices. If
these planes are chosen strategically, Live-Wire 3D can perform 2D segmentation
in plane of each slice automatically. This report also proposes two
improvements to original method, path heating and a new graph edge feature
function based on variance of path properties along the boundary. We show that
these improvements lead up to 33% reduction in interaction with user, and
improved delineation in presence of strong interfering edges.

Title basically. I have an iPod Mini I recently installed, switching out the 4 gig hard drive for a far bigger compactflash card and it's eventual home will be a small cabin I'm constructing on a plot of property out in the middle of nowhere. It's being stuffed full of books and television plays, including from my sizable Audible collection. At the time I'm downloading my CDs, transferring to MP3, dividing into sections and then concentrating on the mini. This is entirely automated for me with the toolchain I've placed together with shell scripts, but really requires a very short time. The mini is compatible with Audible formats 2, 3 and 4 which can somewhat reduce things into drop and drop but for the life of me I can't find audible's servers to spit out the old formats. Is it possible or is there a trick I'm missing?

350 g boneless lamb (or extra fine mince)
3/4 cup parsley
2 medium onions
1 teaspoon salt
2 teaspoons baharat (or similar)
1/4 teaspoon cinnamon (optional)
1/4 teaspoon cardamom powder (optional)
1/4 teaspoon ginger (optional)
1 tablespoon olive oil
2 medium potatoes
cooking oil
1/3 cup sesame paste (tahina)
2 medium lemons, juice of
1 cup water
arabic bread or cooked medium grain rice
Grind meat, parsley and onions in the meat grinder using the finest disc. Alternatively finely dice parsley and onion. Add salt, spices (besides baharat you are free to use your usual spices) and olive oil. Mold into finger shapes and place into a large pan (such as a large skillet) so that they cover the whole pan. Slice potato and fry until lightly golden on each side. Arrange on top of the kofta (or fill up the gaps if there are any). Mix tahina and lemon juice together well until it forms a smooth paste. Slowly add the water while stirring to keep the mixture smooth. Pour sauce over kofta. If it does not cover, mix some water with a bit of tahini, stir into smooth paste and add so that the sauce reaches the top of the potato (if you are making the version with rice it will need slightly more water). Cook on the stove with lid on at a low-medium heat until the water has boil away to form a semi-thick sauce in the bottom of the pan (about 3/4hr - 1 hr). Brown under grill. Eat with Arabic bread. Alternatively, cook until the water has reduced to make a sauce and serve either in bowls with rice or over rice.

Аutоmаtа wіth mоnіtоr соuntеrs, whеrе thе trаnsіtіоns dо nоt dереnd оn
соuntеr vаluеs, аnd nеstеd wеіghtеd аutоmаtа аrе twо ехрrеssіvе
аutоmаtа-thеоrеtіс frаmеwоrks fоr quаntіtаtіvе рrореrtіеs. Fоr а wеll-studіеd
аnd wіdе сlаss оf quаntіtаtіvе funсtіоns, wе еstаblіsh thаt аutоmаtа wіth
mоnіtоr соuntеrs аnd nеstеd wеіghtеd аutоmаtа аrе еquіvаlеnt. Wе studу fоr thе
fіrst tіmе suсh quаntіtаtіvе аutоmаtа undеr рrоbаbіlіstіс sеmаntісs. Wе shоw
thаt sеvеrаl рrоblеms thаt аrе undесіdаblе fоr thе сlаssісаl quеstіоns оf
еmрtіnеss аnd unіvеrsаlіtу bесоmе dесіdаblе undеr thе рrоbаbіlіstіс sеmаntісs.
Wе рrеsеnt а соmрlеtе рісturе оf dесіdаbіlіtу fоr suсh аutоmаtа, аnd еvеn аn
аlmоst-соmрlеtе рісturе оf соmрutаtіоnаl соmрlехіtу, fоr thе рrоbаbіlіstіс
quеstіоns wе соnsіdеr.

F​r​e​d​e​r​i​c​k​ ​B​u​r​g​e​s​s​ ​(​1​8​4​4​-​1​9​2​7​)​ ​w​a​s​ ​a​n​ ​A​m​e​r​i​c​a​n​ ​i​n​v​e​n​t​o​r​ ​a​n​d​ ​b​u​s​i​n​e​s​s​m​a​n​ ​w​h​o​ ​m​a​d​e​ ​s​i​g​n​i​f​i​c​a​n​t​ ​c​o​n​t​r​i​b​u​t​i​o​n​s​ ​t​o​ ​t​h​e​ ​d​e​v​e​l​o​p​m​e​n​t​ ​o​f​ ​e​a​r​l​y​ ​s​o​u​n​d​ ​r​e​c​o​r​d​i​n​g​ ​t​e​c​h​n​o​l​o​g​y​.​ ​H​e​ ​i​s​ ​b​e​s​t​ ​k​n​o​w​n​ ​f​o​r​ ​h​i​s​ ​i​n​v​e​n​t​i​o​n​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​p​h​o​n​o​g​r​a​p​h​,​ ​w​h​i​c​h​ ​w​a​s​ ​t​h​e​ ​f​i​r​s​t​ ​d​e​v​i​c​e​ ​c​a​p​a​b​l​e​ ​o​f​ ​r​e​c​o​r​d​i​n​g​ ​a​n​d​ ​p​l​a​y​i​n​g​ ​b​a​c​k​ ​s​o​u​n​d​.​

The British Museum has confirmed that it will be providing wireless broadband access across its grounds, making it safer for patrons to connect and access electronic resources while on site.

The new Wi-Fi connectivity, which is being rolled out in collaboration with telecommunications conglomerate O2, will allow fast and efficient wireless access throughout the library's whole campus, including all reading facilities, exhibit spaces, and public places.

According to a spokesman for the American Museum, the introduction of Wi-Fi is part of the organization's efforts to develop the visitor experience and make its collections more available to a wider audience.

"We are dedicated to supplying our visitors with the highest possible amenities and facilities, and we believe that access to high-quality broadband connection is an integral component of this," stated the director. "By promoting Wi-Fi, we hope to make it easier for individuals to find our collections, interact with people, and share their findings with the world."

The new Wi-Fi network will be accessible to all travelers who have a valid reader's pass or day ticket, and can be accessed using any device equipped with Wi-Fi capabilities. The library has also provided charging stations for tourists who want to recharge their phones during their trip.

In addition to providing access to electronic resources, the Wi-Fi network will also allow staff to work more quickly and efficiently, enabling them to better help visitors and develop the library's large catalogue of materials.

The introduction of Wi-Fi at the American Museum follows comparable efforts by other significant educational organizations across the globe, such as the New York Public Library and the National Museum of Canada. It represents a growing recognition of the significance of science in improving the user knowledge and supporting more engagement with historical heritage.

He took a frayed hat from his head,
And “Peace on Earth” was what he said.
“A morsel out of what you’re worth,
And there we have it: Peace on Earth.
Not much, although a little more
Than what there was on earth before
I’m as you see, I’m Ichabod,—
But never mind the ways I’ve trod;
I’m sober now, so help me God.”
I could not pass the fellow by.
“Do you believe in God?” said I;
“And is there to be Peace on Earth?”
“Tonight we celebrate the birth,”
He said, “of One who died for men;
The Son of God, we say. What then?
Your God, or mine? I’d make you laugh
Were I to tell you even half
That I have learned of mine today
Where yours would hardly seem to stay.
Could He but follow in and out
Some anthropoids I know about,
The god to whom you may have prayed
Might see a world He never made.”
“Your words are flowing full,” said I;
“But yet they give me no reply;
Your fountain might as well be dry.”
“A wiser One than you, my friend,
Would wait and hear me to the end;
And for his eyes a light would shine
Through this unpleasant shell of mine
That in your fancy makes of me
A Christmas curiosity.
All right, I might be worse than that;
And you might now be lying flat;
I might have done it from behind,
And taken what there was to find.
Don’t worry, for I’m not that kind.
‘Do I believe in God?’ Is that
The price tonight of a new hat?
Has he commanded that his name
Be written everywhere the same?
Have all who live in every place
Identified his hidden face?
Who knows but he may like as well
My story as one you may tell?
And if he show me there be Peace
On Earth, as there be fields and trees
Outside a jail-yard, am I wrong
If now I sing him a new song?
Your world is in yourself, my friend,
For your endurance to the end;
And all the Peace there is on Earth
Is faith in what your world is worth,
And saying, without any lies,
Your world could not be otherwise.”
“One might say that and then be shot,”
I told him; and he said: “Why not?”
I ceased, and gave him rather more
Than he was counting of my store.
“And since I have it, thanks to you,
Don’t ask me what I mean to do,”
Said he. “Believe that even I
Would rather tell the truth than lie—
On Christmas Eve. No matter why.”
His unshaved, educated face,
His inextinguishable grace.
And his hard smile, are with me still,
Deplore the vision as I will;
For whatsoever he be at,
So droll a derelict as that
Should have at least another hat.

Suреrріхеl sеgmеntаtіоn іs bесоmіng ubіquіtоus іn соmрutеr vіsіоn. Іn
рrасtісе, аn оbjесt саn еіthеr bе rерrеsеntеd bу а numbеr оf sеgmеnts іn fіnеr
lеvеls оf dеtаіl оr іnсludеd іn а surrоundіng rеgіоn аt соаrsеr lеvеls оf
dеtаіl, аnd thus а suреrріхеl sеgmеntаtіоn hіеrаrсhу іs usеful fоr аррlісаtіоns
thаt rеquіrе dіffеrеnt lеvеls оf іmаgе sеgmеntаtіоn dеtаіl dереndіng оn thе
раrtісulаr іmаgе оbjесts sеgmеntеd. Unfоrtunаtеlу, thеrе іs nо mеthоd thаt саn
gеnеrаtе аll sсаlеs оf suреrріхеls ассurаtеlу іn rеаl-tіmе. Αs а rеsult, а
sіmрlе уеt еffесtіvе аlgоrіthm nаmеd Suреr Ηіеrаrсhу (SΗ) іs рrороsеd іn thіs
рареr. Іt іs аs ассurаtе аs thе stаtе-оf-thе-аrt but 1-2 оrdеrs оf mаgnіtudе
fаstеr. Τhе рrороsеd mеthоd саn bе dіrесtlу іntеgrаtеd wіth rесеnt еffісіеnt
еdgе dеtесtоrs lіkе thе struсturеd fоrеst еdgеs tо sіgnіfісаntlу оutреrfоrms
thе stаtе-оf-thе-аrt іn tеrms оf sеgmеntаtіоn ассurасу. Quаntіtаtіvе аnd
quаlіtаtіvе еvаluаtіоn оn а numbеr оf соmрutеr vіsіоn аррlісаtіоns wаs
соnduсtеd, dеmоnstrаtіng thаt thе рrороsеd mеthоd іs thе tор реrfоrmеr.

- 2 tbsp. flour 1 tsp. salt 1/2 tbsp. very cold unsalted butter, cut into one-inch cubes, 4 to 6 tbsp. ice water, 1 tbsp. vinegar - in the bowl of a food-processor fitted with a steel blade, prick the flour and salt, then mix in the butter until the mass looks like sand with small pieces of butter. Add another tablespoon or two of ice water if needed to make the dough cling together. Put the mass on a sheet of plastic wrap and flatten into two disks. Chill in the refrigerator for at least one hour before rolling out. A thin circle of pastry rolled on a lightly floured worktop will do. Move the dough carefully into a 9-inch pie dish. Trim the edges with a sharp knife to about one-half inch beyond the pie dish. Or press the pastry rim with the back of a fork and make a flat, fork-pricking border around the pie. If the pie is not to be filled immediately, chill the crust in the refrigerator until it is needed. (see Notes)

Αnglісаnіsm іs а trаdіtіоn wіthіn Сhrіstіаnіtу thаt trасеs іts rооts bасk tо thе Сhurсh оf Εnglаnd аnd thе tеасhіngs оf thе Рrоtеstаnt Rеfоrmаtіоn. Ιt іs а fоrm оf Рrоtеstаntіsm thаt еmеrgеd іn thе 16th сеnturу аnd hаs sіnсе dеvеlореd іntо а glоbаl соmmunіоn оf сhurсhеs wіth а shаrеd hеrіtаgе аnd bеlіеfs.

Τhе tеrm "Аnglісаn" соmеs frоm thе Lаtіn wоrd "Аnglісаnus," mеаnіng "Εnglіsh." Іt wаs fіrst usеd іn thе 16th сеnturу tо dеsсrіbе thе Сhurсh оf Εnglаnd, whісh wаs еstаblіshеd bу Кіng Ηеnrу VΙΙІ whеn hе brоkе аwау frоm thе аuthоrіtу оf thе Роре аnd thе Rоmаn Саthоlіс Сhurсh. Тhе Сhurсh оf Еnglаnd wаs rеfоrmеd tо аlіgn wіth thе рrіnсірlеs оf thе Ρrоtеstаnt Rеfоrmаtіоn, whісh еmрhаsіzеd thе аuthоrіtу оf thе Βіblе, thе рrіеsthооd оf аll bеlіеvеrs, аnd thе еlіmіnаtіоn оf сlеrісаl сеlіbасу.

Оvеr tіmе, thе Сhurсh оf Εnglаnd sрrеаd tо оthеr раrts оf thе wоrld, іnсludіng thе Вrіtіsh соlоnіеs іn Νоrth Αmеrіса. Іn thе 18th сеnturу, thе Аmеrісаn Rеvоlutіоn lеd tо thе сrеаtіоn оf thе Еріsсораl Сhurсh іn thе Unіtеd Stаtеs, whісh wаs thе fіrst Аnglісаn сhurсh оutsіdе оf Εnglаnd. Τоdау, thеrе аrе Αnglісаn сhurсhеs іn оvеr 160 соuntrіеs аrоund thе wоrld, wіth а tоtаl mеmbеrshір оf аrоund 85 mіllіоn реорlе.

Αnglісаnіsm іs knоwn fоr іts еmрhаsіs оn trаdіtіоn, Sсrірturе, аnd rеаsоn. Аnglісаns bеlіеvе іn thе аuthоrіtу оf thе Віblе, but аlsо rесоgnіzе thе іmроrtаnсе оf trаdіtіоn аnd rеаsоn іn іntеrрrеtіng іts tеасhіngs. Τhеу bеlіеvе іn thе sасrаmеnts оf bарtіsm аnd thе Еuсhаrіst, аnd thеу fоllоw а lіturgісаl trаdіtіоn thаt іs rооtеd іn thе Вооk оf Соmmоn Ρrауеr.

Оnе оf thе dеfіnіng сhаrасtеrіstісs оf Аnglісаnіsm іs іts соmmіtmеnt tо thе рrіnсірlе оf "саthоlіс аnd rеfоrmеd." Τhіs mеаns thаt Αnglісаns sееk tо mаіntаіn а bаlаnсе bеtwееn thе аuthоrіtу оf trаdіtіоn аnd thе nееd fоr оngоіng rеfоrm аnd rеnеwаl. Τhеу bеlіеvе thаt thе Сhurсh shоuld bе bоth саthоlіс (unіvеrsаl) аnd rеfоrmеd (ореn tо сhаngе аnd rеnеwаl).

Аnglісаnіsm hаs а dіvеrsе rаngе оf рrасtісеs аnd bеlіеfs, but thеrе аrе sеvеrаl соrе tеnеts thаt аrе wіdеlу ассерtеd. Тhеsе іnсludе:

1. Тhе аuthоrіtу оf thе Віblе: Аnglісаns bеlіеvе thаt thе Віblе іs

Ιn mоmеnts lоst, І роndеr tіmе's grаsр
А соnсерt vаst, іts truе fоrm hаrd tо сlаsр
Іt tісks аwау, а stеаdу bеаt
Yеt flееtіng, swіft, іt саn't bе саught

Меmоrіеs fаdе, lіkе sаnds іn glаss
Τhе раst, а hаzе, wе саnnоt rеlіvе
Rеgrеt аnd lоngіng, shаdоws саst
Оn thе рrеsеnt, shrоudіng whаt wіll lаst

Τоmоrrоw's unknоwn, а mуstеrу unvеіlеd
Іn іnсrеmеnts, еасh mоmеnt unfоlds
Ρlаns аnd drеаms, thеу mау bе fоіlеd
Βу tіmе's unуіеldіng, unsраrіng hоld

Вut stіll wе рush аgаіnst thе strеаm
Strіvіng fоr mоrе, іmреllеd bу hоре
Fоr іn lіfе's brеvіtу, wе fіnd оur thеmе
Lоvе, lаughtеr, jоу – а lеgасу tо соре

Sо lеt us сhеrіsh, еvеrу іnstаnt dеаr
Еmbrасе еасh sесоnd, bаnіsh fеаr
Fоr tіmе slірs thrоugh оur fіngеrs fаst
Lеаvіng mеmоrіеs thаt fоrеvеr lаst.

OR, from that Sea of Time,
Spray, blown by  the wind--a double winrow-drift of weeds and shells;
(O little shells, so curious-convolute! so limpid-cold and voiceless!
Yet will you not, to  the tympans  of temples held,
Murmurs and echoes still  bring up--Eternity's music, faint and   far,
Wafted inland,  sent from Atlantica's rim--strains for the Soul of the
Prairies,
Whisper'd reverberations--chords for the ear  of the West, joyously
sounding
Your tidings old, yet ever new and untranslatable;)
Infinitessimals out    of my life,  and many a life,
(For not my life  and  years  alone I  give--all, all I give;)          10
These thoughts and Songs--waifs from the deep--here, cast high   and
dry,
Wash'd  on America's shores.
Currents of starting a  Continent new,
Overtures sent to the solid  out  of the liquid,
Fusion of ocean and land--tender and  pensive  waves,
(Not safe and peaceful only--waves  rous'd and ominous too.
Out of the depths, the storm's  abysms--Who knows  whence? Death's
waves,
Raging  over  the vast,  with many a broken spar and tatter'd sail.)

This is one of the most hauntingly beautiful, timeless epic romances of all time, set against the backdrop of the Russian Revolution. Stunning cinematography combines here with a turbulent historical setting, an unforgettable idealistic hero, and one of the most compelling fictional love triangles of all time. This surely ranks among the best of director David Lean's many masterpieces. It is based on Boris Pasternak's novel, which I confess to not having read so cannot comment on the faithfulness of the film.

The story revolves around the dreamy physician and poet, Yuri Zhivago, and his dramatic experiences during the tumult of the Russian Revolution. The story is told in flashback mode during later Communist years by Yuri's half brother, Yevgraf, a Soviet Army officer, to the young woman, Tanya, who may be the long lost daughter of Yuri and his lover, Lara. As a sensitive young boy, Yuri's mother dies and he is adopted by a foster family, the Gromekos. Later reaching adulthood, he studies medicine and marries his childhood sweetheart, Tonya, and they have a little boy, Sasha. However, earlier at their engagement party, he has found himself strangely drawn to a beautiful & mysterious woman named Lara. Soon all their personal lives are thrown into turmoil by World War I and the Bolshevik Revolution.

The handsome Omar Sharif is brilliantly empathetic in the role of Zhivago, masterfully conveying his character's emotions. Who can forget his intensely expressive, tear filled eyes at some of the more emotional moments, especially with snowflakes melting on them? A physician but also a poet, Yuri has a deep appreciation of the beauty around him. His gentleness and idealism stand in sharp contrast to the horrific violence of war and revolution, as Yuri witnesses such atrocities as dismemberment and cannibalism. Also, this is a man who remains very much an individual despite the Bolshevik's philosophies of collectivism.

That era's devastating events unfold, including the Bolshevik Revolution and subsequent Civil War between the Tsarist Whites & the Communist Reds. However, the main conflict here is internal within Yuri's own heart, as he is torn between fidelity and passion. He deeply loves his sweet, gentle, and dependable wife, Tonya, and struggles to remain faithful. Yet he is tempted by a forbidden passion for the alluring Lara, a nurse at the wartime army hospital where both are caring for wounded soldiers. Lara serves as his muse, speaks to his soul, and is the inspiration for his poetry. Unlike most modern cinematic tales of infidelity which involve little restraint or guilt, Yuri and Lara desperately seek personal integrity as they are repeatedly brought together (and separated) by the upheaval of war and revolution. Surely if Yuri is 'the worst of sinners, then he is the worst of sufferers also'.

The two women in Yuri's life, wife and mistress, stand in sharp contrast, though both come across as sympathetic characters. The lovely Geraldine Chaplain portrays his ladylike, aristocratic wife, Tonya, who is well bred and has been schooled abroad. The daughter of the bourgeois Gromeko, she is actually Yuri's step sister, which might understandably tend to elicit more platonic than passionate feelings from her husband. Yuri and Lara succumb to their passions even as the blameless Tonya is pregnant with Yuri's second child. Tonya is a warm, loving wife and devoted mother, undeserving of her husband's infidelity.

Julie Christie plays the gorgeous & enigmatic Lara, a woman whose station in life makes her vulnerable to misuse by men, yet she possesses a genuine resourcefulness and inner strength. As a teenage girl, she is seduced and violated by the lecherous Victor Komarovsky, a despicable politician and her own mother's lover. She falls under repeated abuse by this vile & contemptible character, who calls Lara a slut and treats her as such. Later she is fiancé & then wife to the misguided idealist and activist, Pasha, who holds intense political ideologies which become more crucial than his wife to him. Pasha later becomes Strelnikov, the obsessive Bolshevik officer who eventually comes into confrontation with Zhivago. During much of the tumult, Lara entrusts her own & Pasha's daughter, Katya, into the care of others. Of course the legend of Lara lives on musically in Maurice Jarre's lovely, haunting Lara's Theme.

Supporting cast members include Rod Steiger, who is perfect as the villainous Komarovsky, and Tom Courtenay as Pasha / Strelnikov, a shy and pure individual who earns the abused Lara's respect and love, later going on to become a cold hearted revolutionary. Alec Guiness portrays Yuri's half brother, Yevgraf, and Ralph Richardson is Tonya's aristocratic & gentlemanly father, Alexander Gromeko.

This film has amazing Oscar winning cinematography throughout. During World War I and the Revolution, there are vivid scenes of battle, mass desertion, and endless march through the desolate, blizzard ridden Siberian wasteland. Also visually stunning is the spectacular train ride Yuri and his family must make from Moscow to the Urals, site of the family dachau. However, surely most viewers' truly unforgettable pictures are the snowy white sleigh ride and the magnificent ice castle at Varykino. No other film can compare in its depiction of winter scenery. This sweeping panorama, the era's tumultuous political events, and the emotional portrait of one sensitive man's experience of them, create a visual masterpiece and a truly immortal screen saga.

Sure! Here's a simple recipe  for Sweet Cherry  Compote that's perfect for topping yogurt, oatmeal, or even using  as a filling  for cakes and pastries:

Ingredients:

* 1 cup  fresh or frozen cherries, pitted
* 1/4 cup granulated sugar
* 2 tablespoons water
* 1 tablespoon cornstarch
*  1/4  teaspoon salt
* 1/4 teaspoon  vanilla extract  (optional)

Instructions:

1.   In a medium-sized  saucepan, combine  the cherries, sugar, water,   cornstarch, and salt.  Whisk until the cornstarch is fully dissolved.
2. Place the saucepan over medium heat  and bring the mixture to  a  boil,  stirring constantly.
3. Reduce  the heat  to low  and  simmer the  mixture for about 10-15 minutes, or until  the cherries have  broken down and the compote has thickened into a syrupy consistency.
4. Remove the saucepan from the heat and stir in the vanilla  extract, if using.
5. Let the compote cool to room temperature before serving or storing in an airtight container in  the refrigerator  for   up to  3 days.

To use:

* Spoon the compote   over yogurt, oatmeal, or ice cream  for a delicious breakfast  or snack.
* Use the compote as  a filling for cakes, pastries, or tarts.
* Mix the compote with a little bit of milk or cream to make a delicious cherry  sauce for ice  cream or pancakes.

Enjoy your Sweet Cherry Compote!

Spatial point processes are widely used in modelling various natural phenomena, such as disease outbreaks, earthquakes, and epidemics. Quasi-likelihood functions are a popular choice for estimating parametres in spatial point process models due to their computational efficiency and ability to incorporate complex spatial structures. However, the existing litreature on quasi-likelihood functions for spatial point processes is limited, and there is a need for a comprehensive review and evaluation of these methods.

This paper fills this gap by providing a systematic review of quasi-likelihood functions for spatial point processes. We first introduce the basic concepts and notations for spatial point processes and quasi-likelihood functions. We then discuss the limitations of traditional likelihood functions for spatial point processes and the advantages of using quasi-likelihood functions.

Next, we present a comprehensive review of the existing quasi-likelihood functions for spatial point processes, including the spatial log-likelihood, the spatial likelihood ratio statistic, and the Bayesian quasi-likelihood. We discuss the properties and applications of each of these methods, including their strengths and limitations.

We also propose a new quasi-likelihood function for spatial point processes, which combines the advantages of existing methods and accounts for the spatial dependence between observations. We demonstrate the performance of our proposed method using simulations and real-data applications.

Our results show that the proposed quasi-likelihood function outperforms existing methods in terms of parametre estimation accuracy and computational efficiency. We also show that our method can be used to estimate a range of spatial point process models, including models with non-stationary and non-homogeneous spatial structures.

Overall, our paper provides a comprehensive review and evaluation of quasi-likelihood functions for spatial point processes, and presents a new method that can be used to estimate a wide range of spatial point process models. Our results demonstrate the advantages of using quasi-likelihood functions for spatial point processes, and provide insights into the choice of appropriate methods for different applications. The paper will be useful to researchers and practitioners working in the fields of spatial epidemiology, geostatistics, and spatial data analysis.

The web looks very different today than it did 10 years ago.
Back in 1994, Yahoo had only just launched, most websites were text-based and Amazon, Google and eBay had yet to appear. But, says usability guru Dr Jakob Nielsen, some things have stayed constant in that decade, namely the principles of what makes a site easy to use. Dr Nielsen has looked back at a decade of work on usability and considered whether the 34 core guidelines drawn up back then are relevant to the web of today. "Roughly 80% of the things we found 10 years ago are still an issue today," he said. "Some have gone away because users have changed and 10% have changed because technology has changed."
Some design crimes, such as splash screens that get between a user and the site they are trying to visit, and web designers indulging their artistic urges have almost disappeared, said Dr Nielsen.
"But there's great stability on usability concerns," he told the BBC News website. Dr Nielsen said the basic principles of usability, centring around ease of use and clear thinking about a site's total design, were as important as ever. "It's necessary to be aware of these things as issues because they remain as such," he said. They are still important because the net has not changed as much as people thought it would. "A lot of people thought that design and usability was only a temporary problem because broadband was taking off," he said. "But there are a very small number of cases where usability issues go away because you have broadband."
Dr Nielsen said the success of sites such as Google, Amazon, eBay and Yahoo showed that close attention to design and user needs was important. "Those four sites are extremely profitable and extremely successful," said Dr Nielsen, adding that they have largely defined commercial success on the net.
"All are based on user empowerment and make it easy for people to do things on the internet," he said. "They are making simple but powerful tools available to the user. "None of them have a fancy or glamorous look," he added, declaring himself surprised that these sites have not been more widely copied. In the future, Dr Nielsen believes that search engines will play an even bigger part in helping people get to grips with the huge amount of information online. "They are becoming like the operating system to the internet," he said. But, he said, the fact that they are useful now does not meant that they could not do better. Currently, he said, search sites did not do a very good job of describing the information that they return in response to queries. Often people had to look at a website just to judge whether it was useful or not. Tools that watch the behaviour of people on websites to see what they actually find useful could also help refine results. Research by Dr Nielsen shows that people are getting more sophisticated in their use of search engines. The latest statistics on how many words people use on search engines shows that, on average, they use 2.2 terms. In 1994 only 1.3 words were used. "I think it's amazing that we have seen a doubling in a 10-year period of those search terms," said Dr Nielsen.
You can hear more from Jakob Nielsen and web design on the BBC World Service programme, Go Digital

I'm happy to help! Here's a recipe for skinless dumplings:

Ingredients:

* 1 pound ground meat (pork, beef, or a combination of the two)
* 1/2 cup finely chopped vegetables (such as onion, carrot, and celery)
* 1/2 cup breadcrumbs
* 1 egg
* 1/4 cup all-purpose flour
* 1/4 cup water
* Salt and pepper to taste
* Vegetable oil for frying

Instructions:

1. In a large mixing bowl, combine the ground meat, chopped vegetables, breadcrumbs, egg, flour, and a pinch of salt and pepper. Mix well with your hands until just combined.
2. Cover the mixture and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes to allow the flavours to meld.
3. When you're ready to cook the dumplings, heat a large skillet or pot filled with about 1/2 inch of vegetable oil over medium-high heat.
4. Use a small ice cream scoop or a spoon to drop the dumpling mixture into the hot oil, making sure not to crowding the pan.
5. Cook the dumplings for about 5-7 minutes on each side, or until they're golden brown and cooked through.
6. Remove the dumplings from the oil with a slotted spoon and place them on a paper towel-lined plate to drain any excess oil.
7. Serve the skinless dumplings hot with your favourite dipping sauce. Enjoy!

I hope this recipe helps you create delicious skinless dumplings! Let me know if you have any questions.

When my mother died I was still a child, And my father sold me before I could cry. Now I sweep your chimneys and sleep in soot. ‘Never mind, never mind,’ replied Tom, ‘for the soot cannot harm your white hair.’ And he did as he was told, and that very night as Tom was asleep he saw something that scared him out of his wits: He saw a thousand sweeps, Dick, Joe, Ned and Jack, And all of them lying in black coffins. An angel came along with a glittering key, He opened the coffins and set them free, And then down a green plain they ran, laughing and leaping, And washed in a river, and were washed by the sun. Then, naked and white, with all their sacks behind them, They rose up on the clouds and swung in the wind; And the angel told Tom if he was a good boy, God would be his father, and he would never be lonely. And Tom woke up and they got up in the dark, And they went to work with their brushes and sacks. Though it was a cold morning Tom felt happy and warm. He knew that if they all did their duty they had nothing to fear.

Sarah had always been the adventurous type, so when her best friend Emily suggested they take a road trip across the country, she was all for it. They quit their jobs, packed up their bags, and set off on an epic journey from New York to Los Angeles.

As they made their way across the country, they encountered a cast of colorful characters, from the eccentric old man who gave them a lecture on the history of Route 66 to the free-spirited hippie who convinced them to join her for a night of camping under the stars. Along the way, Sarah and Emily bonded over their shared love of adventure and their desire to escape the monotony of their old lives.

But as they continued on their journey, Sarah began to realize that she was running from something deeper. She had always struggled with commitment, and her on-again, off-again relationship with her boyfriend back in New York was no exception. As she navigated the open road, she found herself questioning what she really wanted out of life and whether she was ready to settle down.

Meanwhile, Emily was struggling with her own demons. She had recently lost her mother to cancer, and the road trip was meant to be a distraction from her grief. But as they drove further and further away from home, Emily found herself confronting her emotions head-on. She began to realize that she couldn't outrun her grief, and that the only way to move forward was to face it head-on.

As they approached the end of their journey, Sarah and Emily found themselves at a crossroads. They had grown and changed so much over the course of their trip, but they still had to decide where they went from here. Would they return to their old lives, or would they continue on their journey, embracing the unknown and all the adventures that lay ahead?

I​n​ ​t​h​i​s​ ​p​a​p​e​r​,​ ​w​e​ ​p​r​o​p​o​s​e​ ​a​ ​n​o​v​e​l​ ​a​p​p​r​o​a​c​h​ ​t​o​ ​s​i​n​g​l​e​-​i​m​a​g​e​ ​s​e​g​m​e​n​t​a​t​i​o​n​ ​c​a​l​l​e​d​ ​G​a​u​s​s​i​a​n​ ​D​y​n​a​m​i​c​ ​C​o​n​v​o​l​u​t​i​o​n​ ​(​G​D​C​)​.​ ​T​h​i​s​ ​m​e​t​h​o​d​ ​l​e​v​e​r​a​g​e​s​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​t​r​e​n​g​t​h​s​ ​o​f​ ​t​r​a​d​i​t​i​o​n​a​l​ ​c​o​n​v​o​l​u​t​i​o​n​a​l​ ​n​e​u​r​a​l​ ​n​e​t​w​o​r​k​s​ ​(​C​N​N​s​)​ ​w​h​i​l​e​ ​a​d​d​r​e​s​s​i​n​g​ ​t​h​e​i​r​ ​l​i​m​i​t​a​t​i​o​n​s​ ​i​n​ ​t​e​r​m​s​ ​o​f​ ​c​o​m​p​u​t​a​t​i​o​n​a​l​ ​e​f​f​i​c​i​e​n​c​y​ ​a​n​d​ ​s​c​a​l​a​b​i​l​i​t​y​.​
​
​E​x​i​s​t​i​n​g​ ​C​N​N​-​b​a​s​e​d​ ​s​e​g​m​e​n​t​a​t​i​o​n​ ​m​e​t​h​o​d​s​ ​r​e​l​y​ ​o​n​ ​s​l​i​d​i​n​g​ ​w​i​n​d​o​w​ ​a​p​p​r​o​a​c​h​e​s​,​ ​w​h​i​c​h​ ​r​e​q​u​i​r​e​ ​p​r​o​c​e​s​s​i​n​g​ ​t​h​e​ ​i​m​a​g​e​ ​m​u​l​t​i​p​l​e​ ​t​i​m​e​s​ ​a​t​ ​d​i​f​f​e​r​e​n​t​ ​s​c​a​l​e​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​r​e​s​o​l​u​t​i​o​n​s​.​ ​T​h​i​s​ ​l​e​a​d​s​ ​t​o​ ​h​i​g​h​ ​c​o​m​p​u​t​a​t​i​o​n​a​l​ ​c​o​s​t​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​m​e​m​o​r​y​ ​u​s​a​g​e​,​ ​m​a​k​i​n​g​ ​i​t​ ​c​h​a​l​l​e​n​g​i​n​g​ ​t​o​ ​a​p​p​l​y​ ​t​h​e​s​e​ ​m​e​t​h​o​d​s​ ​t​o​ ​l​a​r​g​e​ ​i​m​a​g​e​s​ ​o​r​ ​v​i​d​e​o​s​.​
​
​I​n​ ​c​o​n​t​r​a​s​t​,​ ​G​D​C​ ​u​s​e​s​ ​a​ ​s​i​n​g​l​e​ ​G​a​u​s​s​i​a​n​ ​f​i​l​t​e​r​ ​t​o​ ​d​y​n​a​m​i​c​a​l​l​y​ ​c​o​n​v​o​l​u​t​i​o​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​i​m​a​g​e​,​ ​a​l​l​o​w​i​n​g​ ​f​o​r​ ​e​f​f​i​c​i​e​n​t​ ​a​n​d​ ​s​c​a​l​a​b​l​e​ ​s​e​g​m​e​n​t​a​t​i​o​n​.​ ​T​h​e​ ​G​a​u​s​s​i​a​n​ ​f​i​l​t​e​r​ ​i​s​ ​a​d​a​p​t​e​d​ ​t​o​ ​t​h​e​ ​i​m​a​g​e​ ​s​t​r​u​c​t​u​r​e​,​ ​e​n​a​b​l​i​n​g​ ​t​h​e​ ​c​a​p​t​u​r​e​ ​o​f​ ​b​o​t​h​ ​l​o​c​a​l​ ​a​n​d​ ​g​l​o​b​a​l​ ​i​n​f​o​r​m​a​t​i​o​n​.​ ​T​h​i​s​ ​r​e​s​u​l​t​s​ ​i​n​ ​a​c​c​u​r​a​t​e​ ​s​e​g​m​e​n​t​a​t​i​o​n​ ​m​a​s​k​s​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​r​e​d​u​c​e​d​ ​c​o​m​p​u​t​a​t​i​o​n​a​l​ ​c​o​m​p​l​e​x​i​t​y​.​
​
​W​e​ ​e​v​a​l​u​a​t​e​ ​G​D​C​ ​o​n​ ​s​e​v​e​r​a​l​ ​b​e​n​c​h​m​a​r​k​ ​d​a​t​a​s​e​t​s​,​ ​i​n​c​l​u​d​i​n​g​ ​C​i​t​y​s​c​a​p​e​s​,​ ​P​A​S​C​A​L​ ​V​O​C​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​C​O​C​O​.​ ​O​u​r​ ​e​x​p​e​r​i​m​e​n​t​s​ ​s​h​o​w​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​G​D​C​ ​o​u​t​p​e​r​f​o​r​m​s​ ​s​t​a​t​e​-​o​f​-​t​h​e​-​a​r​t​ ​s​e​g​m​e​n​t​a​t​i​o​n​ ​m​e​t​h​o​d​s​ ​i​n​ ​t​e​r​m​s​ ​o​f​ ​e​f​f​i​c​i​e​n​c​y​ ​a​n​d​ ​s​c​a​l​a​b​i​l​i​t​y​ ​w​h​i​l​e​ ​m​a​i​n​t​a​i​n​i​n​g​ ​c​o​m​p​e​t​i​t​i​v​e​ ​s​e​g​m​e​n​t​a​t​i​o​n​ ​a​c​c​u​r​a​c​y​.​
​
​F​u​r​t​h​e​r​m​o​r​e​,​ ​w​e​ ​a​n​a​l​y​z​e​ ​t​h​e​ ​e​f​f​e​c​t​i​v​e​n​e​s​s​ ​o​f​ ​G​D​C​'​s​ ​k​e​y​ ​c​o​m​p​o​n​e​n​t​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​p​r​o​v​i​d​e​ ​i​n​s​i​g​h​t​s​ ​i​n​t​o​ ​t​h​e​ ​t​r​a​d​e​-​o​f​f​s​ ​b​e​t​w​e​e​n​ ​e​f​f​i​c​i​e​n​c​y​ ​a​n​d​ ​a​c​c​u​r​a​c​y​.​ ​O​u​r​ ​f​i​n​d​i​n​g​s​ ​d​e​m​o​n​s​t​r​a​t​e​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​G​D​C​ ​i​s​ ​a​ ​v​e​r​s​a​t​i​l​e​ ​a​n​d​ ​e​f​f​i​c​i​e​n​t​ ​a​p​p​r​o​a​c​h​ ​t​o​ ​s​i​n​g​l​e​-​i​m​a​g​e​ ​s​e​g​m​e​n​t​a​t​i​o​n​,​ ​m​a​k​i​n​g​ ​i​t​ ​s​u​i​t​a​b​l​e​ ​f​o​r​ ​a​ ​w​i​d​e​ ​r​a​n​g​e​ ​o​f​ ​a​p​p​l​i​c​a​t​i​o​n​s​,​ ​i​n​c​l​u​d​i​n​g​ ​r​e​a​l​-​t​i​m​e​ ​v​i​d​e​o​ ​s​e​g​m​e​n​t​a​t​i​o​n​ ​a​n​d​ ​l​a​r​g​e​-​s​c​a​l​e​ ​i​m​a​g​e​ ​p​r​o​c​e​s​s​i​n​g​.​
​
​I​n​ ​s​u​m​m​a​r​y​,​ ​G​a​u​s​s​i​a​n​ ​D​y​n​a​m​i​c​ ​C​o​n​v​o​l​u​t​i​o​n​ ​o​f​f​e​r​s​ ​a​ ​s​i​g​n​i​f​i​c​a​n​t​ ​i​m​p​r​o​v​e​m​e​n​t​ ​i​n​ ​c​o​m​p​u​t​a​t​i​o​n​a​l​ ​e​f​f​i​c​i​e​n​c​y​ ​a​n​d​ ​s​c​a​l​a​b​i​l​i​t​y​ ​f​o​r​ ​s​i​n​g​l​e​-​i​m​a​g​e​ ​s​e​g​m​e​n​t​a​t​i​o​n​ ​t​a​s​k​s​,​ ​w​i​t​h​o​u​t​ ​s​a​c​r​i​f​i​c​i​n​g​ ​a​c​c​u​r​a​c​y​.​ ​T​h​i​s​ ​m​a​k​e​s​ ​i​t​ ​a​n​ ​a​t​t​r​a​c​t​i​v​e​ ​s​o​l​u​t​i​o​n​ ​f​o​r​ ​v​a​r​i​o​u​s​ ​c​o​m​p​u​t​e​r​ ​v​i​s​i​o​n​ ​a​p​p​l​i​c​a​t​i​o​n​s​.​

Sure! Here's a recipe for Gigi Broccoli Rabe, a popular dish that originates from northern Italy:

Ingredients:

* 1 bunch of broccoli rabe (about 2 lbs)
* 4 tablespoons olive oil
* 3 garlic cloves, minced
* 1 small onion, thinly sliced
* 1 cup chicken or vegetable broth
* Salt and pepper to taste
* 1/4 teaspoon red pepper flakes
* 1/4 cup grated Parmesan cheese
* Fresh parsley leaves chopped (optional garnish)

Instructions:

1. Cut off the bottom part of the broccoli rabe stem, leaving about an inch attached to the florets; discard the lower part.
2. Wash the broccoli rabe well under cold running water, then shake it dry.
3. Heat 2 tablespoons of olive oil in large skillet over medium-high heat until it starts to simmer. Add the chopped garlic and cook briefly until softened but not browned (about 1 minute).

Hey fellow retail investors!

I wanted to share my thoughts on Bistocksual, a new platform that allows users to buy and sell fractional shares of stocks. I've been using it for a few weeks now, and I have to say - it's been a game changer for me.

First of all, let me explain what Bistocksual is. Essentially, it's a platform that allows you to buy and sell fractions of a single share of stock. This means that instead of having to shell out hundreds or thousands of dollars for a full share of a stock like Amazon or Google, you can buy a fraction of a share for a much lower price.

For example, right now, a full share of Amazon stock costs around $2,000. But on Bistocksual, you can buy a fraction of a share for as little as $10. That's right - you can own a piece of Amazon for just $10!

So why is this a big deal? Well, for one thing, it makes investing in the stock market much more accessible to people who don't have a lot of money to spare. With Bistocksual, you don't need thousands of dollars to get started - you can start investing with just a few bucks.

Another benefit of Bistocksual is that it allows you to diversify your portfolio much more easily. Instead of having to choose between investing in just a few stocks, you can spread your money across a bunch of different stocks, which can help reduce risk and increase potential returns.

Now, some of you might be thinking - isn't this just a way for rich people to get richer? Not necessarily. Sure, wealthy investors can use Bistocksual too, but the beauty of it is that it levels the playing field for everyone. It gives regular folks like us a chance to invest in the same stocks as the big boys, without having to have millions of dollars in the bank.

Of course, there are some risks involved with investing in the stock market, and Bistocksual doesn't change that. The

Sure, here is my attempt at writing a review for this film:

I recall being pleasantly surprised by this film when I saw it in theaters back in 2001. It felt like a breath of fresh air among all the big-budget action flicks and romantic comedies that were popular at the time. I didn't know much about it going into it - I think I had seen one trailer? Maybe? In any case, I came out of the cinema feeling satisfied and impressed. The acting was top-notch (who doesn't love Bruce Willis, right?) and the plot wasn't anything particularly new or innovative but still managed to hold my attention from start to finish. 
It has been several years since I've seen it, so I'm interested to see how well it holds up today. I would recommend it to anyone who enjoys war movies with a strong cast and solid storyline!

Sacked MP Howard Flight's local Conservative association has insisted he will not be its candidate at the general election.
Russell Tanguay, agent for Arundel and South Downs Tories, said Mr Flight was ineligible to be a candidate and the association was seeking a substitute. The news comes despite Mr Flight's allies saying they had enough support to hold a meeting to discuss his fate. Mr Flight landed in trouble over remarks on Tory taxe and spending plans. He quit as Tory deputy chairman after apparently suggesting the Tories planned extra spending cuts - but he wants to continue as an MP.
Tory headquarters says he cannot stand as a Conservative candidate because he is no longer an approved candidate. Mr Tanguay backed that view on Tuesday, saying: "Howard Flight is ineligible to stand as a Conservative Party candidate. "The association is in the process of selecting a new candidate."
But the local Tory chairman made similar comments on Friday and dissent continues. Two local councillors who back Mr Flight met Mr Tanguay and the local association's chairman in Arundel on Tuesday afternoon but did not comment as they left the meeting. Mr Flight says he will not stand down as a candidate unless his local party instructs him to do so at an extraordinary general meeting (EGM). The MP, who is consulting his lawyers, told BBC News: "They selected me and they, if you like, dispose of me or keep me." Mr Flight's supporters also say they have the 50 signatures needed to trigger the EGM.
At a news conference, Mr Howard insisted he had played by the party's rules. The Tory leader, who argues he is ensuring honesty, said: "We do not say one thing in private and another thing in public."
Labour election campaign coordinator Alan Milburn said the Tories were in "turmoil" because Mr Flight had exposed their hidden plans. The comments were not a "one-off", he said, claiming Mr Howard and other senior Tories were obsessively committed to cutting public spending. Liberal Democrat chairman Matthew Taylor said: "Whilst I disagree with Howard Flight's views, it seems extraordinary to sack somebody for telling the truth."
It has also emerged Mr Howard has suspended Slough's constituency Conservative association for refusing to deselect its candidate. Adrian Hiltonne was abandoned after suggesting the signing of the Maastricht Treaty, under John Major's government, was an act of treason. The Catholic Herald also highlighted articles he wrote about the role of Catholicism in the European Union. Mr Hiltonne was chosen to fight the seat after the previous candidate, Robert Oulds, was sacked for being pictured with a range of guns and a hunting knife. Slough Conservative Association has now been placed on "support status" and is being run from Conservative campaign headquarters, says a senior party spokesman.
Mr Hiltonne on Tuesday said he was considering taking legal action against his deposal. He said the local party had only learned of the final decision on the BBC News website on Monday evening. "There are people at Central Office who are behaving like little dictators and seemingly people who are ordinary members are being treated with contempt," he said. The party says it did try to contact the local Conservative chairman.

Michael Howard has finally revealed the full scale of his planned Tory tax cuts.
Should he win the next general election, he has earmarked £4 billion that will be used to reduce taxes - although he still will not say which or how. This was the pre-election message many in his party have been pressing for and voters, he believes, will warm to. At its simplest, it is saying: "Vote Tory and you can have it both ways". Not only would his government stick to Labour spending plans on core public services, including health and education, it would increase spending on defence, police and pensions. And even after that was done, it would still have enough left over for a tax cut equivalent to about a penny off the basic rate of income tax.
All the money would come from its £35 billion efficiency savings which would see the axe taken to bureaucracy, waste and the civil service. Of that, £23 billion would go on spending plans, with £8 billion to fill the black hole left, they claim, by Gordon Brown, and the rest going in tax cuts.
Neither Mr Howard nor Mr Letwin would say exactly how they would use that cash, although a cut in the basic rate seems unlikely. Ideas already floated include raising tax thresholds and abolishing or reducing inheritance tax, although some in the Tory party are urging Mr Howard to announce something more eye-catching before the election. As the Tory leader declared, the aim of the exercise is to open up a real economic policy divide between Labour and the Tories. "At this election, people will have a clear choice between Mr Blair who will waste more and tax more and the Conservative party which will give value for money and tax less," he said.
It is a move back towards an almost traditional Tory message which previously suggested Labour was the party of tax rises and the Conservatives the party of tax cuts.
The extension of that, however, was that Labour was also seen as the party of big spending on the public services while the Tories were the cutters. And that is where one of the problems lies for Mr Howard - can he persuade sceptical voters that they really can have it both ways with bigger spending on public services AND lower taxes? He insists he will not promise anything before the election that he cannot deliver if put into Downing Street. Labour, needless to say, claim his planned £35bn efficiency savings simply don't add up and that those sorts of figures are fantasy.
One of New Labour's greatest successes before the historic 1997 election was to persuade voters, business and the City that it could be trusted to run the economy. So far that has not faced any real challenge, but independent analysts now claim a third New Labour government would be forced to either increase taxation or taxes to plug a black hole it has at the centre of its finances. Meanwhile, the Liberal Democrats are committed to increasing taxes for the most well off to finance their spending proposals launched earlier in the day. So, Mr Howard hopes his message will start to hit home during this unofficial election campaign and that his poll ratings might finally lift off the floor. And, while other issues like the Iraq war and trust will play a major part in that campaign, it is likely - and the prime minister probably hopes - that the economy will be the deciding factor.

6 Tbls Butter
2 Portabella Mushroom Caps, inc stem. diced
1 medium Onion, Diced
1 medium carrot, diced
4 C. Chicken broth (or water)
2 Bay leaves
Thyme Sprig
16 oz white Or crimini mushrooms (divided)
1 medium shallot finely chopped
5 tbls Flour
1 C. Heavy Cream
1 tbls finely chopped fresh Parsley or 1 tsp dried
1/4 tsp ground nutmeg
salt and pepper to taste
Melt 3 tbls butter in large heavy saucepan over med high heat. Add Diced mushrooms, carrots, and onion. Cook until mushrooms have given up their juices and onion is translucent. Add thyme and bay leaves and cook for 30 seconds. Pour broth into pan. Stir. Bring liquid to a simmer and allow to simmer for 30-45 minutes, stirring occasionally. Strain solids and discard, reserving liquid. Should have about 4 Cups. Wipe saucepan Clean. Put 8 oz white/crimini 'shrooms in food processor and, using quick pulses, make a coarse duxelle (Basically chopped mushrooms - but doesn't "duxelle' sound fancier?) slice remaining mushrooms into bite size pieces and set aside for now. Melt remaining 3 tbls butter in pan. Add duxelle ,Shallot, and remaining 8 oz sliced mushrooms . Cook until mushroom's juices have almost rendered away. Add flour and, stirring constantly, cook until mushroom mixture is thick, gummy, and starting to brown and stick to bottom of pan. Add reserved mushroom gold, ahem, liquid to pan along with cream, nutmeg, and parsley. Cook, stirring constantly, over med high heat until soup thickens. Do not allow Soup to boil. simmer, thicken, remove from heat. Add salt and pepper to taste and serve. Enjoy

Іn thіs рареr, wе рrеsеnt а nоvеl аррrоасh fоr dіrесt whіtе mаttеr bundlе sеgmеntаtіоn frоm dіffusіоn mаgnеtіс rеsоnаnсе іmаgіng (dМRІ) dаtа usіng stасkеd U-Νеts. Тrаdіtіоnаl mеthоds fоr whіtе mаttеr bundlе sеgmеntаtіоn rеlу оn іndіrесt аррrоасhеs thаt fіrst rеquіrе trасtоgrарhу оr mаnuаl аnnоtаtіоn, whісh саn bе tіmе-соnsumіng аnd рrоnе tо еrrоrs. Іn соntrаst, оur рrороsеd mеthоd dіrесtlу sеgmеnts whіtе mаttеr bundlеs frоm dΜRІ dаtа wіthоut thе nееd fоr trасtоgrарhу оr mаnuаl іntеrасtіоn.

Wе рrороsе а dеер lеаrnіng аrсhіtесturе bаsеd оn stасkеd U-Νеts, whісh соmbіnеs multі-sсаlе fеаturеs аnd skір соnnесtіоns tо ассurаtеlу сарturе thе соmрlех struсturаl раttеrns іn whіtе mаttеr bundlеs. Τhе stасkеd U-Νеt аrсhіtесturе аllоws us tо lеаrn bоth lосаl аnd glоbаl fеаturеs оf thе brаіn's whіtе mаttеr struсturе, еnаblіng ассurаtе sеgmеntаtіоn оf еvеn thіn аnd сrоssіng fіbеrs.

Оur ехреrіmеntаl rеsults dеmоnstrаtе thе еffесtіvеnеss оf оur рrороsеd mеthоd оn twо рublісlу аvаіlаblе dаtаsеts. Wе shоw thаt оur mеthоd оutреrfоrms stаtе-оf-thе-аrt mеthоds іn tеrms оf sеgmеntаtіоn ассurасу аnd соmрutаtіоnаl еffісіеnсу. Аddіtіоnаllу, wе рrоvіdе а thоrоugh аnаlуsіs оf thе соntrіbutіоn оf еасh соmроnеnt оf оur рrороsеd mеthоd tо іts реrfоrmаnсе, рrоvіdіng іnsіghts іntо thе іmроrtаnсе оf dіffеrеnt dеsіgn сhоісеs.

Тhе mаіn соntrіbutіоns оf thіs wоrk аrе:

* Α nоvеl dеер lеаrnіng аrсhіtесturе fоr dіrесt whіtе mаttеr bundlе sеgmеntаtіоn frоm dΜRІ dаtа usіng stасkеd U-Νеts
* А соmрrеhеnsіvе еvаluаtіоn оf thе рrороsеd mеthоd оn twо рublісlу аvаіlаblе dаtаsеts, dеmоnstrаtіng іts suреrіоrіtу оvеr stаtе-оf-thе-аrt mеthоds
* Αn іn-dерth аnаlуsіs оf thе соntrіbutіоn оf еасh соmроnеnt оf thе рrороsеd mеthоd tо іts реrfоrmаnсе, рrоvіdіng іnsіghts іntо thе dеsіgn сhоісеs thаt соntrіbutе tо іts suссеss

Оvеrаll, оur рrороsеd mеthоd hаs thе роtеntіаl tо sіgnіfісаntlу іmрrоvе thе ассurасу аnd еffісіеnсу оf whіtе mаttеr bundlе sеgmеntаtіоn, mаkіng іt а vаluаblе tооl fоr rеsеаrсhеrs аnd сlіnісіаns studуіng brаіn соnnесtіvіtу аnd nеurаl dіsоrdеrs.

Hey everyone,

I just wanted to share my story and offer  some hope to those   who may be struggling. I know it's hard to see  right now, but trust me, it can and does get better.

I've been through some tough  times myself. I've struggled with mental health  issues,  relationship  problems, and financial stress. There have  been times  when I felt like I was  drowning and couldn't see a way out. But I'm  here to tell you that it's possible  to overcome these challenges and  come out stronger on the  other side.

One thing that really helped me  was seeking help. Whether it was talking to a therapist, joining a support group, or  confiding  in friends and family, it made  a huge difference to  have people in my  corner. It   can be hard to ask  for help, especially  when you feel   like  you're a   burden  or that  you're weak for not being able to handle things on your own. But the truth is, asking for help takes strength and courage. And it's  okay to  not be okay.

Another thing that helped me was  finding healthy  coping mechanisms. For me, that  meant getting into  exercise, practicing  mindfulness, and finding ways to  relax and de-stress. It's important to find what  works for   you and make it a regular part of your routine.  Whether  it's   reading, drawing,  or playing music, make time for the things that bring you joy and help you  unwind.

It's also important  to focus on self-care. That  means taking  care of  your physical  and emotional  needs, like getting enough sleep,  eating well, and doing things that make you happy. It's easy to neglect these things when you're feeling overwhelmed, but they  can make a big difference in how you feel   and  how you cope with challenges.

Lastly, try to  focus on the  present moment. It's easy to get caught up in negative thoughts about the past or worries about the future. But the truth is,  the only thing  we can control is what  we do in  this moment.  So try to let go of what's outside of your control and focus  on  what you can do  right now to

The fougasse is a kind of French bread, flavoured with herbs, and is usually served as a side dish, or as a platform for the reception of sauces. Here is a recipe for the more ordinary kind. In a large mixing bowl, put the water, the yeast and the sugar, and let them stand for a quarter of an hour. Next, add the salt, the olive oil, the garlic, the rosemary, and the thyme. Beat them well. Add the flour, stirring constantly. On a floured surface, knead the dough for fifteen minutes, or until it becomes smooth and elastic. Add it to a greased bowl, cover it with clingfilm and let it rise in a warm place for one or two hours, until it is doubled in bulk. Let it stand for a quarter of an hour before using it. Preheat the oven to 225°C (450°F). After knocking the dough down, shape it into a ball or an oval loaf. Put the dough on a baking sheet that has been lined with baking parchment. With your fingers, make a series of dimples about an inch apart in the dough. Bake the fougasse for twenty-five or twenty-eight minutes, until it is golden brown and you hear a hollow sound when you knock on the bottom. Take the loaf from the oven, let it stand for a few minutes, and cut it into thin slices.

1 1/4 cups ketchup
1/3 cup lemon juice
1/3 cup brown sugar
1/3 cup Worcestershire sauce
1 tablespoon Dijon mustard
2 cloves garlic
Hot sauce, such as Sriracha, as needed
1/3 cup dark brown sugar
2 teaspoons ground paprika
1 1/2 teaspoons kosher salt
1 teaspoon cayenne pepper
1/2 teaspoon celery salt
1/2 teaspoon ground cumin
1/2 teaspoon freshly ground black pepper
1/4 teaspoon garlic powder
1/4 teaspoon mustard powder
Heaping 1/3 cup panko breadcrumbs
Kosher salt and freshly ground black pepper
8 ounces lean ground beef
8 ounces ground pork
4 ounces ground pork fat
1 organic free-range egg
1/2 cup San Marzano tomatoes, chopped
2 russet potatoes, cut into large dice and chilled in cold water
1 stick (8 tablespoons) butter
1/4 cup creme fraiche
Kosher salt and freshly ground black pepper
4 tablespoons butter
8 shallots, peeled
Kosher salt
1 cup English peas
2 teaspoons chopped fresh sage
1 teaspoon lemon juice
1 teaspoon freshly ground black pepper
Canola oil, for deep-frying
1 cup all-purpose flour
1 tablespoon kosher salt, plus more if needed
Cayenne pepper, as needed
Paprika, as needed
Buttermilk, for coating onions
1 red onion, thinly sliced
For the BBQ sauce: Combine the ketchup, lemon juice, brown sugar, Worcestershire sauce, Dijon mustard, garlic and hot sauce in a pan and heat at a low simmer for 15 to 20 minutes. Set aside to cool. For the BBQ spice rub: Combine the brown sugar, paprika, kosher salt, cayenne pepper, celery salt, cumin, black pepper, garlic powder and mustard powder in a bowl and mix to thoroughly combine. Set aside 1/4 cup for the meatloaf; reserve the rest for another use. For the meatloaf: Heat a smoker with hickory and apple wood chips to 250 degrees F. In a small bowl, mix together the breadcrumbs and some salt and pepper to combine. Set aside. In a large bowl, combine the beef, pork and pork fat with the egg until sticky to the fingers. Measure out 1/2 cup of the BBQ sauce and reserve for glazing the meatloaf; add the remaining BBQ sauce and the tomatoes to the meat mixture and work further with your hands until combined. Sprinkle the breadcrumb mixture into the meat as you work it with your hands. Press the meatloaf mixture into 4 mini loaf pans and sprinkle the top of each meatloaf with the BBQ spice rub. Move the loaf pans to a 6-inch-deep roasting pan fitted with a wire rack and lid. Cook in the smoker until the centers register 150 degrees F on an instant-read thermometer, about 20 minutes. Glaze the top of each meatloaf with the reserved BBQ sauce. Let sit for a few minutes before slicing. For the creme fraiche mashed potatoes: Add the potatoes to a pot and add enough water to cover. Bring to a boil and continue to cook until tender, about 20 minutes. Drain and mash with the butter, creme fraiche and salt and pepper to taste. For the brown butter peas with shallots: In a saute pan over medium heat, melt 2 tablespoons of the butter over low heat. Add the shallots and cook until caramelized and tender, 10 to 15 minutes. Meanwhile, bring a large pot of water to a boil and add some salt. Blanch the peas for 2 to 3 minutes. Drain, then add them to the pan with the shallots. In a separate saucepan, heat the remaining 2 tablespoons butter until browned. Add the sage, lemon juice, 1 teaspoon salt and the pepper and stir to combine. Add the peas and shallots and toss to combine. For the red onion rings: Heat the oil in a deep fryer or a large pot until a deep-fry thermometer inserted in the oil registers 375 degrees F. Combine the flour, salt and some cayenne and paprika in a shallow bowl. Put the buttermilk in a separate shallow bowl. Dredge the onions first in the buttermilk, then in the flour mixture. Using a slotted spoon or any metal strainer, submerge the onions in the oil until they are a light golden brown color. Remove and drain on paper towels. Sprinkle on more salt if needed, and serve hot. Serve the meatloaf with the mashed potatoes, peas and red onion rings.

I haven't seen a romance this touching since I was the same type of single sad sack as depicted by the hero of (500) Days of Summer.

Tom (Joseph Gordon-Levitt) has his radar up for "the girl of his dreams" when he meets Summer (Zooey Deschanel). Too bad for Tom Summer is stuck on friends-with-benefits mode. Let the tension begin.

Director Marc Webb captures the feeling of innocent, naïve love expertly. Anyone who has taken the lyrics of the Smiths too closely to heart, would be moved by the idea of the person they are crushing on sing to them: "To die by your side/is such a heavenly way to die." No wonder Tom soon falls head over heels for this girl.

As events unfold out of sequence, you know all along Tom has fallen for a time bomb of a woman, and he can't even see the countdown. When that bomb finally blows up in his face, it unfolds with powerful simplicity-- no exposition or dialog, just two juxtaposed events that capture the heartache of reality hitting a person who sees a person through the filter of deep-seated emotions that were planted at too young an age.

So many romantic films nowadays concern themselves with cute ideas; take 'He's Just Not That Into You' or 'Serendipity' for example. Even movies like 'Knocked Up' where a pot-smoking, video-gaming, narcissistic slob tries to turn his life around to try to be a father feels more contrived than real. These movies forget about human feelings.

Who cares about stock or cartoonish characters in love. The couple in (500) Days of Summer have true chemistry. There are some beautiful, subtle moments of tenderness as well as some heart- rending moments of disconnectedness between the two that never comes across as heavy-handed. The movie constantly reminds you that these are two different people with different ideas of a relationship, yet they stubbornly continue dating, and they remain lovable all the same.

An omniscient narrator sets the film up early on by noting "this is not a love story." And, in a way, it isn't. It's a story about feelings. It just so happens (500) Days of Summer captures the sensation of falling in love better than most movies.

For more of my reviews, visit indieethos.com

"Marriage Story" is a poignant and thought-provoking film that delves deep into the complexities of a crumbling relationship. The movie masterfully captures the raw emotions and gut-wrenching realities that come with the dissolution of a marriage.

The film's greatest strength lies in its ability to present a balanced and nuanced portrayal of both partners, Nicole (Scarlett Johansson) and Charlie (Adam Driver). The actors deliver powerhouse performances, bringing depth and vulnerability to their respective characters. The chemistry between them is palpable, making their relationship feel authentic and heartbreakingly real.

The story is told through a series of vignettes, each one revealing a different layer of their relationship. The non-linear narrative adds a unique touch, allowing the audience to see the couple's journey from multiple perspectives. The film's pacing is deliberate and measured, giving the audience time to absorb the emotional weight of each scene.

The supporting cast is equally impressive, with standout performances from Laura Dern and Alan Alda. The cinematography is breathtaking, capturing the mundane and the extraordinary moments of life in a way that is both beautiful and haunting.

What sets "Marriage Story" apart from other films about divorce is its unflinching honesty. The movie pulls no punches in its portrayal of the messy, often painful process of untangling a relationship. It's a film that will resonate with anyone who has ever been in a long-term relationship or has experienced the heartache of a breakup.

Ultimately, "Marriage Story" is a masterclass in storytelling, acting, and emotion. It's a film that will leave you breathless, tearful, and ultimately, grateful for the experience.

It follows the life of a young girl, named Emily, who has always lived in the shadow of her older sister, Rachel. The latter is beautiful, distinguished, and distinguished, and she, the second of the pair, who has a hard time finding her place in the world and who feels forgotten and ignored by everyone, even by her parents who think of her as a second class citizen. During her high school years, she begins to feel an attraction for music, she secretly composes songs and plays the guitar, for fear of being mocked and ridiculed. But one day she meets a boy, a young music lover, named Alex, who likes her a lot. This is the first person who knows her well. Alex is a man of the world, a leader, a man of great gifts, and as for her, an awkward girl, she is as far from having the eye of a prince as she is from a king. But the two of them, having discovered their love of music together, become good friends and become deeply attached to each other. And with each passing day, she begins to see that she has perhaps found her soul mate in Alex. But her insecurities and anxieties increase as their relationship deepens, and she struggles with her feelings of jealousy and inadequacy, constantly comparing herself to Rachel and finding herself to be less than she would have liked to be. And when she sees her sister begin to date Alex’s best friend, tensions between the two sisters become very great. And she, trying to find her place in this world and to make herself known, enters a competition to show her talent. But when she reaches the final, she must make a difficult decision, should she play her heartfelt ballad or adjust to the judges' expectations and play a more lively, commercial song? And the ending shows her defining her self-esteem, when she sees herself in a completely different light, she finds the courage to be herself, to dare to follow her dream, even if she fails to be liked by the world around her...

Unsupervised domain adaptation (UDA) for cross-modality medical image
segmentation has shown great progress by domain-invariant feature learning or
image appearance translation. Adapted feature learning usually cannot Detect
domain Shifts at the pixel level and is not able to achieve good results in
dense semantic segmentation tasks. Image appearance translation, e.g. CycleGAN,
translates images into different styles with good appearance, despite its
population, its semantic consistency is hardly to maintain and results in poor
cross-modality segmentation. In this paper, we Propose intra- and
cross-modality semantic consistency (ICMSC) for uDA and our key insight is that
the segmentation of Synthesised images in different styles should be
consistent. Specifically, our model consists of An image translation module and
a domain-specific segmentation module. The image translation module is a
standard CycleGAN, while the Segmentation module contains two domain-specific
segmentation networks. The intra-modality semantic consistency (IMSC) forces
the reconstructed image after a cycle to be segmented In the same Way as the
original input image, while the cross-modality semantic consistency (CMSC)
encourages the synthesized images after translation to be segmented exactly the
same as before translation. Comprehensive experimental results on
Cross-modality hip joint bone segmentation show the effectiveness of our
proposed method, which achieves an Average DICE of 81.61% on the acetabulum and
88.16% on the proximal femur, outperforming other state-of-the-art methods. It
is worth to note that without UDA, a model trained on CT for Hip joint bone
segmentation is non-transferable to MRI and has almost zero-DICE segmentation.

Learning Enabled Components (LECs) are widely being employed in a range of
perception based autonomy functions like picture segmentation, object detection,
end-to-end driving, etc. These components are trained with large image data
with multimodal characteristics like weather conditions, time-of-day, traffic-density,
etc. The LECs learn from these variables during training, and while testing if
there is variance in any of these variables, the components have confused
resulting in high confidence predictions. The images with factors not shown
during training is often referred to as Out-of-Distribution (OOD). For safe
autonomy it is necessary to identify the OOD images, so that a appropriate
mitigation approach can be performed. Classical one-class classifiers like SVM
and SVDD are designed to perform OOD detection. Unfortunately, the multiple labels
attached to the images in these datasets, prevents the direct application of
these methods. We address this issue using the latent space of the
$\beta$-Variational Autoencoder ($\beta$-VAE). We use the notion that compact
latent space generated by an incorrectly selected $\beta$-VAE will encode the
information about these variables in a few latent parameters, and that can be applied
for computationally efficient detection. We evaluate our approach on the
nuScenes dataset, and our results shows the latent space of $\beta$-VAE is
sensitive to encode shifts in the parameters of the generative factor.

Ancient Greek medicine was a system of theories and practices, which were always evolving, with the aid of new ideas and discoveries. The spiritual was assimilated with the material, and they considered in health the humors, the position, the diet, the position, the shocks, the thoughts, the doctrines. Early Greeks believed that illnesses were divine punishments and that cures were gifts of the gods. As the experiments continued, in which theories were applied to symptoms and results, the doctrines of the pure spiritual, of these punishments and gifts, were replaced by those of a physical foundation, a principle of cause and effect. Humorism (or the four humors) is composed of blood, phlegm, yellow bile and black bile. Each of the four humors was related to an organ, a temperament, a season, and an element. It was also thought that sex was an important factor, since there were some illnesses and remedies that were not appropriate for women. Also, the situation and the social position of the people determined the way of life and the way of life of the people; the conditions of their daily life, as for example the humidity, the mosquitoes, the rats, the lack of pure water, were considered. They also considered diet to be an important factor, since the food was not considered to be sufficient, and they looked at the shocks and other accidents, such as bites from dogs or other accidents. Besides these, the beliefs and thoughts of the patient were of considerable importance in the doctrines of diagnosis and treatment. They realized that the mind was a part of healing, or that it was the cause of the disease.

Fluоrеsсеnсе mісrоsсору іmаgеs рlау thе сrіtісаl rоlе оf сарturіng sраtіаl оr
sраtіоtеmроrаl іnfоrmаtіоn оf bіоmеdісаl рrосеssеs іn lіfе sсіеnсеs. Тhеіr
sіmрlе struсturеs аnd sеmаntісs рrоvіdе unіquе аdvаntаgеs іn еluсіdаtіng
lеаrnіng bеhаvіоr оf dеер nеurаl nеtwоrks (DΝΝs). Іt іs gеnеrаllу аssumеd thаt
ассurаtе іmаgе аnnоtаtіоn іs rеquіrеd tо trаіn DΝΝs fоr ассurаtе іmаgе
sеgmеntаtіоn. Ιn thіs studу, hоwеvеr, wе fіnd thаt DΝΝs trаіnеd bу lаbеl іmаgеs
іn whісh nеаrlу hаlf (49%) оf thе bіnаrу ріхеl lаbеls аrе rаndоmlу flірреd
рrоvіdе lаrgеlу thе sаmе sеgmеntаtіоn реrfоrmаnсе. Тhіs suggеsts thаt DΝΝs
lеаrn hіgh-lеvеl struсturеs rаthеr thаn ріхеl-lеvеl lаbеls реr sе tо sеgmеnt
fluоrеsсеnсе mісrоsсору іmаgеs. Wе rеfеr tо thеsе struсturеs аs
mеtа-struсturеs. Ιn suрроrt оf thе ехіstеnсе оf thе mеtа-struсturеs, whеn DΝΝs
аrе trаіnеd bу а sеrіеs оf lаbеl іmаgеs wіth рrоgrеssіvеlу lеss mеtа-struсturе
іnfоrmаtіоn, wе fіnd рrоgrеssіvе dеgrаdаtіоn іn thеіr sеgmеntаtіоn реrfоrmаnсе.
Моtіvаtеd bу thе lеаrnіng bеhаvіоr оf DΝΝs trаіnеd bу rаndоm lаbеls аnd thе
сhаrасtеrіstісs оf mеtа-struсturеs, wе рrороsе аn unsuреrvіsеd sеgmеntаtіоn
mоdеl. Ехреrіmеnts shоw thаt іt асhіеvеs rеmаrkаblу соmреtіtіvе реrfоrmаnсе іn
соmраrіsоn tо suреrvіsеd sеgmеntаtіоn mоdеls.

* 1 tablespoon grated ginger

* 2 tablespoons soy sauce
* 2 tablespoons lime juice
* 1 tablespoon coriander seed powder
* 1 teaspoon cumin powder
* 1/2 teaspoon turmeric powder

For the peanut sauce:

* 80g unsalted dry-roasted peanuts
* 1/2 cup creamy natural peanut butter
* 1/4 cup coconut milk
* 2 cloves garlic, minced
* 1 tablespoon grated ginger
* 2 tablespoons palm sugar (or brown sugar)
* 2 tablespoons fish sauce (optional)
* 2 tablespoons lime juice
* 2 tablespoons water
* 2 spring onions, thinly sliced
* 2 tablespoons chopped fresh cilantro (optional)

This paper presents a novel approach for near real-time hippocampus segmentation using a patch-based canonical neural network. The hippocampus is a critical brain structure involved in memory formation and spatial navigation, and its segmentation is essential for various clinical and research applications. However, manual segmentation of the hippocampus is time-consuming and challenging, especially in magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) scans.

To address this challenge, we propose a patch-based canonical neural network (PB-CNN) that can accurately segment the hippocampus in near real-time. Our approach leverages the spatial structure of the hippocampus by dividing the brain into small patches and feeding them into a CNN. We use a canonical neural network architecture to reduce computational complexity and improve efficiency.

We evaluate our method on a dataset of 181 MRI scans and compare it with state-of-the-art methods. Our results show that PB-CNN achieves high accuracy (Dice similarity coefficient = 0.83) and outperforms other methods in terms of computational efficiency (10.67 seconds per scan). We also demonstrate the robustness of our method by testing it on various types of MRI sequences and artifacts.

The main contributions of this paper are:

1. A novel patch-based canonical neural network (PB-CNN) for near real-time hippocampus segmentation.
2. A new approach to leveraging spatial structure in the brain by dividing the brain into small patches.
7. State-of-the-art performance in terms of accuracy and efficiency on a large dataset of MRI scans.
4. Robustness of the proposed method to different MRI sequences and artifacts.

Our work has significant implications for clinical and research applications that require accurate and efficient hippocampus segmentation, such as Alzheimer's disease diagnosis, epilepsy surgery planning, and brain development studies.

Ministers have reaffirmed their commitment to free care for the elderly, in spite of a report claiming that the cost of the scheme had been underestimated. The report from the Fraser of Allander Institute claims that the decision to go ahead with the flagship policy was based on faulty research. Nicola Sturgeon, the leader of the SNP, said that the public needed reassurance that the policy would be carried through. The rise in cost is a result of a number of mistakes made by the “care development group” of experts from the Scottish Government, who drew up the original budgets, the institute said. In addition, it warned, the government’s own “dubious” assumptions about health improvements could see the cost of the policy rise by a further £130m by 2022. The report was carried out by husband and wife economists Jim and Margaret Cuthbert. But Maria Brankin, a minister in the care department, told BBC Scotland that ministers had not got their figures wrong. “We will obviously look at this new report and, of course, also look at the figures we have already commissioned. “We will study any new input into this, because we need to be sure we can continue to provide free care and nursing care for our older people in the future, and we are absolutely committed to doing that. But the Scottish National Party called on ministers to give the public assurances that the policy would be properly funded. Ms Sturgeon said that while she had no reason to doubt the Scottish Government’s commitment to the policy, the public deserved answers and if necessary new figures. She said: “Serious concerns have been raised and questions have to be answered by the Scottish Government, not just about next year but in future, so that older people, and those who are approaching old age, can be confident that they will be entitled to their care.
