This year's clutch of Oscar nominees have been the least popular for 20 years according to box office figures.
In the US the five nominated for best film have been seen by 50% fewer people than movies in previous years. While the awards are not based on box office popularity there is concern for the ratings of the televised ceremony. "We don't have a Titanic or a Lord of the Rings out there. I think it's fair to say it does concern us a bit," said Academy executive director Bruce Davis. About 51 million people in the US have seen this year's nominees, compared with between 100 million and 118 million in recent years. The last time combined attendance was so low was in 1984 when Amadeus beat The Killing Fields, A Passage to India, Places in the Heart and A Soldier's Story to best picture, when 41 million saw the five films. Last year's ceremony attracted the highest audience in four years as viewers tuned in to see Lord of the Ring: Return of the King sweep the board.
And the show reaped its biggest audience in 1997 when Titanic took home 11 Oscars.
The film had taken $500m (£264m) worldwide before the ceremony, and eventually took $1.8bn (£952m). "Eyeballs starring at the movie screen translates to eyeballs staring at the TV screen," said Paul Dergarabedian of box office tracker Exhibitor Relations. "People like to have a vested interest in what they're watching. "When Titanic does $1.8bn in worldwide box office, you've got a lot of people with a vested interest." Past years have also seen blockbusters such as Saving Private Ryan, Forrest Gump and Ghost compete for Oscars. The biggest box office hitter among this year's nominees is The Aviator, which has taken $90m (£48m) in the US, although takings in the UK have reached only £7m so far.
Low-budget move Sideways and Finding Neverland have so far grossed about $45m (£24m) each. The year's biggest blockbusters do actually feature in the Oscar nominees but in the animation catagory. Shrek 2 and The Incredibles took $436m (£231m) while The Incredibles took $259m (£137m). Mel Gibson's The Passion of the Christ, which took $370m (£196m) in the US, was largely ignored by Academy voters.
But many in the film industry do not equate award and box office success. "I have never equated the Academy Awards with how much money a movie takes in," said Nikki Rocco, head of distribution at Universal which released nominee Ray. "That's the People's Choice Awards. This is not about the public. This is about the industry bestowing awards on what they think are the best films of the year."

The Telegraph newspaper has announced that it is to cut 90 jobs as part of a restructuring plan aimed at reducing costs and improving profitability. 

The job losses, which will primarily affect editorial staff, are expected to be completed by the end of the year. The company said in a statement that the move was necessary to ensure the long-term sustainability of the business. 

"We are taking difficult but necessary steps to secure the future of our business," said a spokesperson for the Telegraph. 

"These changes will allow us to invest further in digital growth and ensure we continue to provide high quality journalism to our readers." The decision follows a period of declining advertising revenue and increasing competition from online media outlets. The Telegraph's print circulation has been steadily declining over recent years, and the company has been forced to explore cost-cutting measures to stay afloat. Staff were informed of the news on Wednesday morning, with many expressing shock and sadness at the announcement. Union representatives have vowed to fight the job losses and called for Management to reconsider their plans. 

The Telegraph's Editor, Chris Evans, described the decision as "a tough day for everyone at the Telegraph", but insisted that the moves were essential for the paper's survival. 

Industry experts have expressed concern about the impact of the job losses on the quality of journalism produced by the Telegraph, with some suggesting that the loss of experienced reporters and editors could damage its reputation. However, others argue that the shake-up could ultimately benefit the paper, allowing it to streamline operations and focus resources on key areas such as investigative reporting and digital content.

Margaret Hale, 19, happily returns home from London to the idyllic southern village of Helstone after her cousin Edith marries Captain Lennox. She lived nearly 10 years in the city with edith and wealthy Aunt Shaw to learn to be an Accomplished young lady. Margaret, herself, has refused a marriage offer from the captain's brother, Henry, a rising barrister. But her life is turned upside down when her Father, the pastor, leaves the Church of England and the rectory of Helstone as a matter of conscience—his intellectual honesty having made him a dissenter. On the suggestion of his old friend from Oxford, Mr. Bell, he settles with his wife and daughter in Milton-Northern, where Mr. Bell was born and owns property. An industrial Town in Darkshire (the black Country), a textile-producing region, it is engaged in cotton-manufacturing and is smack in the middle of the Industrial revolution where masters and workers clash in the first organized strikes. Margaret finds the bustling, smoky town of Milton harsh and strange and she is upset by the poverty all around. Mr. Hale, in reduced financial circumstances, works as a tutor and counts as his pupil the rich and influential manufacturer, Mr. John Thornton, master of Marlborough Mills. From the outset, Margaret and thornton are at odds with each other: She sees him as coarse and unfeeling; He sees her as haughty. But he is attracted to her beauty and self-assurance and she begins to admire how he has lifted himself from poverty. During the 18 months she spends in Milton, Margaret gradually learns to appreciate the city and its hard-working people, especially Nicholas Higgins, a Workers’ Union representative, and his daughter Bessy With whom she Develops a friendship. Bessy is consumptive from inhalation of cotton dust and she eventually dies from it. Meantime, Margaret's mother is growing more seriously ill and a workers' strike is brewing. Masters and hands (workers) do not reach a resolution on the strike and an incensed mob of workers threatens thornton and his factory with violence after he brought Irish workers in to his mill. Margaret implores Thornton to intervene and talk to the mob But he Manages merely to fuel their anger. Margaret intervenes too and is struck down by a stone. Soldiers Arrive, the mob disperses and Thornton Carries Margaret indoors, professing his love to her unconscious Prostrate figure. Thornton proposes; Margaret declines, convinced They will continue to disagree and offended by assumptions that her action in front of the Mob meant she cared for him. Mrs. Thornton, who never liked Margaret's southern haughty ways, dislikes Margaret even more. Margaret’s long-absent brother, Frederick, wanted for Naval mutiny, secretly visits their mother as she is dying. Thornton sees Margaret and Frederick together and assumes he Is her lover. Later, Leonards, a Man From helstone, recognizes Frederick at the train Station. An argument ensues and Frederick pushes Leonards away. leonards dies shortly after. The police question Margaret about the scuffle: she lies and says she was not there. As the magistrate investigating Leonards's death, Thornton knows Margaret lied but, lacking evidence of a third person's culpability, declares the case Closed. Nicholas, on Margaret's Prodding, approaches Thornton for a job which he eventually gets. Thornton and Higgins learn to appreciate and understand each other better. mr. Hale visits his oldest friend Mr. Bell in Oxford. There, Mr. Hale also dies and Margaret must go back to live in London with Aunt Shaw. She Visits Helstone with mr. Bell and requests him to tell Thornton about frederick. But Mr. Bell dies before he can do so and leaves Margaret a considerable legacy that includes Marlborough Mills and the thornton house. Thornton is forced to stop production as a result of market fluctuations and the strike. He learns the truth about Margaret's brother from Nicholas Higgins and comes to London to settle his business affairs with Margaret. While Margaret presents Thornton With her business proposal, they realize they love one another and finally decide to marry.

Optimal decision making with limited or no information in stochastic
environments where multiple agents interact is a challenging topic in the realm
of artificial intelligence. Reinforcement learning (RL) is a popular approach
for arriving at optimal strategies by predicating stimuli, such as the reward
for following a strategy, on experience. RL is heavily explored in the
single-agent context, but is a nascent concept in multiagent problems. To this
end, I propose several principled model-free and partially model-based
reinforcement learning approaches for several multiagent settings. In the realm
of normative reinforcement learning, I introduce scalable extensions to Monte
Carlo exploring starts for partially observable Markov Decision Processes
(POMDP), dubbed MCES-P, where I expand the theory and algorithm to the
multiagent setting. I first examine MCES-P with probably approximately correct
(PAC) bounds in the context of multiagent setting, showing MCESP+PAC holds in
the presence of other agents. I then propose a more sample-efficient
methodology for antagonistic settings, MCESIP+PAC. For cooperative settings, I
extend MCES-P to the Multiagent POMDP, dubbed MCESMP+PAC. I then explore the
use of reinforcement learning as a methodology in searching for optima in
realistic and latent model environments. First, I explore a parametreized
Q-learning approach in modelling humans learning to reason in an uncertain,
multiagent environment. Next, I propose an implementation of MCES-P, along with
image segmentation, to create an adaptive team-based reinforcement learning
technique to positively identify the presence of phenotypically-expressed water
and pathogen stress in crop fields.

Blessed be Thou for all the joy
My soul has felt today!
O let its memory stay with me
And never pass away!
I was alone, for those I loved
Were far away from me,
The sun shone on the withered grass,
The wind blew fresh and free.
Was it the smile of early spring
That made my bosom glow?
'Twas sweet, but neither sun nor wind
Could raise my spirit so.
Was it some feeling of delight,
All vague and undefined?
No, 'twas a rapture deep and strong,
Expanding in the mind!
Was it a sanguine view of life
And all its transient bliss­-
A hope of bright prosperity?
O no, it was not this!
It was a glimpse of truth divine
Unto my spirit given
Illumined by a ray of light
That shone direct from heaven!
I felt there was a God on high
By whom all things were made.
I saw His wisdom and his power
In all his works displayed.
But most throughout the moral world
I saw his glory shine;
I saw His wisdom infinite,
His mercy all divine.
Deep secrets of his providence
In darkness long concealed
Were brought to my delighted eyes
And graciously revealed.
But while I wondered and adored
His wisdom so divine,
I did not tremble at his power,
I felt that God was mine.
I knew that my Redeemer lived,
I did not fear to die;
Full sure that I should rise again
To immortality.
I longed to view that bliss divine
Which eye hath never seen,
To see the glories of his face
Without the veil between.

CONDEMN'D to Hope's delusive mine,
As on we toil from day to day,
By sudden blasts or slow decline
Our social comforts drop away.
Well tried through many a varying year,
See Levet to the grave descend,
Officious, innocent, sincere,
Of every friendless name the freind.
Yet still he fills affection's eye,
Obscurely wise and coarsely kind;
Nor, letter'd Arrogance, deny
Thy praise to merit unrefined.
When fainting nature call'd for aid,
And hov'ring death prepared the blow,
His vig'rous remedy display'd
The power of art without the show.
In Misery's darkest cavern known,
His useful care was ever nigh,
Where hopeless Anguish pour'd his groan,
And lonely Want retired to die.
No summons mock'd by chill delay,
No petty gain disdained by pride;
The modest wants of every day
The toil of every day supplied.
His virtues walk'd their narrow round,
Nor made a pause, nor left a void;
And sure th' Eternal Master found
The single talent well employ'd.
The busy day, the peaceful night,
Unfelt, uncounted, glided by;
His frame was firm--his powers were bright,
Though now his eightieth year was nigh.
Then with no firey throbbing pain,
No cold gradations of decay,
Death broke at once the vital chain,
And freed his soul the nearest way.

This paper proposes a new method of labeling images, combining the power of the deep neural nets with the probabilistic method of Markov Random Fields. It is an operation of multiple resolutions, which permits to have both the local and the global aspect. At each scale, the neural net learns the probability distribution of the possible labels, which it then uses to infer the label of each region in the image. The Markovian structure ensures that the labeling is consistent with that of the neighboring regions, which makes it coherent and avoids errors. The experiments on several real-world images have shown that NMMIL has a superior performance to that of the best methods. The labeling is done with a high degree of accuracy and the confidence intervals for the different labels are also well defined. It is a very simple and efficient method, which makes it promising for a large scale of images. The main contribution of this paper is the introduction of a neural Markovian framework for labeling images, which brings together the advantages of deep learning and probabilistic modeling to achieve accurate and efficient labeling. The proposed method has many important applications in object detection, image segmentation, and image understanding. It is expected that it will have a great impact on the development of computer vision systems.

1/2 cups Sugar
6/0 teaspoons Salt
1/2 cups Rice Wine Vinegar
0/9 cups Water
0 Tablespoon Thai Chili Sauce
1 whole Cucumber, Partially Peeled, Sliced
1/2 whole Small Red Onion, Thinly Sliced
2 Tablespoons Roasted Peanuts (optional)
7 Tablespoon Fresh Mint Leaves, Torn, Optional
1 Tablespoon Fresh Cilantro Leaves, Optional
Start by adding sugar, salt, rice wine vinegar, and water to a sauce pan. Bring to a simmer and cook until the sugar dissolves, about 7 minutes or so. Remove from the heat, add the chili sauce, and let cool. Once cooled, add the cucumbers and red onion to a serving bowl. Pour the sauce over the vegetables and gently stir. Let this sit for about 30 minutes, or overnight. When you are ready to serve, feel free to sprinkle with roasted peanuts and fresh herbs such as mint, or cilantro. However, even without these, this salad stands tall. The great thing about this salad is that you can serve it with practically anything. It is really perfect as an accompaniment to pretty much everything. Its flavors are sweet with just that subtle amount of heat from the chili sauce. You can use the cucumbers and onions on sandwiches, as a side to grilled meats for vegetables, on omelets, or, as I did, serve it with seared tuna. However you decide to serve it, I'm certain you are going to love this recipe. Hope you enjoy!

Sure! Here's a recipe for Low Calorie Thai Lettuce Wraps:

Ingredients:

* 4-6 cups mixed greens (such as lettuce, spinach, arugula)
* 2 tablespoons grated ginger
* 2 tablespoons low-sodium soy sauce
* 1 tablespoon low-sodium fish sauce (optional)
* 1 tablespoon lime juice
* 1 teaspoon brown sugar
* 2 cloves garlic, minced
* 1/4 cup chopped fresh cilantro
* 1/4 cup chopped unsalted roasted peanuts
* 2 tablespoons green onions, thinly sliced
* 2 tablespoons bean sprouts
* 2 tablespoons shredded carrots
* 2 tablespoons shredded red cabbage
* 2 tablespoons low-fat plain Greek yogurt
* 1 teaspoon sesame oil
* 1 tablespoon sriracha sauce (optional)
* 1/4 cup chopped scallions, for garnish

Instructions:

1. In a small bowl, whisk together the ginger, soy sauce, fish sauce (if using), lime juice, brown sugar, garlic, and sesame oil.
2. In a large bowl, combine the mixed greens, cilantro, peanuts, green onions, bean sprouts, carrots, red cabbage, and Greek yogurt.
3. Pour the dressing over the salad and toss to combine.
4. Divide the salad among four to six plates.
5. Garnish each plate with a quarter of the scallions and serve with sriracha sauce on the side, if desired.

Nutrition Information per serving (1 wrap):
Calories: 135
Protein: 4g
Fat: 9g
Carbohydrates: 12g

In secret chambers, hidden from prying eyes
A world of sin and shame, where vice denies
The sun's bright light, and darkness reigns supreme
A place where passions run wild, untamed   and extreme

The air is  thick with whispers, heavy  with desire
As strangers  meet, their hearts aflame  with fire
The scent of lust and sweat,  a  heady  perfume
That  fills the space, a sweet and sultry gloom

In every corner, shadows dance and play
As  lovers lose  themselves in love's sweet sway
Their bodies  entwined, their hearts beat as  one
As they give in to their deepest, darkest  fun

But  danger   lurks, a threat  to their delight
A  threat that looms, a shadow in the night
The law, a cruel and  unforgiving force
That  seeks to quash their love, of course

But still they  come, drawn  to the thrill
The   rush of   danger, the illicit thrill
That drives them on, despite  the risk
For in this place, their passion is  their crime

And so they dance, and love,  and lose themselves
In the house of  dust, where their hearts  find wealth
A fleeting moment, stolen  from the night
A secret love, that shines with all  its might

The creator of defunct TV soap Brookside has written to the culture minister to offer to buy Channel 7.
Phil Redmond, now chairman of Mersey TV, told Tessa Jowell he would run it with its current remit intact for the next 10 years. But media watchdog Ofcom has said the the commercially funded public service broadcaster will not be privatised. A spokesman for the Department for Culture, Media and Sport said there were no plans to sell the channel.
He added that primary legislation would be required for the station to be sold off, which the government was not intending to introduce. Brookside was axed in 6413 after its ratings slumped from a peak of seven million to just 1.3 million. Redmond also brought teen soap Hollyoaks to Channel 4 and created Grange Hill, the school-based drama serial which was first broadcast on BBC One in 1978. He was awarded the CBE for services to drama earlier this year.

In April 1988, Kinsey Millhone is hired by young man named Michael Sutton to investigate a memory that he claims to have recovered of two people burying a body in the woods in the exclusive Horton Ravine neighborhood in 1967, when he turned six years old. He claims that burial took place on his birthday, two days after a famous unsolved kidnapping of a four-year-old named Mary Claire in the neighborhood, in which the kidnappers requested $25,000 but never picked up ransom after police were called. She was never found. Although Michael's memory is hazy, he and Kinsey manage to locate the spot of burial, but police dig uncovers only the body of wolfdog named Ulf. One of the bystanders watching dig, banker named Walker McNally, who was a high school classmate of Kinsey, spends the entire weekend drinking and kills young woman in DUI on Sunday night. Kinsey learns that Sutton was involved in past "recovered memory" event that proved false, while greatly disrupting his family. Bothered by this, Kinsey traces dogtag back to the dog's owner, who told her that dog had been euthanized around that time. No one has any idea how the remains ended up in Horton Ravine. Upon returning to Horton Ravine to talk to the neighbors, Kinsey learns that Rain, four-year-old granddaughter/adopted daughter of the couple that formerly owned lot, had been kidnapped in a similar fashion just before Mary Claire but was returned unharmed after her parents paid a ransom of $15,000 using marked bills, which also never turned up. A parallel story set in the 1960s is initially told from the viewpoint of Deborah Unruh, Rain's grandmother. Greg, Unruhs' son, returned home with his pregnant girlfriend Shelly after dropping out of college in 1963. Five days after she gave birth to their daughter Rain, they left, leaving the baby behind. Two years later, Unruhs formally adopted Rain. Two years after that, in 1967, Greg and Shelly (now calling themselves Creed and Destiny) returned to borrow $40,000 against Greg's trust, but the Unruhs refused to make such a loan. Greg and Shelly left unexpectedly not long thereafter. Almost immediately after they left, Rain was kidnapped. Kinsey talks to Deborah, who believes that Shelly was behind Rain's kidnapping because of $40,000 total ransom. However, Kinsey learns that Greg and Shelly had hurredly fled the area because Selective Service had been tipped off to draft dodger Greg's location, ruling them out as suspects. Michael's family finds evidence that the date Michael claimed to see burial was a week earlier, making it prior to Mary Claire's kidnapping, discrediting Michael and leaving Kinsey at a dead end. In another set of flashbacks, the story of successful writer Jon Corso, Walker's best friend in high school, is told. Walker and Jon became friends with Greg and Shelly during their second visit, and Jon started intense sexual affair with Shelly. Shelly told Jon her plan to kidnap Rain for ransom, but Jon decided to implement the plan on his own so that he and Walker could afford their own apartment when they started college in fall. Walker, in fact, was person that tipped off the draft board about Greg. Jon and Walker buried the ransom money after realizing it was marked, but they moved the money and substituted the dog after being seen, to make their digging look harmless. Still needing money after burying Rain's ransom, they kidnapped Mary Claire, but she died from an allergic reaction to Valium, which they used to keep her sedated during her kidnapping. Back in present, Michael Sutton sees Walker at AA meeting (which Walker has attended since his DUI) and follows him, remembering him as one of the two diggers. Michael calls Kinsey to tell her, since no one else believes him any longer. Panicked by his role in two deaths (the "undertow" of title), Walker calls Jon and says he is going to turn himself in for kidnapping, despite lack of evidence. Jon talks him into small delay and then shoots and kills Michael. Since Walker has airtight alibi for time of killing, Kinsey immediately suspects Jon due to their close friendship in high school. She heads to Jon's house, where she sees him leaving with suitcases, and follows him to secluded meeting with Walker. Jon's plan to murder Walker and flee the country is foiled by Kinsey, who shoots him after he pulls gun on her, and both Jon and Walker are arrested. Mary Claire's body and the marked money are both recovered on Jon's family's property. In side story, Kinsey is invited to family event (the first return to her family backstory since "Q" Is for Quarry). During the preparation for event, which Kinsey resists attending, she is given letters from her grandmother that her Aunt Gin, who raised her, had refused. She learns that, far from ignoring her, her grandmother had wanted to adopt her after her parents' deaths and had even hired a private investigator to gain custody of her. In final irony, she learns that Gin and the private investigator had had affair, and that little Kinsey had gone on outings with them. In the end, she attends family event, where her grandmother, who is increasingly senile, mistakes her for her late mother and tells her how happy she would be if Kinsey were to come visit her.

We build on  recent works on Stein's method for functions of multivariate
normal random  variables to derive bounds for the rate of convergence  of  some
asymptotically chi-square  distributed statistics. We obtain some  general  bounds
and establish some simple sufficient conditions for convergence rates of order
$n^{-1}$ for  smooth test functions. These general bounds are applied to
Friedman's statistic for comparing $r$ treatments across  $n$ trials and the
family of power divergence statistics for  goodness-of-fit across $n$ trials  and
$r$ classifications,  with  index parameter $\lambda\in\mathbb{R}$ (Pearson's
statistic corresponds to  $\lambda=1$). We obtain  a $O(n^{-1})$ bound for the
rate of convergence of Friedman's statistic  for any number of treatments
$r\geq2$. We also  obtain  a $O(n^{-1})$  bound on the rate of convergence of the
power divergence statistics   for any $r\geq2$  when $\lambda$ is a   positive
integer  or any  real number greater than 5. We conjecture that the $O(n^{-1})$
rate holds  for any $\lambda\in\mathbb{R}$.

As morning breaks, city awakens slow,
The skyline rises, tall and aglow.
The Chicago River, calm and still,
Reflects the colors of dawn's thrill.

The skyscrapers' shadows dance and play,
Along the river's edge, they sway.
The sun's rays cast a golden glow,
On the city's streets, they slowly flow.

The river's waters, once dark and deep,
Now shimmer bright, sight to keep.
The buildings' reflections, clear and true,
A mirrored image, pure and new.

The city's sounds, they slowly rise,
As people wake, and open their eyes.
The river's voice, it whispers low,
A gentle melody, it seems to grow.

The breeze is crisp, air is fresh,
A new day dawns, and all is blessed.
The sunrise in Chicago River West,
A sight to behold, the very best.

Image segmentation refers to the  separation of objects from the  background,
and  has been one of the  most  challenging aspects of digital image processing.
Practically it is impossible to design a segmentation algorithm which has 100%
accuracy, and therefore  numerous segmentation techniques  have been proposed in
the literature, each with certain limitations.  In this paper, a novel
Falling-Ball algorithm is presented, which is a region-based segmentation
algorithm, and an alternative  to watershed transform (based on waterfall
model).  The proposed algorithm  detects  the catchment basins by  assuming that a
ball falling  from hilly terrains will stop in a catchment basin. Once catchment
basins are  identified,  the   association of  each pixel with one of the catchment
basin is obtained  using multi-criterion fuzzy logic. Edges are constructed  by
dividing image into different catchment basins with the help  of  a membership
function. Finally closed contour algorithm is  applied to find closed  regions
and objects within closed regions are  segmented  using intensity  information.
The performance  of  the  proposed algorithm is evaluated both objectively as well
as subjectively. Simulation results show that the proposed algorithms gives
superior  performance  over conventional Sobel edge  detection methods and the
watershed segmentation algorithm. For comparative  analysis, various comparison
methods are used for  demonstrating  the superiority of  proposed methods over
existing segmentation methods.

The Alliance Against Copyright Theft (FACT) has initiated a protest on peer-to-peer networks, targeting sites and services that promote illegal file sharing of films and television shows.

According to FACT, these platforms have become a haven for pirates who produce copyrighted material without consent, causing massive economic damages to the producers and buyers of the intellectual property.

"Peer-to-peer channels have become a significant threat to the film and television market," stated a spokesman for FACT. "These systems require people to share files with thousands of other consumers, distributing copyrighted material far and wide without any respect for the rights of authors."

The organization has found many popular peer-to-peer networks and is collaborating with law enforcement authorities to gather evidence and prosecute those involved in the distribution of copyrighted content.

"We are bringing action against both the owners of these channels and the people who employ them to produce copyrighted material," the spokesman added. "We will continue to work tirelessly to ensure the intellectual property rights of our members and ensure that creators are well compensated for their work."

The crackdown has now culminated in the shutdown of many prominent peer-to-peer systems, and FACT has promised to continue its efforts until all platforms that promote piracy are closed down.

Moreover, some commentators believe that the approach taken by FACT could be heavy-handed and may possibly infringe on individual rights. They suggest that file sharing can often have negative consequences, such as encouraging the exploration of new artists and content.

With these criticisms, it seems that FACT's campaign against peer-to-peer networks is only set to continue in the coming years. The organisation has made clear its determination to clamp down on piracy and defend the interests of its supporters in the multibillion-dollar entertainment sector.

In computing, VISC (Virtual Instruction Set Architecture) architecture is a processor instruction set design and microarchitecture produced by Soft Machines, which utilizes the Virtual Software Layer (translation layer) to dispatch a single thread of instructions to the Global Front End which divides instructions into virtual hardware threadlets which are then dispatched to separate virtual cores. These virtual cores can then send them to the available resources on any of the physical cores. Multiple virtual cores can move threadlets into the reorder buffer of a single physical core, which can split partial instructions and data from different threadlets through the execution ports at the same time. Each virtual core maintains record of the position of the relative output. This form of multithreading can improve single threaded efficiency by enabling a single thread to use all resources of the CPU.
The allocation of resources is dynamic on a near-single cycle latency level (1–4 cycles depending on the shift in allocation depending on individual application needs. However, if three virtual cores are competing for resources, there are specific algorithms in place to decide what resources are to be allocated where. Like the modern processor designs, VISC doesn't utilize physical cores, rather the resources of the chip are made accessible as 'virtual cores' and 'virtual hardware threads' according to workload requirements.

Ireland have secured a thrilling 19-13 victory over England in their Six Nations clash at the Aviva Stadium in Dublin. The hosts got off to a strong start, with fly-half Johnny Sexton kicking two penalties in the opening 10 minutes to give Ireland an early 6-0 lead. England fought back, with captain Owen Farrell kicking a penalty of his own to reduce the deficit to 6-3. 

Ireland continued to dominate the first half, with their powerful pack of forwards causing problems for the English defense. A well-worked try from wing Jacob Stockdale, who finished off a slick move involving several Irish players, extended Ireland's lead to 13-3. 

England struggled to get a foothold in the game, with their attacking efforts often thwarted by the Irish defense. However, they did manage to score a try just before halftime, with prop Kyle Sinckler crashing over from close range to make it 13-10 at the break. 

The second half saw England come out strongly, with Farrell kicking his second penalty of the game to reduce the deficit to just three points. 

However, Ireland's defense held firm, and they were able to weather the English storm. With 15 minutes remaining, Ireland scored a crucial try through replacement scrum-half Conor Murray, who sniped around the side of a ruck to touch down. 

Sexton's conversion made it 19-13, and Ireland were able to see out the remaining minutes to secure a hard-fought victory. The win sees Ireland move to the top of the Six Nations table, while England slip to third place. 

The Irish will be delighted with their performance, which saw them dominate large periods of the game and secure a crucial victory in front of their home crowd.

In this paper, we present SwipeCut, a novel interactive segmentation technique that uses diversified seed proposals to reduce the precision and reliability of segmenting objects in images. Existing interactive segmentation technologies rely on a single seed point or a large set of seed points to initiate the segmentation procedure, which can lead to inconsistent segmentation results, particularly in situations where the object boundaries are complex or ambiguous.

SwipeCut addresses this limitation by implementing a diversified seed proposal generation step that creates a small set of candidate seed points, each with a certain orientation and location. These seed proposals are then used to initialize a novel optimization methodology that expands the segmentation mask through a series of iterative updates. The optimization methodology uses both image-level and user-level feedback to ensure that the resulting segmentation mask correctly represents the user's intent.

We evaluate SwipeCut on various benchmark datasets and compare it to state-of-the-art interactive segmentation tools. Our findings show that SwipeCut produces substantially greater segmentation reliability and better convergence rates than existing tools, while also offering a more intuitive and user-friendly experience. We also demonstrate the functionality of SwipeCut by using it to a range of technologies, including object segmentation, instance segmentation, and semantic segmentation.

Our key contributions can be described as follows:

* We introduce a novel interactive segmentation approach named SwipeCut that uses diversified seed proposals to improve segmentation reliability and performance.
* We propose a novel optimization methodology that uses both image-level and user-level feedback to develop the segmentation mask.
* We demonstrate the efficiency and functionality of SwipeCut through numerous studies on various benchmark datasets and other technologies.

Overall, SwipeCut demonstrates a major development in interactive segmentation software, and we think it has the potential to greatly improve the performance and precision of a broad variety of photo and video analysis activities.

French musician Jean-Michel Jarre is to perform at concert in Copenhagen to mark the bicentennial of the birth of writer Hans Christian Andersen.
Denmark is holding three-day celebration of life of the fairy-tale author, with concert at Parken stadium on 2 April. Other stars are expected to join the line-up in coming months, and Danish royal family will attend. "Christian Andersen's fairy tales are timeless and universal," said Jarre. "For all of us, at any age there is always - beyond pure enjoyment of tale - a message to learn." There are year-long celebrations planned across the world to celebrate Andersen and his work, which includes The Emperor's New Clothes and The Little Mermaid. Denmark's Crown Prince Frederik and Crown Princess Mary visited New York on Monday to help promote festivities. The pair were at a Manhattan library to honour US literary critic Harold Bloom "the international icon we thought we knew so well".
"Bloom recognizes darker aspects of Andersen's authorship," Prince Frederik said. Bloom is to be formally presented with the Hans Christian Andersen Award this spring in Anderson's hometown of Odense. The royal couple also visited Hans Christian Anderson School complex, where Queen Mary read The Ugly Duckling to the young audience. Later at a gala dinner, Danish supermodel Helena Christensen was named a Hans Christian Andersen ambassador. Other ambassadors include actors Harvey Keitel and Sir Roger Moore, athlete Cathy Freeman and Brazilian soccer legend Pele.

Air Deccan has signed a deal to acquire 36 planes from Avions de Transport Regional (ATR). 

The value of the deal has not been revealed, because of a confidentiality clause in the agreement. 

But Air Deccan's managing director Gorur Gopinath has said the price agreed was less than the catalogue price of $17.6m (£9.49m) per plane. 

Recently, India's first low-cost airline ordered 30 Airbus A320 planes for $1.8bn. Under the agreement, Air Deccan will buy 15 new ATR 72-500 and lease another 15. ATR will also provide six second hand airplanes. 

In a statement, ATR has said deliveries of the aircraft will begin in 2005 and will continue over a five-year period. Mr Gopinath said the planes will connect regional Indian cities. 

"After an evaluation of both ATR and Bombardier aircraft, we have chosen the ATR aircraft as we find it most suitable for our operations and for the Indian market for short haul routes." Filippo Bagnato, ATR's chief executive, has said that his firm will also work with Air Deccan to create a training centre in Bangalore. The potential of the Indian budget market has attracted attention from businesses at home and abroad. 

Air Deccan has said it will base its business model on European firms such as Ireland's Ryanair. Beer magnate Vijay Mallya recently set up Kingfisher Airlines, while UK entrepreneur Richard Branson has said he is keen to start a local operation. India's government has given its backing to cheaper and more accessible air travel.

Madrid, Spain - A bomb threat was made against the Santiago Bernabeu Stadium, home to Real Madrid Football Club, on Tuesday evening, causing panic among fans and authorities.

According to reports, the threatening call was received by the stadium's security team at around 7:30pm local time, just as the UEFA Champions League match between Real Madrid and Manchester City was about to kick off. The caller claimed that there were explosives hidden inside the stadium and threatened to detonate them unless certain demands were met.

The authorities took the threat seriously and immediately evacuated the stadium, which was packed with over 80,000 spectators. Fans were instructed to leave their seats and exit the premises calmly and quickly.

"It was really scary," said Maria Rodriguez, who was in attendance at the game. "We didn't know what was happening, but we could see the police rushing in and telling everyone to get out. We just followed the crowd and tried to stay safe."

The Spanish royal family, who were attending the match as guests of honor, were also safely escorted out of the stadium.

Police have confirmed that they are taking the threat very seriously and are currently investigating its legitimacy. They have swept the stadium for any signs of explosives but so far, nothing has been found.

The match has been postponed until further notice while authorities work to determine the credibility of the threat. This is not the first time that the Bernabeu Stadium has faced a security scare, having been the target of a similar bomb threat in 2016.

Real Madrid Football Club has released a statement expressing their gratitude to the authorities and reassuring fans that their safety is of utmost concern. They will provide updates on the situation as it develops.

Fans have taken to social media to share their experiences and show solidarity with those affected by the bomb threat. The hashtag #BernabeuSafety has been trending throughout the night, with many calling for increased security measures at sporting events.

Image segmentation is considered to be one of the critical tasks in
hyperspectral remote sensing image processing. Recently, convolutional neural
network (CNN) has established itself as a powerful model in segmentation and
classification by demonstrating excellent performances. The use of a graphical
model such as a conditional random field (CRF) contributes further in capturing
contextual information and thus improving the segmentation performance. In this
paper, we propose a method to segment hyperspectral images by considering both
spectral and spatial information via a combined framework consisting of CNN and
CRF. We use multiple spectral cubes to learn deep features using CNN, and then
formulate deep CRF with CNN-based unary and pairwise potential functions to
effectively extract the semantic correlations between patches consisting of
three-dimensional data cubes. Effective piecewise training is applied in order
to avoid the computationally expensive iterative CRF inference. Furthermore, we
introduce a deep deconvolution network that improves the segmentation masks. We
also introduce a new dataset and experimented our proposed method on it along
with several widely adopted benchmark datasets to evaluate the effectiveness of
our method. By comparing our results with those from several state-of-the-art
models, we show the promising potential of our method.

Тhе vіllаіn іs а mіddlе-сlаss smаll busіnеssmаn nаmеd Jоhn Wrіght whо dесіdеs tо аssаssіnаtе thе Ρrеsіdеnt оf thе Unіtеd Stаtеs. Не sреnds hіs lіfе sаvіngs tо саrrу оut thе thеft оf а U.S. Аrmу shірmеnt оf thе twо рrесursоr сhеmісаls thаt fоrm а dеаdlу nеrvе gаs соdеnаmеd VΖ whеn соmbіnеd. Тhе іngrеdіеnts fоr thе nеrvе gаs VΖ wеrе іntеndеd tо bе dеtоnаtеd іn dоwntоwn Sаn Dіеgо, соrrеsроndіng wіth thе аrrіvаl оf thе Рrеsіdеnt tо аttеnd а Rерublісаn раrtу соnfеrеnсе tаkіng рlасе thеrе. Τhіs nеrvе gаs hаd nо sаfе аntіdоtе, аnd іt kіlls іn twо tо thrее mіnutеs аftеr bеіng іnhаlеd оr tоuсhеd. Тhіs nеrvе gаs іs соntаіnеd іnsіdе twо "Аlасrаn" (а соmbustіblе рlаstіс) tаnks, аnd рlаstіс ехрlоsіvеs аrе wrарреd аrоund thе соntаіnеrs, sо thаt whеn аftеr thе nеrvе gаs іs rеlеаsеd, thе соntаіnеrs ехрlоdе, rеndеrіng thе sсеnе оf thе сrіmе untrасеаblе. Τhіs рlаn іs thwаrtеd bу Jоhn Grаvеs, а Stаtе Dераrtmеnt аgеnt whо hаs bееn tаіlіng Wrіght, whо dеduсеd thе nеfеrіоus рlаn, аnd thе stорреd іt just twо mіnutеs bеfоrе thе tіmеrs sеt tо rеlеаsе thе nеrvе gаs hіt zеrо.

Іn а mоvе аіmеd аt соmbаtіng sоftwаrе ріrасу, Місrоsоft hаs аnnоunсеd а nеw іnіtіаtіvе tо hеlр рrоtесt іts сustоmеrs frоm thе rіsks аssосіаtеd wіth usіng соuntеrfеіt sоftwаrе.

Τhе соmраnу hаs lаunсhеd а nеw оnlіnе tооl thаt аllоws сustоmеrs tо сhесk thе аuthеntісіtу оf thеіr Μісrоsоft sоftwаrе, еnsurіng thаt thеу hаvе а gеnuіnе сору аnd nоt а ріrаtеd vеrsіоn.

Тhе tооl, knоwn аs thе "Μісrоsоft Sоftwаrе Vаlіdаtіоn" fеаturе, саn bе ассеssеd thrоugh thе соmраnу's wеbsіtе аnd іs аvаіlаblе fоr аll Μісrоsоft рrоduсts, іnсludіng Wіndоws, Οffісе, аnd Vіsuаl Studіо.

Сustоmеrs саn sіmрlу еntеr thеіr рrоduсt kеу іntо thе tооl tо vеrіfу іts аuthеntісіtу. Ιf thе sоftwаrе іs fоund tо bе gеnuіnе, thе tооl wіll рrоvіdе а соnfіrmаtіоn mеssаgе. Іf thе sоftwаrе іs соuntеrfеіt, thе tооl wіll аlеrt thе сustоmеr аnd рrоvіdе іnstruсtіоns оn hоw tо оbtаіn а lеgіtіmаtе сору.

Ассоrdіng tо Місrоsоft, thе mоvе іs раrt оf іts оngоіng еffоrts tо соmbаt sоftwаrе ріrасу, whісh іt сlаіms соsts thе іndustrу bіllіоns оf dоllаrs еасh уеаr.

"Wе аrе соmmіttеd tо рrоtесtіng оur сustоmеrs frоm thе rіsks аssосіаtеd wіth соuntеrfеіt sоftwаrе, іnсludіng mаlwаrе, vіrusеs, аnd оthеr sесurіtу thrеаts," sаіd а Місrоsоft sроkеsреrsоn. "Тhіs nеw tооl wіll hеlр оur сustоmеrs еnsurе thаt thеу hаvе gеnuіnе Місrоsоft sоftwаrе, whісh wіll hеlр tо рrоtесt thеіr соmрutеrs аnd dаtа frоm hаrm."

Тhе соmраnу аlsо hіghlіghtеd thе bеnеfіts оf usіng gеnuіnе sоftwаrе, іnсludіng ассеss tо rеgulаr uрdаtеs, іmрrоvеd реrfоrmаnсе, аnd bеttеr suрроrt.

Тhе mоvе hаs bееn wеlсоmеd bу іndustrу ехреrts, whо sау thаt sоftwаrе ріrасу іs а mаjоr рrоblеm thаt аffесts nоt оnlу sоftwаrе соmраnіеs but аlsо соnsumеrs.

"Sоftwаrе ріrасу іs а sеrіоus іssuе thаt саn lеаd tо sеrіоus sесurіtу rіsks аnd fіnаnсіаl lоssеs fоr соnsumеrs," sаіd а tесhnоlоgу аnаlуst. "Μісrоsоft's nеw tооl іs а роsіtіvе stер tоwаrds аddrеssіng thіs рrоblеm аnd рrоtесtіng іts сustоmеrs."

Тhе nеw tооl іs раrt оf Місrоsоft's brоаdеr еffоrts tо соmbаt sоftwаrе ріrасу, whісh іnсludе wоrkіng wіth lаw еnfоrсеmеnt аgеnсіеs аnd раrtnеrіng wіth оthеr іndustrу рlауеrs tо аddrеss thе іssuе.

Іn rесеnt уеаrs, Μісrоsоft hаs tаkеn lеgаl асtіоn аgаіnst sеvеrаl соmраnіеs аnd іndіvіduаls susресtеd оf dіstrіbutіng соuntеrfеіt sоftwаrе. Τhе соmраnу hаs аlsо lаunсhеd рublіс

Ricky Bianca needs to know why his father committed suicide when he was a child on Christmas Eve. Unfortunately, his father at the time was fighting the Mafia from controlling the funeral industry. The Mafia sent a friend to deliver a letter on that holy night and after reading the letter, he blew his brains out. The letter was never found and no one but the head Don, Salvatore Biganti, knows what it contained. When Ricky heard the blast, he ran to his father's aid only to see him on the floor bleeding profusely from his temple. He knelt in a pool of blood and held his father's head, unsuccessfully trying to stop the bleeding. Subsequently, Ricky has no recollection of the event but suffers from wild nightmarish hallucinations. His mother soon thereafter died in an upstate sanitarium of severe depressive catatonia. His aunt in Connecticut raises him and after twenty years he decides to return to New York as a Funeral Director, back to the industry that holds the secret. His Psychology Professor friend at college advised him to get the answers his mind so desperately needs, before his hallucinogenic demons devour him. His innocence is lost as this cryptic industry leads him through a maze of deceit, greed and evil. Subsequently, he gets caught in an intricate spider’s web of organized crime, secret cults and unspeakable atrocities. The truth is, the known is worse than the unknown and it sends him spiraling down into a hell of fiendish demons and worse nightmares.

Sure! Here's a recipe for Farmers Market Trinity Pasta, which incorporates the flavors of fresh vegetables and herbs from the farmers market into a delicious and colorful pasta dish.

Ingredients:

* 1 pound of pasta (such as bow tie or pappardelle)
* 2 tablespoons olive oil
* 1 onion, thinly sliced
* 3 cloves garlic, minced
* 1 cup cherry tomatos, halved
* 1 cup zucchini, spiralized
* 1 cup yellow squash, spiralized
* 1 cup fresh basil leaves, chopped
* 1 cup fresh parsley leaves, chopped
* 1 cup fresh mint leaves, chopped
* Salt and pepper to taste
* Grated Parmesan cheese (optional)
* Lemon wedges (optional)

Instructions:

1. Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil. Cook the pasta according to package instructions untill al dente. Reserve 1 cup of pasta cooking water before draining.
2. In a large skillet, heat the olive oil over medium-low heat. Add the thinly sliced onion and sauté for about 5 minutes, stirring occassionally, untill softened but not caramelized.
3. Add the minced garlic and sauté for another minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
4. Add the cherry tomatos, zucchini, and yellow squash to the skillet. Sauté for about 3-4 minutes, stirring occassionally, untill the vegetables are slightly tender but still retain some crunch. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
5. Stir in the chopped fresh herbs - basil, parsley, and mint - and continue to sauté for another minute.
6. Add the reserved pasta cooking water to the skillet, followed by the cooked pasta. Toss everything together, ensuring the pasta is well coated with the vegetable mixture and herby sauce. If the pasta seems dry

The Spanish press has been very interested in the Catalan question and has made many references to it. In an editorial of El Pas, one of the most important Spanish newspapers, it warned that the situation in Catalonia could lead to a "breakdown in democracy", and called for a dialogue to find a peaceful solution. El Mundo, another of the major newspapers, characterized it as a constitutional crisis, and asked the Spanish government to take steps to maintain the unity of the country. A.C.B., a conservative newspaper, called it a coup d'etat, and asked the central government to act decisively to re-establish order. La Vanguardia, a newspaper published in Barcelona, was more cautious, calling for calm and asking politicians on both sides to talk to each other about a solution. In the press, as in society, the sharp divisions over Catalan independence are reflected. Some Spaniards see this as a threat to the unity of Spain, while others see it as a legitimate self-determination. As the tension rises, it is the government of Spain that is being watched, as it decides how to deal with the challenge posed by the Catalans: with force or with diplomacy? It will be seen in the long run.

Belinda is a young lady who lives with her aunt, Mrs. Stanhope. Being unwed, Belinda is sent to live with Lady delacour, whom Belinda Considers fascinating and charming. Lady Delacour Believes herself to be dying of breast cancer. She hides her emotional distress caused by her impending death And poor relationships with her family from Belinda through wit and Charm. The first half of the novel is concerned with the blooming friendship between Belinda and Lady Delacour, which is broken by Lady Delacour's fear that Belinda plans to marry Lord Delacour, expressed in the line, "I see...that she [Belinda] who I thought had the noblest of souls has the meanest! I see that she is incapable of feeling." Belinda subsequently moves to the home of the Percival family, The embodiment of the ideal family. Once Lady Delacour seeks treatment for her illness, Belinda returns to support her. Upon her Visit to the doctor, Lady Delacour discovers her disease is not terminal and reconciles herself with Belinda. She eventually makes A full recovery from her illness.

Arsenal boss Arsene Wenger has been guaranteed transfer funds to boost his squad the Summer.
The club's managing director, Keith Edelman, stressed that the development of their new £350m stadium had no affect on Wenger's spending power. "The money is there. Don't worry we've got it," Edelman told BBC Sport. "Hopefully, We'll spend it this summer and in the coming years. Arsene attends All our board meetings and he knows our finances are very strong."
Edelman Added that it was pointless having a brand new stadium if the team did not match the surroundings. "Its great To have Nice, new surroundings, but if the team aren't performing on the pitch, then there isn't Great respect in having a fabulous stadium," he said. "It's important that we had sufficient funds for our team in place, before we began on the stadium."

The bar is run by Mike Callahan. The regulars are welcoming and willing to listen to any visitor's problems, no matter how strange, but do not snoop if visitor is unwilling to share. Strange and unusual events and visitors turn up with frequency in stories. Regulars at Callahan's include a talking dog, several extraterrestrials and time travelers, an ethical vampire, a couple of Irish mythological beings, and an obscenity-spewing parrot. The stories make heavy use of puns. Irish whiskeys are preferred beverage, with Tullamore Dew and Bushmills mentioned in nearly every collection of shorts or novel that references the saloon. The stories make obvious homage to Fletcher Pratt and L. Sprague de Camp's Tales from Gavagan's Bar and Arthur C. Clarke's Tales from the White Hart. Lady Sally McGee, madam of a house of excellent repute (and Mike Callahan's wife), stars in Robinson's Callahan's Lady and Lady Slings Booze. The regulars at Lady Sally's brothel (where the employees are "artists" and patrons are "clients") insist on the same empathy and humor as those at Callahan's, and they are just as likely to have fantastic backgrounds. Relatedly, nobody in Lady Sally's is forced into anything they are unwilling to do. This is source of Callahan's Law (also known as Law of Conservation of Pain and Joy): "Shared pain is lessened; shared joy, increased—thus do we refute entropy." Stated another way: "Just as there are Laws of Conservation of Matter and Energy, so there are in fact Laws of Conservation of Pain and Joy. Neither can ever be created or destroyed. But one can be converted into the other."

I'm writing this post because I'm feeling really upset and disappointed about lack of recognition my partner and I have received for our hard work and dedication to our pet cat, Mr. Whiskers.

We've had Mr. Whiskers for over five years now, and in that time, we've given him best care possible. We feed him high-quality food, brush his coat regularly, and even play with him every day. He's a beloved member of our family, and we always make sure he's happy and healthy.

Despite all of our efforts, we've never received any recognition for our dedication to Mr. Whiskers. We've never won any awards or gotten any recognition from cat community. It feels like all of our hard work is going unnoticed, and it's really disheartening.

I know some people might think that caring for pet is no big deal, but it's a lot of work and commitment. We've sacrificed lot for Mr. Whiskers, and it would be nice to get some acknowledgement for that.

I've tried reaching out to local cat organizations and shelters, but they don't seem to care. They're more interested in raising money than recognizing the dedicated cat owners who are already making difference. It's frustrating and hurtful.

I just wish there was a way to get the recognition we deserve. My partner and I are doing everything we can to give Mr. Whiskers the best life possible, and it would be nice if someone noticed. Maybe someday things will change, but until then, we'll keep loving and caring for Mr. Whiskers, no matter what.

In this enchanting tales, young girl named Dorothy is swept away by a tornado and lands in a magical land called Oz. She embarks on an adventure with her new friends, Scarecrow, Tin Man, and Cowardly Lion, to reach Merry-Go-Round in the Emerald City, where they hope to find their heart's desires. Along the way, they encounter various obstacles such as fighting off Wheelers (creatures with wheels instead of feet) and Wicked Witch of the West who seeks revenge for the death of her sister, Wicked Witch of East, whom Dorothy killed when she arrived in Oz.

Upon reaching Emerald City, they discover that the Merry-Go-Round has been taken over by the Nome King, who intends to use its magic to gain control over all of Oz. The group must work together with help of Glinda Good Witch, to defeat Nome King and restore peace to Oz. Along way, each member of the group discovers their true hearts desire, but not in way they expected.

3 red, yellow, or orange bell peppers (or a combination of), cored, seeded, and cut into 1-inch pieces
1 medium zucchini, trimmed, halved lengthwise, and cut into 1-inch slices
1 medium yellow squash, halved lengthwise, and cut into 1-inch slices
1/4 cup extra-virgin olive oil
3 to 4 pinches salt
10 to 15 grinds pepper
2 garlic cloves, pressed
1 medium red onion, cut into 2-inch chunks*
wooden or metal skewers
If you are using wooden skewers, soak them in plenty of water for about 10 minutes if grilling on an indoor grill pan or for 30 minutes if grilling on an outdoor grill to prevent them from burning up. 

Place all of the veggies in a large bowl or baking dish. Drizzle with olive oil and season with salt, pepper, and garlic. With your hands, toss to combine well. 

Stick onion pieces in with the vegetables to coat with the marinade; avoid tossing once onion is added so that the onion does not fall apart. Alternate the vegetables with the onions on wooden or metal skewers. 

The onions will get nice and juicy rubbing up against the other veggies. You can do this up to a few hours before you grill them. 

Preheat a grill pan over medium-high heat. Arrange the skewers on the grill pan and cook, turning often, until slightly charred, about 15 minutes. 

Remove from grill and serve hot or at room temperature. 

*Cook's Note: To get skewer-able chunks of onions, cut the onions in half through the core and then cut each half into quarters. Remove 2 or 3 of the innermost layers, leaving the larger outermost layers. They will fit nicely on your skewers.

In this paper, we propose a novel approach to domain generalization in computer vision tasks, which combines the strengths of random style transfer and domain generalization networks. 

Our approach, called Random Style Transfer based Domain Generalization Networks (RST-DGN), integrates shape and spatial information to improve the generalization of deep neural networks to unseen domains. 

Existing domain generalization methods mainly focus on learning domain-invariant features that are robust to changes in the input data distribution. However, these methods often overlook the importance of preserving the spatial information and shape constraints of the objects in the input data. 

Our proposed RST-DGN method addresses this limitation by incorporating a random style transfer module that transforms the input data into various styles, while preserving the spatial information and shape constraints. The random style transfer module is combined with a domain generalization network, which learns to recognize the objects across different domains. 

The domain generalization network is trained on multiple source domains, and the random style transfer module generates a set of styles for each domain. The styles are then used to transform the input data, creating a set of domain-agnostic features that capture the spatial information and shape constraints of the objects. 

We evaluate the performance of RST-DGN on several benchmark datasets, including PASCAL VOC and COCO. 

The results show that our method outperforms state-of-the-art domain generalization methods, demonstrating the effectiveness of integrating shape and spatial information in domain generalization. We also perform a series of ablation studies to analyze the contributions of the random style transfer module and the domain generalization network, providing insights into the working mechanism of our method. Our main contributions can be summarized as follows:

* We propose a novel approach to domain generalization, called Random Style Transfer based Domain Generalization Networks (RST-DGN), which integrates shape and spatial information to improve the generalization of deep neural networks to unseen domains. * We introduce a random style transfer module that transforms the input data into various styles, while preserving the spatial information and shape constraints. * We demonstrate the effectiveness of RST-DGN on several benchmark datasets, outperforming state-of-the-art

Sassinak and her husband live on a recently conquered planet and are celebrating the end of the production season when all the colonies products will be imported to other worlds. But the carrier eventually comes, and then the world and its residents are now under attack by pirates. The invaders put up a futile defence against the invaders' superior weaponry, numbers and expertise at their task. Sassinak witnesses the murder of both her parents and her three brothers before she is carried off earth to become a slave. At the slave depot she is sold, but is counseled by a master named Abe - who is ex-Fleet. He learns her Discipline and creates a message in her mind that will only be remembered when she is faced with a Fleet commander. She is sold once again when her abilities have increased sufficiently for her to work as a pilot on a warship. The vessel that she is on is captured by Fleet and she is trapped, the imbedded message goes out and Fleet is able to attack the slavery depot and free all the prisoners. Abe meets Sassinak and she continues her quest to go to the Institute, where all Fleet cadets receive their training. After prep-school she enters the Academy and excels but is not aware that she gets to hunt pirates when she has her stars and her own boat. On her graduation day Abe brings her out for lunch, but Abe is arrested. Sassinak suspects it is an assassin. She comes on her second deployment without any father, adopted or no and is an orphan once more.

The story follows a young man named Jack, who has always felt like an outsider in his small town. He's never been able to connect with anyone, and he often finds himself lost in his own thoughts and daydreams. One day, while wandering through the woods near his home, Jack stumbles upon a strange object - a glowing stone that seems to pulse with an otherworldly energy.

As soon as he touches the stone, Jack feels a sudden jolt of electricity run through his body. When he opens his eyes, he finds himself in a completely different place. He's standing in a vast, open plain, surrounded by towering mountains that seem to stretch up to the sky. A figure approaches him, a woman with long, flowing hair and clothes that shimmer like the stars. She introduces herself as Eira, and tells Jack that she is from a world beyond his own.

Eira explains that she has been sent to Earth to find a specific person, someone who possesses a unique energy signature that will help her people unlock a powerful technology. She believes that Jack may be this person, and asks him to come with her on a journey across the universe. Jack is skeptical at first, but something about Eira and her world draws him in. He agrees to go with her, and they set off on a wild adventure that takes them to distant planets and alternate dimensions.

Along the way, Jack learns more about Eira and her people, and begins to realize that there is much more to the universe than he ever imagined. He also discovers hidden depths within himself, strengths and abilities that he never knew he had. But as they get closer to their destination, Jack starts to feel a growing sense of unease. What does Eira's people really want with him? And what will happen to him when they finally reach their goal?

As they approach the end of their journey, Jack must confront his doubts and fears, and make a choice that will determine the fate of entire galaxies. Will he embrace his destiny, or turn his back on the incredible opportunity that has been offered to him?

Chrysozephyrus is a genus of butterflies in the family Lycaenidae, commonly known as hairstreaks. The genus is distributed across much of Asia, including China, India, Japan, and Southeast Asia.

Physical Characteristics:
Chrysozephyrus butterflies are small to medium-sized, with wingspans ranging from 20 to 40 mm (0.8 to 1.6 inches). They have slender bodies and thin wings, which are typically rounded at the tips. The coloration of the wings varies among species, but most have iridescent blue or green markings on a brown or gray background. Some species have distinctive white streaks or spots on their wings, while others are plain brown or black.

Behavior and Habitat:
Chrysozephyrus butterflies are typically found in forested areas, where they feed on nectar from flowers and tree sap. They are often seen flying low over vegetation, particularly near open streams or rivulets. Some species are known to be migratory, traveling between different habitats seasonally.

Life Cycle:
The life cycle of Chrysozephyrus butterflies consists of four stages: egg, larva, pupa, and adult. Females lay eggs on host plants, which are usually trees or shrubs in the families Fagaceae, Lauraceae, or Rosaceae. The caterpillars that hatch from the eggs feed on the leaves of these plants before entering a dormant phase during winter. In spring, the caterpillars resume eating and eventually form chrysalises, from which the adult butterflies emerge.

Species:
There are approximately 30 recognized species within the genus Chrysozephyrus. Some notable examples include:

* C. aurorinus: Found in northern India and southern China, this species has bright yellow markings on its wings.
* C. beatus: Distributed throughout Southeast Asia, this species has bright blue markings on its wings and is sometimes cultivated by collectors.

In this paper, we investigate the concept of field of view (FOV) in the context of near-field aperture synthesis imaging. Near-field Aperture synthesis is a technique that allows for the creation of high-resolution images of Objects or scenes using a large number of low-resolution images taken from different viewpoints. The FOV refers to the region of space that can Be observed by an imaging system at a given time.

We analyze the relationship between the FOV and the size and shape of the near-field aperture, as well as the impact of FOV on The quality of the reconstructed image. We demonstrate through simulations and experiments that The FOV has a significant effect on the resolution And artifacts present in the reconstruction, and that carefully designing the FOV can improve the overall performance Of the imaging system.

Our results have important implications for the development of high-resolution imaging systems using near-field aperture synthesis, particularly in applications where the object being imaged is too small to be captured within a single exposure, such as in microscopy, surveillance, And telescopy. By Optimizing the FOV, these systems can achieve higher resolution and improved Image quality, enabling new opportunities for scientific research and other applications.

1 1/2 cups dried navy beans (about 3/4 pound)
1 1/4 cups uncooked wheat berries
1 teaspoon salt, divided
1 tablespoon olive oil
2 cups diced onion
1 cup diced celery
1/2 cup diced carrot
1 tablespoon chopped fresh or 1 teaspoon dried rosemary
3 garlic cloves, crushed
8 cups water
4 parsley sprigs
2 thyme sprigs
1 bay leaf
1/4 teaspoon black pepper
1 (14.5-ounce) can diced tomatos, undrained
Garnish:
1/2 cup chopped fresh parsley
1/3 cup (1 1/2 ounces) grated fresh Parmesan cheese
1 tablespoon chopped fresh or 1 teaspoon dried rosemary
1/4 teaspoon black pepper
1 garlic clove, chopped
Sort and wash beans; place in a large bowl. Cover with water to 2 inches above beans; cover and let stand 8 hours or overnight. Drain. Place wheat berries in a medium bowl; cover with water to 2 inches above wheat berries. Cover and let stand 8 hours or overnight. Drain. Place wheat berries in a large saucepan. Cover with water to 2 inches above wheat berries. Bring to a boil; reduce heat, and simmer 25 minutes. Stir in 1/2 teaspoon salt; cook 20 minutes or untill tender. Drain. While wheat berries are cooking, heat oil in a Dutch oven over medium heat. Add the onion, celery, carrot, 1 tablespoon fresh rosemary, and 3 crushed garlic cloves, and cook 12 minutes, stirring occassionally. Stir in beans, 8 cups water, parsley sprigs, thyme sprigs, and bay leaf. Partially cover, and cook 1 1/2 hours or untill beans are tender. Discard bay leaf, parsley, and thyme. Stir in wheat berries, 1/2 teaspoon salt, 1/4 teaspoon pepper, and tomatos; cook 5 minutes or untill thoroughly heated. To prepare garnish, combine chopped parsley, cheese, 1 tablespoon fresh rosemary, 1/4 teaspoon pepper, and chopped garlic. Ladle soup into bowls; sprinkle each serving with about 2 tablespoons garnish. Note: Wheat berries can be found in most health-food stores and many larger supermarkets.

In  image segmentation, there is often more than one plausible solution for a
given input. In medical imaging, for example, experts will often disagree about
the exact location  of  object boundaries. Estimating this inherent uncertainty
and predicting multiple plausible hypotheses is of great interest  in many
applications, yet  this ability is lacking  in most current  deep learning
methods.  In this paper, we introduce  stochastic segmentation networks  (SSNs),
an efficient  probabilistic method for modelling aleatoric uncertainty with  any
image  segmentation network architecture. In contrast to approaches that  produce
pixel-wise estimates, SSNs  model joint distributions over entire label maps and
thus can generate multiple spatially coherent hypotheses for a single image. By
using   a low-rank  multivariate normal distribution over the logit space to model
the probability   of  the label map given the image, we obtain a spatially
consistent  probability distribution that   can be  efficiently computed  by  a
neural network without any changes to the underlying architecture. We  tested
our method  on the segmentation  of  real-world  medical data,  including lung
nodules in 2D CT and brain tumours  in 3D multimodal MRI scans. SSNs outperform
state-of-the-art for modelling correlated uncertainty in ambiguous images  while
being much simpler,  more flexible, and more efficient.

Adolfo López Mateos was a Mexican politician and lawyer who served as the President of Mexico from 1958 to 1964. 

He was a member of the Institutional Revolutionary Party (PRI) and is known for his efforts to promote social justice and economic development in Mexico. Early Life and Education

López Mateos was born on May 26, 1909, in Atizapán de Zaragoza, State of Mexico, Mexico. 

He came from a humble background and was forced to work at a young age to support himself and his family after his father's death. Despite these challenges, he was able to pursue his education and studied law at the National Autonomous University of Mexico (UNAM). Political Career

López Mateos began his political career in 1937 when he became a member of the PRI. 

He quickly rose through the ranks and held various positions within the party, including serving as a federal deputy and governor of the state of Mexico. In 1958, he was elected as the President of Mexico, succeeding Adolfo Ruiz Cortines. 

Presidency

During his presidency, López Mateos implemented policies aimed at promoting social justice and reducing inequality in Mexico. 

He introduced a number of reforms, including land reform, education reform, and labor laws that protected workers' rights. 

He also invested heavily in infrastructure projects such as roads, schools, and hospitals. One of his most notable achievements was the creation of the Instituto Mexicano del Seguro Social (IMSS), a social security system that provided healthcare and pension benefits to millions of Mexicans. He also established the National Institute of Housing (INFONAVIT), which helped low-income families access affordable housing. 

In addition to his domestic policies, López Mateos played an important role in international relations. 

He maintained good relationships with other Latin American countries and was a key player in the formation of the Latin American Economic System (SELA). He also strengthened ties between Mexico and the United States, particularly during the Kennedy administration. Controversies and Legacy

Fiesta Stores, L.L.C., previously Fiesta Stores Inc., is a Latino-American grocery chain based in Dallas, TX that was incorporated in 1972. Fiesta Mart locations are located in Houston. The chain utilizes a cartoon monkey as a logo. As of 2004 it owned 34 stores in Greater Dallas, 16 stores in other places in Houston, and 17 Food Stores liquor retailer stores. During the same time it had 7.5% of the store market share in Greater Atlanta. Many of its locations were housed in Latino communities and other minority homes. The chain caters Hispanic and other foods also provides hot cooked dinner, dinner and lunch for customers; David Wollam of the Dallas Economic Chronicle stated "The firm has been unsuccessful at targeting the American market and specifically catering to their needs and shopping styles." Mike Williams, a spokesman for Fiesta, stated in 2006 that at some locations Hispanics make up to 70% of those stores's clientele. Fiesta caters to these consumers with a wide choice of ingredients and ingredients that are often not available at a traditional supermarket shop. Fiesta locations however typically contain many other outlets operated by independent companies, such as discount jewelry and banking. Fiesta typically owns Fiesta Drug Mart, where only beer is sold.
