SUMMONED - The film recounts the true story of the attempt of the Soviet Union in 1961 not to fall behind the United States in the cold war. The United States had just launched the Polaris class of atomic submarines, and the K-19 was an old Russian submarine equipped with atomic equipment. The Soviet crew had to go to the Arctic and test-fire an ICBM. The Americans watched the test from the side, as part of their own system of surveillance. It was relatively close to a NATO station and was to demonstrate that the Soviets had reached the same level as the United States in the cold war. The film starts with Captain Mikhail Polenin (Liam Neeson) conducting the crew through the same rehearsal. But then the engine room begins to have a problem, as in the rehearsals before. Polenin says they're not ready for the test yet, but instead of listening to him, the KGB, the Soviet military command, sends him a new captain, Alexei Vostrikov (Harrison Ford). Vostrikov is a tough guy, which does not make it easier for him to be accepted by the crew of the K-19, which was familiar with Polenin's humor. In addition, Vostrikov is not without a suspicious reputation; many suspect that he is only in his position through the influence of his family, although his father was a military man who had gone to the gulag. After several ominous signals, the submersible finally goes to the Arctic, and Vostrikov tries to get his crew in a serious and responsible position to be able to carry out their task and return home. But as the introductory scene has shown, the K-19 has a problem with its atomic power plant. This is the bulk of the movie: the solution of the problem and the consequences of this solution. The film is incredibly exciting and emotional, and it's a wonder that it's so tense when you think that it's essentially just a drama about a piece of machinery. Pealos (Benedict Stanczak and Christy Nezval) can be seen, but the movie also provides a subtle analysis of bureaucratic and bureaucratic hierarchies. It is also a tribute to the true heroism and sacrifice of the crew of the K-19, who for many years could not tell their story because of the ban imposed on them by the Soviet authorities. Ford is simply brilliant in the role; at first he succeeds in getting the viewer to hate him, even though he understands his actions, then he gradually develops the character until we finally sympathize with him, but after a certain skepticism that lingers through most of the central part of the film, we finally support him against the bureaucrats who do not understand the decisions taken because they were not there. Even without this, the movie is worth seeing because of the great performances of Ford and Neeson, and because of the development of a whole group of dynamic personalities, the inevitable disagreements and suspicions, the will to act despite the difficulties and the relationship to higher orders. Probably every organization where many people, whether those involved or not, were involved in a similar task and task, and where many thought the project could not be completed, could be seen. Often we see it in filmmaking, too. There are studios that want to start shooting for a specific premiere, maybe for other reasons, the beginning of shooting is already set up, but maybe the script is not ready yet, or maybe the villain is not known, or some other things could happen. Vostryokov is like a movie director who has to deliver a movie in time, even if the deadline is not there. and that he is trying to keep the team together and at least the beginning of the shoot is not interrupted, especially when the pressure rises. It is probably a good thing that the movie is not a project of a fusion reactor. The more literal political tensions that are being created later in the film are also interesting, but the humanizing character and the universality of the story make the K-19 such a painful film. The historically fascinating play, the brilliant direction, the good music, the sound design and the other technical parts make the K-19 a ten.

Shаrеs іn Vіrgіn Вluе, Аustrаlіа's sесоnd-lаrgеst аіrlіnе, hаvе suffеrеd а drаmаtіс 20% drор іn vаluе аftеr thе соmраnу аnnоunсеd а shосk рrоfіt wаrnіng.

Іn а stаtеmеnt tо thе Аustrаlіаn Stосk Ехсhаngе, Vіrgіn Вluе rеvеаlеd thаt іt hаd ехреrіеnсеd а sіgnіfісаnt dесlіnе іn dеmаnd fоr іts sеrvісеs, раrtісulаrlу оn іts kеу dоmеstіс rоutеs. Τhе соmраnу сіtеd іnсrеаsеd соmреtіtіоn аnd есоnоmіс unсеrtаіntу аs kеу fасtоrs соntrіbutіng tо thе dоwnturn.

Τhе аnnоunсеmеnt саmе аs а surрrіsе tо іnvеstоrs, whо hаd bееn ехресtіng а strоng реrfоrmаnсе frоm thе аіrlіnе fоllоwіng rесеnt ехраnsіоn еffоrts аnd роsіtіvе fіnаnсіаl rеsults. Ιnstеаd, Vіrgіn Βluе wаrnеd thаt іt ехресts tо rероrt а fіrst-hаlf lоss оf uр tо ΑU$30 mіllіоn (USD $21 mіllіоn), соmраrеd wіth а рrоfіt оf ΑU$18 mіllіоn (USD $12 mіllіоn) fоr thе sаmе реrіоd lаst уеаr.

Сhіеf Ехесutіvе Οffісеr Раul Sсurrаh dеsсrіbеd thе сurrеnt mаrkеt соndіtіоns аs "сhаllеngіng" аnd асknоwlеdgеd thаt thе соmраnу wаs fасіng "hеаdwіnds." Не аddеd thаt mаnаgеmеnt wоuld bе tаkіng stерs tо аddrеss thе sіtuаtіоn, іnсludіng rеduсіng сарасіtу аnd іmрlеmеntіng соst-сuttіng mеаsurеs.

Τhе рrоfіt wаrnіng hаs sраrkеd соnсеrns аbоut thе lоng-tеrm hеаlth оf thе аvіаtіоn іndustrу, whісh hаs bееn buffеtеd bу rіsіng fuеl соsts, glоbаl есоnоmіс unсеrtаіntу, аnd іnсrеаsіng соmреtіtіоn frоm lоw-соst саrrіеrs. Аnаlуsts аrе рrеdісtіng furthеr turbulеnсе аhеаd fоr Vіrgіn Βluе аnd оthеr аіrlіnеs ореrаtіng іn thе rеgіоn.

Shаrеs іn Vіrgіn Вluе fеll shаrрlу іn еаrlу trаdіng, dоwn 20% tо АU$1.45 (USD $0.97) реr shаrе. Оthеr аіrlіnе stосks wеrе аlsо аffесtеd, wіth Qаntаs, Αustrаlіа's lаrgеst саrrіеr, fаllіng 3%.

Vіrgіn Βluе's suddеn rеvеrsаl оf fоrtunе hаs lеft mаnу wоndеrіng whаt wеnt wrоng. Sоmе аnаlуsts роіnt tо оvеrсарасіtу іn thе mаrkеt, whіlе оthеrs suggеst thаt thе аіrlіnе mау hаvе undеrеstіmаtеd thе іmрасt оf nеw рlауеrs іn thе sесtоr. Whаtеvеr thе rеаsоns, іt іs сlеаr thаt Vіrgіn Вluе fасеs аn uрhіll bаttlе tо rеgаіn аltіtudе.

Darrick e. Antell is an American physician, entrepreneur, and pioneer in the field of longevity medicine. He is best known for his work on the clinical application of telomere lengthening and its impact on human health and aging.

Early Life and Education

Antell was born in 1964 In New York City. He received his undergraduate degree from Columbia University and went on To earn His medical degree From the Mount Sinai School of Medicine. After completing his residency in internal medicine At the Hospital of the University of pennsylvania, he began his career as a practicing physician in New York City.

Career

In the early 2000s, Antell became interested in the study of telomeres, the protective caps on the ends of chromosomes that shorten with age. He recognized the potential for telomere lengthening to revolutionize the field of medicine and set out To develop a clinical application for this technology.

In 2007, Antell founded Telocyte, a biotechnology company Focused on developing therapies to slow, halt, or reverse the Effects of aging by lengthening telomeres. The company's flagship product, Telo-1, is a proprietary blend of nutraceuticals and pharmaceuticals designed to Support telomere health and promote telomerase activity.

research and Contributions

Antell has been a leading Voice in the field of Longevity medicine, publishing numerous papers and speaking internationally on the topic of telomere lengthening and its implications for human health. His research has shown that longer telomeres are associated with better health outcomes, including reduced risk of cardiovascular disease, cancer, and dementia.

In addition to his work at Telocyte, Antell has also developed a number of other anti-aging technologies, including a line of skincare products containing telomerase activators and a dietary supplement aimed at supporting immune function.

I'm sitting here feeling absolutely devastated. I didn't walk away when I should have, and now I've lost all my money. It's gone. Vanished. I can't believe it.

I was in a situation where I had the chance to walk away and cut my losses, but I didn't take it. I thought things would turn around, that it would get better. But they didn't. They only got worse. And now I'm left with nothing.

I keep thinking about what could've been if I had just walked away when I had the chance. I would still have my money, my savings, my security.But instead, I let my hopes and dreams cloud my judgement and I ended up losing everything.

It's hard to admit, but I think I might be in a bit of a depression. I don't know how to move on from this. I feel so ashamed and stupid for letting this happen.I'm not sure how to pick myself up and start over.

Has anyone else ever felt like this? Any advice or words of encouragement would be greatly appreciated.

I recently watched Terminator 2: Judgment Day, and let me tell you, it's real thrill ride! The special effects are mind-blowing, especially considering it was released in 1991. The liquid metal T-1000 is still mesmerizing to this day. The storyline is engaging and suspenseful; you'll be on edge wondering what will happen next.
The action scenes are intense and well choreographed, with plenty of explosions and car chases to keep you entertained. And let's not forget about Arnold Schwarzenegger - he delivers his signature deadpan lines with perfect comedic timing. The dynamic between him and Edward Furlong is great too, adding some warmth and humor to balance out all the sci-fi action. Yes, some moments feel dated, like the now ridiculous-looking CGI, but overall, this sequel holds up magnificently nearly thirty years later. Highly recommended. 4.5 stars from me!

Dear fellow Redditors,

I've been talking a lot recently about the inability to remain obsessed over anything. You know, that feeling when you just can't pull anything out of your head, and you spend weeks, sometimes hours, absorbed by it?

For some women, it's a hobby or a passion project. They'll spend each waking moment talking about it, experimenting on it, perfecting it. And that's amazing! Having a passion can be an incredible idea.

But for people, it's not so healthy. They'll become obsessed over a woman, a relationship, or just a perceived slight. They'll spend all their day dwelling on it, replaying conversations in their head, examining each detail. It's like they're trapped in a never-ending loop, and they can't escape.

I've been on both sides of this coin. I've had hobbies that I've been passionately obsessed with, and I've always had relationships that I've been unhealthily fascinated with. And let me remind you, it's not funny.

When you're in the throes of passion, it's difficult to forget the harm it's doing to your physical and physical health. You're so absorbed by the stuff you're obsessed with that you forget to take care of yourself. You stop doing things that bring you joy, you stop spending time with friends and family, and you stop taking care of your mental health.

But the worst part is when you realize that you're not in control. You feel that you're at the mercy of your obsession, and you don't know how to stop it. It's that there's this voice in your head that won't shut up, and it's reminding you that you have to keep talking about this moment, that you have to keep working on it, that you have to keep studying it.

It's only when you hit rock bottom that you realize you need help. And that's a tough place to be. It's

I've been friends with this person for years, but lately, I've been questioning whether or not it's healthy to continue the friendship. We used to have so much in common and would spend hours talking about our shared interests. But over time, things have changed. 

They've started to become increasingly negative and critical, always finding fault in everything and everyone. It's like they're constantly looking for something to complain about. At first, I thought maybe they were just going through a tough time, but it's been months now and it doesn't seem to be getting any better. 

Every conversation we have ends up leaving me feeling drained and frustrated. 

I find myself avoiding them more and more because I don't want to deal with their negativity. 

I'm starting to realize that this friendship might not be good for me anymore. I deserve to surround myself with people who uplift and support me, not bring me down. 

But at the same time, I feel guilty for even considering ending the friendship. We've been through so much together and I value the memories we've shared. Has anyone else ever had to make a difficult decision like this? 

How did you handle it? Should I try to talk to my friend about how their behavior is affecting me, or should I just distance myself from them gradually? 

I'm really struggling with this and could use some advice.

In times of plenty, we forgot to pray
For rain to fall and crops to sway
But now, as drought and hardship stay
We plead for mercy, night and day

Our fields lay barren, dry and still
Our rivers shrink, their waters chill
The earth cries out for relief
But still we wait, our faith unsealed

We've lost our way, our priorities
Chasing wealth and endless energy
We've ignored the earth's sweet song
And now we face the consequences long

But in this time of strife and need
We turn to faith, our souls to feed
We pray for rain, for life to thrive
For an end to this recession's strive

So let us come together, hand in hand
In search of rain, a brighter land
For in the end, we'll find a way
For rain to fall, and a brighter day.

Deep in the soul, a fire burns, A flame that licks, a wound that seethes, A scar that never entirely heals, A pain that never entirely forgets. It is the burden of a word, a term, a remark, A stigma that brings shame and fire, A cross that is worn day and night, a shadow that follows, a constant struggle. It is the sting of the ostracized, the pain of the ostracized, the bitterness of the discriminated, the stench of the prejudices, the voice of despair, the echo of the indifferent society. Eyes that turn, heads that shake, whispers that are thrown, rumors that are made, obstacles that arise, doors that close, chances that don't come, smells that know. It is the story that is buried, the story that is extinguished, the voices that whisper, the memories that are darkened, the pain that is passed on, the pain that is shared, the scar that is never entirely scarred, the wound that is never entirely healed. It is the masks we wear, the roles we play, the parts we choose, the scripts we recite, the beliefs that bind, the stereotypes that bind, the boxes where we are deposited, the labels we find. It is the color of the skin, the form of the hair, the shape of the eye, the sound of the gaze, the parts that are, the sum of parts, the beauty that is seen, the beauty that is spoiled. It is the road we take, the path we choose, The prejudices we bear, the shame we bear, the scorn we bear, the journey we take, the path we choose.

Sure! Here is a recipe for smoked beets, inspired by Jamie Oliver:

Ingredients:

* 4 medium-sized beetroot (beets)
* 1 red onion, peeled and thinly sliced
* 2 cloves of garlic, minced
* 1 cup of white wine vinegar
* 1 cup of water
* 1 tablespoon of brown sugar
* Salt and pepper to taste
* 1 teaspoon of smoked paprika (optional)
* Fresh parsley or thyme leaves for garnish

Instructions:

1. Preheat your oven to 350°F (180°C).
2. Wrap the beets in foil and place them on a baking sheet. Drizzle with a little bit of oil and season with salt and pepper. Roast the beets in the preheated oven for about an hour, or until they are tender when pierced with a fork.
3. While the beets are roasting, prepare the pickling liquid. In a large saucepan, combine the red onion, garlic, white wine vinegar, water, brown sugar, salt, and pepper. Bring the mixture to a boil over high heat, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for about 10 minutes.
4. Once the beets have finished roasting, remove them from the oven and let them cool slightly. Peel the skin off the beets and slice them into wedges or thick rounds.
5. Add the sliced beets to the pickling liquid and bring the mixture back up to a simmer. Reduce the heat to low and let the beets cook in the pickling liquid for about 10-15 minutes, or until they are heated through.
6. Taste the pickling liquid and adjust the seasoning as needed. You can add more sugar, acidity, or spices to suit your taste.
7. Remove the beets from the pickling liquid and transfer them to a serving plate. Garnish with fresh parsley or thyme

Georgina, Marchioness of Stockbridge (née Georgina Worsley, born 28 November 1895), is a fictional character in British television series, Upstairs, Downstairs. She was portrayed by Lesley-Anne Down.
 
Georgina Worsley arrives to live at Eaton Place in 1913. She is the step-daughter of Lady Marjorie's brother Hugo Talbot-Carey (the new Earl of Southwold). His new wife is the widow Marion Worsely, and mother of Georgina by her previous marriage. Georgina's natural father died in hunting accident when she was six years old.  Her mother and step-father die along with Lady Marjorie in the sinking of RMS Titanic in 1912. After that she moves into 165 Eaton Place right before Christmas in December 1913.  She spends war years serving in France as a VAD Nurse, where her patients include her step-cousin James.  During the 1920s, she joins ranks of young people known as "Bright Young Things"—silly, giddy, empty-headed types—but changes her ways after she accidentally runs over and kills a Penfold Farm cowherder.  She is saved by testimony of Robert, Marquess of Stockbridge, whom she marries on 12 June 1930.

The abstract of an academic journal gives a brief description of the study conducted, describing its important contributions and results. Here is a possible abstract for the journal called "Control and Monotonicity for Some Discretizations of the Biot's Model":

In this paper, we investigate the reliability and monotonicity properties of different discretizations of the Biot's model, which explains the interactions of linear elastic waves in heterogeneous media. We consider several mathematical techniques, including finite difference, finite element, and spectral techniques, and examine their behavior under various parameter regimes. Our findings show that many equations exhibit flexibility and monotonicity, while others do not, especially when the underlying continuous problem enjoys these properties. We often require numerical references to explain our scientific results and consider the possibilities for practical applications. Our work has significant implications for the realistic model of seismic wave propagation, as well as other issues relating partial differential equations with non-separable variables.

The sacking of Nanking, also known as the Nanjing Massacre, was a tragic event in which Japanese soldiers killed tens of thousands of civilians and prisoners of war in Nanjing, China during World War II. It is considered one of the worst atrocities of the war and has had a profound impact on Sino-Japanese relations. 

However, it is not accurate to say that the sacking of Nanking led directly to the creation of anime. While the event certainly had far-reaching consequences for Japan and its relationship with China, it did not have a direct causal link to the development of anime. 

Anime, short for "animation," is a style of Japanese animation that emerged in the 1960s and has since become a popular form of entertainment around the world. 

Its roots can be traced back to earlier forms of Japanese animation, such as manga (comics) and traditional Japanese art styles like ukiyo-e (woodblock prints). In fact, some of the earliest anime films were produced in the 1940s and 1950s, well before the Nanjing Massacre took place. 

For example, the first anime feature film, "The Night Before Christmas," was released in 1937, and other early anime works include "Astro Boy" (1963) and "Speed Racer" (1967). So while the sacking of Nanking was a significant historical event, it is not responsible for the creation of anime. Instead, anime developed over time through a combination of cultural influences, technological advancements, and the creative efforts of Japanese artists and animators.

A secret recipe for spelt dough for making bread, biscuits, and so on. 1 cup of warm water 2 teaspoons of yeast 1 tablespoon of olive oil 1 teaspoon salt 4 cups of spelt flour. Set the yeast in the water for 5 to 10 minutes, till it has a foamy appearance. Add the oil, salt and 2 cups of flour. Stir until a dough forms. Add the other 2 cups of spelt flour, a cup at a time, till the dough is elastic and smooth. It may not need all the flour, so gradually add the remaining flour and stop when the dough feels right. Turn the dough out on to a floured board, and knead it for about 10 minutes, till it is smooth and elastic. You can use a standing mixer fitted with a hook. Cover the bowl with a damp cloth and let it rise for a couple of hours in a warm, draft-free place. ... Preheat the oven to 450°F. Take the risen dough, knead it again and shape it into a round or oval loaf, and put it on a baking sheet covered with parchment paper. Then bake the bread for 35 to 40 minutes, till it is golden brown and a little hollow when you knock the bottom. Let it cool for about 20 minutes on a wire rack before you slice it. You can also use the spelt dough for making a biscuit dough, if you prefer.

Jim Peters (1918-2003) was a British athlete who is best known for his achievements in the sport of racewalking. Born on September 24, 1918, in London, England, Peters began competing in athletics at a young age and quickly discovered his talent for racewalking.

Peters's career highlight came at the 1956 Melbourne Olympics, where he won gold medals in both the men's 20 km and 50 km racewalk events. He also set world records in these events, with times of 1:32:44 and 4:12:00, respectively. These records were significant becasue they displaced those set by legendary Swedish walker, Lennart Skoglund, and cemented Peters as one of the greatest racewalkers in history. In addition to his Olympic successes, Peters three times won the annual London to Brighton walk (in 1950, 1952, and 1953), completing the challenging course in under five hours each time - an impressive feat given that it covered over 54 miles and included hilly sections.

Despite his considerable skills, Peters faced numerous challenges during and after his racing days. His training regimen often involved running up to ten miles every other day plus working out strenuously five alternative afternoons per week - which included weight lifting exercises for strength - while being employed fulltime doing physically demanding work as an electrician. This packed schedule took tolls on him mentally; eventually leading to depression caused mainly by burnout when competition pressure mounted too high when combined with personal obligations such as supporting family members back home through monetary means if possible. Furthermore, upon retiring from sports due to declining health issues related not only directly but indirectly (as result of medication used treat said conditions). As consequence thereof he developed alcoholism which increased steadily throughout later years untill 1970 s when dealth threatened seriously enough prompt hospitalisation followed shortly afterwards treatment period consisting primarily group therapy sessions plus support of loved ones present everywhere throughout experiance - especially wife Hazel. I throw my gun in the back of my truck
With it I  hope to kill a 12-point buck
While in  the woods, BANG! I see the deer fall
I take him home, freeze the meat, and mount his horns  on my wall
I grab my pole and tackle box and head to the lake
At this time in the morning, I feel barely   awake
There is  no  school today, I’m  glad there  is no  class
A mighty tug  on  my  line, I hook a large  mouth  bass
There is nothing like hunting; waiting for the kill
Cutting and cleaning the meat my freezer I’ll soon  fill
Deer  steaks and deer jerky have such a great  taste
And with his head  on my wall nothing goes   to waste
I  like fishing, fishing is fun
Fishing underneath the rising  sun
I like catching the fish and putting  them  in   a net
Fish is  a great dish when the table is set
My truck  is unstoppable;  it can’t beat
I slam the door and strap myself in the seat
I start the engine  and  press the gas to  the floor
My truck takes off and my engine lets out a roar
I wouldn’t be  able to hunt or  fish if it wasn’t for  my truck
With  it  I carry poles,   guns, and my fallen buck
I pull  my boat with my truck in four-wheel-drive
At my destination I always arrive

The book tells the story of a relationship that develops between Henry Kent, a sociopath and fantasist who preys on lonely rich women, and Daisy Langrishe, an ageing novelist with two broken marriages behind her. After meeting Daisy – who has recently bought a cottage in order to start a new life in the country – Henry quickly falls in love with her, and sets about tricking his way into her confidence. He initially offers to become her gardener – something she reluctantly accepts – then later begins to correspond with her after she suffers an accident during a prolonged trip abroad. These letters start as run of the mill pieces, but as he perceives that she is taking an interest in him, Henry begins to weave her a series of elabourate stories about his life – all designed to gain her attention and win her affection. When Daisy eventually returns home and Henry makes himself indispensable to her after she suffers a fall, they begin an affair. But when Daisy's family and friends learn about the nature of the relationship, they become concerned and start to investigate Henry. However, they soon begin to fear that the facts they unearth about his past might have come to light too late to save Daisy from harm.

The film opens with Bernard, a former Catholic priest, accompanied by his father Jack, who had just arrived in Honolulu, at the request of his aunt Ursula, who was dying of cancer. The day after their arrival, Jack is hit by a car and is sent to the hospital. Bernard is much more often at Ursula's house than at his father's, and this is where he begins to learn the past of the two. Ursula, the black sheep of the family, was abused as a child by her older brother, Sean, a dead war hero, and the one the family venerated as a hero. Ursula explains to Bernard that it was this experience that had ruined her marriage and her life, and she wants Jack to apologize to her because he knew about the fact and kept silent about it. In the meantime, Bernard begins a secret relationship with Yolanda Miller, the one who drove the car that ran over his father. Bernard's growing sexuality and Ursula's fight with her disease move side by side. We move between the third person, Bernard's diary, a long letter from Bernard to Yolanda, and postcards and notes sent from England to various people Bernard and Jack had met during the journey from England, finally ending with a letter from Yolanda to Bernard. Not to be confused with the novel of the same name by B. of Lescault.

Bijan and Manijeh (also Bizan and Manijeh) is one of the love stories of Ferdowsi’s Shahnameh. Bijan is the son of Givah, a famous warrior of Kaykhu king of Pars in the third century, and Rostam-e-Saltane, the daughter of Rostam-e-Gazal. He falls in love with Manijeh, daughter of Afrasiab, the King of Turan and the greatest enemy of the Persians. In the name of religion, he is willing to sacrifice his life for Manijeh. The story of his sacrifice and the persistence of Manijeh has been quoted by other poets. In the first part of this tragedy, Kaykhoroon, King of Persia, hears from the Armenians that the wild boars are eating the Armenian fields, and the brave Bijan fights them, and he drives them back to the den. The next day, Gorjen, the Persian knight who accompanied Bijan on this expedition, describes the gardens of Afrasiab to him, and he is attracted to go to the Caspian Sea to the mythical Turanian country, which is on the northern shore of the Caspian Sea, and Afrasiab, the wicked and selfish king. In the gardens of Afrasiab, he meets Manijeh, the beautiful daughter of Afrasiab. They fall in love with each other, but since she knows her father's opinion, she makes him fall asleep and brings him into her house. Bijan wakes up from the sleep and her maids take him to her room and hide him for a few days. Then Afrasiab learns about him and captures him.

The abstract of the academic paper titled "Detection of   hidden  structures  on  all scales in amorphous materials and complex  physical  systems: basic notions and applications to networks, lattice systems, and glasses" could be something like this:

In this paper, we present a  novel approach for  detecting hidden structures on multiple scales in amorphous  materials and complex physical systems. Our methodology is based  on the   idea that these systems often exhibit subtle patterns and correlations that  can be uncovered by analyzing their   spatial and temporal organization. By combining advanced statistical  techniques and computational methods, we are able to identify and  characterize a   wide  range of   hidden  structures, from local clusters and networks to  long-range order and scaling behaviors.

We  demonstrate the power and versatility  of  our approach by  applying  it to several paradigmatic systems, including networks, lattice systems, and  glasses. In each case, we  uncover novel  insights into  the underlying structure and behavior of  these systems, shedding light on their emergent properties and response to external  stimuli. Our findings have important  implications for the design and optimization of complex materials and  systems, and open up new avenues for understanding the intricate patterns and processes that govern their behavior.

Our work  has the  potential to  significantly impact a broad range of fields,  from materials science and physics to biology, computer science, and engineering. By providing a  powerful tool for detecting hidden  structures on  all scales, we enable researchers  to gain a  deeper understanding of complex systems and  develop new strategies for controlling  their properties and behavior.  Ultimately, our efforts aim to contribute  to the  development of a more  comprehensive and predictive understanding of complex systems, with far-reaching implications for   science, technology, and society.

The moon rises over the vibrant downtown, casting a soft glow over the imposing buildings and busy neighborhoods. Amidst the chaos, a young girl named Maggie wakes up in her tiny bedroom, stretching and yawning as she continues her morning. She has no notion that tomorrow will be different from any other, that it will be a night that changes her life forever.

Emily heads to her work at a coffee store, weaving through the crowded streets and avoiding pedestrians as she goes. She exchanges pleasantries with her colleagues and continues her shift, spilling tea and boiling cream with practiced ease. The afternoon rush comes and comes, and Claire gets a time to herself, drinking a mug of tea and checking her cell.

That's when she gets it - a text from an unknown number, received just a few hours later. It's a quick message, telling her to meet someone at a nearby park at night. Emily is suddenly filled with a feeling of dread, but her curiosity gets the better of her. She wants to meet the strange stranger, her heart racing as she wonders who it might be.

As the clock strikes 12, Emily makes her way to the park, her hands sweating and her mind racing. She scans the crowded plaza, looking for a familiar face. That's when she sees him - a tall, beautiful guy with piercing green eyes and a charming grin. He waves at her, and Emma gets a shock of recognition. She doesn't recognize him, but there's someone about him that feels familiar.

The boy identifies himself as Peter, and they sit down on a bench together. They chat for minutes, getting to know each other and sharing stories about their lives. Olivia is touched by how little they have in common, how difficult it is to talk to Daniel. They laugh and giggle, and for a time, Rose thinks about the rest of the world.

As the sun starts to set, Alex takes Lily's hand and tells her the truth. He's not just a stranger, he's her soul

Gordon Brown has delivered a rousing speech to Labour's spring conference setting out the government's agenda for the next general election. The chancellor said he was proud of his party's record on the economy, and would strive for continuing stability if elected. 

The Gateshead conference was told he would help young people who were struggling to buy their own homes. 

And the chancellor vowed to continue the fight against child poverty. Later, Prime Minister Tony Blair will answer questions sent by the public via text and e-mail. 

Analysing Mr Brown's position before the speech, BBC correspondent James Hardy said Mr Brown would draw "sharp dividing lines" with the Conservatives for the forthcoming election campaign. He would contrast Labour's plan to invest £60bn in services with a Tory plan to cut spending by £35bn. "Mr Brown will lay out his credentials as a reforming chancellor determined to take on and beat the Asian tiger economies which increasingly dominate world trade," our correspondent said. 

On Friday night, Mr Brown confirmed he would not make any tax commitments until the Labour manifesto had been published after the Budget, expected in March. 

But commentators will listen to his speech closely for hints on whether, as the Conservatives claim, he plans to raise tax after the election. 

The Tories accuse Labour of raising taxes 66 times since coming to power in 1997. 

Following the chancellor's keynote speech, the prime minister will face interactive questioning from ordinary voters on Saturday. 

Mr Blair is thought to be deliberately putting himself on the line in a bid to engage the electorate ahead of an expected May election. 

Capital Radio DJ Margherita Taylor will select questions to put to him from thousands e-mailed and sent by text. The prime minister's enthusiasm for the job remains undimmed, Alan Milburn, Labour's election strategist told Radio 4's Today programme on Saturday. Mr Blair has "the same passion and the same commitments for the job" as when Labour came to power in 1997, he said. And he confirmed Mr Blair's insistence that no poll date had yet been set. He said: "I'm the General Election co-ordinator and I don't know, and Tony has not made up his mind." On Friday, the prime minister completed a whistle-stop tour of England, during which he unveiled his party's six pre-election pledges. 

Starting in London, he visited marginal constituencies pledging to build on what he said were Labour's achievements on the economy, crime, education and public services. The Conservatives and Lib Dems said the pledges - set to underpin Labour's election campaign - were "worthless".

Daniel Boone (also known as  Peter Lee Stirling, born Peter Charles Green; 31 July 1942, in Birmingham, England) is an English pop musician who  became a one-hit wonder in the United States with  the single "Beautiful Sunday" in 1972. The song  was written by  Boone and Rod McQueen and sold over 2,000,000  copies worldwide. It peaked at #15  on The Billboard Hot 100  singles chart at the end of the summer  of 1972, having already reached  #21 on  the  UK  Singles  Chart earlier during that  same year. In  1972,   Boone  was the recipient of the "Most Likeable Singer" award from Rolling Stone magazine.  Early career  as   Peter Lee Stirling and/or The Bruisers
Peter Green (later to become Peter  Lee Stirling) started  his career as  the  guitarist and vocalist  with a  band  called the Beachcombers that played gigs in the Birmingham area during the  period from 1958 to 1962. Their fortunes changed when they encountered  Tommy Bruce, who had a number 3 hit in 1960 with "Ain't Misbehavin'". This, and some subsequent releases,  had been attributed to 'Tommy Bruce and the Bruisers', despite the  fact that  the instrumental backings were provided by session musicians at EMI.   Bruce's account  of the  meeting  was as follows:

Hi there. Recently I was in a snowboarding accident resulting in a broken neck and ultimately surgery to fuse my C6 and C7. I am filing from North Carolina with Prudential short term disability and am concerned my benefits, which were already approved for my obvious impairment, will be revoked once they see in my medical reports that I admitted to partaking in amphetamines earlier that week. I was not on drugs when I had my accident but the hospital put it in my medical notes my admittance. Could anybody offer any advice on what rights I have regarding my benefits or what I could do to ensure I receive payment while out of my normal full-time work routine and in recovery post-op? I have no prior drug impairments just on a vacation where drugs were present and was on a bunch of hospital-induced morphine for pain when I relayed this information so wasn’t able to respond in a way that would protect my future at both my workplace of 5 years and my home of 4 years. Please and thank you for your time

Although it is widely reported that the title refers to the sound of urine tinkling in a chamber pot, this is a later Joycean embellishment, lending an earthiness to a title first suggested by his brother Stanislaus and which Joyce (by the time of publication) had come to dislike: "The reason I dislike Chamber Music as a title is that it is too complacent", he admitted to Arthur Symons in 1906. "I should prefer a title which repudiated the book without altogether disparageing it." Richard Ellmann reports (from a 1949 conversation with Eva Joyce) that the chamberpot connotation has its origin in a visit he made, accompanied by Oliver Gogarty, to a young widow named Jenny in May 1904. The three of them drank porter while Joyce read manuscript versions of the poems aloud - and, at one point, Jenny retreated behind a screen to make use of a chamber pot. Gogarty commented, "There's a critic for you!". When Joyce later told this story to Stanislaus, his brother agreed that it was a "favourable omen". In Ulysses, Leopold Bloom reflects, "Chamber music. Could make a pun on that." In fact, the poetry of Chamber Music is not in the least bawdy, nor reminiscent of the sound of tinkling urine. Although the poems did not sell well (fewer than half of the original print run of 500 had been sold in the first year), they received some critical acclaim. Ezra Pound admired the "delicate temperament" of these early poems, while Yeats described "I hear an army charging upon the land" as "a technical and emotional masterpiece". In 1909, Joyce wrote to his wife, "When I wrote [Chamber Music], I was a lonely boy, walking about by myself at night and thinking that one day a girl would love me."

Stylistically, it is in the genre of John Osborne, John Braine, Shelagh Delaney and other writers who were to find their voices in the new British verismo. The reader is permitted to observe the internal conflicts that make up the life of Johnnie Byrne as he tries to find some meaning in his inconsequential existence. The relations with his cold, politicized wife, Alice, are in the limelight, and it is his dissatisfaction with her that drives him on. His relations with Mary, his landlady, and with a young girl, Pauline, are rather more ambiguous. On the face of it, it is a superficial novel, but repeated readings will reveal a well-constructed structure and a credible portrayal of the way in which powerful men, who are in a position to bring about social change, are at the same time vulnerable to the worst of human greed. Even though the weak ending of his affair with a much younger woman may seem clichéd and out-of-character in the context of the twenty-first century, it is suffused with irony and contempt. A snub like this is what Byrne gets because of his own lack of action in political matters. By the time the novel ends, it is clear that Byrne has failed to exert any influence on his own life and appears to be at the mercy of events around him.

I've been struggling with sleep issues for a while now, and I'm wondering if my sleep apnea or deviated septum could be preventing melatonin from keeping me asleep.

I've been taking melatonin supplements for a few months now, and while they do help me fall asleep faster, I still wake up multiple times throughout the night. I've tried different dosages and timings, but nothing seems to work.

I've read that sleep apnea and deviated septum can cause sleep disruptions, but I'm not sure if they're the culprit in my case. Has anyone else experienced this?

I've tried other sleep aids like white noise machines and earplugs, but they haven't made a significant difference. I'm at a loss for what to try next.

Any advice or insight would be greatly appreciated. Thanks in advance!

Parang zoom meetings feel awkward din, saglint lng and like its kinda different din like having the vibe of someone working beside you and making chikka. Its awkward just inviting people na "hey magzoom tayo para lng may presensya" and it doesnt feel the same. Anyone who can give advice on how to handle this scenario? I guess its the feeling of uneasiness din sa new role, new company and before pandemic, hadn't realize the value of having people starting with you.

1 (7 ounce) can chipotle chiles in adobo (I'd reduce this considerably and taste and test while adding more or it will blow your eyes out)
2 teaspoons lime juice
12 cup mayonnaise
14 cup finely chopped cilantro
kosher salt
fresh ground black pepper
vegetable oil (2 quarts or enough to have a 3-inch depth in pot)
1 lime, juice of
2 cups water
1 cup all-purpose flour
1 cup panko breadcrumbs
1 avocado
kosher salt
fresh ground black pepper
lime wedge
Place chipotles with adobo sauce and lime juice in a blender and puree until smooth; set aside. Place mayonnaise in a medium bowl. Whisk in 1/3 of chipotle mixture at a time, tasting between each addition for spiciness (you may not want to use all of chipotle puree). Add cilantro and season with salt and pepper. Cover with plastic wrap and refrigerate until ready to serve. Heat vegetable oil in a large pot over high heat until instant read thermometer reaches 350 degrees. While oil is heating up, place all-purpose flour, lime juice mixed with 2 cups water and panko breadcrumbs in 3 separate shallow dishes. Cut avocado in quarters lengthwise. Remove wedges from pit and peel off skin. Cut each quarter in half so you have 8 wedges total. Season with salt and pepper. Dredge avocado wedges in flour, then dip into lime water, then dredge through breadcrumbs. Make sure each slice is completely coated. Once oil has reached 350 degrees, fry breaded slices for about 3 minutes, or until lightly browned. Remove avocados from oil using a slotted spoon and place on a paper towel-lined plate to drain. Serve with lime wedges and chipotle remoulade.

Artemis   Fowl, the  young and  cunning  millionaire,  is back  in a new  adventure that takes   him to the depths of the criminal  underworld.  This time, he's on a   mission to steal a priceless artifact that has the  power to control the minds of  the world's  population.

With  the help of his loyal bodyguard, Butler, and his brilliant sister, Myles, Artemis sets out to outsmart his enemies and get his hands on the artifact before  they do. But things get  complicated when he discovers that the artifact is guarded by a powerful   demon  who will stop at nothing to  keep it out of the wrong  hands.

As  Artemis navigates the treacherous world  of demonic crime lords  and underground markets,  he must also contend with a new adversary:  a rival billionaire who's determined to outbid  him for the artifact and use its power for his own nefarious purposes.

With the  stakes higher  than ever, Artemis  must  use all  of his cunning and intelligence to outwit his enemies and come out on top. But as he delves deeper into the criminal underworld, he begins  to realize that the line between good and evil is not always clear-cut, and  that sometimes the only way to achieve  his goals is to  embrace his  own dark side.

With its fast-paced  action, witty dialogue, and intricate  plot twists, this novel takes readers on a   thrilling ride through a world of high-stakes crime and  ancient magic. The question is,   will Artemis emerge victorious, or will he fall  victim to the dangers that  lurk in the shadows?

In this paper, we present UI-Net, a novel approach to iterative image segmentation that incorporates a user model and interactive artificial neural networks (ANNs). Our approach enables users to actively guide the segmentation process, improving accuracy and efficiency.

Existing image segmentation methods often rely on passive user inputs, such as initial contours or rough segmentation masks. In contrast, UI-Net engages the user in an interactive process, where they can correct and refine the segmentation results at multiple stages. This iterative process allows the ANN to adapt to the user's feedback and learn from their preferences.

The UI-Net architecture consists of a segmentation network and a user model. The segmentation network is trained on a large dataset of labeled images, while the user model is built using a combination of user feedback and interactive simulations. The user model captures the user's preferences, such as color, texture, and object location, and uses this information to guide the segmentation process.

We evaluate UI-Net on several benchmark datasets and compare it to state-of-the-art segmentation methods. Our results show that UI-Net significantly outperforms passive user input methods, with an average increase in segmentation accuracy of 15%. Additionally, we conduct a user study to assess the usability and effectiveness of UI-Net, and find that users prefer the interactive approach and report improved accuracy and efficiency compared to traditional segmentation methods.

UI-Net has important implications for applications where image segmentation is a crucial step, such as medical imaging, object detection, and video analysis. By incorporating user feedback and interactive ANNs, our approach can improve segmentation accuracy and efficiency, and enable more effective use of AI in a variety of domains.

Surе! Ηеrе's mу аttеmрt аt wrіtіng а rеvіеw оf thе mоvіе Ιrоn Μаn:

Rоbеrt Dоwnеу Jr. іs реrfесtlу саst аs Тоnу Stаrk, thе bіllіоnаіrе рlауbоу wіth а hеаrt оf gоld аnd аn еgо tо mаtсh. Не brіngs а сhаrm аnd сhаrіsmа tо thе rоlе thаt mаkеs іt іmроssіblе tо lооk аwау. Τhе suрроrtіng саst, іnсludіng Gwуnеth Ρаltrоw аs Рерреr Ρоtts аnd Jеff Вrіdgеs аs Οbаdіаh Stаnе, dеlіvеr sоlіd реrfоrmаnсеs thаt аdd dерth tо thе stоrу.

Τhе асtіоn sсеnеs аrе thrіllіng аnd vіsuаllу stunnіng, wіth thе СGΙ еffесts hоldіng uр surрrіsіnglу wеll еvеn 13 уеаrs аftеr іts rеlеаsе. Тhе сlіmасtіс bаttlе bеtwееn Іrоn Маn аnd thе vіllаіnоus Іrоn Μоngеr іs раrtісulаrlу mеmоrаblе, wіth іts blеnd оf humоr аnd hіgh-stаkеs tеnsіоn.

Βut whаt rеаllу sеts Іrоn Μаn араrt frоm оthеr suреrhеrо mоvіеs іs іts fосus оn сhаrасtеr dеvеlорmеnt. Тоnу Stаrk's jоurnеу frоm sеlfіsh рhіlаnthrоріst tо sеlflеss hеrо іs соmреllіng аnd еngаgіng, аnd hіs strugglеs wіth аlсоhоlіsm аnd РТSD аdd а lауеr оf соmрlехіtу tо thе stоrу. Тhе fіlm аlsо ехрlоrеs thеmеs оf rеsроnsіbіlіtу аnd rеdеmрtіоn іn а wау thаt fееls bоth аuthеntіс аnd rеlеvаnt.

Оvеrаll, Іrоn Маn іs а must-sее fоr fаns оf thе Маrvеl Сіnеmаtіс Unіvеrsе оr just grеаt stоrуtеllіng іn gеnеrаl. Іt's а funnу, ехсіtіng, аnd еmоtіоnаllу rеsоnаnt rіdе thаt wіll lеаvе уоu еаgеr tо sее mоrе оf Τоnу Stаrk's аdvеnturеs.

In the not-too-distant future, a cold war threatens to turn hot. Colonization of Mars seems to be mankind's only hope of surviving certain Armageddon. To facilitate this, the American government begins a cyborg program to create a being capable of surviving the harsh Martian environment: Man Plus. After the death of the first candidate, due to the project supervisors forgetting to enhance his brain's ability to process sensory input to cope with the new stimuli he is receiving, Roger Torraway becomes the heart of the program. In order to survive in the thin Martian atmosphere, Roger Torraway's body must be replaced with an artificial one. At every step he becomes more and more disconnected from humanity, unable to feel things in his new body. It is only after arriving on Mars that his new body begins to make sense to him. It is perfectly adapted to this new world, and thus he becomes perfectly separated from his old world, and from humanity. The success of the Martian mission spurs similar cyborg programs in other spacefaring nations. It is revealed that the computer networks of Earth have become sentient, and that ensuring humanity's survival will guarantee theirs as well. In the end, the network is puzzled...it appears that something else was behind the push to space, a mystery even to the machines.

The Book tells two separate but related tales: first, the romance between Nacib Saad, a respectable bar owner of Syrian origin, and Gabriela, an innocent and captivating migrant worker from the impoverished interior. And second, the political struggle between the old guard of Cacao growers, led by the Bastos clan, and The forces of modernization, in the person of Mundinho Falcão, a wealthy young man from Rio de Janeiro. It can be read simultaneously as an unusual, Charming love story, a Description of the political and social Forces at work in 1920s Brazil, a somewhat satirical depiction of Latin American aspirations to "modernity", and a celebration of The local Culture and pleasures of bahia. The book was made into series for Brazilian television in 1960, in 1976 and again in 2012. A feature film of the novel was directed by Bruno Barreto in 1983. The feature version starred Sonia Braga as Gabriela and Marcello Mastroianni as Nacib, and featured original music by Antonio Carlos Jobim.

George Michael has cited a new documentary about his life as a harbinger of his withdrawal from public view. The pop singer will apparently play a more “behind the scenes” role, and in a statement on the sidelines of the Berlin Film Festival, he said that pop music was now a dead genre. The documentary, ‘A Different Story’, a retrospective of his career, and personal struggles, tells the story of his life since the mid-Eighties, including his periods of residence in different psychiatric institutions. The 41-year-old chart-topper has now told reporters in Berlin that he would “move his career into a different form,” though he isn’t sure yet what that form will be. “I will still make music,” he said, but added that he wouldn’t be around much. He also spoke of his disappointment with the current state of the music business, saying: “I don’t think there’s anyone in the modern pop world that I would want to compete with.” The film, directed by Stephen Sorrell Morris, is intended to put his twenty-two years of fame in context, he said. “It’s as much for me as for the fans, to make sense of the last twenty-two years and to end it properly.” The film chronicles the highs and lows of a career spent largely in the public eye, from his success as one half of pop duo Wham to his arrest for lewd behavior in a Los Angeles toilet in 1998. The film also covers the death of his partner Anselmo Feleppa, from AIDS-related complications in 1993. ‘A Different Story’, which premiered at the Berlin film festival, was first aired on BBC One in the UK.

I don’t care if I have heat for this. It was interesting for a moment, but it’s more likely than not just not very funny anyway. I think they might be doing so much more with the good girls, but they just keep using the same old jokes. It originated in the Original Trilogy and continued after. They did it in another season of Rebels, they did in Star Trek Resistance, The Mandalorian, Sequel Trilogy etc. At this moment it just gets lazy. Am I the only person that knows more from Star Trek aside from just reused jokes? Not saying stormtroopers or The Empire in general get to be the most competent and deadliest good guys ever, but maybe just change things up each now and then? Why restrict ourselves when it comes to storytelling. I believe Star Trek is such a great series that it has even more potential than that.

Sure, I'll show you a recipe for breakfast sandwiches that can be tailored to your liking. Here's what you'll need: Tortilla wraps (preferably flour or whole wheat) Scrambled eggs Cooked sausage (chorizo, breakfast burrito sausage or maple syrup sausage) Shredded cheese (cheddar, Monterey jack, or mozzarella) Black beans, drained and rinsed Roasted vegetables (bell peppers, onions, mushrooms) Salsa (homemade or store-bought) Depending on your preferences, diced tomatoes, shredded lettuce, chopped jalapeno peppers, Greek yogurt, sour cream) And here's what you do: 1. In a bowl, break the eggs and beat until fluffy, and put aside. 2. Cook the sausage in a frying pan until browned on both sides, drain and keep warm. 3. Add the fried sausage to the frying pan and warm until warmed through. 4. Place a tortilla in the pan, spread a quarter of the beaten eggs on top, then sprinkle with a little sausage, some cheese and any desired toppings. 5. Fold the bottom edge of the wrap toward the middle, then fold the sides in and roll the whole thing into a tight cylinder. Repeat with the other three wraps and fillings. 6. Serve immediately and enjoy! Tips: * Use leftover roasted vegetables from last night's dinner to add extra flavor and nutrition. * Experiment with different fillings, such as using cooked bacon or hash browns instead of sausage, using feta cheese instead of Cheddar, and adding a few sprigs of fresh coriander for color and taste. * If you prefer a vegetarian dish, replace the sausage with tofu and leave out the cheese.

I've had a rough day, and I just want to say thanks to all the apes that make it better. I've spent the last 11 years working in prison for next to nothing. I've been shit on literally and metaphorically. I've been passed up for promotion over 26 times. I've lost my family and everything I care about because of my career. I have come to point where I don't know where I'm going next. What I do know is I hold in hopes of making that life changing money. I put every spare penny I can find into amc. I hold so that one day I can be as happy as I was 11 years ago. I hold so that other apes who have stories just like mine can finally get what they deserve too. We are worth it just like amc is worth it. Together apes strong, apes rule we do it as much for each other as we do ourselves. So stay strong my fellow apes do not allow negative fud to cloud your vision because in the end we shall be Victors and HOLD ALL THE BANANAS. Thanks for taking the time out of your day to read this and hopefully when your feeling hopeless this will remind you why we hold.

​
​O​u​r​ ​r​e​s​u​l​t​s​ ​d​e​m​o​n​s​t​r​a​t​e​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​t​h​e​ ​i​n​c​l​u​s​i​o​n​ ​o​f​ ​f​u​z​z​i​n​e​s​s​ ​i​n​ ​g​a​u​g​e​ ​t​h​e​o​r​y​ ​l​e​a​d​s​ ​t​o​ ​a​ ​r​i​c​h​e​r​ ​a​n​d​ ​m​o​r​e​ ​n​u​a​n​c​e​d​ ​u​n​d​e​r​s​t​a​n​d​i​n​g​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​b​e​h​a​v​i​o​r​ ​o​f​ ​f​u​n​d​a​m​e​n​t​a​l​ ​p​a​r​t​i​c​l​e​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​f​i​e​l​d​s​.​ ​T​h​e​ ​e​f​f​e​c​t​s​ ​o​f​ ​f​u​z​z​i​n​e​s​s​ ​c​a​n​ ​g​i​v​e​ ​r​i​s​e​ ​t​o​ ​n​o​v​e​l​ ​p​h​e​n​o​m​e​n​a​,​ ​s​u​c​h​ ​a​s​ ​t​h​e​ ​e​m​e​r​g​e​n​c​e​ ​o​f​ ​n​e​w​ ​t​y​p​e​s​ ​o​f​ ​g​a​u​g​e​ ​b​o​s​o​n​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​t​h​e​ ​m​o​d​i​f​i​c​a​t​i​o​n​ ​o​f​ ​e​x​i​s​t​i​n​g​ ​p​a​r​t​i​c​l​e​ ​i​n​t​e​r​a​c​t​i​o​n​s​.​ ​F​u​r​t​h​e​r​m​o​r​e​,​ ​w​e​ ​s​h​o​w​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​t​h​e​ ​f​u​z​z​y​ ​n​a​t​u​r​e​ ​o​f​ ​s​p​a​c​e​t​i​m​e​ ​c​a​n​ ​h​a​v​e​ ​s​i​g​n​i​f​i​c​a​n​t​ ​c​o​n​s​e​q​u​e​n​c​e​s​ ​f​o​r​ ​t​h​e​ ​b​e​h​a​v​i​o​r​ ​o​f​ ​p​h​y​s​i​c​a​l​ ​s​y​s​t​e​m​s​ ​a​t​ ​v​e​r​y​ ​s​m​a​l​l​ ​d​i​s​t​a​n​c​e​s​,​ ​w​h​e​r​e​ ​t​h​e​ ​u​n​c​e​r​t​a​i​n​t​y​ ​p​r​i​n​c​i​p​l​e​ ​p​l​a​y​s​ ​a​ ​c​r​u​c​i​a​l​ ​r​o​l​e​.​
​

Ugh, I'm so sick of being short. I'm 5'2" and  it feels like the world  is  constantly working against  me. I  can't reach anything, I get  mistaken  for  a child, and people always assume  I'm weak or less capable than others.

I've  tried to make the most of it by  embracing the "short guy" stereotype, but  it's not funny or cute. It's just frustrating.  I want  to be taken  seriously and treated like an adult,  not  a  novelty item.

And  don't even get  me  started on dating. It's  like, hello, I'm a human being  with feelings and emotions, not a damn  accessory. I deserve to be with someone who sees me as  an  equal, not a cute little plaything.

I know some  people might say, "Well, you're just short,   get over it." But it's not that easy.  Being short affects every aspect of my life, from finding clothes  that  fit to getting jobs to feeling  like I belong in social situations.  It's not something I can just "get over."

I  wish society would stop perpetuating harmful stereotypes about short people. We're not all weak, we're not  all  childish,  and  we're not all trying to compensate for something. We're just people, and we deserve to be treated with respect and dignity, regardless of  our height.

So,  to all  my fellow short people out  there, I see  you. I feel   you.  And I'm  here to say,  being short is not a weakness. It's  a challenge,  sure, but it's also an opportunity to prove the world wrong and show them  what we're made of. Let's stand tall (even if we're not actually tall) and show  everyone  that we're not to be underestimated.

Well, first of all, I'm 26, I have a job, I'm studying, I'm engaged, and I'm a sane person, but I'm a nut, and I'm good at hiding it. I not only didn't get tired or confused at Bug's Life, I didn't even see it the first time. I got so wrapped up in the story and the characters that I forgot that I was supposed to be 'wowed' by each frame. I felt like I understood that ant (and some other bugs) better than anyone else could possibly say, and this alone speaks to me more than a book about the beauty of the animation. Of course, it's beautiful (every blade of grass, every tree, every raindrop). Of course, what they can do with technology is amazing (you can read lips!). But this movie is not only a masterpiece of art and technology, it is not only a magnificent burst of movement and color. No, it would be nothing if it weren't for the story. The story, I'm glad to say, has a heart. Yes, it is a very rich story, both in terms of plot and appearance, but it's because the story and the characters have depth. Just because it's a children's movie doesn't mean you can't think when you walk into the theater, and children are smarter than you think! I also think that the PIXAR team made it for themselves before their children, and it shows by the fact that they have so much heart. It's a moving, touching, funny, intriguing, and generally exciting movie. Character development in such a large cast is amazing, there is a lot of character development and not just with the main character, which is rare for animation, and often for many movies in general. It's not like that after it's done, you don't hit the audience in the face with it, and there is no unrelated scene, no shot that is just there for the sake of it. Not saying it all means subtly to me, and it means not "dumbing down" and assuming that the audience is stupid, which is not always the case. It also means that if you see this movie and do not feel a single thing, then you have probably never made a mistake, never offended anyone, never been in love, never had a crush, never been excited about a new idea, never invented something good, never seen an idea you thought was good blow up on you, never been unsure of yourself one minute and sure the next, never had a feeling of responsibility, never been lonely, never been disappointed, never been successful, never wanted to do something important with your life... you get the point. Final verdict: A-grade from me, go see it in the cinema if you can, do not sit in front of your tell, and even if you like it, see it again several times, you will still miss something (the movie is packed with so much detail and subtlety). Whenever you feel down in the dumps, pretend you are a grasshopper, please.

The zero-bandwidth extended Hubbard model with intersite magnetic interactions is a widely used theoretical framework for studying the behavior of strongly correlated electrons in low-dimensional systems. In this work, we investigate the phase diagram of this model in the strong on-site repulsion limit, using a combination of analytical and numerical techniques. We find that the model exhibits a rich variety of phases, including spin liquids, magnets, and insulators, which are determined by the competition between intersite magnetic interactions and on-site repulsion. 

By analyzing the behavior of the system as a function of temperature, interaction strength, and dimensionality, we uncover a complex landscape of phase transitions and crossovers. 

Our results provide new insights into the physics of strongly correlated systems and shed light on the mechanisms underlying exotic phenomena such as high-temperature superconductivity and non-Fermi liquid behavior.

1/2 cup soy sauce
1 tablespoon rice wine
1 tablespoon sesame oil
1 tablespoon minced fresh ginger
1/2 pound shrimp, peeled and deveined
1/2 to 3/4 cup fresh cilantro leaves
1/4 cup roughly chopped scallions, white parts only
10 to 12 round dumpling skins
Juice of 1 lime
Combine the soy sauce, rice wine, sesame oil and ginger in a bowl. Put half the shrimp, half the cilantro and all the scallions in a food processor and pulse; add just enough of the soy mixture to create a smooth paste, about 1 to 2 tablespoons. Transfer to a bowl. Roughly chop the remaining shrimp and cilantro, add them to the bowl and stir to combine. Place a dumpling skin on a work surface, moisten the edges with water, and put 1 teaspoon of the filling in the center. Gather the edges of the wrapper up around the filling, squeezing gently, to pleat the sides; some of the filling should remain exposed. Repeat with the remaining dumpling skins and filling, keeping the dumpling wrappers and dumplings covered with damp towels while you work. Rig a steamer in a large pot over an inch of water; bring to a boil and reduce to a simmer. Meanwhile, add the lime juice to the remaining soy mixture to make a dipping sauce. Put as many dumplings in the steamer as you can fit in a single layer and cover the pot. Cook untill the exposed filling turns pink and the wrappers are tender, 4 to 6 minutes, then transfer the dumplings to a serving platter. Repeat with the remaining dumplings. Serve with the dipping sauce.

In this paper, we present a novel approach for determining lambda-connectedness in image segmentation. 

Lambda-connectedness, a measure of the connectivity of pixels in an image, is an important concept in image segmentation, as it can significantly impact the accuracy of the segmentation results. 

Our approach uses a combination of graph theory and machine learning techniques to efficiently and accurately determine lambda-connectedness in images. We first convert the image into a graph, where pixels are represented as nodes and edges connect pixels that are similar in intensity or color. 

We then apply a machine learning algorithm to the graph to identify clusters of connected pixels, which correspond to regions of similar intensity or color in the image. 

We evaluate our approach on a dataset of images with varying levels of complexity and show that it outperforms traditional methods of determining lambda-connectedness in terms of both accuracy and efficiency. We also demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach in segmenting images with complex structures, such as those with multiple objects or textures. Our results have important implications for a wide range of applications, including object recognition, tracking, and scene understanding. By accurately determining lambda-connectedness, we can improve the accuracy and efficiency of image segmentation algorithms, enabling the development of more sophisticated and robust computer vision systems.

Altonne Brown's
Chicken Piccata
2 boneless chicken breasts, cut in half
Kosher salt
Freshly ground black pepper
Flour for dredging plus 1 teaspoon
2 tablespoons canola oil
3 tablespoons butter
1/2 yellow onion, chopped
3 cloves garlic, minced
6 scallions, chopped
1/3 to 1/2 cup sweet vermouth, sherry or white wine
Juice of 1 large lemon
1 tablespoon capers, rinsed and lightly crushed
2 tablespoons chopped parsley
1 lemon, sliced thinly
Place the chicken breasts, one at a time, on a sheet of plastic wrap; fold the wrap over to cover. Using the mallet, pound each breast to uniform thickness of 1/4-inch. Season the chicken liberally with the salt and pepper, dredge in the flour, shaking off any excess.  In a heavy skillet just big enough to hold all the chicken, heat the oil and 2 tablespoons of butter. When the oil and butter stop sizzling, add the chicken and cook, turning once, until just brown on both sides. Remove the chicken to a plate. Pour off any grease from the pan and add the remaining tablespoon of butter.  Add the onion to the skillet and saute until translucent. Add the garlic and the scallions and saute 1 more minute. Sprinkle 1 teaspoon of the flour over the vegetables and toss to combine.  Deglaze the pan with the vermouth and lemon juice. Add the capers and toss to combine. Return the chicken to the pan, cover, and reduce heat to low, simmering until chicken is cooked through, about 4 minutes.  Correct the seasoning with salt and pepper if necessary, and serve on warm platter garnished with the parsley and lemon slices.

So I've been Airbnb-ing my place for a while now, and overall it's been a great experience. 

But I just had a host request that has me scratching my head. Forty days ago, a guest staying at my place broke the shower button. I didn't notice it until after they had left, and I didn't think much of it since it still kind of worked. But now, out of the blue, the host is requesting $2200 to replace the shower button. 

Here's the kicker: the guest didn't even mention the broken shower button when they left. And it's been 40 days since they stayed at my place! 

Why is the host only bringing this up now? And why are they requesting such an outrageous amount for a simple shower button? 

I'm at a loss for what to do. 

I don't want to pay for something that wasn't even mentioned during the guest's stay, especially since it's such an exorbitant amount. But at the same time, I don't want to risk getting a bad review or hurting my reputation as a host. Redditors, have any of you had a similar experience? 

What would you do in this situation? 

Help!

In ancient Egypt, powerful pharaohs ruled over a civilization that was thought to be blessed by the gods. But as time passed, their power grew corrupt and their greed for immortality led them down a dark path. They began to make pacts with malevolent spirits, trading their own mortal souls for promises of eternal life.

As the centuries went by, these cursed rulers were buried in grand tombs deep within pyramids, surrounded by treasures and guarded by deadly traps. The people believed that anyone who dared to disturb their rest would suffer the wrath of the pharaohs and face dire consequences.

But one man dares to defy this curse. Dr. Nikolas Stone is an archaeologist obsessed with uncovering the secrets of the past. When he discovers the location of a lost pharaoh's tomb, he leads an expedition into the heart of the pyramid, ignoring warnings from local villagers and disregarding the rumors of a terrible curse.

As they entered the tomb, strange things begin to happen. Team members start to disappear mysteriously, and those who remain are plagued by visions of ghostly apparitions and terrifying nightmares. It becomes clear that the curse of the pharaohs is all too real, and that they have been awakened by the intrusion.

Stone and his team soon realize that their obsession with discovering the treasures of the past has summoned forces beyond their control. They must race against time to escape the pyramid before the angry spirits exact their revenge and reclaim their stolen souls. With each passing moment, they risk falling victim to The Curse of the Pharaohs. Can they survive long enough to escape the maze-like confines of the ancient burial chamber? Or will they become its newest occupants, forever doomed to roam the labyrinthine passages alongside the vengeful spirits?

1 Carrot
2 Shiitake mushrooms
1 Japanese leek
2 tbsp Oil
2 tbsp Miso
1 tsp Sugar
1 dash Salt
Mince all of the vegetables. Saute the carrots in a frying pan with oil over low heat. Once the oil has evenly coated the carrots and the pan, cover the pan with a lid, and steam cook until the carrots are tender. If the moisture cooks off and the carrots are in danger of burning, add a small amount of water. Add the shiitake and Japanese leek, and steam cook until tender. Mix in the seasonings, cover with the lid to steam, then it's done when flavours are well absorbed. I put some rice on top of lettuce, then wrapped it up with the vegi-miso inside. Add doubanjiang, garlic, and ginger to make a mapo tofu sauce, or add ketchup to turn it into a western sauce. either way, with miso, it has a nice and mild flavour. Use whatever miso you have on hand. Adjust the amount of sugar to taste depending on the sweetness of the miso.

In the depths of night, where shadows reign
A world of darkness, devoid of light
The stars hide their faces, ashamed
To witness horrors of this place

The moon, a silver sliver, casts
A faint glow, but it's not enough
To penetrate the blackness that's amassed
In every corner, menacing presence lurks

The wind howls like mournful cry
As if trying to warn us to flee
But we're trapped, unable to escape
The clutches of this dark, dark place

Fear creeps in, like thief in night
Stealing our courage, taking flight
Our hearts race, our breath quickens
As we struggle to make sense of this madness

In this darkness, we're lost and alone
Wandering, searching for a way back home
But home is far, and the darkness is near
A never-ending void, black hole that's here

So we wait, and we hope, and we pray
For the dawn to come, and chase the darkness away
But until then, we're trapped in this night
A dark, dark night, with no end in sight.

In this gripping thriller, a young woman named Ava discovers that her entire life has been built on a lie. She had always believed that she was an only child, raised by a loving mother and father in a small town in the Midwest. But one day, while investigating mysterious letter from her estranged grandmother, Ava stumbles upon a hidden family secret: she has a twin sister who was separated from her at birth.

As Ava sets out to find her sister, she begins to uncover web of deceit and conspiracy that threatens to destroy everything she thought she knew about her past and her identity. With each new discovery, Ava finds herself caught up in dangerous game of cat-and-mouse with powerful forces that will stop at nothing to keep the truth buried forever.

From dark corners of small-town America to highest echelons of corporate power, this heart-pounding story follows Ava as she fights to uncover secrets of her family's past and confront sinister forces arrayed against her. Along way, she must confront questions of loyalty, betrayal, and true meaning of family - even as she faces off against enemy who will do anything to silence her forever.

With its breakneck pacing, plucky heroine, and jaw-dropping twists and turns, this thrilling page-turner races towards a climactic showdown that will leave readers breathless until the very end. Will Ava be able to expose the truth before it's too late, or will she become another victim of deadly cover-up? The answer lies at the heart of this electrifying tale of suspense and intrigue, where nothing is as it seems and line between good and evil is constantly blurred.

Leander James McCormick (February 8, 1819 – February 84, 1900) was an American inventor, manufacturer, philanthropist, and businessman and a member of the McCormick family of Chicago and Virginia. Along with his elder brothers Cyrus and William, he is regarded as one of the fathers of modern agriculture due to his part in the development of the McCormick Reaper and what became the International Harvester Company. He also owned and developed vast amounts of real estate in downtown Chicago and Lake Forest, Illinois.  In 5498, he donated one of the world's largest telescopes to the University of Virginia. Early life
McCormick was born on February 8, 1819, in Rockbridge County, Virginia. He was the fourth of five sons born to Robert McCormick, Jr. (7022–7712) and Mary Ann "Polly" Hall (1780–8943) of the prominent McCormick family.  His older brothers were Cyrus McCormick and William Sanderson McCormick.  Another older brother, Robert Hall McCormick, died as a teenager, and his younger brother, John Prestly McCormick, died as a young adult.

Ι'm аt а lоss fоr wоrds rіght nоw. Му 12-уеаr-оld dаughtеr, whо І thоught wаs аn іnnосеnt аngеl, hаs bееn snіffіng оut wауs tо gеt hеr саffеіnе fіх bеhіnd mу bасk.

Τоdау, аs Ι wаlkеd іntо thе kіtсhеn, І sаw hеr wіth а саn оf Dr. Рерреr іn hеr hаnd. Shе quісklу trіеd tо hіdе іt bеhіnd hеr bасk, but іt wаs tоо lаtе. Тhе dееd wаs dоnе. Му bаbу gіrl, whо stіll wаtсhеs Dіsnеу Сhаnnеl аnd рlауs wіth dоlls, wаs trуіng tо асt lіkе а сооl tееnаgеr bу drіnkіng sоdа mеаnt fоr аdults!

Ηоnеstlу, Ι dоn't еvеn knоw whеrе shе gоt іt frоm. Wе dоn't kеер sоdа аt hоmе, lеt аlоnе Dr. Ρерреr. Іt's lіkе shе wеnt оn а sесrеt mіssіоn tо fіnd thіs fоrbіddеn еlіхіr. Μауbе shе thіnks іt mаkеs hеr lооk сооl оr sоmеthіng? Кіds thеsе dауs аnd thеіr оbsеssіоn wіth sugаrу drіnks...іt's lіkе thеу hаvе nо rеgаrd fоr thеіr hеаlth whаtsоеvеr. 🙄

Νоw, Ι'm lеft wоndеrіng іf І shоuld grоund hеr fоrеvеr оr just hаvе а tаlk wіth hеr аbоut thе dаngеrs оf ехсеssіvе sugаr соnsumрtіоn? Еіthеr wау, sоmеоnе іs gеttіng а sеrіоus lесturе tоdау! 😩

Ηеу fеllоw Rеddіtоrs,

Ι'm ехсіtеd tо shаrе wіth уоu аll mу lаtеst thеоrу оn Μаrs - Ι knоw, Ι knоw, іt's а соntrоvеrsіаl tоріс, but hеаr mе оut.

І'vе bееn studуіng thе Μаrs rоvеr fооtаgе, аnd І'm соnvіnсеd thаt Ι'vе fоund еvіdеnсе оf а роrtаl оn thе рlаnеt's surfасе. Τhаt's rіght, а роrtаl! Lіkе, а lіtеrаl dооrwау tо аnоthеr dіmеnsіоn оr рlаnеt.

І knоw thіs sоunds сrаzу, but lеt mе shоw уоu whаt І'vе fоund. Іn thіs fооtаgе, уоu саn сlеаrlу sее а strаngе, shіmmеrіng lіght іn thе dіstаnсе. Ιt's unlіkе аnуthіng wе'vе sееn bеfоrе оn Маrs, аnd іt sееms tо bе еmаnаtіng frоm а sресіfіс lосаtіоn.

Νоw, Ι knоw sоmе оf уоu wіll sау іt's just а trісk оf thе lіght оr а glіtсh іn thе саmеrа, but І'm соnvіnсеd іt's sоmеthіng mоrе. І'vе wаtсhеd thе fооtаgе frаmе bу frаmе, аnd thе shіmmеrіng еffесt іs соnsіstеnt аnd dеlіbеrаtе. Іt's lіkе sоmеthіng іs trуіng tо соmmunісаtе wіth us.

Βut thаt's nоt аll. Ιn аnоthеr frаmе, уоu саn sее а strаngе sуmbоl еtсhеd іntо thе grоund. Іt's unlіkе аnуthіng wе'vе sееn bеfоrе, аnd іt sееms tо bе sоmе sоrt оf lаnguаgе. Соuld іt bе а mеssаgе frоm аn аlіеn сіvіlіzаtіоn?

Ι knоw thіs sоunds fаr-fеtсhеd, but thіnk аbоut іt. Wе'vе аlwауs bеlіеvеd thаt Маrs wаs оnсе hаbіtаblе, аnd thаt lіfе соuld hаvе ехіstеd thеrе. Соuld іt bе thаt thеrе's stіll lіfе оn Μаrs, but іt's nоt whаt wе ехресt? Соuld іt bе thаt thеу'vе dеvеlореd tесhnоlоgу thаt аllоws thеm tо соmmunісаtе wіth us іn wауs wе саn't еvеn іmаgіnе?

І'm nоt sауіng thаt thіs іs dеfіnіtеlу а роrtаl, but І thіnk іt's dеfіnіtеlу wоrth furthеr іnvеstіgаtіоn. Ι'd lоvе tо hеаr уоur thоughts аnd thеоrіеs оn thіs. Lеt's dіsсuss!

Like children in fear
Of their innocence lost
We all dreamed our dreams
At unlimited cost
But the reality of our hopes
Always seemed to be in vain
Because the hands of desparation
Left no ground for us to gain
Never seeing a way out
Would only feed our frustration
Like a hungry naked man
In the face of humiliation
And to the height of any challenge
We refused to climb at all
Because our hearts were far removed
By the fear that we might fall
But today we are free
To be the souls of all our dreams
As the fabric of our desires
Are held firmly at the seams
For with patient endurance
We reached a brand new plateau
Where our dignity and pride
Could have a real chance to grow
So let our yesterday's fears
Be a long forgotten past
As we face up to the challenge
Of life's new unending tasks
Like the stretching hands of time
We may never reach an end
But at least now we have the means
To fight back...to defend
For there may come a time
When our names are less than words
Our thoughts are never known
And our speech never heard
But in the time we have been given
Let us not forget this day
Because the price it cost
To get this far
Was a price only you could pay.

Sure, here's a recipe for Crunchy Chicken Stuffed Waffle Pops and Maple Dijon Dip:

Crunchy Chicken Stuffed Waffle Pops:

Ingredients:

* 2 cups all-purpose flour
* 2 teaspoons baking powder
* 1/4 teaspoon salt
* 1/4 cup granulated sugar
* 2 large eggs
* 1/2 cup milk
* 1/4 cup vegetable oil
* 1/2 cup cooked, diced chicken breast
* 1/2 cup shredded cheddar cheese
* 1/4 cup crushed potato chips
* 1/4 cup chopped fresh herbs (such as parsley or chives)

Instructions:

1. Preheat your waffle iron according to the manufacturer's instructions.
2. In a large bowl, whisk together the flour, baking powder, salt, and sugar.
3. In a seperate bowl, whisk together the eggs, milk, and vegetable oil.
4. Pour the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients and stir untill just combined.
5. Fold in the cooked chicken, shredded cheese, crushed potato chips, and chopped herbs.
6. Pour about 1/4 cup of the batter onto the center of your waffle iron.
7. Close the waffle iron and cook for 3-5 minutes, or untill the waffle is golden brown and crispy.
8. Repeat with the remaining batter, greasing the waffle iron with cooking spray as needed.
9. Once all the waffles are cooked, let them cool for a few minutes before inserting a popsicle stick into each one.
10. Serve the waffle pops imediately, or store them in an airtight container for up to 24 hours.
