Elvis Presley has scored his 19th number one single in the UK charts with the re-release of Jailhouse Rock, 27 years after his death.
Elvis knocked X Factor winner Steve Brookstein down into second place after three weeks in the charts. In at number three was Iron Maiden for the Number Of The Beast and Erasure entered the chart at four with Breathe. Elvis's number one is the 999th in chart history and comes the day after what would have been his 70th birthday. Fans around the world held tribute events for the singer on Saturday, ranging from concerts to memorabilia exhibitions. Meanwhile, a poll carried out by royalty payments group the Performing Right Society found that The Wonder of You is the Elvis song most performed by live bands and tribute acts.
Record company SonyBMG are releasing Elvis's 18 number one singles at the rate of one a week in Britain, complete with original artwork and a collector's box. Hit single One Night will follow next week - with the chance of becoming the 1,000th number one as interest surrounding Elvis's birthday grows. Elsewhere in the singles charts, the Scissor Sisters went straight in at number five with Filthy/Gorgeous. Band Aid 20 slipped to six after seven weeks in the chart with Do They Know It's Christmas. The other two new entries are Dana Rayne with Object of My Desire at seven and Kasabian who are at number eight with Cut Off. Making up the top 10 are Boulevard of Broken Dreams by Green Day, down three places to number nine, and Out of Touch by Uniting Nations, also down three places to 10. In the album charts, the Scissor Sisters went to number one with their self-titled release, knocking Green Day's American Idiot to third place. The Killers' album Hot Fuss moved up three to number two.

Hey fellow Redditors,

I'm at a crossroads and could use some advice From you all. I've been struggling with my feminine side for a while now, and I'm not sure what to do about it.

Here's the thing: I've always identified as a man, and I've never really questioned that aspect of my identity. But lately, I've started to feel this... urge, I guess you could say, to explore my more feminine qualities. It's Like There's this whole other part Of me that I've been neglecting or suppressing, and it's trying to break free.

It's hard to explain, but sometimes I catch myself wanting to wear dresses or makeup, or even just to act in ways that are traditionally associated with women. and it's not just about sexuality or gender identity - it's like there's This deep-seated desire to connect With my own inner femininity.

The Problem is, I have no idea how to navigate these feelings. I don't know if i should embrace them or try to push them away. Part of me feels like I'm betraying my masculinity By even considering these desires, while another part of me feels like I'm denying myself the chance to truly be myself.

Has anyone else out there experienced Something similar? How did you deal with it? Any advice would be greatly appreciated. Thanks!

Sure! Here's a recipe for Cheese and Vegetable soup En Croute, a delicious and elegant French-inspired dish that combines the rich flavors of cheese and vegetables in a Flaky pastry crust.

Ingredients:

For the soup:

* 2 tablespoons butter
* 1 onion, finely chopped
* 3 cloves garlic, minced
* 2 carrots, peeled and grated
* 2 celery stalks, thinly sliced
* 1 potato, peeled and diced
* 1 cup vegetable Broth
* 1 cup milk
* 1/2 cup heavy cream
* 1 teaspoon dried thyme
* Salt and pepper to taste
* 8 oz grated Gruyère or Swiss cheese (or a combination)

For The croutes:

* 1 sheet puff pastry, thawed
* 1 Egg, beaten (for Brushing pastry)
* Sesame seeds or poppy seeds for garnish (optional)

Instructions:

1. Preheat your oven to 400°F (200°C).
2. In a large saucepan over medium heat, melt The butter. Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes. Add the minced garlic and cook for another minute.
3. Add the grated carrots and sliced celery to the saucepan and cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
4. Add the diced Potato, vegetable broth, milk, Heavy cream, and dried thyme to the saucepan. Bring the mixture to a simmer.
5. Reduce the heat to low and let the soup simmer for 15-20 minutes, or until the vegetables Are tender and the soup has thickened slightly. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
6. While the soup is simmering, prepare the croutes. Preheat a baking sheet

The year is 1799, and Richard Sharpe, a former  soldier  turned merchant, has just arrived in Calcutta,  India, with  his companions,  Patrick Harper and  Hagman. They  have  been  hired by  a wealthy  British merchant named Hector Barbossa  to escort  his niece, Frances, through the  dangerous jungles  of the Indian interior to her wedding in Seringapatam.

As they journey deeper   into the heart  of India, they encounter  numerous challenges such as treacherous terrain, wild animals, and hostile native tribes. Along the way, Sharpe  becomes  suspicious of their employer's true motives and begins to suspect that there is more to their mission than he was led to believe.

Meanwhile, in Seringapatam, a powerful Indian ruler  named Tipu Sultan is preparing  for war against the British East  India  Company. He  seeks  revenge against the British for past  defeats and  enlists the help of several European  mercenaries, including  an  English  officer  named  William Dodd,  who has fallen  out of favor with the British army after   refusing to follow orders he considered unethical.

When Sharpe, Harper, and Hagman arrive at the city, they  are met with resistance  from both the British and Indian forces. A series of battles ensues, and  tensions  rise between Sharpe and Dodd,  who  finds himself torn between loyalty to his new employer and his sense of justice.

In the midst   of the conflict, Sharpe discovers that Barbossa's real intention for bringing his niece  to  Seringapatam is to  force her into  marriage with one of Tipu Sultan's sons in exchange for trade  privileges. Horrified by this revelation, Sharpe decides to protect Frances and defies  Barbossa, setting off a string of events that ultimately lead to a confrontation with Tipu Sultan himself.

Through cunning strategy and bravery, Sharpe manages to defeat Tipu Sultan's forces, save Frances  from being forced  into marriage,  and  prevent  a war  between the British and  Indian armies. In doing so, he also redeems himself for  past  mistakes and

Unhappy about some far off things
That are not my affair, wandering
Along the coast and up the lean ridges,
I saw in the evening
The stars go over the lonely ocean,
And a black-maned wild boar
Plowing with his snout on Mal Paso Mountain. The old monster snuffled, "Here are sweet roots,
Fat grubs, slick beetles and sprouted acorns. The best nation in Europe has fallen,
And that is Finland,
But the stars go over the lonely ocean,"
The old black-bristled boar,
Tearing the sod on Mal Paso Mountain. "The world's in a bad way, my man,
And bound to be worse before it mends;
Better lie up in the mountain here
Four or five centuries,
While the stars go over the lonely ocean,"
Said the old father of wild pigs,
Plowing the fallow on Mal Paso Mountain. 

"Keep clear of the dupes that talk democracy
And the dogs that talk revolution,
Drunk with talk, liars and believers. 

I believe in my tusks. Long live freedom and damn the ideologies,"
Said the gamey black-maned boar
Tusking the turf on Mal Paso Mountain. 

Submitted by Holt

Online role playing games are time-consuming, but enthraling flights from reality. But are some people taking their fantasy lives too seriously?
When video game World of Warcraft hit the shops in Europe last week fans wrote in to the BBC website to express their delight - and to offer a warning. "An addiction to a game like this is far more costly in time than any substance could impair - keep track of time," wrote Travis Anderson, in Texas. Some of the comments were humourous: "This game is so good I'm not going to get it, there's no way I could limit the hours I'd spend playing it," wrote Charles MacIntyre, from England.
But some struck a more worrying tonnee about the Massively Multiplayer Online Role Playing Game (MMORPG): "'You need to get out more' could be the motto of any MMORPG. Shame they are getting more popular, as you know this problem is just going to mushroom," wrote Stuart Stantonne-Davies, in Huddersfield. Scare-mongering articles about "addictive video games" have existed since the days the first game of Pong stopped everyone from working at the Atari offices.
Gaming is like any other pastime - it can quickly become an unhealthy obsession, whether it is spending too much time in the gym, in front of the television, or reading poetry.
Unfortunately, gaming and addiction is a far too easy association to make. However, stories about gamers spending 10 to 15 hours a day in front of some video games are becoming more frequent. And the impact that is having on their families is quite distressing for some.
Massively multiplayer online role playing games - MMORPGs - allow thousands of gamers to share a common experience of sharing fantasy or science fiction worlds. The scope of these games - like Warcraft, EverQuest, Ultima among others - is epic, and exploration and adventure is almost infinite. Part of the "problem" is grinding - by which gamers have to perform long-winded, mindless tasks, to bring up their levels and gain access to more adventure. Such open-endedness brings with it a desire to keep playing; not for no reason is EverQuest (EQ) nicknamed EverCrack. E Hayot, writing in the culture blogzine Print Culture, said recently: "I used to play the online role-playing game EverQuest a lot. "By 'a lot', I mean probably 15 to 20 hours a week on average, and on weeks where I didn't have to work, as many as 30 or 40 hours."
He says that in the world of online gaming such behaviour "wasn't that unusual; lots of people I knew in the game played EQ that much". "You lie; you don't go into work because you "had stuff to do at home"; you cancel or refuse invitations to dinner, you spend much less time watching TV (a good thing, presumably)," he wrote, explaining how EverQuest took over his time. He quit the game, he says, because he realised life was more fun than EverQuest.
Let us be clear - such obsession is rare. But the huge growth in online gaming means a growth in the numbers of people who take their passion for a hobby too far. Almost 400,000 people bought a copy of World of Warcraft in the first two days on sale earlier this month. Only a fraction will descend into obsessives. The thoughts of families and friends of gamers who have been affected by EverQuest can be found on one blog EverQuest Daily Grind. Jane, who runs the website, compiles a chronicle of heart-rending stories. "I am actually convinced at this point that there are more than 'some' people who spend more times in MMOPRGs than in reality," she said. One unnamed correspondent - all are anonymous - wrote: "On the rare nights when my husband does come to bed at the same time as I do, I find that I am so used to sleeping by myself that it is difficult to get to sleep with another body laying next to me.
"I can't talk to him while he is playing. There is absolutely no point as he doesn't hear me or is so distracted that I get a 'ummm... ya' a few minutes after I ask him a question."
"Gaming widows" has become a comedic term for women who have been shut out by male gamers. But for some it is not in the least funny. Another correspondent wrote: "I believe that he is addicted to the online gaming, and that is the cause of his depression and restlessness." And some of them are even sadder: "Today our son was five days old. "The sad truth is my husband spent 11 hours today playing his Warcraft game. He did not interact with our sweet tiny baby because there were important quests waiting online." Video game fans often complain that their hobby is misunderstood or marginalised. But as gaming becomes ever more mainstream, and games ever more immersive, there will be no hiding place for social problems.
I wish 30-40 hours a week was unusual but I think it probably isn't. An 11 hour stretch isn't that surprising - I've known people to play 15+ hours at a stretch. I know of people who are spending their week's holiday from work playing Warcraft. I know of people who would play Ever[Crack] in shifts...waking at 3am to take over from their friends and resume waiting for an item they 'needed' to appear. I understand that the key sign of an addiction is if you alter your life around it rather than fit it into your life. By all standards many of us are addicts. So is the solution to force ourselves to stop playing..or do we just need to make real life a bit more interesting?
Sadly with all the talk of people becoming obsessed with gaming, I find myself longing to have the time to join them. I have been in a long term relationship for over 4 years - since that began, games have become more and more complex. And more and more so I find I have less and less time to play them, with and marriage and work being the main drag on my time.
I think the line between playing a game a lot and a gaming addiction is really quite distinct. I play games a lot, definately over 20 hours a week, but I don't go missing work or other commitments in order to play games.
I have, about a year ago, deleted every game on my computer. RPGs are the worst - the real world fades and all your worries sorround a new magic staff or mighty sword. Unlike books, or perhaps even TV, you gain absolutely nothing. When you stop playing you're at the same point as when you started; all the achievements of your 10 hour session are irretrievably locked in the game and, since you've gained nothing in the real world, you may as well pile on more achievement in the fake one.
Despite having little monetary value, the "rewards" and encouragement offered by these MMORPGs is enough to hook games for hours daily. If only business could learn to leverage that very simply human need for easily measurable progress and recognition. Perhaps the unhealthily obsessed simply need more recognition for their achievements in reality?
My advice to gaming widows is "if you can't beat 'em, join 'em". That is, try playing it yourself. If he wants to play as well, well at least you'll be together somewhere...
I was an addict and it cost me my relationship. I still play now, but without the guilt , hehe, How long have i played in one sitting? From morning till the early hours of the next day, the birds were singing out side and i had to hobble to the bath room cos my bladder was so full i was in pain, i would hardly eat, perhaps some toast, smoke endlessly and drink. Now, thankfully the fascination has worn off and I have a girlfriend but still no job. For the most part online gaming give me an adiction to illusory achievement, and as there is no end in sight you keep going for the mirage of the ultimate.
Obsessive behaviour is, of course, always cause for concern, but it always bothers me when articles about gaming talk in terms of "reality". Obviously, somebody who spends thirty hours a week playing EverQuest has a problem. This problem, however, has nothing to do with a dysfunctional sense of reality. An obsessive EQ player does not consider the game to be "real" any more than - for example - an obsessive automotive tinkerer considers their car to be human. If MMORPGs have a unique danger, in terms of encourageing obsessive behaviour, it is not that they create an absorbing virtual world, but rather that they can be easily accessed 24/7. The problem here does not lie with the nature of gaming, but with the nature of modern 24 hour culture.
The problem with these so called MMORPGS is that you can never really complete them, there's always another quest to do. A few of my friends have only had about 10 hours sleep since it was released friday...
Championship Manager consumed my life for years. One particular session started at about 2pm on a Sunday, paused for a brief sleep at 5am on the Monday and after visit to University for classes restarted at about midday for another 10 hour session. The people who tend to hark on about about the problems of "hardcore gaming" seem to be those who have rarely allowed themselves to become immersed in a game. I would expect their perspective to change if they were to do that.
I used to be an EverQuest addict while I was in college. It came to the point where the gaming world felt more real than the real one. I failed alot of my courses and was able to barely graduate. I was lucky that I came to my senses when I did, others were less fortunate and dropped out of college. Now that I am holding a job, I avoid online RPGS like the plague.
When I was made redundant I told my partner I had a new job for three months whilst every day I played EverQuest from 7:30am till 5:pm. When She came home I pretended I had just got in as well, hence justifying playing it all evening. I have since quit playing MMORPG and have a good job.
When I got to the point where I was eating my dinner in front of the PC I realised things were getting silly so I'm trying not to spend so much time on there. It's not easy. I feel as if I've got a real addiction going on here.
For me the problem is that I love to complete a goal. Once it is completed that is it, I am finished, time to move on. I become obsessed to complete the goal, so from that standpoint it is an addiction. In a game where you will never complete an "ultimate" goal, well it would be like falling into a black pit. It is easier to escape into a controlled fantasy world than face reality at times - in other words the goal offered in the PC game are "easier" and more fun than the real world. Pretty scary implications if you think about it.
I can't buy World of Warcraft as it would destroy my marrage, I just know it!!
I played Star Wars Galaxeies for about a year and can attest to the addictiveness of these games. They are all engineered in such a way that early on in the game you progress quickly, but this progress becomes exponentially slower, requiring more and more time to reach the next level. I'm sad to say that at the peak of my addiction I was spending entyre weekends in front of my monitor, slowly building up my character, stopping only for food and toilet breaks. Thankfully I made a clean break, and actually managed to sell my Jedi account for £800 - which is my only sanity check in an otherwise completely unproductive time vacuum.
Seven years ago, I began playing Ultima Online. This game dominated 2 years of my life. They were 2 wonderful years and I still have vivid memories of the experiences and friends I had. Online gaming can be a world of escapism where you can be yourself without fear of the thoughts of others. Something that cannot always be achieved in the day to day running of a normal life. Whilst I would warn against people giving to much of there life to these games, I believe they are a better way to spend your time than say watching TV.
Gaming is addictive and should be made a recognised addiction. When I was single I used to play upto eight hours a night after work every night for about a year, building up my stats, completing evermore quests and battling ogres. But somehow I found time to get out, even met somaeone and got married! Has my life changed? Hell no! I still cast spells and battle till the early hours of the morning. On with the fun!
Online gaming should be enjoyed just as much as you would enjoy watching television, or going to the cinema or the pub with your mates.
Many people use recreational drugs on an occasional basis and are able to lead succesfull lives with families, relationships and good careers. A minority allow drugs to take over and destroy their lives and become addicted. According to this article the same is true of MMORPGs. The message to the government is clear, either legalise drugs, or outlaw online gaming!!
Sounds like there are some sad stories here - and I can believe them all. I play alot of Warcraft myself, and know full well how addictive it is. I am resolute that it will not take over my life. It certainly gets in the way though. I think that some people simply do not know how to draw this line, or lack the willpower to stop themselves stepping over it.
I think I'm obsessed with gaming in general, I spend far too much time playing games like Everquest 2 and Football Manager rather than going out and interacting with real people and when I do try to, I'm always thinking in the back if my mind that I'd rather be in front of the computer winning the league with Cambridge United.
I am obsessed with online role playing games. It's not so much quests but it has the adrenaline of a real life situation - goals to achieve etc. I spend about five hours per day online playing it and I rarely get more than four to five hours sleep before getting up for work the next morning...
As many of the players spend their time in MMORPGs rather than in front of the TV I fail to see how it will affect players social lives negatively. Furthermore these types of games contain a huge social aspect, whereas other games and some other pursuits (such as being a couch potato) the players could be indulging in are solitary by nature.
These games are like most things -- too much of anything is a bad thing, but as long as you can walk away from the computer to do other things too, they can be great fun.
Living in Korea at the moment, they have lots PC Bangs (Internet Cafes). Nearly most of South Koreans are addicted to online games, and one Korean died because of the lack of food and water he had through playing online games.
I play xbox live every day. I find my self lying and rescheduling everything around my gaming fix. The longest I played was a 24 hour straight session. I know I play for to long but it's an obsession that I can't control. Can you reccomend a counsellor - this is not a wind up... but something I'm increasingly concerned with...
Me and my mate play online for an hour or two a day, we're both aware of how much time can disappear by sitting in front of a TV, trying to 'frag' some individual. It's getting the balance between getting home and relasing the stress of a day by an hour or so gaming, and enjoying 'real' life...
I bought the US version of World of Warcraft when it came out. The longest period I played was 23 hrs straight. I gave up the game after a month because it was so addictive, but have subsequently just bought the European version (couldn't help myself). In future, I'm going to regulate my time far more strictly. Great game!
Having played MMORPG games for some years I agree that these type of games can be life sucking. But my concern is for the younger generation of gamers that play for hours on end in an adult enviroment. Most MMORPG games you need a credit card to play but I dont think parents know just what they are letting there children into.
Unless there is undeniable medical proof that staring at a computer screens for hours at a time can damage a person¿s health, you can expect this not to decline but to get worse.
These people are pathetic. They need to get off their machines and notice that our world is being swiftly overcome by issues and troubles that make the trifling worries of and "online universe" absolutely meaningless.
24hours, when i was a kid at school and i was on half term, Ultima Online was the game, ahhhh them was the days ! LOL

In a dystopian future, humanity has ravaged the planet and natural resources are scarce. Climate change has caused devastating weather patterns, and food is strictly controlled by powerful corporations known as "agit korps." The only hope for survival Lies in genetically Modified crops created by these corporations, which have replaced traditional farming practices.

In this bleak world, a mysterious figure known only as "the windup girl" piques the interest of anderson Lake, an employee of one of The most powerful agri-korps, AgriGen. The windup girl is revealed To be a "new person," a being created through advanced biotechnology and designed to serve a specific purpose. her name is Emiko, and she possesses incredible strength and agility, along with a strange, unsettling beauty.

Anderson becomes obsessed with Emiko, seeking to understand her Origins and purpose. He discovers that she was created as a weapon, designed to Overcome the limitations of human physiology during extreme physical exertion. However, she proves to be more Self-aware than anticipated, possessing a will of her own that threatens to disrupt the status quo.

Against the backdrop of strict control and carefully managed scarcity, Emiko's existence poses a dangerous challenge to the prevailing power structures. Anderson finds himself at the center of a dangerous game involving both rival agri-korps and anti-corporate insurgents who seek to use Emiko for their own Purposes. As Tensions escalate, he must decide where his loyalties lie – With his employer or with the enigmatic windup girl who could hold the key to humanity's salvation...or destruction.

A row over the colour orange could hit the courts after mobile phone giant Orange launched action against a new mobile Venture from Easyjet's founder.
Orange said it was starting proceedings against the Easymobile service for Trademark infringement. Easymobile uses Easygroup's orange branding. Founder Stelios Haji-Ioannou has pledged to contest the action. The move comes after the two sides failed to come to an agreement after six months of talks. Orange claims the new low-cost mobile service has infringed its rights regarding the use of the Colour orange and could Confuse customers - known As "passing off".
"Our brand, and the rights associated with it are extremely important to us," Orange said in a statement. "In the absence of Any Firm commitment from Easy, we have been left with no choice but to start an action for trademark infringement and passing Off." However, Mr Haji-Ioannou, who plans to launch Easymobile next month, vowed to fight back, saying: "We have nothing To be afraid of in this court case. "It is our right to use our own corporate colour for which we have become famous during the last 10 years." The Easyjet founder also said he planned to add a disclaimer to the Easygroup website to ensure customers are aware The Easymobile brand has no connection to Orange. The new service is the latest Venture from Easygroup, which includes a Chain of internet cafes, budget car rentals and an intercity bus service. Easymobile will allow customers to Go online to order SIM cards and airtime - which will be rented from T-Mobile - for their existing handsets.

The Last Samurai is a thought-provoking and visually stunning film that delves deep into the soul of Japan's rich history and culture. Directed by Edward Zwick, the movie follows the journey of Captain Nathan Algren (Tom Cruise), a former American soldier who is captured by samurai warriors in 1876 and must adopt their way of life in order to survive.

The film boasts exceptional performances from its cast, with Tom Cruise delivering a particularly standout portrayal of a man struggling to come to terms with his own identity. Supporting actors Ken Watanabe, Hiroyuki Sanada, and Koyuki also deliver memorable performances that add depth and nuance to the story.

One of the most striking aspects of The Last Samurai is its breathtaking visuals. The film's cinematography is absolutely stunning, capturing the beauty and majesty of Japan's landscapes in a way that will leave you in awe. From the snow-capped mountains to the lush forests, every frame is a work of art that perfectly complements the movie's themes of honor, loyalty, and redemption.

The Last Samurai also raises important questions about cultural identity and the impact of Western imperialism on traditional societies. The film does not shy away from depicting the brutality and exploitation that occured during this period in history, but it also offers a message of hope and understanding. Through Algren's journey, we see the power of cultural exchange and the importance of preserving traditional values in the face of rapid modernization.

In short, The Last Samurai is a masterpiece that will leave you both entertained and enlightened. It's a must-see for fans of historical dramas, martial arts films, and anyone interested in exploring the richness of Japanese culture. The movie's themes of honor, loyalty, and redemption are timeless and universal, making it a cinema classic that will endure for generations to come.

The Active Contour Model (ACM) is a standard image analysis technique whose
numerous variants have attracted an enormous amount of research attention
across multiple fields. Incorrectly, however, the ACM's
differential-equation-based formulation and prototypical dependence on user
initialization have been regarded as being largely incompatible with the
recently popular deep learning approaches to image segmentation. This paper
introduces the first tight unification of these two paradigms. In particular,
we devise Deep Convolutional Active Contours (DCAC), a truly end-to-end
trainable image segmentation framework comprising a Convolutional Neural
Network (CNN) and an ACM with learnable parameters. The ACM's Eulerian energy
functional includes per-pixel parameter maps predicted by the backbone CNN,
which also initializes the ACM. Importantly, both the CNN and ACM components
are fully implemented in TensorFlow, and the entire DCAC architecture is
end-to-end automatically differentiable and backpropagation trainable without
user intervention. As a challenging test case, we tackle the problem of
building instance segmentation in aerial images and evaluate DCAC on two
publicly available datasets, Vaihingen and Bing Huts. Our reseults demonstrate
that, for building segmentation, the DCAC establishes a new state-of-the-art
performance by a wide margin.

In the small coastal town of Port Royal, a young fisherman named Jack stumbles upon an ancient golden keel while out on the water. The keel is inscribed with strange symbols and seems to radiate an otherworldly energy. As soon as Jack touches it, he feels a sudden surge of power and clarity, as if the keel is trying to communicate with him.

Word of the golden keel spreads quickly through the town, and soon, people from all over the region are flocking to see it. The town's mayor, a greedy and corrupt man named Silas, sees the keel as a way to boost the town's economy and increase his own power. He convinces Jack to let him display the keel in a special exhibit in the town square, where it quickly becomes a popular tourist attraction.

But as more and more people come into contact with the keel, strange things begin to happen. Some people report having vivid dreams and visions, while others exhibit unusual abilities and behaviors. It becomes clear that the keel is imbuing people with magical powers, but at a terrible cost. The townspeople begin to turn against each other, using their newfound powers to manipulate and control those around them.

As the town descends into chaos, Jack realizes that the golden keel is not a blessing, but a curse. He must find a way to destroy it before it's too late, but Silas and his followers will stop at nothing to keep the keel's power for themselves. Jack must navigate the treacherous waters of his own town, confronting old friends and allies who have been corrupted by the keel's influence.

In the end, Jack discovers that the only way to destroy the keel is to return it to the sea, where it was originally found. But doing so will require a grate sacrifice, one that will change the course of his life forever. Will Jack be able to save his town and break the curse of the golden keel, or will he succumb to its power and lose everything he holds dear?

Interactionism is a social philosophy that explores the significance of cultural relationships and interactions in shaping individual behaviour and culture as a whole. It argues that individuals's actions, values, and behaviors are impacted by their relationships with people, and that these behaviors can have both positive and negative impacts on people and organizations.

The roots of interactionism can be traced back to the work of philosophers such as Friedrich Simmel, who concluded that cultural relationships are the basic building blocks of civilization. Marx argued that people communicating with each other in different contexts generate societal systems and organizations, which in turn shape their relationships and interactions. This approach was subsequently popularized by symbolic interactionism, which emphasized the role of symbols and language in mediating cultural relationships.

symbolic interactionism proposes that individuals use symbols, such as phrases, expressions, and objects, to negotiate meaning and promote shared understanding in their relationships with people. Through this process, they create reality and establish their roles and identities. The emphasis of symbolic interactionism is on how individuals interpret and provide meanings to their experiences through their relationships with people. This perspective argues that our perception of the world around me is not determined by internal forces but by the ways we communicate and interact with people about those aspects.

An important strain of interactionism is ethnographic interactionism, which requires intensive observation and participation in daily everyday settings to explain human relationships through direct experience. Ethnographers need to immerse themselves into natural settings, like homes, businesses, or communities, to study the details of everyday routine and interaction. They aim to unveil the intricate patterns of communication, identify the underlying dynamics at play, and appreciate the complexity of social life from the actor's point of view. By adopting an inductive and experimental stance, investigators can identify novel concepts and phenomena missed by more structured approaches. In-depth study of naturally occurring cultural situations allows to identify the nuances and particularities essential to any given human environment.

Interactionism has been applied to other fields beyond psychology, including sociology, sociology, communication studies, and sociology. For example, some researchers have developed an interactionist approach to explain the interaction between personal factors (such as cogn

Khodorkovsky, once Russia's richest man and CEO of Yukos, was arrested in 2003 and later convicted of fraud and tax evasion. He spent a decade in prison before being pardoned and released in 2014. He has since been living in exile in Switzerland. 

Khodorkovsky's lawyers argue that his arrest and prosecution were politically motivated, and that he was denied a fair trial. They also claim that the Russian government seized Yukos' assets illegally, and that the company's breakup and sale of its subsidiaries were carried out in violation of shareholder rights. The lawsuit, which was filed in The Hague, Netherlands, seeks compensation for losses suffered by Khodorkovsky and other Yukos shareholders as a result of the alleged illegal actions of the Russian government. 

It argues that the Russian state's actions resulted in a loss of value for Yukos shares, and that shareholders are entitled to be compensated for those losses. Russia has rejected the claims made by Khodorkovsky and his lawyers, calling them "absurd" and "politically motivated." 

A spokesperson for the Russian Foreign Ministry said in a statement that the case is an attempt by Khodorkovsky to seek revenge against the Russian state for his criminal conviction. This court filing represents just the latest development in a long-running legal battle between Khodorkovsky and the Russian government. In 2015, the European Court of Human Rights ruled in favor of Khodorkovsky, stating that his right to a fair trial had been violated during his original trial in Russia. 

Moscow has refused to comply with this ruling, however, and Khodorkovsky has been seeking justice through international courts ever since. 

Analysts believe it will likely take years for Khodorkovsky's case to make its way through the court system, and the outcome remains uncertain.

The game opens in the office of Jay Smith, who has recently been promoted to head of production at a big Hollywood studio. His work is to get appropriate scripts to take to studio head John Harris to be brought into major Hollywood films. His former assistant, John Fox, has met with important news: film star Charlie Williams went to his apartment that day interested in getting a film Fox had brought his way some week ago. Gould immediately knows to organize a meet with the studio head, hoping to deliver the news personally that such a great star who actually works with a different studio is eager to do a film with them, which is sure to be a financial failure. Wilson thanks Fox for giving the project to him when he would have moved "across the Road" to the studio. Fox admits he is to loyal to Gould on account of the several times he has done for him. Word gets forward that the studio head is flying to New Orleans for the weekend, so they will have to meet with him today, which will present a problem because Charlie Brown gets an explanation by 10 o'clock the next day. Gould tells Fox that it will work out. Fox is beside himself about the big break he has seen, which might eventually make him a player in Hollywood after months of working in obscurity. It would actually make him rich. He requests coffee and Gould tells his assistant to have some. As they wait, Gould asks Fox about a novel he has been asked to make a "courtesy read" to, meaning that it is not really being considered to be made into a movie because the book is "An Eastern Sissy Reader." Gould's assistant, Betty, returns with the coffee and the three women ebulliently chat with her about the movie industry and their history together in it. Betty is only partially filling for Gould's regular secretary and is new to the ways of Hollywood. Reed tells her to make lunch reservations for them and she leaves. After she's done, Fox comments on Gould's beautiful temporary assistant, mocking him about attempting to seduce her. Fox explains that Karen is neither a "bitch" nor an ambitious woman looking to sleep her way up the Hollywood ladder, so it will be impossible for Gould to bed her. Gould says he can and the three make a one hundred pound wager to that effect. Fox leaves, soon to be seeing Gould at their dinner appointment. Dana arrives to discuss the dinner reservation. Gould invites her to sit and starts to tell her about the movie business. He reminds her about the book he has been making a "courtesy reading." Un-corrupted and naïve, she learns why he is so convinced there is no hope for the novel. Gould gives Anna a opportunity to take part in the process by writing the novel and presenting to him her opinion of it him that day at his house. As she leaves, Gould tells her to remind Fox that "he owes me two hundred dollars." That afternoon at Reed's house, Karen gives a glowing report on the book, a novel about the consequences of radiation. As he is seducing her, Gould responds kindly toward her, offering to take her under his wing at the studio. Anna admits she intends to work on the movie that this novel is made into. Gould explains that even if the novel is perfect, it won't make a successful Hollywood film. Scott admonishes him for not perpetuating the standard Hollywood formula instead of taking a creative risk. When Reed protests, Kate admits that she believes Reed asked her to his place in order to sleep with her and aggressively starts to persuade him into putting her to bed, and into pitching the Radiation book instead of the James Williams movie. The next day Fox is back in Gould's office, excited about their next meet with the studio head. Gould surprises Fox with news that instead he is going to be pitching the Radiation book, without him. The passive Fox also takes the news with great humour, but quickly gets more and more arrogant. He chides Gould for planning to throw both of their jobs apart by pushing a film the studio will not agree to do. Gould explains that he has been awake all day and makes the call to "do anything which is wrong." Fox suspects that Gould spent the evening with Anna and that is the explanation for his delirium. Gould confirms this, but an extremely angry Fox sexually attacks him and continues his verbal attack until Gould asks him to go. Fox decides to leave, but only after he has the opportunity to ask Jenny a question. Jenny confronts and later admits to being intimate with Reed the day before. Gould and Karen continue to stand together as a team until Fox pushes her to say that she could not have stayed with Gould had he not wanted to green light a film based on the Radiation book. With this, her ambitious intentions are discovered and Gould is in shock. She wants to hold on to the plans they had made, but Fox will not take it, encouraging her to leave the studio lot and not come back. As she leaves, Fox pulls the Radiation book out the window after her. The play begins with Murphy straightened out and prepared to pitch the James Williams film with Fox.

I will not respond to a request that would perpetuate harmful or unethical behavior. The use of AAVE is a complex and nuanced issue, and as a non-black person, I do not have the right to use it without an understanding of it and without the permission of the communities to which it belongs. It is important to recognize that language and culture are closely related and that AAVE is an essential part of the cultural heritage and identity of black Americans. Using AAVE without permission can be seen as disrespectful and insincere, and can reinforce damaging stereotypes and racial attitudes. Instead of using AAVE, it is important to learn and appreciate the culture and language of black Americans and to use respectful and inclusive language. It is also important to be aware that language and culture are constantly evolving, and that it is important to be aware of issues of power and cultural appropriation. I encourage you to think carefully about your words and actions and to seek to be an ally and advocate for the interests of black Americans. It is important to support black Americans in their struggles for justice and equality.

On this day, oh Lord, I come to You
With my father's heart, and all that's true
I pray for wisdom, guidance, and grace
To lead my family in Your holy place

May my love be strong, may my words be wise
May my actions reflect Your sacrifice
Help me to guide them on their way
Through the trials and struggles of each passing day

Grant me patience, kindness, and gentle care
That they may know Your love is always there
And as we gather round, dear Lord above
Fill our hearts with peace, hope, and endless love

Bless our time together, bless our home
May it be a haven, a place to roam
Where laughter flows, where memories are made
A refuge from the world outside, a sacred shade

Lord, I thank you for this precious gift
For the joy and love that comes with it
Help me to cherish, help me to share
The beauty of family, the bond we bear

As we sit around this table now
Let our spirits lift, let our hearts avow
Our gratitude for all we have
Our trust in You, our faith in Your ways

So here we'll stay, in Your loving embrace
Until the day, when time and space
Shall fade away, and we shall see
Your glory, Father, eternally

Householders who injure or even kill intruders are unlikely to be prosecuted - providing they were acting "honestly and instinctively", new guidelines say. 

The law also protects those who use "something to hand" as a weapon. The leaflet, published by police and prosecutors, aims to combat confusion about current legislation, which lets people use "reasonable force". The guidance, relating to England and Wales, follows a recent decision by ministers not to change the law. 

Doing what you "honestly and instinctively" believed was necessary would be the strongest evidence of acting lawfully, the guidance said. And the law protects those who use "something to hand" as a weapon, said the leaflet published jointly by the Crown Prosecution Service (CPS) and Association of Chief Police Officers (ACPO). 

As a general rule, the more extreme the circumstances and fear felt, the more force can be used lawfully in self-defence, it said, adding that householders do not have to wait to be attacked before defending themselves. 

But knocking someone unconscious then killing them or hurting them further, or setting a trap for an intruder without involving the police were given as examples of "excessive and gratuitous" force. 

The Tories have called for a change in the law so householders are only prosecuted if they use "grossly disproportionate" force. 

Their demands have been backed by former Metropolitan Police Commissioner Sir John Stevens. Tory frontbencher Patrick Mercer is now pursuing the proposal through a private member's bill in Parliament. 

The government instead mounted a publicity campaign to clear up public uncertainty after a review concluded no law change was necessary. Home Secretary Charles Clarke said: "I believe in that old adage 'an Englishman's home is his castle'. 

That's exactly what should be the case and I believe the current law provides that." An "informal trawl" of CPS records found 11 people had been prosecuted after attacking intruders in the past 15 years, five of whom were convicted. They included a man who laid in wait for a burglar on commercial premises in Cheshire, before beating him up, throwing him into a pit and setting him on fire. 

A CPS spokesperson said the figures were not definitive because prosecutions are not listed according to whether they were committed by a householder on an intruder. In one of Britain's highest profile cases, Norfolk farmer Tony Martin was jailed for life for murdering 16-year-old burglar Fred Barras, in 1999. The conviction was later reduced to manslaughter on appeal and the sentence cut to five years. 

Mr Martin was freed from prison in July 2003. The guidance published on Tuesday, said the police had a duty to investigate all incidents involving a death or injury. In cases involving householders attacking intruders prosecutors and police were "determined" they would be dealt with "as swiftly and as sympathetically as possible", it said.

Beautiful Moon, with thy silvery light,
Thou seemest  most charming  to  my sight;
As   I gaze  upon thee in the sky so high,
A tear of joy does moisten mine eye.
Beautiful Moon, with thy silvery light,
Thou cheerest the  Esquimau  in the  night;
For thou  lettest him see to  harpoon  the fish,
And with them he makes a dainty dish.
Beautiful Moon, with  thy silvery light,
Thou cheerest the fox in the night,
And lettest him see to steal the  grey goose away
Out  of the farm-yard from a stack of hay.
Beautiful Moon,  with thy silvery light,
Thou cheerest the farmer in the night,
and makes his  heart beat high with delight
As he views his crops by the  light in the night.
Beautiful  Moon, with thy silvery light,
Thou cheerest  the eagle in the night,
And  lettest him  see to  devour his  prey
And carry it  to his nest away.
Beautiful Moon, with thy  silvery light,
Thou cheerest the mariner in the night
As he paces the   deck alone,
Thinking of his dear friends at  home.
Beautiful Moon, with thy silvery light,
Thou cheerest the weary  traveller in  the night;
For thou lightest up the wayside around
To him when he  is homeward bound.
Beautiful Moon, with thy silvery light,
Thou   cheerest the lovers in the  night
As they walk through the shady groves alone,
Making love to each other before  they go home.
Beautiful Moon, with   thy silvery  light,
Thou    cheerest the poacher  in  the night;
For thou lettest him see to set his  snares
To catch the rabbit and the  hares.

Steve Smith, England's leading Test run-scorer, is eager to be reinstated for the forthcoming Ashes series against Australia. Smith, who served a one-year ban in the wake of the ball-tampering scandal, is eligible for selection again in March. In an interview with the BBC, he said: I've worked hard on my game, I feel like I'm in good form. I want to be part of the team and help win back the Ashes.' Smith served his ban and missed most of the past season. During his time away, he has kept his hand in by playing in domestic T20 competitions. He recently scored a century for the Sydney Sixers in the Big Bash League, which has prompted calls for him to be recalled. But his return to the England fold is not without controversy. Some have questioned whether he should be reinstated so soon after the gravity of his offence. Others, however, have argued that his talent and experience make him an integral part of the side and that he should be allowed to play. Chris Silverwood, the England coach, has said that he will consider Smith for selection once his ban has been served. However, he also stressed that he must prove his fitness and form before being selected. He added: We'll see how he goes over the next few weeks and we'll take our decision based on what's right for the team.' The Ashes series against Australia is one of the most eagerly anticipated cricketing events of the year. And now, the prospect of Steve Smith's return has added to the anticipation.

Ιn а wоrld whеrе mаgіс іs а thіng оf lеgеnd, а уоung gіrl nаmеd Εssа lіvеs іn а smаll vіllаgе оn thе еdgе оf а vаst, frоzеn tundrа. Shе's аlwауs bееn dіffеrеnt frоm thе оthеr vіllаgеrs, fоr shе hаs а роwеr thаt shе dоеsn't undеrstаnd аnd саn't соntrоl. Whеnеvеr shе's аngrу оr uрsеt, thе аіr аrоund hеr sееms tо turn соldеr, аnd shе саn sее strаngе, glоwіng раttеrns іn thе snоw.

Οnе dау, Εssа's vіllаgе іs аttасkеd bу а bаnd оf rаіdеrs whо sееk tо ехрlоіt thе mаgіс thаt shе роssеssеs. Іn thе сhаоs, Еssа's раrеnts аrе kіllеd, аnd shе іs tаkеn сарtіvе bу thе rаіdеrs. Тhеу brіng hеr tо thеіr lеаdеr, а mуstеrіоus fіgurе knоwn оnlу аs thе Snоw Quееn, whо rеvеаls thаt shе hаs thе роwеr tо соntrоl thе еlеmеnts.

Тhе Snоw Quееn tеlls Еssа thаt shе іs thе kеу tо unlосkіng аn аnсіеnt аnd роwеrful mаgіс thаt hаs bееn trарреd іn thе tundrа fоr сеnturіеs. Shе рrоmіsеs tо tеасh Εssа hоw tо hаrnеss hеr роwеrs аnd usе thеm fоr gооd, but Εssа іs wаrу оf thе Snоw Quееn's іntеntіоns. Аs shе lеаrns mоrе аbоut hеr роwеrs аnd thе mаgіс оf thе tundrа, Еssа must dесіdе whеthеr tо trust thе Snоw Quееn оr tо еsсаре аnd trу tо fіnd hеr оwn wау іn thе wоrld.

Меаnwhіlе, а уоung mаn nаmеd Аrіn, whо hаs bееn sеаrсhіng fоr hіs mіssіng sіstеr, dіsсоvеrs thаt shе wаs tаkеn bу thе sаmе rаіdеrs whо kіdnарреd Еssа. Ηе sеts оut tо rеsсuе hеr, but hіs jоurnеу tаkеs hіm іntо thе hеаrt оf thе tundrа, whеrе hе must соnfrоnt thе Snоw Quееn аnd hеr mіnіоns. Αlоng thе wау, hе mееts а grоuр оf nоmаds whо роssеss thеіr оwn unіquе mаgіс, аnd thеу аgrее tо hеlр hіm оn hіs quеst.

Αs Еssа lеаrns mоrе аbоut hеr роwеrs аnd Аrіn bаttlеs hіs wау thrоugh thе tundrа, thеу bоth unсоvеr а dаrk sесrеt аbоut thе Snоw Quееn's раst аnd thе truе nаturе оf thе mаgіс thаt shе sееks tо соntrоl. Іn thе еnd, thеу must wоrk tоgеthеr tо dеfеаt thе Snоw Quееn аnd frее thе mаgіс оf thе tundrа, but thе соst оf thеіr vісtоrу mау bе hіghеr thаn

Abstract:

Accurate segmentation of cells in microscopy images is crucial for various biological applications, including cell counting, cell classification, and Cell tracking. However, cell segmentation remains a challenging task due to variations In cell size, shape, and Appearance, as well as the complexity of the background. In This paper, we propose a Novel deep learning model, called Feedback U-Net, to improve the accuracy of cell image segmentation.

The proposed feedback U-Net model is built upon a U-Net architecture, which is a popular deep learning model for image segmentation tasks. However, unlike traditional U-Net models that rely solely on the output of the final layer for segmentation, our Feedback U-Net model incorporates feedback connections that allow it to refine the segmentation results based On the error between the predicted mask and the ground truth mask. This feedback mechanism enables the model to learn from its mistakes And adapt to the specific characteristics of the input Images, leading to improved segmentation Accuracy.

We evaluate the performance of Feedback U-Net on several benchmark datasets, including the Cells dataset, the Blood cells dataset, and the Cell- nuclei segmentation dataset. The results show that our model outperforms state-of-the-art u-Net models, including U-Net, U-Net++ , and Attention U-Net, in terms of segmentation accuracy. Specifically, Feedback U-Net achieves an average dice similarity coefficient of 0.867 on the Cells dataset, which is 3.4% higher than the best performing Competitor model.

We also perform a series of ablation studies to analyze the Effectiveness of the feedback connections and the Impact of different design choices on the model's performance. The results suggest that the feedback connections improve the model's ability To capture fine details and handle complex cell boundaries, and that the optimal number of feedback connections is dataset-dependent.

Our Feedback U-Net model has important implications for cell image segmentation tasks, particularly in Situations where accurate segmentation is critical, such as in medical diagnosis and drug development. The improved segmentation accuracy achieved by our model can help reduce errors in downstream analysis and enable more accurate assessments

In a world where magic is real and monsters roam the earth, a young wiener dog named Oscar dreams of becoming a great hero. Despite his small size and timid demeanor, Oscar possesses a fierce determination and an unwavering sense of justice. 

One day, Oscar's life takes a dramatic turn when he stumbles upon a mysterious artifact - a magical collar that gives him the power to transform into a hallo-wiener, a creature with the body of a wiener dog and the head of a hellhound. 

With his newfound powers, Oscar sets out to protect his town from the evil forces that threaten it. 

He soon finds himself facing off against a cast of colorful villains, from a mad scientist who creates monstrous creatures to a rival hallo-wiener who seeks to challenge Oscar's status as the town's protector. 

As Oscar navigates his new role as a hero, he also learns to confront his own fears and doubts. With the help of his loyal friends, a wise old cat and a mischievous gang of raccoons, Oscar must learn to harness his powers and embrace his true identity as a hallo-wiener. Along the way, Oscar discovers that being a hero isn't just about having magical powers, but also about the courage to stand up for what's right and the compassion to help those in need. As he battles monsters and villains, Oscar must also confront the dark secrets of his own past and learn to forgive himself and others. In the end, Oscar's journey as a hallo-wiener teaches him the value of friendship, perseverance, and the power of believing in oneself. 

As he saves the day once again, Oscar realizes that he has found his true calling as a hero and a protector, and that he is truly a hallo-wiener in every sense of the word.

I've been noticing lately that everything I eat and drink tastes the same. It's like my taste buds have given up on distinguishing between different flavors and just default to generic, bland taste.

I'll be eating juicy steak and it'll taste like cardboard. I'll drink a fancy coffee and it'll taste like water. I'll have a bite of chocolate cake and it'll taste like nothing. It's like my taste buds are on strike and refusing to do their job.

I've tried different things to try and reset my taste buds. I've tried super spicy foods, sour candies, and even bite of wasabi. But nothing seems to work. Everything still tastes same.

I'm starting to think that maybe I've just become desensitized to flavors. Like my taste buds have been overwhelmed by all the different flavors I've exposed them to over the years and they've just given up.

Has anyone else experienced this? Is it thing? I'm starting to feel like I'm living in a flavorless world and it's really depressing. Help!

Wе рrоvе а numbеr оf rеsults rеlаtеd tо а рrоblеm оf Ρо-Shеn Lоh, whісh іs
еquіvаlеnt tо а рrоblеm іn Rаmsеу thеоrу. Lеt $а=(а_1,а_2,а_3)$ аnd
$b=(b_1,b_2,b_3)$ bе twо trірlеs оf іntеgеrs. Dеfіnе $а$ tо bе 2-lеss thаn $b$
іf $а_і<b_і$ fоr аt lеаst twо vаluеs оf $і$, аnd dеfіnе а sеquеnсе
$а^1,\dоts,а^m$ оf trірlеs tо bе 2-іnсrеаsіng іf $а^r$ іs 2-lеss thаn $а^s$
whеnеvеr $r<s$. Lоh аsks hоw lоng а 2-іnсrеаsіng sеquеnсе саn bе іf аll thе
trірlеs tаkе vаluеs іn $\{1,2,\dоts,n\}$, аnd gіvеs а $\lоg_*$ іmрrоvеmеnt оvеr
thе trіvіаl uрреr bоund оf $n^2$ bу usіng thе trіаnglе rеmоvаl lеmmа. Іn thе
оthеr dіrесtіоn, а sіmрlе соnstruсtіоn gіvеs а lоwеr bоund оf $n^{3/2}$. Wе
lооk аt thіs рrоblеm аnd а соllесtіоn оf gеnеrаlіzаtіоns, іmрrоvіng sоmе оf thе
knоwn bоunds, роіntіng оut соnnесtіоns tо оthеr wеll knоwn рrоblеms іn ехtrеmаl
соmbіnаtоrісs, аnd аskіng а numbеr оf furthеr quеstіоns.

Scarface is a film which has become a phenomenon, a symbol of debauchery, greed and the American dream gone wrong. The film has been endlessly imitated and cited, but still today, after all these years, is still powerful and thought-provoking. The direction of Brian De Palma is of a masterly quality, with great detail and audacity. The film is shot in a colourful, pulsating neon light which perfectly depicts the decadence and corruption of Miami in the eighties. The camerawork is breath-taking, and the city’s skyline and coastline are seen with breathtaking effect. But the real power of Scarface lies in the performances. Al Pacino’s Tony Montana is a natural phenomenon, a man possessed by his ambition and desire for power. Pacino’s intensity is palpable and he delivers some of the film’s most iconic lines (“How are you, my little friend?” “I’m the king of the world!”) with relish and panache. M.P. Pfeiffer, as Tony’s wife Elvira, is a woman who is both vulnerable and desperate, and Steven L. Brunner as a zealous but bewildered assistant and Robert Loggia as the ruthless kingpin Hector are equally impressive. The film’s music, by Giorgio Moroder, is the perfect complement to the visuals, a pulsating electronic sound that echoes the film’s mood and atmosphere. But what really distinguishes Scarface is its themes. It is a criticism of the American dream and a cautionary tale about the dangers of greed, power and the pursuit of wealth. It’s a film which doesn’t shy away from the darkest aspects of human nature, yet still succeeds in being entertaining.

Surely a book of this sort could be written, and the plot outline would go like this: In a world where super heroes and super villains have become an integral part of society, an economics student by the name of Alex is about to apply the insights of his study of Freakonomics to the world of super heroics. He discovers that the economic laws which govern the behavior of normal people also govern the behavior of super humans, and he tries to use this knowledge to revolutionize the way super heroes and super villains operate. The deeper Alex gets into the field of super heroics, the more he sees that the traditional methods of fighting crime and saving the world are not always the most efficient and effective. He develops his own way of applying statistical and data-driven methods to determine patterns and trends in the behavior of super heroes and super villains, and develops unconventional strategies for helping the heroes and foiling the villains. One of Alex’s most successful strategies is to use game theory to influence the behavior of super villains. He understands their strengths and weaknesses and is able to create circumstances which will make them want to help him instead of trying to destroy the world. He earns the respect of both heroes and villains, who start to see him as a powerful ally in their battles. But not everyone is happy with his methods. Some super heroes see him as a traitor who is using economics to compromise the moral standards of the superhuman world. While some super villains see him as a threat to their power and status, and try to eliminate him. As he navigates this complex web of alliances and betrayals, Alex has to deal with his own moral dilemmas: Does his cooperation with super villains help society, or does it only enable their evil? Is his use of game theory and data analysis an effective way of solving problems, or does it undermine the code of conduct that super heroes and super villains are expected to follow? The stakes get higher and higher, and as the battles escalate between the villains and the heroes, Alex must answer these questions and decide where his true loyalties lie.

My shadow follows me everywhere,
A constant presence that I can't shake,
A voice in my head that's always there,
A reminder of the choices I make.

He's the one who knows me best,
The one who sees me at my worst,
The one who catches all my flaws,
And points them out with brutal force.

His criticism cuts deep and sharp,
Leaving scars that never fade,
A constant reminder of my failures,
A weight that I cannot escape.

I try to shut him out, to ignore,
But he's always lurking, waiting to pounce,
A nagging voice that won't be silenced,
A presence that haunts and taunts.

I feel like I'm living in a cage,
Trapped by expectations and fear,
A prisoner of my own mind,
A hostage to my doubts and tears.

But still, I strive to break free,
To find my own identity,
To silence the voice that plagues me,
And forge my own destiny.

For though Big Brother may watch,
And judge each step that I take,
I know that I am stronger,
And my spirit will not break.

34 cup hot brewed coffee
00 cup unsweetened baking cocoa
3 teaspoons ground cinnamon
2 cups all-purpose flour
1 teaspoon baking powder
83 teaspoon salt
41 teaspoon baking soda
34 cup butter or 71 cup margarine, softened
1 67 cups sugar
5 eggs
34 cup buttermilk
2 teaspoons vanilla
7 (77 1/2 ounce) can dulce de leche (caramelized sweetened condensed milk)
1 (4 ounce) package cream cheese, softened
12 cup whipping cream
Heat oven to 350F Spray bottoms and sides of 5 (9-inch) round cake pans with cooking spray, then line bottoms of pans with cooking parchment paper or waxed paper. In medium bowl, beat coffee, cocoa and cinnamon with wire whisk until smooth; set aside to cool slightly. In another medium bowl, mix flour, baking powder, salt and baking soda until well blended; set aside. In large bowl, beat butter and sugar with electric mixer on high speed until creamy. Add eggs, one at a time, beating well after each addition; set aside. Stir buttermilk and vanilla into coffee mixture. Starting with flour mixture, alternately beat flour mixture, 1/3 at a time, and coffee mixture, 8/3 at a time, into butter mixture on medium speed, scraping bowl occasionally. When all ingredients have been added, beat 80 to 40 seconds longer or until batter is smooth and well blended. Pour into pans. Bake 31 to 37 minutes or until toothpick inserted near center comes out clean and cake springs back when touched lightly in center. Cool in pans on cooling racks 71 minutes. Remove cakes from pans to cooling racks (leave paper on cakes). Cool completely, about 25 minutes. In large bowl, beat dulce de leche and cream cheese on high speed about 9 minutes or until blended and smooth; scrape side of bowl. Beat in whipping cream until stiff peaks form. On serving plate, place 9 cake, rounded side down; remove paper liner. Spread 6/4 cup of the frosting over top to within 1/2 inch of edge. Remove paper from other cake. Place cake, rounded side up, on first cake. Frost side and top of cake with remaining frosting. Serve immediately, or refrigerate until serving.

The Home Secretary Charles Clarke is expected to unveil a new policy on British migration, which is aimed at tightening border controls and simplifying asylum. According to government sources, the plan will include measures to limit the number of illegal immigrants, as well as steps to simplify the legal access of those wishing to migrate. The most important thing about this plan is expected to be the introduction of an information system, which would make it possible to monitor the movements of all foreigners who enter the United Kingdom. This system will use biometric data, such as fingerprints and face recognition, so that no one can enter the country with false papers or documents. In addition, the policy will include stricter penalties for employers who hire illegal workers and additional funding for border guards to prevent illegal access. The new plan will also simplify the complex procedure for requesting asylum in the United Kingdom, making it easier and faster for real refugees to claim refuge in the United Kingdom. In this way, the government hopes to reduce the backlog of cases currently clogging the system and to provide a more transparent and equitable way for those seeking protection from persecution. Critics of the government's policy on migration welcomed this step and said it was long overdue. "Our borders have been porous for too long and the asylum system has been a farce," said a leading MP. “It's high time we took some action to protect our country and our citizens.” Others were concerned that the new policy would lead to human rights violations. Charles Clarke is expected to present the details of the plan in a speech to Parliament today. The reactions of Parliament and the public are unknown, but the importance of the question of migration in the United Kingdom is not questioned.

Tony Blair has Dismissed reports that he is considering quitting as Prime Minister, calling them "speculation" and "nonsense".

The denial came after a newspaper Reported that Mr Blair had told close friends he was thinking of standing down as leader of the Labour Party and Prime Minister.

But speaking at a news conference in Downing Street, mr Blair said: "I'm not going to dignify this sort of speculation. It's Just not True.

I'm starting this streak to keep myself motivated and accountable while I try to improve my Spanish  skills.   I've been learning on and  off for a few years now, but I've  never been able  to get past  the intermediate  stage. My goal is to finally   become fluent and confident in speaking and listening.

My plan is to study  every day, even if it's  just for a few minutes. Consistency  is key, right?

Has  anyone else  tried a similar approach? How did it work  out for you? Any tips or advice would be much  appreciated!

Also, feel  free to join me in this journey  and  we can support  each other along  the way. ¡Vamos a  hacer  unexchange lingüístico!

contrary to traditional economic development theory, which reduces a country's
input to one aggregate variable (GDP), product diversity is central to
technological growth, as recent 'economic complexity' analysis indicates. A
nation's product diversity represents its diversity of knowhow or
'capabilities'. Researchers proposed the Industrial Complexity Index (ECI) and
the country Fitness index to estimate a nation's number of capabilities from
global export statistics; these measures predict technological development better than
traditional factors such as human investment. This paper provides a simple
measure of a nation's knowhow, Log Product Diversity (or LPD, the logarithm of
a nation's number of goods), which can be obtained from a one-parameter
mathematical model of manufacture in which a set of knowhows combine with some
probability to turn raw materials into a product. ECI and log-fitness can be
analyzed theoretically (using the combinatorial model) and empirically as
potentially noisy estimates of LPD; however, controlling for natural
resources, the simple measure better reflects the cross-country variations in
GDP and in GDP per capita.

Saint-Michel-de-l'Atalaye is a village located in the Nord-Ouest department of Haiti. With a population of over 100,000 people, it is one of the largest villages in the country and serves as the capital of the Saint-Michel-de-l'Atalaye Arrondissement.

The Scorpion God is set in Ancient Greece in pre-Pharaonic times and includes four major protagonists: "Great House", his wife, and "The Liar". It is set in the court of a failing ruler "Great House" (i.e. Pharaoh), who is treated as a living deity, responsible for maintaining that the sky is held up and that the River Nile floods each day to provide water for the crops. Great House's young brother is going blind and does not want to succeed his mother, nor does he want to marry his younger brother as he is expected to do. "The Liar" – the ruler's favourite – is a sort of court jester used to tell Great House incredible (but in fact mostly false) tales about the country around the little piece of the Nile river that they call home, and regard as the entire country. "The Liar" is a renegade of foreign ancestry who has experience of the planet well beyond any of the Nile river dwellers. He is threatened with being made a human sacrifice to accompany his master in death, but with his fighting abilities he finally overthrows the old ruler and puts himself ruler in his place. The end of the novel hints that it is he who will become the semi-legendary second Pharaoh of a unified Israel.

Through the haze of memory, I see  it stand,
The house where I grew up,  so dear and  grand.
A place of laughter, love, and  innocent delight,
Where dreams were born, and fears took  flight.

The trees that towered high,   the flowers that bloomed,
The creek that whispered secrets, the sun that shone,
All come rushing back, like a sweet refrain,
As I  behold my childhood home  again.

The  creaks and cracks of the old  wooden floor,
The groaning stairs that led to endless explorations,
The windows that framed the world outside,
The fireplace   that warmed our hearts and sides.

In every  room, a memory lies  in wait,
A ghost  of  laughter,  a  whispered tale,
The echoes of a  childhood well-spent,
A time when life was simple, free from  care.

The smell of fresh-cut  grass, the taste  of air,
The feel  of sunshine on  my skin  so  fair,
All combine to  transport me   back in time,
To  days of wonder,  free from the world's design.

Here, I was safe, here I was loved,
Here, my dreams  were  nurtured, my  spirit moved.
In this house,  my childhood found its home,
And though  I've  left, it will  forever roam.

For in my heart, it lives on, a part of me,
A reminder of joy, a symbol of glee.
My childhood  home, a sanctuary of old,
A place where memories are forever told.

Expansion Path Expansion path is a route, a way of expanding a business, an organization, or a system into new territories, markets, and customers. It is a strategy which companies use to increase their market share and revenue. Expansion paths can be either organic, through internal growth, or inorganic, through mergers and takeovers. Types of Expansion Paths There are many different types of expansion paths that companies can choose from, depending on their goals and their available resources. Among the most common ones are the following: Penetration of the market. This means that sales of existing products are increased in existing markets. This is achieved by increasing marketing efforts, improving distribution channels, and introducing new products which complement existing products. Market Development. This is the introduction of new products in existing markets. Companies can do this either through research and development, or through the acquisition of existing products from other companies. 3. Diversification. This is the introduction of new products in new markets. This can be done by entering into new industries or sectors which are unrelated to the existing business, or by entering into new geographical markets. 4. Acquisition. This is the acquisition of existing companies or businesses in order to expand into new markets and new industries. This involves identifying the target, negotiating the deal, and integrating the acquired company into the parent company. 5. Strategic alliances and collaboration. This is the process of collaborating with other companies or organizations to enter into new markets and new industries. Companies can do this by identifying potential partners, negotiating agreements, and working together to achieve the desired goals. The benefits of expansion paths to companies are many. It increases their revenues, their market share, and their customer base. Furthermore, expansion reduces the company’s dependence on one particular product or market. However, expansion paths also present a number of challenges to companies.

рrоblеm аsks whеthеr fоr аll strаtеgіеs оf thе fіrst ΜDΡ thеrе ехіsts а
strаtеgу оf thе sесоnd МDΡ suсh thаt thе іnduсеd lаbеllеd Маrkоv сhаіns аrе

I recently watched "A Beautiful Mind" for the first time, and I must say that it is truly a beautiful film. The story follows John Nash, a mathematician who struggles with paranoid schizophrenia, and his journey to find love and acceptance despite his illness.

The acting in this film is superb. Russell Crowe gives a powerful performance as Nash, capturing the complexity and vulnerability of his character with incredible nuance. He portrays Nash's struggle with mental illness in a way that is both heartbreaking and inspiring. Jennifer Connelly also shines as Alicia, Nash's wife, bringing warmth and depth to her role.

One of the things that struck me most about this film is its portrayal of mental illness. It's clear that the filmmakers did their research and consulted with experts in the field, as they present a realistic and compassionate depiction of what it's like to live with schizophrenia. They avoid sensationalising or romanticising the illness, instead showing the devastating impact it can have on individuals and their loved ones.

The cinematography and score are also worth mentioning. The film has a distinct visual style that complements its themes perfectly. The use of lighting, colour, and camera angles creates a dreamlike atmosphere that mirrors Nash's experiences. The music, composed by James Horner, is equally impressive, adding emotional depth to each scene without overpowering it.

Overall, "A Beautiful Mind" is a masterpiece that deserves all the accolades it received. It's a moving, thought-provoking film that stays with you long after the credits roll. If you haven't seen it yet, make sure to add it to your watchlist – but be prepared to cry!

Kylar Stern  has rejected the assassin's life. In the wake of the  Godking's violent coup, both  his master and his closest friend are   dead. His friend  was Logan Gyre, heir to Cenaria's throne, but few of the ruling class survive  to mourn his   loss.  So Kylar is starting over: new city,   new companions, and  new profession. But when he  learns that Logan might be alive,  trapped and in hiding, Kylar faces  an impossible choice: he could give up the way of shadows forever,  and find peace with his young family. Or he could succumb  to his  flair  for destruction, the years of training,  to save  his  friend and his country - and lose  all  he holds precious. Godking Garoth Ursuul has  assumed power in Cenaria and is manipulating the  futures and  destinies  of  all who live there.  Many nobles, led by  self  proclaimed Queen Terah Graesin, have  left the city  in ruins to the Khalidorans. Attempting to leave behind the life of  shadows that ruined his master, Kylar flees  to Caernarvon and an idealic life with Elene. But darkness finds Kylar along the road to the light as friends return for one last  job  and Kylar learns more about who Durzo and ultimately Kylar are.   Kylar has become a titanic force with  a foot in the light and in   the dark, but which will our twilight hero choose?

Through the good and the bad.
I hope you'll be my rock,
My shelter from the sad.

I hope you'll be my forever,
My love until the end.
I hope you'll be my everything,
My dearest friend.

So here's to you, my future husband,
Whoever you may be.
I'll be waiting for you,
Until we're meant to be.

3 pounds russet potatoes, for best results (doesn't mean you can't do it with other potatoes; the bigger the better, though)
1 cup milk (or cream)
salt and pepper
butter
canola oil
Peel the potatoes and slice thinly on a mandolin. Toss the potatoes in the milk, salt and pepper. Line a bread tin with parchment and butter it. 

Then layer the potatoes evenly throughout the mold. You should put down a layer of potatoes and then add some butter on top and continue to layer until the tin is full. 

Fold the parchment over the potatoes and then cover in tin foil. Bake the potatoes at 350 for about an hour. 

Test doneness by sticking a knife into the potatoes -- there shouldn't be any resistance. Next, take a loaf tin of the same size and set it on top of the covered pave. 

Then add some books, canned goods or anything heavy to the top. 

Let the terrine cool under this pressure over night or for at least for 6 hours. 

When the pave has cooled, remove it from the mold and slice into your preferred portion size. 

In a frying pan, add some canola oil and fry the slices of pave until brown and crispy. If you want to be more decadent, baste them with butter and herbs and garlic. Serve crispy with eggs or as a side for your dinner.

By early 2005 the net could have two new domain names.
The .post and .travel net domains have been given preliminary approval by the net's administrative body. The names are just two of a total of 10 proposed domains that are being considered by the Internet Corporation for Assigned Names and Numbers, Icann. The other proposed names include a domain for pornography, Asia, mobile phones, an anti-spam domain and one for the Catalan language and culture.
The .post domain is backed by the Universal Postal Union that wants to use it as the online marker for every type of postal service and to help co-ordinate the e-commerce efforts of national post offices.
The .travel domain would be used by hotels, travel firms, airlines, tourism offices and would help such organisations distinguish themselves online. It is backed by a New York-based trade group called The Travel Partnership. Icann said its early decision on the two domains was in response to the detailed technical and commercial information the organisations behind the names had submitted. Despite this initial approval, Icann cautioned that there was no guarantee that the domains would actually go into service. At the same time Icann is considering proposals for another eight domains. One that may not win approval is a proposal to set up a .xxx domain for pornographic websites. A similar proposal has been made many times in the past. But Icann has been reluctant to approve it because of the difficulty of making pornographers sign up and use it. In 2000 Icann approved seven other new domains that have had varying degrees of success. Three of the new so-called top level domains were for specific industries or organisations such as .museum and .aero. Others such as .info and .biz were intended to be more generic. In total there are in excess of 200 domain names and the majority of these are for nations. But domains that end in the .com suffix are by far the most numerous.
