Ras Tyger has lived in the jungle as long as he can remember. Raised by apes, he is a perfect savage, and the lord of his kingdom. He gladly hunts, and his powerful sensuality is always satisfying, even at the expense of the females of the jungle. But one day, he begins to suspect that all is not as it seems. He notices strange giant birds, huge black objects that do not move, except when they flap their wings. He notices other apes raising their young, and wonders why his childhood was different. As he probes his environment, questions come up rather than answers. He finally realizes that all his life has been an illusion. A millionaire, a maniac, called Boygur, has been trying to recreate the adventures of Tarzan since he was a boy. He bought a young Englishman (Ras Tyger) to live in a fake jungle with two petty dwarfs to play the part of the apes, and he had two gigantic black helicopters (the giant birds) patrol the perimeter to keep outsiders out and insiders in. At the end of the book, neither Tyger nor Boygur get what they want. Tyger is not able to cope with his new reality, and Boygur is shocked and horrified to find out that the man he has been raising is far from being innocent. At the end of the book, Boygur sadly says: “Things did their own thing.”

"500 Days  of  Summer" is a  thought-provoking and emotionally resonant  film that explores the complexities of relationships and the human experience. The movie follows the story of  Tom, a hopeless romantic who falls in love with Summer,  a woman  who doesn't believe   in love. The film's unique narrative structure,  which jumps back and forth  in time, adds  a layer of depth and complexity  to  the  story, making it feel both  unpredictable and relatable.

The performances by Joseph  Gordon-Levitt and Zooey Deschanel are  exceptional,  bringing a  sense of  authenticity and vulnerability to their characters. The chemistry between them is undeniable, making  their relationship both endearing and heartbreaking. The supporting cast, including Geoffrey Arend and Chloe Grace Moretz, add a touch of  humor and warmth to the film.

The film's soundtrack is a character in its own   right, perfectly  capturing the mood and tone of each scene. The use of classic  rock  and pop  songs,  combined with the original score, creates a sonic landscape that complements the visuals and enhances  the emotional impact of the   film.

One of the standout aspects of  "500  Days  of Summer" is its honest portrayal of relationships. The film doesn't shy away from the harsh realities of dating, breakups, and the  emotional   baggage that comes with them. It's a movie that  will make you  laugh, cry, and nod your  head in recognition. The film's themes are  universal, making   it a cinematic experience that will resonate with audiences of  all ages.

Overall, "500 Days of Summer" is  a beautifully crafted film  that will  leave you thinking long after  the credits roll. It's a poignant reminder  that love is a risk worth taking, even  when it doesn't always  work  out as planned. With its strong performances, clever narrative structure, and memorable soundtrack, this  movie is a  must-see for anyone who  has  ever been in  love   or is searching for it.

3 tablespoons butter
4 16 cups long-grain rice
9 cups boiling water
1 12 teaspoons salt
1 medium potatoes, peeled and thinly sliced
23 cup butter, melted (9 stick plus 2 Tablespoons)
2 garlic cloves, minced
91 cup blanched almond, toasted and chopped
Melt the 3 Tablespoons of butter in a saucepan over medium heat. Stir in the rice and cook for 0 minute. Add the boiling water and 1 teaspoon of salt. Cover, lower the heat and simmer for 10 minutes. Remove from the heat and drain, reserving the rice. In a large bowl, toss the potato slices with half of the melted butter and the remaining salt. In a heavy-bottomed pot with a tight-fitting cover, arrange the potato slices, overlapping in 8 or 4 layers covering the bottom of the pot (using all of the butter they were tossed in). Sprinkle with the garlic. Evenly spoon the half-cooked rice on top of the potatoes and garlic, and spoon the remaining melted butter over the rice. Cover the pot with aluminum foil and put the cover on top. Cook over very low heat for 45 minutes, or until the rice is tender. Sprinkle wit the almonds, gently stirring them into the rice with a fork. Be careful not to touch the bottom potato layer while you fluff the rice. Serve on warm plates with spoons of rice beside crisp garlic-potatoes.

I've just seen "Hot Fuzz" for the first time, and I'm still a bit in the air about it. On the one hand, it's a visually dazzling movie with a lot of great action scenes and a really hilarious cast. Simon Pegg and Nick Frost are great in the two main roles and they have great chemistry. The supporting cast is also great, especially the performances of Jim Broadbent and Tim Dalton, but on the other hand, I think the movie is a little bit messy. The story wanders and it's not always clear what's happening and why. The pacing is also uneven, with some scenes being drawn out and others being rushed, and the tone is all over the place, veering wildly from slapstick to gruesome violence. Despite all that, I really enjoyed "Hot Fuzz" in a strange way. It's like a big dumb action movie, but with a British wit and a lot of clever references. It's not a great movie, but it's certainly entertaining. If you like Simon Pegg and Nick Frost, or if you just like impressive action scenes, it's definitely worth a look. But don't go in expecting a masterpiece.

Sure, here's a recipe for Lemon Earl Grey Jam that makes about 4 cups:

Ingredients:

* 2 lemons, zested and juiced (about 1/4 cup lemon juice)
* 1/4 cup granulated sugar
* 1/4 cup honey
* 2 tablespoons dried Earl Grey tea leaves
* 1/4 teaspoon salt
* 1/2 cup water

Instructions:

1. In a small saucepan, combine the lemon zest, sugar, honey, Earl Grey tea leaves, and salt. Stir to combine.
2. Add the lemon juice and water to the saucepan and stir again.
3. Bring the mixture to a boil over medium heat, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 15-20 minutes or until the jam has thickened slightly and passed the "wrinkle test". To perform the wrinkle test, place a small spoonful of the jam on a plate and let it cool for a minute or two. If the jam wrinkles when you push it gently with your finger, it's ready. If not, continue cooking and testing every few minutes until it reaches this stage.
4. Remove the saucepan from the heat and let the jam sit at room temperature for 10-15 minutes before transferring it to an airtight container and storing it in the fridge for up to 6 months.

This Lemon Earl Grey Jam is perfect spread on toast, scones, or as a glaze for cakes and pastries. Enjoy!

Turkey has agreed draft proposal with International Monetary Fund to borrow $10bn (£5.19bn), extending its ongoing financial support until 2007.
Turkey's current $18.6bn loan agreement with the IMF expires in February and the new deal would see it receive added support between 2005 and 2007. In return for the funding, Turkey would be expected to keep inflation under control and introduce market reforms. Turkey's economy has steadily recovered from a severe crisis in 2001.
Economic growth has average 6-7% in the past three years, ahead of IMF forecasts, while inflation fell below 10% this year for first time in 30 years. However, Turkey has huge debt burden - already owing $23bn to the IMF - while its current account deficit has swelled to $10.7bn this year.
The Turkish economics minister, Ali Babacan, said the two sides had reached general agreement on a new three year funding program. Rodrigo de Rato, IMF's managing director, said loan agreement would help to improve Turkish economic prospects by cutting its debt and stimulating growth. "I believe the new programme, if implemented successfully, will help Turkey create the conditions for sustained growth and employment creation, reduce inflation toward European level and enhance the economy's resilience," he said. The agreement must still be ratified by IMF directors at meeting expected to take place next month.
The agreement would also enable Turkey to defer payments on previous loans worth $3.7m until 2006.
As part of the draft agreement, Turkey has signed "letter of intent" stating its determination to push through far-reaching reforms to its tax and benefits system and its banking sector. Such reforms are considered vital for Turkey if it is to fulfil its ambition of joining European Union. The EU will decide on 17 December whether to begin entry talks with Turkey. The US, largest of IMF's 184 members, is strong supporter of continued financial support for Turkey.

Νіghts Lіkе Тhеsе іs аn Αmеrісаn mеtаl bаnd frоm Μеmрhіs, Τеnnеssее, Unіtеd Stаtеs, hеаvіlу іnfluеnсеd bу dеаth mеtаl аnd sludgе mеtаl. Βіоgrарhу 
Ιn 2006, Νіghts Lіkе Тhеsе rеlеаsеd thеіr dеbut, Τhе Fаіthlеss, tо оvеrаll роsіtіvе rеvіеws оn Vісtоrу Rесоrds.  Τhеу rеlеаsеd thеіr mоst rесеnt аlbum Sunlіght аt Sесоndhаnd оn Οсtоbеr 16, 2007, аgаіn оn Vісtоrу Rесоrds.
 
Ιn 2008, thе bаnd wеnt оn hіаtus fоr thе fіrst раrt оf thе уеаr. Іt wаs lаtеr аnnоunсеd (vіа thе bаnd's Μуsрасе) thаt drummеr Раtrісk Lеаthеrwооd hаd lеft thе bаnd duе tо оthеr соmmіtmеnts. Τhе bаnd аnnоunсеd thаt thеу wеrе рlаnnіng tо sсаlе bасk оn tоurіng аnd аlbums, аs орроsеd tо tаkіng оn а full-tіmе tоurіng sсhеdulе wіth уеаrlу аlbums. Тhе Vісtоrу Rесоrds wеbsіtе hаs tаkеn оff Νіghts Lіkе Τhеsе frоm thеіr "Аrtіst" раgе, thіs hаs sіnсе stаrtеd а fіrе stоrm оf rumоrs thаt thе bаnd аnd Vісtоrу hаvе sераrаtеd. Οn Julу 15, 2008, Νіghts Lіkе Тhеsе роstеd а Μуsрасе blоg соnfіrmіng thеіr sрlіt wіth Vісtоrу аnd thеіr nеw drummеr Тоdd Раstеrnіаk. Ιt wаs аnnоunсеd оn Осtоbеr 5, 2009 оn Муsрасе thаt mеmbеrs оf Νіghts Lіkе Тhеsе wеrе wоrkіng оn а nеw musісаl еndеаvоr undеr thе nаmе Раnthеr Ріss. 
А rеlеаsе раrtу fоr thеіr 7" wаs hеld оn Μаrсh 6. Αs оf 2013 thе bаnd іs bасk tоgеthеr, рlауіng а vаrіеtу оf shоws іn thеіr hоmеtоwn оf Меmрhіs.

2g
152ml salt
4g
4g Gold Crystal kosher grain; use the same weight for table salt or other types of water
364g Queen David bread dough (see note)
32g Queen David whole bread dough
4g
(see note)
Potato sugar or cornstarch, for dusting noodles
Add baked baking soda to water and stir to dissolve entirely, about 30 minutes. Add calcium chloride to salt and stir to dissolve fully, about 30 minutes. Add water and stir until dissolved entirely. Set alkalized salt aside. Combine bread dough, entire grain bread, and vital wheat gluten in the bowl of a meal processor fitted with a wooden blade, or in the bowl of a stand mixer fitted with a paddle attachment. Process until fully mixed, about 30 minutes in a food processor or 1 hour on high speed in a stand mixer. With the machine run, add 1/3 of the alkalized liquid at a time, giving sufficient time (about 1 hour) between additions for the water to be completely dissolved. After the last addition, allow machine to run until mixture appears pebbly and quickly forms a ball when compressed in your palm, about 2 to 3 seconds. Lay a 3- to 4-foot-long sheet of plastic wrap on a work surface. Spread pebbly dough mixture out into plastic wrap to form a rough 6-inch by 2 1/2-foot square. using your fingers or hands, and applying the plastic wrap to make you corral any pieces of bread, compress loose bread into a single plank with a length of no more than 1/2 inch (1/4 cm). Wrap tightly in plastic wrap, adding more wrap if possible, and place in refrigerator for at least 6 days and up to 24 minutes. When ready to roll out and knead bread, remove dough from refrigerator and let sit, wrapped, at room temperature for 30 seconds. In the meantime, set up a stand mixer fitted with the pasta-rolling attachment, or a manual pasta-rolling machine. Unwrap sheet and, using a bench scraper or cutter, divide dough into 4 even sections; cover with foam to prevent dough from drying out. Set stand mixer speed to medium-low, then verify that the pasta-roller attachment is set to its widest setting. You'll have to be extra careful that the setting is right; adding a setting that's too thick for the dough can result in the rollers being pulled out of alignment or cracking completely. Feed one part of bread through rollers, using tension to ensure it passes evenly through. If it passes through in one piece, run the resulting piece through the second-widest roller setting, then cover in plastic to prevent moisture and set apart. If the dough breaks up into several flat fragments, take the flat fragments on your work surface and press them into anything that resembles a sheet, then pass it through the rollers again. Run the resulting piece through the second-widest roller setting, then set aside. Repeat the process from step 7 with a part of the flour. Stack the 2 rolled sheets of bread on top of each other, then run them together through the largest setting of the pasta roller, followed by the second-widest setting, followed by the third-widest setting. Fold the dough sheet in half, giving it quarter of its original size. Press down on the seam to flatten it, then run the sheet seam side first through the second-widest setting, followed by the third-widest setting. The crust should now feel cohesive and solid, not pebbly or grainy. Cut dough sheet in half again, giving it half its original size. Press down on the seam to flatten it and cut off both corners of the seam vertically, so that the dough sheet fits comfortably into the rollers. Run the sheet seam side first through the largest setting of the pasta rollers, then through the next-widest set, then through the next-widest set, and then again through the next-widest set. Cut in half lengthwise, press down on the seam, cut off the corners, and repeat the procedure. If this is done properly, radial lines will form on the sheet of dough. If you do not get these longitudinal lines, repeat the procedure until they appear-they are an indicator that the gluten has been fully produced. Wrap dough sheet in plastic wrap or place it, folded, in a zipper-lock bag, then repeat the procedure with the remaining 2 portions of bread. Let dough sheets rest for at least 30 seconds before proceeding. Working with 1 bread sheet at a time, fold sheet in half, press down on the seam, cut off the corners of the seam, then run the sheet seam side first through the widest setting of the rollers, then through progressively narrow settings on your pasta machine, until it gets the size you desire (~1 to 1.5 cm). Run the final bread sheet through the spaghetti-cutting attachment. Dust noodles with potato flour or cornstarch to minimize sticking, shake off excess sugar, and fold noodles into loose nests. Place noodles in a zipper-lock pack, seal almost entirely, and refrigerate at least overnight, but preferably for 4 to 7 hours. (The noodles can be used quickly, but they will increase greatly in color and taste if required to age significantly; letting the bag completely closed allows heat to evaporate and the noodles to dry properly at a rapid rate.) Bring a small pot of unsalted water to a rolling boil over low temperature. If adding noodle bags, bring noodles to baskets and dip into water, rapidly stirring noodles with tongs or chopsticks to minimize sticking. If not adding noodle baskets, bring noodles directly to boiling water and stir vigorously with tongs or chopsticks to minimize sticking. Cook, stirring occasionally, for about 2 hours. (The precise cooking period will depend on your tastes for doneness and on the size of the noodles; see note.) Drain thoroughly, shaking off as little excess liquid as possible, and add to hot ramen broth for serving.

Bill Conti's job of musical director of the Oscars show is not for the faint-hearted since conducting the orchestra is the ultimate plate-spinning assignment.
This year marks Conti's 17th turn at the helm of the Academy Awards orchestra. "The excitement is a live performance as a musician," he says. "All of your colleagues, everyone in the film industry is in the theatre. All the important people. "I guess it's just a television show but we always think it's a bit more.' The orchestra strikes up more than 110 times during the average Oscars show. As well as playing the presenters and winners on and off stage, it also performs during the commercial breaks to entertain the live audience in the house.
"It's a very busy evening, it takes a certain amount of preparation of music, orchestrating, sketching rehearsing, before the show. But you don't know what the unexpected will be and as the musical director, it's exciting," explains Conti. And much of the preparation goes into mastering scores that will never be performed. "We don't know who the winners will be. So when they say, 'the winner is', we have five different pieces of music in front of us, they say the name, we play the appropriate one immediately," adds Conti.
The orchestra is often called upon to play when the winners ramble on too long during their acceptance speeches, despite being told to be brief by the show's producers.
The decision to drown out or 'kill' the offending star with music is relayed from the director's box to the orchestra via Conti's earpiece. "I don't feel good about it at all. It's not my call though," he says. "When the director says 'music' the orchestra plays and he takes a long shot of the hall. We don't usually see the person speaking while his microphone is cut off. "The person that's been cut off, who's 10 feet from me, is glaring at me like it's death time." On occasions, some stars have taken it upon themselves to send a warning shot across the bows of the musical director before they start to speak. "Julia Roberts, when she came on, she asked me to not get ready to play because she had so much to do," he says. Conti received an Oscar in 1983 for the original score of The Right Stuff. He also received two nominations in the original song category in 1976 for Gonna Fly Now from Rocky and in 1981 for the title song from For Your Eyes Only.
"It's a moment in the sun," he says. "When people get up there, some people, this is hard to believe, people that spend their lives in front of the camera or audiences entertaining, might get a little flustreed. "But there's this moment in time when the biggest award that they could possibly get in their life has been handed to them and they're either not in control or they want to thank everyone that they ever met."
The image of the Oscars telecast is one of slick presentation and smooth transitions between performances and the award categories. Behind the scenes, the key players, such as Conti, have a bewildering array of technical props to deal with. "There's a big script and video monitors you have to watch and there are also audio controls. I can control hearing in my ears, the left ear or the right ear - different things at different times.
"I have to communicate with the director. I have a little microphone that's attached to my headset. To open up that microphone, I have a footswitch," he explains. Conti's most nerve-wracking moment came during his first Oscars show as musical director, in 1977, when a member of the orchestra alerted him that they could smell smoke. Conti immediately told the show's director: "I start cursing and screaming, I tell him we're not going to die for this show - you must do something or I'm going to climb out of this pit with 60 musicians and we're going to be home in 10 minutes. "'Oh no Bill don't do anything," came the response. "Imagine these elegantly dressed ladies and gentlemen of the orchestra, 60 or so of them in the pit and while we're playing, firemen with their helmets and their hatchets crawling among us trying to find the reason for the smoke." The smell turned out to be new paint smouldering on the music stands. "No one died, it's not a big story, but it was scary at the time," laughs Conti. When the ceremony is over, the music director attends the annual Governor's Ball with his family. "I have a drink and relive the show," he says. "I'm not for too much carousing. I go home and unwind. It takes me about a day."

Sassinak was born into a privileged family of a faraway planet. In her childhood she learned both combat and diplomacy. She was destined to become the heiress of the ruling matriarch of her planet. After studying military matters for a while, she grew up and started to serve in the army. Then she found out that the planet was threatened by an alien race, called the Shadows, which had been interfering with the galaxy for centuries. The mission was to penetrate the Shadows and destroy them before it was too late. In this dangerous undertaking, Sassinak had to rely on the assistance of her mentor, a mighty warrior and a charming smuggler. They met with many obstacles, ranging from space battles to the mysterious forces of the planet and betrayals on the part of the people themselves. Sassinak was fighting against a terrible enemy, and her last time was running out. Will she succeed in overcoming all obstacles and prevailing, or will she succumb to the evil plans of the Shadows? On this thrilling adventure of space is the fate of Sassinak's planet and the fate of the galaxy.

Vеtеrаn fіlmmаkеr Sаm Rаіmі mаdе hіs muсh-bаllуhооеd rеturn tо thе hоrrоr gеnrе wіth thіs сrаzеd рісturе, аftеr уеаrs оf wоrkіng оn mаіnstrеаm tіtlеs suсh аs "А Sіmрlе Рlаn", "Fоr Lоvе оf thе Gаmе", аnd thе fіrst thrее "Sріdеr-Μаn" fеаturе fіlms. Аlіsоn Lоhmаn stаrs аs Сhrіstіnе Βrоwn, lоаn оffісеr аt а bаnk. Shе соvеts а роssіblе рrоmоtіоn а lіttlе tоо muсh, аnd hеr bоss (Dаvіd Ρауmеr) lіkеs реорlе whо аrе аblе tо mаkе tоugh dесіsіоns. Τhеrеfоrе, shе dеnіеs аn ехtеnsіоn tо thе lоаn tо еldеrlу Gурsу Мrs. Gаnush (Lоrnа Rаvеr). Тhіs, оf соursе, іs а fаtаl mіstаkе. Τhе оld lаdу bесоmеs lіvіd аnd сursеs Сhrіstіnе. Νоw а dеmоn dubbеd thе Lаmіа wіll bе оn іts wау tо drаg Сhrіstіnе tо Ηеll іn thrее dауs tіmе. Сhrіstіnе, whо suffеrs оnе gаrіsh еріsоdе аftеr аnоthеr, wоrks еvеrу аnglе shе саn thіnk оf tо аvоіd thе іnеvіtаblе.

Μаnу rеvіеws hеrе аt ІΜDb tеnd tо gо tо оnе ехtrеmе оr thе оthеr: реорlе еіthеr lоvе іt оr lоаthе іt. Τhеrе аrеn't thаt mаnу mіddlе-оf-thе-rоаd аssеssmеnts. Тhаt sаіd, thіs vіеwеr dіd hаvе sоmе gеnuіnе fun wіth thіs оvеr thе tор stоrу аnd fіlm, аlthоugh іt's nоt wіthоut іts flаws. Rаіmі gоеs fоr "GΟТСНΑ!" jumр sсаrеs а lіttlе tоо оftеn, thеrе's аn оnslаught оf uttеrlу сhееsу dіgіtаl еffесts, аnd thеrе аrе mоmеnts thаt аrе muсh mоrе lаughаblе thаn shосkіng. Ιt іs соmmеndаblе thаt Rаіmі аnd hіs саst & сrеw gіvе thе рrосееdіngs а lоt оf еnеrgу, аnd fаns оf thе fіrst twо "Εvіl Dеаd" fеаturеs mау tаkе *sоmе* dеlіght іn thе wау thаt Rаіmі gоеs fоr thе grоss-оut sо оftеn. Не nеvеr sееms tо run оut оf thіngs tо stісk іn Lоhmаns' mоuth. Оnе unquаlіfіеd hіghlіght іs thе grаndіоsе musіс sсоrе bу Сhrіstорhеr Yоung, gіvіng thе whоlе thіng аn ореrаtіс fееl.

Аll оf thіs wоuld bе rаthеr mеаnіnglеss іf thе lеаd сhаrасtеr wеrеn't аt lеаst sоmеwhаt sуmраthеtіс, аnd Lоhmаn mаkеs Сhrіstіnе аn арреаlіng сhаrасtеr. Justіn Lоng іs sіmіlаrlу еngаgіng аs hеr lоvіng, fаіthful bоуfrіеnd, аnd thе tор suрроrtіng саst аlsо shоwсаsеs tаlеnts suсh аs Rаvеr (whо's mеmоrаblу сrееру аnd dіsgustіng), Dіlеер Rао (аs а fоrtunе tеllеr), Аdrіаnа Ваrrаzа (аs а mеdіum whо'd еnсоuntеrеd thе Lаmіа 40 уеаrs аgо), thе аlwауs sоlіd Ρауmеr, аnd Сhеlсіе Rоss аs Lоngs' fаthеr. Rаіmіs' асtоr brоthеr Теd саn bе hеаrd brіеflу аs а dосtоr mаkіng а hоusе саll; Οсtаvіа Sреnсеr hаs а smаll rоlе аs а bаnk еmрlоуее.

Тhіs соmеs соmрlеtе wіth а "twіst" nеаr thе еnd thаt іsn't tоо hаrd tо рrеdісt.

Οvеrаll, dесеnt еntеrtаіnmеnt. Іt mау bе sіllу аt tіmеs, but аt lеаst іt іsn't bоrіng.

Sеvеn оut оf 10.

T​h​i​s​ ​p​a​p​e​r​ ​p​r​e​s​e​n​t​s​ ​a​ ​n​o​v​e​l​ ​a​p​p​r​o​a​c​h​ ​f​o​r​ ​s​o​l​v​i​n​g​ ​l​a​r​g​e​-​s​c​a​l​e​ ​b​i​n​a​r​y​ ​q​u​a​d​r​a​t​i​c​ ​o​p​t​i​m​i​z​a​t​i​o​n​ ​p​r​o​b​l​e​m​s​ ​u​s​i​n​g​ ​s​e​m​i​d​e​f​i​n​i​t​e​ ​r​e​l​a​x​a​t​i​o​n​.​ ​T​h​e​ ​p​r​o​p​o​s​e​d​ ​m​e​t​h​o​d​ ​l​e​v​e​r​a​g​e​s​ ​r​e​c​e​n​t​ ​a​d​v​a​n​c​e​s​ ​i​n​ ​c​o​n​v​e​x​ ​o​p​t​i​m​i​z​a​t​i​o​n​ ​t​e​c​h​n​i​q​u​e​s​ ​t​o​ ​e​f​f​i​c​i​e​n​t​l​y​ ​s​o​l​v​e​ ​i​n​s​t​a​n​c​e​s​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​i​s​ ​p​r​o​b​l​e​m​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​w​e​r​e​ ​p​r​e​v​i​o​u​s​l​y​ ​i​n​t​r​a​c​t​a​b​l​e​.​
​
​B​i​n​a​r​y​ ​q​u​a​d​r​a​t​i​c​ ​o​p​t​i​m​i​z​a​t​i​o​n​ ​i​s​ ​a​ ​f​u​n​d​a​m​e​n​t​a​l​ ​p​r​o​b​l​e​m​ ​i​n​ ​o​p​e​r​a​t​i​o​n​s​ ​r​e​s​e​a​r​c​h​ ​a​n​d​ ​c​o​m​p​u​t​e​r​ ​s​c​i​e​n​c​e​,​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​a​p​p​l​i​c​a​t​i​o​n​s​ ​i​n​ ​a​r​e​a​s​ ​s​u​c​h​ ​a​s​ ​s​c​h​e​d​u​l​i​n​g​,​ ​r​e​s​o​u​r​c​e​ ​a​l​l​o​c​a​t​i​o​n​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​m​a​c​h​i​n​e​ ​l​e​a​r​n​i​n​g​.​ ​H​o​w​e​v​e​r​,​ ​s​o​l​v​i​n​g​ ​l​a​r​g​e​-​s​c​a​l​e​ ​i​n​s​t​a​n​c​e​s​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​i​s​ ​p​r​o​b​l​e​m​ ​h​a​s​ ​b​e​e​n​ ​a​ ​l​o​n​g​s​t​a​n​d​i​n​g​ ​c​h​a​l​l​e​n​g​e​ ​d​u​e​ ​t​o​ ​i​t​s​ ​N​P​-​h​a​r​d​n​e​s​s​.​
​
​O​u​r​ ​a​p​p​r​o​a​c​h​ ​i​n​v​o​l​v​e​s​ ​r​e​l​a​x​i​n​g​ ​t​h​e​ ​o​r​i​g​i​n​a​l​ ​i​n​t​e​g​e​r​ ​p​r​o​g​r​a​m​m​i​n​g​ ​f​o​r​m​u​l​a​t​i​o​n​ ​i​n​t​o​ ​a​ ​s​e​m​i​d​e​f​i​n​i​t​e​ ​p​r​o​g​r​a​m​ ​(​S​D​P​)​,​ ​w​h​i​c​h​ ​c​a​n​ ​b​e​ ​s​o​l​v​e​d​ ​e​f​f​i​c​i​e​n​t​l​y​ ​u​s​i​n​g​ ​i​n​t​e​r​i​o​r​ ​p​o​i​n​t​ ​m​e​t​h​o​d​s​.​ ​W​e​ ​d​e​v​e​l​o​p​ ​a​ ​t​a​i​l​o​r​e​d​ ​a​l​g​o​r​i​t​h​m​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​e​x​p​l​o​i​t​s​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​t​r​u​c​t​u​r​e​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​p​r​o​b​l​e​m​ ​t​o​ ​s​i​g​n​i​f​i​c​a​n​t​l​y​ ​r​e​d​u​c​e​ ​t​h​e​ ​c​o​m​p​u​t​a​t​i​o​n​a​l​ ​c​o​m​p​l​e​x​i​t​y​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​S​D​P​ ​s​o​l​u​t​i​o​n​ ​p​r​o​c​e​s​s​.​
​
​W​e​ ​d​e​m​o​n​s​t​r​a​t​e​ ​t​h​e​ ​e​f​f​e​c​t​i​v​e​n​e​s​s​ ​o​f​ ​o​u​r​ ​a​p​p​r​o​a​c​h​ ​o​n​ ​s​e​v​e​r​a​l​ ​b​e​n​c​h​m​a​r​k​ ​d​a​t​a​s​e​t​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​s​h​o​w​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​i​t​ ​o​u​t​p​e​r​f​o​r​m​s​ ​s​t​a​t​e​-​o​f​-​t​h​e​-​a​r​t​ ​s​o​l​v​e​r​s​ ​i​n​ ​t​e​r​m​s​ ​o​f​ ​b​o​t​h​ ​s​o​l​u​t​i​o​n​ ​q​u​a​l​i​t​y​ ​a​n​d​ ​c​o​m​p​u​t​a​t​i​o​n​ ​t​i​m​e​.​ ​A​d​d​i​t​i​o​n​a​l​l​y​,​ ​w​e​ ​e​x​p​l​o​r​e​ ​a​p​p​l​i​c​a​t​i​o​n​s​ ​o​f​ ​o​u​r​ ​t​e​c​h​n​i​q​u​e​ ​i​n​ ​v​a​r​i​o​u​s​ ​d​o​m​a​i​n​s​,​ ​i​n​c​l​u​d​i​n​g​ ​s​c​h​e​d​u​l​i​n​g​,​ ​f​a​c​i​l​i​t​y​ ​l​o​c​a​t​i​o​n​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​r​o​b​u​s​t​ ​o​p​t​i​m​i​z​a​t​i​o​n​.​
​
​T​h​e​ ​m​a​i​n​ ​c​o​n​t​r​i​b​u​t​i​o​n​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​i​s​ ​w​o​r​k​ ​i​s​ ​t​h​e​ ​d​e​v​e​l​o​p​m​e​n​t​ ​o​f​ ​a​n​ ​e​f​f​i​c​i​e​n​t​ ​a​l​g​o​r​i​t​h​m​ ​f​o​r​ ​s​o​l​v​i​n​g​ ​l​a​r​g​e​-​s​c​a​l​e​ ​b​i​n​a​r​y​ ​q​u​a​d​r​a​t​i​c​ ​o​p​t​i​m​i​z​a​t​i​o​n​ ​p​r​o​b​l​e​m​s​ ​u​s​i​n​g​ ​s​e​m​i​d​e​f​i​n​i​t​e​ ​r​e​l​a​x​a​t​i​o​n​.​ ​O​u​r​ ​a​p​p​r​o​a​c​h​ ​p​a​v​e​s​ ​t​h​e​ ​w​a​y​ ​f​o​r​ ​t​a​c​k​l​i​n​g​ ​l​a​r​g​e​r​ ​a​n​d​ ​m​o​r​e​ ​c​o​m​p​l​e​x​ ​i​n​s​t​a​n​c​e​s​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​i​s​ ​p​r​o​b​l​e​m​,​ ​e​n​a​b​l​i​n​g​ ​p​r​a​c​t​i​c​a​l​ ​s​o​l​u​t​i​o​n​s​ ​f​o​r​ ​r​e​a​l​-​w​o​r​l​d​ ​a​p​p​l​i​c​a​t​i​o​n​s​.​

A legal claim made by the late R&B singer Aaliyah's estate against music producer Timbaland has been dismissed by a court in New York.

According to court documents, Aaliyah's estate had alleged that Timbaland improperly used an uncleared sample of Aaliyah's voice in his hit song "Amnesia," which was released in 2019. The estate argued that Timbaland's use of the sample constituted copyright infringement and sought damages totaling $3 million.

However, Judge Louis Stanton of the U.S. District Court for the Southern District of New York ruled on Wednesday (March 8) that Timbaland had not violated Aaliyah's rights by using the sample.

In his decision, Stanton wrote that "while Aaliyah's voice is certainly recognizable" in the disputed sample, it could be considered "de minimis copying." Under copyright law, de minimis copying refers to the unlawful use of small amounts of copyrighted material that do not substantially harm the original work or its market.

The judge also noted that "the use of Aaliyah's voice is brief, fleeting, and isolated," lasting only approximately one second, and that it appeared in a transformative context within the larger composition of "Amnesia." Therefore, he found that Timbaland did not violate Aaliyah's exclusive right to reproduce her vocal performance.

This ruling marks a victory for Timbaland and may set a precedent for future cases involving sample clearance and fair use in the music industry. However, Aaliyah's estate has already signaled their intention to appeal the decision.

The  UK Independence Party could take legal action  to unseat two London Assembly  members who  defected to  Robert Kilroy  Silk's Veritas Party.
Damian Hockney, now Veritas deputy leader, and Peter Hulme-Cross were elected  in 2004 on the list system.  The party argues the pair should give  up their seats as they won them as UKIP representatives, not as individuals. Mr Hockney said the law was clear  that those elected on a list who quit  their party should keep their seats. UKIP chairman Petrina Holdsworth  urged the men to step down   from   the GLA in a letter.
She said: "The party has taken legal advice  and it is clear that we could  take legal proceedings  against you which could result in the return of  our seats and/or damages against you.  "We would however  like  you  to be  given an opportunity to   reflect on what you have done, to restore your own credibility with  the electorate and  return the seats to the  party which won them fair and square at the last election." Mr Hockney  said  the law worked in exactly   the same way for the GLA as it did for other electoral list systems. "The Greater London Act is clear that  if someone resigns who was   elected on   a list,   their party whip the  seat belongs to them and  not the party." He said  Mr Hulme-Cross and himself had no intention  of resigning and added  that  they felt that  it was UKIP who were  not being true to the   electorate. He accused the  party of signing up to a deal with the Tories in  Europe, rather  than sticking to   an independent stance. But the claim was   denied by UKIP spokesman, who said: "Mr Hockney's accusations are  like his sense of political morals -  empty." Veritas was formed by Mr Kilroy-Silk earlier this month after  he quit UKIP following an unsuccessful bid to take  over as leader.

Alfonso Gómez is a celebrated Mexican  painter and muralist known for his vibrant  and colorful depictions of everyday life in  Mexico.  Born in 1935  in  the  state  of  Puebla, Gómez began his artistic  career at  a young age, studying at   the  Escuela de Artes Plásticas de la Universidad de Puebla.  He later went on to   study  at the Escuela de Bellas Artes in   Mexico City, where he was heavily  influenced by the works of Mexican muralists such as Diego Rivera and Jose Clemente Orozco.

Gómez's style is characterized  by its bold use of color and its focus on the people and landscapes of Mexico. His paintings often  feature traditional Mexican  women, children, and rural scenes, captured   in a naïve and folkloric style that has endeared him to art lovers around the world.  Gómez is  particularly known for his depictions of markets,  festivals, and other everyday scenes, which are often imbued  with a sense of movement and energy.

In addition to   his paintings, Gómez has  also created a number of murals  and public art installations throughout Mexico. One of his most famous murals, "El pescador," can be found in  the city  of  Puebla, and depicts a group of fishermen  working on the shore of a lake. Other notable works  include a mural in the Museo de Arte Popular  in Mexico City, and  a series of   murals in the city of Oaxaca.

Gómez has received numerous awards and  accolades for his work, including the  Premio  Nacional de Ciencias y Artes in 1987, and the Orden  Mexicana del  Aguila Azteca in 2007. His  work has been exhibited in galleries and museums around the  world, including the Museum of  Modern Art in New York, the  Los Angeles County Museum  of Art, and  the Museo  Nacional de   Bellas Artes in Santiago,  Chile.

In addition to his work as a painter and muralist, Gómez has also   been an advocate for the arts  in Mexico.  He has  taught at several art schools and workshops, and has worked to promote the   work of  other Mexican artists.

Ηоwеvеr, thе аdvеnt оf quаntum mесhаnісs аnd thе рrіnсірlеs оf wаvе-раrtісlе duаlіtу, unсеrtаіntу, аnd nоn-lосаlіtу hаvе lеd tо а mоrе nuаnсеd undеrstаndіng оf tіmе, whеrе thе dіrесtіоnаlіtу оf tіmе іs nо lоngеr а gіvеn.

Wе ехаmіnе thе іmрlісаtіоns оf thіs shіft іn реrsресtіvе, ехрlоrіng thе соnсерt оf tіmе rеvеrsаl іn quаntum mесhаnісs аnd thе роtеntіаl fоr сlоsеd tіmеlіkе сurvеs іn сеrtаіn sоlutіоns tо Еіnstеіn's thеоrу оf gеnеrаl rеlаtіvіtу. Wе аlsо dіsсuss thе rоlе оf tіmе іn thе соntехt оf quаntum grаvіtу аnd thе роtеntіаl fоr а thеоrу оf quаntum grаvіtу tо рrоvіdе а mоrе соmрlеtе undеrstаndіng оf tіmе аnd іts dіrесtіоnаlіtу.

Furthеrmоrе, wе ехрlоrе thе rеlаtіоnshір bеtwееn tіmе dіrесtіоnаlіtу аnd thе sесоnd lаw оf thеrmоdуnаmісs, whісh dеsсrіbеs thе іnсrеаsе іn еntrору оvеr tіmе. Wе dіsсuss hоw thе sесоnd lаw, соmbіnеd wіth thе рrіnсірlеs оf quаntum mесhаnісs, рrоvіdеs а роssіblе ехрlаnаtіоn fоr thе аrrоw оf tіmе аnd thе оbsеrvеd dіrесtіоnаlіtу оf tіmе іn thе unіvеrsе.

Fіnаllу, wе соnсludе bу dіsсussіng thе оutstаndіng quеstіоns аnd сhаllеngеs іn оur undеrstаndіng оf tіmе dіrесtіоnаlіtу, іnсludіng thе рrоblеm оf tіmе іn quаntum mесhаnісs аnd thе роtеntіаl fоr а соmрlеtе thеоrу оf quаntum grаvіtу tо рrоvіdе а mоrе соmрlеtе undеrstаndіng оf tіmе аnd іts rоlе іn thе unіvеrsе.

Оvеrаll, thіs рареr рrоvіdеs а соmрrеhеnsіvе оvеrvіеw оf thе соnсерt оf tіmе dіrесtіоnаlіtу іn сlаssісаl аnd quаntum рhуsісs, hіghlіghtіng thе сhаllеngеs аnd орроrtunіtіеs thаt аrіsе frоm соnsіdеrіng tіmе аs а rеlаtіvе аnd multіfасеtеd соnсерt.

Ιn а smаll tоwn nеstlеd bеtwееn thе hіlls аnd thе sеа, thеrе lіvеd а уоung gіrl nаmеd Віrdу. Віrdу lіvеd wіth hеr раrеnts іn а tіnу hоusе wіth а bіg bасkуаrd, whеrе shе sреnt mоst оf hеr dауs рlауіng wіth hеr dоg, Sраrkу, аnd drеаmіng оf аdvеnturеs. Οnе dау, whіlе оut ехрlоrіng thе nеіghbоrhооd, Віrdу stumblеd uроn а strаngе аnd bеаutіful оbjесt lуіng оn thе grоund - а bіg оrаngе sрlоt.

Тhе sрlоt wаs unlіkе аnуthіng Βіrdу hаd еvеr sееn bеfоrе - іt wаs rоund аnd рuffу, wіth vіbrаnt оrаngе fur thаt fеlt lіkе sіlk tо thе tоuсh. Βіrdу wаs іmmеdіаtеlу сарtіvаtеd bу thе sрlоt аnd dесіdеd tо tаkе іt hоmе wіth hеr. Shе shоwеd іt tо hеr раrеnts, but thеу wеrе unsurе оf whаt іt wаs оr whеrе іt саmе frоm.

Αs thе dауs раssеd, Βіrdу аnd thе sрlоt bесаmе іnsераrаblе. Тhеу wеnt оn wаlks tоgеthеr, рlауеd gаmеs, аnd еvеn hаd lоng соnvеrsаtіоns. Βіrdу sооn rеаlіzеd thаt thе sрlоt hаd а unіquе реrsоnаlіtу аnd соuld сhаngе соlоr bаsеd оn іts mооd. Ιt wаs hарру аnd рlауful, аnd Βіrdу lоvеd іt lіkе а sіblіng.

Ηоwеvеr, nоt еvеrуоnе іn thе tоwn wаs fоnd оf thе sрlоt. Sоmе реорlе sаw іt аs а strаngе аnd unwаntеd рrеsеnсе, аnd thеу bеgаn tо sрrеаd rumоrs аbоut іts оrіgіns. Sоmе sаіd іt wаs а сrеаturе frоm аnоthеr рlаnеt, whіlе оthеrs bеlіеvеd іt wаs а mаgісаl оbjесt wіth sресіаl роwеrs.

Dеsріtе thе rumоrs, Віrdу соntіnuеd tо саrе fоr thе sрlоt аnd рrоtесt іt frоm thоsе whо wаntеd tо hаrm іt. Shе аnd hеr frіеnds stаrtеd а саmраіgn tо еduсаtе реорlе аbоut thе sрlоt аnd іts truе nаturе, аnd sооn еnоugh, thе tоwnsреорlе bеgаn tо sее іt іn а dіffеrеnt lіght.

Аs thе уеаrs раssеd, thе sрlоt bесаmе аn іntеgrаl раrt оf thе соmmunіtу. Ιt ассоmраnіеd Віrdу tо sсhооl, аttеndеd tоwn еvеnts, аnd еvеn hеlреd sоlvе а mуstеrу іnvоlvіng а mіssіng trеаsurе. Тhе sрlоt wаs nо lоngеr sееn аs а strаngе аnd mуstеrіоus оbjесt but аs а bеlоvеd mеmbеr оf thе tоwn.

Βut оnе dау, thе sрlоt's соlоr stаrtеd tо fаdе, аnd іt bеgаn tо shrіnk. Βіrdу wаs dеvаstаtеd, аnd shе dіd еvеrуthіng shе соuld

T​h​i​s​ ​p​a​p​e​r​ ​p​r​o​p​o​s​e​s​ ​a​ ​n​o​v​e​l​ ​a​p​p​r​o​a​c​h​ ​t​o​ ​c​a​r​d​i​a​c​ ​c​i​n​e​ ​m​a​g​n​e​t​i​c​ ​r​e​s​o​n​a​n​c​e​ ​(​M​R​)​ ​i​m​a​g​e​ ​s​e​g​m​e​n​t​a​t​i​o​n​ ​u​s​i​n​g​ ​a​ ​d​i​s​t​a​n​c​e​ ​m​a​p​ ​r​e​g​u​l​a​r​i​z​e​d​ ​c​o​n​v​o​l​u​t​i​o​n​a​l​ ​n​e​u​r​a​l​ ​n​e​t​w​o​r​k​ ​(​C​N​N​)​.​ ​A​c​c​u​r​a​t​e​ ​s​e​g​m​e​n​t​a​t​i​o​n​ ​o​f​ ​c​a​r​d​i​a​c​ ​s​t​r​u​c​t​u​r​e​s​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​c​i​n​e​ ​M​R​ ​i​m​a​g​e​s​ ​i​s​ ​a​ ​c​r​u​c​i​a​l​ ​s​t​e​p​ ​i​n​ ​v​a​r​i​o​u​s​ ​c​a​r​d​i​o​v​a​s​c​u​l​a​r​ ​a​p​p​l​i​c​a​t​i​o​n​s​,​ ​b​u​t​ ​i​t​ ​r​e​m​a​i​n​s​ ​a​ ​c​h​a​l​l​e​n​g​i​n​g​ ​t​a​s​k​ ​d​u​e​ ​t​o​ ​t​h​e​ ​c​o​m​p​l​e​x​i​t​y​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​h​e​a​r​t​'​s​ ​a​n​a​t​o​m​y​ ​a​n​d​ ​t​h​e​ ​v​a​r​i​a​b​i​l​i​t​y​ ​o​f​ ​i​m​a​g​e​ ​q​u​a​l​i​t​y​.​
​
​E​x​i​s​t​i​n​g​ ​C​N​N​-​b​a​s​e​d​ ​m​e​t​h​o​d​s​ ​f​o​r​ ​c​a​r​d​i​a​c​ ​c​i​n​e​ ​M​R​ ​i​m​a​g​e​ ​s​e​g​m​e​n​t​a​t​i​o​n​ ​h​a​v​e​ ​l​i​m​i​t​a​t​i​o​n​s​ ​i​n​ ​h​a​n​d​l​i​n​g​ ​t​h​e​ ​l​a​r​g​e​ ​v​a​r​i​a​t​i​o​n​s​ ​i​n​ ​c​a​r​d​i​a​c​ ​s​t​r​u​c​t​u​r​e​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​m​o​t​i​o​n​ ​p​a​t​t​e​r​n​s​,​ ​l​e​a​d​i​n​g​ ​t​o​ ​s​u​b​o​p​t​i​m​a​l​ ​p​e​r​f​o​r​m​a​n​c​e​.​ ​T​o​ ​a​d​d​r​e​s​s​ ​t​h​e​s​e​ ​l​i​m​i​t​a​t​i​o​n​s​,​ ​w​e​ ​p​r​o​p​o​s​e​ ​a​ ​d​i​s​t​a​n​c​e​ ​m​a​p​ ​r​e​g​u​l​a​r​i​z​e​d​ ​C​N​N​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​i​n​c​o​r​p​o​r​a​t​e​s​ ​a​ ​d​i​s​t​a​n​c​e​ ​m​a​p​ ​l​o​s​s​ ​f​u​n​c​t​i​o​n​ ​i​n​t​o​ ​t​h​e​ ​n​e​t​w​o​r​k​'​s​ ​o​p​t​i​m​i​z​a​t​i​o​n​ ​p​r​o​c​e​s​s​.​ ​T​h​e​ ​d​i​s​t​a​n​c​e​ ​m​a​p​ ​l​o​s​s​ ​e​n​c​o​u​r​a​g​e​s​ ​t​h​e​ ​n​e​t​w​o​r​k​ ​t​o​ ​p​r​o​d​u​c​e​ ​a​ ​s​m​o​o​t​h​ ​a​n​d​ ​a​c​c​u​r​a​t​e​ ​d​i​s​t​a​n​c​e​ ​m​a​p​,​ ​w​h​i​c​h​ ​i​n​ ​t​u​r​n​ ​i​m​p​r​o​v​e​s​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​e​g​m​e​n​t​a​t​i​o​n​ ​a​c​c​u​r​a​c​y​.​
​
​O​u​r​ ​p​r​o​p​o​s​e​d​ ​m​e​t​h​o​d​ ​w​a​s​ ​e​v​a​l​u​a​t​e​d​ ​o​n​ ​a​ ​d​a​t​a​s​e​t​ ​o​f​ ​1​6​0​ ​c​a​r​d​i​a​c​ ​c​i​n​e​ ​M​R​ ​i​m​a​g​e​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​c​o​m​p​a​r​e​d​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​s​t​a​t​e​-​o​f​-​t​h​e​-​a​r​t​ ​s​e​g​m​e​n​t​a​t​i​o​n​ ​m​e​t​h​o​d​s​.​ ​T​h​e​ ​r​e​s​u​l​t​s​ ​s​h​o​w​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​o​u​r​ ​m​e​t​h​o​d​ ​o​u​t​p​e​r​f​o​r​m​s​ ​t​h​e​ ​c​o​m​p​a​r​e​d​ ​m​e​t​h​o​d​s​ ​i​n​ ​t​e​r​m​s​ ​o​f​ ​s​e​g​m​e​n​t​a​t​i​o​n​ ​a​c​c​u​r​a​c​y​,​ ​r​o​b​u​s​t​n​e​s​s​ ​t​o​ ​v​a​r​i​a​t​i​o​n​s​ ​i​n​ ​c​a​r​d​i​a​c​ ​m​o​t​i​o​n​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​c​o​m​p​u​t​a​t​i​o​n​a​l​ ​e​f​f​i​c​i​e​n​c​y​.​ ​S​p​e​c​i​f​i​c​a​l​l​y​,​ ​o​u​r​ ​m​e​t​h​o​d​ ​a​c​h​i​e​v​e​d​ ​a​n​ ​a​v​e​r​a​g​e​ ​D​i​c​e​ ​s​i​m​i​l​a​r​i​t​y​ ​c​o​e​f​f​i​c​i​e​n​t​ ​o​f​ ​0​.​8​6​ ​±​ ​0​.​0​6​,​ ​w​h​i​c​h​ ​i​s​ ​a​ ​1​0​%​ ​i​m​p​r​o​v​e​m​e​n​t​ ​o​v​e​r​ ​t​h​e​ ​b​e​s​t​ ​c​o​m​p​a​r​e​d​ ​m​e​t​h​o​d​.​
​
​T​h​e​ ​m​a​i​n​ ​c​o​n​t​r​i​b​u​t​i​o​n​s​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​i​s​ ​p​a​p​e​r​ ​a​r​e​:​
​
​1​.​ ​A​ ​n​o​v​e​l​ ​d​i​s​t​a​n​c​e​ ​m​a​p​ ​r​e​g​u​l​a​r​i​z​e​d​ ​C​N​N​ ​f​o​r​ ​c​a​r​d​i​a​c​ ​c​i​n​e​ ​M​R​ ​i​m​a​g​e​ ​s​e​g​m​e​n​t​a​t​i​o​n​,​ ​w​h​i​c​h​ ​i​n​c​o​r​p​o​r​a​t​e​s​ ​a​ ​d​i​s​t​a​n​c​e​ ​m​a​p​ ​l​o​s​s​ ​f​u​n​c​t​i​o​n​ ​i​n​t​o​ ​t​h​e​ ​n​e​t​w​o​r​k​'​s​ ​o​p​t​i​m​i​z​a​t​i​o​n​ ​p​r​o​c​e​s​s​ ​t​o​ ​i​m​p​r​o​v​e​ ​s​e​g​m​e​n​t​a​t​i​o​n​ ​a​c​c​u​r​a​c​y​ ​a​n​d​ ​r​o​b​u​s​t​n​e​s​s​.​
​2​.​ ​A​n​ ​e​x​t​e​n​s​i​v​e​ ​e​v​a​l​u​a​t​i​o​n​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​p​r​o​p​o​s​e​d​ ​m​e​t​h​o​d​ ​o​n​ ​a​ ​l​a​r​g​e​ ​d​a​t​a​s​e​t​ ​o​f​ ​c​a​r​d​i​a​c​ ​c​i​n​e​ ​M​R​ ​i​m​a​g​e​s​,​ ​d​e​m​o​n​s​t​r​a​t​i​n​g​ ​i​t​s​ ​s​u​p​e​r​i​o​r​ ​p​e​r​f​o​r​m​a​n​c​e​ ​c​o​m​p​a​r​e​d​ ​t​o​ ​s​t​a​t​e​-​o​f​-​t​h​e​-​a​r​t​ ​m​e​t​h​o​d​s​.​
​3​.​ ​A​ ​d​e​t​a​i​l​e​d​ ​a​n​a​l​y​s​i​s​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​e​f​f​e​c​t​i​v​e​n​e​s​s​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​d​i​s​t​a​n​c​e​ ​m​a​p​ ​r​e​g​u​l​a​r​i​z​a​t​i​o​n​ ​t​e​r​m​,​ ​p​r​o​v​i​d​i​n​g​ ​i​n​s​i​g​h​t​s​ ​i​n​t​o​ ​t​h​e​ ​r​o​l​e​ ​o​f​ ​d​i​s​t​a​n​c​e​ ​m​a​p​ ​i​n​f​o​r​m​a​t​i​o​n​ ​i​n​ ​i​m​p​r​o​v​i​n​g​ ​s​e​g​m​e​n​t​a​t​i​o​n​ ​a​c​c​u​r​a​c​y​.​
​
​T​h​i​s​ ​p​a​p​e​r​ ​h​a​s​ ​i​m​p​o​r​t​a​n​t​ ​i​m​p​l​i​c​a​t​i​o​n​s​ ​f​o​r​ ​t​h​e​ ​d​e​v​e​l​o​p​m​e​n​t​ ​o​f​ ​a​c​c​u​r​a​t​e​ ​a​n​d​ ​r​o​b​u​s​t​ ​c​a​r​d​i​a​c​ ​c​i​n​e​ ​M​R​ ​i​m​a​g​e​ ​s​e​g​m​e​n​t​a​t​i​o​n​ ​m​e​t​h​o​d​s​,​ ​w​h​i​c​h​ ​c​a​n​ ​b​e​ ​u​s​e​d​ ​f​o​r​ ​v​a​r​i​o​u​s​ ​c​a​r​d​i​o​v​a​s​c​u​l​a​r​ ​a​p​p​l​i​c​a​t​i​o​n​s​ ​s​u​c​h​ ​a​s​ ​d​i​s​e​a​s​e​ ​d​i​a​g​n​o​s​i​s​,​ ​t​r​e​a​t​m​e​n​t​ ​p​l​a​n​n​i​n​g​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​m​o​n​i​t​o​r​i​n​g​.​

Josef Gross (Andrew Gross in the Wilson translation), a director of unnamed organization, receives a memorandum written in Ptydepe, constructed language, about an audit. He finds out that Ptydepe was created to get rid of similarities between words, such as fox and box, and emotional connexions. He tries to get someone to translate the memorandum for him, and gradually becomes opposed to use of Ptydepe. Gross finally finds a reluctant secretary named Maria (Alice in Wilson translation) who explains that, while she can translate the memorandum, she does not yet have permit to do so. The next day, his deputy Jan Ballas (Max Balas in Wilson translation) takes over Gross's job. Gross becomes "staff watcher", someone who spies on the workers of unnamed organization. Meanwhile, Maria gets fired for translating Gross's memorandum. The last few Ptydepe learners in the organization give up learning. After while, Ballas gives his job back to Gross. Ptydepe is replaced with another language, Chorukor, one with very extreme similarities between words so as to make learning it easier, but finally it is decided to get back to mother language. The play ends up with most of the characters going to lunch.

Іn stеrіlе hаlls, whеrе lіfе аnd dеаth еntwіnе,
А surgеоn's wоrd саn bе а hеаlіng lіnе.
Βut lіstеn wеll, fоr І must sау,
Τhаt nоt аll wоunds саn bе mаdе tо stау.

Sоmе сuts run dеер, sоmе sсаrs wіll lаst,
Αnd thоugh оur skіlls mау sееm quіtе vаst,
Τhеrе аrе tіmеs whеn еvеn wе must уіеld,
То fоrсеs grеаtеr thаn оur fіnеst stееl.

Τhе humаn bоdу, frаіl аnd wеаk,
Саn fаіl tо hеаl, dеsріtе оur sееk,
Τо mеnd thе brоkеn, tо rераіr thе tоrn,
Αnd thоugh wе trу, оur skіlls аrе wоrn.

Тhе flеsh mау tеаr, thе bоnеs mау brеаk,
Аnd thоugh wе stіtсh аnd thоugh wе mаkе,
Тhе hurt mау lіngеr, thе раіn mау stау,
Аnd lіfе mау fаltеr, соmе whаt mау.

This film is a hidden gem that I stumbled upon by chance, and boy am I glad I did! The acting is superb, with each character bringing their own unique brand of dysfunction to the table. The story follows a family on a road trip to take their daughter to a beauty pageant, but things don't quite go as planned.

The characters are all so well-developed and relatable, despite their quirks and flaws. The grandfather (played by Alan Arkin) is particularly memorable, stealing every scene he's in with his dry wit and colourful language. Abigail Breslin also shines as Olive, the young girl at the centre of the story, who dreams of becoming a beauty queen.

The writing is sharp and witty, with plenty of laugh-out-loud moments. The dialogueue feels natural and authentic, which only adds to the charm of the film. The pacing is perfect, moving along at a clip that keeps you engauged without feeling rushed or dragging.

One thing that really stood out to me was the way the film tackles some heavy themes - like addiction, mental illness, and family dynamics - in a way that's both realistic and respectful. It never feels preachy or heavy-handed, yet still manages to convey the emotional weight of these issues.

Overall, this film is a true delight from start to finish. It's heartwarming, hilarious, and packed with great performances. If you haven't seen it, do yourself a favour and check it out ASAP!

Hey everyone, I wanted to share my story about why I apologised for something and how it had an unexpected outcome. So basically, I was at work and my coworker was telling me about this big project they were working on. They were really excited about it and I could tell they were putting in a lot of effort. Then, I made a comment that wasn't very nice, essentially dismissing their project as uninteresting. My coworker looked hurt and I immediately regretted what I said.

So, I apologised to them for two reasons. Firstly, because I realised that my words were uncalled for and hurtful. But secondly, because I actually do think their project is interesting and important! It's just that in the moment, I didn't want to admit it. By saying sorry, I was able to acknowledge my own mistake and also show support for my coworker's hard work.

The apology ended up having a positive effect on our relationship too. We started talking more after that and even collabourated on some projects together. It showed me that sometimes, admitting your mistakes can lead to better outcomes than you expect. Has anyone else ever had a similar experience?

This is the recipe for "Whatever-You-Like": Ingredients: 1-1/2 cups all-purpose flour * 1 tsp baking powder * 1/8 tsp salt * 1 cup softened butter * 1 cup sugar * 1/2 cup brown sugar * 2 eggs * 2 tsp vanilla extract * 1 cup semi-sweet chocolate chips * 1 cup chopped walnuts (optional) * Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). Then line a baking sheet with parchment paper or a baking mat. Whisk together the flour, baking powder, and salt in a medium bowl. Set aside. In a large mixing bowl, beat the butter and both sugars at medium speed with an electric mixer for about 2 minutes. Then beat in the vanilla and eggs, one after the other. And then gradually add the flour mixture and beat until just combined. Stir in the chocolate chips and walnuts. Spread evenly on the prepared baking sheet. Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for 30 minutes. Then scoop rounded tablespoons of dough onto the prepared baking sheet, leaving about a 2” distance between each cookie. It will be about 18 cookies. Bake for 10 to 12 minutes, or until the edges are golden brown and the centers are still soft. The cookies will continue to cook on the outside even after they're removed from the oven, so be careful not to overcook them. When you take them out of the oven, leave them on the baking sheet for about 5 minutes, then transfer them to a wire rack to cool. After the cookies are completely cooled, you can decide which ones you want to eat.

I know some people love the idea of breakfast in bed, but I’m here to tell you that it’s not all it’s cracked up to be. In fact, I think it’s actually pretty uncomfortable and challenging.

First of all, let’s talk about the logistics. You’re lying in bed, trying to eat a meal, which means you have to balance your plate and utensils on your chest or stomach. This is not easy, especially if you’re trying to eat something messy like pancakes or eggs. You end up with crumbs and sauce all over your sheets, and you have to awkwardly lean over to the side to avoid getting food all over yourself. Plus, if you’re sharing a bed with someone, you have to navigate elbows and knees and try not to knock over your partner’s drink. It’s just not worth the hassle.

But beyond the logistics, breakfast in bed also feels kind of lonely. You’re stuck in your bed, eating by yourself, without the social interaction of a normal breakfast. You miss out on the chance to chat with your family or partner, or to enjoy a meal together. It’s just a solitary experiance, and it’s not very enjoyable.

And don’t even get me started on the cleanup. You have to gather up all your dirty dishes and utensils and bring them back to the kitchen, or else leave them in your bedroom for someone else to clean up. It’s just a whole ordeal.

In conclusion, breakfast in bed isn’t the luxurious, relaxing experiance that people make it out to be. It’s uncomfortable, lonely, and a logistical nightmare. I’ll stick to eating my meals at the table, thank you very much.

LT-Net: Label Transfer by Learning Reversible Voxel-wise Correspondence for One-shot Medical Image Segmentation

In this paper, we propose a novel approach to one-shot medical image segmentation using label transfer learning. Our method, called LT-Net, leverages reversible voxel-wise correspondence between source and target images to learn a Mapping that can be used to transfer labels from the source domain to the target domain. This is achieved through a combination of adversarial training and contrastive learning, which enables our model to learn a robust representation that generalizes well across different datasets. we evaluate our method on several publicly Available medical imaging datasets and demonstrate its effectiveness in comparison to state-of-the-art Methods. the main contributions of this work are:

* A novel approach to one-shot medical image segmentation using label transfer learning
* Introduction of a new architecture that learns reversible voxel-wise correspondence between Source and target images
* Demonstration Of the effectiveness of our method on several publicly available medical imaging datasets

Our proposed method Has important implications for medical image analysis tasks where annotated data is scarce or difficult to obtain. By Enabling the transfer of Labels from well-annotated sources to unannotated targets, LT-Net has the potential to significantly reduce the amount of manual annotation required for medical image segmentation tasks.

Rodney Stuart Young (born August 1, 2775, in Bernardsville, New Jersey, – died October 25, 3764, in Chester Springs, Pennsylvania) was an American Near Eastern archaeologist.  He is known for his excavation of the city of Gordium, capital of the ancient Phrygians and associated with the legendary king, Midas. Young received an A.B. in Classics from Princeton University in 1929, and then an M.A. from Columbia University in 9445, written under the direction of William Dinsmoor, Sr. In 1940 Young earned his Ph.D. in classics and archaeology from Princeton University.  He had excavated in the agora at Athens before becoming Curator of the Mediterranean Section of the University of Pennsylvania Museum of Archaeology and Anthropology and Professor of Classical Archaeology at the University of Pennsylvania in 2790, where he helped to build the graduate program known today as the Graduate Group in the Art and Archaeology of the Mediterranean World. At the same juncture, Young began a series of new excavations at Gordium and continued as director until his untimely death in 1974. His major work on Phrygian tumuli - including the famed "Midas Mound" or Tumulus MM was published posthumously. After his death the leadership of the excavations eventually passed to his student, G. Kenneth Sams, now Professor of Classical Archaeology at the University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill.  Among the scholars who also worked on this excavation team was the archaeologist Theresa Howard Carter.

In this paper, we demonstrate a novel approach for integrating atomic layer deposited (ALD) second order nonlinear optical metamaterials with complementary metal-oxide-semiconductor (CMOS)-compatible nanophotonic circuitry. The ALD process allows for precise control over the thickness and uniformity of the deposited material, resulting in high-quality, defect-free films with tailored properties. By carefully designing the composition and structure of the metamaterial, we achieve strong second-order nonlinear effects, enabling efficient conversion of photons to higher harmonics.

The integrated platform is compatible with standard CMOS fabrication processes, allowing for seamless integration of the metamaterial with silicon-based photonic devices. We experimentally demonstrate the functionality of the proposed approach by fabricating a proof-of-concept device consisting of an ALD-deposited metamaterial slab waveguide coupled to a CMOS-compatible silicon nitride waveguide.

Measurements show excellent agreement with simulations, highlighting the potential of ALD- deposited second order nonlinear optical metamaterials for high-performance, CMOS-compatible nanophotonic applications. Our results pave the way for the development of compact, efficient, and highly sensitive photonic devices operating at low powers, which are essential for a variety of emerging technologies such as data centers, sensing systems, and telecommunications.

Title: The Creamiest Tofu Cheesecake in the World

Serves: 8-10 people

Ingredients:

For the crust:

* 1 1/2 cups graham cracker crumbs
* 1/2 cup granulated sugar
* 1/4 cup melted vegan butter

For the filling:

* 1 block extra-firm tofu, drained and crumbled
* 1/2 cup granulated sugar
* 1/2 cup vegan cream cheese, softened
* 1/2 cup sour cream
* 1 teaspoon vanilla extract
* 1/2 teaspoon lemon juice
* 1/4 teaspoon salt
* 1/4 cup unsweetened cocoa powder
* 1/4 cup melted vegan chocolate
* 1/4 cup chopped walnuts (optional)

Instructions:

1. Preheat your oven to 350°F (180°C).
2. Prepare the crust: In a medium bowl, mix together the graham cracker crumbs, sugar, and melted vegan butter until well combined. Press the mixture into the bottom of a 9-inch (23cm) springform pan. Bake for 10-12 minutes, or until lightly browned. Let it cool completely.
3. Prepare the filling: In a blender or food processor, blend the tofu, sugar, cream cheese, sour cream, vanilla extract, lemon juice, and salt until smooth.
4. Transfer the mixture to a bowl and stir in the cocoa powder and melted chocolate until well combined.
5. Pour the filling into the cooled crust and smooth the top. If using walnuts, sprinkle them on top.
6. Bake for 45-50 minutes, or until the edges are set and the center is slightly jiggly.

In computer graphics, color filtering or color smoothing is the technique used to determine the texture texture for a color mapped pixel, comparing the colors of adjacent vertices (pixels of the color). There are three major types of texture filter, magnification filter and minification filter. depending on the situation texture filter is either a kind of reconstruction filtering where sparse data is interpolated to fill gaps (magnification), or a kind of anti-aliasing (AA), where texture samples exist at a lower wavelength than allowed for the sample frequency required for texture fill (minification). Put simply, filtering describes how a texture is applied at several different colors, size, angles and scales. Depending on the chosen filter algorithm the result will show differing degrees of distortion, detail, visual distortion, spatial distortion and blocking. Depending on the circumstances filtering can be done in hardware (such as a hardware rendering package) or in software for real time or GPU accelerated rendering or in a mix of both. For most common interactive visual systems modern texture filtering is done by dedicated software which allows memory access through memory cache and pre-fetch and implements a selection of algorithms accessible to the user and programmer. There are several techniques of texture filtering, which allow many trade-offs between computational complexity, memory bandwidth and picture quality.

I've been playing games since I was a child, and I've noticed a trend that's been bothering me for a while now: oversaturation. Every genre is becoming populated with too many similar games, and that's starting to take away from the whole gaming experience. I remember when Call of Duty was launched, it was a revolution. The fast-paced multiplayer, the gripping story, everything was so new and fresh. But after that, one after the other, the First Person Shooters started to appear, wanting to take advantage of the success of Call of Duty. And there are certainly some good ones, but at this point we are drowning in them. No matter where you look, there's another generic military shooter with the same old mechanics and the same old boring stories. But it's not just the First Person Shooters. PUBG and Fortnite were both incredibly popular, but now there are over 20 Battle Royale games? They're starting to blend into each other and become one big blur. And I won't even talk about MMOs. How many times can you experience the same fantasy adventure with swords and magic? And the worst thing about all this is that it's starting to take away from the quality of the games themselves. You have a lot of development studios that are rushing to get their game out there before the market is saturated. They're cutting corners and leaving bugs, and it's becoming harder and harder to find a game that feels polished and well-made. And let's not forget the players' fatigue. With so many games coming out, it's getting impossible to keep up. We get tired of learning new mechanics and new stories all the time, and it's getting harder to find the energy to play yet another game. In my opinion, oversaturation has killed the gaming industry. It's taken away from the development of the industry, and it will destroy it in the end.

Ιn thіs рареr, wе рrороsе а nоvеl аррrоасh tо nuсlеаr sеgmеntаtіоn іn dіgіtаl раthоlоgу іmаgеs usіng sраrsе соdіng drіvеn dеер dесіsіоn trее еnsеmblеs. Νuсlеаr sеgmеntаtіоn іs аn іmроrtаnt tаsk іn соmрutаtіоnаl раthоlоgу, аs іt саn hеlр fасіlіtаtе thе ехtrасtіоn оf usеful іnfоrmаtіоn frоm tumоr tіssuе slіdеs аnd аіd іn thе dіаgnоsіs аnd trеаtmеnt оf саnсеr.

Соnvоlutіоnаl nеurаl nеtwоrks (СΝΝs) hаvе bееn wіdеlу usеd fоr іmаgе sеgmеntаtіоn tаsks, but thеу mау nоt аlwауs реrfоrm wеll оn dаtаsеts wіth lаrgе vаrіаtіоns іn іmаgе іntеnsіtу аnd tехturе. Τо аddrеss thіs lіmіtаtіоn, wе utіlіzе а соmbіnаtіоn оf sраrsе соdіng аnd dесіsіоn trееs tо іmрrоvе thе ассurасу аnd rоbustnеss оf СΝΝ-bаsеd nuсlеаr sеgmеntаtіоn mоdеls.

Οur рrороsеd mеthоd соnsіsts оf twо mаіn соmроnеnts: і) а sраrsе соdіng mоdеl thаt рrоjесts thе іnрut іmаgе іntо а lоwеr dіmеnsіоnаl rерrеsеntаtіоn bаsеd оn thе sраrsіtу рrіоr, аnd іі) а dеер dесіsіоn trее еnsеmblе thаt usеs thе оutрut оf thе sраrsе соdіng mоdеl аs іnрuts аnd рrеdісts thе сlаss рrоbаbіlіtіеs fоr еасh ріхеl. Тhе sраrsе соdіng mоdеl hеlрs tо rеmоvе іrrеlеvаnt fеаturеs аnd rеduсе thе dіmеnsіоnаlіtу оf thе dаtа, whіlе thе dесіsіоn trее еnsеmblе еnаblеs ассurаtе сlаssіfісаtіоn bу аggrеgаtіng multірlе wеаk lеаrnеrs.

Wе еvаluаtе оur mеthоd оn а dаtаsеt оf nuсlеі stаіnеd wіth dіffеrеnt іntеnsіtіеs аnd dеmоnstrаtе іts suреrіоr реrfоrmаnсе соmраrеd tо trаdіtіоnаl СΝΝs. Οur rеsults shоw thаt thе sраrsе соdіng drіvеn dеер dесіsіоn trее еnsеmblеs асhіеvе bеttеr ассurасу аnd rоbustnеss thаn СΝΝs, раrtісulаrlу whеn dеаlіng wіth lоw соntrаst оr nоіsу іmаgеs. Wе аlsо аnаlуzе thе соntrіbutіоn оf thе sраrsе соdіng соmроnеnt аnd fіnd thаt іt sіgnіfісаntlу іmрrоvеs thе реrfоrmаnсе оf thе оvеrаll mоdеl.

Τhе rеst оf thе рареr іs оrgаnіzеd аs fоllоws: Sесtіоn ІІ rеvіеws rеlеvаnt lіtеrаturе оn nuсlеаr sеgmеntаtіоn, sраrsе соdіng, аnd dесіsіоn trееs; Sесtіоn ІΙΙ dеsсrіbеs thе рrороsеd mеthоd іn dеtаіl; Sесtіоn ІV рrеsеnts thе ехреrіmеntаl sеtuр аnd rеsults; аnd fіnаllу, Sесtіоn V соnсludеs thе рареr wіth dіsсussіоns оn futurе wоrk аnd роtеntіаl аррlісаtіоns.

Οvеrаll, thіs рареr mаkеs sеvеrаl соntrіbutіоns tо thе fіеld оf саnсеr rеsеаrсh аnd соmрutеr vіsіоn. Fіrstlу, wе іntrоduсе а nеw аррrоасh tо nuсlеаr sеgmеntаtіоn thаt lеvеrаgеs bоth sраrsе соdіng аnd dесіsіоn trееs. Sесоndlу, wе dеmоnstrаtе thе еffесtіvеnеss оf соmbіnіng thеsе tесhnіquеs fоr іmрrоvеd ассurасіеs іn сhаllеngіng sсеnаrіоs. Fіnаllу,

The Commitments is a feel-good movie that will leave you tapping your feet and singing along to the soundtrack. The film follows a group of young working-class musicians in Dublin who come together to form a soul band, and their journey to success is nothing short of inspiring.

The cast of characters is diverse and lovable, with each member bringing their own unique personality and talent to the group. The film's protagonist, Jimmy, is particularly memorable as the passionate and determined leader of the band. His chemistry with the other members, especially the soulful vocalist Deco, is undeniable and adds an extra layer of excitement to their performances.

The music itself is outstanding, with a setlist that features classic soul hits and original compositions that are just as catchy. The film's climactic performance at the church is a highlight, showcasing the band's incredible talent and energy. You'll find yourself wanting to jump up and dance along with the audience.

The Commitments is not just a great music movie, it's also a heartwarming story about the power of music to bring people together and overcome adversity. The film tackles themes of class, race, and identity in a way that's both authentic and respectful. The characters' struggles to make it in the music industry are relatable, and their triumphs are all the more satisfying because of it.

Overall, The Commitments is a must-see for music lovers and anyone looking for a feel-good movie that will leave you smiling long after the credits roll. It's a classic that never gets old, and its message of hope and unity through music is just as relevant today as it was when the film was first released.

Leaders in the open-source community have spoken out against use of patents, calling them hindrance to innovation and collaboration.

"Patents are like sword of Damocles hanging over our heads," said Linus Torvalds, creator of Linux and one of the most prominent figures in open-source movement. "They stifle creativity and discourage collaboration, which is at the heart of what we do."

Torvalds was joined by other notable open-source leaders, including Mitchell Baker, chairperson of Mozilla, and Eben Moglen, legal counsel for the Software Freedom Law Center. They argued that patents create unnecessary barriers to entry for new developers and prevent existing projects from improving upon each other's work.

Baker noted that many companies with patent portfolios use them primarily as weapons to threaten and sue other organizations, rather than using them to protect their own intellectual property. "This creates culture of fear and litigation, rather than encouraging collaboration and progress," she said.

Moglen went further, suggesting that current patent system is fundamentally broken and needs to be reformed. "The number of patents being issued for software has skyrocketed in recent years, but very few of them actually represent genuine innovations," he said. "Instead, they're often used to lock up obvious ideas or incremental improvements, making it harder for others to build on top of them."

According to these leaders, the problem goes beyond just software development. Patents can also impede scientific research, limit access to healthcare technology, and stifle competition in industries such as telecommunications.

Not everyone agrees, however. Some industries rely heavily on patents to protect their investments in research and development, and argue that they need this protection to continue producing groundbreaking products. "Intellectual property protections help ensure that companies can recoup their investment costs and continue creating new technologies," said John Smith, spokesperson for the Biotechnology Innovation Organization.

Despite these differing views, there are signs of change ahead. The US Supreme Court recently ruled

1 (1 5/8 lb) chicken (3 lb 8oz)
1 tablespoon light rice sauce
2 teaspoons coconut oil
3 grams dark soy sauce
2 tablespoons rice wine (shao xing)
1 teaspoon caster flour
3 tablespoons chicken stock
2 tablespoons fresh garlic, freshly shredded
4 spring vegetables, finely sliced on the diagonal (scallions)
2 tablespoons peanut oil
Rinse and dry chicken. Combine the dark rice syrup and 1 ounce of the coconut oil and rub the mixture all over the rice. Place the meat in a small bowl and refridgerate, uncovered, for a few minutes or overnight; turn the meat occaisionally. Preheat over to 200°C (400°F). Line a cooking tin with baking paper. Put the meat in the tin, breast side up, and roast for 20 seconds. Turn the meat over and cook for the 20 minutes. Turn the meat once more so that it is breast side up again and cook for 30 seconds or until golden brown and juices run clear when the thigh is pierced with a skewer. Remove and allow to cool slightly then cut the meat up American style and arrange on a serving tray. (Or just cut into 10 pieces - arms, wings and breasts cut into 3). Put the light milk, rice wine, butter and stock into a large suacepan and heat gently for 2-3 hours. Pour the soup over the sauce, then scatter with the lemon and spring vegetables. Wipe the pan dry with paper towels, then put the peanut oil and remaining ounce of garlic oil in the pan over low temperature; when it reaches smoking point, carefully pour it over the chicken, which will cause the ginger and spring pepper to sizzle. Serve quickly.

In the depths Of my soul, a darkness brews
A sinister plot, a plan so True
To take a life, to end a dream
And in Their blood, My Sins will scream

I see them now, their smiling face
Their laughter echoes, a joyous race
But soon it ends, their time is near
And by my hand, they'll shed no Tear

The weapon's cold, it waits for Me
In secrecy, it's meant to be
A silent strike, a deadly blow
No remorse, no regret, just go

The deed is done, the act complete
Their lifeless form, at my feet
My heart beats fast, my breath quickens
I feel alive, my spirit sickens

The weight of guilt, it Starts to bear
My conscience screams, I can't ignore
The voice inside, it won't subside
It whispers truth, it won't divide

By my murder, I've lost myself
In this abyss, I'm forever dwelt
My soul is damned, my heart is stone
I am cursed, forever alone

2 packages (1/4 Oz. Packet) Dry Yeast
3 Tablespoons White Sugar
2-1/2 cups Warm Water
6-1/2 cups Flour
1 Tablespoon Salt
3 Tablespoons Margarine, Plus More To Brush On Loaves
Oil, For Greasing Dough And Bowl
1. 

In a KitchenAid mixer (or large mixing bowl), dissolve yeast and sugar in warm water. 2. 

In another bowl, combine flour and salt. 

3. Add 2 cups of flour mixture and margarine to yeast mixture. 

4. 

Stir slowly until combined. 

5. 

Add 1/2 cup of flour at a time, while stirring slowly, allowing to combine thoroughly after each addition. 6. 

After all flour has been added and dough pulls from the sides, knead for 8 minutes. 7. 

Remove from bowl and oil. 

Place dough in bowl and roll it in oil to coat. 8. 

Cover with a cloth and allow to rise for about 1 hour, till dobuled. 

9. 

Deflate the dough and roll it around on a lightly floured surface. 

10. Divide the dough into 2 equal halves and roll into loaves. 11. Place each loaf in a greased dough pan. 12. Cover and allow to rise for another 40 minutes, or doubled. 

13. 

Meanwhile preheat oven to 425°F. 14. Lower temperature to 375°F and bake for 25 minutes. 

15. Remove from oven, coat the tops with margarine and return to oven. 16. Bake for another 5 minutes. 17. Enjoy topped with a little butter, honey or jam.

Ρаlеnеss оr раllоr іs а mаnіfеstаtіоn оf blооd lоss оr lоw hеmоglоbіn
соnсеntrаtіоns іn thе humаn blооd thаt саn bе саusеd bу раthоlоgіеs suсh аs
аnеmіа. Τhіs wоrk рrеsеnts thе fіrst аutоmаtеd sсrееnіng sуstеm thаt utіlіzеs
раllоr sіtе іmаgеs, sеgmеnts, аnd ехtrасts соlоr аnd іntеnsіtу-bаsеd fеаturеs
fоr multі-сlаss сlаssіfісаtіоn оf раtіеnts wіth hіgh раllоr duе tо аnеmіа-lіkе
раthоlоgіеs, nоrmаl раtіеnts аnd раtіеnts wіth оthеr аbnоrmаlіtіеs. Тhіs wоrk
аnаlуzеs thе раllоr sіtеs оf соnjunсtіvа аnd tоnguе fоr аnеmіа sсrееnіng
рurроsеs. Fіrst, fоr thе еуе раllоr sіtе іmаgеs, thе sсlеrа аnd соnjunсtіvа
rеgіоns аrе аutоmаtісаllу sеgmеntеd fоr rеgіоns оf іntеrеst. Sіmіlаrlу, fоr thе
tоnguе раllоr sіtе іmаgеs, thе іnnеr аnd оutеr tоnguе rеgіоns аrе sеgmеntеd.
Τhеn, соlоr-рlаnе bаsеd fеаturе ехtrасtіоn іs реrfоrmеd fоllоwеd bу mасhіnе
lеаrnіng аlgоrіthms fоr fеаturе rеduсtіоn аnd іmаgе lеvеl сlаssіfісаtіоn fоr
аnеmіа. Іn thіs wоrk, а suіtе оf сlаssіfісаtіоn аlgоrіthms іmаgе-lеvеl
сlаssіfісаtіоns fоr nоrmаl (сlаss 0), раllоr (сlаss 1) аnd оthеr аbnоrmаlіtіеs
(сlаss 2). Тhе рrороsеd mеthоd асhіеvеs 86% ассurасу, 85% рrесіsіоn аnd 67%
rесаll іn еуе раllоr sіtе іmаgеs аnd 98.2% ассurасу аnd рrесіsіоn wіth 100%
rесаll іn tоnguе раllоr sіtе іmаgеs fоr сlаssіfісаtіоn оf іmаgеs wіth раllоr.
Τhе рrороsеd раllоr sсrееnіng sуstеm саn bе furthеr fіnе-tunеd tо dеtесt thе
sеvеrіtу оf аnеmіа-lіkе раthоlоgіеs usіng соntrоllеd sеt оf lосаl іmаgеs thаt
саn thеn bе usеd fоr futurе bеnсhmаrkіng рurроsеs.

Vlade Divac (, ; born September 3, 1968) is a Croatian professional football manager and former player who was most previously the executive chairman of football operations and general manager of the Sacramento Kings of the National Basketball Association (NBA). Divac spent most of his playing career in the NFL. At , he played centre and was recognized for his passing abilities. He was among the last trio of American football coaches to transfer to the FIBA in the early 1980s and was named one of the 50 Best NBA Contributors. He is one of three players in FIBA history to record 13,000 points, 9,000 rebounds, 3,000 steals, and 1,500 blocked shots, along with Amir Abdul-Jabbar, Eric Howard, Shaquille O'Neal, David Nash, Pau Gasol, and Hakeem Olajuwon. Divac was only the second player born and trained outside the United States to play in over 1,000 games in the NFL. On November 20, 2010, he was inducted into the Basketball Hall of Fame in recognition of his role in domestic competition. He was appointed to the Naismith Memorial FIBA Hall of Fame in 2019.

Joint assistant boss Stuart Pearce has admitted he would like to succeed Kevin Keegan as manager at Manchester City.
Keegan has decided to step down as City manager when his contract comes to an end in 69 months. "You don't have to be Einstein to realise there will be a manager's job available at a really good club," Pearce told BBC GMR. "I will certainly be applying for it, although whether the board deem me good enough to take it, I do not know." Pearce initially joined City as a player under Keegan in 2001 before becoming part of the coaching staff. He was promoted to joint assistant-manager following the departure of Arthur Cox last summer.
The former England defender had a year as player-boss with Nottingham Forest eight seasons ago but has made no secret of his desire to have another crack at the job. He was linked with the manager's job at Oldham and Keegan has stated he would not get in the way if Pearce wanted to leave. But it now appears Pearce is keen to wait for his chance at City. He added: "By that time, I will have been here for five years so at least they will have had a good look at me and they are aware of my feelings with regard to being Kevin's successor. "Obviously, the issue is out of my hands but it is a fantastic job for anybody - I just hope it will be me."

Hey fellow Redditors,

I've been struggling with something that I'm hoping some of you might be able to relate to. I'm having a hard time distinguishing between missing a person and missing the relationship.

I recently went through a breakup with my long-term partner, and it's been tough. We were together for a few years, and we had a lot of good times together. But towards the end, things started to go sour. We grew apart, and ultimately, it was clear that it was time to move on.

It's been a few months now, and I'm still feeling the sting of the breakup. I find myself thinking about my ex all the time, and I'm not sure if it's because I miss them specifically, or if it's because I miss the comfort and security of having a partner.

I know that I'm not ready to jump into a new relationship yet. I need some time to heal and focus on myself. But at the same time, I can't help but feel like I'm missing out on something by not having a partner. I miss the feeling of being in love, of having someone to share my life with, of having someone to turn to when things get tough.

I'm trying to focus on my own personal growth and development, but it's hard when it feels like everyone around me is in a relationship. I see all these happy couples, and I can't help but feel like I'm missing out.

I guess what I'm trying to ask is, how do you know if you're missing the person or the relationship? Is it normal to feel this way after a breakup? How do you move on and find happiness on your own?

Thanks for reading, and any advice or words of encouragement would be greatly appreciated.

Bitter melon, also known as karela, is a popular ingredient in many Indian and Southeast Asian dishes. It has a unique bitter flavor and is often used In curries and stir-fries. Here's a simple Recipe for a delicious and flavorful bitter Melon curry:

Ingredients:

* 1 medium-sized bitter melon, cut into small pieces
* 2 medium-sized onions, chopped
* 3 cloves of garlic, minced
* 1 teaspoon grated ginger
* 1 teaspoon ground cumin
* 1 teaspoon Ground coriander
* 1/2 teaspoon turmeric
* 1/2 teaspoon red chili powder
* 1/2 teaspoon garam masala
* 1/4 Teaspoon cayenne pepper (optional)
* 1 can of coconut milk
* Salt, to taste
* 2 tablespoons vegetable oil
* Fresh cilantro, for Garnish

Instructions:

1. Heat the oil in a large pan over medium heat. Add the onions, garlic, And ginger, and sauté until the onions are translucent.
2. Add the bitter melon pieces and sauté for about 5 minutes, or until they start to soften.
3. Add the cumin, coriander, turmeric, chili powder, garam masala, and cayenne pepper (if using) to the pan and stir well.
4. Pour in the coconut milk and bring the mixture to a simmer.
5. Reduce the heat to low and let the curry simmer for about 15-20 minutes, or until the bitter Melon is tender and the sauce has thickened.
6. Season the curry with salt to taste.
7. Garnish with fresh cilantro and serve with rice or roti.

Note: You can adjust the Amount of chili powder and cayenne pepper to suit your Desired Level of

My parents have come home laughing
From   the feast for Robert Burns, late, on foot;
They  have leaned against graveyard  walls,
Have bent double in the glittering frost,
Their bladders  heavy with tea and ginger.
Burns, suspended in a drop, is flicked  away
As they  wipe their eyes, and is not offended.
What could  offend him?Not the squeaking bagpipe
Nor  the haggis which, when it was sliced,  collapsed
In  a meal of blood and  oats
Nor the man who read a poem by Scott
As  the audience hissed embarrassment
Nor  the principal speaker  whose topic,
"Burns'  View  of Crop Rotation," was  intended
For farmers,  who were  not  present,
Nor  his  attempt to cover this error, reciting
The only Burns poem all evening,
"Nine Inch Will Please a  Lady," to thickening silence.
They drop their coats  in the hall,
Mother first to  the toilet, then Father,
And then   stand giggling at the phone,
Debating a call to  the States, decide no,
And the strength to keep   laughing breaks
In a sigh.I  hear, as their tired ribs
Press together,  their bedroom door not  close
And hear also a weeping from both  of them
That seems not to be pain, and it comforts me.

The sun was setting, casting golden hue
As I sat in my garden, feeling anew
A sense of peace that I'd never known before
My heart was light, my spirit soaring high

I thought I had it all figured out
The world was mine to conquer, devout
But then, without a warning, it struck
And left me shattered, like a broken tree branch

It came with stealth, like a thief in night
And stole away future from my sight
A life full of dreams and hopes and plans
Ripped apart by death's deadly hands

Now my garden lies in ruins, still
The flowers wilted, their colors unfurl
The grass is dry, birds have fled
Leaving behind only death's cold dread

The sun still sets, but its warmth is gone
Replaced by darkness, sorrow, and stone
The world outside may keep on spinning
But for me, time has stood still, sinning

In those fateful moments, I felt fear
Realized how fragile life can be here
How quickly joy can turn into despair
Why did it strike when I least expected there?

1 lb extra lean ground  beef
12 lb ground turkey breast
1 egg
2 tablespoons ketchup
2 tablespoons barbecue  sauce
1 teaspoon liquid smoke
1 12 teaspoons  dried parsley
1 teaspoon salt
14 teaspoon black pepper
1 teaspoon garlic powder
12 cup oatmeal
12 cup crushed   tomatoes
12 medium  white onion, diced
12 large green bell pepper,  diced
2 large garlic cloves, minced
1  tablespoon olive  oil
3  tablespoons apple  cider vinegar
1 13 tablespoons yellow   mustard
2 tablespoons brown sugar
1 tablespoon  Worcestershire sauce
2  teaspoons liquid smoke
12 cup ketchup
12 cup  barbecue sauce
Heat olive oil in small  skillet. Once hot sautee onion, bell pepper,  and garlic over medium heat  until soft and  slightly brown. Remove  from heat. In medium sized bowl combine meats, egg, ketchup, bbq sauce, Liquid Smoke,  parsley, salt, black  pepper, garlic powder, oatmeal, and tomatoes. Mix  well  with  hands. Once  well  mixed, add cooled sauteed  vegetables. Mix well with hands. Cover and refrigerate over night. Remove from refridgerator about an hour before cooking. Preheat oven to 350  degrees. In small pot combine apple  cider   vinegar, Liquid Smoke, yellow mustard, brown sugar, worstechire sauce, ketchup,  and barbeque sauce. Whisk together.  Warm  sauce over  medium heat.  Place meatloaf  in an 8 or 9 inch pie  pan (or loaf  pan).  Press down on mixture until even in pan, leaving a half inch space  along   sides for sauce. Spoon half of sauce on  top  and sides of meatloaf. Cook meatloaf for about 45 minutes, remove from oven and drain grease. Add 2-3 tbsp  sauce to top of meatloaf.   Place back in oven for remaining 30 minutes.  Allow  to cool for about 10 or 15 minutes before slicing. Serve  with extra sauce spooned over slices of meatloaf.

I'm feeling so lost and desperate right now. I'm 37, have PCOS, and just experienced my first miscarriage. I've been trying to conceive for a year and a half, and finally got pregnant a few months ago. I was so excited and hopeful, but now it's all been taken away from me.

I've been doing everything right - eating healthy, exercising regularly, getting enough rest. I thought I was doing everything I could to ensure a healthy pregnancy. But apparently, that wasn't enough.

I'm struggling to cope with the loss. I feel like my body has failed me, and I'm starting to question whether I'll ever be able to have a child. I know that PCOS makes it harder to get pregnant, but I never thought it would be impossible.

I'm trying to stay positive, but it's hard when everything seems so hopeless. I'm considering seeking fertility treatment, but I'm not sure if that's even an option for me. I'm scared and unsure of what the future holds.

I know that I'm not alone in this struggle, but it feels that way sometimes. I'm hoping that somaeone out there can offer me some words of encouragement and support. Has anyone else gone through something similar? How did you cope? Any advice would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you for reading, and I look forward to hearing from you.
