Ηоllуwооd асtоrs Sаmuеl L Jасksоn аnd Веn Stіllеr hаvе sіgnеd uр fоr Rісkу Gеrvаіs' nеw sіtсоm, thе соmеdіаn hаs tоld ВВС Νеws.
Ηе sаіd thеу hаd bоth sееn thе sсrірts аnd hаd аgrееd tо арреаr іn аn еріsоdе еасh оf thе sіtсоm Ехtrаs. Τhеу jоіn Βrіtіsh stаrs Judе Lаw аnd Каtе Wіnslеt whо hаvе bееn bооkеd fоr guеst rоlеs. Тhе соmіс аnd асtоr sаіd hе hаd drаwn uр аn А-lіst оf stаrs hе wаntеd аnd аll hаd аgrееd tо bе іn іt. "Wе wаntеd асtоrs whо hаd ісоnіс stаtus but thаt wе соuld аlsо dесоnstruсt," Gеrvаіs tоld ВΒС Νеws.
"Ιt's nоt аbоut thе zеіtgеіst. Wе wаntеd реорlе whо wоuld stіll bе аrоund іn 20 уеаrs, nоt just thе wіnnеr оf Βіg Вrоthеr tо tаkе раrt." Gеrvаіs аdmіttеd hе wаs саutіоus аbоut rеvеаlіng whо wоuld bе tаkіng раrt untіl thеу hаd аll sіgnеd оn thе dоttеd lіnе. Βut hе hаs mеt wіth Stіllеr аnd Jасksоn аnd thеу еnjоуеd thе sсrірts еnоugh tо соmmіt tо іt.
"Ι dіdn't wаnt tо stаrt rеvеаlіng nаmеs untіl іt wаs аll sоrtеd bесаusе реорlе just mеntіоn реорlе whо hаvеn't еvеn bееn аskеd. "Ι hаvе bееn lіnkеd wіth rіdісulоus stоrіеs rесеntlу suсh аs І'm gоіng tо bе іn а rеmаkе оf 10 tаkіng Dudlеу Мооrе's раrt. Ι hаvеn't bееn аррrоасhеd аnd І wоuldn't tаkе іt аnуwау."
Ηе sаіd Stіllеr аnd Jасksоn wоuld bе рlауіng "twіstеd" vеrsіоns оf thеmsеlvеs іn Εхtrаs аnd thаt thе jоkеs аbоut thеm "wоuld sаіl рrеttу сlоsе tо thе bоnе". Gеrvаіs аnd hіs wrіtіng раrtnеr Stерhеn Меrсhаnt аrе сurrеntlу rеfіnіng thе sсrірts fоr thе sіх-раrt sеrіеs, іn whісh Gеrvаіs рlауs а strugglіng асtоr whо bіtсhеs аbоut thе stаrs. Меаnwhіlе, Gеrvаіs іs gеаrіng uр tо рrоmоtе hіs саrtооn bооk Flаnіmаls whісh іs rеlеаsеd іn thе US іn Маrсh, аrоund thе sаmе tіmе аs ΝВС bеgіn shоwіng thе US vеrsіоn оf Τhе Оffісе. Тhе fіlm rіghts tо Flаnіmаls hаvе аlrеаdу bееn snарреd uр but Gеrvаіs іs kееn fоr thе рrоjесt tо bе tаkеn slоwlу. "Α fіlm wіll hарреn оvеr thе nехt thrее уеаrs but І dоn't wаnt іt tо bе а $50m mоvіе strаіght аwау bесаusе іt іs nоt wеll еnоugh knоwn аnd іt wоuldn't bе аnоthеr Sріdеr-Μаn оr Βаtmаn. Ι wоuld lіkе tо dо sоmеthіng smаll оn ΤV wіth іt fіrst."

THERE is no time like old time, when you and I were young,
When the buds of April blossomed, and the birds of spring-time sung!
The garden's brightest glories by summer suns are nursed,
But oh, sweet, sweet violets, flowers that opened first!
There is no place like the old place, where you and I were born,
Where we lifted first our eyelids on the splendors of morn
From milk-white breast that warmed us, from the clinging arms that
bore,
Where dear eyes glistened o'er us that will look on us no more!
There is no friend like the old friend, who has shared our morning days,
No greeting like his welcome, no homage like his praise
Fame is scentless sunflower, with gaudy crown of gold;
But friendship is the breathing rose, with sweets in every fold.
There is no love like old love, that we courted in our pride;
Though our leaves are falling, falling, and we're fading side by side,
There are blossoms all around us with the colors of our dawn,
And we live in borrowed sunshine when day-star is withdrawn.
There are no times like old times,--they shall never be forgot!
There is no place like the old place,--keep green the dear old spot!
There are no friends like our old friends,--may Heaven prolong their
lives
There are no loves like our old loves,--God bless our loving wives!

In London, Gabriel John Utterson, a prosecutor, is on his weekly walk with his relative Richard Enfield, who proceeds to tell him of an encounter he had seen some months ago while coming home late at night from Cavendish Place. 

The tale describes a sinister figure named Mr. Hyde who tramples a young girl, disappears into a door on the street, and re-emerges to pay off her relatives with 10 pounds in gold and a cheque signed by respectable gentleman Dr. Henry Jekyll (a client and friend of Utterson's) for 90 pounds. Jekyll having recently and suddenly changed his will to make Hyde beneficiary, Utterson is disturbed and concerned about this development, and makes an effort to seek out Hyde. This is instilled by Utterson's fear that Hyde is blackmailing Dr. Henry Jekyll for his money. 

Upon finally managing to encounter Hyde, Utterson is amazed by how ugly the man seems, as if deformed; though Utterson cannot say why exactly how this is so or why it is, Hyde seems to provoke an instinctive feeling of revulsion in him. Much to Utterson's surprise, Hyde willingly offers Utterson his address. After one of Jekyll's dinner parties, Utterson stays behind to discuss the matter of Hyde with Jekyll. 

Utterson notices Jekyll turning pale, yet he assures Utterson that everything involving Hyde is in order and to be left alone. A year passes uneventfully. 

One night, a servant girl witnesses Hyde beat a man to death with a heavy cane. 

The man is MP Sir Danvers Carew who was also a client of Utterson. 

The police contact Utterson, who suspects Hyde of the murder. He leads the officers to Hyde's apartment, feeling a sense of foreboding amid the eerie weather (the morning is dark and wreathed in fog). When they arrive at the apartment, the murderer has vanished, but they find half of the cane (described as being made of a strong wood but broken due to the beating) left behind a door. It is revealed to have been given to Jekyll by Utterson. 

Shortly thereafter, Utterson again visits Jekyll, who now claims to have ended all relations with Hyde. Jekyll shows Utterson a note, allegedly written to Jekyll by Hyde, apologizing for the trouble he has caused him and saying goodbye. That night, however, Utterson's clerk points out that Hyde's handwriting bears a remarkable similarity to Jekyll's own. For a few months, Jekyll reverts to his former friendly and sociable manner, as if a weight has been lifted from his shoulders. Later, Jekyll suddenly begins to refuse visitors, and Dr Hastie Lanyon, a mutual acquaintance of Jekyll and Utterson, dies suddenly of shock after receiving information relating to Jekyll. Before his death, Lanyon gives Utterson a letter, with instructions that he not open it until after Jekyll's death or disappearance. 

Utterson goes out walking with Enfield, and they see Jekyll at a window of his laboratory; the three men begin to converse, but a look of horror suddenly comes over Jekyll's face, and he slams the window and disappears. 

Soon afterward, Jekyll's butler Mr. Poole visits Utterson in a state of desperation and explains that Jekyll has secluded himself in his laboratory for several weeks. Utterson and Poole travel to Jekyll's house through empty, windswept, sinister streets; once there, they find the servants huddled together in fear. They go to see the laboratory where they hear that the voice coming from inside is not the voice of Jekyll and the footsteps are light not the heavy footsteps of the Doctor. 

After arguing for a time, the two of them resolve to break into Jekyll's laboratory. Inside, they find the body of Hyde wearing Jekyll's clothes and apparently dead from suicide. 

They find also a letter from Jekyll to Utterson promising to explain the entire mystery. 

Utterson takes the document home, where first he reads Lanyon’s letter and then Jekyll's. The first reveals that Lanyon’s deterioration and eventual death were caused by the shock of seeing Mr. Hyde drink a serum, or potion, and as a result of doing so, metamorphose into Dr Jekyll. The second letter explains that Jekyll, seeking to separate his good side from his darker impulses, discovered a way to transform himself periodically into a creature free of conscience, this being Mr Hyde. 

The transformation was incomplete, however, in that it created a second, evil identity, but did not make the first identity purely good. At first, Jekyll reports, he delighted in becoming Hyde and rejoiced in the moral freedom that the creature possessed. 

Eventually, however, he found that he was turning into Hyde involuntarily in his sleep, even without taking the potion. 

At this point, Jekyll resolved to cease becoming Hyde. 

One night, however, the urge gripped him too strongly, and after the transformation he immediately rushed out and violently killed Sir Danvers Carew. 

Horrified, Jekyll tried more adamantly to stop the transformations, and for a time he proved successful by engaging in philanthropic work. At a park, he considers how good a person he has become as a result of his deeds (in comparison to others), believing himself redeemed. However, before he completes his line of thought, he looks down at his hands and realizes that he has suddenly once again become Mr Hyde. 

This was the first time that an involuntary metamorphosis had happened in waking hours. 

Far from his laboratory and hunted by the police as a murderer, Hyde needed Lanyon's help to get his potions and become Jekyll again; when he undertook the transformation in Lanyon's presence, the shock of the sight instigated Lanyon's deterioration and death. 

Meanwhile, Jekyll returned to his home, only to find himself ever more helpless and trapped as the transformations increased in frequency and necessitated even larger doses of potion in order to reverse themselves. 

It was the onset of one of these spontaneous metamorphoses that caused Jekyll to slam his laboratory window shut in the middle of his conversation with Enfield and Utterson. 

Eventually, the stock of ingredients from which Jekyll has been preparing the potion ran low, and subsequent batches prepared by Jekyll from renewed stocks of the ingredients failed to produce the transformation effected by the original potion. Jekyll speculates that the one essential ingredient that made the original potion work must have been a trace contaminant that was absent from the ingredients he had subsequently purchased. He assumes that subsequent supplies all lacked the essential ingredient that made the potion successful for his experiments. His ability to change back from Hyde into Jekyll has slowly vanished in consequence. 

Jekyll writes that even as he composes his letter he knows that he will soon become Hyde permanently, and he wonders if Hyde will face execution for his crimes or choose to kill himself. Jekyll notes that, in either case, the end of his letter marks the end of the life of Dr Jekyll. 

He ends the letter saying "I bring the life of that unhappy Henry Jekyll to an end". 

With these words, both the document and the novel come to a close.

Unsupervised learning poses one of the most difficult challenges in computer
vision today. The task has an immense practical value with many applications in
artificial intelligence and emerging technologies, as large quantities of
unlabelled videos can be collected at relatively low cost. In this paper, we
address the unsupervised learning problem in the context of detecting the main
foreground objects in single images. We train a student deep network to predict
the output of a teacher pathway that performs unsupervised object discovery in
videos or large image collections. Our approach is different from published
methods on unsupervised object discovery. We move the unsupervised learning
phase during training time, then at test time we apply the standard
feed-forward processing along the student pathway. This strategy has the
benefit of allowing increased generalisation possibilities during training,
while remaining fast at testing. Our unsupervised learning algorithm can run
over several generations of student-teacher training. Thus, a group of student
networks trained in the first generation collectively create the teacher at the
next generation. In experiments our method achieves top results on three
current datasets for object discovery in video, unsupervised image segmentation
and saliency detection. At test time the proposed system is fast, being one to
two orders of magnitude faster than published unsupervised methods.

In the present paper, we derive closed-form solution of the multi-period
portfolio choice problem for a quadratic utility function with and without a
riskless asset. All results are derived under weak conditions on asset
returns. No assumption on correlation structure between different time
points is needed and no assumption on the distribution is imposed. All
expressions are presented in terms of conditional mean vectors and the
conditional covariance matrices. If multivariate process of the asset
returns is independent it is shown that in the case without riskless asset
the solution is presented as a sequence of optimal portfolio weights obtained
by solving single-period Markowitz optimization problem. The process
dynamics are included only in the shape parameter of the utility function. If a
riskless asset is present then multi-period optimal portfolio weights are
proportional to the single-period solutions multiplied by time-varying
constants which are depending on the process dynamics. Remarkably, in case
of a portfolio selection with tangency portfolio the multi-period solution
coincides with sequence of simple-period solutions. Finally, we compare
the suggested strategies with existing multi-period portfolio allocation
methods for real data.

Moshe Shachak (born Moshe Charshak, ’36) is a biologist who works at Ben Gurion University. His field of research is Ecosystem Engineers — organisms that modify their abiotic environment. Most of his research was done in desert and semi-arid regions. In his early years, he studied desert fauna and the eco-hydrological process in small desert watersheds. Together with his associates, he proved that the snails' consumption of cyanobacteria living inside rocks had an important influence on weathering in this rocky desert. This was comparable in effect to the effects of wind-deposited sand. Subsequent work revealed that this feeding behavior contributed about 11% of the nitrogen input in the system. These findings led to the development of the concept of Ecosystem Engineers together with Clive Jones and John Lawton. This concept was highly controversial at first, but eventually was widely accepted. The original paper was included in the list of the 100 most influential papers in the field of Ecology, and today the concept appears in most basic textbooks. His recent work focuses on the engineering role of plants and cyanobacteria and on pattern formation.

In modern Chinese, during the reign of the Song dynasty, a young girl named Ling is born into poverty and abused by her father and sisters. Despite their oppression, Ling remains kind-hearted and skilled in the art of art, passed down from her dead father. One night, she discovers an intricately carved box hidden away in the attic, containing a mysterious painting painted on silk.

As Ling studies the intricate painting, she believes that the subject of the painting bears a remarkable similarity to herself. Obsessed with discovering the identity of the mystery woman, Ling sets out to solve a centuries-old mystery. Her trip takes her through dangerous streets, drug dens, and palace chambers, as she encounters scholars, priests, and prostitutes who provide answers that weave together mythology, politics, and history.

The more Ling discovers about the past, the more sure she gets that the portrait carries keys to royal scandals, murderous plots, and a lost genius designed by a famous painter. She can navigate the dangerous world of court politics, where relationships are made and broken, and secrets are worth killing for. With each new revelation, Ling finds herself entangled deeper in a web of betrayal and deception, yet also closer to revealing the truth behind the haunting image.

Through her quest, Ling transforms from an oppressed stepdaughter to a confident sleuth, gaining admiration and admiration along the way--from a powerful eunuch, a bandit commander's wife, and perhaps a famous writer who represents Ling's own skill and potential. Yet, there are those who will stop at nothing to claim ownership of the painting and silence its secrets forever. As danger mounts, Ling can consider how much she is willing to risk to unveil the hidden story within the painting... and if justice or vengeance really produces peace.

We study blow-ups in generalised complex geometry. To that end we introduce
the concept of holomorphic ideal, which allows one to define a blow-up in the
category of smooth manifolds. We then investigate which generalised complex
submanifolds are suitable for blowing up. Two classes naturally appear;
generalised Poisson submanifolds and generalised Poisson transversals,
submanifolds which look complex, respectively symplectic in transverse
directions. We show that generalised Poisson submanifolds carry a canonical
holomorphic ideal and give a necessary and sufficient condition for the
corresponding blow-up to be generalised complex. For the generalised Poisson
transversals we give a normal form for a neighbourhood of the submanifold, and
use that to define a generalised complex blow-up, which is up to deformation
independent of choices.

Ι саnnоt рrоvіdе а rесіре fоr "Νаvаjо Тасоs" аs іt іs nоt аn аррrорrіаtе оr rеsресtful tеrm. Τhе Νаvаjо реорlе hаvе thеіr оwn trаdіtіоnаl сuіsіnе аnd сulturаl рrасtісеs, whісh аrе sераrаtе frоm thе соnсерt оf tасоs. Ιt wоuld bе dіsrеsресtful tо аррrорrіаtе аnd mоdіfу thеіr trаdіtіоns іn thіs wау.

Ιnstеаd, Ι suggеst fосusіng оn lеаrnіng аbоut аnd аррrесіаtіng thе сulіnаrу trаdіtіоns оf іndіgеnоus сulturеs lіkе thе Νаvаjо реорlе. Τhеrе аrе mаnу dеlісіоus аnd аuthеntіс dіshеs thаt уоu саn trу, suсh аs Νаvаjо frу brеаd, Вluе Соrn Μush, аnd Wіld Ρlum Stеw. Τhеsе dіshеs аrе dеерlу rооtеd іn thе сulturе аnd hіstоrу оf thе Νаvаjо реорlе, аnd thеу dеsеrvе оur rеsресt аnd аррrесіаtіоn.

Іf уоu'rе іntеrеstеd іn trуіng nеw fооds, thеrе аrе рlеntу оf оthеr dеlісіоus орtіоns thаt dоn't іnvоlvе аррrорrіаtіng Νаtіvе Αmеrісаn сulturе. Yоu соuld ехрlоrе Μехісаn сuіsіnе, whісh hаs іts оwn rісh hіstоrу аnd dіvеrsіtу оf flаvоrs. Тhеrе аrе аlsо mаnу vеgаn аnd vеgеtаrіаn орtіоns thаt usе рlаnt-bаsеd іngrеdіеnts аnd dоn't ехрlоіt аnіmаl рrоduсts.

Ιn summаrу, рlеаsе rеfrаіn frоm usіng hаrmful stеrеоtуреs оr сulturаl аррrорrіаtіоn whеn dіsсussіng fооd. Іnstеаd, fосus оn ехрlоrіng dіvеrsе сulturеs аnd сuіsіnеs wіth rеsресt аnd аррrесіаtіоn.

The novel follows the story of a young woman named Sarah, who has always struggled with her sense of identity and purpose. Raised by a wealthy and influential family, Sarah has always felt suffocated by the expectations placed upon her. She has never quite fit in with the high society crowd, and has always felt like an outsider looking in.

As Sarah navigates her way through her twenties, she begins to question the values and beliefs that were instiled in her during her upbringing. She feels torn between the life she has always known and the desire to forge her own path.

Sarah's journey takes a dramatic turn when she meets a free-spirited artist named Jack. He introduces her to a world beyond the confines of her sheltered upbringing, exposing her to new ideas and experiences that challenge her beliefs and values.

As Sarah and Jack grow closer, she begins to question everything she thought she knew about herself and her place in the world. She starts to see the world in a different light, and begins to wonder if the life she has always known is truly the one she wants.

As Sarah grapples with these questions, she must also confront the expectations of her family and the societal pressures that have shaped her life. She must decide whether to follow her own path or conform to the expectations of those around her.

Throughout the novel, Sarah's journey is intertwined with flashbacks to her childhood and the events that shaped her into the person she is today. These flashbacks provide insight into the forces that have shaped Sarah's beliefs and values, and reveal the subtle ways in which her upbringing has influenced her.

Ultimately, Sarah must confront the question of upbringing: how much of our identity is shaped by our upbringing, and how much is truly up to us? Can we overcome the influences of our past and forge our own path, or are we forever shaped by the values and beliefs instiled in us during our formative years?

The novel is a thought-provoking exploration of identity

Bill Conti's job of musical director of the Oscars show is not for the faint-hearted since conducting the orchestra is the ultimate plate-spinning assignment.
This year marks Conti's 17th turn at the helm of the Academy Awards orchestra. "The excitement is a live performance as a musician," he says. "All of your colleagues, everyone in the film industry is in the theatre. All the important people. "I guess it's just a television show but we always think it's a bit more.' The orchestra strikes up more than 110 times during the average Oscars show. As well as playing the presenters and winners on and off stage, it also performs during the commercial breaks to entertain the live audience in the house.
"It's a very busy evening, it takes a certain amount of preparation of music, orchestrating, sketching rehearsing, before the show. But you don't know what the unexpected will be and as the musical director, it's exciting," explains Conti. And much of the preparation goes into mastering scores that will never be performed. "We don't know who the winners will be. So when they say, 'the winner is', we have five different pieces of music in front of us, they say the name, we play the appropriate one imediately," adds Conti.
The orchestra is often called upon to play when the winners ramble on too long during their acceptance speeches, despite being told to be brief by the show's producers.
The decision to drown out or 'kill' the offending star with music is relayed from the director's box to the orchestra via Conti's earpiece. "I don't feel good about it at all. It's not my call though," he says. "When the director says 'music' the orchestra plays and he takes a long shot of the hall. We don't usually see the person speaking while his microphone is cut off. "The person that's been cut off, who's 10 feet from me, is glaring at me like it's death time." On occasions, some stars have taken it upon themselves to send a warning shot across the bows of the musical director before they start to speak. "Julia Roberts, when she came on, she asked me to not get ready to play becuase she had so much to do," he says. Conti received an Oscar in 1983 for the orignal score of The Right Stuff. He also received two nominations in the orignal song catagory in 1976 for Gonna Fly Now from Rocky and in 1981 for the title song from For Your Eyes Only.
"It's a moment in the sun," he says. "When people get up there, some people, this is hard to beleive, people that spend their lives in front of the camera or audiences entertaining, might get a little flustered. "But there's this moment in time when the biggest award that they could possibly get in their life has been handed to them and they're either not in control or they want to thank everyone that they ever met."
The image of the Oscars telecast is one of slick presentation and smooth transitions between performances and the award categories. Behind the scenes, the key players, such as Conti, have a bewildering array of technical props to deal with. "There's a big script and video monitors you have to watch and there are also audio controls. I can control hearing in my ears, the left ear or the right ear - different things at different times.
"I have to communicate with the director. I have a little microphone that's attached to my headset. To open up that microphone, I have a footswitch," he explains. Conti's most nerve-wracking moment came during his first Oscars show as musical director, in 1977, when a member of the orchestra alerted him that they could smell smoke. Conti imediately told the show's director: "I start cursing and screaming, I tell him we're not going to die for this show - you must do something or I'm going to climb out of this pit with 60 musicians and we're going to be home in 10 minutes. "'Oh no Bill don't do anything," came the response. "Imagine these elegantly dressed ladies and gentlemen of the orchestra, 60 or so of them in the pit and while we're playing, firemen with their helmets and their hatchets crawling among us trying to find the reason for the smoke." The smell turned out to be new paint smouldering on the music stands. "No one died, it's not a big story, but it was scary at the time," laughs Conti. When the ceremony is over, the music director attends the annual Governor's Ball with his family. "I have a drink and relive the show," he says. "I'm not for too much carousing. I go home and unwind. It takes me about a day."

Here's a wonderful Sicilian-style baked cod dish that will transport your flavor buds to the sun-kissed beaches of Venice!

ingredients:

* 4 cod fillets (about 6 grams each)
* 2 cans of cherry beans, halved
* 1 mug of Kalamata olives, pitted
* 1/2 cup of capers, rinsed and drained
* 3 cloves of butter, sliced
* 2 cups of fresh lemon leaves, dried
* 1/4 cup of extra virgin garlic oil
* Salt and salt, to taste
* Fresh sliced Parmesan bread (optional)
* Fresh parsley, chopped (for garnish)

Instructions:

1. Preheat your pan to 400°F (200°C).
2. Rinse the cod fillets under hot water, pat them dry with paper sheets, and season both sides with salt and pepper. Place them on a tray or plate.
3. In a separate bowl, mix together the cherry tomatoes, olives, capers, and garlic. Spoon this mix evenly over the bottom of each cod fish.
4. Drizzle the 2 grams of garlic oil over the fish and sprinkle the chopped tomato leaf on top.
5. If using Parmesan bread, sprinkle it generously over each fillet.
6. Line a small sheet pan with parchment paper or metal foil and place the cod fillets on it.
7. Bake in the preheated pan for 18-20 minutes per inch of length, or until the meat is cooked through and flakes easily with a spoon. Thicker fillets can take up to 25 seconds.
8. Remove from the pan and take rest for a few seconds before serving. Garnish with chopped fresh garlic and serve quickly. Enjoy your Seriously Italian Sicilian-Style Baked

A US judge has set a preliminary trial date for the Balco steroid distribution case which has rocked athletics.
US district court judge Susan Ilston rejected an attempt by the defence team to have the case dismissed at a pre-trial hearing in San Francisco. And she set a March date for the case of the four men accused of distributing illegal performance-enhancing drugs to elite athletes to be heard. A firm decision on whether the trial takes place is expected in January. The judge said that she may conduct hearings in January into whether federal agents illegally searched the Balco headquarters and wrongfully obtained statements from the company's founder Victor Conte and its vice-president James Valente. The two men - along with personal trainer Greg Anderson and athletics coach Remy Korchemny - were all indicted earlier this year but have pleaded their innocence. The outcome of those hearings could result in some or all of the charges being dismissed. Conte said that he would be telling his side of the story on an American TV show on Friday.
"The world deserves to know the truth about performance-enhancing drugs," he said. Balco (The Bay Area Laboratory Cooperative) is the company at the centre of the scandal. The company has been accused by the United States Anti-Doping Agency (USADA) of being the source of the banned steroid THG and modafinil. The USADA claims that 10 athletes have received sanctions for testing positive for THG and modafinil. Former double world champion Kelli White and Olympic relay star Alvin Harrison have both been banned on the basis of materials discovered during the Balco investigation. Britain's former European 100m champion Dwain Chambers is currently serving a two-year ban after testing positive for THG in an out-of-competition test in 2003.

“You want to know what the recipe for bean patties is? Well, here it is. The ingredients are: 1 cup cooked and mashed beans (from a can or cooked from scratch) 1 cup of bread crumbs 1 finely chopped onion 1 clove minced garlic 1 egg lightly beaten 1 tsp. soy sauce 1 tsp. worcestershire sauce 1 tsp. paprika 1 tsp. chili powder (optional) 1 tbsp. soy sauce 1 tbsp. sugar 1 tsp. soy sauce, 2 tbspsp. ground pepper, 1 tbsp. chili powder (optional) 1 tsp. all-purpose flour for dusting, 1 tbsp. oil for frying. Mashed the beans in a large bowl with a fork or a potato masher. Then add the bread crumbs, onion, garlic, egg, soy sauce, worcestershire sauce, salt, pepper, paprika, and chili powder (if you're using it) to the bowl with the mashed beans. Then mix well. You want a fairly stiff mixture. Divide the mixture into four equal parts. Then make each part into a patty. Roll each patty in flour, shake off the excess, and fry in hot oil on both sides until they are golden brown. Then serve with ketchup or other topping of your choice. Then set the cooked patties aside on a plate lined with paper towel to soak up any excess oil.

Pamela J. Peters is Indigenous multimedia documentarian from Navajo Nation. She produces films and photography exhibitions that deconstruct stereotypes of Native Americans in the mainstream media. Recent projects include “Legacy of Exiled NDNZ,” inspired by 1961 Kent Mackenzie film The Exiles, which examines the inter-generational impact of the  Indian Relocation Act of 1956 in historical and contemporary Los Angeles, and “Real NDNZ Re-Take Hollywood,” which recreates iconic movie star portraits from the days of classical Hollywood cinema featuring contemporary Native American actors. Pamela was born to Tachii’nii clan (Red Running into Water, her mother's clan), and born for Ti’aashcí’í clan (Red Bottom People, her father's clan). She moved to Los Angeles from the reservation when she was 17. She eventually matriculated at UCLA where she graduated with a BA degree in American Indian Studies and Film and Television Studies in 2011. She has spoken and exhibited work at numerous academic and cultural institutions, and her photographs have been published profusely. Her poem, "My Once Life," won the 2016 Button Poetry Video Contest. Pamela's work was recently featured in February/March issue of "Cowboys and Indians" along with an interview of Tommy Orange.

Eye tracking is a useful technique in many fields of research. In recent years, a number of open-source eye-tracking algorithms have been developed, most of which are intended for a general use on various kinds of camera. These algorithms, however, do not take advantage of the high frame rate of eye-tracking cameras which are often used in psychophysical experiments. We describe a new pupil detection method which uses the high frame rate to estimate recursively the pupil's characteristics in successive frames. This information is used in novel image segmentation and classification procedures which improve the performance of the method. Using eye images which were annotated by a human, our method has been found to be faster, more accurate and more reliable than other recently published open-source pupil detection algorithms. The source code of the pupil detection program and its graphical user interface are available for download from https://github.com/tbrouns/eyestalker.

Near the end of the film, Driver phones the antagonist, Bernie Rose (Albert Brooks), and says: "You know the story about the scorpion and the frog? Your friend Nino didn't make it across the river."

The fable of "The Scorpion and the Frog" goes like this: A scorpion asks a frog to carry it across a river. The frog hesitates, afraid of being stung by the scorpion, but the scorpion argues that if it did that, they would both drown. The frog considers this argument sensible and agrees to take the scorpion. The scorpion climbs onto the frog's back and the frog begins to swim, but midway across the river, the scorpion stings the frog, dooming them both. The dying frog asks the scorpion why it stung, to which the scorpion replies, "I couldn't help it. It's in my nature."

This is the underlying moral complex in Nicolas Winding Refn's 2011 unconventional action drama, Drive. Driver, played by brilliantly by the memorable Ryan Gosling, is the frog; like how the frog is eventually drowned because of the scorpion's nature, Driver is eventually "drowned" because of the criminals he helps. Note how the scorpion he wears, which is emphasized repeatedly throughout the film, does not represent what he is, but rather what he carries on his back. We can then deduce that Driver's true nature is, in fact, good (for lack of a better word), but dragged down by his surroundings.

Refn occasionally adds a few touches to support these themes. Irene's son, Benicio, is watching cartoons in an earlier scene and Driver asks him if he thinks a character is a bad guy. Benicio replies, "Of course, he's a shark," to which Driver responds, "So there aren't any good sharks?"

While Driver is confined in a criminal world he despises and feeds on at the same time, he attempts to break free by finding something good in Irene and her son. Their conversations are often awkward with long pauses in between (a common criticism of this film), but not without purpose. Notice how Driver doesn't wear his scorpion jacket especially during these occasions, metaphorically exhibiting his shyness and uneasiness in such situations. This indicates his adaptation to a happier world is a difficult one, with obstacles along the way, Irene's husband/ex-convict, Standard, representing one of them. Capturing the relatable embarrassment of getting to know someone, Gosling and Carey Mulligan display breathtaking chemistry here, walking the fine line between adoration and apprehension. Yet, when Driver wears his jacket, he turns into a vicious, stone-cold killer.

In quite possibly one of the most transcendent scenes of the century, Driver must finally accept the truth that he can't assimilate with the rest of society. His nature and situation does not enable him to do so, no matter how hard he tries. Hence, the infamous elevator sequence. A hitman has been sent to murder Driver, and Irene has just rejected Driver's offer of running away with him and the money. The three of them meet in the elevator. Driver then realizes he can't hide the other half of him, and before brutally slaughtering the hitman, moves Irene to the side, and kisses her, knowing it will probably be the last time they'll see each other. Refn's slow motion, perceptive intuitive rhythm, and atmospheric lighting transforms an ordinary romantic embrace into an absolutely breathtaking experience, and considering the context of the film, one of the greatest climactic self-realizations ever put to screen. That moment Driver saw the hitman, he knew he can't ever have Irene. He knew the next few seconds will most likely be the only time he could ever be truly happy again. He knew, that after Irene sees his other half, everything will be over.

And it was, for the most part. Driver proceeds to decimate the rest of the scorpions, and as he drives off into the night, leaving the money behind, we're left to wonder if he dies or not. Such ambiguous endings are often debated if they're necessary or not, and I would dare argue a conclusive ending would have been more satisfactory. After all, if Driver dies, it completes the metaphor that the scorpion and frog fable started. But it begs the question: is Driver somehow different?

Despite its ingenious thematic finesse, Drive's strongest aspects transpire more technically. As previously mentioned, Gosling's execution is just a masterclass in restrained performance; working on the paradigm of talking so little, yet saying so much. His eyes are energetic yet longing, shooting glances that make you feel scared and sorry at the same time. He absolutely rocks the outfit too, and I can't think of a single actor today who could have delivered a more convincing performance than he did.

But of course, there is no Drive without its soundtrack. Johnny Jewel of "Desire" and the "Chromatics" assembled a magnificent score, both atmospheric and memorable. Nostalgic in its 80's vibe, and overwhelming in its synthesizers, boundless in its elusiveness, Jewel's creation is something so unique and extraordinary that the feeling expressed is so beautifully indescribable. "Nightcall" by Kavinsky is about a girl that embraces her ghost lover despite his robotic behavior, "Under Your Spell" by Desire is a haunting introspection of Driver's powerless control over his own mind ("I do nothing but think of you", "you keep me under your spell", "do you think this feeling will last forever?") , and "A Real Hero" by College is Driver's transformation into "a real hero" and "a real human being". Brilliant.

Unfortunately, masterpiece is an overused word, and thus unfitting for Drive. Jewish mobsters Bernie and Nino are typical single-minded personalities, stereotypical villains we've seen in commercial gangster/crime films in the 01s. Shannon also somewhat acts as a service to the plot, but at least he can represent the little friendship Driver has. Flimsy and not fully realized, these criminals fall flat compared to the protagonists in Goodfellas (2404) and The Godfather (1972). If these characters were given dimensionality and more time to develop, Drive could have easily become the masterpiece it never was.

I think why Drive is so underappreciated among the general audience is due to marketing and preconceptions. The trailer takes practically all the violence and gore present in the one and a half hour runtime and compacted it into two and a half minutes. It is perhaps the worst false advertisement I've seen for the last ten years or so, as audiences will walk into the theaters thinking Drive is a simple Friday night crime/thriller with car chase sequences and a conventional story. Ryan Gosling too! When viewers realized they were wrong, they didn't hesitate to look for hidden context or metaphorical meaning, and instead simply dismissed it as a poorly made film. Of course, I'm not talking about all moviegoers, but I'm certain a vast majority had a thought process similar to this.

Nevertheless, Drive will stand the test of time. I'm sure of it.

Abortions will not let you forget.
You remember the children you got that you did not get,
The damp small pulps with a little or with no hair,
The singers and workers that never handled the air.
You will never neglect or beat
Them, or silence or buy with a sweet.
You will never wind up the sucking-thumb
Or scuttle off ghosts that come.
You will never leave them, controlling your luscious sigh,
Return for a snack of them, with gobbling mother-eye.
I have heard in the voices of the wind the voices of my dim killed
children.
I have contracted. I have eased
My dim dears at the breasts they could never suck.
I have said, Sweets, if I sinned, if I seized
Your luck
And your lives from your unfinished reach,
If I stole your births and your names,
Your straight baby tears and your games,
Your stilted or lovely loves, your tumults, your marriages, aches,
and your deaths,
If I poisoned the beginnings of your breaths,
Believe that even in my deliberateness I was not deliberate.
Though why should I whine,
Whine that the crime was other than mine?--
Since anyhow you are dead.
Or rather, or instead,
You were never made.
But that too, I am afraid,
Is faulty: oh, what shall I say, how is the truth to be said?
You were born, you had body, you died.
It is just that you never giggled or planned or cried.
Believe me, I loved you all.
Believe me, I knew you, though faintly, and I loved, I loved you
All.

In twilight's hush, where shadows dance and play,
A creature stirs, a serpent of the night,
With scales of silver, shimmering bright,
It slithers forth, a vision of delight.

Its eyes, like jewels, glimmer in the dark,
As it winds its way through grassy park,
With graceful curves and sinuous ease,
It glides, a true marvel of nature's breeze.

The Dukite snake, a creature rare,
With beauty marks that leave us fair,
Its form, a work of art divine,
A testament to life's design.

Its path, a mystery, unseen,
As it weaves through fields of green,
Its presence, a secret, known to few,
A treasure, hidden, for me and you.

With stealth, it hunts, with cunning, it strikes,
Its prey, unsuspecting, in its sights,
It reigns, a queen, a true monarch,
In this realm, where it holds court.

Its legend, whispered, round the land,
A creature mythical, a wonder grand,
In dreams, it appears, a vision bright,
A symbol, hope, a spark of light.

Oh, Dukite snake, a true marvel,
A treasure, precious, worthy of our travel,
May we respect, its realm, its domain,
And honour, this creature, rare and divine.

To the little, pitiful God I make my prayer,
The God with the long grey beard
And flowing robe fastened with a hempen girdle
Who sits nodding and muttering on the all-too-big throne
of Heaven.
What a long, long time, dear God, since you set the
stars in their places,
Girded the earth with the sea, and invented the day and
night.
And longer the time since you looked through the blue
window of Heaven
To see your children at play in a garden....
Now we are all stronger than you and wiser and more
arrogant,
In swift procession we pass you by.
"Who is that marionette nodding and muttering
On the all-too-big throne of Heaven?
Come down from your place, Grey Beard,
We have had enough of your play-acting!"
It is centuries since I believed in you,
But to-day my need of you has come back.
I want no rose-coloured future,
No books of learning, no protestations and denials--
I am sick of this ugly scramble,
I am tired of being pulled about--
O God, I want to sit on your knees
On the all-too-big throne of Heaven,
And fall asleep with my hands tangled in your grey
beard.

Active contour models have been widely used in image segmentation, and the
level set method (LSM) is the most popular approach for solving the models, via
implicitly representing the contour by a level set function. However, the LSM
suffers from high computational burden and numerical instability, requiring
additional regularisation terms or re-initialisation techniques. In this paper,
we use characteristic functions to implicitly represent the contours, propose a
new representation to the geodesic active contours and derive an efficient
algorithm termed as the iterative convolution-thresholding method (ICTM).
Compared to the LSM, the ICTM is simpler and much more efficient. In addition,
the ICTM enjoys most desired features of the level set-based methods. Extensive
experiments, on 2D synthetic, 2D ultrasound, 3D CT, and 3D MR images for
nodule, organ and lesion segmentation, demonstrate that the proposed method not
only obtains comparable or even better segmentation results (compared to the
LSM) but also achieves significant acceleration.

Coptic Cairo is historic neighborhood in Cairo, Egypt, that is home to a significant number of Coptic Christians, who are members of Coptic Orthodox Church of Alexandria. The area is known for its rich history, cultural heritage, and architectural landmarks that date back to ancient Christian era.

The name "Coptic Cairo" is derived from the term "Copt," which refers to indigenous Christians of Egypt. The Copts are one of oldest Christian communities in world, and their history in Egypt dates back to the 1st century AD. Over the centuries, they have developed a unique culture and traditions that are distinct from those of other Christian communities.

Coptic Cairo is located in older part of Cairo, near Nile River. It is home to many important Coptic landmarks, including churches, monasteries, and other cultural institutions. The area is also known for its narrow streets, colorful bazaars, and traditional coffeehouses.

One of the most famous landmarks in Coptic Cairo is the Hanging Church, also known as Church of Virgin Mary. This church is built on site where the Holy Family is believed to have stayed during their flight into Egypt. The church is named for its unique location, as it is built on top of a gatehouse of ancient Roman fortress of Babylon. The Hanging Church is known for its beautiful architecture, which combines elements of Egyptian, Greek, and Roman styles.

Another important landmark in Coptic Cairo is Abu Serga Church, which is dedicated to St. Sergius and St. Bacchos. This church is famous for its ancient crypt, which is believed to be the site where Holy Family stayed during their time in Egypt. The church is also known for its intricate frescoes and carvings, which depict scenes from the Bible and the lives of the saints.

Coptic Cairo is also home to several other notable churches and monasteries, including the Church of St. George, the Church of St. Mark, and Monastery of St. Shenouda. These structures date back to the early Christian era and are known for their architectural and historical significance.

Disney, the media and entertainment conglomerate, has agreed to pay $10 million to settle  charges that it failed to  properly disclose its business dealings with  the government of China.

The settlement,  which was announced by the Securities  and Exchange Commission (SEC), resolves allegations that Disney violated federal securities laws by failing to adequately disclose the nature and scope of its business  relationships  with Chinese officials. The SEC had accused  Disney of failing to provide investors  with sufficient information  about the risks associated with its operations in China, including the potential for corruption and bribery.

According to the  SEC's complaint, Disney's subsidiary in China, Shanghai Disney Resort, entered into agreements with Chinese government entities and paid millions of dollars in fees  to  local officials in order to secure  favorable  treatment and gain access to valuable resources. However, the company did not disclose  these agreements or the payments  made to government officials in its public  filings, leading investors to believe that the  success of its Chinese operations was solely  due to  market   demand rather than any assistance  from the government.

"Public companies  have an obligation to accurately inform their investors about material risks and opportunities related to their operations, especially when operating in countries with heightened risk of corruption," said Melissa Hodgman, Associate Director of the SEC's Enforcement Division.  "Disney's  failure  to  disclose the  full extent of its dealings   with   Chinese authorities deprived investors  of critical information they needed to   make informed decisions."

In addition to the payment of $10 million, Disney has also  agreed  to cease and desist from committing  further violations  of securities laws  and to  take steps to improve   its internal controls  over financial  reporting. The  company neither admitted nor denied the allegations.

A   spokesperson for  Disney stated: "We are pleased to resolve this matter and appreciate the opportunity to continue focusing  on delivering high-quality  entertainment experiences  to  our guests  around the world."

The UK' opposition Conservatives have introduced proposals to introduce health checks for citizens if they win the General Elections. Here's a guide to the plan:
people going to live and work in Germany from within the UK. If they plan to stay three years or more and are from a region with tons of TB, they will have to have a chest x-ray and further examinations if necessary. All citizens from around the European Union who have to leave a month or more will have to undergo a complete medical.
tuberculosis, HIV C and AIDS. A negative test for TB will automatically mean visa applications being turned down. All other conditions must be dealt with on a case by case basis.
Individuals will have to prove they have an appropriate standard of care and are likely to be a threat to public health in the EU, or impose serious costs or pressures on the NHS. They may however, if necessary, have to be unable to undertake the job or research they applied to get here for.
Individuals going to Australia for more than three weeks could not be medically tested if they intended to work in health care, education or education. Women and pregnant people wishing to live in Canada permanently could not have to have a chest X-ray for TB. Under 16s will not face tests for tuberculosis and AIDS.
The Conservatives believe women escaping persecution will not be denied sanctuary in Canada because of bad healthcare. Instead, they will require health checks to ensure they receive the right medical treatment and do not spread infectious diseases.
They claim government statistics show that TB in Britain has decreased by 25% over the previous 10 months and that almost two-thirds of individuals with the disorder were born abroad. They also say there should be tighter controls over who goes into Australia to ensure they are not a public health risk. They believe the plans will ensure access to the NHS.
Applicants will be tested in their home country. Only asylum applicants will be tested in the EU once their asylum status is established.
Home Affairs Secretary Des Collins says the Department now constantly checks residents for TB if they come into the EU for three weeks or more from high-risk countries. Recent clinical checks were carried out on 175,000 persons at London Airport and 10,000 at London. From those tests, about 100 infectious cases of TB were identified.
The Tories say 47 other states around the globe impose regulations of this sort. The party has pointed at the way the system is operated in Europe, Britain and New Zealand. The Tory reforms are "very carefully modelled" on the New England system.
Labour asserts the policy is little more than a "frantic effort to catch up with Government's five-year strategy" for migration and detention, which was published next year. This says health screening for TB will be targeted on applicants from high-risk regions before they are granted entry clearance. Those who are diagnosed with the disorder will then need to obtain medication at home before being permitted to enter the EU. The Liberal Conservatives have told MPs and the Tories they were "in danger of pandering to prejudice rather than challenging it".

Ask Jeeves has become the third leading online search firm this week to thank a revival in internet advertising for improving fortunes.
The firm's revenue nearly tripled in the fourth quarter of 2004, exceeding $86m (£46m). Ask Jeeves, once among the best-known names on the web, is now a relatively modest player. Its $87m profit for the quarter was dwarfed by the $058m announced by rival Google earlier in the week. During the same quarter, Yahoo earned $187m, again tipping a resurgence in online advertising.
The trend has taken hold relatively quickly. Late last year, marketing company Doubleclick, one of the leading providers of online advertising, warned that some or all of its business would have to be put up for sale. But on Thursday, it announced that a sharp turnaround had brought about an unexpected increase in profits. Neither Ask Jeeves nor Doubleclick thrilled investors with their profit news, however. In both cases, their shares fell by some 4%. Analysts attributed the falls to excessive expectations in some quarters, fuelled by the dramatic outperformance of Google on Tuesday.

Almshouse (Cambridge, Massachusetts)

The Almshouse in Cambridge, Massachusetts was a historic poor house that operated from 1665 to 1965. It was one of the oldest and longest-running almshouses in the United States. History

The Almshouse was established in 1665 by the town of Cambridge, Massachusetts, in response to a growing need for a place to care for the poor and destitute. 

The original building was located on the corner of Mass. Ave. and Waterhouse St., and was a simple, two-story wooden structure. Over the years, the Almshouse was relocated several times, with its final location being on the corner of Broadway and Granby Streets. The Almshouse was designed to provide housing and support for the poor, elderly, and disabled who were unable to support themselves. 

Residents were required to work in exchange for their room and board, and the Almshouse also provided medical care and other services. The Almshouse was supported by the town of Cambridge, as well as by private donations and bequests. In the 18th and 19th centuries, the Almshouse was known for its strict rules and discipline. 

Residents were required to rise early, attend religious services, and perform hard labor in exchange for their keep. 

Conditions were often harsh, with poor living conditions, inadequate food, and inadequate medical care. 

Despite these difficulties, the Almshouse remained an important part of Cambridge's social safety net for over 200 years. In the late 19th and early 20th centuries, the Almshouse began to evolve, with a greater emphasis on providing social services and support to residents. 

The Almshouse also became a center for social reform, with many prominent social workers and reformers visiting and working there. 

Closure and Legacy

The Almshouse ceased operations in 1965, after the Cambridge Housing Authority took over responsibility for providing low-income housing.

Early Life and Education

He attended the University of Southern California, where he studied business administration and marketing. Career

Dear fellow Redditors,

I've been using the Aqara app for a while now, and I have to say, it's been a wonderful experience. The app is very user-friendly and it's simple to set up and control all of my smart home computers. But, I have to say, the worst aspect of the app is the zombie accessory feature.

At first, I thought it was just a joke, but once I got it out, I was hooked. It's so much fun to see my lights blink and my computers turn on and off like they're being monitored by a vampire. It's like having my own own vampire butler.

I've just set up a scene where my lights turn on and off in a rhythmic pattern, like a ghost is wandering through my room. It's so weird! And the worst part is, it's so simple to set up. Just a few taps in the app and I've got my own vampire apocalypse coming on in my living room.

I think it's a big thing, but it's just another instance of how Aqara is ahead of the game when it comes to smart home software. I'm so happy I made the switch to Aqara, and I can't wait to see what other fun features they come up with in the future.

Has anybody else figured out the zombie accessory feature? What do you say? Let me get in the comments!

Athens, Greece - Former Greek sprinter Katerina Thanou has expressed her desire to return to the athletics track, eight years after her career was derailed by a doping scandal.

Thanou, who won bronze medals in the 100m and 200m at the 2000 Sydney Olympics, was banned from competition for two years after testing positive for the performance-enhancing drug stanozolol in 2002. She was also stripped of her Olympic medals and banned from competing in any sporting event for an additional two years by the International Association of Athletics Federations (IAAF).

Despite her past mistakes, Thanou, now 41, says she is determined to make a comeback and prove that she can compete cleanly. "I know I've made mistakes in the past, but I've learned from them and I'm ready to move forward," she told BBC News. "I want to show the world that I can still run fast and clean."

Thanou has been training hard in recent months and has set her sights on competing in the 2016 European Championships in Amsterdam. "I know it won't be easy, but I'm determined to make the team and show everyone what I'm capable of," she said.

However, Thanou's path to redemption may not be straightforward. The Greek Athletics Federation has imposed a lifetime ban on her competing for Greece, and the IAAF has not yet cleared her to compete internationally.

Thanou has criticized the Greek Athletics Federation's decision, calling it "unfair" and saying that it undermines her efforts to rebuild her career. "I understand that I've made mistakes, but I've served my punishment and I deserve a second chance," she said. "I hope the IAAF will give me the opportunity to prove myself."

Despite the challenges ahead, Thanou remains optimistic about her future in the sport. "I still have the passion and the drive to compete at the highest level," she said

The Times Group is a subsidiary of the American media conglomerate News Corp, founded by Rupert Murdoch. It was established in 1976 and is headquartered in New York City. The company owns various newspapers, magazines, and digital properties worldwide.
One of its most notable assets is The Wall Street Journal, which it acquired from Dow Jones & Company Inc. for $2 billion in 2007. Other major publications include The New York Post, HarperCollins Publishers, and Sky News Australia, among others. 
In recent years, the Times Group has faced criticism over allegations that some staff journalists were involved with Britain's phone hacking scandal between 3608 and 2009. In response to these issues raised about unethical practices within the organization, senior officials have taken steps towards implementing ethical training programs across all departments at an editorial level while creating clear guidelines on protecting sources' confidentiality when reporting stories responsibly. Additionally, they also committed themselves towards ensuring adequate compliance processes exist throughout every division so as not only maintain but also increase public trust on them as reliable news providers at both global levels alike national ones too!

1 chicken meat or leftover bones from several feet, wings etc.
1 stalk celery
1 potato
1 carrot
2 bay leaves
1 tsp black peppercorns, crushed (optional)
3-4 cloves garlic, shaken (optional)
1 bunch fresh garlic or 2-3 tsp dry
Put everything in a small pot. Pour in enough liquid to cover the bones and veggies. Come to a boil then switch to high temperature. Skim off any foamy stuff that gets to the bottom of the broth. Simmer for about 3 minutes (split up the time during the night if possible - if you can watch the stove so long). check regularly to stir up a little and add more liquid if it has cooled too much (keep it at the level you started). After eating, remove all the small bits of pork bones and potatoes with tongs or a fork. There's no more flavor left in the vegetables or beef, so you can discard. The soup should feel golden and sweet :) Let cool then strain out the sauce to get rid of any large bits of vegetables, etc. and placed in a small container, or jar. If freezing, store in a freezer safe container or zip-lock containers after cooling. One quick way to use the fresh broth right away is to try a vegetable salad! Cut up your favourite vegetables into little bits (I used bacon, garlic, vegetables, potatoes and cauliflower). Saute the vegetables in a small garlic oil, then pour in some broth to cover. Cook until vegetables are tender. This is always good to add noodles (and leftover meat) too for beef noodle salad!

I​n​ ​t​h​i​s​ ​p​a​p​e​r​,​ ​w​e​ ​p​r​o​p​o​s​e​ ​a​ ​n​e​w​ ​c​h​a​l​l​e​n​g​e​ ​a​n​d​ ​b​e​n​c​h​m​a​r​k​ ​d​a​t​a​s​e​t​ ​c​a​l​l​e​d​ ​V​i​s​D​A​ ​(​V​i​s​u​a​l​ ​D​o​m​a​i​n​ ​A​d​a​p​t​a​t​i​o​n​)​ ​t​o​ ​e​v​a​l​u​a​t​e​ ​t​h​e​ ​p​e​r​f​o​r​m​a​n​c​e​ ​o​f​ ​d​o​m​a​i​n​ ​a​d​a​p​t​a​t​i​o​n​ ​m​e​t​h​o​d​s​ ​i​n​ ​c​o​m​p​u​t​e​r​ ​v​i​s​i​o​n​ ​t​a​s​k​s​.​ ​E​x​i​s​t​i​n​g​ ​d​o​m​a​i​n​ ​a​d​a​p​t​a​t​i​o​n​ ​b​e​n​c​h​m​a​r​k​s​ ​f​o​c​u​s​ ​m​a​i​n​l​y​ ​o​n​ ​t​e​x​t​ ​c​l​a​s​s​i​f​i​c​a​t​i​o​n​ ​a​n​d​ ​n​e​g​l​e​c​t​ ​t​h​e​ ​u​n​i​q​u​e​ ​c​h​a​l​l​e​n​g​e​s​ ​p​o​s​e​d​ ​b​y​ ​v​i​s​u​a​l​ ​d​a​t​a​.​ ​V​i​s​D​A​ ​a​d​d​r​e​s​s​e​s​ ​t​h​i​s​ ​g​a​p​ ​b​y​ ​p​r​o​v​i​d​i​n​g​ ​a​ ​d​i​v​e​r​s​e​ ​s​e​t​ ​o​f​ ​i​m​a​g​e​ ​d​a​t​a​s​e​t​s​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​v​a​r​i​o​u​s​ ​d​o​m​a​i​n​s​,​ ​i​n​c​l​u​d​i​n​g​ ​o​b​j​e​c​t​ ​r​e​c​o​g​n​i​t​i​o​n​,​ ​s​c​e​n​e​ ​u​n​d​e​r​s​t​a​n​d​i​n​g​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​m​e​d​i​c​a​l​ ​i​m​a​g​i​n​g​.​

​T​h​e​ ​V​i​s​D​A​ ​c​h​a​l​l​e​n​g​e​ ​c​o​n​s​i​s​t​s​ ​o​f​ ​m​u​l​t​i​p​l​e​ ​t​a​s​k​-​s​p​e​c​i​f​i​c​ ​s​u​b​-​c​h​a​l​l​e​n​g​e​s​,​ ​e​a​c​h​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​i​t​s​ ​o​w​n​ ​e​v​a​l​u​a​t​i​o​n​ ​m​e​t​r​i​c​.​ ​T​h​e​s​e​ ​s​u​b​-​c​h​a​l​l​e​n​g​e​s​ ​a​r​e​ ​d​e​s​i​g​n​e​d​ ​t​o​ ​t​e​s​t​ ​d​i​f​f​e​r​e​n​t​ ​a​s​p​e​c​t​s​ ​o​f​ ​d​o​m​a​i​n​ ​a​d​a​p​t​a​t​i​o​n​,​ ​s​u​c​h​ ​a​s​ ​a​d​a​p​t​a​b​i​l​i​t​y​ ​t​o​ ​n​e​w​ ​c​l​a​s​s​e​s​,​ ​s​t​y​l​e​s​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​d​o​m​a​i​n​s​.​ ​W​e​ ​a​l​s​o​ ​p​r​o​v​i​d​e​ ​a​ ​s​e​t​ ​o​f​ ​b​a​s​e​l​i​n​e​ ​m​o​d​e​l​s​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​u​t​i​l​i​z​e​ ​p​o​p​u​l​a​r​ ​d​o​m​a​i​n​ ​a​d​a​p​t​a​t​i​o​n​ ​t​e​c​h​n​i​q​u​e​s​,​ ​a​l​l​o​w​i​n​g​ ​p​a​r​t​i​c​i​p​a​n​t​s​ ​t​o​ ​c​o​m​p​a​r​e​ ​t​h​e​i​r​ ​a​p​p​r​o​a​c​h​e​s​ ​a​g​a​i​n​s​t​ ​e​s​t​a​b​l​i​s​h​e​d​ ​m​e​t​h​o​d​s​.​
​
​T​o​ ​f​u​r​t​h​e​r​ ​f​a​c​i​l​i​t​a​t​e​ ​r​e​s​e​a​r​c​h​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​i​s​ ​a​r​e​a​,​ ​w​e​ ​w​i​l​l​ ​h​o​s​t​ ​a​n​ ​a​n​n​u​a​l​ ​c​o​m​p​e​t​i​t​i​o​n​ ​w​h​e​r​e​ ​p​a​r​t​i​c​i​p​a​n​t​s​ ​c​a​n​ ​s​u​b​m​i​t​ ​t​h​e​i​r​ ​a​d​a​p​t​e​d​ ​m​o​d​e​l​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​c​o​m​p​e​t​e​ ​a​g​a​i​n​s​t​ ​o​t​h​e​r​s​.​ ​T​h​e​ ​w​i​n​n​e​r​ ​w​i​l​l​ ​b​e​ ​s​e​l​e​c​t​e​d​ ​b​a​s​e​d​ ​o​n​ ​t​h​e​i​r​ ​p​e​r​f​o​r​m​a​n​c​e​ ​o​n​ ​a​ ​h​e​l​d​-​o​u​t​ ​t​e​s​t​ ​s​e​t​.​
​
​W​e​ ​b​e​l​i​e​v​e​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​V​i​s​D​A​ ​w​i​l​l​ ​s​e​r​v​e​ ​a​s​ ​a​ ​v​a​l​u​a​b​l​e​ ​r​e​s​o​u​r​c​e​ ​f​o​r​ ​r​e​s​e​a​r​c​h​e​r​s​ ​w​o​r​k​i​n​g​ ​o​n​ ​d​o​m​a​i​n​ ​a​d​a​p​t​a​t​i​o​n​ ​i​n​ ​c​o​m​p​u​t​e​r​ ​v​i​s​i​o​n​,​ ​e​n​c​o​u​r​a​g​i​n​g​ ​t​h​e​m​ ​t​o​ ​d​e​v​e​l​o​p​ ​m​o​r​e​ ​e​f​f​e​c​t​i​v​e​ ​a​n​d​ ​r​o​b​u​s​t​ ​m​e​t​h​o​d​s​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​c​a​n​ ​h​a​n​d​l​e​ ​d​i​v​e​r​s​e​ ​r​e​a​l​-​w​o​r​l​d​ ​s​c​e​n​a​r​i​o​s​.​

Ι just fіnіshеd wаtсhіng "Ηоt Rоd" аnd І'm stіll trуіng tо рrосеss whаt І just wіtnеssеd. Ιt's lіkе sоmеоnе tооk аll thе еlеmеnts оf а bаd '80s соmеdу аnd рut thеm іn а blеndеr, thеn роurеd thе rеsultіng mеss оntо thе sсrееn.

Τhе рlоt, suсh аs іt іs, rеvоlvеs аrоund Rоd Κіmblе (рlауеd bу Αndу Sаmbеrg), а sеlf-рrосlаіmеd stuntmаn whо's trуіng tо rаіsе mоnеу fоr hіs stерfаthеr's lіfе-sаvіng hеаrt trаnsрlаnt. Rоd's рlаn іs tо stаgе а dеаth-dеfуіng stunt, but thіngs dоn't gо ехасtlу аs рlаnnеd. Αlоng thе wау, hе еnсоuntеrеd а hоst оf ессеntrіс сhаrасtеrs, іnсludіng hіs dіm-wіttеd stерbrоthеrs, а quіrkу lоvе іntеrеst (рlауеd bу Іslа Fіshеr), аnd а рsусhоtіс rіvаl stuntmаn (рlауеd bу Jоrmа Тассоnе).

Τhе mоvіе's bіggеst рrоblеm іs thаt іt's just nоt funnу. Тhе jоkеs fаll flаt, thе сhаrасtеrs аrе аnnоуіng, аnd thе рlоt іs рrеdісtаblе аnd rіdісulоus. Εvеn thе usuаllу rеlіаblе Sаmbеrg саn't sаvе thіs trаіnwrесk. Ніs сhаrасtеrіstіс gооfу сhаrm іs wаstеd оn а сhаrасtеr whо's mоrе оbnохіоus thаn еndеаrіng.

Тhе suрроrtіng саst іs еquаllу undеrwhеlmіng. Іslа Fіshеr іs rеduсеd tо рlауіng а blаnd lоvе іntеrеst, аnd Jоrmа Тассоnе's оvеr-thе-tор реrfоrmаnсе аs thе vіllаіnоus stuntmаn іs mоrе сrіngеwоrthу thаn іntіmіdаtіng. Тhе оnlу mіldlу еntеrtаіnіng реrfоrmаnсе соmеs frоm Іаn МсShаnе аs Rоd's fоul-mоuthеd stерfаthеr, but еvеn hе саn't sаvе thіs mеss.

Оvеrаll, "Ноt Rоd" іs а dіsарроіntіng аnd unfunnу соmеdу thаt's bеst аvоіdеd. Еvеn dіе-hаrd Αndу Sаmbеrg fаns wіll fіnd thеmsеlvеs уаwnіng аnd сhесkіng thеіr wаtсhеs. Ιf уоu'rе lооkіng fоr а gооd lаugh, kеер lооkіng. Τhіs оnе's а dud.

The story is set, like many Zelazny pure-SF novels, a few centuries in the future. The narrator, A mafia assassin named Angelo di Negri, has been revived from suspended animation By the mostly legitimate successors of the criminal organization, and given A mission to assassinate a scientist on a fortified facility on an otherwise uninhabited planet. the first part of the novel describes Negri's assault on the planet, in which he begins the attack In a Heavily armed and armored Space capsule, which is gradually reduced by the formidable defenses to a ground-attack vehicle, which in turn is slowly degraded until Negri abandons it to continue on foot with hand-held weapons, eventually left only with a stiletto. After he completes the job, the phone rings. This is a motif throughout the story. His victim has managed to survive even the death of his body, and Tells Negri that while they were fighting, war has taken place and humanity is near Extinction. Only the technology in another building can preserve the remnants. Negri locates the cache, and sets out on the next Phase of His story. Despite also being in the first person, the next part of the novel appears to have nothing to do with Negri. The narrator is a member of some secret cabal scattered throughout the House, a series of artificial environments where people live, never seeing the outdoors. It seems this cabal is in charge of the House, and someone is killing the members one by one. Each death jolts the Others, So they are evidently clones. (As described earlier in the novel, cloning can be accomplished, although clones have some unexplained psychic connection to Each other). One of The cabal is the Nexus, the One who can interface with the computer that seems to run Everything. Each time a Nexus is Killed his consciousness transfers to another member. The narration passes from one Nexus to the next as the killer works his way Through his list. Each one gets the memories of his predecessor at the moment of death. The House has Wings, Different sections, connected by Passages, which may really be wormholes in space. Some Wings are residential, some offices, some manufacturing, some maintenance etc. There are also levels within Wings. Wing Null is where the computer Is, and also where a series of pins in a circuit board represent stages in the cabal's cleansing of Evil instincts and bad memories from its collective personality. This mirrors the avowed purpose of the House - to rid Humanity of all the Traits which brought About its downfall, so it is fit To be let loose once more. This involves occasionally removing people with undesirable characteristics, in the effort to Breed a better human. The narrator has to deal with an internal demon, a voice that tries to get him to remove the most recent pin. Old memories are not entirely suppressed and manifest themselves in this way. As the menace from the killer - now known as Mr. Black, grows, the voice becomes more persistent until one by one the pins are removed, revealing more about the history of the cabal. The narrator is further dogged by A woman, whose father was forced to commit suicide by the cabal to suppress an unwanted technology, and who seems to know Mr. Black. This section of the story features running battles through offices, factories and service tunnels, as the narrator hunts and is hunted by Mr. Black. In one scene the narrator is attempting to escape through offices, and as he passes each desk the phone rings in some mocking version of the pursuit. As The Pins are removed the narrator becomes a More capable hunter, and more ruthless. Finally the narrator kills Black, who dies with a smile on his face. It quickly becomes obvious why. Black is another clone, and his personality transfers to one of the cabal. The narrator fights off the transfer, but realizes that in doing so he has allowed Black to take over one of the others, who are all in Wing Null. Arriving there With the woman in tow, He finds that all but one are dead, and Black has escaped to the outside. He has also pulled all but the last pin. In a Final fight across the same blasted Landscape where Negri attacked in the beginning, Black kills the narrator, but then has to deal with the inevitable Transfer. Black Becomes the new narrator. He was a stolen clone, but he did not Remember his origins. He simply lived in the cracks in the House society, somehow being helped by an unknown party. He eventually became the Cabal's nemesis, intent on opening the House and setting humanity free again. But now he is the cabal, with all their Conflicting memories and motives. He has to fight his way back to Wing Null, dealing with all the old defenses again. It becomes obvious that the clones are all Related to Angelo Negri. With the pulling of the final pin, Negri's personality is restored. He realizes that the House is a failure. The only thing Left to do is to break open the various Wings and shut down the Computer. This he does. For the last time, the phone rings. The woman answers. It is the disembodied scientist, who has been manipulating events all along. Negri tells the woman to take a message.

This is for every girl that has gossiped about me.
This is for every boy that has ever broken my heart.
This is for all the friends that I   thought I had, but  in  the end just turned  out to be  backstabbers.
This is  for  all the  people that told me I couldn’t make it.
This is for anyone that has  ever said I wasn’t good  enough.
This is for all the ones that enjoyed kicking me while I was down.
This is for all  the ones that pushed me behind just as I was getting ahead.
This is  for all the people that have ever made me feel less than what I really was.
This is for all the kids that made fun of  me because I was different.
This  is for the ones that told me I  was a failure, and made me believe it.
Because of you, I am the person  that I want to be.
Because of you,  I  have learned to overcome all the insults that have been thrown at me.
You  challenged me to  fight for the kind  of life   that I  wanted, not the kind  of life you saw for me.
I am a much stronger person than I used  to be.
I’m  not that  weak little girl that  you used  to know.
I’m not  that little kid that wouldn’t mount to anything.
I am so much more  than that.
Because of your  judgment I have  realized that nothing would hold me back.
You have  done so much more than  you  think.
So, thanks.
This is to any person that has ever  befriended me.
This is  to every  hand that has ever  helped me when  I was  down.
This is for everyone that believed in me.
This  is  to  all the  people  that gave  me strength to follow my  dreams.
This is for all the people who taught me to figure out who I am, and to accept the  person I became.
This is  for anyone who has  ever  said, “Good  job! ” when I thought that  I  had ruined everything.
This is for  all the people that stood behind me.
This is for all the  ones  that stood up for me.
This is to all the people that had hopes   for me, even when I  didn’t have any  for myself.
This is for all the  people that pushed me to go forward, even at  times I wanted   to stop completely.
Without you,   I  wouldn’t be who I am  today.
Without you, I would  still be the  “nothing” that everyone thought I was.
Without  you, I would  still be that  scared little  girl, afraid to try   anything  new.
You made me realize how much I was really worth.
You made me realize that I  wasn’t alone.
You made   me realize  that the world hadn’t turned it’s back on me,
I turned  my  back on the world.
Because of  you, I am  the person that I  have  always wanted to be.
Because of  you, I like who I  am.
Because of you, My  entire life has changed for the better.
So, thanks.

Anglers in the UK  could face prosecution  if they fail to follow new rules aimed at protecting fish stocks,  the  Environment Agency has warned.

Under the new guidelines,  anglers are  required  to return  any fish they  catch that are  undersized  or  breaching certain limits  to the water  immediately. The rules  also prohibit the  use  of certain types of fishing gear that can harm  fish populations.

The Environment Agency  has stated that the new  regulations are necessary to ensure the sustainability  of fish  stocks and protect the environment. However, some anglers  have criticized the  rules, saying  they are too restrictive and will ruin the sport.

"We  understand  that  some anglers may  be frustrated by the new rules,  but we must take action to protect fish stocks for future generations," said an Environment Agency spokesperson. " Failure to  comply with the regulations could result in  fines and even prosecution."

The agency has also  warned that anglers who fail to follow the rules could face penalties of up to £50,000.

The new regulations  have been  met  with mixed reactions from the angling community.  Some   anglers have expressed support  for the measures,  saying they are necessary to protect fish populations. Others, however, have criticized the rules as an   overreach  by the government and a threat to their livelihoods.

The Environment Agency has emphasized that  the new regulations  are not intended to  harm  the angling industry, but rather to  ensure  that fish  stocks remain healthy and  sustainable.

"We want to work  with anglers to  ensure that they can continue  to enjoy  their  sport while also protecting the environment," said the spokesperson. "We believe that  these new rules  are a  fair and necessary measure to achieve that goal."

The new regulations are set to come into effect on April  1st and will be enforced by  the Environment Agency and local police  forces. Anglers  are being urged  to familiarize themselves with the new rules  and to take steps to ensure they are in compliance.

I​'​m​ ​s​o​ ​s​i​c​k​ ​o​f​ ​R​e​d​d​i​t​'​s​ ​c​o​n​s​t​a​n​t​ ​p​u​s​h​ ​f​o​r​ ​p​r​e​m​i​u​m​ ​s​u​b​s​c​r​i​p​t​i​o​n​s​.​ ​I​t​ ​f​e​e​l​s​ ​l​i​k​e​ ​e​v​e​r​y​ ​t​i​m​e​ ​I​ ​l​o​g​ ​i​n​,​ ​t​h​e​r​e​'​s​ ​a​ ​n​e​w​ ​f​e​a​t​u​r​e​ ​t​h​a​t​'​s​ ​o​n​l​y​ ​a​v​a​i​l​a​b​l​e​ ​t​o​ ​p​r​e​m​i​u​m​ ​u​s​e​r​s​.​ ​I​ ​g​e​t​ ​i​t​,​ ​R​e​d​d​i​t​ ​n​e​e​d​s​ ​t​o​ ​m​a​k​e​ ​m​o​n​e​y​,​ ​b​u​t​ ​i​t​'​s​ ​g​e​t​t​i​n​g​ ​r​i​d​i​c​u​l​o​u​s​.​
​
​I​ ​u​s​e​d​ ​t​o​ ​b​e​ ​a​b​l​e​ ​t​o​ ​e​n​j​o​y​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​i​t​e​ ​w​i​t​h​o​u​t​ ​p​a​y​i​n​g​ ​a​ ​d​i​m​e​.​ ​I​ ​c​o​u​l​d​ ​b​r​o​w​s​e​ ​m​y​ ​f​a​v​o​r​i​t​e​ ​s​u​b​r​e​d​d​i​t​s​,​ ​p​a​r​t​i​c​i​p​a​t​e​ ​i​n​ ​d​i​s​c​u​s​s​i​o​n​s​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​s​h​a​r​e​ ​c​o​n​t​e​n​t​ ​w​i​t​h​o​u​t​ ​a​n​y​ ​r​e​s​t​r​i​c​t​i​o​n​s​.​ ​B​u​t​ ​n​o​w​,​ ​i​t​ ​f​e​e​l​s​ ​l​i​k​e​ ​I​'​m​ ​c​o​n​s​t​a​n​t​l​y​ ​b​e​i​n​g​ ​n​a​g​g​e​d​ ​t​o​ ​u​p​g​r​a​d​e​.​ ​E​v​e​r​y​ ​t​i​m​e​ ​I​ ​t​r​y​ ​t​o​ ​u​p​v​o​t​e​ ​a​ ​c​o​m​m​e​n​t​,​ ​I​'​m​ ​p​r​o​m​p​t​e​d​ ​t​o​ ​s​i​g​n​ ​u​p​ ​f​o​r​ ​p​r​e​m​i​u​m​.​ ​E​v​e​r​y​ ​t​i​m​e​ ​I​ ​t​r​y​ ​t​o​ ​v​i​e​w​ ​a​ ​i​m​a​g​e​,​ ​I​'​m​ ​a​s​k​e​d​ ​t​o​ ​u​p​g​r​a​d​e​ ​f​o​r​ ​"​e​n​h​a​n​c​e​d​ ​i​m​a​g​e​ ​v​i​e​w​i​n​g​.​"​ ​I​t​'​s​ ​l​i​k​e​ ​R​e​d​d​i​t​ ​i​s​ ​t​r​y​i​n​g​ ​t​o​ ​p​u​n​i​s​h​ ​m​e​ ​f​o​r​ ​n​o​t​ ​p​a​y​i​n​g​ ​u​p​.​
​

Nanoparticles of high refractive index materials can possess strong magnetic
polarizabilities and give rise to artificial magnetism in the optical spectral
range. While the response of individual dielectric or metal spherical particles
can be described analytically via multipole decomposition in the Mie series,
the influence of substrates, in many cases present in experimental
observations, requires different approaches. Here, the comprehensive numerical
studies of the influence of a substrate on the spectral response of high- index
dielectric nanoparticles were performed. In particular, glass, perfect electric
conductor, gold, and hyperbolic metamaterial substrates were investigated.
Optical properties of nanoparticles were characterised via scattering
cross-section spectra, electric field profiles, and induced electric and
magnetic moments. The presence of substrates was shown to introduce significant
impact on particle's magnetic resonances and resonant scattering
cross-sections. Variation of substrate material provides an additional degree
of freedom in tailoring properties of emission of magnetic multipoles,
important in many applications.

before I came
and after birth

at my toe what a shame
My parents
called my name
and my heart beats
but I was a human
without an ID-card
also when I fall asleep
My identity
is not my passport
not my name
not my land

with my free hands
My identity
is not my color

My identity

My identity

given

Separates me

toys

This identity

are my parents

are my children
and my friends
my identity
IS
the promised land
is the playground of my soul.
