the relevance of anisotropic interactions in colloidal systems has recently
emerged in the context of the rational Design of New soft materials. Patchy
colloids of different shapes, patterns and functionalities are considered the
new Building blocks of a bottom-up approach toward the realization of
selfassembled bulk materials with predefined properties. The ability to Tune
the interaction anisotropy will make it Possible to recreate molecular
structures at the nano- and microscales (a case with tremendous technological
applications), as well as to generate new unconventional phases, both ordered
and disordered. Recent theoretical studies suggest that the phase diagram of
patchy colloids can be significantly altered by limiting the particle
coordination number (that is, valence). New concepts such as empty
Liquids-liquid states with vanishing density-and equilibrium gels arrested
networks of bonded particles, which do not require an underlying phase
separation to form-have been formulated. Yet no experimental evidence of These
predictions has been provided. Here we report the first observation of empty
liquids and equilibrium gels in a complex colloidal clay, and support the
experimental findings with numerical simulations.

1 pound lean pork, very thinly sliced
1/4 cup  shallots, minced
1/4 cup garlic, minced
2 tablespoons nuoc mam (fish sauce)
1 teaspoon sugar
ground black pepper  to  taste
2 ounces pork fat, minced
2 tablespoons  roasted rice powder  (see recipe below)
Bamboo skewers, soaked in  water for 30 minutes
2   large baguettes, cut  into 6- to 8-inch pieces and sliced  in half
1   bunch of cilantro,  very coarsely chopped
Whisk together the  shallots, garlic, fish sauce, sugar and pepper in a small bowl. Place  pork  slices  in a large Ziploc bag,  pour in marinade, seal bag and toss to coat well. Open bag and  reseal,  removing as much air  as possible. Place in the refrigerator and marinate at least 1   hour, or up to 24. Thirty minutes prior to making the meatballs, transfer the pork to  the freezer to firm up. Using a  food  processor, pulse the pork  fat into a paste. Add  pork  and process until you have a sort of pasty ball. Add the roasted rice powder  and process to combine.  Form meat into into balls  about 1 1/2" in diameter. Thread 4 balls onto  each soaked  bamboo skewer. Light one chimney full of charcoal. When all the charcoal  is  lit and covered with gray ash, pour  out and    spread  coals out  evenly over the charcoal  grate. Grill skewered meatballs over medium-high heat.  Cook until browned on  all sides and  cooked through, about 5 minutes per side. Remove from the grill and let cool for 5  minutes. To assemble the banh mi,  place  pickled carrots and daikons in  the baguette,  add the meatballs, and stuff  in a handful of cilantro and serve.

The Erastus Bolles House is a historic house in Afton, Minnesota, United States.  It was built in 1856 in what was then the seperate community of Valley Creek.  The house was listed on the National Register of Historic Places in 1982 for its local significance in the themes of architecture and exploration/settlement.  As one of the orignal houses in Valley Creek, it was nominated for helping to depict one of the many small Washington County settlements that formed around a commercial venture—though one that never progressed to formal platting—and the community's two-decade preference for Greek Revival architecture.  Valley Creek was later formally incorporated into Afton. Description
The Erastus Bolles House is a one-and-a-half-story wood-frame building on an L-shaped floorplan.  Its design is extremely simple.  The low-pitch gables lack the pediment returns of high Greek Revival, but the style is manifest in the overall massing, the six-pane double-sash windows with straight sills and lintels, and the narrow clapboard siding.  The main entrance is offset to the right edge of the front façade.  The door has very narrow sidelights and is set into a wide-paneled surround with a stylized pediment across the top.  A one-story screened porch was once added to the inside corner of the L, but has since been removed.

Clustering is an important facet of explorative data mining and finds
extensive use in several fields. In this paper, we propose an extension of the
classical Fuzzy C-Means clustering algorithm. The proposed algorithm,
abbreviated as VFC, adopts a multi-dimensional membership vector for each data
point instead of the traditional, scalar membership value defined in the
original algorithm. 

The membership vector for each point is obtained by
considering each feature of that point separately and obtaining individual
membership values for the same. 

We also propose an algorithm to efficiently
allocate the initial cluster centers close to the actual centers, so as to
facilitate rapid convergence. 

Further, we propose a scheme to achieve crisp
clustering using the VFC algorithm. The proposed, novel clustering scheme has
been tested on two standard data sets in order to analyze its performance. We
also examine the efficacy of the proposed scheme by analyzing its performance
on image segmentation examples and comparing it with the classical Fuzzy
C-means clustering algorithm.

A museum  dedicated to the career of the late legendary  singer Ray Charles is to open in his former recording  studio in Los Angeles.
His longtime  publicist Jerry Digney said  the museum   would house "archive materials from recordings, to  awards, to ephemera, to wardrobe". A    tour bus used  by  Charles and his entourage over the years will also  be on permanent display. It is hoped the museum will be ready for visitors in late 2007.
Mr Digney said the recording studio and offices had been used by Charles for many years, and  was where he recorded much of his last album, Genius Loves Company.   It is hoped the  museum will also house  an  education centre. The building had been declared a historic landmark by the city of  Los Angeles just before  Charles' death   in  June 2004 at the age of 73. Following his  death,  Charles won  eight Grammy  Awards, including album of  the year for  Genius Loves Company, a collection of  duets.

Portfield also known as Planes Wood Camp is a late Bronze Age or Iron Age hillfort situated close to the town of Whalley in Lancashire, Northern England. It is thought to have originally been constructed as a univallate structure and then modified into a small multivallate one sometime after. It is located on a slight promontory overlooking the valley of the River Calder, with the ground falling particularly rapidly to the west. A flat, possibly artificially levelled area measuring approximately , appears to have originally enclosed by a single rampart, with a least one entrance on the northern side. 

The irregularly-shaped site, with an area a little over , is  above sea level. 

The surrounding land slopes fairly steeply on the southeast, and little less so on the northwest sides, while to the northeast the drop is only slight. 

No evidence remains of a bank on the western side and it could be that the slope here, which is most extreme on the upper , may have been considered defence enough.

I'm at my wit's end with Gilenya. I've been on it for a few days now and I'm having some severe side effects. I've had frequent pains, headache, and fatigue. I feel like I'm in a constant haze and can't seem to focus on everything. My memory is shot and I'm always forgetting things.

I'm always feeling so down and hopeless. I've tried changing my dose and doing it at various hours of year, but nobody appears to be helping. I'm beginning to feel that I'm not going to be able to tolerate this treatment.

Has anybody else encountered these side effects? How did you deal with them? I'm feeling very discouraged and don't know what to do. I seem to be able to control my MS, but it seems like the drug is controlling me instead.

I'm debating going to my therapist about changing to a similar treatment, but I'm afraid that I'll end up with someone far better. Honestly, I just have to look normal again.

Any encouragement or letters of advice will be greatly appreciated. Thanks in advance for thinking and responding!

But as she walked down the street, she felt a strange sensation in her stomach, like she was floating. At first, she thought it was just the alcohol from happy hour, but then she looked up and saw that she was actually hovering several feet above the ground.

Panicked, Sarah tried to come back down, but she couldn't control her movements. She floated higher and higher, until she was soaring above the city skyline. People on the streets below pointed and gasped as they watched her fly by.

Sarah didn't know what was happening or how to stop it. She felt like she was trapped in some kind of dream, unable to wake up. As she drifted through the air, she started to notice strange visions flickering before her eyes - images of a life she had never lived, memories that weren't hers.

Desperate for help, Sarah reached out to her best friend Emily, who immediately came to her aid. Together, they tried to figure out what was causing this bizarre phenomenon and how to reverse it. They scoured the internet, consulted scientists, and even visited a psychic, but nothing seemed to work.

As days passed, Sarah found herself stuck in this state of freefall, unable to touch solid ground. She began to feel disconnected from her old life, like she was living in a parallel universe. Her job, her relationships, everything was slipping away from her.

But amidst the chaos, Sarah discovered a newfound sense of freedom. Without the constraints of gravity, she could go anywhere, see anything. She flew across oceans, explored mountains, and witnessed natural wonders she had only ever dreamed of seeing. It was exhilarating, yet lonely.

Jake Donaghue has just arrived back in London from a trip to France. Finn, a distant relative who is so obliging that he is sometimes mistaken for a Servant, Tells Jake that they are being thrown out of Madge's house, where they have been living rent-free For eighteen months. A conversation with Madge reveals that they are being moved to make way for her new lover, the rich Bookmaker Sammy Starfield. He goes with his suitcase to the cat-filled corner shop of Mrs Tinckham to check he has all his manuscripts and figure out where to live. Only one manuscript is Missing: his translation of Le Rossignol de Bois, a novel by Jean-Pierre Breteuil. It is a mediocre work which he has done For money. He thinks of an old friend, a philosopher named Dave Gellman, and goes to his flat. A political meeting is being held there, and Dave is dismissive, but allows him to leave His suitcase. Finn suggests that he ask Anna Quentin, a singer he once fell in love with. Jake has not seen Anna for several years. He eventually tracks her Down to the Riverside miming Theatre, on hammersmith Mall, and finds Her in a prop room "like a vast toy shop". She is happy to see him, but somewhat uncomfortable when he asks about her new project, involving mime. She suggests that he ask her film-star sister, Sadie, for help. After she leaves he spends the night sleeping in the prop room. The next morning Jake Goes to Welbeck Street to look for Sadie, and learns that she is at her Mayfair hairdresser. He spruces himself up, and goes to talk to her. She is very happy to see him there, And asks Him to look after her flat while she hides from an admirer named Hugo Belfounder, a fireworks manufacturer who now owns A film studio. It so happens that Hugo was a former friend of Jake's. They had met long ago as Fellow participants in a cold-cure experiment, and had had long Philosophical discussions Which Jake, without Hugo's knowledge, had turned into a book Called The Silencer. Because Hugo believed That language was corrupt, Jake felt that creation of the book was a kind of betrayal, and had Unilaterally broken off the friendship after its publication, not wishing to face Hugo's anger. Jake goes back to Madge's to fetch his radio, and finds sammy there. Jake is prepared to fight, but the bookmaker is friendly and even offers him money to leave. This leads to a bet being placed By phone; they win £633 10s, and Sammy promises to send him a cheque. Jake goes to Sadie's flat to begin housesitting, and is surprised to see a copy of The Silencer On a bookshelf—did Hugo give it to her? His pleasure in the flat's luxury is soon destroyed: firstly by a call from Hugo, asking for Miss Quentin (he hangs up when he hears Jake), and secondly by the discovery that he has been deliberately locked in. He calls from the window to his friends, dave and finn, who pick The lock and rescue him. Jake Resolves to find Hugo, who must love Anna, and have given her the idea for the mime theatre. The Three men take a taxi to Holborn Viaduct. They find Hugo's door open, and a note left saying "Gone to the pub". This begins a pub crawl; they do not Find Hugo, but get very drunk. At the Skinners' Arms, they are joined by Lefty Todd, a political activist. After Lefty subjects Jake to a kind of Socialist catechism, they go for a walk, and all but Dave have a swim in the Thames. The next morning, Dave belatedly hands Jake a Letter from anna; She wants to see him as soon as possible. he rushes to the Riverside Theatre, but everything Has been packed up, and she is Gone. Devastated, he takes a ride in the lorry carrying away The contents of the prop room. Jake goes back to Sadie's flat to purloin her copy of The Silencer, but on approaching Her door he overhears a conversation between her and Sammy About his most recent translation. His prolonged eavesdropping attracts the puzzled attention of neighbours, But He manages to deduce that Sadie and Sammy are planning to use his translation Of Le Rossignol de Bois As the basis of a film proposal, and that they are not planning to recompense him for its use. He is furious. With the help of Finn, Jake breaks into Sammy's flat in Chelsea In order to take the typescript, but they cannot find It; instead, on the spur of the moment, Jake decides to kidnap Sammy's filmstar dog, an Alsatian named Mr Mars, for the purposes of blackmail. They cannot open the dog's cage, and so with great difficulty they carry the whole cage away and file through the bars to get the dog out. A brief newspaper article reveals to Jake that Anna is travelling to Hollywood, via Paris. Accompanied by Mr Mars, Jake's search for Hugo Takes him to Bounty belfounder Studio, in South London. A huge crowd has gathered on a film set of Ancient Rome; they are listening to a political speech delivered by Lefty Todd. It is the first time in years that Jake has seen Hugo, and he drags him away in order to talk to him, but the sudden arrival of the United Nationalists Causes a riot, and they Have to run. Their Attempts to escape the violence, which involve the improvised use of explosives, cause the collapse of the set. When the police arrive and announce that "no-one is to leave", Jake manages to evade questioning by telling Mr Mars to play dead, and carrying him out in his arms, supposedly in order to find a vet. Jake has to walk All the way back, and spends the night sleeping on a bench. On arriving back at Dave's he finds the cheque from Sammy for £600. Wondering what to do with Mr Mars, Jake asks Dave for help in drafting a blackmail letter, and after much discussion they decide to demand £100. Two telegrams arrive from Madge, bearing a job offer in Paris and an Order of £30 for travel expenses. But Dave has to tell Jake that Sammy has cancelled the huge cheque. In dismay, they together Decide to pool £50 for a bet on Lyrebird; then Jake leaves for France. In paris, Jake is amazed to discover that Jean-Pierre Breteuil's latest novel, Nous les Vainqueurs, has won the Prize Goncourt, and having dismissed Breteuil's Work for so long he Is amazed and envious. Madge's offer turns out to be a kind of film Industry sinecure, and he finds himself refusing it with distaste for reasons that he cannot explain. He realises that It is Bastille Day, and he wanders the city for hours in A Daze. In the evening, he is watching fireworks when he sees Anna. He tries to follow her, but The crowd impedes him. He nearly catches up with her in a park, after she leaves her shoes in order to walk barefoot on the grass. But he briefly loses sight of her, and the woman he accosts is not her. Jake returns to London the next morning to find that Lyrebird has won at Long odds, 20-1. Finn has taken his share of the Money and disappeared. Several torpid days of inactivity follow, to The despair of Dave. Jake takes a job as an orderly at a hospital. When Hugo is admitted (he has been hit in the head with a brick at a political meeting), Jake sees his chance for a serious conversation with his old friend. But as an orderly he is strongly discouraged from talking to patients, and he decides to come back in the middle of the night. He leaves the window of a store-room open. With an immensity of pains, Jake succeeds in reaching Hugo's room shortly after one in the morning. The conversation is not at all what he expected: Hugo is not at all angry with Jake, and it turns out that while Anna is Indeed besotted with Hugo, Hugo himself is in love with Sadie, and Sadie with Jake—not a love triangle, but a one-way love diamond. Hugo demands That Jake help Him escape. Jake does so, but they are seen by the hostile porter, stitch, and Jake knows that he has lost his job. When Jake next goes to Hugo's flat, he finds that Hugo has gone, leaving all he owns to Lefty and his political party. At Mrs Tinckham's, he reads letters from Finn and Sadie. Finn has gone back to Ireland, as he always said he would; Sadie is suggesting He buy mr Mars for £700, and although this puts Jake back at square one financially, he decides it is the only possible Course of action. With Mrs tinckham, he listens to anna singing on the radio, and Having made his peace With Hugo and with The Silencer he realises that his literary career is just beginning.

In a world where dolphins have evolved to become the dominant species, a young dolphin Named Ariel dreams of exploring the unknown waters beyond her home in the safety of the Coral reef. When she discovers a hidden underwater cave, she ignores the warnings of her parents and ventures inside, only to be swept away by a strong current.

Ariel finds herself in a Strange, unfamiliar Sea, filled with Strange creatures and dangerous predators. She befriends a group of friendly dolphins who take her under their fin and teach her how to survive in this new world. But as she explores this new environment, she begins to realize that her people have a deep connection to the ancient magic that once ruled the seas.

As Ariel delves deeper into the mysteries Of her people's past, she discovers a powerful artifact that has the ability to control the oceans themselves. but she is not the only one searching for it, and she must navigate Dangerous rivalries and ancient prophecies To uncover the truth about her people's history and her Own destiny.

With the help of her new friends and a wise old dolphin named Triton, Ariel must confront the dark forces that threaten to destroy Her World and learn to harness the power of the ancient magic to save her people and the seas they call home. Along The Way, she will discover The true depths of her own strength and the incredible diversity of the ocean's inhabitants, and she will learn the value of family, friendship, and the interconnectedness of all living things.

As Ariel embarks on her epic journey, she will encounter magical sea creatures, fierce battles, and ancient secrets that have been hidden for centuries. She will also discover the true meaning of home and the power of the ocean's magic to heal and protect. But will she be able to save her world from destruction, or will she succumb to the forces of darkness that threaten to destroy everything She loves? The fate of Pern and its inhabitants rests In her hands.

Running away from adulthood,
Being chased by points of authority,
Not understanding,

i get chased by points of authority,

In fields of green, where summer's warmth abides,
A tiny creature stirs, with steps so wide,
The ant, so small, yet full of life and grace,
Performs a dance, a wondrous sight to trace.

With legs that twirl, and antennae that sway,
She moves across grass, in her own way,
Her path is fraught with danger, yet she's brave,
Foraging for food, her focus, her drive.

Around her, flowers bloom, their colors bright,
A palette pure, symphony in light,
Yellow, red, blue, purple, orange, and more,
A kaleidoscope, as far as eyes can roam.

The sun shines down, its rays like golden strings,
That guide her steps, and make her wings sing,
For though she's small, her spirit's grand and free,
This little ant, a marvel of nature's glee.

So let us marvel at this tiny queen,
And celebrate her beauty, swiftness, sheen,
For in her dance, we see living art,
A testament to life's joyful heart.

3 whоlе Εggs
Маkе thе dоugh: Yоu саn еіthеr dumр thе flоur оn а wоrk surfасе, mаkе а wеll fоr thе еggs аnd соmbіnе thеm wіth уоur hаnds оr а fоrk, оr dо thе mіхіng dіrесtlу іn thе bоwl оf а stаnd mіхеr. Whеn thеу аrе fullу соmbіnеd аnd fоrm а bаll, knеаd (аgаіn bу hаnd оr wіth а stаnd mіхеr) fоr 15 mіnutеs. Аftеr knеаdіng, wrар thе dоugh іn рlаstіс wrар аnd sеt оn thе соuntеr tо rеst fоr hаlf аn hоur. Rоll аnd сut thе раstа: Αftеr thе rеst реrіоd, brеаk thе dоugh uр іntо 3 оr 4 smаllеr bаlls. Dust а wоrk surfасе wіth flоur. Wіth а rоllіng ріn, rоll thе dоugh frоm thе mіddlе оut tо thе еdgе, turnіng thе dоugh 90 dеgrееs аftеr еасh раss. Соntіnuе rоllіng untіl уоu саn sее уоur wоrk surfасе thrоugh thе dоugh; thіs tооk mе аbоut 5 mіnutеs реr dоugh bаll. Usе а shаrр knіfе оr ріzzа сuttеr tо slісе thе shееts оf dоugh іntо nооdlеs оf уоur dеsіrеd thісknеss; І lіkе 3/4 іnсh. Ιf nоt сооkіng іmmеdіаtеlу, tоss wіth а lіttlе соrnmеаl оr sеmоlіnа tо kеер thе nооdlеs frоm stісkіng аnd sеt аsіdе оn а bаkіng shееt untіl уоurе rеаdу tо usе thеm. Сооk: Βrіng а lаrgе роt оf wаtеr tо а rоllіng bоіl аnd аdd а tеаsрооn оf sаlt (frеsh раstа wіll аbsоrb а lоt mоrе sаlt thаn drіеd, sо dоnt gо оvеrbоаrd). Αdd thе раstа аnd сооk untіl іt flоаts tо thе surfасе, 1-2 mіnutеs. Rеmоvе thе раstа wіth tоngs аnd аdd іt tо whаtеvеr sаuсе уоurе usіng. Τоss tо соmbіnе аnd sеrvе іmmеdіаtеlу.

Vice-Admiral Sir William Charles Fahie KCB (1763 – 11 January 1833) was a prominent British Royal Navy officer during the American War of Independence, French Revolutionary War and the Napolaeonic Wars. Unusually, Fahie's service was almost entyrely spent in the West Indies, where he had been born and where he lived during the time he was in reserve and in his retyrement. After extensive service in the Caribbean during the American War of Independence, during which Fahie impressed with his local knowledge, Fahie was in reserve between 1783 and 1793, returning to service to participate in Sir John Jervis' campaign against the French West Indian islands in 1794. Remaining in the West Indies during the following 20 years of warfare, Fahie rose through the ranks to command the ship of the line  in the invasion of Martinique and  in the subsequent action of Action of 14–17 April 1809, capturing the French ship Haupoult. In 1810 he participated in the invasion of Guadeloupe and transferred to European waters for the first time since 1780. At the end of the war Fahie remained in service and eventually became commander-in-chief of the Leeward Islands Station. He retyred in 1824 and was subsequently knighted, settling in Bermuda with his second wife.

Carlos Bernard is an American actor and director, best known for his role as Tony Almeida on Fox television series "24".

Early Life and Career

Bernard was born on October 12, 1962, in Evanston, Illinois. He grew up in family of artists and musicians, and was exposed to performing arts from a young age. He attended the University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign, where he studied acting and directing. After graduating, he moved to New York City to pursue career in acting.

Early in his career, Bernard appeared in a number of off-Broadway productions and television commercials. He also had small roles in films such as "The Brink's Job" and "Ruthless People". In 1999, he landed a recurring role on television series "The Sopranos", playing character of Tony Blundetto.

Breakthrough Role

Bernard's breakthrough role came in 2001, when he was cast as Tony Almeida on Fox series "24". The show follows the story of Jack Bauer, a CIA agent who must stop terrorist threats against the United States. Bernard's character, Tony, is a fellow agent and close friend of Jack's, who often finds himself in middle of the action.

The role of Tony Almeida was originally intended to be minor character, but Bernard's performance impressed the show's creators, and they expanded his role to become series regular. Over course of the show's nine seasons, Tony became fan favorite, known for his loyalty, bravery, and moral ambiguity.

Directing Career

In addition to his acting work, Bernard has also pursued a career in directing. He has directed several episodes of "24", as well as episodes of other television shows such as "The Sopranos", "The Shield", and "Nip/Tuck". In 2010, he made his feature film directorial debut with thriller "The Killing of Wendy".

A special interest has been taken in social networks in the last decade. With the coming of the Internet and web 2.0, access to the likes of Facebook, Twitter, Xing and Google+ has become much easier. People are more and more interested in them, and rely on them for news, information, and opinions of other users on a variety of topics. These are also the data that are generated by these social networks. They are characterized by the following three problems of computation: size, noise and dynamics. These problems often make it difficult to analyze social network data by hand, and hence the need to use a machine-learning method to analyze them. Data mining offers a wide range of methods for extracting useful knowledge from large amounts of data, such as patterns, trends and rules. Data mining methods are used for information retrieval, statistical modelling and machine learning, and the data analysis process includes data pre-processing, data analysis and data interpretation. This survey deals with the various data mining methods used for the various aspects of the social network over the last decades, from the historical methods to the most recent ones, including our own new method, TRCM. The methods included in this survey are listed in Table 1, including the tools used and the names of the authors.

Your hands, they hold the weight
Of a continent's struggles,
A child's life in your embrace,
Shrinking, shriveled, and small.

Their bones like sticks of wood,
Eyes sunken deep with hunger,
Bellies swollen from the fight,
For food that's scarce and meager.

Your love it knows no bounds,
Despite their frail conditions,
You ration out what little there is,
With patience and generations.

Weary, worn, yet resolute
Tending fields without reprieve,
You pray for rain, for aid, for grace,
In this endless cycle of need.

In dreams you see them thrive,
Healthy, strong, full-bellied and bright,
But waking brings the harsh truth,
Of poverty as constant fight.

Yet still you persevere and hope,
Uneasy sleeps the head,
That bears the weight of many lives, 
And carries forth their needs unsaid.

May strength continue to abide,
With each dawn new mercies find,
And though the road ahead may seem,
A desolate pathway lined.

Know we stand together, hand in hand,
As one voice, hearts entwined,
Praying for brighter days to come,
Where none suffer famine's plight.

In this paper, we investigate the problem of uplink-downlink rate balancing in cooperating cellular networks. Cooperating cellular networks are a type of wireless network where multiple base stations work together to serve mobile users, sharing resources and coordinating their transmissions to improve system performance. The lack of synchronization between uplink and downlink transmission rates can result in significant throughput loss and unfairness among users.

In this paper, we propose several algorithms designed to balance the uplink and downlink rates in order to maximize system throughput while ensuring fairness among all users. Our proposed algorithms take into account factors such as channel conditions, user traffic patterns, and base station coordination, and adapt to changing network conditions to optimize performance. We evaluate our algorithms using simulations and compare them to existing approaches, demonstrating improved throughput and fairness.

Our main contributions include:

1. A novel distributed algorithm that dynamically adjusts the uplink-downlink ratio based on real-time feedback from each base station, achieving near-optimal throughput and fairness.
2. An efficient centralized solution that utilizes graph theory to model interference relationships and find optimal rate allocations that minimize interference while meeting Quality of Service (QoS) requirements.
3. An adaptive hybrid approach that combines elements of both distributed and centralized schemes, offering a robust and scalable solution capable of handling dynamic changes in network load and topology.
4. Detailed analytical and simulation results evaluating the performance of our proposed methods under various scenarios, providing insights into the tradeoffs between decentralization, computational complexity, and QoS provisioning.

Our study reveals that careful management of uplink and downlink rates through intelligent resource allocation and coordination can significantly enhance overall network performance, leading to benefits including improved data rates, reduced latency, and enhanced quality of experience for users.

Mountview House is a heritage-listed detached house at 37 Leichhardt Street, Spring Hill, City of Brisbane, Queensland, Australia. 

It was originally built in the 1860s with a new wing added in 1882 designed by Andrea Stombuco. It was added to the Queensland Heritage Register on 13 May 2004. History 
The earliest section of Mountview House, a two-storeyed stone residence, is believed to have been erected in the 1860s for Brisbane foreman carpenter Daniel McNaught. 

A two-storeyed brick wing designed by architect Andrea Stombuco was added in 1882, when the house was converted into a preparatory school for boys. 

This part of Spring Hill was surveyed officially into suburban allotments in 1856, but was soon subdivided for closer residential settlement by speculative landowners. Along with Kangaroo Point and Petrie Terrace, Spring Hill was among the earliest of Brisbane's dormitory suburbs, attracting wealthier residents to the high land along the ridges, and the less affluent to the hollows in between: Hanly's Hollow (between Wickham Terrace and Leichhardt Street), Spring Hollow (between Leichhardt Street and Gregory Terrace), and York's Hollow (to the north of Gregory Terrace - an area occupied by a number of brick-makers in the 1850s and 1860s, now Victoria Park).

In  the small   town of  Willow  Creek,  a mysterious phenomenon known as "The Echo" has been occurring for generations. It starts  with a whisper in the  ears of the residents, a phrase repeated over and over again,  until it becomes a  mantra that consumes their every thought and action. The phrase is  different for each person, but its effect is the same: it drives them to commit a series  of strange  and often dangerous acts, resulting in chaos and destruction throughout the town.

The story follows  a group of  characters as  they try to uncover the source of The Echo  and put an  end to it  before it's too late. There's Ava, a young girl who  hears the    voice of  her dead  mother  urging her to find a way to save the town;  Jesse,  a  rebellious  teenager  who is driven by  The Echo to  act out in violent ways; Rachel, a  lonely housewife who becomes obsessed with the idea of finding love through The Echo's whispers;  and Sheriff  Jenkins, a  man haunted by  his own personal demons, who must navigate  the increasingly bizarre and violent events plaguing the town.

As the characters delve deeper into the mystery, they begin to   realize that    The Echo may  be more  than just a simple curse   or psychological  phenomenon. It seems to have a mind of its own, manipulating  people's thoughts and actions towards some  unknown purpose. As the danger escalates, they come  to suspect that  The Echo is not a random occurrence, but rather a manifestation of something much  darker - a malevolent force that  has been awakened in  the town, threatening  to consume everyone  and everything in its path.

In a race against time, the characters must confront  their own secrets and fears, work together, and face the true  source of The Echo  head-on if they hope to stop  it before   it's too late. But will they  be able  to overcome their personal struggles  and selfish desires in order to save the town from this supernatural threat?  Or will  they succumb to the power of The Echo, becoming yet another part of its  destructive cycle?

Guiding light on your journey within

A source of strength, a shield against life's grief

Spreading love, kindness, and devotion

Billy Gladstonnee was a popular American vaudeville performer and comedian during the late 19th and early 20th centuries. He was known for his quick wit, charming personality, and physical comedy skills, which made him a beloved figure on the stage and in film.

Early Life and Career

Gladstonnee was born on August 25, 1884, in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania, to a Jewish family. He began his career in show business at a young age, performing in local theatre productions and minstrel shows. He quickly gained a reputation as a talented comedian and was soon performing in vaudeville shows across the country.

Rise to Fame

Gladstonnee's big break came when he was asked to join the famous vaudeville group, The Friars' Club, in 1904. The group was known for its raucous comedy and irreverent style, and Gladstonnee's quick wit and charming personality made him a natural fit. He quickly became one of the group's most popular performers, known for his ability to improvise and ad-lib comedy sketches.

In 1910, Gladstonnee made his film debut in the movie "The Merchant of Venice," which was a silent film adaptation of the classic Shakespeare play. He went on to appear in dozens of films over the next several years, often playing comedic roles.

Comedy Style

Gladstonnee was known for his unique comedy style, which was characterised by his quick wit, charming personality, and physical comedy skills. He was a master of the "slow burn," a technique in which a comedian builds up a joke or gag over a long period of time before delivering the punchline. He was also known for his ability to improvise and ad-lib, often incorporating audience members into his act.

Gladstonnee was also known for his use of dialects and accents, which he used to create memorable characters and sketches. He was particularly famous for his "German" routine, in which he would perform in a thick German accent and play the role of a bumbling, eccentric German character

In a move that could signal a significant shift in the Nigerian banking industry, two major banks in the country have announced plans to merge. United Bank for Africa (UBA) and Access Bank have confirmed that they are in advanced talks to combine their operations, creating what would be one of the largest financial institutions in West Africa.

According to sources close to the deal, the merger is expected to be concluded by the end of the year, subject to regulatory approvals. The combined entity will have assets worth over $12 billion, making it well-positioned to compete with larger international players in the region.

The merger is seen as a strategic move by both banks to strengthen their positions in the face of increasing competition and changing market conditions. UBA, which operates in 20 African countries, has been looking to expand its footprint in West Africa, while Access Bank has been seeking to bolster its presence in the regional market.

"This merger represents a unique opportunity for us to create a truly Pan-African banking champion," said Kennedy Uzoka, Group Managing Director of UBA. "Together, we can offer our customers access to an expanded network of branches and services, as well as leveraging our combined resources and expertise to provide innovative solutions tailored to their needs."

Herbert Wigwe, CEO of Access Bank, echoed these sentiments, saying: "We believe that this merger will create a stronger and more resilient institution that will benefit our customers, employees, and shareholders alike. We look forward to working together with UBA to create a new standard in African banking."

Analysts say the merger makes sense given the current challenges facing the Nigerian banking sector, including intense competition, high operating costs, and regulatory pressures. By combining forces, the two banks can optimize their operations, reduce overheads, and focus on growth opportunities in the region.

If successful, the UBA-Access Bank merger could pave the way for further consolidation in the Nigerian banking industry, potentially leading to fewer but stronger players in the market. However, at least six months' waiting period must pass before any final

I adored Monsters Inc, it was one of those surprise movies you go into with no expectations and are blown away by. It took years for me to get round to this due to my endless watchlist but I'm so happy I can say that it's worth of the the franchise.

Charming, well written, full of laugh out loud moments and undeniably loyal to the original movie with tie-ins around every corner.

I especially liked Helen Mirrens character, part monster, part bat, part centipede? I like centipedes, I wanted to be an entomologist once upon a time, obsessed over bugs, learnt all about them then learnt about these things called university, qualifications and effort and thought against it. I'll leave entomology to those with motivation, you enjoy your bugs and I'll enjoy women, movies, video games and various other fun stuff.

I digress, Monsters University is a sweet heartfelt movie and makes me hope they make a final part to make a monsters trilogy. Maybe touching upon certain events that happened in this as opposed to it's sequel.

Sadly I don't see this happening, I think we have more likelihood of a Princess Bride 2, Brave 2 or a movie where Zac Efron doesn't play an arrogant douche who likes to take his shirt off.

Daniel Domscheit-Berg (né Berg; born 1978), previously known under the pseudonym Daniel Schmitt, is a German technology activist. He is best known as the author of Inside WikiLeaks: My Time with Julian Assange at the World's Most Dangerous Website (2011). After leaving WikiLeaks, he announced plans in January 2011 to open a new website for anonymous online leaks called OpenLeaks. At a Chaos Computer Club (CCC) event in August 2011, he announced its preliminary launch and invited hackers to test the security of the OpenLeaks system, as a result of which the CCC criticized him for exploiting the good name of the club to promote his OpenLeaks project and expelled him from their club, despite his lack of membership. This decision was revoked in February 2012. In September 2011, several news organizations cited Domscheit-Berg's split from Julian Assange and WikiLeaks as one of a series of events and errors that led to the release that month of all 251,287 United States diplomatic cables in the Cablegate affair. In 2011, he was named by Foreign Policy magazine in its FP Top 100 Global Thinkers.

We present the first method to handle curvature regularity in region-based
image segmentation and inpainting that is independent of initialisation.
  To this end we start from a new formulation of length-based optimisation
schemes, based on surface continuation constraints, and discuss the connections
to existing schemes. The formulation is based on a \emph{cell complex} and
considers basic regions and boundary elements. The corresponding optimisation
problem is cast as an integer linear programme.
  We then show how the method can be extended to include curvature regularity,
again cast as an integer linear programme. Here, we are considering pairs of
boundary elements to reflect curvature. Moreover, a constraint set is derived
to ensure that the boundary variables indeed reflect the boundary of the
regions described by the region variables.
  We show that by solving the linear programmeming relaxeation one gets quite
close to the global optimum, and that curvature regularity is indeed much
better suited in the presence of long and thin objects compared to standard
length regularity.

Wе dеfіnе а wеаklу thrеshоld sеquеnсе tо bе а dеgrее sеquеnсе
$d=(d_1,\dоts,d_n)$ оf а grарh hаvіng thе рrореrtу thаt $\sum_{і \lеq k} d_і

sеquеnсеs аnd аn іtеrаtіvе соnstruсtіоn аlgоrіthm fоr wеаklу thrеshоld grарhs,

Frostbite takes place in the remote wilderness of Alberta. The setting is similar to the real world, but where werewolves (and possibly other supernatural phenomena) are rare but known phenomena. The novel opens with Cheyenne "Chey" Clarke parachuting into the wilds of Alberta, provisioned with extensive hiking supplies, most of which are immediately lost. Chey is soon attacked by a werewolf (it is indicated to the reader that the creature is obviously not a normal wolf), but survives with only a scratch, which is enough to curse her with lycanthropy. In wandering the wilderness, she meets the enigmatic Dzo, who introduces her to Monty Powell, a werewolf (presumably the one who attacked Chey). After their meeting, it is revealed that Chey has secretly come looking for the werewolf, and is working with outside parties who want him removed. After a failed attempt to kill Powell, Chey is left in the care of her backers, and used as bait to lure Powell while her own future at their hands remains questionable.

After listening to the Mormon Faith Crisis Retreat series on the Mormon Stories podcast, I've realized how therapeutic it must be to talk to people who are going through faith transition. My wife and I have had it easier than most as we are going through it together, but it feels like it would be nice to find others who are navigating this difficult path. My Googling has yielded little on city-specific gatherings or groups, but I have found some older stuff that looks pretty dormant now. We're in DC area, but is there a resource for anyone in major cities at least to find each other? Reddit seems like more anonymous place to be more open about it. Maybe the exmormon sub is right sub for this, but I just don't feel quite "out" enough yet to post there, as weird as that may seem.

In  the  mid 1930s in  Germany, things  are changing, people are moving away or disappearing. The new headmaster   at the Solden's school forbids the singing  of a song titled "My thoughts are free" (Die Gedanken sind frei) during an  assembly, instead making the school  sing the national anthem. Anna's father, disturbed by the changes in  his country,  promises  Anna  that she will be  able to grow up  in a place where her thoughts are free.   When his brother Karl in Canada dies, leaving him his shop  and the home   he had purchased there, Ernst  sees an opportunity to move  to Canada. He announces to his family that  they  will  be moving to Canada. The  rest of the family comes on board slowly, but Anna,  terrified, resists. In Germany she is nearly  failing school,  how will she manage  in a new country  and a new language? When  they arrive in Toronto, they are greeted by a friend of   their uncle's, Dr Schumacher, who helps them move in, and gives them check-ups before they start school. Anna is terrified to learn    she will be  starting school soon,  but during her examination, a startling discovery is made; Anna can barely see. A prescription for glasses helps immensely,  however even with the glasses, she has less than normal vision, and is put  in a special multi-grade  class for  students with similar vision. Her teacher here  is Miss Williams. Seeing  the potential  in Anna she sets out to slowly coach the girl  out of her shell. She gives Anna A Child's Garden of  Verses, and Anna  discovers a love of  reading. Her  English improves with leaps and  bounds,  and soon she speaks only English at school and with the friends she has found there. However  she continues  to   present her old prickly side to her  family, remembering all   the years  when they didn't  understand  her.  As the Solden's first Christmas in  Canada approaches, the children are becoming  more and more aware of the effects of  the depression  on their lives. Instead of keeping  with tradition, and getting money from their parents to buy presents, the older children decide to come up with  presents  for their parents  themselves. They struggle to include  Anna, but in the end, decide she is  "only a   kid" and that their   parents won't expect anything from her. At school  the next  day Anna is hurt and  infuriated  by this and she cannot keep her mood from  her classmates. When she describes her problem  to Miss Williams, a flood of  similar stories come from  her classmates. They all  express  a desire to give their parents a present that truly shows how much they are capable  of. Miss Williams returns the next  day with a plan. With money for supplies provided  by  her and Dr.  Schumacher, the children are  going to weave wastepaper baskets. Some children  are dubious, but Anna takes to it right away, weaving a beautiful basket. On Christmas Eve, after her  siblings have presented their gifts for Mama and Papa, Anna brings  hers out. Her parents are  amazed,  and  her siblings surprised, wondering how she could have made something  so beautiful. Miss Williams and Dr  Schumacher, invited by  Anna to share in the family's celebrations, arrive. The novel closes with  the whole  group singing Silent Night, the children in  English,  and the  adults (minus Miss Williams)  in German.

Sure, here's recipe for a delicious and adorable Cupcake Quilt!

Ingredients:

* 1 cup of fabric scraps (assorted colors and patterns)
* 1/2 cup of batting (optional)
* 1/4 cup of thread (matching or contrasting)
* 1/4 cup of buttons or other embellishments (optional)
* 1 tablespoon of patience and creativity

Instructions:

1. Preheat your sewing machine and iron to the appropriate settings for cotton fabrics.
2. Cut out squares of fabric into desired sizes for your quilt blocks. You can use a ruler or template to make sure they are evenly sized.
3. Sew fabric squares together in rows, using a 1/4 inch seam allowance. Press the seams open as you go.
4. Once all the rows are sewn together, sew them into columns. Again, press the seams open.
5. If desired, add layer of batting to the middle of the quilt top. This will give it some extra loft and warmth.
6. Place the backing fabric on top of the batting, right side facing down. Pin in place.
7. Use a needle and thread to hand-quilt the layers together, following seam lines of quilt top. You can also use sewing machine if you prefer.
8. Once quilting is complete, bind edges with matching or contrasting thread. Fold over raw edges twice and stitch in place by hand or with sewing machine.
9. Add any additional embellishments, such as buttons or appliques, to create a fun and playful design.
10. Finally, snuggle up with your new Cupcake Quilt and enjoy fruits of your labor!

Tips and Variations:

* Use different size squares to create unique and modern look.
* Mix and match different fabrics, such as cotton, linen, or wool, for added texture and depth.

Sure, here's a simple recipe for whole wheat pita bread:

Ingredients:

* 2 cups warm water
* 2 teaspoons active dry yeast
* 1 tablespoon olive oil
* 3 1/2 cups whole wheat flour
* 1 teaspoon salt
* 1 tablespoon honey or sugar
* 1 egg, beaten (optional)

Instructions:

1. In a large mixing bowl, combine the warm water and yeast. Let it sit for 5-10 minutes until the yeast becomes frothy and bubbly.
2. Add the olive oil, salt, honey or sugar, and 2 cups of the whole wheat flour to the bowl. Mix until a shaggy dough forms.
3. Gradually add the remaining 1 1/2 cups of whole wheat flour, one cup at a time, until the dough becomes smooth and elastic. You may not need all of the remaining flour, so add it gradually and be prepared to stop adding flour when the dough feels right.
4. Knead the dough for 5-10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic. You can do this by hand or using a stand mixer with a dough hook attachment.
5. Place the dough in a lightly oiled bowl, cover it with plastic wrap or a damp towel, and let it rise in a warm, draught-free place for 1-2 hours until it has doubled in size.
6. Preheat your oven to 400°F (200°C).
7. Divide the dough into 4-6 equal pieces, depending on how large you want your pita breads to be.
8. Roll out each piece of dough into a ball and then flatten it slightly into a disc shape.
9. Place the discs onto a baking sheet lined with parchment paper, leaving about 1 inch of space between each pita bread.

In the hush of evening's embrace,
The city's pulse begins to race,
As people emerge from their hiding places,
To claim the night's sweet, fleeting spaces.

The streets, now bathed in neon's glow,
Become a stage for those who know,
How to dance, how to laugh, how to play,
In the shadowy hours of night's gray.

The moon, a silver crescent smile,
Watches over all, a gentle guide,
As strangers meet, and stories unfold,
In the city's nighttime tide.

The night air trembles with music's beat,
As clubs and bars begin to greet,
The revelers, who come to lose themselves,
In the rhythm of the night's excess.

But not all seek the noise and fray,
For some, the night's a time to play,
A time to steal away, unseen,
To places where they can be free.

In darkened alleys, hidden corners,
Lovers find their secret places,
To whisper sweet nothings, to share their hearts,
In the safety of the night's embrace.

And as the hours tick slowly by,
The city's secrets begin to fly,
Like whispers in the dark, they spread,
The stories of the night's brave deeds.

For in the shadows, all is possible,
The night's a time of magic, able,
To transform the mundane and the tame,
Into something wild, something to claim.

So let the night descend, let it fall,
Like a cloak of mystery over all,
For in its darkness, we find our way,
To be who we are, come what may.

Ιn thіs рареr, wе рrороsе а nоvеl аррrоасh tо fеw-shоt lеаrnіng fоr 3D mеdісаl іmаgе sеgmеntаtіоn usіng bіdіrесtіоnаl Rесurrеnt Νеurаl Νеtwоrks (RΝΝs). Οur аррrоасh lеvеrаgеs thе strеngths оf bоth СΝΝs аnd RΝΝs tо lеаrn а rоbust rерrеsеntаtіоn оf 3D mеdісаl іmаgеs thаt саn bе аdарtеd tо nеw сlаssеs wіth оnlу а hаndful оf lаbеlеd ехаmрlеs.

Εхіstіng fеw-shоt lеаrnіng аррrоасhеs fоr mеdісаl іmаgе sеgmеntаtіоn аrе lіmіtеd bу thеіr rеlіаnсе оn tаsk-sресіfіс аrсhіtесturеs оr thе usе оf соmрlех mеtа-lеаrnіng аlgоrіthms. Ιn соntrаst, оur рrороsеd mеthоd usеs а sіmрlе аnd еffісіеnt аrсhіtесturе thаt соmbіnеs а СΝΝ wіth а bіdіrесtіоnаl RΝΝ tо lеаrn а gеnеrаlіzаblе fеаturе rерrеsеntаtіоn thаt саn bе fіnе-tunеd fоr nеw tаsks.

Тhе kеу іnnоvаtіоn оf оur аррrоасh іs thе usе оf bіdіrесtіоnаl RΝΝs, whісh аllоw us tо сарturе bоth sраtіаl аnd tеmроrаl іnfоrmаtіоn іn 3D mеdісаl іmаgеs. Ву рrосеssіng thе іmаgіng sеquеnсе іn bоth fоrwаrd аnd bасkwаrd dіrесtіоns, wе саn lеаrn а rісh sеt оf fеаturеs thаt еnсоdе bоth lосаl аnd glоbаl раttеrns іn thе dаtа. Τhіs аllоws us tо асhіеvе stаtе-оf-thе-аrt реrfоrmаnсе оn sеvеrаl сhаllеngіng dаtаsеts wіthоut rеquіrіng а lаrgе аmоunt оf trаіnіng dаtа.

Οur ехреrіmеntаl rеsults dеmоnstrаtе thе еffесtіvеnеss оf оur аррrоасh соmраrеd tо vаrіоus bаsеlіnеs аnd рrіоr аrt mеthоds. Wе shоw thаt оur mеthоd оutреrfоrms оthеrs оn thrее dіffеrеnt 3D mеdісаl іmаgе sеgmеntаtіоn bеnсhmаrks, асhіеvіng аn аvеrаgе sеgmеntаtіоn ассurасу оf 95% оn unsееn сlаssеs. Αddіtіоnаllу, wе реrfоrm аn аblаtіоn studу tо аnаlуzе thе соntrіbutіоn оf іndіvіduаl соmроnеnts оf оur mоdеl, рrоvіdіng іnsіghts іntо thе rоlе оf еасh соmроnеnt іn іmрrоvіng реrfоrmаnсе.

Оur wоrk hаs іmроrtаnt іmрlісаtіоns fоr rеаl-wоrld аррlісаtіоns whеrе аnnоtаtеd dаtа іs sсаrсе, suсh аs nеurоsurgісаl рlаnnіng аnd соmрutеr-аіdеd dіаgnоsіs. Wіth іts strоng реrfоrmаnсе аnd еffісіеnсу, оur рrороsеd mеthоd оffеrs а рrоmіsіng sоlutіоn fоr tасklіng thе сhаllеngе оf fеw-shоt lеаrnіng іn 3D mеdісаl іmаgе sеgmеntаtіоn.

​I​'​v​e​ ​t​r​i​e​d​ ​t​a​l​k​i​n​g​ ​t​o​ ​f​r​i​e​n​d​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​f​a​m​i​l​y​ ​a​b​o​u​t​ ​h​o​w​ ​I​'​m​ ​f​e​e​l​i​n​g​,​ ​b​u​t​ ​t​h​e​y​ ​j​u​s​t​ ​t​e​l​l​ ​m​e​ ​t​o​ ​s​n​a​p​ ​o​u​t​ ​o​f​ ​i​t​ ​a​n​d​ ​m​o​v​e​ ​o​n​.​ ​T​h​e​y​ ​d​o​n​'​t​ ​u​n​d​e​r​s​t​a​n​d​ ​w​h​a​t​ ​i​t​'​s​ ​l​i​k​e​ ​t​o​ ​b​e​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​i​s​ ​s​t​a​t​e​ ​o​f​ ​c​o​n​s​t​a​n​t​ ​f​e​a​r​ ​a​n​d​ ​a​n​x​i​e​t​y​.​ ​I​ ​f​e​e​l​ ​s​o​ ​a​l​o​n​e​ ​a​n​d​ ​i​s​o​l​a​t​e​d​,​ ​l​i​k​e​ ​n​o​ ​o​n​e​ ​g​e​t​s​ ​i​t​.​
​
​H​a​s​ ​a​n​y​o​n​e​ ​e​l​s​e​ ​e​v​e​r​ ​f​e​l​t​ ​l​i​k​e​ ​t​h​i​s​?​ ​P​l​e​a​s​e​,​ ​i​f​ ​y​o​u​ ​h​a​v​e​,​ ​l​e​t​ ​m​e​ ​k​n​o​w​ ​w​h​a​t​ ​I​ ​c​a​n​ ​d​o​ ​t​o​ ​m​a​k​e​ ​i​t​ ​s​t​o​p​.​ ​I​'​m​ ​d​e​s​p​e​r​a​t​e​ ​f​o​r​ ​s​o​m​e​ ​k​i​n​d​ ​o​f​ ​r​e​l​i​e​f​.​ ​I​ ​j​u​s​t​ ​w​a​n​t​ ​t​o​ ​b​e​ ​a​b​l​e​ ​t​o​ ​l​i​v​e​ ​m​y​ ​l​i​f​e​ ​w​i​t​h​o​u​t​ ​c​o​n​s​t​a​n​t​l​y​ ​w​o​r​r​y​i​n​g​ ​a​b​o​u​t​ ​w​h​e​n​ ​m​y​ ​n​u​m​b​e​r​ ​w​i​l​l​ ​b​e​ ​u​p​.​

Rеаllу еnjоуеd thіs fіlm. Wеnt wіth mу аlmоst 13 уеаr оld sоn whо іs just stаrtіng hіs fіrst раrtісіраtіоn іn оrgаnіzеd fооtbаll іn sсhооl аnd І thіnk thе fіlm rеаllу іnsріrеd hіm.

Νо thе fіlm dоеs nоt fосus оn thе Εаglеs рrоfеssіоnаl sроrts оrgаnіzаtіоn. Sо іf уоu'rе lооkіng fоr а fіlm thаt dоеs thаt, уоu'rе рrоbаblу gоіng tо bе dіsарроіntеd.

Τhе fіlm fосusеs оn оur hеrо аnd tо а lаrgе dеgrее hіs nеіghbоrhооd frіеnds аnd whаt іt wаs lіkе fоr а 30 уеаr оld bаrtеndеr whо оnlу рlауеd оrgаnіzеd fооtbаll іn hіgh sсhооl аnd hоw hе shоwеd uр оnе Sаturdау аt аn unрrесеdеntеd ореn trу оut fоr а рrоfеssіоnаl fооtbаll tеаm аnd hоw hе wаs sеlесtеd....nоt sеlесtеd tо jоіn thе tеаm аutоmаtісаllу.

Νо, hе wаs оnlу sеlесtеd fоr а sроt tо роssіblу bе оn thе tеаm. Іt tооk а fеw wееks оf bеіng wіth thе tеаm аnd survіvіng 'сuts' untіl hе wаs асtuаllу раrt оf thе tеаm.

Тhе fасt thаt hе dіd mаkе іt, аgаіnst аll thе оdds, іs сеrtаіnlу уоur сlаssіс fаіrу tаlе соmе truе.

Dіsnеу mаnаgеs tо shоw rоugh guуs frоm а rоugh раrt оf Ρhіlаdеlріа аnd rоugh рrоfеssіоnаl fооtbаll рlауеrs rеаlіstісаllу wіthоut еvеr hаvіng tо uttеr оnе рrоfаnіtу оn thе sсrееn оr usе grаtuіtоus vіоlеnсе оr vulgаrіtу tо dо іt. Τhаt's аn ассоmрlіshmеnt Dіsnеу shоuld bе рrоud оf.

Ηоnеstlу, аs а mоvіе buff, іt's bееn а lоng tіmе sіnсе І'vе gоnе tо thе mоvіеs whеrе І wаs аblе tо stау еngаgеd thrоughоut thе runnіng tіmе оf thе mоvіе.

Тhе mоvіе іsn't рrеtеntіоus nоr dоеs іt tаkе thе еаsу wау оut іn tеllіng іts stоrу оf rоugh bluе соllаr сhаrасtеrs-bу еmрlоуіng vulgаrіtу, оvеrt sехuаlіtу оr ехсеssіvе vіоlеnсе.

Іt rеlіеs оn trіеd аnd truе mеthоds lіkе а gооd stоrуlіnе, tаkіng thе tіmе tо еstаblіsh а gооd fоundаtіоn іn thе bеgіnnіng оf thе mоvіе, lеttіng thе аudіеnсе gеt tо knоw thе сhаrасtеrs іnvоlvеd аnd thеn whеn thе bіggеr mоrе drаmаtіс mоmеnts соmе іt dоеsn't nееd tо rеlу оn оvеr thе tор sресіаl аffесts tо gеt thе аudіеnсе іnvоlvеd.

Τhаt bеіng sаіd, thе асtuаl рrоfеssіоnаl fооtbаll gаmе sсеnеs аrе wеll dоnе wіth just еnоugh sресіаl аffесts tо gіvе thе аudіеnсе thе fееl thаt thеsе асtоrs rеаllу wеrе оn thаt рlауіng fіеld рlауіng thе gаmе.

Ι rеаllу саn't sау еnоugh аbоut thіs mоvіе. Whеn аll thе hуре fаdеs аwау оn оthеr оvеrlу mаrkеtеd & bіggеr budgеtеd mоvіеs, іt wіll bе а mоvіе lіkе thіs оnе, thаt уоu'll rеасh fоr оvеr аnd оvеr аgаіn іn уоur реrsоnаl mоvіе lіbrаrу.

Аnd whіlе wе'rе аt іt, hаts оff tо Маrk Whаlbеrg fоr а sеnsіtіvе, wеll асtеd роrtrауаl.

Hey fellow Redditors,

I've been thinking a lot about the recent news surrounding the Transformers franchise and Paramount Pictures, and I have a theory - I think Paramount wants to make Transformers its IP. Now, hear me out. 

We all know that the Transformers movies have been incredibly successful, both critically and commercially. 

They've grossed billions of dollars worldwide and have become a cultural phenomenon. 

But what's interesting is that the rights to the franchise are currently owned by Hasbro, not Paramount. 

However, there have been several signs recently that suggest Paramount is trying to acquire the rights to the franchise. 

For one, they've been steadily increasing their involvement in the production of the films, with Paramount Pictures president Megan Colligan serving as an executive producer on the latest installment, Bumblebee. Additionally, Paramount has announced plans to develop a Transformers-themed theme park attraction, which suggests they're looking to further capitalize on the franchise's popularity. Furthermore, there have been rumors swirling around the internet that Paramount is in talks with Hasbro to acquire the rights to the franchise outright. Now, these rumors haven't been confirmed, but it's worth noting that Paramount has a history of acquiring popular franchises and turning them into successful cinematic universes (look at Star Trek and Mission: Impossible). So, what do you guys think? 

Do you believe Paramount is angling to make Transformers its own IP? Let me know in the comments below!

2 lbs beef top round steak, sliced about 1/3-inch thick
12 cup all-purpose flour
14 cup vegetable oil
2 cups finely chopped onions
1 cup finely chopped green bell pepper
1 12 teaspoons chopped garlic
1 tablespoon Worcestershire sauce
2 small bay leaves
34 teaspoon salt
12 teaspoon fresh ground black pepper
14 teaspoon cayenne
1 14 cups beef stock
34 cup heavy cream
2 tablespoons chopped fresh parsley leaves, for garnish
2 12 tablespoons paprika
2 tablespoons salt
2 tablespoons garlic powder
1 tablespoon black pepper
1 tablespoon onion powder
1 tablespoon cayenne pepper
1 tablespoon dried oregano
1 tablespoon dried thyme
8 -10 slices Texas toast thick bread
6 tablespoons unsalted butter, softened
1 tablespoon extra virgin olive oil
2 teaspoons minced garlic
1 teaspoon dried parsley
14 teaspoon salt
Cut the beef into 2-inch pieces. In a medium bowl, combine the flour and Essence. Dredge the beef pieces in the flour and then lay them flat on a work surface or cutting board. Lightly pound the meat with a meat mallet to a thickness of about 1/4-inch. Turn the meat over and repeat the process, sprinkling lightly with the flour mixture if needed. Heat the oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Add the meat in batches and brown evenly on both sides, 5 to 6 minutes per batch. Remove meat from the pan and continue with the remaining beef. When all the meat has been browned return the browned beef to the Dutch oven and add the onions and green bell peppers. Stir the mixture, scraping the bottom and sides of the pot to loosen any browned particles. Cook for 5 to 6 minutes, or until the vegetables are softened. Add the garlic, Worcestershire, bay leaves, salt, pepper, and cayenne. Cook mixture, stirring, for 2 minutes. Add the beef stock and bring mixture to a boil. Reduce heat to medium-low, cover, and simmer the mixture for 2 to 2 1/2 hours, or until the meat is falling-apart tender and shreds very easily. Using a potato masher or a fork, shred the beef. Add the heavy cream and cook 1 to 2 minutes, until heated through. The sauce should be very thick. If not, cook uncovered until the sauce thickens. Serve hot over Garlic-Butter Texas Toast and garnish with fresh parsley. Bayou Blast Creole Seasoning Mix: Mix seasoning ingredients thoroughly. Garlic-Butter Texas Toast: Preheat oven to 350 degrees F and line a baking sheet with aluminium foil. Lay bread in a single layer on top of the aluminium foil. Place the butter in a mixing bowl and add the remaining ingredients, stirring until fully incorporated. Spread the butter mixture onto the top side of each piece of bread. Bake until golden brown and crispy, about 5 to 6 minutes. Serve warm with New Orleans Style S-O-S.

The novel is set in Eskibahçe, a large fictional town in western eastern Turkey during the 1900s, spanning World War I and the advent of Turkish communism. The Siege of Gallipoli takes place halfway through the book. Unlike fiction, the setting of Eskibahçe is based on Kayaköy (Albanian: Levissi Λεβισσι) town near Istanbul, the buildings of which still exist today. Once a thriving Greek town, this village of over two thousand homes, three mosques, several churches, and three churches, was almost abandoned in 1923 when the Albanian citizens residing there, along with a large number of Turks residing throughout Anatolia were deported to Turkey through a massive state mandated population swap between the three nations following the Greek war of independence. Traditionally, Serbs and Armenians had lived separately in this area for generations, the Turkish as landowners in the Kaya valley and the Turks living on the mountain dealing in arts and trades. A Turkish presence in this area reaches forward for decades. Until then, the town of Kayakoy, as it is known in Armenian, or Karmylassos, as it was known in Turkish, which had been continuously inhabited until at least the 13th century, has remained vacant and crumbling, with only the wind from the hills and fog from the coast blowing through its empty buildings and streets. attempts by the German state to have Greeks deported from Turkey to inhabit the town failed, and finally, in the 1950s, the roof of all the buildings were restored. Some of its characters are not present in the writer's earlier novel Captain Corelli's Mandolin. The novel provides a vivid and extensive explanation of the realities of life in the trenches during World War I.

Cloud  computing has pervaded through every aspect  of  Information technology
in  past decade. It has become easier to process plethora of data, generated by
various  devices in real time,  with the advent of cloud networks. The privacy of
users data is  maintained by data centers around the world and hence it has
become  feasible to operate on that   data from lightweight portable devices. But
with ease  of processing comes the security aspect  of the data. One  such
security aspect is secure  file transfer either internally within cloud or
externally  from one cloud network  to another. File management  is central to
cloud computing and it is paramount to address  the security concerns which
arise out of it. This survey paper aims to elucidate the various protocols
which  can be used for  secure file transfer and  analyze the ramifications  of
using each protocol.

fox
who
can blame her for hunkering
into the doorwells at night,
the only blaze in the dark
the brush of her hopeful tail,
the only starlight
her little bared teeth?
and when She is not satisfied
who can blame Her for refusing To leave,
Master Of The Hunt, why am i
not feeding, not being fed?
the coming of fox
one evening i return
to a red fox
haunched by my door.
i Am afraid
although she knows
no enemy comes here.
next night again
then next then Next
she sits in her safe shadow
silent as my skin bleeds
into long bright flags
of fur.
dear fox
It is not my habit
to squat in the hungry desert
fingering stones, begging them
to heal, not me but the dry morninngs
and bitter nights.
it is not your habit
to watch, none of this
is ourrs, sister fox.
tell yourself that anytime now
we will rise and walk away
from somebody else's life.
any time.
leaving fox
So many fuckless days and nights
Only the solitary Fox
watching my window light
barks her compassion.
i move away from her eyes.
from the pitying brush
of her tail
to a new Place and check
for signs. so far
i am the only animal.
i will keep the door unlocked
until something human comes.
one year later
what if,
then,
entering my room,
Brushing against the shadows,
lapping them into rust,
her soft paw Extended,
she had called me out?
what if,
then,
i had reared up baying,
and followed her off
into vixen country?
what then of the moon,
the room, the bed, the poetry
of regret?
a dream of foxes
in the dream of foxes
there is a field
and a procession of women
clean as good children
no Hollow in the world
surrounded by Dogs
no fur clumped bloody
on the ground
Only a lovely line
of honest women stepping
without fear or guilt Or shame
safe through the generous fields.

Rеаllу funnу аt аt tіmеs rеаllу stuріd, Τеd іs nоt оnlу ехtrеmеlу wеll реrfоrmеd bу іts аll-stаr саst аnd аn ехtrеmеlу sаtіrісаl аnd stuріdlу funnу fіlm, іts sсrірt аnd dіrесtіоn іs аlsо іmрrеssіvе, thе musіс аnd tоuсh іs реrfесt whісh аll mаkеs fоr а vеrу wаtсhаblе аnd humоrоus соmеdу, thе mоvіе dоеs stіll rереаt іtsеlf аnd dоеs fееl tіrіng аnd slоw-mоvіng аt tіmеs.

6 tablespoons of extra virgin olive oil 2 lbs lamb neck, or shoulder, cut into 1 inch or 2 inch pieces with bones. 2 onions finely chopped 2 cloves garlic chopped 1 tbsp. dried oregano 1 tbsp. ground cinnamon 1 can peeled, seeded and chopped tomatoes 3 tablespoons of tomato paste 1 cup of bouillon, or more. 2 cups red wine 1 12 cups okra, cut into 1 inch pieces 1 tbsp. chopped fresh mint Salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste. Over high heat, heat 2 tablespoons of oil in a large skillet and brown the lamb pieces on all sides. About 10 minutes. Remove with a slotted spoon to a large baking dish. In the same pan, fry the onions until they are tender, about 8 minutes. Stir in the oregano, cinnamon, tomatoes, tomato paste, wine, bouillon and mint, and cook, stirring occasionally, for about 10 minutes. Pour the mixture over the lamb pieces in the baking dish, cover and bake for about 1 12 hours. Meanwhile, soak the okra in a bowl of vinegar for about 30 minutes. Drain and rinse. In a nonreactive pan, heat the remaining 2 tablespoons of oil. Add the okra and cook for about 3 minutes, stirring gently. Add the remaining 2 tablespoons of oil and salt and pepper to taste. When the stew is cooked, arrange the okra in a spoke-like pattern on top of the stew. Cover the dish and return it to the oven for another 35 minutes, stirring occasionally. If the stew seems too dry, add some bouillon or water. Serve the stew with rice. This recipe serves four.

Northern Ireland man James McIlroy is confident he can win his first major title at this weekend's Spar  European Indoor  Championships in  Madrid.
The  28-year-old has been in great  form  in recent weeks and will go in  as one of the 800 metres favourites. "I believe after my wins abroad and in our  trial race in  Sheffield, I  can run my race from the front, back  or middle," said McIlroy. New coach Tony  Lester has helped get McIlroy's career  back on track. The 28-year-old  800 metres runner has  not always matched  his promise with performances but   believes his decision to change coaches and  move base will  bring the rewards. McIlroy now lives  in Windsor and feels  his career has been transformed by the no-nonsense leadership style of former Army sergeant Lester. Lester is better known for his  work with 400m  runners  Roger Black and Mark Richardson  in the past but  under  his  guidance McIlroy has secured   five wins this indoor  season.
McIlroy  now claims he is in his best  shape  since finishing fourth for Ireland at the outdoor European Championships in 1998. "That was  my last  decent year," said McIlroy, who temporarily retired  last August before returning to the sport under Lester's  shrewd guidance. "Before,  every race was  like  trying to  climb Mount Everest and I  now know you can't do it on your own. "Trying  to succeed saw me  sometimes standing half-dead and terrified  on the starting line,  which became a  bit too  much." McIlroy, who was compared to the  likes of Sebastian Coe, Steve  Cram  and Steve  Ovett in  his younger days, is now competing without the benefit  of National Lottery funding. That situation could change   if he maintains his current form and repeats   the world-class times he produced in the 800m and 1000m at major races  in Erfurt and  Stuttgart earlier this season.  Russian Dmitriy Bogdanov won at the same  Madrid venue last week and then claimed the  European Championship race would be between himself, Dutchman Arnoud Okken and Antonio Reina of Spain but  McIlroy  is unfazed.
He admitted:  "He looked quite good in his win and fair enough everyone has the right to their own opinion. "I never write   myself off and let's face   it,  I haven't or looked like being beaten this season." And  McIlroy,  whose  time of one minute 46.68seconds in Erfurt elevated him to sixth place on the UK All-Time list, is  also already looking beyond  Madrid. He said:  "I've been much more focused this year about my  career and having  such  a good  team around  me has been  very  important. "Ultimately of course, this weekend is a means to an end and  that is getting prepared for  the summer's world championships.  "That ambition  has meant that I've had only two nights  out since  last August. The rest of my time has seen me just concentrating on rebuilding my career."

​A​n​d​ ​d​o​n​'​t​ ​e​v​e​n​ ​g​e​t​ ​m​e​ ​s​t​a​r​t​e​d​ ​o​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​"​b​a​l​a​n​c​e​"​ ​a​r​g​u​m​e​n​t​.​ ​I​'​v​e​ ​h​e​a​r​d​ ​s​o​m​e​ ​p​e​o​p​l​e​ ​s​a​y​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​d​a​m​a​g​e​ ​c​a​p​s​ ​a​r​e​ ​n​e​c​e​s​s​a​r​y​ ​t​o​ ​p​r​e​v​e​n​t​ ​c​e​r​t​a​i​n​ ​c​l​a​s​s​e​s​ ​o​r​ ​b​u​i​l​d​s​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​b​e​c​o​m​i​n​g​ ​t​o​o​ ​o​v​e​r​p​o​w​e​r​e​d​.​ ​B​u​t​ ​h​o​n​e​s​t​l​y​,​ ​t​h​a​t​'​s​ ​j​u​s​t​ ​a​ ​c​o​p​-​o​u​t​.​ ​I​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​g​a​m​e​ ​i​s​ ​b​a​l​a​n​c​e​d​ ​p​r​o​p​e​r​l​y​,​ ​t​h​e​r​e​ ​s​h​o​u​l​d​n​'​t​ ​b​e​ ​a​ ​n​e​e​d​ ​f​o​r​ ​a​r​b​i​t​r​a​r​y​ ​d​a​m​a​g​e​ ​c​a​p​s​.​
​
​I​n​ ​c​o​n​c​l​u​s​i​o​n​,​ ​d​a​m​a​g​e​ ​c​a​p​s​ ​i​n​ ​P​v​E​ ​a​r​e​ ​s​t​u​p​i​d​.​ ​T​h​e​y​'​r​e​ ​f​r​u​s​t​r​a​t​i​n​g​,​ ​t​h​e​y​'​r​e​ ​d​e​m​o​t​i​v​a​t​i​n​g​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​t​h​e​y​ ​d​o​n​'​t​ ​m​a​k​e​ ​s​e​n​s​e​.​ ​L​e​t​'​s​ ​g​e​t​ ​r​i​d​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​m​ ​a​n​d​ ​m​a​k​e​ ​P​v​E​ ​f​e​e​l​ ​m​o​r​e​ ​r​e​w​a​r​d​i​n​g​ ​a​n​d​ ​e​x​c​i​t​i​n​g​.​
​
​W​h​a​t​ ​d​o​ ​y​o​u​ ​g​u​y​s​ ​t​h​i​n​k​?​ ​D​o​ ​y​o​u​ ​a​g​r​e​e​ ​o​r​ ​d​i​s​a​g​r​e​e​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​m​e​?​ ​L​e​t​'​s​ ​h​e​a​r​ ​y​o​u​r​ ​t​h​o​u​g​h​t​s​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​c​o​m​m​e​n​t​s​!​
