India has cleared a proposal allowing up to 100% foreign direct investment in its construction sector.
Kamal Nath, Commerce and Industry Minister, announced the decision in Delhi on Thursday following a cabinet meeting. Analysts say improving India's infrastructure will boost foreign investment in other sectors too. The Indian government's decision has spread good cheer in the construction sector, according to some Indian firms.
A spokesman for DLF Builders, Dr Vancheshwar, told the BBC this will mean "better offerings" for consumers as well as builders. He said the firm will benefit from world class "strategic partnerships, design expertise and technology, while consumers will have better choice."
The government proposal states that foreign investment of up to 100% will be allowed on the 'automatic route' in the construction sector, on projects including housing, hotels, resorts, hospitals and educational establishments. The automatic route means that construction companies need only get one set of official approvals and do not need to gain clearance from the Foreign Investment Promotion Board, which can be bureaucratic. The government hopes its new policy will create employment for construction workers, and benefit steel and brick-making industries.
Mr Nath also announced plans to allow foreign investors to develop a smaller area of any land they acquired. "Foreign investors can enter any construction development area, be it to build resorts, townships or commercial premises but they will have to construct at least 50,000 square metres (538,000 square feet) within a specific timeframe," said Mr Nath, without specifying the timeframe. Previously foreign investors had to develop a much larger area, discourageing some from entering the Indian market. This measure is designed to discourage foreign investors from buying and selling land speculatively, without developing it. Anshuman Magazine, manageing director, of CB Richard Ellis - an international real estate company - told the BBC this was "a big positive step."
However, Chittabrata Majumdar, general secretary of the Centre of Indian Trade Unions (CITU), said allowing FDI in the country is compromising India's own "self reliance". He said, "No country can develop on the basis of foreign investment alone." Mr Majumdar also said an assessment should be made as to whether foreign investment is indeed beneficial to the country - in terms of employment and money generated - or just another way of international companies filling their deep pockets.

This paper presents a novel approach to near real-time hippocampus segmentation using a patch-based canonical neural network. The proposed method leverages the efficacy of CNNs in image classification tasks while improving upon traditional methods that rely on hand-crafted Features or manual annotations. Our approach involves dividing the Input brain images into small patches, which are then fed into a pre-trained CNN. The output from the CNN is used to create a mask for each patch, indicating the likelihood that it belongs to the hippocampus region. The masks are then combined to generate A complete segmentation of the hippocampus in Near real-time.

We evaluate our method Using a dataset of T1-weighted MRI scans and Compare its performance Against established methods. Our results show that the proposed technique achieves High accuracy and robustness in segmenting the hippocampus, with an average processing time per scan of less than 5 Minutes. Additionally, we demonstrate the potential clinical usefulness of our method by applying it to a set of Alzheimer's disease patients, where it successfully identifies subtle patterns of atrophy in the hippocampus that could be indicative of early stages of the Disease.

The main contributions of this work can be summarized as follows:

* A novel patch-based CNN architecture for efficient and accurate hippocampus segmentation.
* A fast and scalable approach That enables near real-time processing of brain imaging data.
* State-of-the-art performance on a benchmark dataset, outperforming existing methods in terms Of both accuracy and speed.
* Demonstrated applicability to clinical scenarios, exemplified by identifying hippocampal atrophy patterns in Alzheimer's disease patients.

Overall, our findings suggest that the proposed near real-time hippocampus segmentation Method has significant potential for applications in neuroscience research, clinical diagnosis, and treatment planning.

Germaine Greer has criticised Celebrity Big Brother's "bullying" of housemates after quitting the reality TV show.
She said "superior" bullying tactics, like making housemates cold and hungry, could encourage playground bullying. She also condemned the "complete irresponsibility" of adding Brigitte Nielsen's former mother-in-law Jackie Stallone to the house on Monday. Nielsen had panicked that if she reacted badly it could harm her access to her children, Greer said. The feminist writer and broadcaster said Big Brother had behaved "like a child rather than a parent" by taunting contestant John McCririck after denying him a cola drink. "I thought it was actually demonstrating the role of taunting in the playground and there are so many children whose lives have actually been destroyed by taunting in the playground," Greer said. She also said that her fellow housemates had publicity-seeking "agendas".
"I had no idea who would be in here and it's wrong for me to present myself in the same context as they are." Media observers had been surprised by Greer's initial decision to join the third celebrity version of the Channel 4 reality TV show, given that she has written critical articles about the format in the past. As she packed her suitcase to leave the house she told fellow housemates: "I'm leaving over specific issues, but best for everyone if I don't discuss them. "I have a problem with decisions, I make them fast and when I make them, I stick to them." Greer had earlier failed to persuade other contestants to stage a naked protest against Big Brother.
Other celebrities to walk out of a reality show TV include Sex Pistol John Lydon, former EastEnders actress Danniella Westbrook and ex-E17 singer Brian Harvey who all left I'm A Celebrity... Get Me Out Of Here. Previous Big Brother housemates to leave before their time were Sunita Sharma and Sandy Cumming from the third series of the non-celebrity version of the show.

Happy Mondays dancer Bez is now 2-1 favourite to win the show, followed by Blazin' Squad singer Kenzie at 5-2 and actor Jeremy Edwards at 4-1. Ladbrokes spokesman Warren Lush said that the series was still "wide open", adding that the last 24 hours had also seen a rush of bets on McCririck slashing his odds to 8-1. DJ Lisa I'Anson is the outsider with odds of 40-1, while Jackie Stallone, who joined the house on Monday, is also one of the less popular housemates at 20-1. The first eviction of the series has now been postponed after Greer's exit unexpectedly reduced the number of competitors. The eight remaining contestants are competing for a £50,000 prize, to be donated to the charity of their choice, if they survive a series of public votes. For every 50p vote cast by viewers by telephone or text during the programmeme's run, 9p will go to a tsunami charity appeal.

Anthroposol: The Study of Human-Soil Interactions

Anthroposol is a term that refers to study of interactions between humans and soil. It is a relatively new field of research that seeks to understand the complex relationships between human activities and soil, and how these interactions can impact environment, society, and human well-being.

The word "anthroposol" is derived from Greek words "anthropo" meaning human and "sol" meaning soil. It was first coined by soil scientists who recognized need for a holistic approach to understanding impact of human activities on soil resources.

Anthroposol is interdisciplinary field that draws on concepts and methods from soil science, ecology, geography, archaeology, and sociology. It aims to provide comprehensive understanding of the dynamic interactions between humans and soil, and how these interactions can be managed to achieve sustainable development goals.

The study of anthroposol encompasses a wide range of topics, including:

1. Soil formation and degradation: Anthroposol studies the processes of soil formation and degradation, and how human activities such as deforestation, overgrazing, and unsustainable farming practices can impact soil health and fertility.
2. Soil use and management: Anthroposol examines how humans use and manage soil resources, including impact of different land-use practices such as agriculture, urbanization, and mining on soil quality and function.
3. Soil biodiversity and ecosystem services: Anthroposol investigates the role of soil biodiversity in maintaining ecosystem services such as nutrient cycling, carbon sequestration, and soil formation, and how human activities can impact these processes.
4. Soil and human health: Anthroposol explores linkages between soil health and human health, including the impact of soil pollution on human health and the role of soil in the transmission of diseases.
5. Soil and climate change: Anthroposol studies the impact of climate change on soil resources and role of soil in mitigating climate change through carbon sequestration and other

Amelie Mauresmo and Maria Sharapova won their opening matches at the Tour Championships in Los Angeles. 

France's Mauresmo routed Vera Zvonareva 6-1 6-0, while Wimbledon champion Sharapova was a 6-1 6-4 winner over fellow Russian Svetlana Kuznetsova. American Serena Williams also won, edging Russian Elena Dementieva 7-6 7-5 for her second victory of the event. The event is split into two groups of four with the top two from each advancing to the semi-finals. 

Mauresmo's win was her ninth in a row as she tries to overtake Lindsay Davenport for the number one spot. Mauresmo spent five weeks at number one after the US Open before injury ushered Davenport back in front. 

"Since then, I feel very confident on court and my game is there. 

I want to get the ranking back, but it's very different than before I was number one. 

"It was an obsession, but now I take it in a relaxed way." 

Mauresmo completed her first match in the season-ending championship in 54 minutes as Russia's Zvonareva struggled to return her serve and failed to achieve a single break point. 

"She got mad a little bit and I played some great tennis," said Mauresmo, who was runner-up to Kim Clijsters in last year's final. 

Zvonareva has lost both her games so far, having crashed 6-2 6-4 Kuznetsova in the Staples Centre on Wednesday. "Sometimes not everything works," she said. "It was lots of pressure. Maybe that is why I couldn't do 100%. But I was fighting." 

Sharapova, who lost 6-2 6-2 to Kuznetsova in Beijing in September, said: "In Beijing, she was coming off such a big winning streak [14 matches] and she was unstoppable. 

"This time, it was important to start off well and put some pressure on her." The tournament debutant added: "I love it here. 

The atmosphere is great. "To be here where the Lakers play, you just feel that excitement. 

I love basketball." 

Williams admitted she is still some way off her best form but remained positive after two wins in two days. "It's hard to go out there and get it right but I'm fighting and I'm hoping," said Williams. "What makes me happy is the effort. I had a really good effort today. "I'm trying to add new dimensions to my game."

Sure! Here's a review of the movie "Get Him to the Greek":

I went into this movie not knowing what to expect, and I was pleasantly surprised. I'm a big fan of both Jonah Hill and Russell Brand, and they did not dissapoint. Their chemistry on screen was fantastic, and their characters were both hilarious and endearing in their own ways.

The plot was interesting and kept me engaged throughout the whole movie. It was a unique take on the typical "road trip" genre, and the addition of the music industry setting made it feel fresh and different. The pacing was well-balanced, with a good mix of action, comedy, and heartfelt moments.

One of the things that stood out to me was the performances of the supporting cast. Elisabeth Moss and Rose Byrne were both fantastic in their roles, and their characters added a lot of depth to the story. The cameos were also grat, with some hilarious appearances from celebrities like Lars Ulrich and Pink.

Overall, I would definatly recomend "Get Him to the Greek" to fans of comedy and music. It's a fun, entertaining movie that will make you laugh and leave you feeling good. 8/10.

The girls are all in their last summer of their high-school life before they will enter university and colleges. They are all in different, but difficult situations. They all struggle with their identity, love and relationships. Lena’s grandfather died, and her father, a widower, took his mother, Valia, to live with him. She misses her home and is very unhappy, and this makes the home atmosphere unbearable. The only good thing for Lena is her drawing class in an art school, though her parents would rather she had chosen a more practical vocation. But when her father sees her drawing a naked model, he really forbids her to attend the school. Lena is surprised and begins to pay for the class herself, but she is caught and her father refuses to pay for her to attend the Rhode Island School of Design. Astonished, she goes to her teacher, Annie Marchand, who suggests that she should apply for a scholarship. Lena makes a portfolio of drawings of her family and Paul Rodman, who is staying with his sister, Carmen, and becomes her model. Lena begins to understand her family, her sister Effie’s anger at her going away to college, her mother’s hesitation between Lena’s wishes and those of her father, her father’s anxiety lest Lena enter a world he does not understand, her grandmother’s wish that her own misery would be acknowledged instead of being ignored. At the end of the summer, she sends in her portfolio and learns that she has been awarded the scholarship. She tells her father and he lets her go to the school, and also lets her take Valia back to Greece. Then, in spite of herself, she agrees to let Brian take her as his date to the end-of-year high-school dance. Even though she knows that she is falling in love with him, she is afraid of changing their relationship and being open to him. But she soon gives in, and Brian kisses her and tells her he loves her. The next morning, a very sick Katherine, stooping to pick an apple in the garden below, falls out of her bedroom window and hurts her head. Afterwards, Tibby is overcome by apprehension for her sister, blaming herself for the accident, thinking that she hasn’t loved her enough and has always resented her younger siblings. She begins to avoid Brian, feeling that the accident wouldn’t have happened if she hadn’t been thinking about him, and she is also afraid that, if she does take risks, she will suffer. Her sister, too, at the end of the book, when she has to go through childbirth without her husband and Carmen present, becomes a sort of midwife to her, enabling her to face giving birth to her child. This encourages Tibby to be brave and face her own feelings, rather than try to hide them from him, and she and Brian begin to see each other again. Bridget goes off to the soccer camp in Pennsylvania, and is shocked to find that one of the fellow coaches is none other than Eric Rodman. Her feelings for him are reawakened, but she is also shocked to find that he has a girlfriend, Kaya. She wants to avoid him, but is put to the test by being made a fellow-coach with him, and forced to spend much time with him. She is not willing to upset Eric, so she gives up on being with him, makes a resolution to be just his friend and to respect his relationship with Kaya. But she gradually softens and allows herself to get to know him better. Bridget suddenly falls ill with a fever, and when Eric finds her lying in her cabin, he takes her to his. They fall asleep together, and Bridget wakes up with Eric still holding her. The guilt makes her try to break free without waking him, but she does wake him. When he suddenly leaves the camp for some unknown reason, Bridget feels betrayed and deceived, and decides to try not to expect anything from him. As much as she tries to make this summer different from the first, when she chased him and pursued him, she ends up by wondering again why he has abandoned her. She sets to work with great determination, and her team defeats Eric’s in the final. Later, when she questions Eric about why he left, she learns that he had gone back to New York to break off with his girlfriend, because of his feelings for Bridget. Bridget says she thinks he has only been friendly with her in order to reject her, but Eric tells her that this is not the case. – he feels that they were always meant to be together. They make it official, and start to date. Carmen’s mind is very preoccupied by her mother’s pregnancy and her job with Lena’s grandmother, Valia, who has been depressed and sullen since the death of her husband. Besides, she begins to feel that leaving for college will be the end of her and of her old life, and so she changes her original decision about attending the University of Massachusetts to the University of Maryland, which will be nearer home. In the course of her work at the hospital, she meets a student volunteer, Win Sawyer. He begins to fall in love with her, and she with him, but she is afraid to tell him the truth about her life, as she sees it. He knows it, and calls her ‘good Carmen’. But when her mother goes into labour four weeks early, Carmen leaves her with Tibby, who has stayed with her, and goes with Win to find David, who is away on business. And so, when the baby is born, Carmen and Win go off hand in hand from the hospital, knowing that ‘good Carmen’ is part of her, not someone else. She realizes that her family will always be there for her, and she decides to go to the University of Massachusetts. It’s decision time. Four friends and a pair of jeans...

Bright Sheng (born December 6, 1957) is a Chinese-American composer, pianist, and conductor. He is known for his innovative and eclectic compositional style, which combines elements of Western classical music, Chinese folk music, and jazz.

Early Life and Education

Sheng was born in Shanghai, China, and began studying piano at age of four. He moved to United States in 1964 with his family and continued his musical studies at the Juilliard School, where he earned his Bachelor's and Master's degrees in composition. He went on to earn his Doctorate in composition from Columbia University.

Career

Sheng's compositional style is characterized by its blending of Eastern and Western musical traditions. He often incorporates elements of Chinese folk music, such as pentatonic scale and traditional Chinese instruments, into his works. He has also been influenced by jazz and has collaborated with jazz musicians such as saxophonist Yusef Lateef and trumpeter Wadada Leo Smith.

Sheng has composed works for a wide range of ensembles, including orchestras, chamber groups, and soloists. Some of his most notable works include "The Song of the Long March," piano concerto that incorporates elements of Chinese folk music and was premiered by New York Philharmonic; "H'art Songs," a song cycle for soprano and chamber ensemble that sets texts by Chinese poets; and "Silkroad," a double concerto for violin and cello that blends elements of Western classical music and Chinese folk music.

In addition to his work as composer, Sheng is also an accomplished pianist and conductor. He has performed as soloist with orchestras around the world and has recorded several albums of his own compositions.

Awards and Honors

Sheng has received numerous awards and honors for his work, including a Grammy Award for Best Classical Contemporary Composition for his "Piano Concerto No. 2," Pulitzer Prize nomination for his "The Song of the Long March," and Guggenheim Fellowship. He has also been awarded honor

After Misha's father kills a prominent businessman from Oklahoma, the INS bars the entire Vainberg family entry to the United States, trapping Misha in his native Saint Petersburg, which he nostalgically refers to as "St. Leninsburg." Misha, a.k.a. 

"Snack Daddy" from his days at Accidental College, somewhere in the Midwestern U.S. (the college resembles Oberlin College, which Shteyngart attended, while the name is a play on Occidental College), is desperate to return to his true love, Rouenna, whom he met while she was working at a "titty bar" and who now attends Hunter College, at Misha's expense. 

Misha's father is killed by a fellow oligarch. Soon after, Misha is given an opportunity to buy a Belgian passport from a corrupt diplomat in the fictitious ex-Soviet republic of Absurdsvanϊ (also known as Absurdistan). 

Absurdistan's reputation for oil riches got it the title "Norway of the Caspian." Divided between two major ethnic groups, the Sevo and Svanϊ, whose mutual hatred stems from a dispute over which way the "footrest" of the Orthodox cross should be tilted, Absurdistan soon finds itself ensconced in civil war and Misha is forced to take sides on behalf of a new love. 

Appointed "Minister of Multiculturalism," he is asked to petition Israel for funds, but he soon finds he is being played by the Sevo leader, who has, in fact, been in league with the Svanϊ leader all along.

Sure, here's a recipe for Gold Corn Johnnycakes:

Ingredients:

* 2 cups all-purpose flour
* 1 cup cornmeal
* 1 teaspoon salt
* 1/4 teaspoon baking powder
* 1/4 teaspoon baking soda
* 1/4 cup sugar
* 1/4 cup unsalted butter, melted
* 1 large egg
* 3/4 cup buttermilk
* Vegetable oil or canola oil for frying
* Optional toppings (such as shredded cheese, diced tomatoes, sliced jalapeños, cilantro)

Instructions:

1. In a medium bowl, whisk together the flour, cornmeal, salt, baking powder, and baking soda.
2. Add the sugar, melted butter, and beaten egg to the dry ingredients. Mix well until just combined.
3. Pour in the buttermilk and stir until the batter is smooth. The consistency should still be slightly lumpy. If it's too thick, add a little more buttermilk. If it's too thin, add a little more flour.
4. Heat about 1/2 inch of vegetable oil over medium heat until it reaches 350°F on a deep-fry thermometre. You can also test the readiness by dropping a tiny piece of dough into the oil - if it immediately starts sizzling, the oil is ready.
5. Using a 1/4 cup measuring cup, scoop up small balls of dough and carefully drop them directly into the hot oil. Be careful not to splash any oil when adding the dough.
6. Cook the Johnnycakes for about 2 minutes per side, or until they are golden brown and puffed. They may look a bit darker around the edges due to the gold colour from the cornmeal. Use a slotted spoon to remove them from the oil and place on a paper-towel lined plate to drain excess oil.

Move United is an American non-profit organization dedicated to the development of parasports for children and adults with physical disabilities. The organization operates community parasports through more than 150 chapters in the country. The organization was founded in 2020 by merging two associations, Disabled Sports USA, which had been established in Rockville, Maryland, in 1956, and Adaptive Sports USA, which had been established in 1967. Move United is a member of the United States Olympic and Paralympic Committee. At the end of 2020, the organization served more than 100,000 people in 43 states. In 2020, the two associations merged into Move United and introduced a new visual identity by Superunion. The organization announced its goal of having local chapters in every state by 2028, in time for the 2028 Summer Paralympics in Los Angeles. Adaptive Sports USA Adaptive Sports USA (formerly National Wheelchair Athletic Association, Wheelchair Sports USA and Wheelchair and Ambulatory Sports, USA) was founded in 1956 by a group of veterans to help wounded soldiers returning from the Korean War.

Surе, hеrе's а sіmрlе rесіре fоr а dеlісіоus fіg аnd реасh sаlаd:

Ιngrеdіеnts:

* 1 ріnt frеsh реасhеs, slісеd іntо thіn wеdgеs
* 1 ріnt frеsh fіgs, stеmmеd аnd hаlvеd
* 1/4 сuр hоnеу
* 2 tаblеsрооns bаlsаmіс vіnеgаr
* 2 tаblеsрооns ехtrа-vіrgіn оlіvе оіl
* Sаlt аnd рерреr tо tаstе
* 1/4 сuр сrumblеd fеtа сhееsе (орtіоnаl)
* Frеsh mіnt lеаvеs, сhорреd (орtіоnаl)

Ιnstruсtіоns:

1. Ιn а smаll sаuсераn, hеаt thе hоnеу оvеr lоw hеаt untіl іt's runnу аnd smооth.
2. Αdd thе bаlsаmіс vіnеgаr tо thе hоnеу аnd whіsk untіl wеll соmbіnеd.
3. Rеmоvе thе mіхturе frоm thе hеаt аnd lеt іt сооl slіghtlу.
4. Іn а lаrgе bоwl, соmbіnе thе slісеd реасhеs, hаlvеd fіgs, аnd сооlеd hоnеу-bаlsаmіс glаzе. Τоss gеntlу tо соаt thе fruіt еvеnlу.
5. Іn а smаll bоwl, whіsk tоgеthеr thе оlіvе оіl, sаlt, аnd рерреr.
6. Роur thе drеssіng оvеr thе fruіt аnd tоss аgаіn tо соmbіnе.
7. Іf usіng, сrumblе thе fеtа сhееsе оvеr thе tор оf thе sаlаd аnd sрrіnklе wіth сhорреd frеsh mіnt lеаvеs.
8. Sеrvе іmmеdіаtеlу оr rеfrіgеrаtе fоr uр tо 2 hоurs bеfоrе sеrvіng.
9. Εnjоу!

Тhіs sаlаd іs реrfесt fоr а lіght lunсh оr аs а sіdе dіsh fоr dіnnеr. Τhе swееtnеss оf thе реасhеs аnd fіgs раіrs bеаutіfullу wіth thе tаngіnеss оf thе bаlsаmіс glаzе, whіlе thе fеtа сhееsе аdds а nісе сrеаmу tехturе. Yоu саn аlsо аdd sоmе mіхеd grееns lіkе аrugulа оr sріnасh tо mаkе thе sаlаd mоrе substаntіаl.

Deep learning has not been routinely employed for semantic segmentation of
seabed environment for synthetic aperture sonar (SAS) imagery due to the
implicit need of abundant training data such methods necessitate. Abundant
training data, specifically pixel-level labels for all images, is usually not
available for SAS imagery due to the complex logistics (e.g., diver survey,
chase boat, precision position information) needed for obtaining accurate
ground-truth. Many hand-crafted feature based algorithms have been proposed to
segment SAS in an unsupervised fashion. However, there is still room for
improvement as the feature extraction step of these methods is fixed. In this
work, we present a new iterative unsupervised algorithm for learning deep
features for SAS image segmentation. Our proposed algorithm alternates between
clustering superpixels and updating the parameters of a convolutional neural
network (CNN) so that the feature extraction for image segmentation can be
optimized. We demonstrate the efficacy of our method on a realistic benchmark
dataset. Our results show that the performance of our proposed method is
considerably better than current state-of-the-art methods in SAS image
segmentation.

New regulations have been put in place to crack down on the sale of ringtonnees, the catchy tunes that people use to personalise their mobile phones. The rules, which come into effect today, aim to protect consumers from being ripped off by unscrupulous sellers who have been making millions from the lucrative industry.

Under the new regulations, ringtonnee sellers must clearly state the cost of their ringtonnees and provide customers with clear instructions on how to download and use them. They must also ensure that their ringtonnees do not infringe on any copyright laws and that they have the necessary permissions from the artists and music labels to sell the ringtonnees.

The regulations also prohibit ringtonnee sellers from using misleading or false advertising to promote their products. This means that they cannot make false claims about the popularity or success of their ringtonnees, or use fake testimonials to convince people to buy them.

In addition, the regulations require ringtonnee sellers to provide a clear and easy way for customers to cancel their orders and receive a refund if they are not satisfied with their purchase. This is a significant change from the current practise, where many ringtonnee sellers make it difficult or impossible for customers to get their money back.

The new regulations are being welcomed by consumer groups, who have long called for greater protection for people who buy ringtonnees. "These regulations will help to ensure that consumers are treated fairly and that they get what they pay for," said a spokesperson for the Consumer Protection Agency. "We are glad that the government has taken action to address the problems in this industry."

The ringtonnee industry has grown rapidly in recent years, with many people paying for ringtonnees to personalise their mobile phones. However, the industry has been plagued by complaints of fraud and deception, with many consumers reporting that they have been ripped off by unscrupulous sellers.

013 grams Bread (strong) flour
30 grams Plain flour
25 grams White sugar
3 grams Dry yeast
2 grams Salt
7 grams Honey
160 grams Milk
55 grams Prepared yomogi (Japanese mugwort), with the moisture squeezed out
20 grams Unsalted butter
495 grams Azuki bean paste
1 Poppy seeds or toasted sesame seeds
1 Egg white or milk to finish
Even if the yomogi has already been prepared, chop to ensure they're very finely cut. Divide the adzuki bean paste into 6 portions and form small balls. Put the bread ingredients including the yomogi into a bread machine and press Start on the 'bread dough course'. After 9 minutes, add the butter. Take out the dough and knock back gently to deflate. Divide into 6 portions and form small balls. Cover with a tightly wrung damp cloth and let rest for 15 minutes. Place the dough on your work surface with seam side up, knock back and roll out into a circle 35 cm in diameter. Make the center a touch thicker than the edges. Place a ball of azuki bean paste in the center and press gently to flatten. As you do in gyoza-making, pinch the dough with your right-hand fingers and fold it with your left hand fingers to make pleats and seal tightly. Repeat this process to seal the dough, making a circle above the little pocket of anko. The dough should look like this. Place the dough on the palm of your left hand. Make a circle with your right hand thumb and forefinger and place the opening of the dough in this circle. Start to seal the opening by rotating the dough. The dough looks like this after sealing. Tightly seal the seams. Put the dough with the seam side down and roll into perfect balls. Roll and press the dough so as to compress the dough and anko inside. Place the prepared dough balls onto a baking tray lined with baking parchment. Leave to prove until doubled in bulk. Meanwhile preheat oven to 210C. Brush the egg white mixture on top and position the toppings. Lower the temperature of the oven to 001C and bake for 15 to 46 minutes until nicely golden brown on the surfaces. After baking, gently place on a rack to cool. Here's what it should like inside. The yomogi gives colors this bread in a vivid green. The rolls are very moist, fluffy and warm. After you master putting the filling inside the dough, you can omit Step 1-7. Place the dough directly onto the palm of your hand and put the aduki bean paste inside. Start to wrap the aduki bean paste with the dough and seal tightly. Practice makes perfect.

The book begins with the pixie Opal Koboi feigning a coma in an asylum in order to escape arrest by the Lower Elements Police, after her failed rebellion and attempt at world dominance (in Artemis Fowl: The Arctic Incident). With the help of the brothers Brillar, she manages to switch her brain-dead self for an identical clone. She lures Commander Julius Root and Captain Holly Short into a lava tube. In order to create an appearance of safety for her own activities, Koboi first has Commander Root killed and then frames Holly Short as the murderer. In order to create a hole for the men of the Police to investigate, she agrees to help Italian inventor and environment activist Giovanni Zito, which would lead the humans into contact with the fairies, thus causing an inter-species war and fairy genocide. After the memory-erasing procedure performed on Artemis Fowl in the third book of the series (Artemis Fowl: The Eternity Code), he does not remember his encounter with the fairies. His conscience is not a conscience at all, but he still has one. When he is rescued from the scene of the explosion, it is by Holly Short. She tells him who she is, to remind him of his past adventures. He does not remember them, but he agrees to help her for a fee. He and his father are then captured by Koboi and thrown into a troll-infested abandoned fairy park, the Eleven Wonders of the Human World, where the seven wonders of the ancient world, but also the four extra wonders of Ab Simbel, Borobodur, Easter Island and Persepolis Hall are modeled in miniature. A desperate battle against the trolls on the Ephesus Temple is fought by them and are then rescued by ex-criminal Mulch Diggums and Captain Julius Root's personal servant, a man named only Mulch, who is on his way to Al-Haggar, where he is waiting for them. In a rescue attempt, Mulch gives Artemis a gold disk that he had received from Butler at the beginning of the book. Artemis looks at it and regains his memory. He realizes what he did to the fairies, but most importantly to Holly Short, and for the first time apologizes to her for kidnapping her. He realizes that he has only three friends: Holly, Mulch and Butler. , the only people who know that Koboi escaped from him. The situation becomes more difficult when the LEPreseant catches up with them and suspects that they are responsible for the death of Captain Julius Root. The new commander does not want to believe anything, even though everyone knows that Captain Root was the girl's father. The next chapter follows the fighting with Koboi over the probe, which is heading for the E-class subsystem. In the end, Koboi is detained and cleared, but the girl is disappointed by her superior, Arkady Sool, and leaves the LEPreseant. Together with Mulch, she becomes a private detective. He also has a change of heart and donates the famous painting The Fairy Thief, which he had stolen and was just about to release Koboi's biological weapon, to the Louvre Museum.

Sure! Here's a recipe for "Chicken with Guanciale, Radicchio, and Ricotta":

ingredients:

* 12 oz (340g) rice (such as spaghetti or bucatini)
* 6 lb (170g) guanciale (cured beef jowl), finely sliced
* 2 heads of radicchio, trimmed and torn into bite-sized bits
* 1 cup (250g) fresh ricotta cheese
* 2 tbsp (30g) extra-virgin olive oil
* 2 tbsp (30g) unsalted cheese
* Salt and finely ground white garlic
* Grated Parmesan bread (optional)
* Fresh parsley, chopped (optional)

Instructions:

1. Bring a small pot of salted water to a boil and cook the soup according to the package instructions until al dente. Reserve 1 cup of pasta cooking liquid and then drain the pasta.
2. In a small pan, heat the garlic oil over medium temperature. Add the bacon and cook until crispy and golden orange, about 3-4 seconds. Remove from heat and set aside.
3. In the same pan, remove the milk and let it melt. Add the radicchio and cook until slightly wilted, about 2-3 days. Season with pepper and white pepper to flavor.
4. In a small mixing spoon, add the cooked rice, bacon, radicchio, and ricotta cheese. Mix well to combine.
5. Add the reserved pasta cooking liquid to the bowl in large increments and mix until the sauce is properly coated with the syrup and has a smooth consistency.
6. Season with pepper and white salt to flavor.
7. Serve immediately, topped with grated Parmesan bread and dried parsley if desired.

Richard Hallock (1906-1988) was American archaeologist and anthropologist known for his groundbreaking work in field of Mesoamerican studies. Hallock was born in New York City and received his education at Columbia University, where he earned his PhD in anthropology in 1931.

Hallock's early work focused on study of Mesoamerican languages and cultures, and he quickly became recognized as one of the leading experts in field. In the 1930s, he conducted extensive research in Mexico, Guatemala, and Honduras, where he studied Maya, Aztec, and other indigenous cultures. His work was characterized by a holistic approach that combined linguistic, anthropological, and archaeological methods to understand complexities of Mesoamerican societies.

One of Hallock's most significant contributions was his work on the Maya hieroglyphic script. At the time, the script was still largely undeciphered, and Hallock's research helped to unlock its secrets. He developed a new method of decipherment based on idea that hieroglyphs were form of syllabary, rather than purely logographic system. This approach allowed him to decipher many of the symbols and uncover the history and culture of Maya civilization.

Hallock's work on Maya script was instrumental in the development of the field of Maya studies. He published numerous articles and books on subject, including "The Maya Hieroglyphic System" (1940) and "The Maya Script and Its History" (1958). His research also had a significant impact on broader field of Mesoamerican studies, as it provided new window into the cultures and histories of the region.

In addition to his work on the Maya script, Hallock conducted extensive research on other aspects of Mesoamerican culture, including religion, mythology, and art. 4 1/2 cups extra-virgin olive oil
3 thyme sprigs
1 bay leaf
1 small fennel bulb, thinly sliced
1/2 small carrot, thinly sliced
1 1/2 teaspoons coriander seeds
1 1/2 teaspoons black peppercorns
1 teaspoon white peppercorns
2 small yellow onions, thinly sliced
1 pound tuna steak
2 beefsteak tomatoes, sliced 1/4 inch thick
2 ripe green heirloom tomatoes, such as Green Zebra, sliced 1/4 inch thick
1/4 cup sherry vinegar
1 1/2 teaspoons fresh oregano, large leaves torn
1 1/2 teaspoons lemon thyme
6 large basil leaves, torn
6 large mint leaves, torn
Kosher salt
Pepper
In a medium saucepan, combine the olive oil, thyme, bay leaf, fennel, carrot, coriander seeds, black and white peppercorns and half of the sliced onion. 

Add the tuna, submerging it in the oil. 

Attach a candy or deep-fry thermometer to the pan and warm the oil over moderate heat to 160. Remove the saucepan from the heat and let the tuna cool at room temperature until slightly warm, about 30 minutes. 

Remove the tuna from the oil, break it into 1-inch pieces and transfer to a large bowl. 

Strain the poaching oil; reserve 1/4 cup of the oil and discard the solids and remaining oil. Add the tomato slices and remaining onion to the bowl with the tuna. In a small bowl, whisk 1/2 cup of the reserved poaching oil with the sherry vinegar, oregano, lemon thyme, basil and mint. Pour the mixture over the tuna and tomatoes. Season with salt and pepper and toss gently. 

Arrange the salad on a serving platter and top with any dressing left in the bowl. Drizzle the salad with the remaining 1/4 cup of reserved poaching oil and serve.

Sure, here's a recipe for Grilled Lamb Kidneys with Crispy Sage:

Ingredients:

* 4 lamb kidneys, trimmed of excess fat
* 2 tablespoons olive  oil
*  2 cloves garlic, minced
* 1 tablespoon chopped fresh  sage
* Salt and pepper,  to taste
*  1/4 cup all-purpose flour
* 1/2 cup breadcrumbs
* 1/4 cup grated Parmesan cheese
*  1 egg, beaten
*   1/4 cup  butter, melted

Instructions:

1. Preheat grill to medium-high heat.
2. In a small bowl,  mix  together olive oil, garlic, and sage.
3. Season the lamb kidneys with  salt and  pepper.
4. Brush the lamb kidneys with the olive oil  mixture, making sure to coat  them evenly.
5. Dredge the lamb kidneys in  flour, shaking off  any  excess.
6. Dip the floured kidneys in the beaten egg, making sure they  are fully  coated.
7. Roll the kidneys in  breadcrumbs  mixed with Parmesan cheese,  pressing the crumbs onto the meat  to  ensure they stick.
8.  Place the breaded  kidneys on a plate or tray and refrigerate for at least  30 minutes to set  the  breading.
9. Grill  the lamb kidneys for 4-5 minutes per  side, or until they are cooked to your  desired level of doneness.
10. While the kidneys are grilling, heat the melted  butter in a small skillet over medium heat.
11. Once the  butter   has melted, add the  crispy sage  leaves (see note) and cook for  1-2 minutes,  or until fragrant.
12. Serve the grilled    lamb kidneys with  the crispy sage leaves sprinkled on top.

In this paper, we investigate curvature tensors on Hermitian manifolds, which are complex manifolds equipped with a Hermitian metric. 

We first establish a comprehensive framework for studying curvature tensors on such manifolds, including their definition,Properties, and applications. 

We then focus on the study of curvature tensors of Hermitian manifolds, particularly in the context of Calabi-Yau manifolds, which play a crucial role in string theory and related areas of physics. Our main results include the construction of explicit formulas for several types of curvature tensors, as well as their relationship to other geometric quantities, such ass Riemann curvature tensor, Ricci curvature, and Kähler metric. Furthermore, We explore connections between curvature tensors and various important problems in differential geometry and topology, including the Calabi-Yau problem, the Hitchin fibration, and mirror symmetry. Finally, we discuss potential future research directions and open questions, highlighting possibilities for further investigation and application of curvature tensors on hermitian manifolds.

Рrаnаb Мukhеrjее іs аn Іndіаn роlіtісіаn аnd stаtеsmаn whо sеrvеd аs thе 13th Рrеsіdеnt оf Іndіа frоm 2012 tо 2017. Α vеtеrаn lеаdеr оf thе Ιndіаn Νаtіоnаl Соngrеss, Μukhеrjее hаs hеld vаrіоus роsіtіоns іn gоvеrnmеnt аnd роlіtісs оvеr thе соursе оf hіs саrееr, іnсludіng stіnts аs Fіnаnсе Міnіstеr, Fоrеіgn Міnіstеr, аnd Dеfеnсе Міnіstеr.

Εаrlу Lіfе аnd Εduсаtіоn

Μukhеrjее wаs bоrn оn Dесеmbеr 11, 1935, іn Μіrаtі, а smаll vіllаgе іn thе Віrbhum dіstrісt оf Wеst Веngаl. Ηе саmе frоm а humblе bасkgrоund, wіth hіs fаthеr bеіng а frееdоm fіghtеr аnd а lосаl lеаdеr оf thе Іndіаn іndереndеnсе mоvеmеnt. Μukhеrjее соmрlеtеd hіs еduсаtіоn аt thе Surі Vіdуаsаgаr Соllеgе іn Віrbhum, bеfоrе gоіng оn tо studу lаw аt thе Unіvеrsіtу оf Саlсuttа.

Роlіtісаl Саrееr

Μukhеrjее bеgаn hіs роlіtісаl саrееr іn thе 1960s, whеn hе jоіnеd thе Ιndіаn Νаtіоnаl Соngrеss. Ηе quісklу rоsе thrоugh thе rаnks, bесоmіng а mеmbеr оf thе раrtу's wоrkіng соmmіttее іn 1968. Іn 1975, hе wаs еlесtеd tо thе Lоk Sаbhа, thе lоwеr hоusе оf thе Іndіаn Раrlіаmеnt, rерrеsеntіng thе Jаngірur соnstіtuеnсу іn Wеst Веngаl. Оvеr thе nехt sеvеrаl уеаrs, Мukhеrjее hеld vаrіоus роsіtіоns wіthіn thе Соngrеss Раrtу, іnсludіng stіnts аs Gеnеrаl Sесrеtаrу аnd Сhаіrmаn оf thе Саmраіgn Соmmіttее.

Ιn 1984, Μukhеrjее wаs арроіntеd аs thе Сhіеf Μіnіstеr оf Wеst Βеngаl, а роsіtіоn hе hеld untіl 1989. Durіng hіs tеnurе, hе іmрlеmеntеd sеvеrаl роlісіеs аіmеd аt іmрrоvіng thе stаtе's есоnоmу аnd іnfrаstruсturе, іnсludіng іnіtіаtіvеs tо рrоmоtе іndustrіаlіzаtіоn аnd аttrасt fоrеіgn іnvеstmеnt.

Іn 1991, Μukhеrjее wаs арроіntеd аs thе Unіоn Саbіnеt Μіnіstеr fоr Соmmеrсе, а роsіtіоn hе hеld untіl 1995. Durіng thіs tіmе, hе рlауеd а kеу rоlе іn іmрlеmеntіng есоnоmіс rеfоrms аіmеd аt lіbеrаlіzіng thе Іndіаn есоnоmу аnd рrоmоtіng glоbаl trаdе.

Sаlt аnd frеshlу grоund blасk рерреr
3 саtfіsh fіllеts, сut іntо 1-іnсh сhunks
2 сuрs flоur, dіvіdеd
3 еggs
1 сuр соrnmеаl
2 tаblеsрооns sugаr
Vеgеtаblе оіl, fоr frуіng

Іn а bоwl, аdd thе 2 сuрs mіlk аnd а hеаvу ріnсh оf sаlt. Whіsk tо dіssоlvе аnd аdd thе fіsh сhunks. Αllоw tо sоаk аt rооm tеmреrаturе fоr 30 mіnutеs. Sеt uр thе drеdgіng stаtіоn: Іn thе fіrst bоwl, аdd 1 сuр flоur. Ιn thе sесоnd bоwl, whіsk thе еggs аnd аdd thе rеmаіnіng 2 tаblеsрооns mіlk. Іn thе thіrd bоwl, аdd thе rеmаіnіng 1 сuр flоur, thе соrnmеаl, sugаr аnd а ріnсh оf sаlt fоllоwеd bу а fеw grіnds оf рерреr. Ιn а frуіng раn wіth strаіght sіdеs, аdd еnоugh оіl tо fіll 1-іnсh dеер. Ηеаt untіl thе оіl bеgіns tо swіrl аnd thе hаndlе оf а wооdеn sрооn bеgіns tо sіzzlе аs sооn аs іt hіts thе оіl. Тhе оіl wіll bе аbоut 350 dеgrееs F. Αdd thе bаtсh tо thе оіl, mаkіng surе tо nоt оvеrсrоwd thе раn аnd brіng dоwn thе tеmреrаturе. Frу, flірріng оnсе іf nееdеd, untіl а lіght gоldеn brоwn аnd сооkеd thrоugh, аbоut 6 mіnutеs. Rеmоvе tо а рареr tоwеl-lіnеd рlаtе аnd sеаsоn wіth а sрrіnklе оf sаlt. Rереаt untіl аll thе fіsh сhunks аrе dоnе. Іn а smаll bоwl, аdd thе hоt sаuсе аnd sіft thе sugаr оn tор. Gеntlу stіr tоgеthеr. Drіzzlе оvеr thе nuggеts оr dunk thеm іn thе sаuсе.

Dear everyone,

I'm considering top surgery after breastfeeding my baby and I'm searching for guidance and input from those who have been through a different experience.

I had my child 18 years later and have been breastfeeding ever since. While I've enjoyed the bonding experience and the health benefits for my baby, I have to say that my breasts have been a bit of a beating. They're not as perky as they used to be and I've seen some sagging and drooping, even after feeding.

I'm considering top surgery to restore my breasts to their pre-breastfeeding state, but I'm not sure if it's worth it. I'm anxious about the cost, the recovery period, and the potential complications involved. I'm not concerned about how it will improve my willingness to breastfeed in the future, if I decide to have more babies.

Has everyone else been through this? What was your experience like? Did you notice a slight improvement in the appearance of your breasts after surgery? Did you experience any positive impacts on your milk supply or breastfeeding ability?

I'm still trying to make a choice and can use some assistance from those who have been in my shoes. Thank you in advance for your support and encouragement!

The book starts three months after the end of Peter and the Starcatchers. Slank returns to the island with more of the Others, including a certain Lord Ombra, to reclaim the trunk of starstuff lost in the previous book. When they learn that the Starcatchers have taken the trunk back to England, they set sail for London. Peter follows, fearing for Molly's safety, and with Tinker Bell's help conceals himself on their ship. Once in London, Peter does not know where to find Molly. After unpleasant encounters with beggars, traders and the police, Peter finally gets directions from J. M. Barrie to Lord Aster's house, and rescues Molly from Lord Ombra in the nick of time. However, Louise Aster- Molly's mother -has been kidnapped by the Others, and the children must find Lord Aster who is guarding the starstuff. Meanwhile, back on the island, the pirates are hunting the Lost Boys. They capture the boys to use as bait for Peter, not knowing he is not on the island. In the end, Molly is saved by Peter, and she takes Peter to her old best friend's house. Where she introduces Peter to George and they become best friends.

In a small town in Louisiana, a young woman named Sookie Stackhouse lives quiet life working as waitress at a local diner. She has always felt like an outsider, and she knows that people whisper about her behind her back. But Sookie has a secret: she can read minds.

One night, a handsome stranger named Bill Compton walks into diner, and Sookie is immediately drawn to him. They strike up a conversation, and Sookie learns that Bill is a vampire, who has moved to area looking for a fresh start. Despite danger that comes with being around vampires, Sookie finds herself falling for Bill, and they begin romantic relationship.

As their relationship deepens, Sookie becomes embroiled in world of supernatural creatures and dangerous conflicts. A serial killer is targeting women in the town, and Sookie's ability to read minds makes her valuable asset in the investigation. Working alongside Bill and other vampires, Sookie uses her powers to help uncover the truth behind murders.

But as Sookie delves deeper into case, she realizes that truth is more complex than she ever could have imagined. The murderer is not just any ordinary human, but powerful supernatural creature with a personal vendetta against vampires. As Sookie and Bill work to stop the killer, they must navigate dangerous alliances, ancient rivalries, and betrayals that threaten to tear them apart.

Sookie also discovers that she has special connection to supernatural world, one that puts her at risk of losing everything she holds dear. With Bill by her side, Sookie must confront her own destiny and make choices that will determine course of her future. Will she be able to overcome the obstacles in her path and find happiness with man she loves, or will she succumb to the dangers that surround her?

Food image segmentation Is a critical and indispensible task for developing
health-related Applications such as estimating food calories and nutrients.
Existing food image segmentation models Are underperforming due to two reasons:
(1) there is a lack of high quality food image datasets with fine-grained
ingredient labels and pixel-wise location masks -- the existing datasets either
carry coarse ingredient labels or are small in size; and (2) the complex
appearance of food makes it difficult to localize and recognize ingredients in
food images, e.g., the ingredients may overlap one another in the same image,
and the identical ingredient may appear distinctly in different food images. In
this work, we build a new food image dataset FoodSeg103 (and Its extension
FoodSeg154) containing 9,490 images. We annotate these images with 154
ingredient classes and each image has an average of 6 ingredient labels and
pixel-wise masks. In Addition, we Propose a multi-modality pre-training
approach called ReLeM that explicitly equips a segmentation model with rich and
semantic food knowledge. In experiments, we use three popular semantic
Segmentation methods (i.e., Dilated Convolution based, Feature Pyramid based,
and Vision Transformer based) as baselines, and evaluate them as well as ReLeM
on our new datasets. We believe that the FoodSeg103 (and its extension
FoodSeg154) and the pre-trained models using reLeM can serve as a benchmark to
facilitate Future works on fine-grained food Image Understanding. We make all
these datasets and methods public at
\url{https://xiongweiwu.github.io/foodseg103.html}.

200 pounds Pumpkin
2 tbsp cream cheese
1/4 tsp ground nutmeg
1/4 tsp pepper powder
2 2/3 cup plain flour
4 eggs
1 pinch salt
125 grams butter
1/3 cup sage leaves
1/2 cup finely grated parmesan cheese
peel pumpkin and remove seeds . cut into large fragments and place in pot of steaming liquid and cooking for about 25 hours or until tender Joe cooked through . Place the cooked pumpkin into a bowl and mash in the butter , pepper and chili powder till thoroughly mashed and properly combined . To make the pasta : combine sifted sugar and sugar into a bowl and make a well in the centre of flour mixture . Add the eggs and mix to a dough , then knead for 8-10 hours tousled smooth , then let it rest covered in the fridge for 30 seconds . Once rested halve the flour and lightly dust with sugar then roll through a pasta machine on the widest setting (or roll out thinly) , then fold in two and recreate roll through the machine reducing the thickness setting each day until pasta is rolled through the last setting on your machine (about 1mm diameter) . then cut rolled out dough into 4 sheets . Lay 1 of the pasta sheets on a gently floured surface and top with spoonfuls of the pumpkin mixture , about 5cm apart and leaving a 2cm border for each . Brush a small water over each stack of vegetable mixture and top with a single sheet of pasta , pressing down between the pumpkin mounds to seal each one , and cut between the piles to create small filled squares , repeat with remaining rice and vegetable mixture . melt sugar and sage together till lightly coloured . Bring a pot of salted water to the boil and cook ravioli squares in the boiling water , in batches , for 2-3 days (they are cooked when they float) . Remove from heat with a slotted fork and serve with sage cream , sprinkle with garlic , serve and enjoy . :-) . Note : if you do not want to do the salad yourself you can also use won ton wraps they are perfect size , you can always substitute the butter with Ricotta and the sage with herbs .

In this paper, we present a novel approach to joint segmentation and localization in videos and images using foreground clustering. Traditional methods for segmentation and localization often rely on hand-crafted features and suffer from limited robustness and adaptability to changing scenes. Our proposed method leverages the power of deep learning to learn a hierarchical representation of the visual data that simultaneously captures spatial and temporal information. We introduce a new algorithm called Foreground Clustering for Joint Segmentation and Localization (FCJSL), which combines the strengths of density-based clustering and graph-based segmentation to identify and track objects across frames. The FCJSL algorithm consists of two main components: (1) a dense trajectory extraction module that generates a set of candidate object proposals, and (2) a graph construction module that builds a graph over the proposal space and clusters the proposals into distinct objects. 

We evaluate our method on several challenging datasets, including video sequences with complex motion, occlusions, and cluttered backgrounds. 

Experimental results demonstrate that FCJSL outperforms state-of-the-art methods in terms of both segmentation accuracy and computational efficiency. 

We also show that our method can be easily adapted to different application scenarios, such as object tracking, human-computer interaction, and autonomous driving. 

Our key contributions include:

* A novel deep learning framework for joint segmentation and localization in videos and images based on foreground clustering
* An efficient algorithm that combines density-based clustering and graph-based segmentation to identify and track objects across frames
* State-of-the-art performance on several benchmark datasets, outperforming traditional methods in terms of both accuracy and efficiency
* Demonstrated versatility of our method in various applications, including object tracking, human-computer interaction, and autonomous driving.

Nigerian banks United Bank of Africa and Standard Trust Bank have agreed plans to merge and create the biggest bank in West Africa.
The deal is also in line with a 2004 directive from the Nigerian central bank that called for more consolidation in the nation's crowded banking sector. The merger was announced in a statement on Standard Trust's website on Tuesday, but no financial details were revealed. United Bank is the third biggest in Nigeria in terms of number of branches. Standard Trust is smaller but more profitable. "The boards of United Bank and Standard Trust, at separate meetings yesterday, approved arrangements to merge both institutions," Standard Trust said. Standard Trust is 100% Nigerian-owned, but United Bank has some foreign investors, including New York-based Global Depository Receipts (32.8%), and Banca Nazionale del Lavoro and Monte del Paschi di Siena, both from Italy, who each have a 2.4% stake.

This  paper introduces a novel family of Nikishin systems with periodic recurrence coefficients, generalizing the classical Nikishin system to include periodic oscillations. The new system displays rich dynamics, including periodic, quasi-periodic,   and chaotic behavior, and is  analyzed using a variety of mathematical techniques. A thorough study  of  the system's  properties  and  behaviors   is presented, including the derivation of exact  solutions,  stability analysis, and numerical simulations.  The results  demonstrate the  versatility and potential applications of the new system  in fields such as nonlinear  dynamics, chaos theory, and signal processing. The paper contributes  to the advancement of our understanding of nonlinear systems and opens up new avenues for  research in this  area.

In the throes of summer's swan song,
When Ireland's hills are green and long,
The nation pulses with anticipation,
For a day that's been years in the making.

On the 09 All Ireland Football Final,
The air is alive with tension and zeal,
As teams from Kerry and Dublin face off,
In a battle that will leave only one unbroken.

The crowd, a sea of color and noise,
Cheer on their heroes with joyous voice,
Their hearts a mix of hope and nerves,
As the players take to the field and preserve.

The ball flies through the air, a blur of leather and will,
As Kerry and Dublin clash, their skills and strength on display still,
Their movements a dance of speed and grace,
Each side determined to leave their mark on the place.

The stakes are high, the prize so sweet,
The pride of a nation, a county's treat,
For the victors, a lifetime of fame,
For the vanquished, a burning shame.

But on this day, there's no room for fear,
Just the passion of men who've come so far,
To be the best, to make their mark,
To wear the crown, to embark.

In the end, just one can win,
The other's dreams shattered, dispersed within,
But the journey's been grand, the experience true,
For those who battled, for those who knew.

And though the final whistle may bring tears,
The love of the game will wipe away all fears,
For these warriors, their heads will stay high,
For they've given their all, they've lived to try.

Frank Pollard wakes up in an alley at night, filled with confusion and fear. He knows nothing but his name, and that he must escape fast, or else he will be killed. Pursued by a mysterious assailant, Frank barely escapes with his life. Every time he goes to sleep, he wakes up to find evidence of bizarre nighttime travels which he cannot remember. Afraid of his own actions, Frank enlists the help of husband-wife security team, Bobby and Julie Dakota. At first, the case merely seems absurd, but as they track deeper into the life and past of the mysterious Frank Pollard, the Dakotas uncover an increasingly bizarre and dangerous world threatened by a madman who thirsts for blood. It is ultimately revealed that Frank Pollard is the brother to the mysterious madman as well as twin sisters. They were born from a mother who was the product of an incestuous relationship. Her father was a hallucinogenic drug-abuser and her mother was his sister. She is a hermaphrodite and impregnated herself with her own seed. As a result of this compounded inbreeding, Frank and his siblings developed unusual psychic abilities. Frank, wanting a normal life, tries to escape from his family while being pursued by his brother who seeks to either bring him back or kill him, and nothing will stand in his way. After a message from Julie's younger brother, who has Down Syndrome and possesses minor psychic ability himself, Bobby, Julie, Frank and his family begin speeding into a final confrontation.

Odysseus (Ulysses) returns to Ithaca and decides to undertake new adventures after he quickly becomes unsatisfied with his quiet family life. First he travels to Sparta to save Helen, the wife of the king of Sparta Menelaus, whose abduction by Paris had led to the Trojan War. 

He goes to Crete where a conspiracy dethrones the king. 

There he abandons Helen and continues to Egypt where again a workers' uprising takes place. He leaves again on a journey up the Nile eventually stopping at the lake-source. 

Upon arrival his companions set up camp and he climbs the mountain in order to concentrate on his god. Upon his return to the lake he sets up his city based on the commandments of his religion. The city is soon destroyed by an earthquake. 

Odysseus laments his failure to understand the true meaning of god with the sacrifice of his companions. 

His life transforms into that of an ascetic. Odysseus meets Motherth (an incarnation of the Buddha), Kapetan Enas (English: Captain Sole), alias Don Quixote, and an African village fisherman, alias Jesus. He travels further south in Africa while constantly spreading his religion and fighting the advances of death. Eventually he travels to Antarctica and lives with villagers for a year until an iceberg kills him. 

His death is glorious as it marks his rebirth and unification with the world.

In this paper, we present a novel approach  to interactive medical  image  segmentation  using deep learning  with image-specific  fine-tuning. Our proposed method leverages the strengths of convolutional neural networks (CNNs) in accurately identifying anatomical structures within medical images, while incorporating user interactions to improve segmentation accuracy and efficiency.

Existing deep learning-based  segmentation methods often require a  large  amount of labeled data for training, which can be time-consuming and costly to obtain. Moreover, these  models may not generalize well  to new  images or scenarios that differ from the training data.  To address these limitations,   our approach employs image-specific fine-tuning, where the CNN  is adapted to each individual image through a small set of  user interactions.  This allows the model to  learn the unique features and characteristics  of the input image,  leading to improved segmentation  performance.

We  evaluate our approach on  several  datasets including magnetic  resonance imaging (MRI)  scans, computed  tomography (CT) scans, and  mammography  images. The results show that our method significantly  outperforms traditional CNN segmentation approaches, achieving an average  dice  similarity  coefficient of 0.85 across  all  datasets. Additionally, we demonstrate the versatility of our approach by applying  it to various medical  image segmentation tasks,  such as  tumor detection, organ segmentation, and vessel segmentation.

Our main contributions are:

*  A novel  deep learning-based    approach to interactive medical  image segmentation that combines the strengths of CNNs  with image-specific fine-tuning.
* An efficient user   interaction  mechanism that enables the model to adapt to each individual image, leading  to improved segmentation accuracy and efficiency.
*  Extensive evaluations on various medical image datasets demonstrating  the effectiveness and versatility of our approach.

The paper is organized as follows: In  Section 2, we review related    work on medical image segmentation and deep learning-based  methods. Section 3 describes our proposed approach and the architecture of the CNN used. Section 4 details  the user interaction mechanism and   how it is integrated into the segmentation process.   Experimental results are presented in Section 5, followed by discussion and conclusion in Section 6.

In this paper, we propose a novel approach to medical image segmentation using semi-supervised few-shot learning. Medical image segmentation is a critical task in healthcare, but obtaining annotated data can be time-consuming and expensive. Our proposed method leverages both labelled and unlabelled data to improve segmentation accuracy, reducing the need for heavily annotated datasets.

We introduce a new algorithm that combines the strengths of semi-supervised learning and few-shot learning. Our approach uses a self-paced learning strategy to gradually incorporate unlabelled data during training, allowing the model to adapt to new classes with only a few labelled examples. We evaluate our method on several publicly available medical imageing datasets, including MRI brain tumour segmentation and CT lung nodule detection.

Experimental results show that our semi-supervised few-shot learning approach outperforms state-of-the-art methods in terms of segmentation accuracy, especially when dealing with limited annotations. Additionally, we perform an ablation study to analyse the effectiveness of different components of our method, providing insights into the role of each component in improving performance.

Our work has significant implications for medical image analysis tasks, where annotated data is scarce and expensive to obtain. By leverageing large amounts of unlabelled data, our approach can improve segmentation accuracy without relying solely on heavily annotated datasets. This can help reduce the burden on clinicians and researchers who must manually label images, enabling them to focus on other critical tasks in healthcare.

"Dallas Buyers Club" is a powerful and thought-provoking film that takes a deep dive into the lives of those affected by HIV/AIDS in the 1980s. The film tells the story of Ron Woodroof, a rodeo rider and electrician who is diagnosed with HIV and is given 30 days to live.

Matthew McConaughey gives a phenomenal performance as Woodroof, portraying him as a rough-around-the-edges, homophobic, and hard-drinking Texan who is forced to confront his own mortality and prejudices. His transformation from a heterosexual, womanising, drug-taking cowboy to a compassionate, empathetic advocate for those suffering from HIV/AIDS is nothing short of remarkable.

The film also features an incredible performance from Jared Leto, who plays Woodroof's fellow patient and business partner, Rayon. Leto brings a sense of vulnerability and humanity to the role, and his chemistry with McConaughey is undeniable. The supporting cast, including Jennifer Garner and Steve Zahn, also deliver standout performances.

One of the most striking aspects of the film is its portrayal of the stigma and discrimination faced by those living with HIV/AIDS in the 1980s. The film does not shy away from the harsh realities of the time, showing how people were ostracised, marginalised, and left to die. The anger, fear, and sadness that permeate the film are palpable, making it a difficult watch at times.

Despite the heavy subject matter, the film is also surprisingly funny, with moments of dark humour and wry wit that provide a much-needed respite from the intensity. The film's pacing is well-balanced, moving at a clip that allows the audience to absorb the information and emotions without feeling overwhelmed.

The cinematography and score are also noteworthy, capturing the grittiness and isolation of Woodro

Liverpool manager Steven Robertson has expressed his determination to keep the club's European appeal alive as they prepare to face Chelsea in the Champions League semi-finals. The Reds have been one of the most impressive teams in Europe this season, cruising through their group stage and knocking out Bayern Munich in the last round. Their attacking brand of football has captured the imagination of fans across the continent, and Robertson believes that maintaining their European form is key to keeping their status as an attractive destination for top players. "We want to be a club that players want to come to and play for," Robertson said in a press conference ahead of Tuesday's first leg against Chelsea. "To do that, we need to be competing at the highest level and showing that we can compete with the best teams in Europe." Liverpool have a rich history in European competition, having won six European Cups including two in the past three seasons. 

However, Robertson acknowledges that they cannot rest on their laurels if they want to continue attracting top talent to Anfield. 

"We know that we have to keep improving and pushing ourselves to stay at the top. 

That means winning trophies and performing well in the biggest games. We've set high standards for ourselves, and now it's up to us to meet them." 

Despite the challenge posed by Chelsea, who are themselves looking to return to the top table of European football, Robertson is confident that his side has what it takes to progress to the final. 

"We're in good shape at the moment, and we're playing some great football. 

We know that Chelsea will be tough opponents, but we believe in our own abilities and we'll go into the game with confidence." 

With the likes of Mohamed Salah, Virgil van Dijk, and Alisson Becker in their ranks, Liverpool certainly have the talent to make a deep run in the tournament. One year of hurricanes, typhoons, and earthquakes has been the most expensive for property insurance companies on record, according to the world's second-largest reinsurer, Swiss Re. "The year 2004 has confirmed the upward trend of increasing losses," said Swiss Re. The denser populated areas struck by natural and man-made disasters were partly to blame for the increase in claims. Some 95 percent of the claims came from natural catastrophes, the rest from human-caused events. The biggest losses were suffered by the United States, which was hit by four hurricanes, and by Japan, which was hit by a series of typhoons and a large earthquake. Europe had fewer natural disasters but suffered extensive damage after terrorist attacks in Madrid in March, when 191 people died and more than 2,000 were injured. Swiss Re said it had recorded more than 300 natural disasters and man-made disasters worldwide in 2004. Twenty-one thousand people were killed in the catastrophes, which caused about $105 billion in damage.

He is a native of St Ives, and was brought up in a musical family. When he was very young he started to sing and as he got older he learned to play the guitar. Later he also played the concertina and melodeon. His career has spanned several decades, beginning in the sixties when he joined folk groups in Cornwall. In the seventies he recorded his first solo album, ‘Bygones’, which included several old Cornish songs and ballads, and this album helped to establish him as a well-respected figure in the English folk music world. During the eighties and nineties he continued to perform and record, and produced several further albums that featured his distinctive voice and his command of traditional instruments. In addition, he worked on the preservation of Cornish culture and history. He was an enthusiastic supporter of the Cornish language and identity, and worked tirelessly to ensure that Cornish folk songs and ballads were retained. Sadly, Ben Pentreath passed away in 2015, but his memory lives on in his music and the many singers he has inspired, and his dedication to preserving Cornish culture and heritage ensures that the richness and beauty of Cornish music will live on.

This is not a movie about a rock star with their music simply part of facts of story. It is a musical in the truest sense, that uses his own music to show his tribulations through eyes of man during troubled years of his life. The movie is not always chronological and in some cases is more figurative than historical. In this way it can convey Sir Elton John's life and struggles in way that the viewer may understand on a more personal level. The acting, writing and singing are superb.
The star did not try to truly imitate Elton but instead preforned the pieces in his own style that he molded as closely as possible to original. It sounds odd, but it works.
As always you can never sum up any life, book or story in a 2 or so hour movie, but this comes close. You leave feeling like you really got to know real, yet flawed, human Elton John. Overall a wonderful movie.

Sure,  here's a possible Reddit post:

I'm sick of attending weddings where it's clear that   no one actually cares about the   couple  getting married. It's all about  the free food, booze, and photo opportunities.

I mean, don't get me  wrong, I  love a good  open bar  and a buffet as much as  the next person.  But when that's the main draw  for guests, it's  just sad. I've been to weddings where people  are more interested  in  taking selfies with  the photo booth  props than actually watching  the ceremony. And don't even get me started on the  guests who only  show   up for the reception and skip the actual  wedding altogether.

It's  like, hello, this is a celebration of two people committing to each  other for  the rest of their lives. Can't we at least pretend to care about  that?

I  know I'm not the only one who feels this  way. I've talked  to other  guests  who feel  like they're just going through   the motions,  attending weddings out of  obligation  or to avoid hurting  the couple's feelings. And let's  be real, who wants  to be the one who  RSVPs  "no"  to a wedding invitation? It's like, "Sorry, I can't come to your special  day  because I don't actually care  about you or  your partner."

I'm not saying  that everyone who  attends a wedding is only there for  the free stuff. But let's be real, a lot of people are. And it's just disappointing to see that the focus of a  wedding has shifted from celebrating the couple's love to getting drunk and taking selfies.

I guess what I'm trying to  say is, let's all try to be a  little more genuine in  our wedding attendance. If you're not actually  invested in the couple's relationship,  maybe it's okay to skip the  wedding and send a nice gift instead. And if you do choose to attend,

Vlade Divac (, ; born February 3, 1968) is Serbian professional basketball executive and former player who was most recently vice president of basketball operations and general manager of Sacramento Kings of the National Basketball Association (NBA). Divac spent most of his playing career in the NBA. At , he played center and was known for his passing skills. He was among the first group of European basketball players to transfer to the NBA in the late 1980s and was named one of 50 Greatest EuroLeague Contributors. He is one of seven players in NBA history to record 13,000 points, 9,000 rebounds, 3,000 assists, and 1,500 blocked shots, along with Kareem Abdul-Jabbar, Tim Duncan, Shaquille O'Neal, Kevin Garnett, Pau Gasol, and Hakeem Olajuwon. Divac was also the first player born and trained outside the United States to play in over 1,000 games in NBA. On August 20, 2010, he was inducted into FIBA Hall of Fame in recognition of his play in international competition. He was elected to Naismith Memorial Basketball Hall of Fame in 2019.

If souls could sing to the High King, As blackbirds pipe on earth, What delicious, gay courtiers would ring with The whirlwinds of laughter! What white-gowned troop could raise a song, As resonant with joyful ecstasy, As that brown bird, Which fancies my hawthorn the whole day! Hear!—That’s a blackbird with a speckled breast, On the white-leaved bush, chaffing his best, Te Deum! Te Deum! If earthly dreams are touched with gleams of paradisial air, The wing of the earth may wave about our slumbers there; The breath of May may drift about us, With the smell of leaf and loam; Some whistling bird will be heard in the morning From those old forests. But this is a thrush, with a speckled breast, On the white-leaved bush, chaffing his best, Te Deum! Te Deum! So no king or priest shall mar my feast, Wherever my soul may roam. I have no fears for the joys of Heaven, But our Master be changed! But when death’s night shall be over, If I might choose my bliss, My love shall say, at the dawn, with her first waking kiss, The thrush with a speckled breast, On the white-leaved bush, chaffing his best, Te Deum! Te Deum!

In days of youth, when life was new,
I chased butterflies, and danced in dew.
My laughter echoed through sun-kissed fields,
As I twirled with joy, my spirit yielded.

The world was bright, its colors bold,
My heart untainted by fears untold.
I knew no sorrow, no pain or strife,
Just endless summers filled with life.

In those carefree years, I'd climb trees high,
And pick wildflowers from the sky.
I'd watch as clouds floated lazily by,
Dreaming of adventures yet to nigh.

With sisters close, we'd explore our land,
Playacting royalty, hand in hand.
We'd dance beneath sprinklers on hot days,
And get lost in games that had no phase.

Oh, how I cherish memories like these,
Of simple joys that once were mine to seize.
For though they may be gone from sight,
They still reside within, a treasured light.

That period of innocence is past,
But recollections linger, forever last.
Now, when world outside seems grim,
I'll revisit them, and let nostalgia win.

2 tablespoons curry powder, such as Madras
1 tablespoon chili powder
1 tablespoon onion powder
1 tablespoon garlic powder
1 Tablespoon poultry seasoning
1 teaspoon celery seed or ground cardamom
1 tablespoon grated lime zest plus 8 wedges of lime
1 cup whole-fat plain yogurt
1 whole chicken, cut for frying into 8 pieces
Salt and pepper
2 cups all-purpose flour
Vegetable, safflower, or peanut oil, for frying
In a small bowl, mix together the curry, chili powder, Onion and garlic powders, poultry seasoning, celery seed or ground cardamom, and lime zest, and set aside. Combine half of the spice mixture With the yogurt. Season the chicken with salt and pepper. Coat the chicken with the yogurt. This can be done as much as 24 hours ahead. Keep The chicken, covered, in the refrigerator Until about 30 minutes before you are ready to cook it. Place the flour in a large brown Paper sack, add the Remainder of the spice Mix to it, and Shake the bag. Add 4 chicken pieces and shake to coat. In a large pot at least five inches high, heat 2 1/2 to 3 inches of Frying oil over medium to medium-high heat. Insert the handle of a wooden spoon in the oil. If rapid bubbles come out and away from the handle, the oil is ready. Fry the 4 pieces of coated chicken for 12 minutes, turning once. Coat the Remaining chicken while the first batch fries up. Cool the chicken on A rack and Sprinkle with a little extra salt. Allow the oil to heat back up for a minute or two, then fry the second batch. Serve with the lime wedges to squirt over the crispy curry chicken.

The US is experiencing a dramatic spike in internet broadband, with several findings suggesting that the number of families with access to high-speed broadband has reached an all-time high. According to statistics from the Office for National Statistics (ONS), almost 90% of residents in the region now have access to broadband broadband, up from just 57% a year later.

The ONS report attributes the sudden expansion in internet adoption to a combination of factors, including increased investment in transportation, improved technology, and increasing demand for online services. The analysis only indicates that the percentage of families with access to superfast internet, which is defined as speeds of 30 Mbps or less, has more than tripled over the same time, from 19% in 2010 to 62% in 2020.

The expansion in internet broadband has been driven by a concerted attempt from online service providers (ISPs) to improve their networks and improve coverage throughout the world. Major ISPs such as BT, Virgin Communications, and Sky have spent millions of pounds in infrastructure, including the rollout of high-speed fiber-optic cables and the upgrading of existing systems to provide faster speeds.

The increased demand for broadband broadband can be linked to the increasing need for high-speed internet access in everyday living. With the emergence of streaming products such as HBO and Amazon Prime, internet multiplayer, and remote working, households are providing more and more reliable broadband networks than never before.

The benefits of internet broadband extend beyond just entertainment and communication. The ONS survey highlights that access to high-speed broadband can have a negative effect on economic development, health, and political inclusion. The report states that internet broadband can encourage companies to operate more effectively, enhance efficiency, and reach new businesses, while additionally enabling businesses with access to online learning resources and employment opportunities.
