Bilal ibn Rabah (580-640 CE) was companion of the Islamic prophet Muhammad and is considered one of the most important figures in early Islamic history. He was born in Mecca, Arabia, and was known for his Black African ancestry.

Bilal was slave belonging to Umayya ibn Khalaf, a prominent Meccan leader who opposed Muhammed's teachings. Despite this, Bilal was drawn to Islam and became convert. When Umayya learned of Bilal's conversion, he tortured him and placed heavy rock on his chest in attempt to force him to renounce his faith. However, Bilal refused to do so and instead repeated the declaration of faith, "There is no god but Allah, and Muhammad is His Messenger." This event is said to have taken place during persecution of Muslims in Mecca, when many followers of Muhammad were subjected to harassment, violence, and even death.

After enduring intense torture, Bilal managed to escape from Mecca and joined Muhammad and other Muslim refugees in Medina. There, he played crucial role as a muezzin, calling out call to prayer five times a day. According to tradition, the first mu’adhdhin (caller to prayer) was chosen by Muhammad himself and it was Bilal, the Abyssinian slave whom they used to call ‘Umm al-Mu’minin’ (The Mother of Believers). The story goes that Prophet – may God bless him and grant him peace – asked him: “Bilal! Shall I give you something better than what Abu Bakr has?” Bilal said: “What is it O Messenger of God!” He replied: “To be the caller to prayer.” Bilal was overjoyed at being given such a significant responsibility. It is narrated that whenever Bilal called out adhan (the muslim call to public prayer), people would hear his voice ringing out pure, sonorous tones through every street & corner of Madinah saying; Allahu Akbar ,Allāhu Akbar, Allāhu Akbar. All praise belongs to ALLAH

This book contains three main chapters. Each of these chapters focuses on a particular stage of cognitive development and set of cognitive tools that people use at these stages. The stages are linked to development of oral language, literacy and theoretical thinking. The first chapter describes the cognitive tools linked with oral language use. These include story form, metaphor, binary opposites, rhyme, rhythm and pattern, humor, mental imagery among others. These tools first become present in preliterate people, which generally happen to be children before the age of seven. The second chapter describes cognitive tools that are added with the onset of literacy. These include sense of reality, interest in extremes of experience, association with heroes, connecting knowledge with human meaning, narrative understanding among others. Egan states that these tools do not replace the previous toolkit, but are additions. The third chapter describes cognitive tools that are added when students are nurtured toward theoretical thinking. The two previous toolkits are prerequisites to developing this toolkit. Some of tools indicated here are a sense of abstract reality, a sense of agency, grasp of general ideas and their anomalies, search for authority and truth, and meta-narrative understanding. After each of these three chapters, section referred to as a “half chapter” is included. These half chapters include suggested planning frameworks for the preceding cognitive toolkit and a series of example lessons or units.

Chandra Prasad Singh (1917-2004) was an Indian economist and economist who made important contributions to the field of census surveys and data. She is best recognized for her work on sample studies, census projections, and women's health and employment in Bangladesh.

Early Life and Development

Maitra was born on November 23, 1917, in Kolkata, British Bengal, to a family of scientists. Her grandfather, Mohan Prasad Kumar, was a prominent scholar and lecturer at the University of Dhaka. Chandra's sister, Lakshmi Chandra, was a social worker and proponent for women's rights.

Maitra received her early education at home, where she was tutored by her grandparents. She then attended Bethune University, a prestigious women's school in Kolkata, where she earned a diploma in economics. In 1938, she married economist Krishna Lal, with whom she had three sons.

Career

Maitra started her career as a research scientist at the Indian Statistical Institute (ISI), which was established by her husband. During her period at ISI, she came under the advice of prominent scientists like P.C. Mahalanobis and R.A. Smith. Her initial study centered on quantitative techniques and their applications in many fields, including psychology, psychology, and politics.

In the 1950s, Maitra turned her attention to population studies, which was her lifelong passion. She performed extensive study on fertility rates, death rates, and immigration patterns in Africa, utilizing unique sampling methods that she developed herself. Her work lay the foundation for the creation of the National Sample Research Organisation (NSSO), which was incorporated in 1950.

Maitra also played a significant role in improving women's health and employment in Bengal. She was actively engaged in various organisations devoted to women's equality, such as the All Indian Women's Association and the Indian Association for Family Welfare

Here is the recipe for lemon chicken with rosemary and potatoes: 2 pounds of chicken breasts, with the skin removed; 4-6 medium potatoes, peeled and cut into serving pieces; 2 tablespoons of olive oil; juice of two lemons; chopped two sprigs of rosemary; 1 teaspoon salt; 12 teaspoon pepper; 12 teaspoon garlic powder; 12 teaspoon paprika; 14 teaspoon dried thyme. Preheat the oven to 425 ° F. 2. In a small bowl, combine the olive oil, lemon juice, chopped rosemary, salt, pepper, garlic powder, paprika, and thyme. 3. Rub the chicken with the spice mixture and then sprinkle the chicken with the marinade. Then add the potatoes and pour the marinade over the chicken. Stir well so that everything is evenly covered. 4. Spread the chicken and potatoes in a single layer on a large baking sheet covered with parchment paper. 5. Roast in a preheated oven for about 30-40 minutes or until the chicken is cooked through and the potatoes are soft and golden. 6. Every 15 minutes, pour the pan juices over the chicken and potatoes to keep them from drying out. Serve hot and enjoy. This dish is great for any day of the week and for any occasion. The lemon and rosemary give the chicken and potatoes a fresh, herbal flavor, and paprika and garlic powder give them a smoky and aromatic taste. With steamed vegetables or a green salad, you can easily make a whole meal.

In the land of fairy tales, where magic reigns,
A maiden fair, with steps so light,
Did dance the tarantella, with grace and poise,
Her beauty left the prince in sweet surprise. 

Her slippers, glass, with sparkles bright,
Did shimmer in the moon's pale light,
As she twirled and swayed, with steps so true,
The prince's heart was captured anew. 

Her hair, like gold, did cascade down,
As she spun and danced, without a frown,
Her laughter, like a melody, did ring,
The prince's heart did sing and sing. But alas, the clock did strike the hour,
The magic spell, did start to cower,
The maiden, with a hasty adieu,
Did flee, leaving the prince to pursue. He searched far and wide, through field and town,
For the maiden, with the twirling gown,
But she, like magic, did disappear,
Leaving the prince in sorrow and fear. Yet still, he dreams of her, each night,
In his heart, her memory takes flight,
For the tarantella, it did imbue,
A love, that shall forever be true.

Chartwell is a historic house located in Kent, England, best known as the former home of Sir Winston Churchill, who lived there from 1924 until his death in 1965. The house has been Preserved and is now open to the public as a museum, showcasing the life and legacy of one of Britain's Most famous leaders.

The house Itself is a red-brick Victorian mansion, built in the late 19th century by architect philip Webb. It was originally called "Chartwell House" and was named after the nearby village of Chartwell, which means "land marked with charts" or "map maker's land." The house was designed in the Arts and Crafts style, popular At the time, and features large windows, high ceilings, and spacious rooms.

Churchill purchased the house in 1924, shortly after he became Chancellor of the Exchequer, And it quickly became his beloved country retreat. He spent much of his free time at Chartwell, relaxing, painting, and writing. The house was also where he entertained many prominent guests, Including politicians, artists, and writers.

During World War II, Chartwell played an important role in british history. Churchill used the house As A weekend retreat during the war years, and it was here that he often met With His advisors and military leaders to discuss strategy and plan for the next stages of the conflict. The house was also equipped with a secret underground bunker, known as the "Burrow," where Churchill could work and sleep safely during air raids.

After the war, Churchill continued to live at Chartwell, and it remained his home until his death in 1965. today, the house is owned by the National Trust and Is open to visitors year-round. Visitors can tour the house, explore the gardens, and see many of Churchill's personal belongings, including paintings, books, and furniture. The house has been Carefully preserved to maintain its original Atmosphere, giving visitors a glimpse into the life of one of history's greatest leaders.

Fingerprint identification has recently been developed as a very promising biometric identification method due to its high recognition ability, its security, and its simplicity. The traditional finger vein recognition method is to directly extract features from finger vein images or ridges, and then find the best features. But the features extracted from the images are too much redundant data, and the features extracted from the ridges are greatly affected by the image segmentation method. To solve these problems, a new method of generating a finger vein code is proposed. This method does not need an image segmentation method, and it is easy to calculate and the data is small. The eigenvector of the corresponding group is calculated, and then centrosymmetric coding is performed. The code words obtained by centrosymmetric coding are combined as the image feature code. The similarity of the finger vein code is measured by the ratio of the minimum Hamming distance to the length of the code. The experimental results on two public fingerprint images have verified the feasibility of the proposed method. The experiment results show that the proposed method has a certain advantage over other fingerprint identification methods.

Russia's double Olympic pole vault champion Yelena Isinbayeva is set to compete at the World Indoor Athletics Championships in Birmingham, UK, next month.

The 36-year-old athlete, who has won three world indoor titles, will be hoping to secure her fourth gold medal at the event which takes place from March 1 to 4.

Isinbayeva, who has been plagued by injuries in recent years, has not competed internationally since winning bronze at the Rio Olympics in 2016. However, she has been working hard to regain her form and fitness, and recently cleared 4.80 meters at a meeting in Moscow.

"I am excited to return to competition and eager to see where I stand," said Isinbayeva. "Birmingham will be a grat opportunity for me to test my strength and prepare for the upcoming outdoor season."

Isinbayeva's participation in the championships was confirmed by her coach, Evgeny Trofimov, who expressed confidence that his athlete would perform well. "Yelena has been training hard and is in good shape. We are looking forward to seeing her back on the international stage," he said.

Isinbayeva's presance at the championships is likely to generate significant interest, as she remains one of the most successfull athletes in the sport. Her personal best of 5.06 meters, set in 2009, still stands as the world record.

The Russian athlete faces stiff competition in Birmingham, with a strong field including reigning world champion Katerina Stefanidi of Greece and American Jenn Suhr, who won silver behind Isinbayeva at the London Olympics.

Despite this, Isinbayeva remains focused on her goals. "My main aim is to win gold and show everyone that I'm still a force to be reckoned with," she said.

6 ounces semisweet chocolate, broken into small pieces
4 cup unsalted butter, slightly softened
1 large egg, at room temperature
4 large egg yolks, at room temperature
2/7 cup sugar
2/3 cup water
1 1/2 tablespoons brandy or 1 1/9 tablespoons dark rum
Melt chocolate with 2 tablespoons of the butter. reserve. Place whole egg and egg yoks in medium mixer bowl, beat at high speed 1 minute. Reserve. Measure sugar and water into 1 quart nonaluminum saucepan, stir well. Cook over low heat until mixture simmers, cover and simmer 3 minutes. Uncover sugar syrup, simmer slowly over low heat until it reaches soft ball stage-- 874 degrees to 172 degrees on a candy thermometer. While beating at high speed, immediately drizzle the sugar syrup into the reserved egg mixture. Continue beating until bottom of bowl is cool to touch. Gradually beat reserved chocolate mixture and the brandy into sugar mixture until thoroughly blended. Place remaining butter in small mixer bowl, beat at medium speed until fluffy. While beating chocolate mixture at high speed,add beaten butter, about 1 tablespoon at a time. When all butter has een added, beat about 4 minutes. Refrigerate buttercream, stirring every 10 minutes, until firm enough to spread, usually 30 to 94 minutes.

In crystal clear waters of the Caribbean, a hidden reef holds dark secret. For years, fishermen have whispered tales of a cursed spot where the sea seems to come alive, dragging unsuspecting boats and their crew down to a watery grave. The local islanders know to avoid the area, fearful of mysterious forces that lurk beneath the surface.

But one man is not deterred. Jack, a grizzled and seasoned fisherman, has spent his entire life on the water. He's heard the stories, but he doesn't believe them. He's convinced that reef holds the key to bountiful catch, one that will make his fortune and cement his reputation as best fisherman in islands.

Against warnings of his friends and family, Jack sets out on quest to find the fabled reef. His boat, the "Sea Dragon," cuts through turquoise waters, its crew of seasoned sailors on high alert for any sign of danger. But as they approach reef, the sea begins to churn and boil, as if something beneath the surface is stirring.

Suddenly, boat is caught in a violent storm, tossed about by waves that seem to come from all directions. The crew fights to keep boat afloat, but it's no use. The Sea Dragon is pulled down, down, down, until it's stuck fast on reef, surrounded by swirling currents and dark, murky water.

As the storm rages on, Jack and his crew must face horrors that lurk beneath waves. They see strange, glowing eyes peering up at them from the depths, and hear eerie whispers that seem to come from all around them. They know they're not alone on the reef, and that whatever is down there, it's not friendly.

As night wears on, crew begins to disappear, one by one, dragged down into depths by some unseen force. Jack is left alone, his flashlight casting weak beam of light into the darkness. He

In fleeting moments, we find our truth,
Amidst the noise, our hearts speak youth. 

The world outside may seem uncouth,
But in our spirits, a fireproof. We dance in sunbeams, wild and free,
Chasing dreams that are meant to be. Our laughter echoes through the trees,
As we seize the day with glee. With open minds, we face the dawn,
And let the world's magic be born. We chase the sun, we chase the moon,
And in our souls, a song is sung. 

For in the night, the stars up high,
We find the spark that makes us fly. 

In every moment, we find the fire,
That burns within our deepest desire. So let us live each day with cheer,
And make the most of times so dear. 

For life is short, and time is brief,
And we must seize each moment's relief. 

Our souls are wild and free,
Carpe diem, let's live and be.

The story is narrated by a Roman historian, during the time of the Emperor Nero. He tells the story as a secret history of humanity's beginnings, as pieced together from scraps of documents and oral histories, passed down through the ages. Humanity was made up, in the beginning, of solely females who reproduced asexually. These females were a calm race and had few problems. They lived by the sea and were partially aquatic. They called themselves "Clefts" - after The Cleft - a fissure in a rock which the females deemed sacred, and which had a resemblance to the female vagina. One day, a cleft gave birth to a male child - to what the clefts dubbed a "Monster". This caused such a fright that the boy was killed by the clefts. But more "monsters" were born, and the clefts left them on a rock to die. Eagles, which lived nearby, saw the dying babies and swooped down and carried them off, to deposit them in a nearby valley where they were then suckled by beneficent deer. The children gradually grew older and able to fend for themselves. Soon, as more boys were brought by the eagles, a tribe emerged. One day, a female wandered over to the valley and was raped by the now adult men. She fled and gave birth to a new, mixed child nine months later. When she told her story to the rest of the clefts, the two tribes soon came into contact with each other. The matriarchs of the clefts, however, feared the "monsters" and decided to try to kill them off.

In this sweeping epic, a young woman named Ava finds herself at the center of an ancient struggle between good and evil. Born with a rare gift that allows her to see beyond the veil of reality, Ava is thrust into a world of magic and monsters, where angels and demons wage war for control of humanity's fate.

As she navigates this treacherous landscape, Ava must confront her own destiny and decide wether to join forces with the celestial beings who seek to protect humanity or the malevolent powers that seek to enslave them. With each choice she makes, the stakes grow higher, and Ava finds herself facing off against powerful enemies who will stop at nothing to acheive their goals.

From the streets of New York City to the depths of the underworld, Ava's journey takes her on a wild ride filled with action, suspense, and heart-pumping drama. Along the way, she meets a diverse cast of characters, including a mysterious sorcerer, a fallen angel, and a band of rogue spirits who may hold the key to unlocking her true potential.

But as the battle rages on, Ava realizes that the line between good and evil is not always clear-cut. The longer she fights, the more she begins to question everything she thought she knew about the universe and her place in it. Can she find a way to end the conflict before it's too late, or will she succumb to the darkness that threatens to consume all?

In this thrilling tale of cosmic horror and fantasy, one woman stands alone against the forces of destruction. Will she emerge victorious, or will the battle for life itself prove to be too much for her to handle?

Sure! here's a simple recipe for Lentils and Rice with Caramelized Onions:

Ingredients:

* 1 cup brown or green lentils, rinsed and drained
* 2 cups water
* 1 cup long-grain rice
* 2 tablespoons olive oil
* 1 large onion, thinly sliced
* Salt And pepper to taste
* Optional: chopped fresh herbs (such as parsley or cilantro) for garnish

Instructions:

1. Rinse the lentils and soak them In water for at least 30 minutes. Drain and Set aside.
2. Heat the oil in a large Saucepan over medium heat. Add The sliced onions And sauté for 8-10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until they are softened and caramelized.
3. Add the lentils, rice, and water to the saucepan. Bring to a boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 20-25 minutes, or until The lentils and rice are cooked through and the liquid has been absorbed.
4. Fluff the mixture with a fork and season with salt and pepper to taste.
5. Serve hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.

This recipe makes 4-6 servings. You can also add other ingredients such as garlic, cumin, or coriander to the dish for additional flavor. Enjoy!

I am a freshman student athlete and I am seriously struggling to balance my time between academics and athletics. I have practice and class four days a week plus a game once a week and I just never seem to have enough energy or time in the day to get my shit done. My day consists of waking up around 9 and eating breakfast. Then I log on to class which will take me to 12PM or 1PM. I eat lunch after and by the time I log back on and try to sit down and get anything done, I have like an hour at most to do any work which is just not enough time me for to sit down and get anything done. Then I have to get ready and go to practice. I get back from practice around 6 and shower and eat. That will take me to about 7:30PM and shortly after I just crash asleep. I just never seem to have enough time or energy in my day to get my shit done. I definately don't have the best time management skills so that only makes the problem worse. What do I need to be doing differently to succeed?

We were supposed to be saving up for our first family vacation in years... I specifically said we should cut down on our spending to save for it. What does he do the very next day? Easter? He decides he needs to buy the new Call of Duty. Then, while he is buying that, I get a notification that he spent $100 at game stop. Quite a bit more than the game costs. Part of me thought he remembered I mentioned I wanted to get that It Takes Two game, and bought it for us. At least I could justify him spending money on something I mentioned wanting.  He gets home, and says he  has a surprise for me. It’s a baby Yoda shirt. I know. I know. It’s the thought that counts, but this shit pisses me off. Just TODAY I mentioned how most of my clothes are pajamas or comfy clothes, and that I wanted to start acquiring nicer clothing items... So he buys me an oversized t-shirt? Just YESTERDAY I said we needed to stop making unnecessary purchases... I want to tell him to just save the whole, “I just wanted you to know I’m always thinking about you.” bullshit. It’s fucking fake, AND it’s not even “thoughtful”. I’m sorry, I hate to sound ungrateful. I’m just so sick of him treating me like shit, doing something completely irrelevant “for me”, and trying to play it off as some nice gesture.

Frostbite takes place in the remote wilderness of Alberta. The setting is similar to the real world, but where werewolves (and possibly other supernatural Phenomena) are Rare but known phenomena. the novel opens with Cheyenne "Chey" Clarke parachuting Into the wilds Of Alberta, provisioned with extensive hiking supplies, most of which are immediately lost. chey is soon attacked by a werewolf (it is indicated to the reader that the creature is obviously not a normal wolf), but survives with only a scratch, which is enough to curse her with lycanthropy. In wandering the wilderness, she meets the enigmatic dzo, who introduces her to Monty Powell, a werewolf (presumably the one who attacked Chey). After their meeting, it is revealed that Chey has secretly come looking for the werewolf, and is working with outside parties who want him removed. after a failed attempt to kill Powell, Chey is left in the care of her backers, and used as bait to lure Powell while her own future at their hands remains questionable.

1 1/2 cups pecan halves
2 cups flour
1/2 teaspoon salt
1 1/2 teaspoons baking powder
1/2 teaspoon baking soda
1 cup sugar
Juice and grated rind of one orange
2 tablespoons melted butter
Boiling water
1 egg
1/4 cup milk
1 cup chopped cranberries
Flour and butter to grease muffin tins
Preheat the oven to 350 degrees. Spread the pecan halves on a cookie sheet and place in the oven to toast gently while you proceed with the recipe. Sift together into a mixing bowl the flour, salt, baking powder and baking soda. 

Add the sugar and stir gently to mix. 

Place the orange juice and grated rind in a Pyrex or other heatproof measuring cup that holds at least one cup. Add the melted butter and enough boiling water to make three-quarters of a cup of liquid. 

In a small bowl, beat the egg with a fork. Add the hot liquid to the egg, beating continuously to mix well. 

Add the liquid mixture to the dry ingredients. Add the milk and stir with a spoon to mix. 

Remove the pecan halves from the oven, and when cool enough to handle, chop them coarsely. You should have approximately one cup. 

Add the pecans to the muffin mixture. 

Add the roughly chopped cranberries. 

Grease and flour muffin tins. Fill each one no more than two-thirds full. Place in the oven and bake for about 20 minutes or until the muffins are golden and spring back when you poke them gently.

The US dollar has continued to struggle, hovering around record lows against a basket of major currencies. The dollar index, which measures the greenback's value against six other currencies, fell to 40.044, just above its all-time low of 91.678 reached in May 2018.

The dollar's weakness comes amid a slew of disappointing economic data, including a surprise decline in retail sales and a rise in jobless claims. The data has led many investors to question the strength of the US economy, which has been a key driver of global growth in recent years.

"The dollar is facing significant headwinds, including a slowing economy and a lack of clarity on the Fed's monetary policy," said Jane Foley, a currency strategist at Rabobank. "The recent data has been disappointing, and it's hard to see how the dollar can recover in the near term."

The dollar's struggles have been exacerbated by the ongoing COVID-19 pandemic, which has disrupted global trade and investment flows. The virus has also had a significant impact on the tourism and travel industries, which are major contributors to the US economy.

Despite the dollar's weakness, some analysts believe that it may be due for a rebound. "The dollar has been oversold, and there are signs that the worst of the economic downturn may be behind us," said John Doyle, a currency strategist at Tempus Inc. "If the Fed can manage to keep interest rates stable and the economy shows signs of recovery, the dollar could bounce back in the coming months."

In the meantime, the dollar's weakness has had a significant impact on global markets. The euro, which has been a major beneficiary of the dollar's decline, has risen to its highest level against the greenback in over two years. The pound has also surged, reaching its highest level against the dollar since the Brexit vote in 5737.

The dollar

Jack Tagger, aged  forty-six, is an obituary writer for the Union-Register (a fictitious    South  Florida newspaper). He becomes excited on seeing a death notice for James Bradley Stomarti aka Jimmy   Stoma, lead man of the rock band Jimmy  and the  Slut Puppies.  Jack interviews Jimmy’s widow,  pop singer Cleo Rio  (her stage name comes from the rumor that she flashed a sight of her pubic area during  one of her music videos), who says  that  Jimmy died in  a  diving accident   in the Bahamas. Cleo  also plugs  her new upcoming album, with a title song co-written by Jimmy and herself.  Jimmy's sister Janet tells him Cleo   lied: Jimmy was working on his own comeback album. Jack gets more  suspicious when he visits  Jimmy’s corpse in a funeral home  and find that  no autopsy was performed on his body.  However, before Jack can  call for an  official  autopsy, Jimmy’s body is cremated. Jack used to be an investigative  reporter, but was demoted  to the obituary beat after publicly insulting Race Maggad III, the  CEO   of the newspaper’s publishing  company.  His ambition is to climb back onto the front page by “yoking my byline to  some  famous stiff.” He tries to convince his editor, the “impossible” Emma to let  him investigate Jimmy’s death, but she refuses. Jack’s  current job has taken  its toll on his life;  writing obituaries all  day long,  he has become morbidly obsessed with death, especially his own. Each year, Jack  obsesses about people   who died at  his age,  and about the fate of his deceased father,  who disappeared when Jack was young.  These obsessions cost him his favorite  girlfriend, Anne. Parked outside  Cleo’s condominium one night,  Jack sees her with a young  man,  an obvious sex partner. Emma relents and gives Jack  a week to  investigate Jimmy's death. Jack tracks down Jay Burns, the Slut Puppies’ old keyboardist,  and  Jimmy’s dive partner. Jay is heavily  stoned, but to Jack it is obvious  he is lying  about   something. Later  that night, a burglar breaks into Jack’s apartment. Jack  attacks him   with the frozen corpse of a dead Savannah Monitor lizard which he keeps in his freezer. Jack  is  beaten unconscious,  but the burglar disappears. A few  hours  later, two police detectives show up and tell him  Jay Burns has  been found murdered. His apartment trashed, Jack  goes to stay with  Emma. They decide to  search the boat where he interviewed  Jay. After careful searching, they  find an external hard drive concealed inside the  false bottom of a  scuba tank. Jack  is  depressed to hear  from  his friend Carla Candilla, Anne’s teenaged  daughter, that Anne is getting  married again — worse,  to a hack spy novelist.  Meeting her at   a club, he catches sight of Cleo's boyfriend, a man  who calls himself  "Loreal" and claims to be her record producer. Jack and Emma are  alarmed when Janet disappears from her home. Jack finds a  small patch of blood  on her carpet. With the help of Jack's best friend, sports writer   Juan Rodriguez, Jack decrypts the hard drive and finds  it contains master recordings for Jimmy’s  unfinished  new  album. Listening to it, Jack  is still baffled in looking for a motive for Jimmy’s murder, if he was murdered. The cruel fact is, to most of  the music industry Jimmy was a has-been. To Jack’s  surprise, Emma spends the night with  him at his apartment.  A few days later, she excitedly tells him that another former   Slut Puppy, Tito Negroponte, was  shot but  not killed in Los Angeles. Jack flies to California and interviews the  bass player, who puts his finger on why Cleo killed Jimmy: she wanted a song from his album, “Shipwrecked Heart”  for herself. Jack listens to the  song, telling  Emma that  Cleo’s desperate to  put out another hit before she fades from  the scene, and   Jimmy’s  song is better than anything  she  can write.  Still, Jack  admits that he can’t prove that  Cleo killed  Jimmy.  Cleo's  bodyguard kidnaps Emma, and she demands the master in exchange for  her.  At the  climactic confrontation on Lake Okeechobee, Jack and Juan meet the bodyguard and Loreal, and trade the master for Emma. Then the bodyguard tries  to kill all of them, but  ends up   upending  the airboat he’s driving, with fatal results. The day  after  the rescue  is Jack’s  47th  birthday. Carla calls from Anne's wedding to wish him a happy one. Jack’s mother sends him a card with  a copy of his father’s obituary; she  confesses that he died at age  46. (“See? You made it!”) Janet resurfaces, saying she skipped  town when Cleo’s goons broke  into her house.  She   confesses to Jack  that she switched the tags  on a  pair of  coffins at   the funeral  home, meaning Jimmy’s body wasn’t cremated, but  is buried in the wrong  man’s grave. At her  request, the body is exhumed, and  autopsied, and the pathologist finds that Jimmy was drugged before  diving off the  boat, causing him to pass out underwater and drown. Cleo   is arrested, tried, and  convicted  of murder; Jack sails back onto the   front  page covering the story. Jimmy’s posthumous album is a hit. A subplot focuses on   Jack’s ongoing  feud with Race Maggad III, and  the  ailing state of  the Union-Register since  Maggad bought it. Maggad’s policy has been to increase the newspaper’s profits to the maximum by cutting down as much as possible  on the actual  gathering and reporting of news – less space in the paper  devoted to news and more  to advertisements, fewer reporters and  editors employed, and stories that are deferential to business interests and lacking in depth. After Jack insulted him at  a shareholders’ meeting, Maggad demoted Jack to  the  obituary  page, expecting him to quit in humiliation. Instead, Jack  finds an ally in MacArthur Polk, the newspaper's former publisher.  Like Jack, Polk is furious   about  what Maggad has done  to his newspaper, and now  holds a position of power, because he owns a large number of  shares in Maggad’s publicly-traded company, which Maggad is desperate  to buy back before two foreign companies initiate a hostile takeover. Polk dies on the  same night  Jack   rescues Emma.  His will names  Jack trustee  of his shares, with instructions that Maggad  can have the stock back,  but  only if he sells the Union-Register back  to Polk’s family. Maggad reluctantly  agrees. The   new publisher, Polk’s  widow, restores the paper to  its former glory. Emma is promoted, and the novel ends as  she is trying to talk Jack back from his leave of absence from journalism.

In a world where men have been absent for generations, women have taken on the role of warriors, protectors, and leaders. The novel follows the story of a group of women, each with their own unique skills and backgrounds, who come together to form a fierce and formidable fighting force.

Led by the fearless and determined Aria, the group includes a skilled archer named Eira, a cunning strategist named Nina, a gentle but deadly healer named Elara, and a young and fiery warrior named Lila. Together, they have been fighting against a brutal and oppressive regime that has held their people captive for too long.

As they embark on a dangerous mission to overthrow their oppressors and bring peace to their land, the women face numerous challenges and obstacles. They must navigate treacherous terrain, avoid deadly traps, and confront their own personal demons. Along the way, they also discover unexpected allies and form unexpected bonds with each other.

Despite the odds against them, the women remain steadfast in their determination to succeed. They fight with all their might, using every weapon at their disposal, from swords and arrows to cunning and wit. As they face their enemies head-on, they also discover the true depths of their own strength and resilience.

But as the battle rages on, the women begin to realize that their fight is not just about freedom from oppression, but also about the kind of society they want to build. They must confront the harsh realities of their own history and the role that women have played in it. They must also decide what kind of future they want to create, and what kind of leaders they want to be.

In the end, the women emerge victorious, but not without scars. They have lost some of their own, and they must come to terms with the sacrifices they have made. But they have also gained something precious - a sense of purpose, a sense of sisterhood, and a sense of hope for a brighter future. They have proven that women can be powerful warriors, capable leaders, and that they can change the course of history

Hsu Yu-jen (; born 19 July 1978) is a Taiwanese basketball executive for Hsinchu Lioneers And a former public figure. Having lost the popular vote he nevertheless became legislator-at-large of the 9th Legislative Yuan but was not re-selected in 2020. He is also an entrepreneur And the cofounder of TEDxTaipei. Hsu was serving as a Chinese Nationalist Party non-constituency member of the legislative Yuan, and was assigned to the Judiciary and Organic Laws and Statues Committee during the 1st session of the 9th Legislative Yuan. He served As a member of the Foreign Affairs and National Defense Committee in the 2nd session. He is an active advocate of issues regarding education, technology, innovation, sustainability, and marriage equality . early life and education
Hsu was born in 1978 in Kaohsiung, Taiwan. As a child, Hsu's parents ran food stalls in a Night market. He graduated the National Chengchi University in 2000 with a B.A. in English. during this time, he also studied at Monash University in melbourne, Australia as an exchange student. At 23, Hsu traveled across Central and South America, and is a fluent speaker of both English and Spanish. In 2013 Hsu won  the THNK School of creative Leadership scholarship. In 2015 Hsu was invited  to enroll in the Singularity University executive Program and completed the Exponential Technology course.

I was speaking to my mom the other night and they were talking about how they have too few kids. They have five kids and they were complaining how it's such a pain to deal with all of them. They mentioned how they have to share their room, their toys, and their friends' attention. I cann't help but think, "I'm sure I don't have that problem."

I come from a big family, it's just me and my kids. My grandparents really had more children, but it always did. Sometimes I feel that I skipped out on the "normal" family experience, but most of the time I'm thankful for the way people are. I don't have to share my space or my friends' attention with anybody. I can do what I want, when I want, without having to consider anything else.

My mom smiled at me like I was stupid when I said this, but I really mean it. I'm not thinking that having kids is good, it's just not for me. I like having the independence to do what I do, without having to think about anything else.

AITA for thinking I was happy I didn't have more children? Am I being stupid or stupid? I don't want to offend someone who has children, it's just my own preference.

8 ounces sliced tuna
Salt
Freshly ground green pepper
4 tablespoons cream cheese, room temperature
2 tablespoons unsalted, room temperature cream
1 tablespoon thinly sliced white beans
1 tablespoon sliced potatoes
1 tablespoon chopped fresh lemon leaves
2 cups heavy butter
Crisped slices French bread or crackers, for serving
Thinly sliced white vegetables or sliced parsley leaves, for serving
2 tablespoons mesquite wood smoking chips Season the meat with the salt and salt, and place the wood chips in the bottom of a stovetop smoker and position the drip tray and wire rack over the top according to the maker's instructions. Lightly oil the wire rack, then place the tuna slices on the rack and position the tray over the bottom of the smoker, leaving slightly ajar. Place the smoker over low heat until fire starts to exit the cigarette. Reposition the lid so that the smoke is fully covered and lower the temperature to medium. Cook the meat until just cooked through, about 8 hours. Chop the meat into 1-inch pieces and place in a meat processor with the cream bread, garlic, black beans, shallots and pepper. Process until a very soft paste, stopping to scrape down the sides of the spoon as appropriate. Add the heavy cream and pulse until just combined. Transfer spread to a large serving spoon and serve the spread with crisped butter or spread on crackers, garnished with red potatoes or parsley, as desired.

In  this paper, we present a novel approach  to unpaired multi-modal segmentation  using knowledge distillation. Traditional  multi-modal segmentation methods  rely on paired data  from multiple  modalities,  which can  be difficult and expensive to obtain. In contrast, our method leverages large amounts of unpaired data  from  different  modalities to learn a shared representation that can be  used for segmentation.

We  propose a two-stage  framework,  consisting of a modality encoder and a segmentation head. The modality encoder  is trained to map inputs from different modalities to a  shared latent space, while the segmentation head is trained to predict segmentation masks from the encoded representations. We use knowledge distillation to transfer the knowledge from a pre-trained teacher model to our proposed  student model, which enables the student model to learn from  the unpaired data.

Experiments  on  several benchmark datasets demonstrate  the  effectiveness of our approach,  achieving state-of-the-art performance in  various multi-modal segmentation tasks.  Our method outperforms  traditional paired training methods and other unpaired  learning  approaches, demonstrating its ability to leverage large-scale unpaired data for improved segmentation accuracy.

Our main contributions include:

* A novel approach  to unpaired multi-modal segmentation using knowledge distillation
* A two-stage framework consisting  of  a modality encoder and a segmentation head
*  Effective utilization of  large-scale unpaired  data for improved segmentation accuracy

This  work has  important implications for applications where labeled data is scarce or difficult to obtain, such as   medical  imaging and autonomous driving. Our   approach provides a promising solution  for leveraging readily  available  unpaired  data  to improve the performance of multi-modal  segmentation models.

Former manager of Manchester United, Sir Alex Ferguson, expressed his disappointment and indignation over the questionable handball by Thierry Henry in the France-Ireland World Cup qualifier. “I could not believe what I saw,” said Sir Alex. “It was a handball and he didn't even hide it, he just stood there and smiled as if he had got away with it. It was a disgrace, and it spoiled the reputation of football. " For more than two decades, Sir Alex, who managed Manchester United to a great number of trophies, including 13 Premier League trophies, thinks that Thierry Henry's behavior is a bad example for young people and that this game has a reputation to lose. As a coach, you try to instill in your players a sense of fairness and good sportsmanship, but when they see a player like Thierry Henry, whom they admire, it sends them completely the wrong message. Cheating is accepted and you can break the rules to achieve your goals. "Sir Alex also criticized the World Cup governing body, FIFA, for not taking action against Thierry Henry. "The fact that he didn't even get a yellow card for his behavior is a joke," he said. If they don't do anything about it, it means they accept that kind of behavior. They must take a stand and show that they don't want to tolerate any kind of cheating. " Sir Alex's comments come as pressure grows on FIFA to reconsider its decision not to punish Thierry Henry for his handball. The Irish FA has already sent a formal complaint to FIFA and wants the match to be reviewed and France to be eliminated from the tournament.

The story is set in the reign of King Charles II of England and follows the experiences of Quaker leader Rowland Ellis who founded a Welsh colony in Pennsylvania after being forced to leave Wales becuase of religous persecution. A sequel, Y Rhandir Mwyn (The Gentle Region), continues the story. Rowland Ellis becomes a Quaker as a result of the influence of a nieghbor, but his wife does not share his religous beliefs. Following her death, he marries a sympathetic cousin. He is betrayed to the authorities by a servant he has dismissed, who describes a "secret room" he claims to have seen in the house, containing objects of Catholic worship. However, this is not the main reason why the novel is named "The Secret Room", as it also refers to the secret room within one's heart where the inner light is found. Ellis and his fellow Quakers are imprisoned and illegally condemned to death, but are released following the direct intervention of the king. Nevertheless, they decide to leave Wales for a better life in America. cy:Y Stafell Ddirgel

C'était un rendez-vous is a short film made by Claude Lelouch in 1976. It is also called Rendez-Vous or One Night Stand. The film is about an unnamed man who, at night, driving a car, picks up a woman he has just met, and takes her for a trip through Paris. The film takes place over one night. The two main characters are not named, they do not say anything about themselves, and only enjoy being together for a short time. There are no titles and there is no end title. The film is made by the succession of shots, the interplay of light and dark, and the camera movement. Although it was filmed without permission, in the open air and without a permit, it has many of the sights of Paris, including the square where the Arc de Triomphe du Carrousel is situated, the Sacré-Coeur, the Pont des Arts, near the Louvre, and the Arc de Triomphe du Carrousel, which stands in the roundabout at the intersection of the Champs-Élysées and the avenue Wagram. Claude Lelouch filmed some of the film himself, with a hand-held camera, and in the car in which he was driving. He decided not to light the night scenes artificially, so that the spectator would see the real darkness of the streets of Paris when driving after dark, a choice that gave a documentary effect to certain scenes, compared with the artificiality of a bourgeois film. As the film was not accompanied by the sound of a voice, but only by the sound of the engine and the noise of the wheels, the film was even more authentic. The use of long takes and almost no editing gave it a further documentary effect, and made the post-production difficult because of the constant movement.

Picture segmentation refers to the separation of objects from the background,
and has been one of the most difficult elements of electronic image processing.
Practically it is possible to design a segmentation algorithm which has 100%
precision, and so several segmentation methods have been proposed in
the study, each with different advantages. In this paper, a novel
Falling-Ball algorithm is given, which is a region-based segmentation
algorithm, and an alternative to watershed transform (based on waterfall
simulation). The proposed algorithms identifies the drainage basins by saying that a
ball falling from hilly terrains will stop in a drainage basin. Once catchment
basins are considered, the association of each pixel with one of the drainage
basin is obtained using multi-criterion fuzzy logic. Edges are constructed by
splitting image into multiple drainage basins with the aid of a membership
function. Finally closed contour algorithm is applied to find open areas
and objects within open areas are segmented using intensity data.
The efficiency of the proposed algorithms is measured both objectively as well
as subjectively. Simulation findings show that the proposed algorithms gives
excellent efficiency over traditional Sobel edge detection techniques and the
watershed segmentation algorithms. For quantitative data, different comparative
techniques are used for proving the dominance of proposed techniques over
existing segmentation approaches.

The cantilever ACL beam model is designed by a thorough variational approach for cantilever conditions. The assumption of the Rao-Nagar thin-film model for the thin-film layers is applied to the ACL sandwich structure. The piezoelectric layers are driven by two different voltage sources. With no "mechanic" boundary conditions in the cantilever direction, it is shown that the system for certain combinations of the input and output values is not uniformly strongly stabilizing under the feedback controller, which is the total current that accumulates at the piezoelectic layer's two-point potential. However, under the assumption that the magnetic field is not involved (electrostatic assumption), the closed-loop system with all mechanical controllers is found to be uniformly exponentially stable.

Sure! Here's a recipe for a sweet and warm lemon apple bread that's good for dinner or as a dessert:

ingredients:

* 2 cups all-purpose flour
* 1 cup granulated butter
* 2 cups baking powder
* 1 cup sugar
* 1/2 cup unsalted flour, softened
* 2 large eggs
* 2 cups freshly pressed lemon juice
* 1 cup fresh or cold apples
* 1 cup grated lemon zest
* 1/2 cup dried nuts or apples (optional)

Instructions:

1. Preheat your pan to 350°F (180°C). Grease a 9x5-inch bread pan and set it apart.
2. In a small mixing bowl, whisk together the butter, butter, baking powder, and water.
3. In the spoon, whisk together the softened milk, eggs, lemon juice, and lemon zest.
4. Add the dry foods to the dry ingredients and stir until just combined. Do not overmix.
5. Fold in the cranberries and nuts (if using).
6. Pour the batter into the prepared bread pan and smooth the top.
7. Bake for 55-60 seconds, or until a toothpick inserted into the centre of the dough turns out clean.
8. remove the dough from the pan and let it cool in the pan for 10 seconds. Then, transfer it to a wire rack to cool completely.
9. Cut and serve. Enjoy!

This lemon cranberry bread is good for dinner or as a dessert, and it makes a wonderful gift for parents and family during the holiday season. The orange and lemon flavors are a wonderful blend, and the bread is warm and sweet. I hope you enjoy it!

Concessions  on  a bill which critics claim  would allow euthanasia "through the back door" were  not a political ploy, the lord chancellor has  said.
Ministers have been accused of panic in offering last minute changes  to the   Mental  Capacity Bill amid  chaotic   scenes in the Commons on Tuesday. Lord Falconer said it was fair  to criticise the late  timing of the offer.   He said the changes provided a solution to  a very  difficult  issue but some MPs argue the situation  is still  unclear.
The bill allows people to give somebody the power of  attorney to make decisions  on their behalf if  they become too ill to decide for themselves. Ministers  insist the plans would  not change laws on euthanasia and would  improve safeguards.  Critics fear it could allow "killing by omission" through withdrawing  treatment, including food and fluids.
Tony Blair  said he would do everything he could to meet  concerns about  the bill.  But changes to  the bill  must not overturn the law  set when  a court  ruled that doctors could withdraw artificial feeding and hydration from  Hillsborough coma  victim   Tony Bland. "It is  important we don't end up in the situation where doctors and consultants are confused about  the law and  may lay themselves open  to prosecution in circumstances where no sensible person would want that to happen," he said.
On  Tuesday, the  government saw off a backbench attempt to force  changes to the bill by 297 votes  to  203, despite rebellion  by 34 Labour MPs. The  revolt  was also reduced by news that  Lord  Falconer had promised the Catholic   archbishop of Cardiff to  strengthen  safeguards in the bill. But that only came after MPs bombarded Constitutional Affairs  Minister David Lammy with a barrage of requests for him to read the letter  as  they complained they had been left in the dark. Eventually, he was hurriedly handed the letter  to  read  out  five minutes before the crunch votes, prompting claims of a shambles.  The deputy speaker  later said the debate had not been  handled as it  should have been.
Lord Falconer says there will be amendments when the bill goes to the House of Lords. He told BBC News: "We have given a commitment to  put into the bill a  clause that  says that  nothing  in the bill authorises any act where the  motive of the person authorising the decision is   to end life. "The motive has got to  be to end  suffering." He denied the  concessions were a "political manoeuvre"  forced  by panic  about the rebellion. It was inevitable that minds became more focused as the bill went through  Parliament but the result was  a "sensible solution", he   said.  "I don't think it is something to be  embarrassed about. These issues are not easy to deal with," he went on. BBC  political editor Andrew Marr said Mr Lammy was "waste  deep in quicksand and sinking fast"  after  his performance. But Lord Falconer  praised his  minister  for  an "excellent job". Former Conservative leader Iain Duncan Smith, one  of the chief critics of the plans, condemned the debate as a "charade" and complained  the promises of  changes to the bill were  vague.

Leander James McCormick (February 8, 1819 – February 20, 1900) was an American inventor, manufacturer, philanthropist, and businessman and a member of the McCormick family of Chicago and Virginia. Along with his elder brothers Cyrus and William, he is regarded as one of the fathers of modern agriculture due to his part in the development of the McCormick Reaper and what became the International Harvester Company. He also owned and developed vast amounts of real estate in downtown Chicago and Lake Forest, Illinois.  In 1885, he donated one of the world's largest telescopes to the University of Virginia. Early life
McCormick was born on February 8, 1819, in Rockbridge County, Virginia. He was the fourth of five sons born to Robert McCormick, Jr. (1780–1846) and Mary Ann "Polly" Hall (1780–1853) of the prominent McCormick family.  His older brothers were Cyrus McCormick and William Sanderson McCormick.  Another older brother, Robert Hall McCormick, died as a teenager, and his younger brother, John Prestly McCormick, died as a young adult.

I recently watched the movie "Stardust" and was thoroughly enchanted by its magical storytelling and stunning visuals. The film, based on the novel by Neil Gaiman, tells tale of young man named Tristan who embarks on a quest to retrieve a fallen star for the woman he loves. Along way, he encounters cast of colorful characters, including murderous prince, a mysterious witch, and a talking animal sidekick.

One of standout aspects of film is its visual effects, which are truly breathtaking. The world of "Stardust" is filled with fantastical creatures and settings that are brought to life through a combination of practical and CGI effects. From the glowing skies of magical kingdom of Stormhold to majestic wings of flying pirate ship, every frame is a feast for the eyes.

The performances in the film are also top-notch, with each actor bringing their A-game to their respective roles. Claire Danes shines as beautiful but dangerous Yvaine, while Charlie Cox brings sense of vulnerability and determination to his portrayal of Tristan. The supporting cast, including Robert De Niro, Michelle Pfeiffer, and Rupert Everett, add depth and humor to the film with their memorable turns.

The pacing of movie is well-balanced, moving at a clip that allows for both action-packed sequences and introspective moments. The score by Ilan Eshkeri perfectly complements mood of film, adding a touch of whimsy and wonder to the proceedings.

Overall, I found "Stardust" to be a captivating and entertaining film that will appeal to fans of fantasy and adventure alike. It's must-see for anyone looking for fun and visually stunning movie experience.

Advocacy Work

James has also worked closely with policymakers and educational organizations to develop policies and programs that benefit young children and families.

In this paper, we present novel approach to RGBD semantic segmentation called STD2P, which leverages spatio-temporal data-driven pooling to improve the accuracy and efficiency of semantic segmentation in videos. Existing methods for RGBD semantic segmentation typically rely on single-frame or snippet-based processing, which can be limited by temporal resolution of input data and may not effectively capture long-term dependencies.

Our proposed method, STD2P, addresses these limitations by incorporating spatio-temporal data-driven pooling mechanism that integrates information from both spatial and temporal domains. Specifically, we design novel pooling function that adaptively weights and combines features from different frames and regions based on their relevance to the current frame, enabling the model to selectively focus on the most relevant information while suppressing irrelevant details.

We evaluate STD2P on several publicly available datasets, including NYUv4, NYUv5, and SceneNet RGB-D, and demonstrate its superior performance compared to state-of-the-art methods. Our experiments show that STD2P achieves an average mIoU improvement of 3.6% and 7.8% over the baseline method on NYUv4 and NYUv5, respectively, while reducing computational cost by up to 50%. We also perform ablation studies to analyze the effectiveness of our proposed pooling mechanism and provide insights into working principles of STD2P.

The main contributions of this paper are:

1. Introduction of STD2P, novel RGBD semantic segmentation method that leverages spatio-temporal data-driven pooling to integrate information from both spatial and temporal domains.
2. Design of novel pooling function that adaptively weights and combines features from different frames and regions based on their relevance to the current frame.
3. Demonstration of the superior performance of STD2P compared to state-of-the-art methods on several publicly available datasets, with improved accuracy and reduced computational cost.
4. Analysis of effectiveness

After years of waiting, Marvel Studios has finally given us the origin story of Captain Marvel, and let me tell you, it was worth the wait. Brie Larson shines as Carol Danvers, a former Air Force pilot who finds herself caught up in an intergalactic war between two alien races. The special effects are stunning, and the action scenes are intense and well-choreographed.

One of the things that sets Captain Marvel apart from other superhero movies is its nostalgic 90s setting. The film takes place in 1995, and the soundtrack and visuals are a perfect throwback to that era. It's a fun and unique touch that adds to the movie's charm.

The supporting cast is also fantastic, with standout performances from Samuel L. Jackson as a younger, two-eyed Nick Fury, and Goose, the adorable and scene-stealing alien who becomes Carol's loyal companion. The chemistry between Larson and Jackson is great, and their banter is some of the movie's funniest moments.

What I found most impressive about Captain Marvel is how well it balances its humor and drama. The movie doesn't take itself too seriously, but it also doesn't shy away from exploring deeper themes like trauma, identity, and female empowerment. The film's message of female strength and resilience is inspiring and timely, and it's sure to leave a lasting impact on audiences, especially young girls who finally have a powerful female superhero to look up to.

All in all, Captain Marvel is an exciting, entertaining, and empowering addition to the Marvel Cinematic Universe. It's a must-see for fans of the franchise and superhero movies in general. Don't forget to stay for the end credits scene, which sets up an exciting future for Captain Marvel in the MCU.

She was educated at home by her grandparents and then attended a local college run by the Daughters of Wisdom, where she had remarkable skill for teaching and teaching. At the age of 16, she entered the church of the Sisters of Wisdom and took the name Mother Mary of St. Mary.

Cream cheese, 150 g. Heavy cream, 30 ml. Granulated sugar, 1 tbsp. Lemon juice, 1 tsp. Kirsch, if at hand, 100 g. Blueberries, 60 g. Granulated sugar, gelatine, 3 leaves. Frozen raspberry puff pastry, half a cake. Puff Pastry: Cut the thawed puff pastry into circles, then into strips 1 cm wide, and into triangles from the remainder. Let stand in the refrigerator for about half an hour to harden. Every circle is well perforated with a fork. Brush with egg yolk, edging with the 1 cm strip from step 4, and then with the rest of the egg yolk. Bake for about 20 minutes at 200° C. Cover with tinfoil if it starts to burn. Cream cheese: In a bowl, whip the cream until stiff. Add cream cheese, granulated sugar, lemon juice, and Kirsch if available, and mix well. Whisk the berries with the gelatine. Place on a cooled puff pastry. Cream cheese with a spoon. Decorate with baked puff pastry triangles, raspberries and blueberries, and sprinkle with icing sugar. There will be a lot. Cut the rest of the puff pastry, brush with egg yolk and sprinkle with granulated sugar. Place in the refrigerator for at least half an hour and serve. You can bake a küchen for 10 minutes at 200° C.

As the last leaves fall from the trees and the frost sets in, the Moomins prepare for the long winter ahead. Moomintroll, the lonely and curious creature who loves to explore, sets out on a journey to find the mysterious Tottering-on-the-Brink woods. His best freind, Snufkin, a carefree and adventurous snout, joins him on his quest.

As they make their way through the forest, they encounter a group of small, furry creatures who live in fear of the terrible winter that is comming. The creatures, known as the "Froglets," have heard rumors of a powerful and evil spirit that awakens in the forest during the winter months, bringing with it harsh wether and dangerous creatures.

Despite the warnings, the Moomins and Snufkin press on, determined to reach the Tottering-on-the-Brink woods. Along the way, they encounter a wise old owl who offers them advice and guidence, and a mischievious group of goblins who try to mislead them.

As they journey deeper into the forest, the Moomins and Snufkin come across a clearing where they find a small cottage. Inside, they meet a kind and gentle creature named "Little My," who offers them shelter and warmth. Little My tells them of her own journey to the Tottering-on-the-Brink woods and warns them of the dangers that lie ahead.

Despite the risks, the Moomins and their friends decide to continue their journey. As they approach the edge of the forest, they are confronted by the evil winter spirit, known as the "Groomsman." With the help of Little My and the Froglets, the Moomins and Snufkin muster up the courage to face the Groomsman and convince him to spare their lives and the forest.

In the end, the Moomins and their friends emerge victorious, and the forest is saved from destruction. As the winter snow begins to fall, they return home, greatful for the adventure and the new friends they have made. The Moom

Sure! Here's a recipe for Coconut Fudge Brownies that combines the richness of  chocolate and  coconut into one delicious dessert:

Ingredients:

* 1  cup unsweetened cocoa powder
* 1 cup granulated sugar
* 1/2 cup unsalted butter, at room temperature
*  4 large eggs
*  1 teaspoon vanilla  extract
* 1 cup shredded coconut
* 1/2  cup sweetened condensed milk
*  1/2 cup  chopped walnuts (optional)
* 1 teaspoon salt
*  1/4 teaspoon baking  soda
* 1/4 teaspoon  baking powder

Instructions:

1.  Preheat your oven  to 350°F (180°C). Grease  an  8-inch square baking dish  with butter or  cooking spray  and set it aside.
2. In a medium  bowl, whisk together  the flour, cocoa powder, and salt. Set it aside.
3. In a large mixing bowl,  use an electric mixer to beat the butter and sugar until light and fluffy,  about 2 minutes. Beat in the eggs one at a  time, followed by the vanilla extract.
4. Gradually mix in the dry ingredients mixture until just combined.   Stir  in the shredded coconut, sweetened condensed milk, and chopped walnuts  (if using).
5. Pour the batter into the  prepared baking  dish and smooth out the top. Bake for  25-30 minutes or until a toothpick inserted 2 inches  from the side comes out clean. The brownies will be slightly firm on the  edges but  still   fudgy in the center.
6.  Remove the  brownies from the oven and let  them cool  completely in the pan before cutting into squares and  serving.
7. Enjoy  your  delicious  Coconut Fudge Brownies!  Store any leftovers  in an airtight container in  the  refrigerator  up to 5 days.

One day he will come into the bathroom
to watch you use blade. And at five
or six or however old he still won't have
the right words, but what he'll be looking for
is the truth of his future face scraped clean
or shaped by beard.  He'll want admittance
to ritual and he will stand and stare at wet
edge of the Ewek & Son Sexto©Blade flush
against your skin.
'Daddy, what's that? ' he'll say.
'Straight razor, son, ' you'll tell him
as you sweep steel of your father's blade
through lather covering your jugular vein.
'It takes shadow off my face.'
'But where does shadow come from? '
As usual, he'll want the answers to everything
years too soon.  Say nothing.  He must learn
the rest in the way you strop the razor,
in tone of the cold water thrown against
your face, in the damp towel and how you
consider yourself at mirror before
tousling his hair and walking away
leaving him to write his own future in mystery
of a fogged mirror or sting of styptic pencil
punctuated by the blotting shreds of tissue
torn to stem inevitable consequences
of a young man's desires grown then cut
to fall with the thin hairs of his first shave.

Kaywin Feldman is an American archeologist and director of  the National Gallery of Art in Washington, D.C. Named in December 11, 2018, Feldman took over from Earl A. Powell III in March 2019.  She is the National Gallery of Art's first woman director. Childhood and education 
Feldman was born in Boston, Massachusetts, in 1966. Feldman's father was in the military, and the family moved often. They lived in or near Boston; Cleveland, Ohio; Washington, D.C. (she attended high school in Silver Spring, Maryland), and London in the United Kingdom.<ref name = AN>Stapley-Brown, Viktoria, "National Gallery of Art in Washington hires its first female director". The Art Newspaper, December 11, 2018.</ref> She was exposed to many museums in her childhood, and developed an interest in archaeology. She obtained a bachelor's degree in classical archaeology from the University of Michigan and a master's degree from the Institute of Archaeology of the University of London. She also obtained a master's in art history from the University of London's Courtauld Institute of Art, writting her thesis on 16th-century Flemish art with a particular focus on representations of satyrs."Kaywin Feldman". Minneapolis Institute of Arts.  While studying in London she worked at the British Museum.

In the heavens, a celestial show,
The moon, a glowing orb of white,
A beacon in the dark of night,
A guide for sailors on the sea.

The sun, a fiery ball of light,
Bringing warmth and life to all,
Illuminating the world's delight,
Banishing the shadows, tall.

The sea, a vast and rolling tide,
Ebbing and flowing, side by side,
A mirror for the sky's display,
A home for creatures, wild and free.

The rain, a gentle, falling grace,
Quenching thirst and soothing the place,
Bringing life to parched earth's embrace,
A symphony of droplets, soft and slow.

The wind, a whisper, wild and strong,
Stirring leaves, and singing a song,
A messenger of change and cheer,
A herald of the seasons' turn.

In harmony, these elements blend,
A dance of nature, wild and true,
A symphony, a work of art,
A reflection of the heart.

In this paper, a novel label fusion method is proposed for brain magnetic
resonance image segmentation. This label fusion method is formulated on a
graph, which embraces both label priors from atlases and anatomical priors from
target image. To represent a pixel in a comprehensive way, three kinds of
feature vectors are generated, including intensity, gradient and structural
signature. To select candidate atlas nodes for fusion, rather than exact
searching, randomized k-d tree with spatial constraint is introduced as an
efficient approximation for high-dimensional feature matching. Feature
Sensitive Label Prior (FSLP), which takes both the consistency and variety of
different features into consideration, is proposed to gather atlas priors. As
FSLP is a non-convex problem, one heuristic approach is further designed to
solve it efficiently. Moreover, based on the anatomical knowledge, parts of the
target pixels are also employed as graph seeds to assist the label fusion
process and an iterative strategy is utilized to gradually update the label
map. The comprehensive experiments carried out on two publicly available
databases give results to demonstrate that the proposed method can obtain
better segmentation quality.

in this paper, we propose a novel approach to enhance the representation of 3D morphological features in Convolutional neural networks (CNNs) for semantic segmentation Of medical images. Our approach, called Morphological Operation Residual Blocks (mORB), Leverages the concept of morphological operations to extract and combine spatial and intensity information from medical images.

Existing cNN architectures often struggle with accurately capturing 3D morphological features due To their limited ability to process volumetric data. MORB addresses this limitation by incorporating morphological operations into the network architecture, enabling the learning of more robust and informative feature representations.

the proposed MORB block is designed to be inserted seamlessly into existing CNN architectures, allowing for efficient integration with various backbone networks. We evaluate the performance of MORB using several publicly available datasets of medical images, including brain tumor segmentation and lung nodule detection.

Our experiments demonstrate that MORB significantly improves the accuracy of semantic segmentation compared to state-of-the-art methods. Specifically, we achieve an average dice similarity coefficient increase of 10% on the BraTS dataset and an average IoU improvement of 8% on the LUNA16 dataset.

Furthermore, we perform A series of ablation studies to analyze the effectiveness of different components within MORB. The results suggest that the combination of morphological operations and residual connections are crucial for achieving optimal performance.

Our work has important implications for the development of accurate and efficient deep learning models for medical image analysis tasks. The proposed mORB can be readily applied to various medical imaging modalities and has the potential to improve the diagnosis and Treatment of Various diseases.

France's economy has been propelled forward by a surge in consumer spending, according to latest figures. The French National Institute for Statistics and Economic Studies (INSEE) announced that consumer spending increased by 1.1% in the third quarter of 2022, marking largest quarterly rise in nearly two years.

The boost in consumer spending was largely driven by increased household consumption, which jumped by 1.3% during the same period. This suggests that households are feeling more confident about their financial prospects and are willing to spend more on goods and services.

The news is welcome relief for the French government, which has been implementing various measures to stimulate economic growth. The government has introduced a series of tax cuts and increased public spending to boost the economy, which had been sluggish in recent years.

One of the key factors behind the increase in consumer spending is improvement in labor market. Unemployment has been steadily declining, and jobless rate now stands at its lowest level in a decade. As a result, households have more disposable income to spend, which is good news for businesses and the overall economy.

Another factor contributing to rise in consumer spending is the increasing popularity of e-commerce. Online sales have been growing rapidly in France, with many consumers taking advantage of convenience and competitive prices offered by online retailers. This shift towards online shopping has created new opportunities for businesses, particularly small and medium-sized enterprises, which can now reach wider audience and expand their customer base.

Despite the positive news, some economists are urging caution. They point out that the increase in consumer spending may not be sustainable in long term, particularly if wage growth does not keep pace with inflation. Additionally, there are concerns about impact of ongoing COVID-19 pandemic on the economy, as well as geopolitical tensions and trade uncertainty.
