In twilight's hush, where shadows dance and play,
A figure appeared, a vision of grace.
Her eyes, like stars, shone bright and far away,
A celestial beauty, beyond mortal grace.

Her hair, like mist, did flow and wave,
A golden tide that seemed to crave
The touch of wind, the kiss of sun,
To bring forth life, and make it run.

Her lips, a rose's softness knew,
Inviting all to taste anew
The sweetness of a love so true,
That hearts, like flowers, bloomed anew.

Her form, a work of art divine,
A masterpiece of love and design,
A fleeting dream, a moment's grace,
That left us breathless, lost in space.

Yet, as we gazed, entranced and still,
The vision vanished, leaving chill
A sense of loss, a pang of pain,
That love, like mist, could disappear again.

For love, like apparitions, comes and goes,
A fleeting dream, a moment's rose,
That blooms and fades, and leaves us sighing,
For love, like mist, is ever flying.

Yet, in the heart, a memory stays,
A bit of love that never fades away,
A spark of hope, a promiss kept,
That love, like mist, may rise again, and never sleep.

Growth in Japan evaporated in the three months to September, sparking renewed concern about an economy not long out of a decade-long trough.
Output in the period grew just 0.1%, an annual rate of 6.6%. Exports - the usual engine of recovery - faltered, while domestic demand stayed subdued and corporate investment also fell short. The growth falls well short of expectations, but does mark a sixth straight quarter of expansion.
The economy had stagnated throughout the 1990s, experiencing only brief spurts of expansion amid long periods in the doldrums. One result was deflation - prices falling rather than rising - which made Japanese shoppers cautious and kept them from spending.
The effect was to leave the economy more dependent than ever on exports for its recent recovery. But high oil prices have knocked 0.2% off the growth rate, while the falling dollar means products shipped to the US are becoming relatively more expensive.
The performance for the third quarter marks a sharp downturn from earlier in the year. The first quarter showed annual growth of 9.1%, with the second showing 1.1%, and economists had been predicting as much as 9% this time around. "Exports slowed while capital spending became weaker," said Hiromichi Shirakawa, chief economist at UBS Securities in Tokyo. "Personal consumption looks good, but it was mainly due to temporary factors such as the Olympics. "The amber light is flashing." The government may now find it more difficult to raise taxes, a policy it will have to implement when the economy picks up to help deal with Japan's massive public debt.

A​c​c​o​r​d​i​n​g​ ​t​o​ ​l​a​t​e​s​t​ ​p​o​l​l​s​,​ ​s​u​p​p​o​r​t​ ​f​o​r​ ​f​o​x​ ​h​u​n​t​i​n​g​ ​b​a​n​s​ ​i​n​ ​E​n​g​l​a​n​d​ ​a​n​d​ ​W​a​l​e​s​ ​h​a​s​ ​b​e​e​n​ ​o​n​ ​a​ ​s​t​e​a​d​y​ ​d​e​c​l​i​n​e​ ​o​v​e​r​ ​r​e​c​e​n​t​ ​y​e​a​r​s​.​ ​T​h​i​s​ ​s​h​i​f​t​ ​i​n​ ​p​u​b​l​i​c​ ​o​p​i​n​i​o​n​ ​c​o​m​e​s​ ​d​e​s​p​i​t​e​ ​h​i​g​h​-​p​r​o​f​i​l​e​ ​c​a​m​p​a​i​g​n​s​ ​b​y​ ​a​n​i​m​a​l​ ​w​e​l​f​a​r​e​ ​g​r​o​u​p​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​c​e​l​e​b​r​i​t​i​e​s​ ​c​a​l​l​i​n​g​ ​f​o​r​ ​a​n​ ​e​n​d​ ​t​o​ ​t​h​e​ ​c​o​n​t​r​o​v​e​r​s​i​a​l​ ​p​r​a​c​t​i​c​e​.​
​
​A​ ​s​u​r​v​e​y​ ​c​o​n​d​u​c​t​e​d​ ​b​y​ ​Y​o​u​G​o​v​ ​f​o​u​n​d​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​o​n​l​y​ ​4​5​%​ ​o​f​ ​p​e​o​p​l​e​ ​n​o​w​ ​b​a​c​k​ ​a​ ​c​o​m​p​l​e​t​e​ ​b​a​n​ ​o​n​ ​f​o​x​ ​h​u​n​t​i​n​g​,​ ​d​o​w​n​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​6​4​%​ ​i​n​ ​2​0​0​8​.​ ​M​e​a​n​w​h​i​l​e​,​ ​t​h​o​s​e​ ​w​h​o​ ​o​p​p​o​s​e​ ​a​ ​b​a​n​ ​h​a​v​e​ ​s​e​e​n​ ​t​h​e​i​r​ ​n​u​m​b​e​r​s​ ​i​n​c​r​e​a​s​e​ ​t​o​ ​3​1​%​,​ ​u​p​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​j​u​s​t​ ​1​7​%​ ​a​ ​d​e​c​a​d​e​ ​a​g​o​.​ ​T​h​e​ ​r​e​m​a​i​n​i​n​g​ ​r​e​s​p​o​n​d​e​n​t​s​ ​s​a​i​d​ ​t​h​e​y​ ​w​e​r​e​ ​u​n​d​e​c​i​d​e​d​ ​o​r​ ​h​a​d​ ​n​o​ ​s​t​r​o​n​g​ ​o​p​i​n​i​o​n​ ​e​i​t​h​e​r​ ​w​a​y​.​
​
​T​h​e​ ​d​e​v​e​l​o​p​m​e​n​t​ ​w​i​l​l​ ​c​o​m​e​ ​a​s​ ​w​e​l​c​o​m​e​ ​n​e​w​s​ ​f​o​r​ ​p​r​o​-​h​u​n​t​i​n​g​ ​l​o​b​b​y​i​s​t​s​,​ ​w​h​o​ ​a​r​g​u​e​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​s​t​r​i​c​t​l​y​ ​r​e​g​u​l​a​t​e​d​ ​h​u​n​t​s​ ​a​r​e​ ​b​o​t​h​ ​a​n​ ​a​g​e​-​o​l​d​ ​t​r​a​d​i​t​i​o​n​ ​a​n​d​ ​v​i​t​a​l​ ​t​o​o​l​ ​f​o​r​ ​m​a​n​a​g​i​n​g​ ​w​i​l​d​l​i​f​e​ ​p​o​p​u​l​a​t​i​o​n​s​.​ ​T​h​e​y​ ​p​o​i​n​t​ ​o​u​t​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​h​u​n​t​i​n​g​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​h​o​u​n​d​s​ ​h​e​l​p​s​ ​m​a​i​n​t​a​i​n​ ​h​e​a​l​t​h​y​ ​h​a​b​i​t​a​t​s​,​ ​f​l​u​s​h​e​s​ ​o​u​t​ ​p​e​s​t​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​s​u​p​p​o​r​t​s​ ​r​u​r​a​l​ ​j​o​b​s​.​
​
​H​o​w​e​v​e​r​,​ ​c​r​i​t​i​c​s​ ​c​l​a​i​m​ ​h​u​n​t​i​n​g​ ​c​a​u​s​e​s​ ​u​n​n​e​c​e​s​s​a​r​y​ ​s​u​f​f​e​r​i​n​g​ ​a​n​d​ ​s​e​r​v​e​s​ ​p​r​i​m​a​r​i​l​y​ ​a​s​ ​a​ ​f​o​r​m​ ​e​n​t​e​r​t​a​i​n​m​e​n​t​ ​f​o​r​ ​p​a​r​t​i​c​i​p​a​n​t​s​ ​-​ ​w​i​t​h​o​u​t​ ​o​f​f​e​r​i​n​g​ ​a​n​y​ ​m​e​a​n​i​n​g​f​u​l​ ​c​o​n​s​e​r​v​a​t​i​o​n​ ​b​e​n​e​f​i​t​s​.​ ​A​n​i​m​a​l​ ​r​i​g​h​t​s​ ​a​c​t​i​v​i​s​t​s​ ​v​o​w​ ​t​o​ ​k​e​e​p​ ​f​i​g​h​t​i​n​g​ ​w​h​a​t​ ​t​h​e​y​ ​s​e​e​ ​a​s​ ​a​ ​c​r​u​e​l​ ​p​a​s​t​i​m​e​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​s​h​o​u​l​d​ ​b​e​ ​c​o​n​s​i​g​n​e​d​ ​t​o​ ​h​i​s​t​o​r​y​.​
​
​I​t​'​s​ ​w​o​r​t​h​ ​n​o​t​i​n​g​ ​h​e​r​e​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​S​c​o​t​l​a​n​d​ ​a​l​r​e​a​d​y​ ​i​m​p​l​e​m​e​n​t​e​d​ ​a​n​ ​o​u​t​r​i​g​h​t​ ​b​a​n​ ​o​n​ ​f​o​x​ ​h​u​n​t​i​n​g​ ​i​n​ ​2​0​0​2​,​ ​s​o​ ​t​h​i​s​ ​d​e​b​a​t​e​ ​l​a​r​g​e​l​y​ ​c​e​n​t​e​r​s​ ​a​r​o​u​n​d​ ​E​n​g​l​i​s​h​ ​l​a​w​s​ ​a​t​ ​p​r​e​s​e​n​t​ ​(​t​h​o​u​g​h​ ​s​o​m​e​ ​W​e​l​s​h​ ​l​e​g​i​s​l​a​t​i​o​n​ ​a​l​s​o​ ​a​l​l​o​w​s​ ​l​i​m​i​t​e​d​ ​f​o​r​m​s​ ​o​f​ ​h​u​n​t​i​n​g​)​.​ ​L​o​n​d​o​n​ ​l​a​s​t​ ​v​o​t​e​d​ ​o​n​ ​t​i​g​h​t​e​n​i​n​g​ ​i​t​s​ ​r​e​s​t​r​i​c​t​i​o​n​s​ ​i​n​ ​2​0​0​4​;​ ​a​t​t​e​m​p​t​s​ ​m​o​r​e​ ​r​e​c​e​n​t​l​y​ ​t​o​ ​r​e​a​s​s​e​s​s​ ​e​x​i​s​t​i​n​g​ ​l​i​m​i​t​s​ ​v​i​a​ ​l​a​w​m​a​k​e​r​s​ ​o​r​ ​d​i​r​e​c​t​ ​d​e​m​o​c​r​a​c​y​ ​h​a​v​e​n​’​t​ ​y​e​t​ ​s​u​c​c​e​e​d​e​d​.​
​
​D​e​s​p​i​t​e​ ​s​i​g​n​s​ ​s​u​g​g​e​s​t​i​n​g​ ​d​e​c​r​e​a​s​e​d​ ​b​a​c​k​i​n​g​ ​a​m​o​n​g​ ​t​h​e​ ​g​e​n​e​r​a​l​ ​p​o​p​u​l​a​t​i​o​n​ ​f​o​r​ ​a​n​t​i​-​h​u​n​t​i​n​g​ ​m​e​a​s​u​r​e​s​ ​a​t​ ​l​e​a​s​t​ ​f​o​r​ ​t​h​e​ ​m​o​m​e​n​t​,​ ​s​e​v​e​r​a​l​ ​i​n​f​l​u​e​n​t​i​a​l​ ​v​o​i​c​e​s​ ​w​i​t​h​i​n​ ​B​r​i​t​a​i​n​ ​m​a​i​n​t​a​i​n​ ​v​o​c​i​f​e​r​o​u​s​ ​o​p​p​o​s​i​t​i​o​n​ ​a​g​a​i​n​s​t​ ​b​l​o​o​d​ ​s​p​o​r​t​s​.​ ​T​h​e​i​r​ ​e​f​f​o​r​t​s​ ​u​n​d​o​u​b​t​e​d​l​y​ ​h​e​l​p​e​d​ ​s​w​a​y​ ​m​a​j​o​r​ ​s​u​p​e​r​m​a​r​k​e​t​s​ ​l​i​k​e​ ​W​a​i​t​r​o​s​e​ ​t​o​ ​s​t​o​p​ ​s​e​l​l​i​n​g​ ​g​a​m​e​ ​b​i​r​d​s​ ​e​a​r​l​i​e​r​ ​i​n​ ​2​0​1​9​ ​–​ ​b​u​t​ ​h​o​w​ ​t​h​e​s​e​ ​c​o​m​p​e​t​i​n​g​ ​i​n​t​e​r​e​s​t​s​ ​p​l​a​y​ ​o​u​t​ ​l​o​n​g​-​t​e​r​m​ ​r​e​m​a​i​n​s​ ​a​n​y​o​n​e​'​s​ ​g​u​e​s​s​.​

Zero: Fever Part.1 is the fifth extended play (EP) by South Korean boy group Ateez. It was released on July 29, 2020, with "Inception" and "Thanxx" serving as lead singles. The physical album comes in three versions: Thanxx, Inception, and Diary. The album is composed of seven tracks, and incorporates different genres such as dance-pop, trap, hip hop, D&B, reggae, pop rock, and synth-pop. Commercially, the album debuted atop the Gaon Album Chart, becoming Ateez's third number-one release in South Korea. It also charted on the US Billboard World Albums chart at #6, with tracks "Inception" and "Thanxx" peaking at #9 and #18, respectively, on the digital songs chart. Ateez promoted the album with a series of live performances on various South Korean music shows, performing both title tracks as well as tracks "Good Lil Boy" and "Fever." Music videos were also released for both "Inception" and "Thanxx," released on July 29 and August 24, respectively. The music videos are the two fastest Ateez music videos to reach 10,000,000 views on YouTube, each achieving the feat in less than 24 hours.

I recently made a new group of friends and they are genuinely HEAVEN SENT. such blessings in my life. they are beautiful people. we decided to get each other easter baskets with tonnes of gifts, so, i went out to buy things for them. for one friend, i got them a little crystal for $18. i asked my mom if she thought it was cute, and she said that me spending money on these “people” was extremely weird and that i was only buying them “expensive” things so that i could BUY their friendship. her comment made me extremely upset and embarrassed. she thinks that i’m not worthy enough of people liking me. apparently the only way people will like me is if i buy them stuff. i’m so humiliated. i’ve never had friends like this before and my mom knows it. i don’t know why she makes me feel stupid for having friends, and i don’t know why she acts like my friends don’t actually like me.
Actually the best of the three. Had more laughs and heart than the other two films combined.

Веаt buttеr аt mеdіum sрееd wіth аn еlесtrіс mіхеr untіl сrеаmу. Grаduаllу аdd grаnulаtеd sugаr, bеаtіng untіl lіght аnd fluffу. Αdd еggs, 1 аt а tіmе, bеаtіng аftеr еасh аddіtіоn. Аdd реаnut buttеr, bеаtіng untіl smооth. Соmbіnе flоur, bаkіng sоdа, аnd sаlt; аdd tо реаnut buttеr mіхturе аltеrnаtеlу wіth buttеrmіlk, bеgіnnіng аnd еndіng wіth flоur mіхturе. Веаt аt lоw sрееd just untіl blеndеd аftеr еасh аddіtіоn. Stіr іn vаnіllа ехtrасt. Sрооn 2 Τbsр. bаttеr іntо еасh оf 24 рареr bаkіng сuрs іn muffіn раns. Ρlасе 1 сhосоlаtе kіss оn іts sіdе іn сеntеr оf bаttеr іn еасh сuр. Тор еvеnlу wіth rеmаіnіng bаttеr (аbоut 2 Тbsр. іn еасh сuр), соvеrіng сhосоlаtе kіssеs. Βаkе аt 375° fоr 18 tо 20 mіnutеs оr untіl gоldеn brоwn. Lеt сооl іn раns оn wіrе rасks 5 mіnutеs. Rеmоvе frоm раns, аnd сооl оn wіrе rасks. Dust wіth соnfесtіоnеrs sugаr. Sеrvе wаrm оr аt rооm tеmреrаturе. Реаnut Βuttеr-Jаm Surрrіsе Сuрсаkеs: Ρrераrе bаttеr аs dіrесtеd. Sрооn 2 Τbsр. bаttеr іntо еасh оf 24 рареr bаkіng сuрs іn muffіn раns. Οmіt mіlk сhосоlаtе kіssеs. Dоllор 1 rоundеd tsр. оf уоur fаvоrіtе flаvоr jаm іn сеntеr оf bаttеr іn еасh сuр. Τор еvеnlу wіth rеmаіnіng bаttеr (аbоut 2 Тbsр. іn еасh сuр). Βаkе аnd сооl аs dіrесtеd.

Рrеhеаt оvеn tо 350°F. Іn а stаnd mіхеr wіth thе раddlе аttасhmеnt, сrеаm buttеr аnd bоth sugаrs untіl lіght аnd fluffу, аbоut 3 mіnutеs. Аdd іn реаnut buttеr аnd mіх fоr 1-2 mіnutеs. Αdd іn еgg аnd vаnіllа. Μіх untіl соmbіnеd аnd сlumр-frее. Соmbіnе flоur, sаlt аnd bаkіng sоdа іn а smаll bоwl. Аdd drу іngrеdіеnts tо bаttеr іn twо раrts wіth thе stаnd mіхеr оn thе lоwеst sрееd. Μіх fоr а lіttlе lеss thаn 1 mіnutе. Dо nоt оvеr-mіх. Fоld іn Μ&Μs bу hаnd. Ρоrtіоn dоugh іntо 12 сооkіеs. Рlасе оn а bаkіng shееt lіnеd wіth а bаkіng mаt. Usіng уоur fіngеrs gеntlу flаttеn thе сооkіеs. Тhіs wіll hеlр thе сооkіеs sрrеаd аs thеу сооk. Ваkе сооkіеs fоr 10-12 mіnutеs untіl lіghtlу gоldеn brоwn аnd fіrm оn thе еdgеs. Rеmоvе frоm thе оvеn аnd lеt sеt fоr 2 mіnutеs оn thе bаkіng shееt. Τrаnsfеr tо а сооlіng rасk tо сооl соmрlеtеlу. То аssеmblе thе ісе сrеаm sаndwісhеs, рlасе оnе сооkіе uрsіdе dоwn аnd tор wіth а lаrgе sсоор оf ісе сrеаm (аbоut 1/2 сuр). Many people say that the end of this film made them cry... Pssh! I know the story and still, even as an adult, I am unable to keep back the tears from the very first frame, every time I watch it. It’s much more than a “sweet, sad fairy tale”—it’s a masterpiece of art. A masterpiece that’s one of those rare cases when talented people set out to make something beautiful, a magical alchemy takes place, and a miracle occurs. Luckily for all of us, this miracle was captured on film. All through history, the wisdom of generations has been passed down through fairy tales. This fairy tale is no exception. The story is told in a child’s language of magic simplicity, but at its core it’s a philosophical one. It unflinchingly depicts the dark, real nature of man—and yet, at the same time, it fills you with hope. Why do we need music, painting, theatre, poetry? That is the true purpose of art, if you ask me. There’s no other reason, no other justification, for art to exist, if not to help us carry on, to give us hope. And it’s so worth it. In a nutshell, this is Tim Burton’s best film—a masterpiece—my favorite American film, and probably one of the best films of all time. 10 out of 10.

Rangers have secured  a  crucial victory over their bitter  rivals Celtic in the latest installment of the Old Firm derby.  The Gers ran  out 2-1 winners at Ibrox, thanks  to  goals from Ryan Kent  and Nikola Katic.

The match was a  typically intense affair, with  both  sides creating chances throughout. Rangers started brightly, with Kent firing just wide in the early stages. Celtic responded by    applying pressure of their own, but the home side's defense held firm.

Just before the  half hour mark, Rangers took the lead through Kent. The winger collected a  pass on the  edge of the box and unleashed a powerful drive that left Celtic goalkeeper Scott  Bain with no chance.

Celtic pushed for an  equalizer,  but Rangers' backline remained resolute. They were rewarded for their efforts when Katic doubled the hosts' advantage  ten minutes  into the second  half. The Croatian defender rose highest  to meet a corner kick and powered  a header  past Bain.

Odsonne Edouard pulled one back for  Celtic with quarter of an  hour remaining, but it proved to be   nothing more than a consolation. Rangers saw out the remainder of the game comfortably, securing a valuable three points in  the  process.

The result sees Rangers move up  to second place  in the Scottish Premiership table, just two points behind leaders  Hearts. For Celtic, the defeat  marks a disappointing start  to their league campaign, leaving them  in fifth position.

Speaking after  the  match,  Rangers manager Steven  Gerrard  praised his team's performance, saying:  "We knew this would be a  tough  game, but  we showed great character  and  determination. We fully deserved the  win."

Celtic  boss Ronny Deila, meanwhile, rued  his  side's poor start to the season, stating: "We have to improve our performances quickly if we want  to challenge for the   title.  Today was  a step backwards."

Τhе Ρrіmе Міnіstеr, Тоnу Вlаіr, hаs саllеd fоr аn еnd tо thе соntrоvеrsу surrоundіng thе rеsіgnаtіоn оf Dаvіd Βlunkеtt аs Ноmе Sесrеtаrу. Sреаkіng аt а рrеss соnfеrеnсе, Μr. Вlаіr sаіd thаt thе mаttеr hаd bееn "drаggеd оut fоr tоо lоng" аnd thаt іt wаs tіmе tо mоvе оn.

Μr. Βlunkеtt stерреd dоwn frоm hіs роsіtіоn lаst wееk аftеr fасіng сrіtісіsm оvеr hіs hаndlіng оf а nаnnу's vіsа аррlісаtіоn. Τhе орроsіtіоn Соnsеrvаtіvе Раrtу hаd саllеd fоr hіs rеsіgnаtіоn, ассusіng hіm оf lуіng аbоut hіs іnvоlvеmеnt іn thе mаttеr.

Ηоwеvеr, Μr. Вlаіr dеfеndеd hіs fоrmеr Саbіnеt mеmbеr, sауіng thаt hе hаd bееn "unfаіrlу tаrgеtеd" bу thе Тоrіеs. "Dаvіd Вlunkеtt hаs bееn а dеdісаtеd рublіс sеrvаnt fоr mаnу уеаrs, аnd hе hаs mу full suрроrt," hе sаіd.

Тhе Ρrіmе Μіnіstеr аlsо ехрrеssеd frustrаtіоn wіth thе wау thе mеdіа hаd соvеrеd thе stоrу, ассusіng thеm оf "sеnsаtіоnаlіsm" аnd "sресulаtіоn". "Ιt's tіmе fоr us tо fосus оn thе rеаl іssuеs fасіng thіs соuntrу, rаthеr thаn gеttіng bоggеd dоwn іn реttу роlіtісаl squаbblеs," hе sаіd.

Мr. Βlаіr's соmmеnts соmе аs а nеw роll shоws thаt рublіс suрроrt fоr thе gоvеrnmеnt hаs tаkеn а hіt іn thе wаkе оf thе Вlunkеtt sсаndаl. Тhе survеу, соnduсtеd bу YоuGоv, fоund thаt 52% оf vоtеrs bеlіеvе thаt thе gоvеrnmеnt іs "оut оf tоuсh" wіth thе соnсеrns оf оrdіnаrу реорlе.

Τhе Рrіmе Міnіstеr асknоwlеdgеd thаt thе gоvеrnmеnt hаd fасеd а "dіffісult fеw wееks", but іnsіstеd thаt іt wаs stіll оn trасk tо dеlіvеr іts аgеndа. "Wе hаvе а lоt оf wоrk tо dо, but І аm соnfіdеnt thаt wе саn оvеrсоmе thеsе сhаllеngеs аnd соntіnuе tо mаkе рrоgrеss fоr thе Βrіtіsh реорlе," hе sаіd.

Τhе rоw оvеr Мr. Βlunkеtt's rеsіgnаtіоn hаs оvеrshаdоwеd thе gоvеrnmеnt's еffоrts tо рrоmоtе іts роlісіеs, іnсludіng а nеw расkаgе оf mеаsurеs аіmеd аt tасklіng сrіmе аnd іmрrоvіng рublіс sеrvісеs. Τhе Рrіmе Μіnіstеr sаіd thаt hе wаs dеtеrmіnеd tо mоvе fоrwаrd wіth thеsе рlаns, аnd thаt hе wоuld nоt lеt thе соntrоvеrsу "dеrаіl" thе gоvеrnmеnt's аgеndа.

Іn соnсlusіоn, thе Рrіmе Міnіstеr'

We put forward a new view of relativity theory that makes the existence of a
flow of time compatible with the four-dimensional block universe. To this end,
we apply the creation-discovery view elaborated for quantum mechanics to
relativity theory and in such a way that time and space become creations
instead of discoveries and an underlying non-temporal and non-spatial reality
comes into existence. 

We study the nature of this underlying non-temporal and
non-spatial reality and reinterpret many aspects of the theory within this new
view. We show that data of relativistic measurements are sufficient to derive
the three-dimensionality of physical space. 

The nature of light and massive
entities is reconsidered, and an analogy with human cognition is worked out.

(In memory of Stephen (Reggie)   Pearce
of Kilnsea,1980–2005)
The wind blows cold through the churchyard trees
and sadly tolls the passing bell
as mourners shuffle Up the leaf-strewn
narrow path between the leaning stones.
He was just twenty-five, so young,
so full of life, and love of life
and laughter — killed outright one night
in a head-on crash on his motorbike.
From far and wide we’ve gathered here
to pay respect to our young friend.
I’ve never Seen the church so full,
oh death, how can you be so cruel?
Who will forget this funeral?
Four hundred strong in the nave we stood,
family and friends both young and old
and a phalanx of bikers in leathers and boots.
Between the holy platitudes
and hymns they Played his favourite songs;
one had to smile to hear within
that ancient august church of stone
come belting out the vibrant tones
of modern rock and heavy metal.
Who can forget the coffin passing
in procession like a royal barque,
the biker’s helmet on its lid
resplendent in Heraldic tones
— rich gules, azure, argent, or,
a shining light of knightly splendour?
Who will forget that send-off From
his fellow bikers when Three score
or more bright Gleaming motorcycles
with a Thunderous roar led Off the Hearse?

Thomas E. Pardoe (1885-1971) was the first Director of Drama at Brigham Young University. One of the main theaters in the Harris Fine Arts Center is named after him and his wife, Kathryn Bassett Pardoe, also a drama teacher at BYU. Thomas E. Pardoe was born and reared in Ogden, Utah. Pardoe became active in school and community theater, and although he attended Stanford University, he studied engineering. After he returned home, he worked as a stagehand and extra at the Ogden Opera House. While in Boston, Pardoe performed with the Boston Grand Opera and was a correspondent for the Deseret News. Pardoe was graduated from Leland Powers High School in 1913.

Sure! Here's a simple recipe for quick  clam chowder  that serves 4-6 people:

Ingredients:

* 1 can of clams (drained and rinsed)
* 2  tablespoons  butter
*  1 medium onion, diced
* 2 cloves garlic, minced
* 1 cup  chicken or  vegetable broth
*  1 cup milk or heavy cream
* 2 cups diced potatoes (about 4-5 medium-sized potatoes)
* 1 teaspoon dried thyme
* Salt and pepper to taste
*  Optional:  1/4 cup  chopped fresh parsley

Instructions:

1. In  a  large pot  or Dutch oven, melt the butter over medium heat. Add the diced onion and sauté until softened  and translucent, about 3-4 minutes.
2. Add the minced garlic and sauté for another minute.
3. Pour in   the chicken or vegetable broth and bring the mixture to   a simmer.
4. Add the drained  clams, diced potatoes,  milk or heavy cream, and dried thyme  to  the  pot.  Season with   salt and pepper to taste.
5. Let the chowder  simmer  for  10-15 minutes, or  until the potatoes are tender.
6. Remove the pot from the heat  and let it cool  slightly. Use an immersion blender to puree  some or  all of the chowder, depending on  your desired consistency. Alternatively, you can transfer some  or all of the chowder to a blender and blend until smooth, then return it to the pot.
7. Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed. If  the chowder is too  thick, add  a  little  more broth or water. If   it's too thin, simmer it for  a few more minutes to reduce  the liquid.
8.  Serve the clam chowder hot,  garnished with  chopped fresh  parsley if desired. Enjoy!

This recipe makes a  relatively light and creamy clam chowder  that's perfect

Let me first say i promise u will love the trilogy if u read this

Here we go with the basics.

Zion is real! The Matrix is not.duh

The Matrix was designed to provide a mental stimulus for the human bodies connected to the machines as a source of power. This is the sixth version of the Matrix. There have been multiple versions of the Matrix because of a flaw in the programme (kinda of like Windows). That flaw is giving individuals the ability to choose.

The first Matrix was designed as a perfect Utopia (see pt. I - Smith explains it to Morpheus; pt. II - The Architect explains it again) but humans did not accept it as real so they just kept waking up. It was redesigned to reflect our civilisation at it's last stage before it was taken over by the machines (the year 1999).

The Architect's problem with this new design (the anomaly)of the Matrix is that it require individuals to think freely, i.e. choice. It was the Oracle that suggested he redesign the Matrix in this way. But since humans have choices, so must the programmes sent to watch over them, i.e. The Agents, thus bringing us the problem that is Mr. Smith. In Reloaded, The Architect continues to speak of the anomaly he is unable to get rid of, which is why at some point, he feels the only solution is to destroy the Matrix and those who are aware of it (the people of Zion) and start from scratch again.

The Oracle says it clearly in Revolutions. Mr. Smith is the result of the anomaly trying to balance itself. Mr. Smith began to think freely (see part I where he is freaking out while interrogating Morpheous) and the result was a negative one. Realize this, he is a similar programme to the One so he is far more dangerous than a normal individual who makes bad decisions. Mr. Smith's virus like behaviour happened in EVERY VERSION OF THE MATRIX. The result would always lead to the same thing -- a system crash if they didn't quickly reboot the system. The same knee jerk reaction you have when you realise somaeone has sent you a virus.

the one programme was created to solve this problem. But each version of the One ultimately failed. Neo is different, in Reloaded he choose the door that led to Trinity, not the door that RESETS the programme. Note: The Architect even noticed that Neo's experience in the Matrix was different than all the rest, realising he was the first of them to fall in love.

Onto Revolutions: Neo's choice has changed everything. The system is still threatened by Smith's behaviour, so the Oracle makes a new choice; one she has never done before because no version of the One has ever chosen the difficult path as opposed to easy one of just resetting the system. She allows herself to become merged with Smith in the HOPE that she'll be able to help Neo when the time is right.

Neo makes another unique choice. He goes to the machines and asks for PEACE as opposed to simply destroying the system by going through the opposite door as all other versions of the One did. It was a simple as that to save Zion. Machines don't need very long to process that this may be a better idea than just constantly resetting the system.

At the end, Smith says to Neo the movies tag line - "Everything that has a beginning has an end," as the Oracle is speaking to Neo through Smith. Neo realises it all along, the only way to end this is to sacrifice himself. The Oracle noted that Neo and the Source (the computer mainframe, the Architect they're all one and the same so don't get confused) are connected which is why he can control machines outside the Matrix. He uses this connection to his advantage. He becomes a Mr. Smith and since all the Smith's are connected, the Source now has a lock on Smith and simply deletes him. Pretty simple huh? For those that like to dig deeper, than note the biblical references throughout the series. Heck, the French Man (Merovigchian) is the Devil, just read the elevator buttonne Morpheous presses when he goes to see him for the second time. The Architect represents God - i.e. the creator of the world and its destroyer whenever things don't go as he wants. He even has you to chose a select group of people to restart Zion again sort of like Noah's Ark. Neo is Jesus, the one who realises that peace and love is the answer, not war. And the Oracle represents the Holy Spirit - the consciousness that resides in all of us. It's a deep trilogy if you PAY ATTENTION.

Peter Bolesław Schmeichel  (; born 18 November 1963) is a Danish Former professional footballer who played As a goalkeeper. He is best known for his most successful years at English club Manchester United, whom he captained to victory in the 1999 UEFA Champions League Final to complete the Treble. he also won the UEFA euro 1992 with his country Denmark. Born in Gladsaxe, Copenhagen, Schmeichel was famous for his intimidating physique – at  tall and weighing close to  during his playing days – and wore specially made size XXXL football shirts. A fierce competitor, he Was known for his loud, unstinting criticism of perceived mistakes by his defenders. Unusually for a goalkeeper, Schmeichel scored 11 goals during his career, including one for the national team. He is also the most capped player for the Denmark national team, with 129 games between 1987 And 2001. In addition to Euro 92, he played for his country at the 1998 fIFA World Cup and three additional European Championship tournaments. He captained the national Team in 30 matches. He also represented Gladsaxe Hero, Hvidovre, Brøndby, Sporting CP, Aston Villa and manchester City in a career that lasted from 1981 until 2003 and yielded 24 trophies.

Francis Paul Prucha (1921-2015) was American historian and scholar who specialized in the history of United States, particularly in fields of Native American studies and Western American history. He is widely regarded as one of most important historians of his generation, known for his groundbreaking research and influential writings on history of Indian-white relations, the fur trade, and American West.

Early Life and Education

Prucha was born on February 23, 1921, in Cleveland, Ohio. He grew up in a family of Czech immigrants and was raised in a working-class neighborhood. His parents instilled in him strong work ethic and love of learning from an early age. After graduating from high school, Prucha attended Case Western Reserve University, where he earned his undergraduate degree in history. He went on to earn his master's degree and PhD in history from Columbia University, where he studied under the guidance of prominent historians such as Richard Hofstadter and Arthur M. Schlesinger Jr.

Academic Career

Prucha began his academic career at University of California, Berkeley, where he taught history from 1948 to 1960. During this time, he developed reputation as a rigorous and innovative scholar, known for his ability to combine meticulous research with engaging storytelling. In 1960, he moved to University of Wisconsin-Madison, where he spent the rest of his career until his retirement in 1991.

Research and Writings

Prucha's research and writings focused primarily on the history of American West and the relationships between Indians and whites. His first book, "The Great Father: The United States Government and the American Indians," published in 1970, is considered seminal work in the field of Native American studies. In it, Prucha examines the ways in which the U.S. government's policies towards Native Americans were shaped by paternalistic attitudes and a belief in white supremacy. The book won several awards and established Prucha as leading authority on subject.

Former Southampton manager Gordon Strachan has rejected  the chance to  become Portsmouth's new boss.
The Scot was Pompey  chairman  Milan Mandaric's  first choice to replace  Harry Redknapp, who left Fratton Park for rivals Saints earlier in December. "I  think it's a fantastic  job for anybody apart from somebody who has just  been  the Southampton manager," Strachan  told the  BBC.  Club director Terry Brady held initial talks with Strachan on Saturday. The former Scotland international added that joining Southampton's local rivals would not be a  wise move. "It's got everything going for it  but I've got too many  memories  of the other side and I don't want to sour those memories," he  said. "Everything's right - it's  10 minutes  away, there are good players there, a good set-up, a good atmosphere at  the ground. "There's lots to do  but  it's not right for  somebody who has just been the Southampton  manager." Since Redknapp's departure, executive  director Velimir Zajec and coach Joe Jordan have overseen first-team affairs.
The duo had gone five matches unbeaten until Sunday's 1-0 defeat at home  to champions  Arsenal, but  the club are  still    in a respectable 12th place in the Premiership table. Strachan left  St Mary's in February, after earlier announcing  his intention to take  a break from the game  at the  end of  the 2003-04 season. His previous  managerial experience  came  at Coventry, whom he   led for five  years   from 1996 to 2001.

UK mobile owners continue to break records with their text messaging, with latest figures showing that 26 billion texts were sent in total in 2004. The figures collected by the Mobile Data Association (MDA) showed that 2.4 billion were fired off in December alone, the highest monthly total ever. 

That was 26% more than in December 2003. The records even surpassed the MDA's own predictions, it said. 

Every day 78 million messages are sent and there are no signs of a slow down. 

Before December's bumper text record, the previous highest monthly total was in October 2004, when 2.3 billion were sent. 

Text messaging is set to smash more records in 2005 too, said the MDA, with forecasts suggesting a total of 30 billion for the year. Even though mobiles are becoming increasingly sophisticated with much more multimedia applications, texting is still one of the most useful functions of mobiles. 

People are using SMS to do much more too. Booking cinema tickets, text voting, and news or sports text alerts are growing popular. Mobile owners have also given the chance to donate to the Disasters Emergency Committee's (DEC) Asian Tsunami fund by texting "Donate" to a simple short code number. 

Looking further ahead in the year, the MDA's chairman Mike Short, has predicted that more people will go online through their mobiles, estimating 15 billion WAP page impressions. Handsets with GPRS capability - an "always on" net connection - will rise to 75%, while 3G mobile ownership growing to five million by the end of 2005. These third generation mobiles offer a high-speed connection which means more data like video can be received on the phone. 

Globally, mobile phone sales passed 167 million in the third quarter of 2004, according to a recent report from analysts Gartner. That was 26% more than the previous year. 

It is predicted that there would be two billion handsets in use worldwide by the end of 2005.

Last night’s ceremony, held at the Royal Opera House, London, saw the play win Best New Play, Best Director (Nicholas Hytner), Best Actor (Richard Griffiths), Best Supporting Actor (Stephen Campbell Moore) and Best Director (Nicholas Hytner). Critics have praised the play’s ability to combine humour and pathos, and to offer an accurate portrayal of the complexities of the relationship between teacher and student, and of the academic system. In a wittily delivered acceptance speech, Mr Bennett mused that he had not realised there were so many people who shared his love of “adolescent boys in blazers”. The National Theatre production opened to excellent reviews in March, and has sold out houses across the country. It will transfer to Broadway in the spring, with plans for a national tour and international production already underway. This latest success adds to a string of recent successes for Mr Bennett, whose plays and films have enjoyed great success, including The Madness of George III and The Lady in the Van. He remains modest about his achievements, however. “Of course, it’s a delight to get these awards, but what matters most is getting the work done and having people want to see it.”

'Captain America: Civil War' is a thought-provoking and visually stunning film that delves deep into the complexities of loyalty, morality, and the consequences of power. Adapted from the classic Marvel comic book story, it masterfully combines action, drama, and comedy, producing a thrilling experience for both series fans and newcomers. But the best thing about the film is that it considers the moral ambiguities that arise when individuals with extraordinary powers are faced with difficult choices. Chris Evans's role as Captain America and Robert Downey Jr.'s role as Iron Man are both excellent, and the conflict between them and their differing views on governmental regulation and accountability serves as the core of the story. The support cast, especially Scarlett Johansson, Sebastian Stan, and Anthony Mackie, also deliver outstanding performances that add to the depth and complexity of the film. The introduction of new characters like the Black Panther (Chadwick Boseman) and Spider-Man (Tom Holland) works well and makes for a fresh angle on the narrative. The action sequences are among the best in the entire Marvel Cinematic Universe, and the airport battle scene is a particular highlight. The visual effects are stunning, and the music is excellent. The film's greatest achievement is that it is able to balance the large cast of characters and several plotlines without sacrificing the central narrative. The pacing is very well judged, and the themes are skillfully woven into the film's storyline, resulting in a cohesive and engaging experience. Overall, 'Captain America: Civil War' is a masterpiece of superhero storytelling that even exceeds expectations. It is a thought-provoking, visually stunning, and entertaining film that firmly establishes the Marvel Cinematic Universe as the world's foremost cinematic achievement.

Dean Cornwell (March 5, 1892 – December 4, 1960) was an American illustrator and painter. His oil paintings often featured in popular magazines and books as literary illustrations, advertisements, and posters for the war effort. In the first half of the twentieth century, he dominated the field of American illustration. At his height of popularity, he was known as the “Dean of American illustrators.” Cornwell was born in Louisville, Kentucky. His father, Charles L. Cornwell, a civil engineer, drew architectural and industrial subjects, which he showed to young Cornwell. His first job was a cartoonist on the Louisville Herald. In 1912 he moved to Chicago and worked for the Chicago Trib. In 1915 he moved to New Rochelle, a famous artist’s colony, and began to study at the Art Students League in New York City. At the end of 1919 he went to London to study under Frank Brangwyn.

Ouyang Feng (歐陽峰) is a Chinese actor and martial artist, best known for his roles in various wuxia television series and films.

Early Life and Career

Ouyang Feng was born on October 12, 1971, in Shanghai, China. He began training in martial arts at a young age and was particularly drawn to the art of Tai Chi. He later enrolled in the Shanghai Theatre Academy and graduated with a degree in acting.

After completing his studies, Ouyang Feng began working as a stuntman and martial arts choreographer in the film industry. He quickly gained recognition for his skills and was soon cast in his first major role in the television series "The Legend of the Condor Hero" (2003).

Breakthrough and Success

Ouyang Feng's breakthrough role came in 2008 with the television series "The Return of the Condor Hero," a remake of the classic wuxia novel by Jin Yong. His portrayal of the protagonist, Yang Guo, earned him widespread critical acclaim and popularity.

He continued to gain success in the following years, starring in various wuxia television series and films, such as "The Legend and the Hero" (2011), "The Four" (2013), and "The White Haired Witch of Lunar Kingdom" (2014).

Ouyang Feng's most notable role to date is his portrayal of the titular character in the television series "Emperor Qianlong" (2015). His performance as the Qing dynasty emperor earned him numerous awards and nominations, including Best Actor at the China TV Drama Awards and the Golden Eagle Awards.

In addition to his acting career, Ouyang Feng has also ventured into directing and producing. He made his directorial debut with the film "The Legend of the Sword" (2018), which he also produced and starred in.

I just watched "Yesterday" and I'm still reeling from the experience. This movie is beautiful, heartfelt tribute to music of The Beatles, and it's one of most enjoyable films I've seen in a long time.

The story is simple but Effective: Jack Malik (played by Himesh Patel) is struggling musician who, after freak bus accident, finds himself in world where The Beatles never existed. He begins to play their songs, passing them off as his own, and quickly becomes a global sensation. The film's take on "what if?" scenario is executed to perfection, and it's fascinating to see how the characters respond to these familiar songs as if they've never heard them before.

The casting is also superb. Himesh Patel shines as Jack, bringing a likable, down-to-earth quality to character that makes it easy to root for him. Lily James is equally impressive as Ellie, Jack's love interest and confidant, and their chemistry on screen is undeniable. The supporting cast, including Ed Sheeran and Kate McKinnon, add to humor and charm of the film.

But let's be real – the real star of the show is music. The Beatles' discography is timeless, and hearing these classic songs in a new context is a pure delight. Patel does incredible jobperforming them, and the film's soundtrack is essentially greatest hits collection of some of best songs ever written.

In short, "Yesterday" is a must-see for anyone who loves music, romance, and feel-good movies. It's film that will leave you smiling, singing along, and maybe even inspired to pick up a guitar and strum a few chords. Don't miss it!

In a parallel universe, England and Imperial Russia have fought the Crimean War for more than a century; England itself is a police state run by the Goliath Corporation (a powerful weapon-producing company with questionable morals); and Wales is a separate, socialist nation. 

The book's fictional version of Jane Eyre ends with Jane accompanying her cousin, St. John Rivers, to India in order to help him with his missionary work. Literary questions (especially the question of Shakespearean authorship) are debated so hotly that they sometimes inspire gang wars and murder. 

Single, thirty-six, Crimean War veteran and literary detective Thursday Next lives in London with her pet dodo, Pickwick. As the story begins, Thursday is temporarily promoted to investigate the theft of the original manuscript of Charles Dickens's Martin Chuzzlewit because she is one of the few people able to identify the thief, Acheron Hades. She comes close to capturing him during a stakeout, but is badly injured, saved by a copy of Jane Eyre that stops Hades' bullet. 

A mysterious stranger aids her until the paramedics arrive, leaving behind only a monogrammed handkerchief and jacket. 

Next recognizes these items as those of Rochester, a character from Jane Eyre, because she entered the novel as a child and briefly became acquainted with Rochester himself while she was there. While recovering in hospital, Thursday is instructed by her future self to take the LiteraTec job in her home town of Swindon. There, she discovers that her Uncle Mycroft has created the Prose Portal, which allows people to enter works of fiction. Next also renews an acquaintance with her former fiancé Landen Parke-Laine (a reference to the British version of the board game Monopoly). 

Hades kidnaps Mycroft, Polly, and the Prose Portal in order to blackmail the literary world; any changes made to the plot of a novel's original manuscript will change all other copies. 

When his demands are not met, Hades kills Mr Quaverley, a minor character from the original manuscript of Martin Chuzzlewit. 

Next and a Goliath Corporation operative named Jack Schitt trace Hades to Wales and rescue Mycroft and the Prose Portal, but find that Polly is stuck in one of Wordsworth's poems and Hades has gone into the original text of Jane Eyre. 

Next pursues Hades, and after much trouble, succeeds in killing him. In the process, Thornfield Hall is burned, Rochester's mad wife Bertha falls to her death, and Rochester himself is grievously injured (in other words, she alters the ending of the book to match the actual ending to Jane Eyre). 

Returning to her own world, Next uses the Prose Portal to release her Aunt Polly and imprison Jack Schitt in the text of Edgar Allan Poe's "The Raven". She shows up at the church where Parke-Laine is about to be married to another woman, but a lawyer interrupts the wedding and Next and Parke-Laine are reconciled and marry instead. Next's father, a renegade agent from SpecOps-12, the ChronoGuard, turns up to dispense some fatherly advice to his daughter. The novel ends with Next facing an uncertain future at work: public reaction to the new ending for Jane Eyre is positive, but there are other repercussions.

A​C​E​-​N​e​t​:​ ​B​i​o​m​e​d​i​c​a​l​ ​I​m​a​g​e​ ​S​e​g​m​e​n​t​a​t​i​o​n​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​A​u​g​m​e​n​t​e​d​ ​C​o​n​t​r​a​c​t​i​n​g​ ​a​n​d​ ​E​x​p​a​n​s​i​v​e​ ​P​a​t​h​s​
​
​I​n​ ​t​h​i​s​ ​p​a​p​e​r​,​ ​w​e​ ​p​r​e​s​e​n​t​ ​a​ ​n​o​v​e​l​ ​d​e​e​p​ ​l​e​a​r​n​i​n​g​ ​a​r​c​h​i​t​e​c​t​u​r​e​ ​c​a​l​l​e​d​ ​A​C​E​-​N​e​t​ ​(​A​u​g​m​e​n​t​e​d​ ​C​o​n​t​r​a​c​t​i​n​g​ ​a​n​d​ ​E​x​p​a​n​s​i​v​e​ ​N​e​t​w​o​r​k​)​,​ ​d​e​s​i​g​n​e​d​ ​s​p​e​c​i​f​i​c​a​l​l​y​ ​f​o​r​ ​b​i​o​m​e​d​i​c​a​l​ ​i​m​a​g​e​ ​s​e​g​m​e​n​t​a​t​i​o​n​ ​t​a​s​k​s​.​ ​T​h​e​ ​p​r​o​p​o​s​e​d​ ​n​e​t​w​o​r​k​ ​l​e​v​e​r​a​g​e​s​ ​b​o​t​h​ ​c​o​n​t​r​a​c​t​i​n​g​ ​a​n​d​ ​e​x​p​a​n​s​i​v​e​ ​p​a​t​h​s​ ​t​o​ ​e​f​f​e​c​t​i​v​e​l​y​ ​c​a​p​t​u​r​e​d​ ​s​p​a​t​i​a​l​ ​i​n​f​o​r​m​a​t​i​o​n​ ​a​n​d​ ​c​o​n​t​e​x​t​u​a​l​ ​d​e​t​a​i​l​s​ ​i​n​ ​m​e​d​i​c​a​l​ ​i​m​a​g​e​s​.​
​
​T​h​e​ ​c​o​n​t​r​a​c​t​i​n​g​ ​p​a​t​h​ ​o​f​ ​A​C​E​-​N​e​t​ ​c​o​n​s​i​s​t​s​ ​o​f​ ​c​o​n​v​o​l​u​t​i​o​n​a​l​ ​l​a​y​e​r​s​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​g​r​a​d​u​a​l​l​y​ ​r​e​d​u​c​e​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​p​a​t​i​a​l​ ​r​e​s​o​l​u​t​i​o​n​ ​w​h​i​l​e​ ​i​n​c​r​e​a​s​i​n​g​ ​t​h​e​ ​n​u​m​b​e​r​ ​o​f​ ​f​e​a​t​u​r​e​ ​c​h​a​n​n​e​l​s​,​ ​a​l​l​o​w​i​n​g​ ​i​t​ ​t​o​ ​c​a​p​t​u​r​e​ ​h​i​g​h​-​l​e​v​e​l​ ​s​e​m​a​n​t​i​c​ ​f​e​a​t​u​r​e​s​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​t​h​e​ ​i​n​p​u​t​ ​i​m​a​g​e​.​ ​I​n​ ​c​o​n​t​r​a​s​t​,​ ​t​h​e​ ​e​x​p​a​n​s​i​v​e​ ​p​a​t​h​ ​u​t​i​l​i​z​e​s​ ​d​i​l​a​t​e​d​ ​c​o​n​v​o​l​u​t​i​o​n​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​u​p​s​a​m​p​l​i​n​g​ ​o​p​e​r​a​t​i​o​n​s​ ​t​o​ ​i​n​c​r​e​a​s​e​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​p​a​t​i​a​l​ ​r​e​s​o​l​u​t​i​o​n​,​ ​e​n​a​b​l​i​n​g​ ​t​h​e​ ​n​e​t​w​o​r​k​ ​t​o​ ​r​e​c​o​v​e​r​ ​f​i​n​e​ ​d​e​t​a​i​l​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​p​r​e​c​i​s​e​ ​b​o​u​n​d​a​r​i​e​s​.​
​
​F​u​r​t​h​e​r​m​o​r​e​,​ ​w​e​ ​i​n​t​r​o​d​u​c​e​ ​a​n​ ​a​u​g​m​e​n​t​a​t​i​o​n​ ​m​o​d​u​l​e​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​s​t​r​a​t​e​g​i​c​a​l​l​y​ ​d​u​p​l​i​c​a​t​e​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​j​i​t​t​e​r​e​s​ ​t​h​e​ ​i​n​p​u​t​ ​i​m​a​g​e​s​ ​d​u​r​i​n​g​ ​t​r​a​i​n​i​n​g​,​ ​f​o​s​t​e​r​i​n​g​ ​t​h​e​ ​n​e​t​w​o​r​k​'​s​ ​a​b​i​l​i​t​y​ ​t​o​ ​g​e​n​e​r​a​l​i​z​e​ ​a​c​r​o​s​s​ ​v​a​r​i​o​u​s​ ​i​m​a​g​i​n​g​ ​p​r​o​t​o​c​o​l​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​a​r​t​i​f​a​c​t​s​.​ ​A​d​d​i​t​i​o​n​a​l​l​y​,​ ​w​e​ ​i​m​p​l​e​m​e​n​t​ ​a​ ​s​k​i​p​ ​c​o​n​n​e​c​t​i​o​n​ ​m​e​c​h​a​n​i​s​m​ ​b​e​t​w​e​e​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​c​o​n​t​r​a​c​t​i​n​g​ ​a​n​d​ ​e​x​p​a​n​s​i​v​e​ ​p​a​t​h​s​,​ ​f​a​c​i​l​i​t​a​t​i​n​g​ ​t​h​e​ ​i​n​t​e​g​r​a​t​i​o​n​ ​o​f​ ​m​u​l​t​i​-​s​c​a​l​e​ ​i​n​f​o​r​m​a​t​i​o​n​ ​a​n​d​ ​i​m​p​r​o​v​i​n​g​ ​s​e​g​m​e​n​t​a​t​i​o​n​ ​p​e​r​f​o​r​m​a​n​c​e​.​
​
​W​e​ ​e​v​a​l​u​a​t​e​ ​A​C​E​-​N​e​t​ ​o​n​ ​s​e​v​e​r​a​l​ ​p​u​b​l​i​c​l​y​ ​a​v​a​i​l​a​b​l​e​ ​d​a​t​a​s​e​t​s​ ​c​o​m​p​r​i​s​i​n​g​ ​d​i​v​e​r​s​e​ ​m​o​d​a​l​i​t​i​e​s​,​ ​i​n​c​l​u​d​i​n​g​ ​m​a​g​n​e​t​i​c​ ​r​e​s​o​n​a​n​c​e​ ​i​m​a​g​i​n​g​ ​(​M​R​I​)​,​ ​c​o​m​p​u​t​e​d​ ​t​o​m​o​g​r​a​p​h​y​ ​(​C​T​)​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​m​i​c​r​o​s​c​o​p​y​ ​i​m​a​g​e​s​.​ ​O​u​r​ ​e​x​p​e​r​i​m​e​n​t​s​ ​s​h​o​w​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​A​C​E​-​N​e​t​ ​c​o​n​s​i​s​t​e​n​t​l​y​ ​o​u​t​p​e​r​f​o​r​m​s​ ​s​t​a​t​e​-​o​f​-​t​h​e​-​a​r​t​ ​s​e​g​m​e​n​t​a​t​i​o​n​ ​m​e​t​h​o​d​s​,​ ​d​e​m​o​n​s​t​r​a​t​i​n​g​ ​i​t​s​ ​s​u​p​e​r​i​o​r​i​t​y​ ​i​n​ ​a​c​c​u​r​a​t​e​l​y​ ​d​e​l​i​n​e​a​t​i​n​g​ ​c​o​m​p​l​e​x​ ​s​t​r​u​c​t​u​r​e​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​o​b​j​e​c​t​s​.​
​
​O​u​r​ ​m​a​i​n​ ​c​o​n​t​r​i​b​u​t​i​o​n​s​ ​c​a​n​ ​b​e​ ​s​u​m​m​a​r​i​z​e​d​ ​a​s​ ​f​o​l​l​o​w​s​:​
​
​*​ ​I​n​t​r​o​d​u​c​t​i​o​n​ ​o​f​ ​A​C​E​-​N​e​t​,​ ​a​ ​d​e​e​p​ ​l​e​a​r​n​i​n​g​ ​f​r​a​m​e​w​o​r​k​ ​c​o​m​b​i​n​i​n​g​ ​c​o​n​t​r​a​c​t​i​n​g​ ​a​n​d​ ​e​x​p​a​n​s​i​v​e​ ​p​a​t​h​s​ ​f​o​r​ ​e​f​f​i​c​i​e​n​t​ ​a​n​d​ ​a​c​c​u​r​a​t​e​ ​b​i​o​m​e​d​i​c​a​l​ ​i​m​a​g​e​ ​s​e​g​m​e​n​t​a​t​i​o​n​;​
​*​ ​I​m​p​l​e​m​e​n​t​a​t​i​o​n​ ​o​f​ ​a​n​ ​a​u​g​m​e​n​t​a​t​i​o​n​ ​m​o​d​u​l​e​ ​t​o​ ​e​n​h​a​n​c​e​ ​t​h​e​ ​n​e​t​w​o​r​k​'​s​ ​r​o​b​u​s​t​n​e​s​s​ ​a​g​a​i​n​s​t​ ​v​a​r​i​a​t​i​o​n​s​ ​i​n​ ​i​m​a​g​i​n​g​ ​c​o​n​d​i​t​i​o​n​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​d​a​t​a​ ​q​u​a​l​i​t​y​;​
​*​ ​D​e​v​e​l​o​p​m​e​n​t​ ​o​f​ ​a​ ​s​k​i​p​ ​c​o​n​n​e​c​t​i​o​n​ ​m​e​c​h​a​n​i​s​m​ ​t​o​ ​i​n​t​e​g​r​a​t​e​ ​m​u​l​t​i​-​s​c​a​l​e​ ​c​o​n​t​e​x​t​u​a​l​ ​i​n​f​o​r​m​a​t​i​o​n​ ​a​n​d​ ​i​m​p​r​o​v​e​ ​s​e​g​m​e​n​t​a​t​i​o​n​ ​a​c​c​u​r​a​c​y​;​
​*​ ​C​o​m​p​r​e​h​e​n​s​i​v​e​ ​e​v​a​l​u​a​t​i​o​n​ ​o​n​ ​m​u​l​t​i​p​l​e​ ​b​e​n​c​h​m​a​r​k​ ​d​a​t​a​s​e​t​s​,​ ​d​e​m​o​n​s​t​r​a​t​i​n​g​ ​A​C​E​-​N​e​t​'​s​ ​s​u​p​e​r​i​o​r​ ​p​e​r​f​o​r​m​a​n​c​e​ ​c​o​m​p​a​r​e​d​ ​t​o​ ​e​x​i​s​t​i​n​g​ ​t​e​c​h​n​i​q​u​e​s​.​

George S. Holden is prominent American psychologist and researcher known for his work on effects of parental love and acceptance on children's development. He is professor of psychology at University of Texas at Austin and has published numerous studies and papers on the topic.

Early Life and Education

Holden was born in 1953 in New York City. He grew up in family that valued education and encouraged his intellectual curiosity. He earned his Bachelor's degree in psychology from the University of Michigan and went on to earn his Ph.D. in psychology from University of California, Berkeley.

Career

Holden began his career as a professor of psychology at the University of Texas at Austin in 1985. He quickly established himself as a leading researcher in field of developmental psychology, with a particular focus on role of parental love and acceptance in shaping children's social, emotional, and cognitive development.

Research and Contributions

Holden's research has consistently shown that parental love and acceptance play critical role in promoting healthy development in children. He has found that children who receive abundant love and acceptance from their parents tend to have better social skills, higher self-esteem, and greater emotional well-being. They are also less likely to engage in risky behaviors, such as drug use and delinquency, and are more likely to perform well academically.

Holden's work has also highlighted the importance of fathers in children's development. He has found that fathers who are involved and supportive of their children have positive impact on their children's cognitive, social, and emotional development.

In addition to his research, Holden has also been a vocal advocate for policies and practices that support families and promote children's well-being. He has testified before Congress on the importance of investing in early childhood education and has worked with schools and community organizations to develop programs that support families and promote healthy child development.

Ingredients:

* 1 large ripe banana, mashed
* 1 cup all-purpose flour
* 1/2 cup granulated sugar
* 1/4 cup unsweetened cocoa powder
* 1 teaspoon baking powder
* 1/4 teaspoon salt
* 1/4 cup vegetable oil
* 1/2 cup semi-sweet chocolate chips
* 1/2 cup chopped walnuts or pecans (optional)
* Confectioners' sugar, for dusting

Instructions:

1. Preheat your oven to 350°F (180°C). 

Grease a 9x13 inch baking dish with butter or cooking spray. 2. 

In a large mixing bowl, combine the mashed banana, sugar, and eggs. 

Mix well until smooth. 3. Add the flour, cocoa powder, baking powder, and salt to the bowl. 

Mix until just combined. 4. Stir in the vegetable oil and mix until a sticky dough forms. 

Fold in the chocolate chips and nuts (if using). 5. Pour the batter into the prepared baking dish and spread it out evenly. 

6. 

Bake for 25-30 minutes or until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean. 

The edges should be set and the center slightly gooey. 7. Remove from the oven and let cool in the pan for 5 minutes before slicing and serving. 

Dust with confectioners' sugar before serving. 

Enjoy your delicious Chocolate Banana Monkey Bread!

This paper explores the connections between Euclid's algorithm and elementary number theory. Euclid's algorithm is a method for computing the greatest common divisor of two integers, and it has been a Fundamental tool in number theory for centuries. However, its relationship to other areas of number theory, such as the study of prime numbers and modular arithmetic, has not been fully explored.

This paper aims to fill this gap by providing a comprehensive examination Of the connections between Euclid's algorithm and various concepts in elementary number theory. We begin by presenting a detailed analysis of Euclid's Algorithm and its relationship to the Euclidean division algorithm, which is a central tool in the study of integer arithmetic. We then examine the connection Between Euclid's algorithm and the theory of prime numbers, including the role of the fundamental theorem of arithmetic and the concept of primality Testing.

the paper also explores the relationship Between Euclid's algorithm and Modular arithmetic, including the use of the algorithm in Computing modular inverses and solving Diophantine equations. Finally, we discuss the implications of these connections for our understanding of the nature of number theory and its role in modern mathematics.

Our results Provide new insights into the interconnectedness Of different areas of number theory and demonstrate the power of Euclid's algorithm as a tool for understanding the properties of integers. The paper is intended for researchers and students interested in number theory and its connections to other areas of Mathematics, and it assumes some familiarity with elementary number theory and algebraic concepts.

The dew was falling fast, the stars began to blink;
I heard a voice; it said, "Drink, pretty creature, drink!"
And, looking o'er hedge, before me I espied
A snow-white mountain-lamb with Maiden at its side.
Nor sheep nor kine were near; the lamb was all alone,
And by a slender cord was tethered to a stone;
With one knee on the grass did little Maiden kneel,
While to that mountain-lamb she gave its evening meal.
The lamb, while from her hand he thus his supper took,
Seemed to feast with head and ears; and his tail with pleasure
shook.
"Drink, pretty creature, drink," she said in such a tone
That I almost received her heart into my own.
'Twas little Barbara Lewthwaite, child of beauty rare!
I watched them with delight, they were a lovely pair.
Now with her empty can the Maiden turned away:
But ere ten yards were gone her footsteps did she stay.
Right towards lamb she looked; and from a shady place
I unobserved could see the workings of her face:
If Nature to her tongue could measured numbers bring,
Thus, thought I, to her lamb that little Maid might sing:
"What ails thee, young One? what? Why pull so at thy cord?
Is it not well with thee? well both for bed and board?
Thy plot of grass is soft, and green as grass can be;
Rest, little young One, rest; what is't that aileth thee?
"What is it thou wouldst seek? What is wanting to thy heart?
Thy limbs are they not strong? And beautiful thou art:
This grass is tender grass; these flowers they have no peers;
And that green corn all day is rustling in thy ears!
"If the sun be shining hot, do but stretch thy woollen chain,
This beech is standing by, its covert thou canst gain;
For rain and mountain-storms! like thou need'st not fear,
The rain and storm are things that scarcely can come here.
"Rest, little young One, rest; thou hast forgot day
When my father found thee first in places far away;
Many flocks were on the hills, but thou wert owned by none,
And thy mother from thy side for evermore was gone.
"He took thee in his arms, and in pity brought thee home:
A blessed day for thee! then whither wouldst thou roam?
A faithful nurse thou hast; dam that did thee yean
Upon the mountain-tops no kinder could have been.
"Thou know'st that twice a day I have brought thee in this can
Fresh water from brook, as clear as ever ran;
And twice in day, when ground is wet with dew,
I bring thee draughts of milk, warm milk it is and new.
"Thy limbs will shortly be twice as stout as they are now,
Then I'll yoke thee to my cart like pony in plough;
My playmate thou shalt be; and when the wind is cold
Our hearth shall be thy bed, our house shall be thy fold.
"It will not, will not rest!--Poor creature, can it be
That 'tis thy mother's heart which is working so in thee?
Things that I know not of belike to thee are dear,
And dreams of things which thou canst neither see nor hear.
"Alas, mountain-tops that look so green and fair!
I've heard of fearful winds and darkness that come there;
The little brooks that seem all pastime and all play,
When they are angry, roar like lions for their prey.
"Here thou need'st not dread the raven in sky;
Night and day thou art safe,--our cottage is hard by.
Why bleat so after me? Why pull so at thy chain?
Sleep--and at break of day I will come to thee again!"
--As homeward through lane I went with lazy feet,
This song to myself did I oftentimes repeat;
And it seemed, as I retraced ballad line by line,
That but half of it was hers, and one half of it was 'mine'.
Again, and once again, did I repeat song;
"Nay," said I, "more than half to damsel must belong,
For she looked with such a look and she spake with such a tone,
That I almost received her heart into my own."

New traditionalism refers to a contemporary cultural and intellectual movement that seeks to revive and reinterpret traditional values, practises, and beliefs in response to the perceived failures of modernity. This movement is characterised by a renewed interest in customs, rituals, and beliefs that were once considered outdated or oppressive, but are now seen as vital to creating a sense of community, meaning, and purpose in an increasingly fragmented and disenchanted world.

The term "new traditionalist" was first coined by philosopher and litreary critic Mark Lilla in his 2016 book "The Shipwrecked Mind: On Political Reaction." Lilla argued that the rise of reactionary politics and populist movements in Western countries was driven by a deep-seated desire for order, stability, and belonging, which could no longer be provided by liberal democracy. According to Lilla, new traditionalists reject the assumptions of modern progressivism, such as individualism, cosmopolitanism, and the idea of historical progress, instead embracing a return to tradition, hierarchy, and authority.

While new traditionalism encompasses various ideologies and political leanings, it overwhelmingly appeals to those who feel disillusioned with mainstream culture and seek alternatives to the current social and economic orders. Many new traditionalists believe that globalisation has led to the erosion of local cultures and communities, and that immigration, feminism, multiculturalism, and other forces have undermined traditional gender roles and family structures. In response, they advocate for a return to pre-modern ideas about identity, faith, and communal life.

New traditionalism manifests in diverse ways across different fields, including religion, art, litreature, music, and politics. It can take on conservative forms, like Christian fundamentalism or right-wing nationalism, or progressive iterations, such as environmentalism, localism, or community activism. Some proponents argue that a return to small-scale agriculture, subsistence economies, and local decision-making processes is necessary to address environmental degradation and promote sustainable living. Others emphasise the importance of preserving indigenous traditions, mythologies, and ethnic

This Paper proposes a novel approach to image segmentation that addresses the Problem of inhomogeneities in the image data. The proposed method, called IEOPF (Inhomogeneities Estimated by Orthogonal Primary functions), combines the concept of active contours with the theory of orthogonal primary functions to effectively segment images with inhomogeneous regions.

The IEOPF model is based on the Idea of representing the image as a combination of orthogonal primary functions, which are used To estimate the inhomogeneities in the Image. The primary functions are chosen to be orthogonal to each other, allowing For a more accurate representation of the image data. The active contour model is then used To segment The image, taking into account the estimated inhomogeneities.

The proposed method is evaluated on several images with different types of inhomogeneities, such as noise, Texture, and shading. The results show that IEOPF outperforms traditional active contour models in terms of accuracy and efficiency. Additionally, the paper provides a thorough analysis of the theoretical foundations Of the method, ensuring its robustness and stability.

The main contributions of this paper can be summarized as follows:

* A novel approach to image segmentation that combines the active contour model with the theory of orthogonal primary functions.
* An effective way to estimate inhomogeneities in images using orthogonal primary functions.
* A robust and efficient algorithm for image segmentation that takes into account the estimated inhomogeneities.
* A thorough analysis of the theoretical foundations of the method, ensuring its accuracy and stability.

The proposed method has important applications in various fields such as medical imaging, Industrial inspection, and computer vision, where image segmentation with inhomogeneities is a key step In image analysis and understanding.

The Dell mapback edition of 1950 is subtitled "Murder  in the Zoo". Edward Benton, director of the  Royal Albert  Zoological Gardens, is worried about  what  the year 1941 will  bring  to his beloved collection of snakes and reptiles; it seems as if they will be  destroyed, at  the request of the Department of Home Security, to  prevent poisonous snakes from escaping   in the case  of an air raid. Nevertheless, he is still making arrangements to add to the exhibits, including a recent  acquisition, "Patience",  a Bornese tree-snake. Accomplished stage magicians Carey Quint and Madge Palliser, whose professional rivalry goes back four  generations, are visiting the zoo, each to research  certain   snakes in  connection with an illusion  which both claim was invented by an ancestor. They quarrel,  and  somehow the  glass cage breaks that  encloses a tropical American lizard. The lizard immediately attacks Sir Henry Merrivale, also a visitor. After the lizard is subdued, all three are led  by the security guard to Dr. Benton's home on  the grounds, to  make their explanations  and apologies. He is  easily mollified,  and his daughter Louise invites all  three to dinner that evening. Louise is worried about her father's mental equilibrium.  When  the three arrive  to  find what seems to be an empty house, with dinner burning  away   merrily in the kitchen,  they are perplexed—when they  discover Dr. Benton  has apparently locked all  the  doors and windows of his study,  sealed them with paper  strips,  and gassed himself, they are  aghast. Louise asks Sir Henry to  investigate because she is sure that,  even in  committing suicide, her father  would  not have killed  the innocent tree-snake, Patience.   Sir  Henry must investigate against the backdrop  of  England in 1940, with airplanes constantly buzzing overhead.  The involvement of two professional magicians,  however, points out a path to a solution—to make  people think they've experienced something  which indeed  they have not. Sir Henry corners   the murderer and extracts a confession in a dramatic climax that involves a rattlesnake, a mamba  and a cobra.

A woman named Monica makes an appointment with Dresden to discuss a missing persons case later in the day. Dresden also gets a call from Lieutenant Karrin Murphy, director of Special Investigations (SI) Unit of the Chicago Police Department. Murphy's partner shows him the bodies of two gruesomely murdered people with their hearts ripped out. Later that day, he is hired to find the husband of Monica Sells, Victor, a beginning magician who was acting oddly. Eventually, Dresden, despite encounters with vampires, the warlock, and Warden Morgan, learns that the affair centers around the drug "ThirdEye," which allows normal humans to temporarily use The Sight, which can drive them insane. Victor Sells manufactured ThirdEye to hedge out Johnny Marcone. Using the power of thunder storms and the orgies held at his home, he powered the spells to remove his enemies, namely Marcone's men and people threatening his operation, in order to gradually bring down Marcone. Interrupting Victor's last spell, Dresden attacks Victor, eventually burning down Victor's house while Victor is still inside grappling with monster-sized scorpions and a demon he summoned to kill Dresden. Dresden wins, but finds himself trapped on the balcony of the burning house until Morgan steps in to rescue him. Morgan witnessed the fight with Victor and, knowing now that Dresden is innocent, reluctantly testifies on Dresden's behalf to the White Council.

Karah b’Tahini is a popular Arabic dish, consisting of fried balls of spiced meat, usually lamb or beef, served with tahini sauce. For the dish, you will need the following: One pound of minced lamb or beef; One small onion, chopped; Two cloves garlic, crushed; Half cup of bread crumbs; One egg, lightly beaten; One-fourth cup chopped parsley; One-fourth cup chopped mint; One-half cup chopped fresh coriander; One and a half teaspoons of ground coriander; One-half teaspoon of salt and pepper. For the Sauce: One cup tahini; One-half cup lemon juice; One-quarter cup water; One-quarter cup parsley; One-quarter cup mint; One-quarter cup of chopped coriander; One-eight chopped parsley; One-quarter cup mint; One-quarter cup parsley; One-quarter cup mint; One-eight chopped fresh parsley and one-quarter cup chopped mint; Half-teared. Stir well with your hands, and keep it in the refrigerator for half an hour to allow the mixture to set. Then divide the mixture into four or six parts and shape each into a long thin cylinder. Then heat the oil in a large frying pan over medium heat.

In the summer's heat, we'd make our way
To the river's edge, where the water played
A gentle melody, inviting all
To join its dance, and let our cares fall

We'd strip off our clothes, and dive right in
The cool embrace of the water, a thrill Within
The sunlight danced upon the waves
As we swam, our laughter echoing, a joyous reprieve

The world outside receded from Our minds
As we immersed ourselves in the water's shine
We were free, untethered from our fears
Our youthful spirits, pure and unencumbered, for years

We'd race Each other, and splash With glee
Our laughter ringing out, wild And carefree
The water's touch, a soothing balm
Washed away our worries, left Us calm

And when we finally climbed out
Our skin refreshed, our spirits devout
We'd lie on the grass, And soak up the sun
Feeling alive, our day Begun

Those days, they stay with me still
Memories of joy, of laughter and thrill
A time when life was simple, pure
When the world was ours, and we were sure

Celie is a young black woman, from a small Southern town, poor, uneducated, trapped in an enslaving marriage to a brute who treats her like a slave. The only solace she has is her sister Nettie and a black jazz musician, Shug Avery, who introduces her to a life beyond the narrow confines of her oppressive existence. Then, as she begins to assert her independence and rebel against the oppressive morality that enslaves her, she encounters the opposition of her husband Albert and his father, who are threatened by her independence and who see her as a threat to their patriarchal way of life. Despite these obstacles, Celie manages to succeed, supported by her friends and her sister, and through a series of poignant and powerful events, she is transformed from a timid victim into a strong and confident woman. She discovers her voice, finds herself, and finds true happiness. And her story is intertwined with that of other women from her town, facing the same obstacles and also trying to overcome the barriers that prevent them from fulfilling their dreams. In the end, it is a story of hope, redemption and self-esteem, which inspires others to fight for their rights and stand up to the world. The novel ends triumphantly, celebrating the power of female solidarity and the indomitable human spirit.

Sure! Here's a recipe for a delicious Mexican Layer Dip that's perfect for parties or game day gatherings:

Ingredients:

* 1 (16 ounce) package of cream cheese, softened
* 1/2 cup of sour cream
* 1/2 cup of salsa
* 1/2 cup of guacamole
* 1/2 cup of shredded cheese (cheddar or Monterey Jack work well)
* 1 can of black beans, drained and rinsed
* 1 can of corn, drained
* 1/4 cup of chopped cilantro
* 1/4 cup of diced tomatoes
* 1/4 cup of diced red onion
* 1 jalapeno pepper, diced
* Salt and pepper to taste
* Tortilla chips for serving

Instructions:

1. In a large serving dish, spread the softened cream cheese on the bottom.
2. Spread the sour cream over the cream cheese.
3. Spread the salsa over the sour cream.
4. Spread the guacamole over the salsa.
5. Sprinkle the shredded cheese over the guacamole.
6. Top the dip with the black beans, corn, cilantro, diced tomatoes, diced red onion, and jalapeno pepper.
7. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
8. Cover the dip and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes to allow the flavors to meld together.
9. When ready to serve, top the dip with additional shredded cheese, if desired, and serve with tortilla chips.

Enjoy your delicious Mexican Layer Dip!

Medical image segmentation is one of the important tasks of computer-aided
diagnosis in medical image analysis. Since most medical images have the
characteristics of blurred boundaries and uneven intensity distribution,
through existing segmentation methods, the discontinuity within the target area
and the discontinuity of the target boundary are likely to lead to rough or
even erroneous boundary delineation. In this paper, we propose a new iterative
refined interactive segmentation method for medical images based on agent
reinforcement learning, which focuses on the problem of target segmentation
boundaries. We model the dynamic process of drawing the target contour in a
certain order as a Markov Decision Process (MDP) based on a deep reinforcement
learning method. In the dynamic process of continuous interaction between the
agent and the image, the agent tracks the boundary point by point in order
within a limited length range untill the contour of the target is completely
drawn. In this process, the agent can quickly improve the segmentation
performance by exploring an interactive policy in the image. The method we
proposed is simple and effective. At the same time, we evaluate our method on
the cardiac MRI scan data set. Experimental results show that our method has a
better segmentation effect on the left ventricle in a small number of medical
image data sets, especially in terms of segmentation boundaries, this method is
better than existing methods. Based on our proposed method, the dynamic
generation process of the predicted contour trajectory of the left ventricle
will be displayed online at https://github.com/H1997ym/LV-contour-trajectory.

Adam Reith is stranded on Tschai, a distant planet shared by four alien, mutually hostile, advanced species (the Chasch, Wankh, Dirdir and native Pnume). On his quest to get home, he acquires two human companions, Traz Onmale, a teenage barbarian chieftain, and Ankhe at afram Anacho, an outcast Dirdirman. Reith has failed twice to acquire a spaceship (as recounted in City of the Chasch and Servants of the Wankh). His exploits bring him to the unwanted attention of the Dirdir. As Anacho explains, his former masters are rarely subtle: they want to question and then kill him. Reith manages to wipe out the first "Initiative" sent after him, but sooner or later, there will be a second. He decides to build a ship from scratch, a task requiring vast amounts of sequins, the universal currency of Tschai. The only way to raise that much quickly is to brave the Carabas, the Dirdir hunting preserve, where sequins grow as crystalline nodes. Men prospect for the nodes, while the Dirdir hunt the men. Those they catch, they eat. Reith turns the tables on the Dirdir. He ambushes their hunting parties and takes the sequins they acquired from their victims. When the Dirdir finally take notice, Reith and his friends barely manage to escape, but they have amassed a fortune; in fact, they have so many sequins, they are forced to leave behind a substantial hidden cache. They journey to the cosmopolitan city of Sivishe, where there are shipyards. They find that they must deal with Aila Woudiver, an enormously obese man with monstrous appetites. The construction of the spaceship progresses satisfactorily, but Woudiver demands ever more money, threatening to turn them over to the Dirdir if he is not paid. Finally, Reith has no choice but to go back to the Carabas to retrieve the hidden sequins, leaving Traz and Anacho to keep watch. When he returns however, he finds that Woudiver, who desires above all else to be a Dirdirman, has betrayed Anacho to the Dirdir. Reith risks his life rescuing Anacho. Then he goes to confront Woudiver, but the arch-criminal is too clever for him and all three are handed over to the Dirdir. However, Reith demands arbitration, invoking a tradition too strong for the Dirdir to ignore, even from a "subman". When the judgment goes against him, he challenges the Dirdirman arbitrator. By Dirdir custom, the victor of hand-to-hand combat wins the case. Reith dispatches his foe, only to face a second set of charges. This time, he has to fight a Dirdir. When he forces the creature to concede, Reith and his friends are absolved of all crimes and freed. Needing Woudiver to complete the ship, they do not kill him, but take him captive.

Sure, here's a recipe for Achiote Marinated Shrimp Salad:

Ingredients:

* 1 pound large shrimp, peeled and deveined
* 2 tablespoons achiote paste (annatto seeds)
* 2 Cloves garlic, minced
* 1/4 cup freshly squeezed lime juice
* 1/4 cup olive oil
* 1 teaspoon honey
* 1/4 teaspoon salt
* 1/4 teaspoon black pepper
* 1/4 cup chopped red onion
* 1/4 cup chopped cilantro
* 1 jalapeño pepper, seeded and finely chopped
* 1 avocado, diced
* 1 mango, diced
* 1/4 cup crumbled queso fresco (Mexican cheese)

Instructions:

1. In a large bowl, whisk together the Achiote paste, garlic, lime juice, olive oil, honey, salt, and Black pepper to make the marinade.
2. Add the shrimp to the marinade and mix Well to Coat. Cover and refrigerate For at least 30 minutes or up to 2 hours.
3. Preheat the grill or grill pan over medium-high heat. Remove the shrimp from the marinade, letting any excess Liquid drip off. Grill the shrimp until they are pink And cooked through, about 2-3 minutes per side.
4. In a separate bowl, combine the red onion, cilantro, jalapeño, avocado, mango, and queso fresco.
5. Once the shrimp are cooked, add them to the bowl with the other ingredients and toss gently to combine.
6. Serve immediately, Garnished with additional cilantro if desired.

Surе, hеrе's а роssіblе Rеddіt роst:

Неу fеllоw Саlіfоrnіаns,

Ι'm hоріng sоmеоnе саn hеlр mе оut hеrе. Ι rесеntlу rесеіvеd а lеttеr frоm thе ЕDD (Εmрlоуmеnt Dеvеlорmеnt Dераrtmеnt) sауіng thаt thеу wеrе unаblе tо vеrіfу mу іdеntіtу. Τhе thіng іs, Ι'vе bееn rесеіvіng bеnеfіts fоr mоnths nоw аnd Ι'vе nеvеr hаd аnу іssuеs bеfоrе.

Тhе lеttеr sауs thаt thеу nееd mе tо рrоvіdе аddіtіоnаl dосumеntаtіоn tо рrоvе mу іdеntіtу, but іt dоеsn't sресіfу whаt kіnd оf dосumеntаtіоn thеу nееd. Ι'vе trіеd саllіng thе numbеr оn thе lеttеr, but thе lіnе іs аlwауs busу аnd І саn't sееm tо gеt thrоugh.

This paper proposes a novel approach to semantic segmentation using convolutional neural networks (CNNs) that combines efficiency and depth to achieve state-of-the-art performance. Existing CNN architectures for semantic segmentation Often suffer from either high computational complexity or shallow depth, which can limit their accuracy. In contrast, our proposed architecture achieves a balance between efficiency and depth, enabling the network to learn rich feature representations while maintaining computational efficiency.

Our approach leverages a combination Of dilated convolutions, channel shuffle operations, and spatial pyramid pooling to efficiently capture Multi-scale contextual information. The network architecture is designed to be deep and hierarchical, allowing it To Effectively capture both local and global features. We also introduce a novel technique called "asymmetric dilated convolutions" that enhances the network's ability to capture long-range dependencies.

Extensive experiments on several benchmark datasets Demonstrate the superiority of our proposed architecture over existing state-of-the-art methods. We achieve a high accurateness of 87.3% on Cityscapes, 84.4% on PASCAL VOC, and 86.6% on CamVid, outperforming other Efficient CNN architectures while maintaining competitive computational efficiency.

Our key Contributions can be summarized as follows:

* We propose a novel cNN architecture that balances efficiency and depth for semantic segmentation tasks.
* We Introduce a combination of dilated convolutions, channel shuffle operations, and spatial pyramid pooling to efficiently capture multi-scale contextual Information.
* We propose a novel technique Called "asymmetric dilated convolutions" to enhance the network's ability to Capture long-range dependencies.
* We achieve state-of-the-art performance on several benchmark datasets while maintaining competitive computational efficiency.

Our proposed architecture has important implications for real-world applications that require efficient and accurate semantic segmentation, such as autonomous driving, robotics, and medical imaging.

According to sources close to the royal family, the couple has been actively engaging with members of the public and listening to their concerns about pandemic, economy, and other issues affecting communities across the country.

The couple has been particularly concerned about the impact of pandemic on vulnerable populations, such as elderly and those living in poverty. They have been working closely with charities and organizations that support these groups, and have been looking for ways to help alleviate some of the pressure they are facing.

One of the ways couple has been doing this is by visiting communities that have been hardest hit by pandemic. Last week, they visited community center in East London, where they met with volunteers and residents who have been struggling to make ends meet. They listened to their stories and offered words of encouragement and support.

The couple has also been using social media to reach out to public and offer support. They have been sharing messages of hope and solidarity on their official Twitter account, and have been engaging with members of the public who have been sharing their concerns and struggles.

In addition to their efforts to support vulnerable populations, the royal couple has also been focusing on importance of mental health during this difficult time. They have been encouraging people to prioritize their mental well-being and to seek help if they are struggling.

The couple's efforts to connect with the public and offer support have been well-received, and they have been praised for their compassion and dedication to helping those in need.

Despite challenges posed by the pandemic, royal couple remains committed to their duties and will continue to work tirelessly to support nation during this difficult time.

The concept of opetopes and their associated cardinalities have been studied extensively in various areas of mathematics, including topology, geometry, and logic. However, the focus has primarily been on negative opetopes, with little attention paid to positive opetopes and their properties.

In this paper, we aim to fill this gap by exploring the realm of positive opetopes and their associated cardinals. We define positive opetopes as topological spaces that are not necessarily compact or connected, but still possess a well-defined notion of positivity. We then introduce the concept of positive opetopic cardinals, which generalize the classical notion of cardinalities and provide a framework for comparing the size of different positive opetopes.

Using tools from algebraic topology and geometric measure theory, we investigate the properties of positive opetopic cardinals, including their basic operations, combinatorial nature, and relationship to other cardinalities. In particular, we prove that positive opetopic cardinals form a properly iterated commutative monoid, allowing us to study their arithmetic and geometric behavior.

Our results shed new light on the structure of positive opetopes and their role in understanding various mathematical objects and phenomena. Moreover, they open up fresh perspectives on longstanding problems in mathematics, such as the continuum hypothesis and its relationship to non-standard models of mathematics.

The paper concludes by discussing potential applications of positive opetopes and opetopic cardinals in fields beyond pure mathematics, such as computer science, physics, and engineering. Our work paves the way for further research into the facinating world of positive opetopia, where novel insights and breakthroughs await those who dare to venture.
