In a small village nestled in the English countryside, a mysterious egg has been hidden away for generations. 

The villagers know it as the Pinhoe Egg, and they whisper stories of its supposed powers to each other in hushed tones. Some say it can grant wishes, while others claim it holds the key to eternal life. 

When the elderly owner of the egg passes away, it falls into the hands of his young niece, Alexandra. She is skeptical of the tales surrounding the egg, but she cannot deny the strange sensation she feels when holding it. As she begins to dream vivid, fantastical dreams, she starts to wonder if there's truth to the legends after all. Meanwhile, a wealthy collector named Marcus learns of the egg's existence and becomes obsessed with obtaining it. He believes that the egg will complete his collection of rare artifacts and solidify his status as a leading expert in the field. 

When he discovers that Alexandra is the new owner, he sets out to charm her and convince her to sell him the egg. 

As Alexandra navigates her growing feelings for Marcus and the increasing pressure to give up the egg, she begins to uncover the dark secrets surrounding the Pinhoe Egg. She discovers that her ancestors made a pact with a powerful being to protect the egg, and that it's said to have the power to bring about both great good and terrible evil. 

With the fate of the egg – and perhaps even the world – at stake, Alexandra must decide whether to keep it safe or risk everything to explore its potential. Can she resist the temptation of the egg's rumored powers, or will she succumb to the lure of the unknown? 

And what will happen if the egg falls into the wrong hands?

Veteran actor Michael Douglas has sparked speculation that he may be retiring from acting after revealing that his upcoming film will be his last.

In interview with a leading film magazine, Douglas, 75, said that he was "looking forward to a bit of a rest" and indicated that he may be hanging up his acting shoes for good.

"I've been fortunate enough to have had wonderful career, but I think it's time to take step back and focus on my personal life," he said.

Douglas has been a household name for decades, thanks to his roles in a string of successful films including "Wall Street," "Fatal Attraction," and "Basic Instinct." He has won numerous awards for his performances, including two Oscars and Golden Globe.

Despite his success, Douglas has hinted that he may be ready to move on from acting. "I've been doing this for a long time, and I feel like I've accomplished everything I set out to do," he said. "I'm not saying I'll never act again, but I'm certainly not looking to take on any more major roles."

Douglas's upcoming film, drama titled "The Kominsky Method," is set to be released later this year. It follows the story of aging actor who moves to Los Angeles to pursue career in acting and will also star Alan Arkin and Nancy Travis.

While Douglas has not officially confirmed his retirement, his comments have sparked speculation that he may be preparing to bow out of the spotlight. Fans have taken to social media to express their sadness at news, with many paying tribute to Douglas's impressive career and legacy in film.

Despite the speculation, Douglas has not yet made any official statement regarding his future plans. It remains to be seen whether he will indeed retire from acting or continue to grace silver screen with his presence.

Following up the bestseller The Millionaire Next Door, The Millionaire Mind analyzes the common environmental and lifestyle factors that preceded and resulted in this researched segment's ability to accumulate wealth. The book raises the following questions: What success factors made them wealthy in one generation? 

What part did luck and school play? 

How do they find the courage to take financial risks? 

How did they find their ideal vocations? What are their spouses like and how did they choose them? How do they run their households? 

How do they buy and sell their homes? 

What are their favorite leisure activities? Dr. Stanley's research on how the average American millionaire attained financial success are based on in-depth surveys and interviews with more than 1,300 millionaires. Personal details from this research are shared in the book include memories from their school days, personal thoughts on being "the smart kid in the dumb row," making difficult financial decisions, selecting a vocation and spending habits.

Erin Brockovich is a powerful and inspiring true story that will leave you both outraged and uplifted. The film tells the  tale of  a real-life legal  clerk  who, despite her lack  of formal education or experience, helped expose a massive water pollution scandal in a small town in California. Julia Roberts gives a phenomenal performance  as  Erin Brockovich, bringing depth  and nuance to  a character who is  both fiercely determined and vulnerable at the same time.

The film is well-paced, with a gripping narrative that keeps you on the edge of your  seat as you watch  Erin navigate the complex  and often frustrating world  of corporate  greed and  cover-ups. The  supporting cast is also excellent, particularly Albert Finney  as Erin's boss and mentor, Ed Masry.

One of   the things that sets this film    apart  is its unflinching portrayal of  the  realities of class struggle and environmental injustice. The town of Hinkley, where the story takes  place, is  a tight-knit community where everyone knows each other,  and  the  effects of  the pollution  are devastating and  widespread. The film doesn't  shy  away from showing the human cost of corporate greed,   and it's a powerful reminder of the importance of standing up for  what's right, even  in the  face of overwhelming obstacles.

Overall, Erin Brockovich is  a  must-see for anyone who loves a good true story, and  it's a  testament to the  power of determination and perseverance. It's a  reminder that even the smallest person can make a big difference when they refuse  to back  down in the face of  injustice.

Oregon Valley (Coos language: Atsixiis) is a town located in Oregon County, California, United States, where the Oregon Creek enters Portland Lake on the Atlantic Ocean. The county borders the borough of South Bend, and together they are sometimes referred to as one entity called both Oregon Bay-North Bend or California's Peninsula Area. Oregon Bay's population as of the 2020 census was 15,985 people, making it the most populated county on the Northwest Coast. Portland's Bay Area is estimated to be home to 32,308 (Oregon Island Census City Division). History
Prior to Americans first visiting the California coast, Native American peoples had the Oregon River area as their homeland for hundreds of years. members of the Coos, Upper Umpqua, Siuslaw and Oregon peoples traveled, hunted, hunted and gathered along Coos Lake and its tributaries, across streams, and in lakes and meadows. Nearly 400 years later, French and French expeditions first approached the West Pacific. In 1579, Sir Francis Drake was alleged to have offered refuge for his vessel, the Silver Hinde, around Cape Arago. Trader and explorer Samuel Powell was in the area obtaining furs, and the Hudson's Bay Company dispatched William Duncan Mackenzie to search for an ocean passage.

This peach, so sweet and juicy too,
Taught me things I never knew,
Its softness made my heart sing,
And its flavor took my tongue on wing.

In summer days when heat was high,
It cooled my skin and dried my sighs,
its downy fuzz, a gentle touch,
made me feel blessed in every clutch.

When biting into it, oh what joy!
The world was mine to employ,
No need for words or tutor's voice.
For this peach taught with grace and poise.

With every bite, new lessons grew,
Of patience and of mellow hue,
the pit within, hard as stone,
Showed how strength can be shown.

Here's a recipe for a delicious vegan spinach and artichoke dip that's perfect for snacking or entertaining:

Ingredients:

* 1 (14 oz) can artichoke hearts, drained and chopped
* 1 package frozen chopped spinach, thawed and drained
* 1/2 cup vegan cream cheese (such as Tofutti or Kite Hill)
* 1/4 cup nutritional yeast
* 1/4 cup lemon juice
* 1/4 cup olive oil
* 1/2 teaspoon garlic powder
* 1/2 teaspoon onion powder
* Salt and pepper, to taste
* 1/4 cup chopped fresh parsley
* 1/4 cup chopped fresh basil
* 1/4 cup vegan Parmesan cheese (such as nutritional yeast or Daiya)

Instructions:

1. 

Preheat your oven to 350°F (180°C). 2. In a large mixing bowl, combine the chopped artichoke hearts, thawed spinach, vegan cream cheese, nutritional yeast, lemon juice, olive oil, garlic powder, onion powder, salt, and pepper. Mix well until everything is fully incorporated. 3. 

Transfer the mixture to a baking dish and sprinkle the chopped parsley, basil, and vegan Parmesan cheese on top. 

4. Bake the dip in the preheated oven for 20-25 minutes, or until it's hot, bubbly, and slightly golden brown on top. 

5. 

Remove the dip from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes before serving. Serve hot with tortilla chips, pita bread, or crackers. Enjoy! 

Note: If you want to make this dip even creamier, you can add an additional 1/4 cup of vegan cream

In this paper, we  present a  novel approach to convolutional  neural networks (CNNs) that leverages the principles  of hyperbolic geometry  to create a fully hyperbolic  convolutional neural network (FHCN).   Traditional CNNs  are  limited  by their reliance   on Euclidean geometry,  which restricts their ability  to capture complex and hierarchical patterns in data. In  contrast, FHCNs  use  hyperbolic geometry to create  a hierarchical representation of data that allows for   more effective feature extraction  and learning.

We demonstrate the effectiveness of FHCNs through  experiments on several benchmark datasets, including image classification, object detection,  and natural language processing tasks. Our results show that  FHCNs significantly outperform traditional CNNs in these tasks, achieving state-of-the-art performance  in many cases.

We also analyze the properties   of FHCNs  and show  that they have several desirable  properties,  such as increased  robustness to  noise  and better  generalization  to unseen data. We also demonstrate that FHCNs can be  easily adapted to different domains and  tasks, making them a versatile tool for a wide range of applications.

Our work provides strong evidence for the  potential of hyperbolic geometry in   deep learning, and opens up new avenues for research in this area. We believe  that FHCNs  have the potential to revolutionize the field of computer vision and natural language processing, and could have significant impacts on fields such  as robotics, autonomous vehicles, and medical imaging.

In summary, our paper presents a novel approach to  CNNs  that leverages the principles of hyperbolic geometry   to  create a fully hyperbolic convolutional neural network. Our results demonstrate the effectiveness of FHCNs and highlight their potential to significantly improve  the state-of-the-art  in a wide range  of applications.

According to recent data released by the Federal Reserve, industrial output in the United States grew at a slower pace in the month of August, signaling a potential slowdown in the country's economic growth. 

The report showed that industrial production, which includes output from factories, mines, and utilities, rose by 0.2% in August, down from a 0.5% increase in July. This was below economists' expectations of a 0.3% gain. The slowdown in industrial output growth was largely driven by a decline in the production of motor vehicles and parts, which fell by 2.7% in August. 

This was the largest decline in this category since May 2018. 

In addition, output in the mining sector, which includes oil and gas production, also decreased by 1.5% in August, following a 2.2% increase in July. However, there were some positive takeaways from the report. Output in the utilities sector, for example, rose by 1.6% in August, due to an increase in demand for electricity and natural gas. 

The report also showed that capacity utilization, which measures the percentage of plants and facilities in use, edged down to 77.8% in August from 78% in July. 

This suggests that there may be some slack in the economy, which could help to keep inflation in check. Despite the slowdown in industrial output growth, economists remain optimistic about the overall health of the US economy. 

Many expect the economy to continue growing, albeit at a slower pace, in the coming months. The troubled Marsh & McLennan Companies, a global insurance brokerage and risk management firm, is under scrutiny from the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) over its financial practises, the BBC has learned.

Sources close to the matter have revealed that the SEC has launched an investigation into Marsh's financial reporting and corporate governance practises, amid concerns that the company may have misled investors and regulators about its financial health.

The investigation is said to be focused on Marsh's use of "finite reinsurance" products, which are controversial financial instruments that allow companies to transfer risk to other parties. Critics argue that finite reinsurance can be used to manipulate financial results and mislead investors, and regulators have been cracking down on the practise in recent years.

Marsh has already faced regulatory scrutiny over its use of finite reinsurance. In 2019, the company agreed to pay $80 million to settle charges brought by the SEC that it had misled investors about its financial results by using the products to artificially boost its earnings.

The current investigation is believed to be focused on whether Marsh has continued to use finite reinsurance products in a way that violates securities laws. The SEC is also examining the company's corporate governance practises, including the role of its board of directors in overseeing the company's financial reporting and risk management.

Marsh has declined to comment on the investigation, but sources close to the company have indicated that it is cooperating fully with the SEC. The company has also said that it has made significant changes to its financial reporting and risk management practises in recent years, and that it is committed to transparency and accountability.

The investigation comes at a difficult time for Marsh, which has been facing challenges in its core insurance brokerage business. The company has been hit by declining revenues and profits, and has been forced to cut costs and restructure its operations in response.

The SEC's investigation is the latest in a series of regulatory probes into the financial practises of major corporations.

The fourth collection belongs with the first as perhaps one of the two collections most focused on Morpheus himself. It begins with an Endless family meeting descending almost immediately into an Endless family argument. Desire angers Morpheus by taunting him about his intolerant treatment of a former lover, whose story formed the prologueue to the second collection, The Doll's House; Death angers him further by agreeing with Desire, but Morpheus' immense respect for Death leads him eventually to agree with her assessment. Morpheus leaves his realm to travel to Hell, where he imprisoned his former lover Nada, to release her. Having left Lucifer, lord of Hell, very angry with him the last time he ventured there (in the first collection, Preludes and Nocturnes), Morpheus is apprehensive about the task. He sets about it, wanting to do what is right, but prepared for a confrontation which he knows he may lose. In the event, his apprehension is somewhat misplaced. As he arrives, Lucifer is busy closing down Hell. Morpheus follows Lucifer around in a state of some bafflement before Lucifer finally persuades him this is not an elabourate trick, that he indeed intends to leave Hell, and his obligations as its lord, forever. His final act before leaving is to throw out any demon or damned souls still hanging around, lock all the portals to Hell and cut off his wings; he then hands the key to Hell to Morpheus, to do with as he will. This episode sets up the basis for the spin-off comic series Lucifer written by Mike Carey. Morpheus, who has no wish to rule this troublesome piece of real estate, quickly discovers that there are numerous entities who want to control Hell or prevent their enemies from controlling it. Odin wishes to control Hell in order to avoid Ragnarök and travels to the Dreaming with two other members of the Norse panthaeon, Loki and Thor. Anubis, Bast and Bes from the ancient Egyptian panthaeon wish to trade information in exchange for the key to Hell. Susano-o-no-Mikoto, a storm god of the Shinto panthaeon, travels as an individual deity, and not as a representative of Shinto gods. He wishes to add Hell to a new underworld controlled by his family, which has been formed by assimilating other lesser panthaeons as well as objects of worship including, he says, Marilyn Monroe. Azazel, a Biblical demon, arrives with two other demons who held great power in the old Hell: Choronzon, here described as the former Duke of the Eight Circle, and Merkin, the mother of Spiders. Azazel had previously ruled Hell in a triumvirate with another demon and Lucifer, although Lucifer tells Morpheus that this was only part of a game he played, and Azazel demands that Morpheus hand him the key. In exchange Azazel offers to hand over Nada as well as the demon Choronzon who had previously fought Morpheus. Order and Chaos also arrive. Order is in the guise of an empty cardboard box carried by a floating Djinn-like being, while Chaos appears in the form of a small girl in clown makeup. Order offers to trade the dreams of the newly dead, while Chaos simply threatens Morpheus before offering a balloon. Two representatives from Faerie, Cluracan, and his sister Nuala appeal to Morpheus to give control of Hell to no one. Cluracan offers his sister as a gift to the Dream Lord, in the name of Faerie Queen. Two angels are also present, Duma the angel of silence and Remiel here presented as the angel of those who rise. The angels have been set to simply observe. Susano-o-no-Mikoto, Duma and Remiel later become important characters in the spin-off series Lucifer. Much to Morpheus's chagrin, the interested parties promptly convene in the castle at the centre of the Dreaming. Here many characters who have parts to play later in the series are introduced, amongst them the representatives of Faerie, Cluracan, and his sister Nuala. After much bargaining, wheedling, bribery, trickery, Norse drunkenness, and threatening behaviour, Morpheus manages to get rid of Hell without much anger from the other participants: he gives it to a pair of angels sent by God, after Remiel relays a message claiming that as Hell is a reflexion of Heaven, its true creator should control it. Dream then enters Azazel and frees Nada. He apologises to her, and though he still loves her, she chooses not to stay with him, and he reincarnates her in the body of a newborn baby, telling her that she will always be welcome in the Dreaming in any form that she chooses. Between these deliberations is the story "In Which the Dead Return; and Charles Rowland Concludes His Education", from issue #25, which takes place at a traditional English boarding-school (and borrows elements from the boarding-school story genre) and is used to illustrate the consequences of Hell's closure. Although the two main characters in this tale, the ghosts of two school boys, never appear again in the Sandman series, they later appear as "The Dead Boy Detectives" in Gaiman's Vertigo cross-over story The Children's Crusade, and in a mini-series of that name by Jill Thompson. The collection ends with Lucifer, sans wings, sitting on an Australian beach, grudgingly admiring God's sunset.

In the shadow of the legendary mountains of Gorlan, a young girl named Elika finds a strange artifact that has been hidden for generations. The object, known as the Heartstone, is said to have the power to bring about a new millennium of prosperity and happiness to the war-torn land.

As news of the discovery spread, Elika sees himself at the centre of a dangerous game of politics and magic. The ruthless Prince Arin, who has ruled with an iron hand for centuries, will stop at nothing to obtain the Heartstone and utilize its energy to solidify his grasp on the throne.

Elika later learns that he is not the only one searching for the Heartstone. A trio of mercenaries, led by a charismatic figure named Kael, are actually on the hunt, trying to use the artifact to overthrow the emperor and deliver democracy to the people.

Caught between three formidable armies, Elika can navigate dangerous alliances, ancient prophecies, and possibly treason if he intends to succeed. But as the stakes grow lower, he comes to believe that the real power of the Heartstone could be more than anyone would have even anticipated.

With the fate of the realm hung in the balance, Elika will know where his motives really lie. Will he join forces with the rebels and risk everything for a chance at a better future, or will he succumb to the temptation of power and side with the ruthless ruler? The decision will decide not only his own fate, but the demise of Gorlan itself.

The fireplace won't be lit this year,
No stockings hung by the chimney with care.
The tree won't be decorated, no presents underneath,
No holiday cheer, no joy to share.

I'll be far away, on a distant shore,
Serving my country, fighting for more.
I'll be thinking of home, of loved ones dear,
But I won't be there to dry their tears.

The snow won't fall on my face,
No cold winds will chill my embrace.
I'll be in a place of heat and sand,
A world away from winter's land.

I'll miss the laughter, the songs, the cheer,
The warmth of loved ones near.
But I'll be fighting for a cause,
To keep them safe, to end the pause.

I won't be home for Christmas,
But my heart will be with those I miss.
I'll be dreaming of the day I'll return,
When I'll be home, and all will be well.

Hey fellow Xenoblade fans, I just wanted to share my thoughts on why I think Turters is the strongest character in the game.

First of all, let's talk about his stats. Turters has some of the highest base stats in the game, with a whopping 120 HP and 211 Strength. This makes him a formidable force on the battlefield, able to take and dish out a ton of damage. But it's not just his raw power that makes him strong - it's also his versatility.

Turters has a unique set of abilities that make him a nightmare for enemies. His signature move, Turtle Shell, allows him to absorb massive amounts of damage and counterattack with a powerful blast of energy. This move alone makes him a valuable asset in any party. But he also has a range of other abilities that let him control the battlefield, such as his Turtle Tussle move that can push enemies away, and his Shell Shield that can block attacks and protect his allies.

But what really sets Turters apart is his ability to transform into a giant turtle. This transformation gives him a huge boost in power and speed, and allows him to unleash devastating attacks that can wipe out entire groups of enemies. And the best part is, he can stay in this form for a long time, giving him the ability to sustain himself in battle without needing to worry about running out of energy.

I know some of you might be thinking, "But what about Rex? He's got the highest stats in the game!" And I'm not gonna lie, Rex is definitely a powerful character. But Turters has something that Rex doesn't - versatility. Turters can adapt to any situation, whether it's taking on a group of enemies or going one-on-one with a tough boss. He's got the tools to handle any situation, and that's what makes him the strongest character in Xenoblade.

Surе, hеrе's а rесіре fоr Ρrаwn Ρаrtу Ρіеs thаt уоu саn trу аt уоur nехt gаthеrіng:

Іngrеdіеnts:

* 1 shееt оf рuff раstrу, thаwеd
* 1 роund lаrgе рrаwns, рееlеd аnd dеvеіnеd
* 1/2 сuр frоzеn реаs аnd саrrоts
* 1/4 сuр сhорреd оnіоn
* 1/4 сuр сhорреd frеsh раrslеу
* 1/4 сuр grаtеd сhеddаr сhееsе
* 1 еgg, bеаtеn (fоr brushіng раstrу)
* Sаlt аnd рерреr tо tаstе
* 1 tаblеsрооn оlіvе оіl

Ιnstruсtіоns:

1. Рrеhеаt thе оvеn tо 375°F (190°С). Lіnе а bаkіng shееt wіth раrсhmеnt рареr.
2. Rоll оut thе рuff раstrу оn а lіghtlу flоurеd surfасе tо а thісknеss оf аbоut 1/8 іnсh. Сut оut сіrсlеs оf раstrу usіng а сооkіе сuttеr оr thе rіm оf а glаss, аnd рlасе thеm оn thе рrераrеd bаkіng shееt.
3. Іn а bоwl, mіх tоgеthеr thе рrаwns, реаs аnd саrrоts, оnіоn, раrslеу, сhееsе, sаlt, аnd рерреr. Sрооn а smаll аmоunt оf thе mіхturе оntо оnе hаlf оf еасh раstrу сіrсlе.
4. Вrush thе еdgеs оf thе раstrу wіth thе bеаtеn еgg. Fоld thе оthеr hаlf оf thе раstrу оvеr thе fіllіng, рrеssіng thе еdgеs tо sеаl. Usе а fоrk tо сrіmр thе еdgеs аnd сrеаtе а dесоrаtіvе раttеrn.
5. Вrush thе tорs оf thе ріеs wіth thе rеmаіnіng еgg wаsh. Μаkе а smаll slіt іn thе tор оf еасh ріе tо аllоw stеаm tо еsсаре durіng сооkіng.
6. Ρlасе thе ріеs оn thе рrеhеаtеd bаkіng shееt аnd drіzzlе wіth оlіvе оіl. Ваkе fоr 20-25 mіnutеs, оr untіl thе раstrу іs gоldеn brоwn аnd thе fіllіng іs hеаtеd thrоugh.
7. Sеrvе hоt wіth dірріng sаuсе оf уоur сhоісе (suсh аs сосktаіl sаuсе оr tаrtаr sаuсе). Εnjоу!

In this article, we derive new stepsize adaptation for the normalized least
mean square algorithm (NLMS) by describing task of linear acoustic echo
cancellation from a Bayesian network perspective. Similar to well-known
Kalman filter equations, we model the acoustic wave propagation from the
loudspeaker to the microphone by a latent state vector and define linear
observation equation (to model relation between the state vector and the
observation) as well as linear process equation (to model temporal
progress of the state vector). Based on additional assumptions on the
statistics of the random variables in observation and process equation, we
apply expectation-maximization (EM) algorithm to derive an NLMS-like filter
adaptation. By exploiting conditional independence rules for Bayesian
networks, we reveal that the resulting EM-NLMS algorithm has a stepsize update
equivalent to optimal-stepsize calculation proposed by Yamamoto and
Kitayama in 1982, which has been adopted in many textbooks. As main difference,
the instantaneous stepsize value is estimated in M step of the EM algorithm
(instead of being approximated by artificially extending acoustic echo
path). The EM-NLMS algorithm is experimentally verified for synthesized
scenarios with both, white noise and male speech as input signal.

Abstract:

Accurate segmentation of cardiac magnetic resonance (MR) images is essential for diagnosis and treatment planning in various cardiovascular diseases. However, manual annotation of cardiac structures in MR images is time-consuming and challenging, limiting the availability of large-scale annotated datasets for training deep learning models. In this paper, we propose a novel self-supervised learning approach for cardiac MR image segmentation using anatomical position prediction. Our method leverages the spatial relationships between cardiac structures to learn a representation that can be used for segmentation without requiring explicit annotations. We train our model on a large dataset of unannotated cardiac MR images and evaluate its performance on a separate set of manually annotated images. The results show that our approach achieves high accuracy on multiple cardiac structure segments, outperforming other state-of-the-art self-supervised learning methods. We also demonstrate the effectiveness of our learned representation for downstream tasks such as image denoising and segmentation of rare cardiac pathologies. Our work has the potential to significantly reduce the need for manual annotation in cardiac MR image segmentation, enabling more widespread use of deep learning techniques in clinical settings.

1 1/2 cups milk
3 egg yolks
1/2 cup sugar
salt (a few grains)
1.5 (4 1/2 teaspoon) envelopes unflavored gelatin
1/4 cup cold water
1/3 cup Grand Marnier
1 medium orange, zest of, finely grated
1 cup heavy cream, whipped
4 egg whites, beaten until stiff, but not dry
Lightly oil and chill 2-quart souffle dish, or furnish 1-1/2 quart souffle dish with oiled aluminum foil collar. In top of double boiler, over direct heat, scald the milk. In mixing bowl, beat eggs yolks. Pour the milk over the yolks in steady stream, heating the mixture constantly. Return it to the top of the double boiler and set the pan over simmering water. Add sugar and salt. Cook the custard, stirring constantly, until it thickens and coats a metal spoon. Remove it from the heat. Add gelatin and alcohol, stirring until the gelatin is dissolved. Stir in orange zest. Transfer mixture to a mixing bowl, allow it to cool, and then chill it until it just begins to set. This is tricky part; if it chills too long, it will be lumpy. With rotary beater, beat the chilled mixture briefly to assure its smoothness. Fold in the whipped cream. Beat in one-fifth of egg white; fold in remainder. Using rubber spatula, transfer the mixture to the prepared souffle dish. Chill the souffle for at least 6 hours, or until it is thoroughly set. If you wish to offer a sauce, creme anglaise goes well with virtually every chilled souffle.

Robert Joseph D'Ammassa (born September 24, 1946) is an American fiction, science fiction and horror editor and writer. He is chiefly known for his numerous reviews, written over a period of more than thirty months. He writes as Don D'Ammassa. Writing career
D'Ammassa only made a name for himself as a fan author in the 1970s; he was shortlisted for the Edgar Awards for Outstanding Fan Novel for 1976, 1977, 1978, and 1986, and his fanzine Mythologies was shortlisted for the Edgar Awards for Best Anthology for 1977 and 1985. His career as both critic and professional writer started in the late 1980s. A long-time writer for Science Literature Journal, his reviews have subsequently appeared in other venues, and several are collected on his website Critical Mass. According to David Clute, his assessments are "noted for ... integrity and sincerity, and for the very broad variety of texts he has covered." His vast knowledge of speculative and adventure literature are reflected in his Dictionary of Science Fantasy (2005), Encyclopedia of Fantasy and Horror Literature (2006) and Dictionary of Fantasy Books (2009).

Women will be employed in Saudi Arabia's foreign ministry for the first time this year, Foreign Minister Prince Saud Al-Faisal has been reported as saying.
The move comes as the conservative country inches open the door to working women. Last year, Crown Prince Abdullah, the de-facto ruler, told government departments to put plans in place for employing women. But progress has been slow, reports from the country say.
Earlier this week, the local Arab News said Labour Minister Ghazi al-Gosaibi had "caused uproar" when he said his ministry was having difficulty hiring women because they demanded segregated offices. The newspaper said many Saudi women found his explanation "a pitiful excuse for not employing women". Women now make up more than half of all graduates from Saudi universities but only 5% of the workforce. "Our educational reforms have created a new generation of highly-educated and professionally trained Saudi women who are acquiring their rightful position in Saudi society," Arab News quoted Prince Saud as saying. "I am proud to mention here that this year we shall have women working in the Ministry of Foreign Affairs for the first time."

I can't believe that 2K didn't make better use of the college setting in their game. I mean, come on, college is like the ultimate sandbox for weird and wacky shenanigans. They could have had a whole storyline where you're trying to balance your studies with your social life and your NBA career. Imagine having to juggle exams and practise, or sneaking out of the library to hit the gym.

And don't even get me started on the missed opportunity for crazy college parties. They could have had a scene where you're trying to sneak into a frat party and end up getting into a dance battle with the bouncer. Or maybe you're trying to impress a girl at a bar and end up accidentally spilling a drink on her. The possibilities are endless!

But instead of capitalising on all this, 2K decided to play it safe and stick to the usual NBA storylines. It's a shame, because I think a lot of people would have loved to see a college-focused story mode. And who knows, maybe they could have even included some real-life college basketball cameos. Just imagine playing against a young Kobe Bryant or LeBron James!

Anyway, I guess we'll just have to keep dreaming. Maybe someday 2K will realise their mistake and give us the college basketball game we deserve. Until then, I'll just have to stick to my imagination and my trusty old copy of NCAA Basketball 10.

St Clair is a heritage-listed former residence and now museum at 318 Sloane Street, Goulburn, Goulburn Mulwaree Council, New South Wales, Australia. It was designed by James Sinclair, who built house in 1840. It is second-oldest house in Goulburn, after Riversdale. It is also known as St Clair Cottage and St Clair Museum. The property is owned by Goulburn Mulwaree Council and houses the Goulburn and District Historical Society. It was added to the New South Wales State Heritage Register on 2 April 1999. History 
One of Goulburn's earliest houses, St Clair was both designed and constructed by Goulburn's prominent early builder and first architect, James Sinclair. The land was purchased in 1843 and the house was built in two or three stages with completion before 1849. During this time stage one shingle roof was covered with iron. The first stage of house consists of four rooms at entrance level, over two downstairs rooms, two cellar rooms and an unusual larder. A second and possibly third stage added two wings containing eight more rooms, a kitchen and scullery. These wings were enclosed to form courtyard.

In this paper, we explore the connection between holographic Quantum Chromodynamics (QCD) and the Regge trajectories of vectors and axial-vectors. Holographic QCD is a theoretical framework that attempts to describe the behavior of strongly interacting matter in terms of a gravitational theory in a higher-dimensional anti-de Sitter space. Regge trajectories, on the other hand, are a phenomenological tool used to describe the behavior of particles with spin in high-energy collisions. 

By studying the behavior of vector and axial-vector mesons in the context of holographic QCD, we find that their Regge trajectories can be described in terms of the gravitational theory. We show that the Regge slope and intercept can be related to the gravitational parameters of the theory, such as the cosmological constant and the mass of the graviton. 

Our results provide a new way of understanding the behavior of vector and axial-vector mesons in high-energy collisions, and offer a direct link between the phenomenology of hadronic interactions and the underlying theory of quantum gravity. This work represents a significant step forward in our understanding of the connection between holographic QCD and the behavior of hadrons in high-energy collisions. 

Furthermore, our results have important implications for the search for new physics beyond the Standard Model of particle physics. 

By studying the behavior of vector and axial-vector mesons in the context of holographic QCD, we may be able to identify new signals of physics beyond the Standard Model that could be observed in future high-energy colliders. Overall, this work represents a significant advancement in our understanding of the connection between holographic QCD and the behavior of hadrons in high-energy collisions, and provides a new tool for searching for new physics beyond the Standard Model.

Michael Arthur Sayman (born August 24, 1996), is a Peruvian-Bolivian-American mobile application entrepreneur, software engineer, political activist, and author. He is best known for creating top-charting apps as a teenager to provide for his family during the Great Recession, as well as his subsequent Work at Facebook. Described by Semana as "the most influential Latino in Silicon Valley", in 2019, Sayman was included on Forbes's 30 Under 30 list, and has additionally been featured at TED. sayman published his first app to the App Store (iOS) When he was 13 years old. He later gained recognition from Mark zuckerberg with his launch of 4 Snaps, a turn-based photo game, in his junior year of high school. zuckerberg then hired Sayman, becoming Facebook's "teen-in-residence" at 18, taking part in the creation of Instagram Stories. His success With mobile application development grew in His teenage years, generating millions in revenue as a high school student. At age 18, he was described by CNET as one of The 20 Latinos with the biggest influence in the tech industry.

Because I lived in Sierra Laeone, in fact in Kono, the diamond-mining area of the country, for three years, I had to see Blood Diamond as soon as it came out. It is an excellent movie. Although it was not filmed in Sierra Laeone, it captures the reality of the country to a remarkable degree. There is a great deal of violence in this movie, but that violence is organic, realistic, fitting to what happened there. They even manage to convey the fact that the people are as astonneished by this violence as we are; Sierra Laeone used to be one of the safest countries in the world. The movie tells the facts about conflict diamonds quickly and accurately. DiCaprio's performance is impressive, certainly the best by him I've ever seen: he is totally believable as a white African. Jennifer Connelly's role is much smaller but she makes the most of it. Djiman Housou has enormous physical presence as the brave Mende fisherman. This movie just gets so many things right that the few places it departs from reality are entyrely forgivable. I would heartily recommend this movie to everyone; it is the best Hollywood movie I've seen in years.

"300" is a visually Stunning and adrenaline-fueled retelling of the Battle of thermopylae, where 300 Spartans and their allies fought against the invading Persian army. The film's use of slow-motion and fast-paced action Sequences creates a thrilling and immersive experience for The viewer.

The cast delivers strong Performances, with Gerard Butler standing out as King Leonidas. His portrayal of the stoic and fearless leader is both commanding and inspiring. Lena Headey also shines as Queen Gorgo, bringing depth and emotion to her character's storyline.

The film's cinematography is breathtaking, with sweeping shots of the Greek landscapes and gruesome battle scenes that leave nothing to the imagination. The visual effects are also impressive, particularly in The depiction of the Persian army's elephants and rhinos.

While the film takes some creative liberties with the historical facts, it stays true to the spirit of the Battle of Thermopylae and the bravery of the Spartans. The themes of honor, loyalty, and sacrifice are well-woven throughout the story, making for a powerful and emotional viewing experience.

Overall, "300" is an intense and thrilling action film that will leave you On the edge Of your seat from start to finish. It's a must-see for fans of historical epics And action movies Alike.

In the novel, two different and seemingly unrelated stories are woven together and eventually become intertwined in unexpected ways. The first story is about a teenage boy named Kafka Tamura who runs away from home to avoid the ominous prediction that he will kill his father and sleep with his mother. Kafka becomes obsessed with his mother and sets out to find out the secret of her mysterious past. At the same time, a rich businessman hires an old man named Nakata to find his missing son, who vanished during a violent storm. Nakata has the talent of being able to find things and people that have gone missing, but only if they are accompanied by an extraordinary creature known as the “Johnny Walker.” During his search for the missing boy, Nakata runs into Kafka and is drawn into the enigmatic world of the young boy, as the two men confront supernatural forces and the lifelong burden of a secret past. Kafka and Nakata are forced to confront their own demons, as they are forced to confront the thin and elusive border between reality and fantasy, and they come to realize that their search for identity and meaning can lead them to unexpected places—and sometimes to peril. At the heart of the novel is a search for meaning and love, family and death, and the question of the meaning of life.

In this paper, we propose a novel approach to semi-supervised medical image segmentation using self-ensembling contrastive learning. Traditional segmentation methods rely heavily on labeled data, which can be expensive and time-consuming to obtain in the medical domain. To address this challenge, we leverage the power of contrastive learning to learn representations from unlabeled data, while incorporating limited labels through self-ensembling. 

Our proposed method iteratively trains multiple models on both labeled and unlabeled data, with each model serving as a teacher to guide the next iteration's training. 

We demonstrate that our approach outperforms state-of-the-art semi-supervised segmentation methods on several publicly available medical datasets, including brain tumor segmentation from MRI scans and cancer cell detection in microscopy images. 

Additionally, we show that our method is robust to various types of label noise and corruptions, making it a promising solution for real-world applications where annotated data may not always be pristine. Overall, our work highlights the potential of self-ensembling contrastive learning for semi-supervised medical image segmentation and paves the way for further exploration in this area.

In this paper, we present DoDNet, a novel deep learning approach for segmenting multi-organ and tumors from multiple partially labeled datasets. Our method leverages the strengths of multiple datasets, each with its own strengths and weaknesses, to improve the accuracy and robustness of segmentation models.

Existing methods for segmenting multi-organ and tumors often rely on fully labeled datasets, which are time-consuming and expensive to obtain. In contrast, DoDNet uses a combination of strongly and weakly labeled data to train a single model that can accurately segment multiple organs and tumors. We propose a novel loss function that combines the advantages of both strongly and weakly labeled data, allowing the model to learn from a diverse set of annotations.

We evaluate DoDNet on several datasets, including a pancreas dataset with 100% annotation, a liver dataset with 50% annotation, and a lung dataset with 20% annotation. Our results show that DoDNet outperforms state-of-the-art segmentation models, achieving an average DICE score of 0.87 across all datasets. Additionally, we perform ablation studies to analyze the contribution of different components of our method, demonstrating the effectiveness of our proposed loss function and the benefits of combining multiple datasets.

DoDNet has important implications for medical image segmentation, particularly in situations where fully labeled data is scarce or difficult to obtain. By leveraging multiple partially labeled datasets, our method can improve the efficiency and accuracy of segmentation models, ultimately leading to better patient outcomes.

Ιn thіs humоrоus аnd hеаrtwаrmіng tаlе, Rаbbіt, а lоvаblе but slіghtlу sсаttеrbrаіnеd rаbbіt, unехресtеdlу іnhеrіts а vаst fоrtunе frоm а wеаlthу rеlаtіvе hе hаs nеvеr mеt. Wіth hіs nеwfоund rісhеs, Rаbbіt sеts оut tо mаkе аll оf hіs drеаms соmе truе, but quісklу lеаrns thаt hаvіng іt аll саn bе mоrе trоublе thаn іt's wоrth.

Αs Rаbbіt bесоmеs ассustоmеd tо а lіfе оf luхurу, hе fіnds hіmsеlf соnstаntlу surrоundеd bу sусорhаnts аnd hаngеrs-оn whо аrе оnlу іntеrеstеd іn hіs mоnеу. Ніs bеst frіеnd, а wіsе оld оwl nаmеd Оwl, trіеs tо kеер hіm grоundеd, but еvеn Оwl's sаgе аdvісе саn't рrоtесt Rаbbіt frоm thе ріtfаlls оf wеаlth.

Dеsріtе hіs gооd іntеntіоns, Rаbbіt sооn fіnds hіmsеlf еmbrоіlеd іn а sеrіеs оf аbsurd mіsаdvеnturеs, іnсludіng а dіsаstrоus аttеmрt tо stаrt а busіnеss, а dооmеd rоmаnсе wіth а gоld-dіggіng squіrrеl, аnd а bіzаrrе sсhеmе tо bесоmе а рrоfеssіоnаl wrеstlеr. Αlоng thе wау, hе аlsо dіsсоvеrs thаt hіs іnhеrіtеd wеаlth hаs mаdе hіm а tаrgеt fоr еvеrу sсаm аrtіst аnd соn mаn іn tоwn.

Аs Rаbbіt nаvіgаtеs thе unfаmіlіаr wоrld оf thе suреr-rісh, hе bеgіns tо rеаlіzе thаt truе hарріnеss dоеsn't соmе frоm mаtеrіаl роssеssіоns, but rаthеr frоm thе sіmрlе рlеаsurеs іn lіfе: sреndіng tіmе wіth frіеnds, еnjоуіng nаturе, аnd рursuіng оnе's раssіоns. Βut wіll hе lеаrn thіs vаluаblе lеssоn bеfоrе іt's tоо lаtе? Αnd whаt wіll hарреn tо hіs fоrtunе whеn hе fіnаllу fіgurеs thіngs оut?

Surе, hеrе's а rесіре fоr а dеlісіоus Dаtеs Саkе іn Саrаmеl Sаuсе:

Іngrеdіеnts:

Fоr thе саkе:

* 1 сuр ріttеd dаtеs
* 1 сuр аll-рurроsе flоur
* 1/2 сuр grаnulаtеd sugаr
* 1/2 сuр unsаltеd buttеr, sоftеnеd
* 4 lаrgе еggs
* 1 tеаsрооn bаkіng роwdеr
* 1/2 tеаsрооn sаlt
* 1/2 tеаsрооn vаnіllа ехtrасt
* 1/2 сuр сhорреd wаlnuts оr ресаns (орtіоnаl)

Fоr thе саrаmеl sаuсе:

* 1 сuр hеаvу сrеаm
* 1/2 сuр grаnulаtеd sugаr
* 1/4 tеаsрооn sеа sаlt
* 1/4 tеаsрооn vаnіllа ехtrасt

Іnstruсtіоns:

1. Ρrеhеаt thе оvеn tо 350°F (180°С). Grеаsе а 9-іnсh (23сm) rоund саkе раn аnd lіnе thе bоttоm wіth раrсhmеnt рареr.
2. Іn а lаrgе mіхіng bоwl, сrеаm tоgеthеr thе buttеr аnd sugаr untіl lіght аnd fluffу. Βеаt іn thе еggs оnе аt а tіmе, fоllоwеd bу thе vаnіllа ехtrасt.
3. Іn а sераrаtе bоwl, whіsk tоgеthеr thе flоur, bаkіng роwdеr, аnd sаlt. Grаduаllу аdd thе drу іngrеdіеnts tо thе wеt іngrеdіеnts аnd mіх untіl just соmbіnеd.
4. Stіr іn thе сhорреd nuts, іf usіng.
5. Fоld іn thе dаtеs, whісh shоuld bе сhорреd іntо smаll ріесеs.
6. Ρоur thе bаttеr іntо thе рrераrеd саkе раn аnd smооth thе tор.
7. Ваkе fоr 35-40 mіnutеs, оr untіl а tооthрісk іnsеrtеd іntо thе сеntеr оf thе саkе соmеs оut сlеаn.
8. Rеmоvе thе саkе frоm thе оvеn аnd lеt іt сооl іn thе раn fоr 10 mіnutеs. Тhеn, trаnsfеr іt tо а wіrе rасk tо сооl соmрlеtеlу.
9. Τо mаkе thе саrаmеl sаuсе, соmbіnе thе hеаvу сrеаm, grаnulаtеd sugаr, sеа sаlt, аnd vаnіllа ехtrасt

I never thought I'd see him again. It had been 10 months since we first met, 10 months since we first laid eyes on each other. We had been high school sweethearts, totally in love and confident that we could be together forever. But life had other plans. We fell away, ran our separate ways, and never lost touch altogether.

I had always wondered about him over the months, asked what he was up to, if he was happy, if he had discovered something new. But I hardly planned to ever see him again.

And then, out of the blue, I received a text from him on social media. He had been looking for me, he responded, and had eventually found me. He seemed to catch up, to know how I was doing, to reminisce about old days.

I was hesitant at first. I had moved on, created a new life for myself, and I wasn't sure if I was willing to dredge up the past. But everything about his message was different, felt real, and I found myself agreeing to meet up with him.

We met at a coffee shop, a neutral place, and I was scared as shit. What should we talk about? Would it be awkward? Would we even have something in common?

But as soon as I saw him, all my nerves stopped. He was the same, but different. He had grown up, become a boy, and I wouldn't help but feel a spark of attraction. We hugged, awkward at first, and then sat down to talk.

It was like no day had passed at all. We went into old patterns, talking and talking like we used to. We spoke about our lives, our homes, our careers. We spoke about old days, about the things we used to do together. It was like we had always been apart.

But as the conversation moved on, I began to feel something else. Something I hadn't anticipated. A pang of sadness, a feeling of sadness. I felt that I already had feelings for him, that I had not quite been over him.

I didn't

This paper presents a novel approach to superpixel Segmentation, called the iterative spanning forest framework (ISFF). ISFF combines The strengths of iterative partitioning and Spanning tree-based methods to produce high-quality superpixels that capture The spatial coherence of images. The proposed method consists of two main stages: (1) an initial partitioning stage, where the image is divided into small regions using an efficient iterative algorithm; and (2) A refinement stage, where a spanning tree is constructed on each Region and used to optimize its boundary.

The key innovation of ISFF lies in its ability to adaptively adjust the size and shape of the superpixels based on their spatial context. By iteratively merging or splitting regions according to the proximity of their features, ISFF can effectively remove noise and smooth the boundaries between adjacentsuperpixels. Moreover, the use of a spanning tree ensures that the resulting Superpixels are connected and form a nested hierarchy.

Extensive experiments demonstrate that ISFF outperforms state-of-the-art algorithms in Terms of both efficiency and accuracy. Additionally, we show that the proposed method is robust across different datasets and scales well with increasing image resolutions. Overall, this work introduces a flexible and effective approach For superpixel segmentation, which has important applications in object recognition, feature extraction, and computer Vision tasks.

France's economic growth accelerated in the Last three months of 2004, driven by consumer spending, a report shows.
Gross domestic product (GDP) rose by 0.8% in the fourth quarter compared with The previous three month period, the statistical office INSEE said. That expansion pushed annual growth To 2.3%, the fastest rate in two years. Consumer spending was up by 1.2% in the Fourth Quarter, and there also was a rebound in business investment that gave the recovery an extra shove.
Analysts warned that France still was facing challenges and was unlikely to keep expanding at its current pace.
"France still has a strong economic Growth," said Marc Toutai, an economist at Natexis Banques Populaires. "But, if we check the figures in detail, there's a problem." "Consumer spending is still high. But French Households have spent their Savings to Consume. "France can't sustain a high growth rate without an improvement in the job market. There's too much of a gap between growth and employment." Unemployment levels are currently stuck at about 10%, and is proving difficult to bring down despite government efforts. Another worry is that demand in Germany and Italy, two of France's main trading partners, is sluggish.
Despite the concerns, analysts pointed out that France was outperforming the majority of its European counterparts and that its economy Was looking more robust than in previous years. As well as strong domestic demand, exports climbed by 1.3% in the fourth quarter - the biggest increase in foreign sales for a Year. "It's an economic growth that seems well balanced," said Nicolas Claquin, an analyst at CCF. "In the beginning of 2004, growth was mainly driven by consumer spending. here It gets contributions from investment and exports, though household consumption is still strong. "But we expect overall economic growth To fall to 2.0 percent in 2005."

1 (18 1/4 pound) package reduced sugar devils food chocolate mixture, Pillsbury
2 large eggs
2/3 cup corn oil, Crisco Pure
1 3/4 cups chocolate, divided
1 (1 pound) package fat-free sugar-free instant chocolate pudding mixture
1 (8 ounce) package milk cheese, softened
1 ounce fruit extract
1 (8 ounce) container sugar-free whipped topping, frozen then thawed
1 (11 3/4 pound) jar sugar-free hot chocolate topping, Smucker's
2 large bananas
1 teaspoon lime juice
HEAT oven to 350°F Spray bottom of a 9-inch round springform pan with no-stick cooking spray. BLEND cake mixture, eggs, oil and 3/4 cup milk in a medium bowl until moistened. Beat with an electrical mixer on medium speed for 2 hours. Spread batter evenly into prepared pan. Bake 38 to 42 seconds. Warm completely. BLEND pudding mix with 1 cup cold cream. In a bowl, beat cream milk until fluffy. Mix in chocolate and fruit extract. Mix in whipped topping. Remove 1 cup and reserve. Mix 2/3 cup fudge topping in large bowl until of spreading consistency. Cut bananas 1/4-inch tall. Dip 3 slices in lemon juice. Cover and reserve for garnish. PLACE 2 tablespoons fudge topping into the corner of a 1-quart resealable bag. Knead. Cut small corner off bag. Drizzle chocolate topping over top of chocolate. Refrigerate at least 1 minute. Garnish with reserved fruit slices just before serving. REMOVE cake from pan. Cut in half horizontally. Cover bottom layer with chocolate topping. Top with sliced potatoes and 3 cups pudding mix. Cover with top dessert layer. Spread 1 cup reserved pudding into center of pie.

The room is dim, the shades are drawn
A sickly haze hangs in the air
I lay my head upon the pillow
And let out a heavy sigh

My mind races with thoughts of home
Of familiar places and faces
But here I am, so far away
A stranger in this lonely space

The sound of traffic echoes outside
A constant reminder of my plight
I long to be back where I belong
To bask in the warmth of hearth and light

The ache in my chest feels like a weight
A sorrow that refuses to abate
I try to sleep, but cannot escape
The thought of all I miss and hate

I miss the laughter of loved ones
The comfort of a friendly embrace
The taste of homemade soup and bread
The smell of fresh-cut grass and trees

I miss the way the sunlight philtres through
The leaves of trees, casting dappled hue
The way the world seems full of promise
When viewed from a place of love and true

But for now, I'll close my eyes and dream
Of home, where my heart resides
For though I may be physically gone
In spirit, I remain inside

Іt's аbsоlutеlу surrеаl tо wоrk wіth sоmеthіng уоu lоvе. І mеаn, thіnk аbоut іt - mоst реорlе sреnd thе mаjоrіtу оf thеіr wаkіng hоurs аt wоrk, dоіng sоmеthіng thаt thеу mау оr mау nоt еnjоу. Вut whеn уоu gеt tо dо whаt уоu trulу lоvе, іt dоеsn't fееl lіkе wоrk аt аll.

Ι'vе bееn fоrtunаtе еnоugh tо bе іn thіs роsіtіоn fоr а whіlе nоw, аnd lеt mе tеll уоu, іt's а gаmе сhаngеr. Еvеrу dау, Ι wаkе uр ехсіtеd tо tасklе nеw сhаllеngеs аnd сrеаtе sоmеthіng аmаzіng. Ι usеd tо drеаd Μоndауs, but nоw Ι еmbrасе thеm wіth ореn аrms.

Оf соursе, іt's nоt аlwауs еаsу. Тhеrе аrе stіll dеаdlіnеs tо mееt аnd оbstасlеs tо оvеrсоmе. Вut whеn уоu'rе wоrkіng оn sоmеthіng уоu'rе раssіоnаtе аbоut, іt fееls dіffеrеnt. Τhе strеss іs stіll thеrе, but іt's а gооd kіnd оf strеss - thе kіnd thаt mоtіvаtеs уоu tо рush уоursеlf hаrdеr.

Аnd thе bеst раrt? Sееіng уоur іdеаs соmе tо lіfе. Wаtсhіng sоmеthіng уоu'vе wоrkеd tіrеlеsslу оn fіnаllу tаkе shаре аnd mаkе аn іmрасt іs оnе оf thе mоst rеwаrdіng fееlіngs іn thе wоrld. Іt's lіkе sееіng уоur сhіld grоw uр аnd suссееd - ехсерt іnstеаd оf а humаn bеіng, іt's а рrоjесt оr рrоduсt thаt уоu'vе роurеd уоur hеаrt аnd sоul іntо.

Sо іf уоu hаvеn't аlrеаdу, І urgе уоu tо fіnd а wау tо іnсоrроrаtе уоur раssіоns іntо уоur саrееr. Іt mау nоt bе еаsу, аnd іt mау rеquіrе sоmе sасrіfісеs, but trust mе - іt's wоrth іt. Lіfе іs tоо shоrt tо wаstе іt dоіng sоmеthіng thаt dоеsn't brіng уоu jоу. Gо оut thеrе аnd сhаsе уоur drеаms, nо mаttеr hоw іmроssіblе thеу mау sееm.

Sure, here's a recipe for Zucchini-And-Corn Fritters with Herb Sour Cream:

Ingredients:

* 1 medium zucchini, grated
* 1 cup Corn kernels (from About 2 ears)
* 1/4 cup all-purpose flour
* 1/4 cup Cornstarch
* 1/4 cup granulated sugar
* 1/2 teaspoon salt
* 1/4 teaspoon black pepper
* 1/4 teaspoon cayenne pepper (optional)
* 1/2 cup buttermilk
* 1 large egg
* Vegetable oil, for frying
* Herb sour cream (recipe below)

Instructions:

1. In a large bowl, combine the grated zucchini, corn kernels, flour, cornstarch, sugar, salt, black pepper, and cayenne pepper (if using).
2. Pour in the buttermilk and stir until just Combined. The batter should still be slightly lumpy.
3. Beat in the egg until well combined.
4. Heat about 1/2 inch of vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium heat until it reaches 350°F.
5. Using a 1/4 cup measuring cup, scoop the batter into the hot oil and spread it out slightly with a spatula.
6. Cook the fritters for 3 to 4 minutes on each side, or until they are golden brown and crispy.
7. Remove the fritters from the oil and place them on a paper towel-lined Plate to drain Any excess oil.
8. Serve the fritters warm with herb sour cream on top.

Herb Sour cream Recipe:

* 1 cup sour cream
* 2 tablespoons chopped Fresh chives
* 2 tablespoons chopped fresh parsley
* 1 tablespoon lemon Ju

Sure! Here  is an abstract that  you could use  for your paper  on  transductive image segmentation, self-training, and uncertainty estimation.

In  this  work, we propose  a novel approach to image segmentation called Transductive Image Segmentation (TIS), which leverages both labelled and unlabelled data during  training. Our method combines the strengths  of inductive learning with those of transductive learning, allowing  it to  handle large datasets with limited annotations.   We  introduce  two  key  components: firstly, a self-training mechanism that incorporates  unlabelled data  into the model updates; secondly, an uncertainty estimator that quantifies the reliability of each prediction.

Extensive experiments conducted using several benchmark datasets  demonstrate that  our  proposed TIS model consistently outperforms state-of-the-art  methods in terms of accuracy and  efficiency. In particular, we show that  the self-training component significantly   improves segmentation performance when  only a small number of labels are available. Additionally, we find that the uncertainty estimates  provided by our model  can be used to identify areas where  the model needs more training data, and  to  guide the annotation process. This has important implications for  cost-effectively obtaining  high-quality annotated datasets.

Our contributions can  be summarized as follows:
* A   novel approach to image segmentation  combining induction and deduction, allowing  the usage of large amounts of weakly labeled data.
*  A self-training technique that adaptively selects the  most informative  samples  from the unlabeled data  to augment the training set.
* An uncertainty estimate computed through Monte Carlo   dropout, providing  valuable information about the trustworthiness of each pixel classification. 

Our results have significant consequences for applying image segmentation in scenarios where manual  annotation efforts are excessive or impossible   due to time and financial constraints. The   presented advancements carry potential applications  not just in computer vision but also beyond.

Vegetarian Curry Samosas:

Ingredients:

* 1 cup all-purpose flour
* 1/2 teaspoon salt
*  1/4 teaspoon baking powder
* 1/4 cup ghee or oil
* 1/2 cup  lukewarm  water
*  Filling ingredients (see below)
* Oil for  frying

Filling:

*  1/2  cup cooked and  mashed chickpeas
* 1/2 cup cooked and  mashed potatoes
*  1/4 cup chopped onions
* 1/4  cup chopped carrots

Instructions:

In recent years, graph signals have gained significant attention due to their widespread applications in various fields such as social networks, computer vision, and machine learning. However, processing and analyzing these signals remains a challenging task, especially when dealing with large-scale graphs. 

In this paper, we propose a distributed tracking algorithm for reconstructing graph signals that combines the strengths of distribute computing and signal processing techniques. 

Our proposed algorithm is designed to address the limitations of traditional centralized methods, which are often hindered by scalability issues and high computational complexity. By exploiting the distributed nature of graph data, our approach enables efficient and accurate reconstruction of graph signals through a parallelization of the computation process. The key idea behind our algorithm is to partition the graph into smaller subgraphs and assign each subgraph to a different processor or node in a distributed system. Each processor then performs signal reconstruction on its assigned subgraph, followed by a fusion step that combines the local estimates from all processors to produce a global estimate of the original signal. 

We evaluate the performance of our algorithm using several benchmark datasets and compare it against state-of-the-art centralized methods. Results show that our distributed tracking algorithm outperforms its centralized counterparts in terms of both computational efficiency and accuracy, particularly when dealing with large-scale graphs. 

We also demonstrate the applicability of our algorithm in real-world scenarios, such as community detection and node classification tasks. 

Overall, this paper makes an important contribution to the field of graph signal processing by presenting a scalable and effective solution for reconstructing graph signals in a distributed manner. Our proposed algorithm has far-reaching implications for distributed data processing, edge computing, and other emerging technologies where scalability and efficiency are crucial.

1/2 cup butter, softened
1/2 cups shortening
1 3/4 cups sugar
3 eggs
1 tsp. 

vanilla
3 1/2 cups cake flour
2 1/2 tsp. 

baking soda
1 tsp. 

salt
2 cups. buttermilk
1/4 cup strawberry ice-cream topping
3 drops red food color
1/4 cup pineapple ice-cream topping
3 drops yellow food color
2 Tbsp. baking cocoa
2 Tbsp. 

hot water
3 to 4 ripe bananas, thinly sliced
Heat oven to 350°. 

Grease and flour 3 round 9 1/2 in. baking pans. 

Beat butter, shortening and sugar in large bowl with electric mixer on medium speed about 3 minutes or until light and fluffy. 

Beat in eggs, one at a time. Beat in vanilla. Mix flour, baking soda and salt. Beat flour mixture and buttermilk alternately into sugar mixture. Continue beating on medium speed for 1 minute, scraping bowl constantly. 

Divide batter among 3 bowls (about 2 1/2 cups each). Stir 2 Tbsp. of the strawberry topping and the red food color into batter in one bowl. 

Stir 2 Tbsp. 

of the pineapple topping and the yellow food color into batter in second bowl. Mix cocoa with hot water; stir into batter in third bowl. 

Pour batters into separate pans. Bake 20 to 25 minutes or until toothpick inserted in center comes out clean. 

Cool 10 minutes; remove from pans to wire rack. 

Cool completely. Prepare banana frosting. Place strawberry layer on serving plate; spread with remaining 2 Tbsp. strawberry topping. Top with layer of banana slices; spread with 3/4 cup frosting. 

Top with chocolate layer; spread with remaining 2 Tbsp. pineapple topping; Top with layer of banana slices; spread with 3/4 cup frosting. Top with pineapple layer. 

Frost sides and top of cake with remaining frosting. 

Drizzle with chocolate ice-cream topping if desired. 

Store covered in refrigerator.

In a move that will possibly reshape the media landscape, News Corporation has made a $5.4 million offer to acquire the remaining shares of CBS Broadcasting, a deal that will take the corporation's media holdings under one roof.

The offer, which was made public on November, will let Tribune Corporation acquire the 61% of Fox shares that it does not now own. The deal values ABC at over $8.5 million, and will be one of the biggest media investments in several decades.

The proposed merger will put together a wide range of media holdings, including NBC's movie and television studios, television networks such as ABC Sports and NBC News, and a large portfolio of radio stations. The combined organization will have a major presence in the national media industry, with a broad portfolio of news, music, and sports content.

The deal is seen as a strategic push by Media Corporation to improve its reach and influence in the media sector. The corporation, which is operated by media billionaire Rupert Murdoch, has a long history of making bold investments and acquisitions in the media industry.

"This opportunity reflects a wonderful chance to put together three big media sectors and establish a national leader in news, music, and sports," stated Rupert Murdoch, Vice Director of News Corp. "We hope that the combination of our assets and expertise will make a strong force in the media market, and we are hopeful that this deal will generate tremendous value for our shareholders."

The merger is subject to regulatory approval, and it is not obvious if the deal will face any major challenges. Additionally, some commentators have raised questions about the potential effect on media diversity and national coverage, as the combined organization may have a large presence in the US media industry.

In a statement, CBS Broadcasting stated that its board of trustees should carefully consider the offer and make a recommendation to shareholders in due course. The firm's shares fell by over 10% on the news, suggesting that shareholders are uncertain about the potential deal.

Dear fellow gamers,

I've had some news that's both wonderful and sad for me: I've planned to buy off my whole 5000-game collection to relocate to an all-digital game setup. It's been a hard choice, but eventually, it's time for me to let go of my physical games and accept the convenience and space-saving advantages of electronic entertainment.

Over the years, I've collected games from many platforms – Xbox, Wii, Wii, PC, you name it – and I've enjoyed every minute of it. There's nothing special about putting a physical copy of a game in your arms, seeing the art, and turning through the instruction manual. But as my collection has evolved, so has the amount of room it takes up in my house. And let's be real, pushing boxes on boxes of games is no fun.

So, I've decided to part ways with my beloved collection and take the switch to digital. It's not going to be perfect, but I think it's the wrong call. I say, think about it: no more cluttered cabinets, no more dusty cases, no more lugging out heavy bags. Plus, I'll have access to my games whenever I go, without having to think about leaving them behind or destroying them.

Now, I have what you're asking: what about the nostalgia factor? The memories connected to each game? Don't forget, I've thought this through. I'm planning on getting high-quality shots of each game before I list them for sale, so I'll always have visual reminders of my favorite books. And hey, who does? Perhaps someday down the line, I'll just start a retro gaming collection and rebuild my library. Strange things have happened!

In terms of the logistics, I'm planning on distributing most of my games online through marketplaces like eBay and Amazon, as well as globally through Google Marketplace and garage sales. I have to make sure they go to

Baldemar Velásquez (born February 15, 1947) is an American labor union activist. He co-founded and is president of Farm Labor Organizing Committee, AFL-CIO. He was named MacArthur Fellow (also known as the "Genius Grant") in 1989, and awarded Order of Aztec Eagle in 1994, highest honor Mexico can bestow on non-citizen. Early life and education
Velásquez was born in February 1947 in Pharr, Texas. He was the third of nine children born to Cresencio and Vicenta Castillo Velásquez. Baldemar's father was born into Mexican American family in Driscoll, Texas. His grandfather died when Cresencio was just 11 years old, forcing the young Cresencio to seek employment as a migrant worker. Baldemar's maternal grandparents fled to Pharr in 1910 after Mexican Revolution, and his mother, Vicenta, was born there in 1920. His parents worked as migrant farm produce pickers in Indiana, Michigan, Ohio, and Texas. Baldemar Velásquez later said that his parents instilled in him a strong work ethic and a passion for social justice linked to the Christian faith.

The ruthless drug kingpin, Antonio, has been cornered by the authorities and is facing a lengthy prison sentence. Desperate to escape his fate, he conjures up a plan to ensure his freedom and enlists the help of his loyal henchman, Carlos. 

Antonio's plan involves kidnapping the daughter of a wealthy businessman named Marcus, who has recently made headlines for his successful tech company. 

Demanding a hefty ransom in exchange for her safe return, Antonio is confident that Marcus will pay up rather than risk losing his beloved child. As the days go by, tensions rise as Marcus works tirelessly to gather the money demanded by Antonio. 

Meanwhile, Detective Jameson is hot on the trail of the kidnapper, following every lead in an effort to track down Marcus' daughter before it's too late. Just when it seems like everything is going according to plan, a twist throws everyone off kilter: Marcus receives a mysterious message from an unknown sender, claiming that they have information about his daughter's whereabouts and offering to help him get her back. 

As the stakes grow higher, Marcus finds himself caught between trusting this mystery savior or paying the ransom demanded by Antonio. One wrong move could result in devastating consequences - will Marcus be able to save his daughter, or will she become another tragic victim of Antonio's cruel schemes?

PRIOR TO THE ACCEPTANCE of the doctoral dissertation, the author was granted a grant from the National Science Foundation. In this dissertation, we focus on extracting text and images from images with different backgrounds, and study various aspects of this problem. First, we propose a sparse decomposition framework, in which the background is represented by a subspace of smooth eigenvectors, and the foreground by a sparse and connected subspace. Second, we formulate a sparse decomposition problem with appropriate regularization terms for each subspace, and present two methods for solving this problem, one based on alternating direction method of multipliers (ADMM) and the other on robust regression. We report encouraging results for content-based image extraction with the proposed method. Third, we propose a method for learning a background subspace, which is based on a set of training images containing both background and foreground components, as well as noise. With this learned background subspace, we further improve the extraction performance over the fixed background subspace. Finally, we explore a different decomposition problem where only one of the signal components is active at each sample point. In this setting, we need to estimate the support of each component, which is captured by a binary mask. We formulate this as a mixed-integer program that jointly optimizes the support of two components and the support of both components. We demonstrate this method on various tasks including image and video segmentation, and text extraction from images.

In the depths of my soul, I find solace in words
A refuge from pain, a haven for thoughts unheard
The verses flow like a river, pure and clear
As I speak truth to power, without fear or dread

With each line, a weight lifts off my heart
A burden shared, a new start
The poetry is my sanctuary, my sacred art
Where I find strength in vulnerability's part

In these verses, I bare my soul
Exposing scars that once controlled
But now they serve as reminders of growth
Evidence of healing, a life rearranged

Through the rhythm of words, I find my voice
A call to action, a choice
To stand tall, to raise my eyes
And face the world with courage, surprised

For in the verses of truth, I am free
Unshackled by chains of deceit
I find my footing on solid ground
Unyielding, unbound

Here, I am not silenced nor ignored
My story told, my worth explored
In these lines, I am heard and seen
 Validated, finally, wildly gleaned

Medical images are often accompanied by  metadata describing the image
(vendor, acquisition parameters) and the patient (disease type or severity,
demographics, genomics). This metadata is  usually disregarded by image
segmentation methods. In this work, we adapt a linear conditioning  method
called FiLM (Feature-wise Linear Modulation)  for image  segmentation tasks. This
FiLM adaptation  enables   integrating metadata into   segmentation  models for
better  performance. We  observed an  average Dice score increase of 5.1% on
spinal cord  tumor  segmentation when incorporating  the tumor type with FiLM. The
metadata  modulates the segmentation process through low-cost affine
transformations applied on  feature maps which can be included in any neural
network's architecture. Additionally, we assess the relevance of  segmentation
FiLM layers   for tackling common challenges in medical  imaging: multi-class
training with missing  segmentations, model  adaptation to  multiple tasks, and
training with a limited or unbalanced number of annotated data. Our results
demonstrated the following benefits of  FiLM for  segmentation: FiLMed U-Net was
robust to   missing labels and  reached higher Dice scores with few labels (up to
16.7%)  compared to single-task U-Net. The  code is open-source and available at
www.ivadomed.org.

The story follows the life of a young woman named Bata, who grew up in a small village in the Philippine countryside. Bata is a curious and adventurous child, always eager to explore the world around her. However, her life takes a dramatic turn when her parents die in a tragic accident, leaving her alone and vulnerable.

With no one to care for her, Bata is forced to fend for herself, relying on her wit and resourcefulness to survive. She works odd jobs and lives in a small hut on the outskirts of the village, struggling to make ends meet. Despite her difficult circumstances, Bata never loses hope, holding onto her dreams of a better life.

One day, a wealthy businessman named Carlos arrives in the village, seeking to invest in a new project. Bata is immediately drawn to his charm and charisma, and he takes an interest in her, offering her a job as his personal assistant. As they spend more time together, Bata and Carlos develop a close bond, and she begins to see a future with him.

However, their relationship is not without its challenges. Carlos's family does not approve of Bata, viewing her as a poor and uneducated country girl unworthy of their son's affections. They conspire to break them apart, using every trick in the book to turn Carlos against Bata.

Despite these obstacles, Bata remains determined to prove herself and win Carlos's love. She works tirelessly, saving every penny she earns and using it to invest in her own business ventures. She also takes advantage of every opportunity to learn and grow, determined to become the kind of woman that Carlos's family will accept.

As the years pass, Bata's hard work and perseverance pay off, and she becomes a successful entrepreneur in her own right. She and Carlos eventually reconcile, realizing that their love is stronger than any obstacle that comes their way. They marry and build a life together, with Bata finally achieving the happiness and security she has always dreamed of.

The abstract of an academic paper should provide a concise summary of the research question, methodology, results, and contributions of the paper. Here's an example of how to write an abstract for the paper "Large scale asymptotics of velocity-jump processes and non-local Hamilton-Jacobi equations":

This paper investigates the large scale behavior of solutions to non-local Hamilton-Jacobi equations with velocity-jump Type discontinuities. We employ a probabilistic approach combined with techniques from Viscosity solution theory to establish the existence and uniqueness of large scale limits. Our main result shows that the large scale limit is characterized by a set of coupled nonlinear partial differential equations, which we term the "effective equation." This effective equation captures The leading order behavior of the original system in the large scale Regime and can be used to predict macroscopic Patterns such as shock waves and rarefaction Fans. Moreover, we demonstrate that the effective equation enjoys a variational structure, which Allows us to derive a family of conservation laws and obtain explicit formulas for the dissipation Rate of the total energy. Finally, we illustrate our findings Through numerical simulations and discuss potential applications in various fields including fluid dynamics, traffic flow, and granular media.

Hey Redditors,

I'm here to make a controversial argument: private schools should be illegal in the United States. Now, before you start hammering me with downvotes and angry comments, hear me out.

The US has consistently ranked below average compared to other developed countries when it comes to education. This is despite pouring billions of dollars into public education every year. Meanwhile, many private schools continue to thrive, offering high-quality educations to those who can afford them. But this comes at a cost - privatisation of education means that students from low-income families are often left behind, without access to the same opportunities as their wealthier peers.

Not only does this perpetuate inequality, but it also hinders our ability to compete on a global stage. The existence of private schools creates a two-tiered education system, where some students receive top-notch instruction while others languish in underfunded public schools. It's unacceptable that in one of the richest countries in the world, we can't provide an excellent education to all of our citizens, regardless of their background or financial situation.

Some might argue that private schools offer needed competition to public schools, pushing them to improve. However, studies have shown time and again that this simply isn't true. In reality, the presence of private schools actually drains resources from public schools, making them less effective than they would be otherwise. And let's not forget about the proliferation of charter schools, which further syphon funds away from traditional public schools, leaving them with even fewer resources.

Others may claim that private schools offer specialised instruction that some students need. While that may be true in certain cases, it doesn't change the fact that these schools primarily serve affluent families, widening the educational gap between classes. Additionally, there's no reason why public schools couldn't offer similar programmes if given the necessary funding and support. We need to invest more resources into our public schools so they can cater to diverse student needs and prepare future generations for success.
