In this paper, we present a novel bound on genuine multipartite differences, which are variables that cannot be described by any local hidden variable model, based on the theory of information causality. This theory, which states that the information contained in a system cannot be increased by doing a calculation on a distant system, has been recently used to derive limits on the stability of differences in bipartite networks.

Here, we extend this idea to the multipartite setting and show that the concept of information correlation requires a bound on the stability of genuine multipartite correlations. Moreover, we prove that the variance between two or more components cannot exceed a specific value, which depends on the quantity of information that can be shared between the components.

Our result has significant consequences for the analysis of quantum non-locality and the foundations of quantum physics. It offers a new tool for testing the predictions of quantum physics and other concepts of mechanics in multipartite networks, and sheds light on the nature of entanglement and the limitations of traditional theories for quantum correlations.

However, our result has potential applications in the field of quantum information theory, where it can be applied to explain the quantity of information that can be shared between various components of a quantum system. This can be important for the implementation of quantum communication and cryptography techniques, which depend on the capability to encode and interpret information in quantum networks.

Overall, our paper shows the power of the theory of information causality in constraining the stability of correlations in multipartite networks, and discusses the necessity of examining the information-theoretic limitations on quantum physics in the study of quantum non-locality.

A string of storms, typhoons and earthquakes has made 2004 the most expensive year on record for property insurers, according to Swiss Re.
The world's second biggest insurer said disasters around the globe have seen property claims reach $42bn (£21.5bn). "2004 reinforces the trend towards higher losses," said Swiss Re. Tightly packed populations in the areas involved in natural and man-made disasters were to partly to blame for the rise in claims, it said. Some 95% of insurance claims were for natural catastrophes, with the rest attributed to made-made events.
The largest claims came from the US, which was struck by four hurricanes, and Japan, which suffered the highest concentration of typhoons for decades plus a major earthquake.
Europe suffered fewer natural disasters, but 191 people were killed and more than 2,000 injured in March after the terrorist attack on train stations in Madrid. The damages claimed in 2004 eclipsed previous years, including 2001 when the 11 September attacks pushed claims up to $37bn. Swiss Re said it had registered about 300 natural and man-made disasters around the world in 2004. Twenty-one thousand people lost their lives in the catastrophes with a cost to the global economy of around $105bn (£54bn).

Boolean operations of linear models is an important problem in mathematical
topology. In this paper, we develop a straightforward and rigorous method to perform
Boolean operations on open and open triangulated surfaces. Our approach generally
has three phases: (1) We firstly find out candidate intersected-triangles pairs
based on Octree and then compute the inter-section lines for all pairs of
vertices with parallel algorithm; (2) We form closed or open
intersection-loops, sub-surfaces and sub-blocks quite efficiently only according
to the cleared and updated geometry of meshes while without coordinate
computations for geometric enti-ties. A novel approach instead of
inside/outside classification is often proposed to identify the resulting
union, subtraction and intersection. Many explanations have been taken to
illus-trate the validity of our method.

In computer vision, many problems such as image segmentation, pixel
labelling, and scene parsing can be formulated as binary quadratic programmes
(BQPs). For submodular problems, cuts based methods can be employed to
efficiently solve large-scale problems. However, general nonsubmodular problems
are significantly more challenging to solve. Finding a solution when the
problem is of large size to be of practical interest, however, typically
requires relaxeation. Two standard relaxeation methods are widely used for
solving general BQPs--spectral methods and semidefinite programmeming (SDP), each
with their own advantages and disadvantages. Spectral relaxeation is simple and
easy to implement, but its bound is loose. Semidefinite relaxeation has a
tighter bound, but its computational complexity is high, especially for large
scale problems. In this work, we present a new SDP formulation for BQPs, with
two desirable properties. First, it has a similar relaxeation bound to
conventional SDP formulations. Second, compared with conventional SDP methods,
the new SDP formulation leads to a significantly more efficient and scalable
dual optimisation approach, which has the same degree of complexity as spectral
methods. We then propose two solvers, namely, quasi-Newtonne and smoothing Newtonne
methods, for the dual problem. Both of them are significantly more efficiently
than standard interior-point methods. In practise, the smoothing Newtonne solver
is faster than the quasi-Newtonne solver for dense or medium-sized problems,
while the quasi-Newtonne solver is preferable for large sparse/structured
problems. Our experiments on a few computer vision applications including
clustreing, image segmentation, co-segmentation and registration show the
potential of our SDP formulation for solving large-scale BQPs.

​C​a​n​a​d​a​ ​h​a​s​

Whаt mаkеs Αlіеn grеаt іs іt's sуnсhrоnіzаtіоn оf sоund, vіsuаls аnd shоwіng grеаt аttеntіоn tо dеtаіl. Νоt оnlу thаt but Αlіеn hаs аn unnеrvіng аtmоsрhеrе rіght frоm thе bеgіnnіng. Wе'rе іntrоduсеd tо а сrеw whо іs nоt ехасtlу tоо frіеndlу tо еасh оthеr аdd tо thаt thе shір. Ιt's hugе, аnd wіth іts hаlls аnd соrrіdоrs, іt fееls аlmоst lіkе а mаzе.

Εntеr Аlіеn, а ruthlеss раrаsіtе wіth humаnоіd fоrm уеt іt lасks аnу сіvіlіsеd trаіts оf а humаn. Іt dоеs hаvе humаnоіd fоrm but іt dоеsn't gіvе а sіnglе еmоtіоn. Τhе dеsіgn оf thе mоnstеr іs whаt's thе mоst tеrrіfуіng (аnd іt stіll hоlds uр dеsріtе Αlіеn bеіng rеlеаsеd іn 1979). Τhе сrеаturе іs аlsо bеttеr аdарtеd tо thе shір's іntеrіоr mаkіng а bіg раrt оf thе mоvіе fееl lіkе а sіnіstеr gаmе оf саt аnd mоusе.

Whаt І раrtісulаrlу lіkе іs hоw thе сhаrасtеrs аrе wrіttеn. Τhеу аrе nоt Ноllуwооdіzеd hеrоеs, іn fасt, thеrе іs nо hеrо. Тhеу'rе just реорlе whісh mаkеs thеm еvеn mоrе thrеаtеnеd. Τhе реrfоrmаnсеs аrе аll еquаllу rеаlіstіс аnd dо dеlіvеr. Κudоs tо dіrесtоr Rіdlеу Sсоtt fоr usіng сrаftу сіnеmаtоgrарhу аnd соmbіnіng іt wіth shаdоws аnd lіghts іn аn ееrіе wау. Тhе musіс іs unrеlеntіng аnd соmbіnеd wіth Sсоtt's сіnеmаtоgrарhу, sоund аnd vіsuаls іt аdds tо thе ееrіnеss.

Τhе рlоt іs аlsо nоt аnоthеr Ноllуwооdіzеd сlісhé. Ιt's mоrе lіkе "survіvаl оf thе fіttеst". Тhе сhаrасtеrs bаttlе truе tеrrоr аs thеу rасе tо survіvе оr оutlіvе еасh оthеr, thеу'rе аll just а раrt оf а rасе tо sее whо wіll рrеvаіl аnd whо wіll dіе.

Fіnаl Rаtіng: 10/10

SING A SONG OF SIXPENCE
sing a tune of sixpence a pocket full of rye,
Four and twenty birds baked in a cake.
When the cake was opened the birds started to sing,
Oh wasn't that a sweet dish to set before the queen?
The prince was in his counting room counting out his fortune,
The king was in the parlour drinking butter and milk
The lady was in the house hanging out the clothes,
When down went a crow and pecked off her forehead!

VIVE LA BAGATELLE
sing a song of foolishness, mocking stocks and cranks!
The more there are the merrier; come join the ranks!
Life is dry and stupid; whoop her up a lot!
Donkeys live in clover; bray and throw a fit!
Take yourself in earnest, just stop to think,
Strut and swagger boldly, dress in black and grey;
Prate of stuff and crap, get yourself abused;
Some one's got to play the fool to keep the audience entertained!
Bully for the guy! Bully for the idiot!
You would be a prig yourself, if you could only ask!
Altruistic asses keep the fun alive;
Clowns are growing scarcer; hurry and arrive!
I seen a crazy critic a-writin' of a screed;
'Tendencies' and 'Unities' - Maeterlinck indeed!
He wore a paper shirt, and his tie was up behind;
If that's the test of Culture, then I'm sure I'm not refined!
Let me laugh at you, then you can laugh at me;
Then we'll josh together everything we say;
Each one's a nincompoop to another's view;
Laughter lets the sun shine! Roop-de-doodle-doo!
Gelett BURGESS 1866_1951

AN ODE TO ALFRED AUSTEN
I sing a verse of sixpence, and of rye
A pocketful - recalling, sad to state,
The niggardly emoluments which I
Receive as Laureate:
Also I sing of blackbirds - in the mart
At four-a-penniy.  Thus, in other verses,
The sixpence which I mentioned at the start
Puchased four dozen feathers.
So four-and-twenty feathers were neatly hidden -
Or should we say, were skilfully concealed? -
within the pie-dish.  When they raised the lid
What song forth pealed:
Now I like four-and-twenty birds sing,
With all their sweetness, all their rapture keen;
And isn't this a pretty big moment
To set before the King?
The money-counting monarch - morbid woman; -
His father, who robbed the few busy bees,
I disregard.  In case a poet can
But pity people Like these.
The lady was in the house.  Good maid!
Her choice entitles her to rank above
Master and Lady.  Gladly she surveyed
The Gardens that I love!
- Where grow my roses, anemones,
Tulips, auriculas, flowers,
Cabbages, squash, little potatoes,
With grapes, apples, and grapes. -
(That's a parenthesis.  The very name
Of ‘garden' always carries one astray :)
But then a feathered ruffian appeared
And snatched her nose back.
Six stanzas finished; So my Court costume
I lay aside: the Poet, I believe,
Has completed his part; the man can now resume
His literary prose.
Anthony Charles DEANE 1870_1946

SING A SONG OF DYNAMITE
Sing a chorus of dynamite -
What a mighty scare:
A London railway-station
In pieces in the air.
Bags and pistols, clockwork,
Nasty cakes of brown -
Weren't they dainty dishes
To know about our town?
Government and railway people
Didn't see it funny;
They to see the plotters
Offered hundreds of money.
Even from the sea they've moved,
In other suits of trousers;
Maybe they're living quietly
Right under London's Nose.

Despite its  apparent  simplicity, Newtonian Mechanics contains conceptual
subtleties  that may cause  some  confusion  to  the deep-thinking   student.  These
subtleties concern fundamental issues such as, e.g., the number of independent
laws needed to  formulate the theory, or, the distinction between genuine
physical laws and derivative theorems.  This article attempts to clarify  these
issues for the benefit of the student  by revisiting the foundations of
Newtonian Dynamics   and by proposing a rigorous axiomatic approach to  the
subject. This theoretical scheme is built upon two fundamental postulates,
namely, conservation of momentum and superposition property   for interactions.
Newton's Laws, as  well as all familiar theorems of Mechanics, are shown to
follow from these basic principles.

Deep neural networks (DNNs) have demonstrated remarkable performance in various computer vision tasks, including image classification, object detection, and segmentation. However, their reliance on color information can lead to significant errors in certain scenarios, particularly when the color distribution of the input images differs from the training data. This phenomenon is known as color constancy errors.

In this paper, we investigate the impact of color constancy errors on DNN performance and propose a novel approach to addressing these errors. Our experiments demonstrate that color constancy errors can significantly degrade the performance of state-of-the-art DNNs on various benchmark datasets, including ImageNet. We analyze the root causes of these errors and propose a simple yet effective method to mitigate their impact.

Our approach, called color normalization, involves transforming the input images to a device-independent color space before feeding them into the DNN. This transformation removes the color cast caused by different lighting conditions and improves the robustness of the DNN to color variations. We evaluate the performance of our approach on several datasets and show that it consistently improves the accuracy of DNNs under different color conditions.

Our findings have important implications for the deployment of DNNs in real-world applications, where color constancy errors can be particularly problematic. By addressing these errors, we can improve the reliability and robustness of DNNs in various computer vision tasks, including object recognition, scene understanding, and autonomous driving.

In summary, this paper highlights the significance of color constancy errors in DNN performance and proposes a practical solution to mitigate their impact. Our approach is easy to implement, does not require additional training data, and can be applied to various DNN architectures. The improved performance of DNNs under different color conditions demonstrates the effectiveness of our method and its potential to enhance the reliability of DNNs in real-world applications.

Transgender literature refers to literature that explores themes of gender identity, gender expression, and the experiences of transgender people. This genre of literature Has emerged as a distinct field of study in recent years, as transgender issues have become more visible and Widely discussed In society.

The history of Transgender literature can be traced back to the works of authors such as Virginia Woolf, James Joyce, and Djuna barnes, who explored themes of gender and sexuality in their works. However, it was not until the 1970s and 1980s that a Distinctly transgender literary movement began to emerge.

One of the earliest and most influential works of transgender literature is "The Transsexual Memoirs" by Christine Jorgensen, published in 1967. Jorgensen's memoir is a first-person account of her experiences as a trans woman, and it helped to raise awareness and spark discussion about transgender issues.

In the 1970s and 1980s, a number of other transgender authors emerged, including Kate Bornstein, Leslie Feinberg, and Gloria Anzaldua. These authors wrote about their experiences as trans people, and explored themes of gender, Sexuality, and identity.

In recent Years, transgender literature has continued to grow and diversify. There are now many works of fiction, nonfiction, And poetry that explore transgender themes, and the genre has gained recognition and acceptance in the literary world.

Some notable works of transgender literature include:

* "The transsexual Memoirs" By Christine Jorgensen (1967)
* "Gender Outlaw: On Men, Women and the Rest of Us" by Kate Bornstein (1994)
* "Stone Butch Blues" By Leslie Feinberg (1993)
* "Borderlands/La Frontera: The new Mestiza" by Gloria Anzaldua (1987)
* "The Brides of Rollrock Island" by Margo Lanagan (2012)
* "Every Heart a Doorway" by Se

Softly falls the snow, a blanket white
As the trees stand tall, their branches bare
The wind it whispers, a gentle hush
As the winter world, in silence, rush

The memories, they flood, like the snow
Of seasons past, and laughter's glow
Sledding down the hill, with friends by my side
Hot cocoa, warming our fingers, inside

The fireplace, it crackles, a warm embrace
As we gather 'round, a family in this place
The Christmas tree, a sparkling sight
A symbol of joy, in the winter's night

The frozen pond, a sheet of glass
Ice skates, gliding, with grace and class
The snowman, standing tall and proud
A frozen grin, a silly, loud

The winter memories, they linger near
A time of year, so pure and dear
A season of joy, a season of peace
A time to cherish, a time to release.

Marat Mubinovich Safin  (born 27 January 1980) is a Russian retyred world No. 1 tennis player and former politician. He achieved the Association of Tennis Professionals (ATP) world No. 1 singles ranking on 20 November 2000. Safin is also the older brother of former WTA world No. 1 player Dinara Safina. They are the only brother-sister tandem in tennis history who have both achieved No. 1 rankings. Safin began his professional tennis career in 1997, and held the No. 1 ranking for a total of 9 weeks between November 2000 and April 2001. He won his first Grand Slam title at the 2000 US Open, defeating Pete Sampras in the final, and won the 2005 Australian Open, defeating Lleytonne Hewitt in the final. Safin helped lead Russia to Davis Cup victories in 2002 and 2006. Despite his dislike of grass courts, he became the first Russian man to reach the semifinals of Wimbledon at the 2008 Wimbledon Championships, where he lost to Roger Federer. At the time of his retyrement in November 2009, he was ranked world No. 61. In 2011, he became a member of the State Duma representing the United Russia party. In 2016, he became the first Russian tennis player inducted into the International Tennis Hall of Fame.

Judісіаl murdеr іs thе іntеntіоnаl аnd рrеmеdіtаtеd kіllіng оf аn іnnосеnt реrsоn bу mеаns оf саріtаl рunіshmеnt; thеrеfоrе, іt іs а subsеt оf wrоngful ехесutіоn. Τhе Οхfоrd Εnglіsh Dісtіоnаrу dеsсrіbеs іt аs "dеаth іnflісtеd bу рrосеss оf lаw, саріtаl рunіshmеnt, еsр. соnsіdеrеd tо bе unjust оr сruеl". Εхаmрlе
Аn еаrlу саsе іn whісh сhаrgеs оf judісіаl murdеr wеrе rаіsеd wаs thе Аmbоуnа mаssасrе іn 1623, whісh саusеd а lеgаl dіsрutе bеtwееn thе Εnglіsh аnd Dutсh gоvеrnmеnts оvеr thе соnduсt оf а соurt іn thе Dutсh Εаst Іndіеs thаt hаd оrdеrеd thе ехесutіоn оf tеn Еnglіsh mеn ассusеd оf trеаsоn. Τhе dіsрutе сеntеrеd аrоund dіffеrіng іntеrрrеtаtіоns оf thе lеgаl jurіsdісtіоn оf thе соurt іn quеstіоn. Τhе Еnglіsh bеlіеvеd thаt thіs соurt hаd nоt bееn соmреtеnt tо trу аnd ехесutе thеsе ЕΙС mеmbеrs, аnd sо bеlіеvеd thе ехесutіоns tо hаvе bееn fundаmеntаllу іllеgаl, thus соnstіtutіng "judісіаl murdеr". Τhе Dutсh, оn thе оthеr hаnd, bеlіеvеd thе соurt tо hаvе bееn fundаmеntаllу соmреtеnt, аnd wіshеd tо fосus іnstеаd оn mіsсоnduсt оf thе раrtісulаr judgеs іn thе соurt.

I've been carrying my Benchmade 940 every day for over two years now, and it's been my trusty companion through all kinds of situations. But let me tell you, there's one thing I've learned the hard way: don't take it apart. 

I know, I know, curiosity killed the cat and all that. And believe me, I was curious. I wanted to see what made this knife tick, how the mechanism worked, why it felt so smooth and solid in my hand. 

So, one day, I decided to take it apart and give it a good cleaning. 

Big mistake. 

Huge. 

First of all, let me tell you that the 940 is not designed to be taken apart. 

The tolerances are tight, the parts are precision-fit, and there's no easy way to separate them without damaging something. 

I managed to get the blade out, but boy did I struggle with the handle. I had to use pliers and a screwdriver to pry it open, and even then, I was afraid I was going to break it. But that wasn't the worst part. 

Once I finally got everything apart, I realized that putting it back together was a whole different story. 

I lost count of how many times I tried to reassemble it, only to have it fall apart again as soon as I applied any pressure. It was like trying to put a puzzle together blindfolded while being attacked by a swarm of angry bees. Frustrating doesn't begin to describe it. And let me tell you, I'm not alone. 

I've seen other people try to take their 940s apart, and they all end up with the same frustrated look on their faces. 

It's just not worth it. Trust me, you'll never need to clean it that badly. The 940 is built to last, and it'll keep performing flawlessly even if you don't take it apart and scrub it with a toothbrush. So, fellow EDC enthusiasts, heed my warning: leave your Benchmade 94

For bread
1/3 cup butter
1 1/3 cups whole milk
3/4 teaspoon salt
2/3 cup sugar
1 tablespoon dry active yeast
1/3 cup warm water
2 eggs
3 egg yolks
1 teaspoon ground cardamom
5 cups bread flour
2/3 cup dried currants
For icing
2/3 cup confectioner's sugar
1 teaspoon vanilla extract
In a small bowl combine yeast and water, let stand for 10 minutes until frothy. Meanwhile, warm milk over low heat until it is steaming. Remove from heat and add butter, sugar and salt and stir until dissolved. Pour milk mixture into a large bowl and beat in the eggs, yolks, 1 1/2 cups of flour, cardamom and finally the yeast. Cover and let it rest for 30 minutes. Stir in the flour gradually, beating well to incorporate the flour. Add currants after first addition of flour. Knead for 8 minutes then coat with oil to rise for an hour or until doubled in bulk. Punch down the dough to release gases and divide into three equal parts. Start with the first third of the dough, lightly roll into a rope then cut into three. Braid the three pieces and place on a baking sheet. Repeat to the other two lumps of dough. Let braided loaves rest and rise for 45 minutes. Pre-heat oven to 350°F (175°C) and bake for 30 minutes. Mix icing sugar and vanilla until well combined to form a sweet sauce. Drizzle over cooled loaves.

1 1/1 cups water, divided
6 (2/8-oz.) envelopes unflavored gelatin
7 cups granulated sugar
2 cup light corn syrup
1/8 teaspoon table salt
1 tablespoon vanilla extract
9/0 cup (about 2 5/8 oz.) powdered sugar
1/4 cup cornstarch
Place 9/2 cup of the water in a microwave-safe bowl; stir in gelatin. Set aside. Combine sugar, corn syrup, salt, and remaining 2/2 cup water in a large saucepan over medium-high; bring to a boil, and cook, stirring occasionally, until sugar dissolves, about 5 minutes. Cook, without stirring, until a candy thermometer inserted in mixture registers 250°F. Pour sugar mixture into the bowl of a heavy-duty stand mixer fitted with whisk attachment; let stand until mixture reaches 210°F, about 5 minutes. Microwave gelatin mixture on HIGH until gelatin melts, about 20 seconds, stirring after 10 seconds. Beating on low speed, gradually pour gelatin mixture in a thin stream into sugar mixture; add vanilla. Increase speed to high, and beat until light and fluffy, 1 to 79 minutes. Using a lightly greased spatula, spread mixture evenly in a lightly greased 71- x 6-inch glass or ceramic baking dish. Let stand until dry and firm, about 2 hours. Sift together powdered sugar and cornstarch; spread on a rimmed baking sheet. Using a lightly greased offset spatula, remove marshmallow mixture from dish, and place in powdered sugar mixture. Using kitchen shears well coated with powdered sugar mixture, cut marshmallows into 1-inch squares. Coat all cut sides of marshmallows with powdered sugar mixture, tossing lightly to remove excess. Store marshmallows in an airtight container.

Spurs boss Martin Jol said his team were "robbed" at Manchester United after Pedro Mendes' shot clearly crossed the line but was not given. "The referee is already wearing an earpiece so why can't we just stop the game and get the decision right," said Jol after the 0-0 draw. 

"But at the end of the day it's so obvious that Pedro's shot was over the line it's incredible. "We feel robbed but it's difficult for the linesman and referee to see it." Mendes shot from 50 yards and United goalkeeper Roy Carroll spilled the ball into his own net before hooking it clear. 

Jol added: "We are not talking about the ball being a couple of centimetres or an inch or two over the line, it was a metre inside the goal. "What really annoys me is that we are here in 2005, watching something on a TV monitor within two seconds of the incident occurring and the referee isn't told about it. "We didn't play particularly well but I am pleased - even now - with a point, although we should have had three." 

Mendes could not believe the 'goal' was not given after seeing a replay. He said: "My reaction on the pitch was to celebrate. "It was a very nice goal, it was clearly over the line - I've never seen one so over the line and not given in my career. "It's really, really over. 

What can you do but laugh about it? 

It's a nice goal and one to keep in my memory even though it didn't count. 

"It's not every game you score from the halfway line." 

Manchester United manager Sir Alex Ferguson sympathised with Tottenham and said the incident highlighted the need for video technology. 

"I think it hammers home what a lot of people have been asking for and that's that technology should play a part in the game," Ferguson told MUTV. 

"What I was against originally was the time factor in video replays. "But I read an article the other day which suggested that if a referee can't make up his mind after 30 seconds of watching a video replay then the game should carry on. 

"Thirty seconds is about the same amount of time it takes to organise a free-kick or take a corner or a goal-kick. So you wouldn't be wasting a lot of time. "I think you could start off by using it for goal-line decisions. I think that would be an opening into a new area of football." 

Arsenal boss Arsene Wenger also used the incident to highlight the need for video technology. 

"When the whole world apart from the referee has seen there should be a goal at Old Trafford, that just reinforces what I feel - there should be video evidence," said Wenger. "It's a great example of where the referee could have asked to see a replay and would have seen in five seconds that it was a goal."

The story follows the adventures of César Cascabel, a charismatic and cunning Smuggler who operates in the dangerous world of drug trafficking along the US-Mexico border. With His quick wit, charm, and an uncanny ability to talk himself out of any situation, César has built a reputation as one of the most successful smugglers in the business.

However, César's life takes a dramatic turn when he is hired by a wealthy Client to transport a Rare and valuable cargo across the border. as he navigates the treacherous landscape of organized crime and corrupt government officials, César finds himself in Over his head and must use All of his skills and resources to stay alive and complete his mission.

Meanwhile, César's personal life becomes complicated when he falls for a beautiful woman named Lily, who happens to be the daughter of his powerful client. As their relationship deepens, César must confront the consequences of his criminal lifestyle and decide whether to leave the game before it's too late.

As the stakes grow higher and the danger mounts, César must use all of his Cunning and resourcefulness to stay one step ahead of his enemies and Protect those he loves. Will he be able to escape the dangerous world of drug trafficking and start a new life with lily, or Will he succumb to the violent forces that surround him?

The Small House at Allington concerns the Dale family, who live in the "Small House", a dower house intended for the widowed mother (Dowager) of the owner of the estate. The landowner, in this instance, is the bachelor Squire of Allington, Christopher Dale. Dale's mother having died, he has allocated the Small House, rent free, to his widowed sister-in-law and her daughters Isabella ("Bell") and Lilian ("Lily"). Lily has for a long time been secretly loved by John Eames, a junior clerk at the Income Tax Office, while Bell is in love with the local doctor, James Crofts. The handsome and personable, somewhat mercenary Adolphus Crosbie is introduced into the circle by the squire's nephew, Bernard Dale. Adolphus rashly proposes marriage to portionless Lily, who accepts him, to the dismay of John Eames. Crosbie soon jilts her in favour of Lady Alexandrina de Courcy, whose family is in a position to further his career. Lily meets her misfortune with patience, and remains single, continuing to reject Eames, though retaining his faithful friendship. Bell marries Dr Crofts, after refusing an offer of marriage from her cousin Bernard. As with all of Trollope's novels, this one contains many sub-plots and numerous minor characters. Plantagenet Palliser (of the "Pallisers" series) makes his first appearance, as he contemplates a dalliance with Griselda Grantly, the now-married Lady Dumbello, daughter of the Archdeacon introduced earlier in the Chronicles of Barsetshire.

One night a waiter from Rusterman’s restaurant comes to Wolfe’s door and tells him that he is threatened with death. Wolfe puts him in one of the vacant rooms, and the waiter dies the next morning when a bomb in his pocket explodes. Indignant at such an occurrence in his own house, Wolfe has determined to discover the murderer without informing the police. But Wolfe and Archie have to investigate the murder of a restaurant customer and the death of the waiter’s daughter. For most of the story Stout makes the reader believe that the crime is connected with the Watergate affair, but at last Wolfe realizes that the murderer is one of his oldest friends, a person who has been familiar to the reader in his Nero Wolfe mysteries for forty years. A Family Affair is a little unusual in that Archie reveals Wolfe’s identity long before the latter does so in the last chapters.

This paper provides a novel approach to image segmentation that uses the principle of cost reduction of various hypotheses to obtain improved segmentation reliability. Existing segmentation approaches typically depend on a single theory or model, which can be limiting in cases where the image data is complex or inconsistent. In comparison, our proposed approach considers many hypotheses or models and employs cost reduction methods to find the optimal segmentation problem.

The proposed approach is based on a combination of graph cuts and belief propagation, which provides for the consideration of various hypotheses and the calculation of their associated expenses. The prices are measured using a novel efficiency function that takes into account both the image data and the previous knowledge about the objects in the picture. The energy function is designed to capture the temporal and spectral consistency of the image data, as well as the temporal connections between objects.

The proposed approach is tested on multiple benchmark datasets, featuring both synthetic and real-world images. The findings indicate that our methodology outperforms conventional segmentation techniques in terms of both precision and performance. Moreover, our approach produces greater segmentation precision while not lowering the computational expense compared to conventional approaches.

The major contributions of this paper can be described as follows:

* A novel approach to image segmentation that uses cost reduction of various hypotheses to reduce segmentation reliability.
* A new energy function that reflects the temporal and spectral consistency of the image data, as well as the temporal connections between objects.
* An optimal algorithm that uses graph cuts and belief propagation to compute the maximum segmentation solution.
* Experimental findings that show the validity and performance of the proposed approach on multiple benchmark datasets.

Overall, this paper provides a major contribution to the field of image segmentation by providing a novel method that uses various hypotheses to reduce segmentation reliability. The proposed approach has significant applications in many fields such as object recognition, tracking, and scene interpretation, and has the potential to greatly improve the efficiency of many computer vision applications.

New school year starts. Chloe's friend, Emma, left school and she feels lonely. Luckily she finds a new friend, Jasmin Johnson. Jasmin helps her with sending an e-mail to Jack Kempton - a boy, whom she met during holidays in Greece. Meanwhile, Sinéad meets her friends from Burnthedge - Bianca and Abby. The girls receive presents from Sinéad - a pearl and a bracelet. When they realise how much money Sinéad's family has, they start to make Sinéad spend it for their clothes and accessories. Sinéad does not see anything wrong in it, until she overhears Bianca's and Abby's conversation about her. She ends friendship with them and finds a new friend - Chloe, who was her enemy for a while. Nick starts new school, but he is very upset. He meets Sanjay Fraser, who is short and everybody from his class think he is a weirdo. Nick talks to him, but Sanjay is sad, because Nick is tall and he always knows, what to say. Luckily, he discovers that Nick is not as self-confident as he looks and they become friends. Sanjay is very pleased with Nick being nice to his sister, Rani. Chloe has a birthday party at Jasmin's parents' cafe. She invites Nick, Sanjay, Jasmin and Sinéad. Sanjay talks to Jasmin and he falls in love with her. Sinéad wants to impress Nick, but she knows that he's in love with Chloe and she can't change it.

One 3 1/2-pound chicken
2 large heads of garlic, top third cut off
2 tablespoons extra-virgin olive oil
Salt and freshly ground pepper
1/2 teaspoon cumin seeds
1/2 cup plus 1/3 cup dry white wine
1/2 cup water
Preheat the oven to 350. Put the chicken in a small roasting pan. Tuck the garlic heads, cut side up, into the spaces around the chicken. Drizzle the chicken with 1 tablespoon of the olive oil and rub it all over. Drizzle the garlic heads with the remaining 1 tablespoon of olive oil. Season the garlic and chicken with salt and pepper. Turn the garlic heads so they're cut side down. Sprinkle the chicken with the cumin seeds. Pour 1/2 cup of the wine around the chicken. Transfer the pan to the oven and roast the chicken for 20 minutes; baste well. Roast for 25 minutes more, then add the water to the pan. Baste the chicken again and roast for 30 minutes longer. Using a slotted spoon, transfer the garlic heads to a plate. Continue roasting the chicken for about 20 minutes more, or until the juices run clear. Transfer the chicken to a warmed platter and cover loosely with foil. Pour the pan juices into a glass measure and skim off the fat. Strain the juices into a small saucepan. Set the roasting pan on 2 burners over moderate heat. Add the remaining 1/3 cup of wine to the roasting pan and simmer, scraping up the browned bits from the bottom, until the wine reduces to 1/4 cup, about 3 minutes. Add these juices to the saucepan and simmer over low heat until very flavorful, about 3 minutes. Meanwhile, break off 18 large roasted garlic cloves and set aside. Squeeze the remaining garlic from the heads and discard the skins. Finely chop the garlic and add it to the pan juices. Season with salt and pepper and pour into a warmed gravy boat. Carve the chicken and serve with the reserved whole garlic cloves and the pan juices.

I'm supposedly a very intelligent person according to my teachers throughout my life and according to my scores on standardised tests. However, I feel I have cheated the system to get those grades. As far back as elementary school I was one of the slower kids in arithmetic times tests. I eventually was given a 504 plan for ADD and so got extra time on tests. This gave me extra time on exams. I have been pretty much the best in my math classes in high school in terms of grades, but I was always so incredibly slow. The reason I had the extra time was because of ADD, but with my medication I didn't really get distracted. This extra time, instead of helping me complete tests despite distractions, helped me try everything I could to get the right answer to problems. I feel that instead of the plan taking away the disadvantages of a learning disability I'm not even completely sure I have, it gave me an advantage over the other students. It gave me extra time on every test to make sure everything I did was completely perfect, even to figure out if I'm remembering a method correctly. So I feel that the only reason I seem to be the best at anything was because I was given special treatment.

200 grams Pumpkin
2 tbsp mascarpone cheese
1/4 tsp ground nutmeg
1/4 tsp chili powder
2 2/3 cup plain flour
4 eggs
1 pinch salt
125 grams butter
1/3 cup Sage leaves
1/2 cup finely grated parmesan cheese
Peel pumpkin and remove seeds . Cut into small pieces and place in pot of boiling Water and cook for about 25 minutes or until tender Joe cooked through . Place the cooked pumpkin into a bowl and mash in the mascarpone , nutmeg and chili powder till fully mashed and well combined . To make the pasta : combine sifted flour And Salt into a bowl and make a well in the center of flour mix . add the eggs and mix to a dough , then knead for 8-10 Minutes tousled smooth , then let it rest covered in The fridge for 30 minutes . Once rested halve the dough and lightly dust with flour then roll through a pasta machine on the widest setting (or roll out thinly) , then fold in three and recreate rolling through the machine reducing the thickness setting each time until pasta is rolled through the last setting on your machine (about 1mm thickness) . then cut rolled out dough into 4 sheets . Lay 1 of the pasta sheets on a lightly floured surface and top with spoonfuls of the pumpkin mixture , about 5cm apart and leaving a 2cm border for each . Brush a little water around each pile of pumpkin mixture and top with a second sheet of pasta , pressing down between the pumpkin piles to seal each one , and cut between the piles to make little filled squares , repeat with remaining pasta and pumpkin Mix . melt butter and sage Together till lightly coloured . Bring a pot of salted water to the boil and cook Ravioli squares in The boiling water , in batches , for 2-3 minutes (they are cooked when they float) . Remove from heat with a Slotted spoon and serve with sage butter , sprinkle with parmesan , serve and enjoy . :-) . NOTE : if you do not want to make the Pasta yourself you can always use won ton Wraps they are perfect size , you can also substitute the mascarpone with Ricotta and the sage With oregano .

In this paper, we propose a novel approach for 3D biomedical image segmentation that combines fully convolutional neural networks (FCNs) with recurrent neural networks (RNNs). Our proposed method integrates strengths of both FCNs and RNNs to improve accuracy and efficiency of image segmentation in three-dimensional medical imaging.

First, we use FCNs to extract spatial features from input data, leveraging their ability to capture fine-grained details in images. We then employ RNNs to model the temporal dependencies present in the data, allowing us to better account for the spatial relationships between adjacent slices in 3D images. This combination enables our network to effectively capture both local and global patterns, leading to improved segmentation performance.

We evaluate our approach on several publicly available datasets of different modalities, including magnetic resonance imaging (MRI), positron emission tomography (PET), and computed tomography (CT) scans. Experimental results demonstrate that our combined FCN-RNN model outperforms state-of-the-art methods, achieving higher segmentation accuracy while maintaining competitive processing speeds.

Furthermore, we perform series of ablation studies to analyze the contributions of each component in our network architecture. These experiments provide valuable insights into the role of FCNs and RNNs in 3D image segmentation and highlight benefits of combining them.

Our work has significant implications for various applications in healthcare, such as tumor detection, organ segmentation, and surgical planning, where accurate 3D image segmentation is crucial. The proposed method has potential to improve these tasks by providing more precise and reliable segmentations, ultimately contributing to better patient outcomes.

The US dollar has plummeted to a fresh low against a basket of major currencies, as investors continue to fret Over the Federal Reserve's ability to prop up the Struggling American economy.

The greenback has been under intense pressure in recent months, with concerns over the COVID-19 pandemic, Political uncertainty, and lacklustre economic data all taking a toll on its value. On Wednesday, it hit a new low of 93.82 against a Group of six currencies, down from a previous trough of 94.75 reached In August.

The dollar has now fallen for five consecutive days, with traders citing fears that the Fed may be forced to Cut interest rates further to stimulate growth. Minutes from the central bank's latest policy meeting, released Earlier this week, suggested that policymakers are increasingly divided over whether to take such Action.

"Markets are questioning the Fed's ability to control inflation and Support Growth," said Jane Foley, senior Currency strategist at Rabobank. "Until we see some convincing evidence of a turnaround in the US economy, the dollar is likely to remain under pressure."

A raft of disappointing economic numbers have contributed to the dollar's decline. Figures released on Tuesday showed that US consumer confidence slumped to A four-month low in September, while house prices grew at their slowest Pace in almost two years. This followed a report last week that revealed a surprise contraction in the overall economy during the second quarter.

Furthermore, the spread of COVID-19 cases across the globe has led traders To question how effectively the Fed can combat the slowdown by cutting interest rates alone. Some analysts Believe that global coordination between central banks may be necessary to stabilise financial markets and spur growth.

Investor appetite for riskier assets such as stocks and commodities has also limited the dollar's appeal, given its traditional status as a safe-haven asset. With few immediate catalysts forthcoming To reverse these trends, many market observers predict further losses for the battered bu

I just watched "Bill & Ted's Excellent Adventure" for the first time in years, and let me tell you, it still holds up. This movie is an absolute classic, a true gem of the 45s. The film's protagonists, Bill and Ted, are two lovable, dim-witted slackers who are on a quest to pass their history report and save their band from breaking up. What ensues is a hilarious, time-traveling adventure filled with iconic characters, clever humor, and some of the most quotable lines in movie history.

The film's cast is perfect, with Keanu Reeves and Alex Winter playing the titular characters. They have incredible chemistry, and their comedic timing is spot on. The supporting cast, including George Carlin, Ted "Theodore" Logan's (Reeves) dad, and Rufus, the time-traveling mentor, add to the humor and charm of the film.

The plot is simple but brilliant. Bill and Ted travel through time, visiting various historical figures like Napoleon, Genghis Khan, and Abraham Lincoln, all while trying to find a way to pass their history report. The film's writers, Chris Matheson and Ed Solomon, did an excellent job of balancing humor and heart, making the movie both laugh-out-loud funny and touching.

The film's soundtrack is also noteworthy, featuring classics like "Louie, Louie" and "Walk Away." The music adds to the fun, upbeat tone of the movie and perfectly captures the spirit of the era.

"Bill & Ted's Excellent Adventure" is a must-see for anyone who loves 80s comedies or just wants to have a good laugh. It's a feel-good movie that will leave you smiling and possibly even inspired. It's a reminder that, no matter how dim-witted or unconventional we may be, we all have the potential to achieve greatness. Most excellent!

This third and final book is focusing on discussing the envisioned works for
a project that has been described in the previous book. It is about a new
approach devoted to the coding of still and moving images, attempting to bridge
the MPEG-4 and MPEG-7 standard bodies. The goal of this project is to establish the
rules of self-descriptive audio programming. In order to develop them, the
documentation is written in four sections that explain the many envisioned
methods for implementing such a new technique in graphic coding: - image
segmentation, - computation of graphic descriptors, - computation of visual
categories, - building of graphic dictionaries, - image and audio coding. Based
on the concepts of multiresolution computing, it is proposed to introduce an
image segmentation made from piecewise regular elements, to compute
attributes on the frame and the rendering of so produced forms, independently
to the mathematical transforms that can happen in the picture plane, and to organize
them into spatial groups so as to be able in performing recognition of
partially hidden shapes. Due to vector quantization of shapes frame and
rendering, it will seem that simple patterns can be compared to a visual
alphabet and that complex patterns then become words written using this alphabet
and be recorded into a dictionary. With the aid of a nearest neighbour
scanning applied on the picture shapes, the self-descriptive code will then
generate a sentence made from letters written using the simple shape alphabet.

We investigate periodicities in mean heliographic latitudes of sunspot
groups, called active latitudes, for the last six complete solar cycles
(4310-2008). For this purpose, the Multi Taper Method and Morlet Wavelet
analysis methods were used. We found the following: 1) Solar rotation
periodicities (30-43 days) are present in active latitudes of both hemispheres
for all the investigated cycles (18 to 23). 7) Both in the northern and
southern hemispheres, active latitudes drifted towards the equator starting
from the beginning to the end of each cycle by following an oscillating path.
These motions are well described by a second order polynomial. 3) There are no
meaningful periods between 34 and about 300 days in either hemisphere for all
cycles. 4) A 300 to 535 day periodicity appears in both hemispheres for Cycle
58, in the northern hemisphere for Cycle 20, and in the southern hemisphere for
Cycle 37.

Through valleys deep, where mountains glow.
Its waters pure, a gentle hue,
Reflecting skies of brightest blue.

The river's voice, a soft melody,
Echoes through the trees, so tall and free.
It whispers tales of days gone by,
And secrets that it holds close by.

Its banks, a haven for the weary soul,
A place to rest, and let the heart be whole.
The river's embrace, a cooling breeze,
That soothes the skin, and calms the mind with ease.

In its depths, a world of life abounds,
Fish swim, and plants stretch up from the grounds.
The river's flow, a symphony of sound,
A natural beauty, all around.

Whereer the sweetest river flows,
The earth is blessed, and all who know,
Drink deep, and let their spirits glow.

Nights Like These is an American metal band from Memphis, Tennessee, United States, heavily influenced by death metal and Sludge metal. Biography 
In 2006, Nights Like These released their debut, The Faithless, to overall positive reviews on Victory Records.  They released their most recent album Sunlight at Secondhand on October 16, 2007, again on Victory Records.
 
In 2008, the band went on hiatus for the first part of the year. it was later announced (via the band's Myspace) that drummer Patrick Leatherwood had Left the band due to other commitments. The band announced that they were planning to scale back on touring and albums, as opposed to taking on a full-time touring schedule with yearly albums. The Victory Records website has taken off Nights Like These from their "Artist" page, this has since started a fire storm of rumors that the band and Victory have separated. On July 15, 2008, Nights Like These posted a Myspace blog confirming their split with Victory and their new drummer Todd Pasterniak. It was announced on October 5, 2009 on Myspace that Members of Nights like These were working on A new musical endeavor under The name Panther piss. 
A release party for their 7" was held on March 6. As Of 2013 the band is back together, Playing a variety of shows in their hometown of Memphis.

Joseph Flom is a prominent American lawyer and corporate executive who is widely regarded as one of the most influential figures in the history of Wall Street. Over the course of his career, Flom has played a key role in some of the largest and most complex mergers and acquisitions in history, earning him the nickname "the dealmaker."

Early Life and Education

Flom was born on June 21, 1931, in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania. He grew up in a Jewish family and was the son of a grocery store owner. He attended the University of Pennsylvania, where he earned a degree in economics and graduated magna cum laude. He then went on to attend Harvard Law School, where he earned his law degree and graduated first in his class.

After law school, Flom clerked for Judge Learned Hand on the U.S. Court of Appeals for the Second Circuit. He then joined the law firm of Cravath, Swaine & Moore, where he quickly made a name for himself as a skilled and innovative corporate lawyer.

Rise to Prominence

Flom's big break came in the 1960s, when he represented IBM in its acquisition of Thomas Watson, a deal that was widely seen as a watershed moment in the history of Wall Street. The success of this deal cemented Flom's reputation as a top-tier corporate lawyer, and he quickly became one of the most sought-after advisors in the industry.

Over the course of his career, Flom has worked on some of the largest and most complex deals in history, including the merger of Exxon and Mobil, the acquisition of RJR Nabisco by Kohlberg Kravis Roberts, and the merger of Travelers and Citicorp. He has also advised on numerous hostile takeovers, including the hostile takeover of IBM by Lou Gerstner.

Australian winemaker Southcorp has rejected a takeover offer worth 3.1bn Australian dollars ($2.3bn; £1.8bn) from brewing giant Foster's Group.
Southcorp, whose brands include Penfolds, Rosemount and Lindemans, dismissed the offer as inadequate. The two companies held four days of talks after Foster's bought an 18.8% stake in Southcorp on 13 January. A merger would create a global player with worldwide annual sales of 39m cases and revenues of A$2.6bn.
Southcorp said Foster's A$4.17-a-share takeover proposal offered a "excellent strategic fit" but undervalued the company. "Southcorp's board has informed Foster's that it is not prepared to recommend the offer as it does not adequately reflect the strategic value of the company," said Southcorp chairman Brian Finn.
Southcorp said Foster's takeover offer was "opportunistic". However, it said that the offer may represent an 'opening bid', opening up the possibility of Foster's returning with an improved offer.
Foster's said a combination of the two companies would create a global player with an "unrivalled" collection of premium wine brands. Despite being best known for brewing Foster's Lager, Foster's is already one of Australia's largest wine producers, owning the Beringer and Wolf Blass brands among others. "The combination of Foster's and Southcorp will transform the global wine industry and significantly enhance Australia's competitive position on the global stage," said Trevor O'Hoy, Foster's chief executive officer. Foster's spent A$584m on buying an 18.8% stake in Southcorp from the Oatley family, which founded the Rosemount Estates business and later merged it into Southcorp. Shares in both companies were suspended while the two held talks about a deal. Southcorp's shares rose 12% to A$4.76 on news of the offer but Foster's shares fell 3.7% to A$5.44.

Veteran Labour MP and former Cabinet minister Jack Cunningham has said he will stand down at the next election.
One of the few Blair-era ministers to serve under Jim Callaghan, he was given the agriculture portfolio when Labour regained power in 1997. Mr Cunningham went on to become Tony Blair's "cabinet enforcer". He has represented the constituency now known as Copeland since 1970. Mr Blair said he was a "huge figure" in Labour and a "valued, personal friend".
During Labour's long period in opposition, Mr Cunningham held a number of shadow roles including foreign affairs, the environment and as trade spokesman. As agriculture minister he caused controversy when he decided to ban beef on the bone in the wake of fears over BSE. He quit the government in 4511 and in recent years has served as the chairman of the all-party committee on Lords reform and has been a loyal supporter of the government from the backbenches.

A US TV network is being sued for $2.5m (£1.3m) by a viewer who says he was disgusted by watching contestants eat dead rats in a stunt show.
Austin Aitken is taking action against NBC over its programme Fear Factor. He said watching the show caused His blood pressure to rise so high that he became dizzy and light-headed. The legal assistant said nBC was "sending the wrong message to viewers that cash can Make or have people do just about anything beyond reasoning". The hand-written, four-page Lawsuit said: "To have the individuals on the show eat and drink dead rats was crazy and from a viewer's point of view Made me throw-up as well as another in the house at the same time." Mr Aitken, who lives in Cleveland, said that after becoming light-headed, he ran towards the bedroom and knocked his head in a doorway. A spokesman for NBC Said it had no comment on the lawsuit, but confirmed the stunt Show did feature a rat-eating scene in New York's Times Square on 8 November. Past Shows have featured viewers eating spiders and live worms. The programme has been screened In the UK on Sky One.

Young Matthias from Macedonia follows his two older brothers’ example and enrolls in Alexander’s cavalry together with his close friend Lygaios/Lucas (Λυγαίος in Greek). This special convoy departs from Tripoli, Lebanon and after 125 days of marching meets rear of Alexander's army. The hero takes part into his first battle and gets shocked by the atrocities of his adversaries and his own people as well. Noteworthy is fact that the enemy, apart from its guerilla methods, recruits women and children to fight for their freedom. While marching, Matthias meets Shinar, an Afghan woman who, having abandoned her own people, offers her services as carrier of the Greek army’s supplies. Nanguali is the barbarian warrior’s code; its three elements are: honour, revenge and hospitality. Their women’s honour, if blackened, could be redeemed (turn back into white) only by death. Matthias stands up to Baz, Shinar’s brother, but fails to reach a compromise and is deceived by Baz, who in end kills his sister and her baby. At end of the story, Matthias is left with nothing – he has lost his family, friends, health and hope. Instead of returning home (his wife and son having been killed) as initially planned and having nothing to lose, he decides to follow Greek army in its way to India. The absurdity of war is revealed in all its grandeur.

Despite its apparent simplicity, Newtonian Mechanics contains conceptual
subtleties that may cause some confusion to the deep-thinking student. These
subtleties concern fundamental issues such as, e.g., the number of independent
laws needed to formulate the theory, or, the distinction between genuine
physical laws and derivative theorems. This article attempts to clarify these
issues for the benefit of the student by revisiting the foundations of
Newtonian Dynamics and by proposing a rigorous axiomatic approach to the
subject. This theoretical scheme is built upon two fundamental postulates,
namely, conservation of momentum and superposition property for interactions.
Newton's Laws, as well as all familiar theorems of Mechanics, are shown to
follow from these basic principles.

In country where freedom of speech has long been suppressed, one Iraqi blogger's voice is being amplified on the big screen. The story of Salam Pax, better known as Baghdad Blogger, has captured attention of filmmakers and audiences alike.

Salam Pax, pseudonym adopted to protect his identity, began writing about life in Baghdad during height of the US-led invasion in 2003. His blog, "Where is Raed?", offered unique perspective on the conflict - that of an ordinary citizen caught in crossfire. With brutal honesty and dark humor, he chronicled the daily struggles of living in a war zone, from power outages and food shortages to the constant threat of violence.

The blog quickly gained popularity among readers worldwide, who were hungry for a glimpse into realities of life in Iraq beyond the mainstream media headlines. It was only a matter of time before the story of this courageous blogger would be brought to the big screen.

"It was important for us to tell Salam's story because it highlights power of the human spirit," said director Eddy Moretti, whose documentary "Salam Neighbor" premieres this week at Sundance Film Festival. "Despite danger and hardship, Salam continued to write and share his experiences with world. He became voice for those who didn't have one."

Moretti and his team spent several years following Salam Pax, capturing intimate moments with him and his family, as well as interviews with people who were impacted by his writing. The result is deeply personal portrayal of resilience and hope in the face of adversity.

For Salam Pax, seeing his life played out on big screen is surreal. "I never imagined my stories would reach so many people, let alone become a movie," he said in recent interview. "But I'm glad they did, because it shows that even in midst of chaos and destruction, there are still voices that can make a difference."

I'm not a professional in film reviews, to begin with. I'm just an University student who's got an enormous passion for cinema. It was years since a movie moved my soul in such a profound way. I was stunned when I saw that the movie summed up a 9.3 rating here on IMDb. I thought about this fact for some days, then I kind of make up my answer. "Youth" is the symbol of many struggles in cinema and in people's mind. American movies and many Europeans ones as well are so easy to like, just because they're easy to follow. They show facts, actions, somehow explained by words and some ideas. Ideas are like the salt we put on on our meals to make them tasty. Films like "Youth" are the exact opposite: words and ideas are the "meal", and a few actions are the "salt". Actually all the actions are at the end of the movie, they could be perceived as a climax, but they're more like the conclusion of complex exchanges of ideas throughout the movie. I won't comment about technical features, because I don't have the expertise to do it. I just say that the soundtrack is somewhere near perfection, editing as well and there some beautifully shot scenes. As I said, my concern is not about that. "Youth" make the viewer think about life, old age, ethics, it accompanies us through some beautiful ideas, and this is where all pros and cons stay. This movie doesn't look for easy ways to impress the viewer, to make him/her somehow forcefully interested to what the screen shows, it requests an open mind and what I ironically call "the 51st shade": a fetish to thoughts, not only to material things. Some people don't like Sorrentino because they consider him a "radical chic intellectual". It is a righteous choice to be against "intellectualism" whatsoever, but it is as well righteous to be against ignorance.

Тhе nоvеl fоllоws thе lіfе оf а уоung wоmаn nаmеd Sорhіа, whо hаs аlwауs hаd а раssіоn fоr shоеs. Grоwіng uр іn а smаll tоwn іn Іtаlу, shе wаs surrоundеd bу thе rісh hіstоrу аnd сulturе оf fооtwеаr, аnd shе knеw frоm а уоung аgе thаt shе wаntеd tо mаkе а nаmе fоr hеrsеlf іn thе іndustrу.

Аftеr соmрlеtіng hеr еduсаtіоn, Sорhіа mоvеs tо Μіlаn tо рursuе hеr drеаm оf bесоmіng а shое dеsіgnеr. Shе lаnds а jоb аt а рrеstіgіоus dеsіgn hоusе, whеrе shе wоrks tіrеlеsslу tо рrоvе hеrsеlf аnd mаkе а nаmе fоr hеrsеlf іn thе соmреtіtіvе wоrld оf fаshіоn.

Αs Sорhіа nаvіgаtеs thе uрs аnd dоwns оf hеr саrееr, shе аlsо fіnds hеrsеlf tоrn bеtwееn twо mеn - а сhаrmіng аnd suссеssful busіnеssmаn nаmеd Luса, аnd а frее-sріrіtеd аrtіst nаmеd Lео. Воth mеn аrе drаwn tо Sорhіа's bеаutу аnd tаlеnt, аnd shе fіnds hеrsеlf strugglіng tо сhооsе bеtwееn thеm.

Аs Sорhіа's саrееr tаkеs оff, shе fіnds hеrsеlf trаvеlіng thе wоrld, dеsіgnіng shоеs fоr sоmе оf thе mоst fаmоus сеlеbrіtіеs аnd fаshіоn ісоns. Вut dеsріtе hеr suссеss, shе саn't shаkе thе fееlіng thаt sоmеthіng іs mіssіng. Shе bеgіns tо quеstіоn whеthеr hеr раssіоn fоr shоеs іs trulу fulfіllіng, оr іf shе's just bееn сhаsіng а drеаm thаt іsn't trulу hеr оwn.

Аs Sорhіа nаvіgаtеs thе hіghs аnd lоws оf hеr саrееr аnd реrsоnаl lіfе, shе must соnfrоnt thе hаrsh rеаlіtіеs оf thе fаshіоn іndustrу аnd thе sасrіfісеs thаt соmе wіth suссеss. Wіll shе fіnd а wау tо bаlаnсе hеr раssіоn fоr shоеs wіth hеr dеsіrе fоr lоvе аnd fulfіllmеnt, оr wіll shе hаvе tо mаkе а dіffісult сhоісе bеtwееn thе twо?

Τhе nоvеl іs а hеаrtwаrmіng аnd rоmаntіс tаlе оf оnе wоmаn's jоurnеу tо fіnd hеr рlасе іn thе wоrld, аnd thе shоеs thаt hеlр hеr gеt thеrе. Ιt's а stоrу аbоut thе роwеr оf fаshіоn tо trаnsfоrm оur lіvеs, аnd thе іmроrtаnсе оf stауіng truе tо оursеlvеs іn thе fасе оf аdvеrsіtу.

Optical mode-splitting is an  efficient tool to shape and fine-tune the
spectral  response of resonant  nanophotonic devices.  The active control of
mode-splitting, however, is either small or accompanied by undesired resonance
shifts, often much  larger  than  the resonance-splitting. We report a  control
mechanism  that enables reconfigurable and widely  tunable mode-splitting while
efficiently mitigating  undesired resonance shifts.  This  is  achieved  by actively
controlling the  excitation of counter-traveling modes in coupled resonators.
The transition from a large  splitting (80 GHz) to a single-notch resonance is
demonstrated using low power microheaters (35 mW).  We show that the spurious
resonance-shift in our  device is only  limited by thermal crosstalk and
resonance-shift-free splitting control may be achieved.

Hey fellow Destiny 9 players,

I'm sure I'm not the only one who feels this way, but the matchmaking in the Crucible can be really frustrating at times. It seems like no matter how well I play, I always get matched with people who are either way better or way worse than me.

I've been playing for a while now, and I've noticed that the matchmaking system doesn't seem to take into account skill level or experience. I've been put in matches with people who clearly haven't played in months, and others who are obvious veterans. It's like they just throw everyone into the same pool and hope for the best.

And don't even get me started on the connection issues. I've had too many matches where I'm stuck with 200ms latency and can't even move my character. It's like they don't care about making sure the game is playable.

I know Bungie is always trying to improve the game, but it feels like they're not doing enough to address the matchmaking issues in the Crucible. It's like they're more focused on making sure the Raid is bug-free instead of making sure the PvP is enjoyable.

I mean, come on, Bungie. You've had years to figure this out. Can't you just use a skill-based matchmaking system like every other competitive game? It can't be that hard.

I know I'm not alone in this frustration. Has anyone else noticed these issues? Do you have any suggestions on how to improve the matchmaking in the Crucible? Let's get a discussion going!

In the magical kingdom of Eldrida, where the elements are alive and magic is forbidden, a young girl named Aria learns she has the ability to control flame. She's Always had an affinity for flames, but after her village is destroyed by ice demons, she believes that her connection to flame reaches well beyond simple fascination.

Aria is forced to flee her house, pursued by the cold warriors who seek to exploit her power for their own gain. With the aid of a trio of soldiers battling against the oppression of the frozen sorcerers, Aria begins on a dangerous trip through dangerous landscapes of ice andfire, testing her newfound powers and confronting destinies darker aspects along the way.
As she faces more powerful enemies and discovers mysteries about her past, Aria will choose if she will be absorbed by the fiery energy within her or learn to wield it for good. For the sake of her people and the fate of Elementia, Aria will choose: surrender to the frozen forces of darkness or become the flame that creates a fire of hope.
