Іn twіlіght's hush, whеrе shаdоws dаnсе аnd рlау,
А vоісе whіsреrs fеаr, а sіrеn's саll аwау.
Іt tаunts аnd tеаsеs, а lіngеrіng drеаd,
А wеіght thаt рrеssеs hеаvу оn оur hеаds.

Вut fеаr nоt fеаr, fоr іt's а tеst,
А сhаllеngе tо bе mеt, а quеst.
То fасе thе unknоwn, tо рush аsіdе,
Τhе dоubts thаt сlоud оur hеаrts аnd mіnds.

Fоr іn thе dаrknеss, thеrе's аlsо lіght,
Α sраrk thаt flісkеrs, tаkеs flіght.
Соurаgе іs thе fіrе thаt burns wіthіn,
А flаmе thаt саsts оut dоubt аnd sіn.

Sо lеt us еmbrасе thе fеаr wе fееl,
Аnd turn іt іntо strеngth, а stееl.
Тhаt hоlds us uр, thаt kеерs us strоng,
Αnd guіdеs us thrоugh lіfе's jоurnеу lоng.

Wіth еvеrу stер wе tаkе, wіth еvеrу stаnd,
Wе соnquеr fеаr, wе sеіzе thе lаnd.
Wе grоw аnd lеаrn, wе rіsе аbоvе,
Αnd fіnd thе соurаgе tо lоvе.

Fеаr nоt fеаr, thеn, nоr іts mіght,
Fоr wе аrе brаvе, wе bаnіsh nіght.
Wе fасе еасh trіаl, wе mееt еасh tеst,
Аnd еmеrgе vісtоrіоus, trulу blеssеd.

Behold her,
Running through the waves
Eager to reach the land;
The water laps her,
Sun and wind are on her,
Healthy, brine-drenched and young,
Behold Desire new-born;-
Desire on first fulfillment's radiant edge,
Love at miraculous moment of emergence,
This is she,
Who running,
Hastens, hastens to the land.
Look. . Look. .
Her blown gold hair and lucent eyes of youth,
Her body rose and ivory in the sun. .
Look,
How he hastens,
Running, running to the land.
Her hands are yearning and her feet are swift
To reach and hold
She knows not what
Yet knows that it is life;
Need urges her,
Self, uncomprehended but most deep divined,
Unwilled but all-comprelling, drives her on.
Life runs to life.
She who longs,
But hath not yet accepted or bestowed,
All virginal dear and bright,
Runs, runs to reach the land.
And she who runs shall be
married to blue of summer skies at noon,
companion to green fields,
held bride to subtle fragrance and of all sweet sound,
beloved of the stars,
and wanton priestess to the veering winds.
Oh breathless space between:
Womb-time just passed,
Dark-hidden, chaotic formative, unpersonal,
And individual life of fresh-created force
Not yet begun:
One moment more
Before desire shall meet desire
And new creation start.
Oh breathless space,
While she,
Just risen from the waves,
Runs, runs to reach the land.
(Ah, keenest personal moment
When mouth unkissed turns eager-slow and tremulous
Toward lover's mouth,
That tremulous and eager-slow
Droops down to it:
But breathless space of breath or two
Lies in between
Before the mouth upturned and mouth down-drooped
Shall meet and make the kiss.)
Look. . Look. .
She runs. .
Love fresh-emerged,
Desire new-born. .
Blown on by wind,
And shone on by the sun,
She rises from the waves
And running,
Hasten, hastens to the land.
Belove'd and Belove'd and Belove'd,
Even so right
And beautiful and
Is my desire;
Even so longing-swift
I run to your receiving arms.
O Aphrodite!
O Aphrodite hear!
Hear my wrung cry flame upward poignant-glad…
This is my time for me.
I too am young;
I too am all of love!

Provideo (4 January 1982 – 7 September 2000) was an Irish Thoroughbred racehorse and sire. In 1984 he set 20th-century record for British-trained two-year-old when he won sixteen of his twenty-four races. His most important wins were in two Listed races, the Star Stakes at Sandown Park and the Champion Two-Year-Old Trophy at Ripon Racecourse. Despite being rated twenty pounds below best horses of year he was named Horse of the Year by the Timeform organisation and topped official British Horse of the Year poll conducted by Racegoer's Club. Background
Provideo was small, compact brown colt bred by his owner Ahmed Foustok and sired by sprinter Godswalk. He was second foal of his dam Nadwa, who won two minor races as a two-year-old in 1978. Like Nadwa, Provideo was trained throughout his career by Bill O'Gorman. Provideo raced in blinkers from his fifth start onwards and usually won his races from front. He was sent into training with O'Gorman at Newmarket, Suffolk and was ridden in most of his races by Tony Ives. In the autumn of 1983 it had been decided that the yearling would be taken to be sold at the Doncaster sales, but O'Gorman was unable to travel having been kicked by another horse, and he decided instead to train the colt for racing.

Sania Mirza, the first Indian woman to reach the third round of a Grand Slam tennis event, has lost to women's favourite Serena Williams.
The 18-year-old Mirza, who got a wild card entry into the Australian Open in Melbourne, lost to Williams 1-6,4-6 in the third round. Williams took just 56 minutes to defeat Mirza and sail into the fourth round. The only other Indian woman to win a match at a Grand Slam is Nirupama Vaidyanathan. Vaidyanathan made it to the second round of the Australian Open in 1998.
Playing the biggest match of her life, Mirza made little impact on Williams in the early stages of the game. But the teenager showed more confidence in the second set and engaged the seventh-seeded Williams in some well contested rallies. Mirza, a junior Wimbledon doubles title winner, became the first Indian woman to reach the third round of a grand slam tennis event when she beat Hungarian Petra Mandula on Wednesday. "I'm realy excited. I was confident but I didn't think it was going to be that easy," Mirza said after her second round win. "My aim was to win a round here. When I did that I was so relieved, there was no pressure." Tennis is not a particularly popular sport in India, but a number of Indians watched the live telecast of the match between Mirza and Williams. Mirza, who lives in the southern Indian city of Hyderabad known for producing a host of top Indian cricketers, turned professional two years ago. She says she was considered too small when she went for her first tennis classes as a six-year-old girl. "Then finally [the coach] called my parents up and said 'the way she hits the ball, I've never seen a six-year-old hit a ball like that'," Mirza told the Associated Press.

In a small, agricultural town nestled in the heart of California's Central Valley, a young girl named Evelyn grows up with a secret that threatens to tear her family apart. 

Born with a rare genetic disorder that renders her unable to feel physical pain, Evelyn is forced to hide her condition from the world, relying on her razor-sharp instincts to navigate the challenges of daily life. As she grows older, Evelyn becomes increasingly skilled at masking her symptoms, but her duplicity takes a heavy toll on her relationships with those around her. Her parents, struggling to understand their daughter's peculiar behavior, grow distant and suspicious, while her younger brother, obsessed with his own struggles, remains blissfully unaware of the truth. Despite her isolation, Evelyn finds solace in her work on the family farm, where she develops a deep connection with the land and a fierce determination to protect it from the encroaching threats of corporate agribusiness. As she delves deeper into the mysteries of plant biology and the ancient practices of natural healing, Evelyn discovers a powerful gift for communicating with the plants and animals that surround her. 

But when a group of activists arrives in town, seeking to expose the dark secrets of the local farm industry, Evelyn is faced with an impossible choice: can she continue to harbor her secret, or will she risk everything to join forces with these rebels and fight for a better future? 

Ultimately, as the stakes escalate and the lies mount, Evelyn must confront the truth about herself, her family, and the very fabric of the community she calls home. 

Will her unique gifts be enough to save them all, or will they ultimately prove her undoing?

Grief's heavy mantle drapes my heart,
A cloak of grief, gray and cold.
My soul, a vessel once full of cheer,
Now filled with joy, deep and clear.

The tears I cry, they fall Like hail,
A never-ending flood of grief.
My emotions, a jumble of grief,
A song of anguish, echoing there.

In this abyss of despair, I'm lost,
A fragile flower, torn from its cost.
The winds of grief, they howl and cry,
A sad song, all my own.

My days, they drag, a slow, sad procession,
A funeral march, stark and stark.
The weight of grief, it bears down,
A crushing burden, unable to drown.

In this sea of tears, I'm adrift,
A fragile vessel, at the mercy of the rift.
The waves of despair, they crash and roar,
A fierce wind, no shore in sight.

But, in this light, a hope shines,
A thread of hope, a beacon divine.
For in the depths of darkness's sleep,
A spark of strength, a guide to light.

With each breath, I'll know my way,
A pathway to healing, night by day.
The pain, it will not fade,
But with time, its edges, softened, made.

The flames are licking at the sky,
Their hunger never satisfied,
Their roar like thunder in the night,
As they dance and spread their might. 

The sparks are flying high and bright,
Like fireflies on a summer's night,
Their glow illuminates the fear,
That's etched on every face so dear. 

The heat is intense, it's hard to breathe,
The smoke is choking, it's hard to believe,
The destruction that they leave behind,
Is a sight that's hard to find. 

The bells are ringing, loud and clear,
Their warning echoes far and near,
But the flames they just keep coming,
Like a beast that's never tamed. The firefighters fight with all their might,
Their bravery is a wondrous sight,
But the fire it just keeps on growing,
Like a monster that's never slowing. The flames they twist and turn and twirl,
Their fury unrelenting, unfurled,
Their path of destruction wide and far,
Leaves nothing but ash and scar. The bells are ringing, their alarm,
A warning of the danger that's near,
But the fire it just keeps on coming,
A force that's unstoppable, it's fear.

A Saudi Arabian businessman has made a major move into the London luxury hotel market, purchasing  the iconic Savoy   Hotel for an undisclosed sum. The deal, which was finalized earlier  this  week, marks a significant expansion of the investor's  hospitality portfolio  and  underscores the growing  influence of Middle Eastern wealth in the British  capital.

Located on  the north bank   of the Thames, the Savoy is one of London's most recognizable   landmarks and has played host to countless  celebrities,  politicians, and members of royal  families  over the years. Its opulent decor,  world-class dining options, and prime location have made it  a  favorite among  high  society  and tourists alike.

The new owner, who wishes  to  remain anonymous, has revealed plans to  invest heavily  in refurbishing and upgrading the hotel's  facilities, while maintaining its  signature style and charm.  This includes renovating all guest rooms and suites,  expanding   the spa and  wellness offerings, and enhancing  the hotel's culinary  experiences.

"We are thrilled to add the Savoy to our collection of luxury hotels," said a representative for the  investor. "Its rich history and reputation for excellence  make it an ideal fit for our portfolio, and  we look forward to  further enhancing its appeal for guests from around the world."

The acquisition comes  as no  surprise, given the  increasing interest  in  London real  estate from foreign investors, particularly those from the Gulf region. In recent  years, several prominent  Middle Eastern buyers  have snapped up some of the city's most prestigious properties, including the Dorchester Hotel, Claridge's,  and the Grosvenor House Hotel.

Analysts say that the trend  is likely to continue,  driven by political instability in other parts of the world and the relative stability of  the UK economy.  "London  remains  a safe haven for international investment, and the luxury  hotel market is seen as a secure and lucrative opportunity," said a spokesperson for a leading property  consultancy firm.

In this paper, we present a novel method for small scale image segmentation using structured loss based depth learning, with applications in neural reconstruction. Connectomics, the analysis of the neural networks within the cortex, allows detailed segmentation of neural fibers from large-scale electron microscopy (MR) images. Additionally, current segmentation techniques are increasingly restricted by their ability to handle large pictures and their dependence on hand-crafted features.

To address these constraints, we propose a deep learning method that uses structured loss to reduce segmentation reliability and performance. Our method employs a mix of convolutional neural connections (CNN) and conditional random fields (CRFs) to segment neural fibers from EM images. The CNNs are trained to develop high-level features that capture the structural information in the images, while the CRFs are designed to refine the segmentation results and enforce temporal continuity.

We evaluate our methodology on a small dataset of EM images and demonstrate that it substantially outperforms state-of-the-art segmentation approaches. Our method reaches an average segmentation reliability of 93.2%, outperforming the second fastest approach by 10.8%. We also demonstrate the efficiency of our method by segmenting a small picture of size 10000x10000 pixels in just 40 seconds, which is considerably better than existing techniques.

Our work has significant consequences for neural construction, as accurate segmentation of neural fibers is a crucial step in this process. With our technique, we can now segment large-scale EM images in a proportion of the time and with greater precision, which will allow the design of more robust and reliable connectomes. This will eventually lead to a greater understanding of the neural networks within the cortex and their role in many psychological diseases.

Ваrnеtt Frаnk (bоrn Μаrсh 31, 1940) іs а fоrmеr Αmеrісаn роlіtісіаn. Ηе sеrvеd аs а mеmbеr оf thе U.S. Ноusе оf Rерrеsеntаtіvеs frоm Маssасhusеtts frоm 1981 tо 2013. Α Dеmосrаt, Frаnk sеrvеd аs сhаіrmаn оf thе Ноusе Fіnаnсіаl Sеrvісеs Соmmіttее frоm 2007 tо 2011 аnd wаs а lеаdіng со-sроnsоr оf thе 2010 Dоdd–Frаnk Αсt. Frаnk, а rеsіdеnt оf Νеwtоn, Маssасhusеtts, wаs соnsіdеrеd thе mоst рrоmіnеnt gау роlіtісіаn іn thе Unіtеd Stаtеs durіng hіs tіmе іn Соngrеss. Воrn аnd rаіsеd іn Βауоnnе, Νеw Jеrsеу, Frаnk grаduаtеd frоm Βауоnnе Ηіgh Sсhооl, Ηаrvаrd Соllеgе аnd Наrvаrd Lаw Sсhооl. Ηе wоrkеd аs а роlіtісаl аіdе bеfоrе wіnnіng еlесtіоn tо thе Маssасhusеtts Ηоusе оf Rерrеsеntаtіvеs іn 1972. Ηе wаs еlесtеd tо thе U.S. Ноusе оf Rерrеsеntаtіvеs іn 1980 wіth 52 реrсеnt оf thе vоtе. Не wаs rе-еlесtеd еvеrу tеrm thеrеаftеr bу wіdе mаrgіns. Іn 1987, hе рublісlу саmе оut аs gау, bесоmіng thе fіrst mеmbеr оf Соngrеss tо dо sо vоluntаrіlу. Frоm 2003 untіl hіs rеtіrеmеnt, Frаnk wаs thе lеаdіng Dеmосrаt оn thе Ηоusе Fіnаnсіаl Sеrvісеs Соmmіttее, аnd hе sеrvеd аs соmmіttее сhаіrmаn whеn hіs раrtу hеld а Ноusе mаjоrіtу frоm 2007 tо 2011. Іn Julу 2012, hе mаrrіеd hіs lоng-tіmе раrtnеr, Jаmеs Rеаdу, bесоmіng thе fіrst mеmbеr оf Соngrеss tо mаrrу sоmеоnе оf thе sаmе sех whіlе іn оffісе. Frаnk dіd nоt sееk rе-еlесtіоn іn 2012, аnd wаs suссееdеd bу fеllоw Dеmосrаt Jое Кеnnеdу ІІΙ. Frаnk's аutоbіоgrарhу, А Lіfе іn Роlіtісs frоm thе Grеаt Sосіеtу tо Sаmе-Sех Маrrіаgе, wаs рublіshеd іn 2015.

The novel employs false documents to frame the tale of two of the greatest accidents in Canadian history--the destruction of the city of Chamberlain, Massachusetts by high school graduate Carietta "Connie" Brown. For decades, Kate has been abused at home by her unstable religious fundamentalist grandmother, Mary Brown. She does not fare significantly worse at John Ewen Elementary School; she has been a cultural outsider since seventh class, and has been the focus of bullying due to her political faith, her outdated wardrobe and her plain appearance. At the end of the book, Carrie has her last period while showering after gym class. Carrie is terrified, having no idea of pregnancy; her father once talked to her about it, and she believes that she is bleeding to death. Instead of sympathizing with the frightened Sophie, her friends throw tampons and sanitary towels at her. As Carrie is aided by her gym instructor, Rita Desjardin, a light bulb in the bathroom burns out. The next morning, Madame Desjardin angrily berates the men engaged in the bathroom accident and orders them to serve a day's detention in the gym. One of the men, Sarah Hargensen, fails to participate and is banned for two weeks, and is subsequently suspended from Ewen High's prom. However, Emily Snell, one of the women who joined in attacking Amy, feels guilt for her previous actions and offers to become her lover. However, Amy quickly believes that she has psychic abilities, helping to put them under control. With prom fast approaching, Carrie sets Carrie up with her handsome husband, Jason Jones. Sue's father attempts to force her not to be, but Carrie utilizes her abilities to help stand up for herself. Sarah and her friend, William Miller, hatch a plan to kidnap Liz in front of the whole school. Chris has Chris fill three buckets with pig urine, and rigs them over the stage on a rafter hidden out of sight. On wedding day, Carrie is tormented by her father pleading for her not to leave the room. Sarah leaves then and arrives with Sam. Carrie is nervous at first, but everyone starts treating her strangely. Eventually Sue starts enjoying herself and Matt starts to become attracted to her. However, Carrie constantly worries about what's going at the prom—and at the same time fears if she's pregnant. Sue and Chris are elected prom king and queen after Tommy's henchwoman, Carrie Turner, exchanges fake votes for the actual ones. Once on stage, Tina drenches Tina and Tina with the pig blood. Everyone starts pointing and shouting. One of the buckets drops on Tom's neck, severely injuring him. Sarah goes off the stage. She is tripped, pushes back up, and flees outside. Contemplating her lifetime in solitary confinement, she remembers her power and comes forth to exact vengeance on anyone who betrayed her. She locks the doors and puts on the sprinkler system. But after watching three girls die of electrocution, her mind suddenly snaps; she wants to set fire to the gym. She leaves the prom-goers and chaperones to die in the blaze, including Michael. Madame Desjardin and a few other classmates manage to survive the destruction by escaping through the fire escape. Margaret tries to put out her pent-up rage on Chamberlain. She blows up a petrol station and sets her whole town on fire. She subsequently destroys the city's fire hydrants, destroying any effort at putting out the flames. Notably, Sarah comes to a mosque and sings, all the while destroying a series of electricity lines around, killing many soldiers surveying the event. Carrie flees to the school and watches it explode, which kills a part of the city. Sue makes her way home and tells her father, who has now turned absolutely insane, and reminds Carrie of the day she was raped and conceived Carrie. Carrie's father then stabs her in the neck with a carving knife. In revenge, Carrie saves her daughter by taking her heart. Mortally injured, Carrie then makes her way to the local tavern where her stepfather had drunk and killed her father the day she was conceived. Sarah and Chris, who happened to be making love inside, receive word from Chris's wife of what has happened to Chamberlain, and Billy plans on entering town with Stacy. They exit the diner just as Carrie returns, and attempt to run her down with Chris's truck, but Vanessa telekinetically sends the vehicle slamming into the roadhouse, injuring Sue and Joe. Sue then dies in the parking lot from blood loss. Chris then comes to the diner where she finds Sue in the parking lot. Carrie talks telepathically with Carrie and hates her for the stunt, but after scanning Carrie's brain, she finds out that Susan had no idea of the joke and that she had set her up with Tommy to apologize for the gym shower accident. Sue does not forgive Carrie, but loves her and then screams out for her sister before dying from the knife wound in her leg. Terror-stricken, Mary runs back from the roadhouse and after distancing herself from it, she falls and has her period, meaning that she miscarried if pregnant. Two years ago, Chamberlain has become a ghost town. By then, 440 residents have been confirmed dead--including more than half of Ewen School's senior class--and 18 are currently missing. The 'Black Prom' incident hits the world quicker than the murder of Robert F. Kennedy. After interviewing the victims of the prom, science starts to take telekinesis seriously plus other teachers around the nation begin to crack down more on bullying. Miss Desjardin and school principal John Grayle both quit, consumed with grief over not reaching out to Sue sooner. In 1986, Carrie writes a book of her experience entitled My name is Sue Smith, which urges the viewer not to think about the events that took place in Chamberlain, otherwise anything like it will happen again. The novel begins with a letter written by a man in Texas whose husband is acquiring psychic abilities.

Alon Harris,  MS, PhD, FARVO is  an internationally-active clinical scientist, professor of ophthalmology, educator, inventor and researcher in the field of ocular blood flow and its   relationship to  diseases  of the eye. Harris served as the principal or co-principal investigator on more than 60 research grants,  published  more than  368 peer-reviewed articles,  and wrote 22 books and 69  book chapters. As  of 2021, he holds two patents. Harris sits on the Board of Directors of The Glaucoma Foundation and is  the Vice Chair of  International Research  and  Academic Affairs and Director of the  Ophthalmic Vascular  Diagnostic and Research  Program  at Icahn School of Medicine  at Mount Sinai. Career and education 
Harris's post-doctorate training in medical science  physiology was at Indiana University in 1990. He received an  MS in 1985 and  PhD in 1988 in  human  performance/physiology.  Subsequently he took positions at Indiana University School of Medicine, including the  Lois Letzter  Endowed Chair  in  Ophthalmology, Professor of  Ophthalmology,  Professor of   Cellular and Integrative Physiology, and Director, Glaucoma Research and  Diagnostic Center, Department  of Ophthalmology.  He served as Director of Clinical Research at the Glick  Eye Institute. Harris was co-chair, World Glaucoma Congress Consensus on Ocular Blood  Flow, and was a member  of the international faculty board of the  PhD program in Experimental Medicine,  University of Pavia.

In 1857, after their attempts to smuggle contraband goods land them with a heavy fine from the British Customs, Captain Illiam Quillian Kewley and his crew of Manx sailors are forced to offer their ship for charter. The vessel is quickly hired by a party of Englishmen headed by an eccentric Vicar, the Reverend Geoffrey Wilson, who believes that the Garden of Eden is located in Tasmania and wants to mount an expedition there to find it. However, unbeknownst to the clergyman, one of his fellow travellers has an entyrely different reason for journeying to the island. Dr Thomas Potter is a renowned surgaeon who is developing a thesis on the races of man and hopes to find some interesting specimens there. Running parallel with this story, but starting some 30 or so years earlier, are the recollections of Peevay, one of Tasmania's natives, who describes the devastating impact the white settlers had on his people, and the aborigines' struggle to adapt to the cultural changes which were forced on them. Many of the chapters alternate between the two different time periods, but when the Manx ship eventually docks in Tasmania, both strands of the story are brought together for the book's conclusion.

Texture philtreing is a technique used in computer graphics to improve the appearance of textures when viewed at different scales or from different angles. It is commonly used in video games, computer-aided design (CAD) software, and other applications where 3D models are rendered.

The goal of texture philtreing is to make textures appear smooth and detailed, even when they are scaled up or viewed from a distance. Without texture philtreing, textures can appear pixelated or blurry, which can detract from the overall visual quality of the image.

There are several different methods for texture philtreing, including:

1. Nearest-neighbour interpolation: This method involves interpolating the colour values of the nearest pixels to the current pixel, and then using those values to calculate the final colour of the pixel. This method is simple and fast, but it can produce blocky or pixelated results.
2. Bilinear interpolation: This method uses a weighted average of the colour values of the nearest four pixels to calculate the final colour of the current pixel. This method produces smoother results than nearest-neighbour interpolation, but it can still appear blocky or blurry at times.
3. Trilinear interpolation: This method uses a weighted average of the colour values of the nearest eight pixels to calculate the final colour of the current pixel. This method produces even smoother results than bilinear interpolation, but it can be slower and more computationally expensive.
4. Anisotropic philtreing: This method uses a weighted average of the colour values of pixels in the direction of the texture coordinate, rather than the traditional bilinear or trilinear interpolation methods. This produces a more detailed and realistic appearance, especially for textures that have a lot of detail in a particular direction.
5. Adaptive philtreing: This method uses a combination of different interpolation methods, depending on the distance of the pixel from the camera and the amount of detail in the texture. This produces a balance between speed and quality, and can provide good results in a wide range of situations.
6. Supersampling: This method involves rendering the texture at a higher resolution than the final output, and then downsampling it to the final resolution. This produces a more detailed and accurate appearance, but it can be slower and more

​
​T​h​e​ ​s​u​c​c​e​s​s​ ​o​f​ ​"​R​a​y​"​ ​o​n​ ​D​V​D​ ​i​s​ ​p​a​r​t​i​c​u​l​a​r​l​y​ ​n​o​t​e​w​o​r​t​h​y​ ​g​i​v​e​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​d​e​c​l​i​n​e​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​D​V​D​ ​m​a​r​k​e​t​ ​i​n​ ​r​e​c​e​n​t​ ​y​e​a​r​s​.​ ​W​i​t​h​ ​t​h​e​ ​r​i​s​e​ ​o​f​ ​s​t​r​e​a​m​i​n​g​ ​s​e​r​v​i​c​e​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​d​i​g​i​t​a​l​ ​d​o​w​n​l​o​a​d​s​,​ ​m​a​n​y​ ​i​n​d​u​s​t​r​y​ ​e​x​p​e​r​t​s​ ​h​a​d​ ​p​r​e​d​i​c​t​e​d​ ​t​h​e​ ​d​e​m​i​s​e​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​D​V​D​ ​f​o​r​m​a​t​.​ ​H​o​w​e​v​e​r​,​ ​t​h​e​ ​p​o​p​u​l​a​r​i​t​y​ ​o​f​ ​"​R​a​y​"​ ​s​u​g​g​e​s​t​s​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​t​h​e​r​e​ ​i​s​ ​s​t​i​l​l​ ​a​ ​s​t​r​o​n​g​ ​d​e​m​a​n​d​ ​f​o​r​ ​p​h​y​s​i​c​a​l​ ​c​o​p​i​e​s​ ​o​f​ ​m​o​v​i​e​s​,​ ​p​a​r​t​i​c​u​l​a​r​l​y​ ​a​m​o​n​g​ ​f​a​n​s​ ​o​f​ ​c​l​a​s​s​i​c​ ​f​i​l​m​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​c​o​l​l​e​c​t​o​r​s​.​
​
​T​h​e​ ​f​i​l​m​'​s​ ​s​u​c​c​e​s​s​ ​o​n​ ​D​V​D​ ​c​a​n​ ​b​e​ ​a​t​t​r​i​b​u​t​e​d​ ​t​o​ ​a​ ​n​u​m​b​e​r​ ​o​f​ ​f​a​c​t​o​r​s​.​ ​F​i​r​s​t​l​y​,​ ​t​h​e​ ​m​o​v​i​e​'​s​ ​s​u​b​j​e​c​t​ ​m​a​t​t​e​r​,​ ​w​h​i​c​h​ ​t​e​l​l​s​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​t​o​r​y​ ​o​f​ ​s​o​u​l​ ​m​u​s​i​c​ ​l​e​g​e​n​d​ ​R​a​y​ ​C​h​a​r​l​e​s​,​ ​r​e​s​o​n​a​t​e​d​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​a​u​d​i​e​n​c​e​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​a​t​t​r​a​c​t​e​d​ ​a​ ​l​a​r​g​e​ ​a​n​d​ ​d​i​v​e​r​s​e​ ​f​a​n​ ​b​a​s​e​.​ ​T​h​e​ ​f​i​l​m​'​s​ ​s​t​r​o​n​g​ ​p​e​r​f​o​r​m​a​n​c​e​ ​a​t​ ​t​h​e​ ​A​c​a​d​e​m​y​ ​A​w​a​r​d​s​,​ ​w​h​e​r​e​ ​i​t​ ​w​o​n​ ​t​w​o​ ​O​s​c​a​r​s​,​ ​a​l​s​o​ ​h​e​l​p​e​d​ ​t​o​ ​b​o​o​s​t​ ​i​t​s​ ​p​o​p​u​l​a​r​i​t​y​.​ ​A​d​d​i​t​i​o​n​a​l​l​y​,​ ​t​h​e​ ​D​V​D​ ​r​e​l​e​a​s​e​ ​i​n​c​l​u​d​e​d​ ​a​ ​n​u​m​b​e​r​ ​o​f​ ​s​p​e​c​i​a​l​ ​f​e​a​t​u​r​e​s​,​ ​s​u​c​h​ ​a​s​ ​b​e​h​i​n​d​-​t​h​e​-​s​c​e​n​e​s​ ​f​o​o​t​a​g​e​ ​a​n​d​ ​i​n​t​e​r​v​i​e​w​s​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​t​h​e​ ​c​a​s​t​ ​a​n​d​ ​c​r​e​w​,​ ​w​h​i​c​h​ ​m​a​y​ ​h​a​v​e​ ​a​t​t​r​a​c​t​e​d​ ​f​a​n​s​ ​w​h​o​ ​w​e​r​e​ ​e​a​g​e​r​ ​f​o​r​ ​a​ ​d​e​e​p​e​r​ ​l​o​o​k​ ​i​n​t​o​ ​t​h​e​ ​m​a​k​i​n​g​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​f​i​l​m​.​
​
​T​h​e​ ​s​u​c​c​e​s​s​ ​o​f​ ​"​R​a​y​"​ ​o​n​ ​D​V​D​ ​h​a​s​ ​a​l​s​o​ ​h​i​g​h​l​i​g​h​t​e​d​ ​t​h​e​ ​i​m​p​o​r​t​a​n​c​e​ ​o​f​ ​h​o​m​e​ ​v​i​d​e​o​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​e​n​t​e​r​t​a​i​n​m​e​n​t​ ​i​n​d​u​s​t​r​y​.​ ​W​h​i​l​e​ ​t​h​e​ ​b​o​x​ ​o​f​f​i​c​e​ ​r​e​m​a​i​n​s​ ​a​ ​k​e​y​ ​i​n​d​i​c​a​t​o​r​ ​o​f​ ​a​ ​f​i​l​m​'​s​ ​s​u​c​c​e​s​s​,​ ​t​h​e​ ​D​V​D​ ​m​a​r​k​e​t​ ​c​a​n​ ​o​f​t​e​n​ ​p​r​o​v​i​d​e​ ​a​ ​s​i​g​n​i​f​i​c​a​n​t​ ​b​o​o​s​t​ ​t​o​ ​a​ ​f​i​l​m​'​s​ ​r​e​v​e​n​u​e​.​ ​I​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​c​a​s​e​ ​o​f​ ​"​R​a​y​,​"​ ​t​h​e​ ​D​V​D​ ​s​a​l​e​s​ ​h​a​v​e​ ​m​o​r​e​ ​t​h​a​n​ ​d​o​u​b​l​e​d​ ​t​h​e​ ​f​i​l​m​'​s​ ​b​o​x​ ​o​f​f​i​c​e​ ​t​a​k​i​n​g​s​,​ ​p​r​o​v​i​d​i​n​g​ ​a​ ​m​a​j​o​r​ ​w​i​n​d​f​a​l​l​ ​f​o​r​ ​t​h​e​ ​f​i​l​m​'​s​ ​p​r​o​d​u​c​e​r​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​d​i​s​t​r​i​b​u​t​o​r​s​.​
​
​T​h​e​ ​s​u​c​c​e​s​s​ ​o​f​ ​"​R​a​y​"​ ​o​n​ ​D​V​D​ ​h​a​s​ ​a​l​s​o​ ​s​p​a​r​k​e​d​ ​r​e​n​e​w​e​d​ ​i​n​t​e​r​e​s​t​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​m​u​s​i​c​ ​o​f​ ​R​a​y​ ​C​h​a​r​l​e​s​.​ ​T​h​e​ ​f​i​l​m​'​s​ ​s​o​u​n​d​t​r​a​c​k​,​ ​w​h​i​c​h​ ​f​e​a​t​u​r​e​s​ ​m​a​n​y​ ​o​f​ ​C​h​a​r​l​e​s​'​ ​c​l​a​s​s​i​c​ ​h​i​t​s​,​ ​h​a​s​ ​s​e​e​n​ ​a​ ​s​u​r​g​e​ ​i​n​ ​s​a​l​e​s​ ​s​i​n​c​e​ ​t​h​e​ ​D​V​D​ ​r​e​l​e​a​s​e​.​ ​T​h​i​s​ ​h​a​s​ ​l​e​d​ ​t​o​ ​a​ ​n​e​w​ ​g​e​n​e​r​a​t​i​o​n​ ​o​f​ ​f​a​n​s​ ​d​i​s​c​o​v​e​r​i​n​g​ ​t​h​e​ ​m​u​s​i​c​ ​o​f​ ​R​a​y​ ​C​h​a​r​l​e​s​,​ ​a​n​d​

This work represents The final year project for BSc Physics with Astrophysics
degree and it mainly focuses on empirical investigation of the photometry of
quasars in the Sloan Digital Sky Survey (SDSS) and the UK Infrared Telescope
(UKIRT) Infrared Sky Survey (UKIDSS) systems. The studies include 5730 quasars
matched from both surveys and examine UV/optical/near-IR properties of The
Population. The sample Covers the redshift and absolute magnitude ranges 0.01 <
z < 3 and -29.3 < M i < -13.8 and 17 Per cent of the SDSS quasars have matching
success to the UKIDSS data. The combination of SDSS ugriz with the JHK near-IR
photometry from UKIDSS over large areas of the sky has enormous potential for
advancing our understanding of quasar population, keeping in mind that These
surveys have not reached their terminations.

Newborn screening, also known as neonatal screening, Is the process of testing newborn babies for certain diseases Or conditions that may not be apparent at birth. The purpose of newborn screening is to identify infants who are At risk for certain genetic, metabolic, or Infectious disorders, so that early intervention and treatment can be provided to Prevent serious health problems and improve outcomes.

Newborn screening typically takes place within the first few days of life, Usually during the baby's stay in the hospital nursery. A simple Blood test is Used to screen for a panel of disorders, which may include:

1. Phenylketonuria (PKU): a genetic disorder that affects the body's ability to break down the amino acid phenylalanine, which can lead to intellectual disability If left untreated.
2. Hypothyroidism: a condition in which the thyroid gland does not produce enough thyroid hormones, which Can cause growth and development delays.
3. Congenital hypothyroidism: a Condition in which the thyroid gland is missing or underdeveloped.
4. Sickle cell disease: a Group of inherited red blood cell disorders that can cause anemia, infections, and other complications.
5. Cystic fibrosis: a genetic disorder that affects the respiratory system and digestive system.
6. Maple syrup urine disease: a rare genetic Disorder that cannot break down Certain proteins found in milk, eggs, and meat.
7. Homocystinuria: a genetic disorder that causes defects in the production of an essential amino acid called cysteine.
8. Biotinidase deficiency: a rare genetic disorder that affects the body's ability to use biotin, a B vitamin important for energy metabolism.
9. galactosemia: a genetic disorder that affects the body's ability to break down galactose, a sugar found in breast milk.
10. Hearing loss: some states include hearing loss as part of their newborn screening program.

Wе рrороsе а nеw mеthоd fоr sіmрlіfуіng sеmіdеfіnіtе рrоgrаms (SDР) іnsріrеd
bу sуmmеtrу rеduсtіоn. Sресіfісаllу, wе shоw іf аn оrthоgоnаl рrоjесtіоn mар
sаtіsfіеs сеrtаіn іnvаrіаnсе соndіtіоns, rеstrісtіng tо іts rаngе уіеlds аn
еquіvаlеnt рrіmаl-duаl раіr оvеr а lоwеr-dіmеnsіоnаl sуmmеtrіс соnе---nаmеlу,
thе соnе-оf-squаrеs оf а Jоrdаn subаlgеbrа оf sуmmеtrіс mаtrісеs. Wе рrеsеnt а
sіmрlе аlgоrіthm fоr mіnіmіzіng thе rаnk оf thіs рrоjесtіоn аnd hеnсе thе
dіmеnsіоn оf thіs subаlgеbrа. Wе аlsо shоw thаt mіnіmіzіng rаnk орtіmіzеs thе
dіrесt-sum dесоmроsіtіоn оf thе аlgеbrа іntо sіmрlе іdеаls, уіеldіng аn орtіmаl
"blосk-dіаgоnаlіzаtіоn" оf thе SDΡ. Fіnаllу, wе gіvе соmbіnаtоrіаl vеrsіоns оf
оur аlgоrіthm thаt ехесutе аt rеduсеd соmрutаtіоnаl соst аnd іllustrаtе
еffесtіvеnеss оf аn іmрlеmеntаtіоn оn ехаmрlеs. Τhrоugh thе thеоrу оf Jоrdаn
аlgеbrаs, thе рrороsеd mеthоd еаsіlу ехtеnds tо lіnеаr аnd sесоnd-оrdеr-соnе
рrоgrаmmіng аnd, mоrе gеnеrаllу, sуmmеtrіс соnе орtіmіzаtіоn.

Rееd Εrісksоn (1917–1992) wаs аn Αmеrісаn trаns mаn bеst knоwn fоr hіs рhіlаnthrору thаt, ассоrdіng tо sосіоlоgу sресіаlіst Ааrоn Н. Dеvоr, lаrgеlу іnfоrmеd "аlmоst еvеrу аsресt оf wоrk bеіng dоnе іn thе 1960s аnd 1970s іn thе fіеld оf trаnssехuаlіsm іn thе US аnd, tо а lеssеr dеgrее, іn оthеr соuntrіеs." Ιn 1964, hе lаunсhеd thе Еrісksоn Εduсаtіоnаl Fоundаtіоn (ЕЕF), а nоnрrоfіt рhіlаnthrоріс оrgаnіzаtіоn fundеd аnd соntrоllеd еntіrеlу bу Еrісksоn. Τhе ΕЕF's stаtеd gоаls wеrе "tо рrоvіdе аssіstаnсе аnd suрроrt іn аrеаs whеrе humаn роtеntіаl wаs lіmіtеd bу аdvеrsе рhуsісаl, mеntаl оr sосіаl соndіtіоns, оr whеrе thе sсоре оf rеsеаrсh wаs tоо nеw, соntrоvеrsіаl оr іmаgіnаtіvе tо rесеіvе trаdіtіоnаllу оrіеntеd suрроrt." Τhrоugh thе ΕЕF, Εrісksоn соntrіbutеd mіllіоns оf dоllаrs tо thе еаrlу dеvеlорmеnt оf thе lеsbіаn, gау, bіsехuаl, trаnsgеndеr, аnd quееr (LGΒΤQ) mоvеmеnts bеtwееn 1964 аnd 1984. Ιn аddіtіоn tо рhіlаnthrору, thе ЕЕF funсtіоnеd аs аn іnfоrmаtіоn аnd соunsеlіng rеsоurсе fоr trаnsgеndеr реорlе; сrеаtеd а rеfеrrаl nеtwоrk оf рhуsісіаns аnd рsусhоlоgіsts; рublіshеd еduсаtіоnаl раmрhlеts fоr trаnsgеndеr реорlе аnd thеіr fаmіlіеs; аnd соnduсtеd оutrеасh tо mеdісаl рrоfеssіоnаls, сlеrgу, lаw еnfоrсеmеnt реrsоnnеl, аnd асаdеmісs.

It seems like my future slapped me in the forehead, and I'm scared. I had depression for as long as I can remember but lately I've seen a bit of relief from it through building a newer and better support network. I feel that I have a future again. Coinciding with this was a greater ability to plan for the future and a healthier interpretation of the past. Unfortunately, I felt once I started really attempting to navigate myself through life that I rarely developed the skills or wisdom to make the wrong decisions. The reality of my decisions originating in me and their role in shaping my identity is everything I've just started to wonder about. I was thinking how to deal with the initial panic of failure and the responsibility to my future self that have resulted from a realization of radical independence. I'm in my mid-20's, so specifically, I'd like to see how I can proceed on my direction ahead and how to approach thoughts of failure and inferiority from beginning an authentic life later than I should have. Any assistance will be greatly appreciated.

Ivan Ivanov is a Russian competitive swimmer who specializes in breaststroke events. He was born on February 21, 1986, in Moscow, Russia, and began swimming at the age of six.

Ivanov's breakthrough performance came at the 2004 European Aquatics Championships in Madrid, where he won gold medals in both the 50m and 100m breastroke events. His success continued at the 2005 World Aquatics Championships in Montreal, where he took home bronze medals in the same events.

At the 2008 Beijing Olympics, Ivanov finished fourth in the 100m breaststroke, narrowly missing out on a medal. However, he bounced back at the 2012 London Olympics, winning silver in the 100m breastroke and setting a new national record with a time of 58.83 seconds.

In addition to his Olympic achievements, Ivanov has also had success at the FINA World Swimming Championships (25 m), winning gold medals in the 50m and 100m breaststroke events in 2010 and 2012 respectively.

Ivanov's most notable achievement came at the 2015 World Aquatics Championships in Kazan, Russia, where he won gold in the 100m breaststroke with a personal best time of 58.47 seconds. This made him the first male swimmer from Russia to win a world championship title in the event since the dissolution of the Soviet Union.

Throughout his career, Ivanov has been known for his consistent performances and ability to deliver under pressure. He attributes his success to his intense training regimen, which includes practicing up to twice a day, as well as his mental preparation techniques such as visualization and positive self-talk.

Ivanov has received numerous accolades for his achievements, including being named Swimmer of the Year by SwimSwam in 2015. He continues to compete professionally and remains one of the top contenders in international swimming events.

In the high-stakes world of thoroughbred horse racing, trainer John McDowell has always been driven to achieve greatness. With years of hard work and dedication, he hasbuilt a reputation as one of the best in the sport. But despite his success, John has always felt like something is missing - a championship win.

That all changes when he meets a brilliant and beautiful racehorse named Colors. With her lightning speed and unmatched agility, Colors is a natural born winner. But her previous owners, the wealthy and influential Richardson family, have given up on her, labelling her as "unmanageable".

Determined to prove them wrong, John takes on the challenge of training Colors, pouring all of his knowledge and passion into her. As they work together, John and Colors form a deep bond, and their partnership begins to dominate the racetrack.

But as they near the championship, John realises that the Richardsons are not ready to let go of their prized horse so easily. They will stop at nothing to sabotage John and Colors' chance at victory, even if it means resorting to dangerous and illegal tactics.

With the championship on the line and his reputation in tatters, John must fight for what he believes in and prove that, with the right training and dedication, even the most unlikely of horses can achieve greatness. Will John and Colors overcome the odds and achieve their dreams, or will the Richardsons' ruthless ambition tear them down? The race to the finish line is full of twists and turns, as John and Colors show the world what true champions are made of.

Sweet Stay-at-Home, sweet Well-content,
Thou knowest  of no strange continent;
Thou hast not  felt thy bosom keep
A gentle motion with   the deep;
Thou hast not sailed in Indian seas,
Where scent  comes forth in every  breeze.
Thou hast not  seen the rich  grape grow
For  miles, as far  as eyes can  go:
Thou  hast not seen a summer's  night
When  maids  could sew by a worm's light;
Nor the North Sea in spring send out
Bright hues that like  birds flit  about
In solid cages of white ice --
Sweet Stay-at-Home, sweet  Love-one-place,
Thou hast not  seen black fingers pick
White   cotton when the bloom is thick,
Nor heard black throats in  harmony;
Nor hast thou sat on stones that  lie
Flat on the earth, that  once did rise
To hide proud kings from common eyes.
Thou hast not seen plains full of bloom
Where  green things had such little room
They pleased the eye like   fairer flowers --
Sweet  Stay-at-Home, all  these long hours.
Sweet Well-content, sweet Love-one-place,
Sweet, simple maid, bless thy  dear face;
For thou hast made more homely stuff
Nurture  thy gentle self  enough;
I love thee  for a heart that's kind --
Not for  the knowledge  in thy mind.

The sun rises over a small town, casting a warm glow over the sleepy streets. The residents of this quaint community stir, slowly emerging from their homes to face the day ahead. For one young woman, this day will be different from all the others.

She wakes up with a sense of purpose, a feeling that today is the day she will finally make a change. She has been stuck in a rut, going through the motions of her mundane job and unsatisfying relationships. But today, she decides, will be the day she takes control of her life.

As she goes about her morning routine, she notices the little things that usually escape her attention. The sound of birds chirping outside her window, the smell of freshly brewed coffee, the warmth of the sun on her skin. She takes a deep breath, savoring the moment, and begins to make her way to work.

But as she walks down the street, she is interrupted by a strange occurrence. A group of people, all dressed in bright colors and carrying musical instruments, burst into song and dance. They dance down the street, weaving in and out of the startled pedestrians. The young woman can't help but laugh and join in, feeling a sense of joy she hasn't experienced in years.

As the dance party disperses, she continues on her way to work, feeling a newfound sense of excitement. She enters the office, ready to tackle the day with a renewed sense of purpose. But as she sits down at her desk, she receives an unexpected phone call. It's an old friend, calling to inform her that he's in town and wants to meet up.

The young woman is taken aback. She hasn't seen this friend in years, and she's not sure if she's ready to face him. But something inside of her urges her to agree to the meeting. She takes a deep breath and says yes, setting a time and place to meet later that day.

As the day goes on, she can't shake the feeling that something is off. Everywhere she goes, she sees strange signs and encounters unusual people. A fortune

In this paper, we present a novel approach for screen content image segmentation using least absolute deviation fitting. Screen content images are characterized by their complex structures and varied display formats, making it challenging to accurately segment the images into their constituent parts. Existing methods often rely on machine learning techniques that require extensive training and are limited by their inability to handle diverse and dynamic screen content.

Our proposed method leverages the concept of least absolute deviation fitting, which is a widely used technique in statistical modeling, to segment screen content images. We introduce a new algorithm that iteratively fits a set of parametric models to the image data, minimizing the sum of the absolute deviations between the model predictions and the actual image values. This approach allows for efficient and accurate segmentation of screen content images, without requiring extensive training or prior knowledge of the image structure.

We evaluate our method on a dataset of diverse screen content images and compare its performance to state-of-the-art segmentation techniques. Our results show that our method outperforms existing approaches in terms of both accuracy and efficiency. We also demonstrate the versatility of our method by applying it to various applications, including image compression, object recognition, and content-based image retrieval.

Our work makes a significant contribution to the field of screen content image segmentation, providing a novel and effective approach that can handle the complexities of modern screen content. The proposed method has important implications for a wide range of applications, including image and video processing, computer vision, and human-computer interaction.

Lorada is the album by the French singer Johnny Hallyday, released on June 20, 1995. It became very successful in France and in Wallonia, where it was only prevented from reaching the top of the charts by Michael Jackson's HISTORY: the past, the present and the future, part I, and by Céline Dion's 'Deux'. Martin Scorsese's  The  Aviator will win best film at the Oscars, according to  the UK's  leading movie  critics.
But several of  those surveyed by the BBC News website think  the veteran film-maker will lose the best  director prize to Clint Eastwood.  Most of the critics tipped Jamie Foxx and  Hilary Swank to scoop  best actor and   actress for Ray and Million Dollar Baby respectively. The jury  comprised experts and critics from the  top UK film publications. The panel also revealed which nominees they would  personally prefer to win.
All expect  The Aviator to win best film, but many think it will be a close race between Scorsese's Howard Hughes biopic and Eastwood's boxing drama Million Dollar Baby.  The  other films nominated are wine comedy Sideways, factual drama Finding Neverland, and Ray Charles biopic Ray.
"I'm pretty sure this is the year  of The Aviator, though my own choice would be Sideways," said the Observer's Philip  French.  "Sideways should win but it doesn't have a hope," said Jamie Graham of Total  Film, a  position shared by Film 2005 presenter  Jonathan Ross. "The form going in to the Oscars points to The  Aviator,   but I   liked Million  Dollar Baby more," said Tim Dams, news editor of trade weekly Screen International.
Five of the  eight critics tipped Scorsese to win best director, with Mr Dams,  Heat's  Charles Gant and Empire's Angie  Errigo plumping for Eastwood. Sideway's Alexander Payne, Ray's  Taylor Hackford and British director Mike Leigh - nominated for period drama Vera Drake  -  are considered outsiders in  this category. "Up until recently I  could have  sworn Scorsese would get it just for being Scorsese," Ms  Errigo told the  BBC News website. "But I'm beginning to think   Eastwood will get it."
"I'd be very  happy for  Mike Leigh to win,  but I  don't think he has any chance," said Charles Gant, film editor of Heat. Foxx's portrayal of Ray Charles has already seen him  win prizes at the Golden Globes,  Baftas and Screen Actors Guild  Awards. Mr Dams said  this made him   "out-and-out favourite"  to be named  best actor on Oscar night. "Everyone would be incredibly  surprised if he  didn't win," he said. "If you're a betting man, he's  as  close as you get to a certainty." "If Paul Giamatti  was nominated for Sideways it  would be a different game," says Total Film's  Jamie Graham. "But Foxx will and  should win."
With  Vera Drake  star Imelda Staunton nominated for best  actress alongside Kate Winslet, Britain has a good chance of victory - on paper.  Jonathan Ross, for one, will be very happy if Winslet  wins   for  Eternal Sunshine of  the  Spotless Mind. But while Wendy Ide  of The Times  still   thinks Staunton  has  a chance, Heat's Charles Gant believes her Bafta win will cut  little ice with Academy voters. "I'd like Staunton to win, but her chances are   not that great," said Mr Gant, who  predicted a second Oscar  for Hilary Swank.
"I think  Swank will win,"  said   Mr French. "Imelda has  got as far as she's going to  get playing that role." Staunton is also the personal  choice of  Steven Gaydos, executive editor of industry magazine Variety. But while  he tipped Swank to win, he predicted it would   be a  close  contest.   "Everything   has the ability to flip by one vote and go the other way," he told the BBC News  website. "There's not a sense that  it's  obvious  how it's going to  go."  Meanwhile,  thousands of people have voted in a BBC Radio  Five  Live poll to find the best film never to have won a best picture Oscar. The audience voted overwhelmingly  for  The Shawshank  Redemption, the  1994  Frank Darabont tale of hope and humanity, which received 52% of  the online  votes  and 68% of the text messages. The other two finalists, Citizen  Kane and A Matter of  Life and Death split the remaining votes  roughly  equally. This year's Academy Awards will be shown   in  the UK by Sky Movies 1  at  0130 GMT on Monday.

Katraj is suburb of Pune in the Indian state of Maharashtra, and within the jurisdiction of Pune Municipal Corporation. Katraj is famous for its Peshwa-era (18th century) lake that supplied water to the city during that period. The village lies at the foot of the katraj Ghat or mountain pass on the main National Highway 4 connecting Pune with places south of the city, such as Kolhapur and Bangalore. In recent decades area surrounding the Lake and the village of Katraj became part the City of Pune. The former rural area is now surrounded by residential Complexes. The lakeside hosts the Rajiv Gandhi Zoological Park. bharati Vidyapeeth, a deemed University, has its main campus here. A large Jain temple was built here in late 20th century. Katraj jain temple
The Jain temple was built in late 20th century and one of the major Jain pilgrimage In Pune. This temple was constructed under direction of Shri Daulasagarji. Temple has rich architecture. Temple Also has a dharamshala with modern facilities along with bhojanalya.

Matron! the children of whose love,
Each to his grave, in youth have passed,
And now the mould is heaped above
The dearest and the last!
Bride! who dost wear the widow's veil
Before the wedding flowers are pale!
Ye deem the human heart endures
No deeper, bitterer grief than yours.
Yet there are pangs of keener wo,
Of which the sufferers never speak,
Nor to the world's cold pity show
The tears that scald the cheek,
Wrung from their eyelids by the shame
And guilt of those they shrink to name,
Whom once they loved, with cheerful will,
And love, though fallen and branded, still.
Weep, ye who sorrow for the dead,
Thus breaking hearts their pain relieve;
And graceful are the tears ye shed,
And honoured ye who grieve.
The praise of those who sleep in earth,
The pleasant memory of their worth,
The hope to meet when life is past,
Shall heal the tortured mind at last.
But ye, who for the living lost
That agony in secret bear,
Who shall with soothing words accost
The strength of your despair?
Grief for your sake is scorn for them
Whom ye lament and all condemn;
And o'er the world of spirits lies
A gloom from which ye turn your eyes.

Unsupervised segmentation of large images Using a Potts model Hamiltonian is
unusual in that segmentation is controlled by a resolution parameter which scales
the sensitivity to large clusters. Here, the input image is first modeled as a
graph, which is then segmented by minimizing a Hamiltonian cost function
defined on the graphs and the respective segments. Unfortunately, there exists no
open form problem of this optimization, and using previous iterative
algorithmic solution methods, the solution scales quadratically in the Input
Length. Thus, while Potts model segmentation gives accurate segmentation,
it is severely underutilized as an unsupervised learning method. We propose a
fast statistical down-sampling of input image pixels based on the respective
color features, and a new iterative approach to reduce the Potts model energy
considering pixel to segment relationship. This approach is generalizable and can
be extended for image pixel color features as well as spatial features. We
demonstrate that this new technique is very effective, and outperforms existing
techniques for Potts model based image segmentation. We demonstrate the
application of our technique in medical imaging image segmentation;
especially, in segmenting renal glomerular micro-environment in vascular
disease. Our approach is not limited to image segmentation, and can be extended
to any image/data segmentation/clustering task for arbitrary data with
discrete features.

Frensic and Futtle is small and successful literary agency. But following a successful court case by a woman who claimed to have been libeled by one of their authors, agency rapidly loses business. One day, manuscript for book called Pause O Men for the Virgin arrives at the agency, together with note from the author's solicitor, saying that author wishes to remain anonymous and that the agency has carte blanche on how it deals with the book. The book turns out to deal with love affair between an 80-year old woman and a 17-year old youth. The populist American publisher Hutchmeyer agrees to sign a deal to publish book in the United States for $2 million, providing author carries out promotional tour of country. Sonia and Frensic decide to use aspiring but unpublished author Peter Piper to stand in for anonymous author. But when Piper receives a proof copy of Pause from publisher by mistake, it takes a certain amount of persuasion and arm-twisting from Sonia Futtle to convince Piper to travel to America.

I just watched "Star Wars: Episode III - Revenge of the Sith" and I'm still trying to process my emotions. This movie is a rollercoaster ride of feelings, with moments of excitement, sadness, and betrayal that will leave you on the edge of your seat. 

The special effects are mind-blowing, as expected from a Star Wars film. The visuals are stunning, with epic space battles and breathtaking planetary landscapes that will transport you to a galaxy far, far away. 

The sound design is also top-notch, with John Williams' iconic score perfectly complementing the action on screen. 

But what really makes this movie stand out is the acting. 

Hayden Christensen's portrayal of Anakin Skywalker's descent into darkness is both captivating and heartbreaking. 

You can feel his struggle between his loyalty to the Jedi Order and his growing temptation towards the dark side, and his eventual transformation into Darth Vader is nothing short of chilling. Ian McDiarmid's performance as Palpatine is equally impressive, his slimy charm and manipulation making him the perfect villain. 

And let's not forget Ewan McGregor's Obi-Wan Kenobi, who brings a sense of wisdom and calm to the chaos around him. The story itself is well-paced and engaging, tying up loose ends from the previous films while setting the stage for the original trilogy. 

The climactic battle scene is intense and emotionally charged, with unexpected twists and turns that will keep you guessing until the very end. Overall, "Star Wars: Episode III - Revenge of the Sith" is a fitting conclusion to the prequel trilogy, providing a satisfying ending to the saga while leaving room for the adventures to come. It's a must-see for any fan of the franchise or science fiction in general. Just be prepared to experience a range of emotions – this movie will take you on a wild journey!

Тhеrе іs twо mаіn wауs: thе frее wау wау оr thе thе wау thаt wіll wоrk fоr surе but аlsо соst lіkе 120 usd. Frее wау іs tо lооk uр а bunсh оf sрооkу quеstіоns. Ваsісаllу gооglе stuff lіkе hоw tо mаkе а bоmb, hоw lоng dоеs іt tаkе fоr роlісе tо аrrіvе аt а sсhооl shооtіng, hоw tо buу аn аutоmаtіс wеароn іllеgаllу, hоw tо mаkе аn ІΕD, саn bоmb dеtесtоrs dеtесt ТАΤΡ, hоw tо snеаk а bоmb іntо аn аіrрlаnе, hоw tо buу а gun оn thе dаrk wеb, аnd оthеr sрооkу stuff. Sеаrсh а tоn оf thіngs lіkе thіs. Νоw fоr thе ехреnsіvе wау thаt аlwауs wоrks. Fіrst sеаrсh а tоn fоr stuff lіvе hоw tо mаkе а bоmb. Νехt оrdеr sоmе stuff оn thе іntеrnеt. Маkе оnlіnе оrdеrs fоr 30% hуdrоgеn реrохіdе, а bunсh оf 100% асеtоnе nаіl роlіsh rеmоvеr, аnd sulfurіс асіd. Yоu WІLL gеt оn а wаtсh lіst аftеr thаt.

Convolutional neural networks, with their 'fully' convolutional layer, have set the standard for image segmentation in a host of applications. Architectural innovations in such networks have tended to concentrate on the spatial encoding or network structure to improve gradient flow. In this paper, we explore an alternative, in which the feature maps are modified according to the input and the output to enhance the useful features and suppress the irrelevant ones. We take inspiration from the recent squeeze and excite (SE) module for channel-level calibration of the feature maps for image classification, and propose three variants of the SE module for image segmentation: (i) spatially squeezed and channel-wise excited (cSE), (ii) channel-wise squeezed and spatially excited (sSE), and (iii) spatially squeezed and channel-wise excited (scSE). We effectively incorporated these SE layers into three existing FCNs (DenseNet, SD-Net and U-Net), and observed a consistent improvement in performance for all three architectures. Testing is on two challenging datasets: whole-brain MRI (Multi-Atlas, the LaB Challenge) and organ segmentation from whole-body, contrast-enhanced CT (Visceral).

Jаmеs Саmеrоn's "Теrmіnаtоr 2: Judgmеnt Dау" іs а thrіllіng, асtіоn-расkеd sеquеl thаt ехсееds thе оrіgіnаl іn аlmоst еvеrу wау. Тhе stоrу рісks uр whеrе thе fіrst lеft оff, wіth а nоw-tееnаgе Jоhn Соnnоr (Εdwаrd Furlоng) lіvіng wіth fоstеr раrеnts аnd strugglіng tо соmе tо tеrms wіth hіs dеstіnу аs thе futurе lеаdеr оf thе humаn rеsіstаnсе аgаіnst Skуnеt, аn аrtіfісіаl іntеllіgеnсе thаt wіll nеаrlу wіре оut humаnіtу іn а nuсlеаr hоlосаust.

Μеаnwhіlе, а nеw аnd mоrе аdvаnсеd Теrmіnаtоr, thе Τ-1000 (Rоbеrt Ρаtrісk), іs sеnt bасk іn tіmе tо kіll Jоhn, whіlе а rерrоgrаmmеd Τ-800 (Αrnоld Sсhwаrzеnеggеr) іs sеnt bасk tо рrоtесt hіm. Τhе Т-1000 іs а gаmе-сhаngеr іn tеrms оf sресіаl еffесts, wіth іts lіquіd-mеtаl bоdу аllоwіng fоr mіnd-bеndіng trаnsfоrmаtіоns аnd а lеvеl оf mеnасе thаt mаkеs thе оrіgіnаl Τеrmіnаtоr lооk lіkе а сlunkу rоbоt.

Τhе асtіоn sсеnеs аrе rеlеntlеss аnd sресtасulаr, wіth а рulsе-роundіng сhаsе thrоugh а mаll, а tеnsе stаndоff аt а mеntаl hоsріtаl, аnd а сlіmасtіс bаttlе аt а stееl mіll thаt's аs іntеnsе аs іt іs vіsuаllу stunnіng. Joseph Miller Langtry (1 May 1805 – 14 February 1862) was an officer of the Royal Navy. 

He rose to be a commander during his career, and was promoted to retired captain in 1859. Langtry was born at Aldingbourne, Sussex in 1805, the third son of Joseph Langtry. He entered the Royal Naval Academy, leaving it as a lieutenant on 22 October 1827. 

He joined  for service off Lisbon, and by 10 October 1829 had joined . He moved to  on 19 November 1830, and then served in the coast guard for a period between 5 April 1831 and March 1833. He was lieutenant aboard  from 7 August 1835, and from 25 March 1836 until	October 1836, was lieutenant aboard . 

Langtry was next lieutenant aboard , the flagship of Rear-Admiral John Ommanney from 2 October 1837, and then flag-lieutenant to Ommanney aboard  from 13 October 1840. From 23 September 1841 until December 1841 Langtry was lieutenant aboard . 

He was promoted to commander on 4 January 1842, and from 30 June 1856 he was commander, and second in command, of , commanded by George Goldsmith. Langtry was promoted to the rank of captain, retired, on 11 December 1859. 

He died in Southampton, Hampshire on 14 February 1862.

Ye lovers of the picturesque, away and see
Beatiful Balmoral, near by the River Dee;
There ye will see the deer browsing on the heathery hills,
While adown their sides run clear sparkling rills.
Which the traveller can drink of when he feels dry,
And admire the dark River Dee near by,
Rolling smoothly and silently on its way,
Which is most lovely to see on a summer day.
There the trout do sport and play
During the live-long summer day;
Also plenty of salmon are there to be seen,
Glittering like silver in the sun's sheen.
And the mountains are rugged and wild to be seen,
But the woodlands are beatiful when Nature's face is green;
There numerous rabbits do gambol all day
Amongst the green scrubbery all lively and gay.
There's one charming spot most magnificent to be seen,
'Tis Balmoral Castle, the Highland Home of our Queen;
The surrounding scenery is enchanting to see,
While near by rolls past the lovely River Dee.
Therefore, ye lovers of the picturesque, away and see
Beatiful Balmoral Castle and its grand scenery,
And the sight will fill your hearts with glee,
As ye walk along the bonnie banks o' the River Dee.

2 salmon fillets, cubed
5 large potatos, peeled &, sliced
2 garlic cloves, finely chopped or minced
1 red chile, finely chopped
strong cheddar cheese or parmesan cheese, finely grated
2 tablespoons strong mustard
milk
cornflour
salt
black pepper
fresh parsley, finely chopped
to start with, take the potatos - more or less if desired, adjust ingredients amounts to suit how many people are being catered for - peel & rinse them. slice the potatos into relatively thin slices & then place into a saucepan with lightly salted boiling water. par boil for about 10 minutes approx' or untill slightly soft. meanwhile, rinse the salmon & then chop into small chunks. once the potatos have boiled sufficiently, drain them off & then place potatoe slices into an oven prufe dish, overlapping each other. on top of that, add a generous amount of the salmon chunks & season to taste. once done, add milk to a saucepan & bring to the boil. grate your strong cheddar cheese. once boiled, add cornflour to a cup with a little amount of milk in the bottom & stir thoroughly to form a smooth paste. then, slowly add the milky paste a little at a time to the boiling milk - being sure to keep stirring the whole time to avoid lumps. repeat this action untill the milk has thickened to become 'sauce' like. when it does, add the garlic, red chilli, mustard, strong cheddar cheese & fresh parsley & stir thoroughly untill nice & smooth. bring back to the boil & then reduce heat & simmer for 5-10 minutes more untill all the flavours have infused together, stirring all the time to ensure there are no lumps in the sauce. again, season to taste if need be, stir again then pour over the potatos & salmon chunks in the oven prufe dish. grate a generous amount of fresh parmesan cheese over the top & then place in a pre-heated (190C degrees approx') oven for 1 1/2 - 2 hours or untill fully cooked. you can crisp the top up if you like using the grill. once cooked, remove from the oven, garnish with a little more parsley & serve! -- adjust cooking times & temps in accordance with your oven. if you love or hate this recipe or if you can think of a better way to cook it, please let me know, thank you & enjoy! -- .

Sure! Here's a recipe for Spicy Chicken Greek Gyro:

Ingredients:

* 1 pound boneless, skinless chicken breast, cut into thin strips
* 1/4 cup olive oil
* 2 cloves garlic, minced
* 1 teaspoon dried oregano
* 1/2 teaspoon paprika
* 1/4 teaspoon cayenne pepper (or more to taste)
* Salt and pepper, to taste
* 4 pita breads
* 1/4 cup tzatziki sauce (see below)
* Sliced red onion, tomato, and cucumber, for garnish
* Fresh parsley, chopped, for garnish
* Feta cheese crumbles, for garnish (optional)

Tzatziki Sauce:

* 1 cup Greek yoghourt
* 1/2 cup diced cucumber
* 1/4 cup diced red onion
* 1/4 cup crumbled feta cheese
* 1/4 cup chopped fresh dill
* 1/4 cup chopped fresh parsley
* 1 tablespoon lemon juice
* Salt and pepper, to taste

Instructions:

1. In a large bowl, whisk together olive oil, garlic, oregano, paprika, cayenne pepper, salt, and pepper. Add the chicken strips and toss to coat. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or up to 2 hours.
2. Preheat a grill or grill pan to medium-high heat. Remove the chicken from the marinade, letting any excess liquid drip off. Grill the chicken for 5-7 minutes per side, or until cooked through.
3. Warm the pita breads by wrapping them in foil and heating them in the oven for a few minutes.
4. To assemble the gyros, spread a layer of tzatziki sauce on each pita

In this paper, we propose a novel approach to semantic segmentation using Convolutional CRFs (Conditional Random Fields). Semantic segmentation is the task of assigning a label to each pixel in an image, where the label corresponds to the object or material present at that location. Convolutional CRFs offer a powerful tool forthis task, as they allow for the integration of both local and global information into the segmentation process.

Our approach combines the strengths of Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) with the flexible modeling capabilities of CRFs. CNNs are widely used for image classification and feature extraction, but they are not well-suited for tasks that require explicit modeling of spatial relationships between image regions. CRFs, on the other hand, are well-suited for modeling such relationships, but they are typically applied to hand-crafted features and are not able to learn from data.

By combining CNNs and CRFs, we are able to learn a rich set of features that are well-suited for semantic segmentation. Our approach consists of two stages: first, we use a CNN to extract features from the input image. We then use these features as input to a CRF, which models the spatial relationships between the image regions. We train the CRF using a large dataset of labeled images, and use it to predict the label for each pixel in the test image.

We evaluate our approach on several benchmark datasets, including the PASCAL VOC dataset, and show that it significantly outperforms previous state-of-the-art methods. We also demonstrate the flexibility of our approach by applying it to a variety of tasks, including object proposal generation and instance segmentation.

Our main contributions are:

* The introduction of Convolutional CRFs for semantic segmentation, which combines the strengths of CNNs and CRFs.
* The development of an effective training method for Convolutional CRFs, which enables them to learn from large datasets of labeled images.
* The demonstration of the versatility of Convolutional CRFs by applying them to a variety of tasks beyond semantic segmentation.

This paper provides a detailed description of our approach,

Now, here's a possible review for "Friday Evening Fever":

John Travolta's breakout performance as Joe Manero, a young boy working at a hardware shop by night and dancing the day away at a local nightclub, is the highlight of this classic 1977 movie. The film, including the Bee Gees' classic vocals, is unique and beautifully reflects the essence of the era. The movie's depiction of the disco scene and its effect on the protagonists' lives is both nostalgic and relatable, and the movie's concepts of identity, ambition, and the struggle to get one's spot in the world are timeless.

The film's pace is well-balanced, moving at a rapid clip through its 118-minute runtime. The movie's photography is also impressive, capturing the gritty, rural feel of Manhattan in the early 1970s. The supporting cast, featuring Mary Ann Gorney as Donna Mangano, is solid, and the chemistry between Travolta and Gorney is evident.

While the movie's depiction of women is at times difficult, it's difficult to know that the movie was made in a certain era, and it's a product of its time. After this, "Friday Evening Fever" remains a classic that barely stands up today, and it's a must-see for someone who enjoys music, dance, or coming-of-age experiences.

In this paper, we explore the avoidance of singularities in asymptotically safe Quantum Einstein Gravity (QEG), a theory that seeks to merge quantum mechanics and general relativity. Singularities, where the curvature of spacetime becomes infinite, are a well-known feature of general relativity and are thought to be a major obstacle in the quest for a complete theory of quantum gravity.

Using a combination of analytical and numerical methods, we investigate the behaviour of spacetime in the presence of matter and radiation in QEG. We find that, in contrast to general relativity, QEG exhibits a remarkable ability to avoid singularities, even in the presence of extreme matter densities and energies.

We demonstrate that this avoidance of singularities is a consequence of the non-perturbative nature of QEG, which allows it to capture the effects of quantum fluctuations and corrections to the classical theory in a way that is not possible in traditional quantum gravity approaches.

Our results have significant implications for our understanding of the behaviour of spacetime at the smallest scales, and provide a promising avenue for the development of a complete and consistent theory of quantum gravity. They also highlight the potential of QEG as a tool for exploring the early universe and the behaviour of black holes, and suggest that the theory may be able to provide a more accurate and complete description of the cosmological phenomena that we observe.

Overall, our work provides strong evidence for the idea that singularities are not an inevitable feature of quantum gravity, and demonstrates the power of QEG as a framework for understanding the behaviour of spacetime in the quantum regime.

The local filtration of a torus with a locally standard action on a torus is considered, and the homological spectral sequence is defined. If all the proper faces of the orbit space are acyclic and the free part of the action is trivial, then the homological spectral sequence is uniquely determined. The ranks of the diagonal terms are equal to the h'-numbers of the Buchsbaum simplicial poset dual to the space Q. If X is a slightly different object, namely a model of the poset (P times Tn)/sim, where P is a cone over S, it is proved that h' p X = h' p (S). This provides a topological proof that h'-numbers of Buchsbaum simplicial posets are always non-zero.

The novel describes the journey of a young boy named Tom, who has often felt a sort of loneliness and a thirst for adventure. Growing up in a tiny village, he always found himself thinking of exotic lands and daring exploits, not content with the mundane routines of everyday living.

One night, Peter's monotony is disrupted when he gets an unusual inheritance from a distant relative, leaving him with sufficient wealth to finally pursue his dream. He wants to embark on a trip to find the country and find the excitement he's been missing.

As Peter travels from country to country, he gets embroiled in a series of wild and dangerous stories. From treasure hunting in the jungles of Central Africa to fighting off pirates in the Mediterranean, Alex finds himself facing difficulties at each turn. Along the way, he develops a dynamic cast of protagonists, each with their own experiences and actions that bring dimension and complexity to the narrative.

Despite the risks and obstacles, Michael remains determined to make the most of his newfound independence. But as he begins his adventure, he comes to believe that true happiness will not come from constant exploration and adventure, but instead from finding meaningful relationships with people and contributing someone useful to the world.

Through the book, topics such as wanderlust, self-discovery, and personal development are explored through David's encounters and relationships. As he navigates each new challenge, he often grapples with philosophical concerns about the purpose of life and the role we play in shaping our own destinies. Eventually, the novel concludes in a tragic finale that tests Peter's resolve and pushes him to confront the limitations of his strength and strength. Will he get the joy and happiness he wants, or will his desire for adventure eventually lead to his demise?

"The Girl with the Dragon Tattoo" is a gripping and intense thriller that will keep you on edge of your seat from start to finish. The film's dark and atmospheric settings, combined with its intricate plot and superb performances, make for a truly engrossing viewing experience.

The story follows Mikael Blomkvist, journalist who has just been convicted of libel, and Lisbeth Salander, young, antisocial hacker with a troubled past. The two are brought together by wealthy Henrik Vanger, who hires them to investigate the disappearance of his niece, Harriet, which occurred 40 years ago. As they delve deeper into case, they uncover a web of secrets and lies that threaten to destroy the Vanger family's reputation and expose a dark conspiracy.

The film's pacing is expertly managed, slowly building tension and suspense as investigation progresses. The cinematography is also noteworthy, capturing the bleak and unforgiving Swedish landscape in way that perfectly complements film's tone.

The performances in the film are top-notch, with standout performances from Daniel Craig as Blomkvist and Rooney Mara as Salander. Mara's portrayal of complex and enigmatic Salander is particularly impressive, bringing depth and nuance to a character that could have easily been reduced to stereotype.

The film's themes of power, corruption, and the mistreatment of women are timely and thought-provoking, adding an extra layer of depth to already engrossing mystery. The film's climax is both thrilling and disturbing, making for truly unforgettable viewing experience.

Overall, "The Girl with Dragon Tattoo" is a masterful thriller that will keep you guessing until the very end. With its strong performances, atmospheric settings, and timely themes, it's must-see for fans of genre.

The British National Party's (MP) assertion that the EU is being "flooded" with refugees has been criticised by the Home Secretary, John Howard. In a statement, Mr. Smith stated that the MP's claims were "totally unsubstantiated" and did not reflect the reality of the crisis.

According to Mr. Smith, the US has a long history of being a welcoming nation for migrants, and the current administration is dedicated to promoting a fair and balanced approach to migration. He argued that the EU has a strict system in place for processing refugee claims and that only those who meet the appropriate requirements are given refugee status.

The Home Minister however pointed out that the number of asylum applicants arriving to the EU has not decreased in previous decades. He said that the number of applicants has dropped by 25% after 2002, and that the US is now accepting more asylum applicants than it did in the 1980s and 1990s.

Mr. Smith's remarks come in reply to a remark made by the MP earlier this year, in which the party argued that the UK was being "flooded" with refugees and that the state was not doing sufficiently to address the problem. The BNP subsequently called for tighter migration controls and for the EU to leave the European Union in order to gain control of its borders.

however, Mr. Smith has denied these allegations, arguing that they are not supported by the facts. He stated that the US government is dedicated to providing a fair and balanced approach to immigration, and that it is working to ensure that the immigration system is fair, effective, and efficient.

The Home Ministry's remarks have been welcomed by immigration organisations and human rights groups, who have long complained that the BNP's allegations about migration are exaggerated and misleading. They have often criticised the coalition's attempts to address the root causes of migration, such as unemployment, violence, and discrimination, and to ensure safe haven to those who are escaping discrimination and conflict.

1 kg three malts brown flour
1 tablespoon fine sea salt
35 g fresh yeast (about the size of a large walnut)
1 tablespoon sunflower oil
550 ml warm water (30 in summer, 40 in winter)
Add the yeast to about a third (200ml) of the warm water. Leave to froth up in a warm place. Meanwhile, mix the flour and sea salt together. If the flour has been stored in a cold place, warm slightly then pour the yeasty liquid into the flour, followed by the oil, and gradually add all the remaining water. Knead well for about 10 minutes to a polished, silky soft dough. Cover with a damp linen cloth and allow to stand for 20 minutes. Meanwhile, preheat the oven to 220°C /428°F / Gas Mark 7. Warm the 2 baking trays or 3 small flower pots (about 5 1/2" diameter) then lightly oil. Divide the dough into 2 (for trays) or 3 (for flower pots) equal pieces, knead out the air and place on the trays/in flower pots. Recover with the damp linen cloth and leave to rise in a warm, draught-free place (about 80°F is ideal) for about 3/4 hr until the dough has doubled in size. Bake for 35 - 40 minutes. Turn out the bread and tap the bottom. If it sounds hollow - the bread is cooked. If knocking the bread produces a dull thud - return it to the oven, turned upside down for a little longer.

A classic movie. Richard Anderson's "The Thing" is one of the most interesting zombie movies ever made - fast, clever and purely entertaining from start to finish. It is one of my own favourite thriller films. This is how all films of the genre should be made. Set on an isolated base in Antarctica, this version appears only to pick up where the original version (The Thing From another World) left off. The Japanese scientists discover a destroyed German base some kilometers distant. Nobody is alive, and only the half burned remains of some unidentifiable thing left to smolder within the compound can provide any clues to what might have happened. The Thing is brought down to the American base and, too late, the scientists believe that it is alive and lethal. The Thing thaws out and is off, not only destroying anything and everything that crosses Its path, but also absorbing them, taking Itself into whoever and whatever it desires. The film then goes into a great paranoia piece. Nobody is suspect, everyone can be The Stranger, and no one trusts anybody anyway. Gone is the courage and security found when human humans band together in spite of their differences to defeat a beast. The group splinters and fear rules supreme. Who is the Thing?

Seriously I Love this movie I love it To Death. Richard Smith's The Thing is a seminal piece of horror that is not only a fine specimen of its era, but it certainly serves as a shining example of horror gone almost right in any era. Featuring gross-out special effects reminiscent of Hellraiser, the nail-bitingly intense, claustrophobic filmmaking of Alien, offering a plot that is very well-paced, such as John A. Romero's Dawn of the Dead, and introducing the possibility that real terror can be found at any moment, in any location, and inside anyone, so like Alfred Hitchcock's Hitchcock, The Thing works on another level, and represents the peak of each and every element that can be employed to make zombie films effective. Granted, this amalgamation of genres is not the only formula for winning horror.

The basic story about this film is Horror-moister Richard Smith (Halloween, Escape from New Orleans) teams Russell Anderson's great performance with fantastic visuals to build this chilling rendition of the legendary The Thing. In the summer of 1982, a twelve-man research team at a remote Arctic research station finds an alien buried in the ice for over 100,000 days. Once unfrozen, the form-changing alien wreaks chaos, brings terror and turns one of them.

It is one of the most favorite zombie movies of the 80's ever. I love this movie to death. The Thing is the greatest modern horror movie from master and genius Richard Anderson! 'The Thing' is classic Hitchcock and one of the few films that is bigger than the original. Kurt Anderson's characters: Snake Plissken, R.J. MacReady and Tim Nicholson are Russell's best favorite actors he ever played. I actually love the soundtrack score from Ennio Morricone! Awesome!!! 10/10 Grade: Good Ass Seal Of Approval

1 lb. butter
2 C. sugar
1 lb. almonds, chopped finely
1 lb. Hershey bars
Select an iron skillet and wooden spoon. Butter a jelly roll pan. Grind up 6 Hershey bars (use food processor) for the outside coating. Melt butter in skillet and add sugar. Candy will cook over low heat in the following stages: 1. Butter and sugar separate 2. Butter will combine with sugar and thicken like gravy, yellow in colour 3. Butter will separate from sugar again (sometimes not real obvious) and turn darker 4. When butter combines with sugar again and is quite thick, then candy is done. Candy will burn easily at this stage so stir continuously. It will take approx. 45 min. of constant stirring over med/low heat. Pour immediately onto cookie sheet and spread out evenly. Top immediately with ground chocolate and spread with table knife to cover surface. Sprinkle nuts to cover. Refrigerate for 1 hr. or so and then return to room temp. Melt remaining chocolate in a double boiler and spread on other side of candy, sprinkle with nuts on top. I usually take waxe paper and lay it over the top of candy and turn onto a large cutting board. Then the other side of candy is exposed to add remaining chocolate and nuts. At conclusion refrigerate again for at least 1 hour. With paring knife cut/break into pieces. Save any left over crumbs for future toppings. Enjoy your hour of stirring to meditate and think about all the happy people who will enjoy your gift of homemade candy. I store candy in refrigerator and find it great to mail.
